PN CN112649043-A
TI Indoor environment monitoring system comprises adjusting unit and alarm unit electrically connected with controller, and exhaust fan, air inlet fan, filter and humidifier are electrically connected with controller.
AU TAO J
AE SUZHOU MULTITECH AUTOMATION EQUIP CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202143447P
AB    NOVELTY - Indoor environment monitoring system comprises an adjusting unit and an alarm unit electrically connected with a controller. The adjusting unit is provided with an exhaust fan, an air inlet fan, a filter and a humidifier. The exhaust fan, the air inlet fan, the filter and the humidifier are electrically connected with the controller. A monitoring unit is provided with a temperature sensor, a humidity sensor, a carbon dioxide sensor, a carbon monoxide sensor and a PM2.5 concentration detection sensor. The temperature sensor, the humidity sensor, the carbon dioxide sensor, the carbon monoxide sensor and the PM2.5 concentration detection sensor are electrically connected with the controller.
   USE - Used as indoor environment monitoring system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: is simple to operate and convenient to use; can automatically detects and adjusts indoor environment condition; and has high intelligent degree and large-scale popularization and use.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the indoor environment monitoring system.

PN CN212920174-U
TI Raw material weighing device for producing supercritical carbon dioxide foaming insulation board, has weighing barrel fixedly connected on bracket, where weighing barrel is located below discharging port of raw material barrel.
AU QIAN S
   JIANG L
   WANG F
   MENG X
   JIANG Y
   WANG B
   DU Y
   WU S
   XUE B
   WANG X
AE SHANDONG JISHUN ENERGY-SAVING BUILDING (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202141392P
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a raw material weighing device for producing supercritical carbon dioxide foaming insulation board, comprising a bracket; a raw material barrel and a weighing barrel fixedly connected on the bracket, and a weighing sensor set below the weighing barrel; the weighing barrel is located below the discharging port of the raw material barrel; the discharging port of the raw material barrel is provided with an intermittent feeding device. The utility model can intermittently release the material in the weighing barrel, and can set the weight of the material released in the material barrel for one time; when the weight in the weighing barrel reaches the specified weight, the intermittent feeding device can be stopped in the intermittent time of releasing the material, so as to improve the weighing precision of the material.

PT P
PN CN112169715-A; CN112169715-B
TI Preparing conductive polymer composite gel,            comprises e.g. heating melamine foam in the atmosphere            of nitrogen or nitrogen and carbon dioxide, soaking            three-dimensional continuous porous carbon in the gel            solution, and molding.
AU LIN Z
   CAO L
   QIN C
AE UNIV JINAN (UJIN-C)
GA 202107359E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing conductive polymer composite gel,                comprises (i) taking melamine foam, cleaning and                drying, (ii) heating the melamine foam in the                atmosphere of nitrogen or nitrogen and carbon                dioxide to obtain the three-dimensional continuous                porous carbon, and (iii) soaking the                three-dimensional continuous porous carbon in the                gel solution, and then molding to obtain the                conductive polymer composite gel.
   USE - The method is useful in preparing conductive                polymer composite gel.
   ADVANTAGE - The conductive polymer composite gel:                successfully overcomes the technical problems of                the existing method that the conductive material is                difficult to disperse in the gel and the conductive                material is easy to aggregate; is structurally                connected to each other to form continuous                conductive path; and has excellent                conductivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                conductive polymer composite gel, obtained by above                mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The cleaning in step (i)                  includes cleaning using ethanol and/or deionized                  water. After cleaning in step (i), the method                  further includes soaking the melamine foam in the                  vinegar-acrylic emulsion. The soaking step                  includes soaking the cleaned melamine foam in the                  vinegar-acrylic emulsion for 1-3 hours. The                  heating reaction in step (ii) includes reacting                  at 500-1000 degrees C for 2-5 hours. In step                  (ii), heating and reacting the melamine foam                  under nitrogen and carbon dioxide atmosphere; the                  method of the heating reaction includes placing                  the melamine foam into the tube furnace,                  introducing nitrogen, heating at 400-600 degrees                  C (preferably at 500 degrees C) at 1-3 degrees                  C/minute (preferably at 2 degrees C/minute),                  maintaining for 0.5-1.5 hours (preferably 1                  hour), adding carbon dioxide on the basis of                  nitrogen, heating at 700-900 degrees C at heating                  rate of 1-3 degrees C/minutes (preferably at 2                  degrees C/minute), maintaining for 1-3 hours                  (preferably for 2 hours), and then naturally                  cooling to room temperature to obtain                  three-dimensional continuous porous carbon. The                  method comprises maintaining the pressure in the                  tube furnace at 8-12 MPa after the nitrogen is                  introduced; and maintaining the pressure in the                  tube furnace at 10-15 MPa after introducing                  carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The                  viscosity of the vinegar-acrylic emulsion is                  500-1000 cPS.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC A04-G07; A05-B01; A10-E05B; A12-E01
IP B01J-013/00; C01B-032/05

PT P
PN EP3750940-A1
TI Producing polyether carbonate polyol, comprises            e.g. adding alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide into            H-functional starter substance in presence of double            metal cyanide catalyst to obtain reaction mixture            containing polyether carbonate polyol.
AU VOR D E L
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
GA 2020C52867
AB    NOVELTY - Producing polyether carbonate polyol,                comprises (i) adding alkylene oxide and carbon                dioxide into H-functional starter substance in the                presence of a double metal cyanide catalyst or a                metal complex catalyst based on the metals zinc                and/or cobalt to obtain reaction mixture containing                polyether carbonate polyol, and (ii) adding at                least one component (K) into the reaction mixture                containing polyether carbonate polyol, where the                component includes at least one compound including                monocarboxylic acid, polycarboxylic acid,                hydroxycarboxylic acid and/or vinylogous carboxylic                acid. The compounds include phosphorus-oxygen bond                or compounds of phosphorus which forms at least one                phosphorus-oxygen (P-O) bonds through reaction with                hydroxy (OH)-functional compounds excluded from                component (K).
   USE - The method is useful for producing polyether                carbonate polyol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes volatile constituents i.e.                cyclic propylene carbonate after separation of                volatile constituents.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises (iii) thermally reducing content of                  volatile constituents from polyether carbonate                  polyol at 80-200 degrees C before step (ii), (iv)                  adding 5-2000 ppm, preferably 30-500 ppm at least                  one component (K) into the reaction mixture                  containing polyether carbonate polyol obtained                  from step (iii). The step (ii) is carried out by                  adding 5-2000 ppm, preferably 30-500 ppm                  component (K) into the reaction mixture. The step                  (i) comprises (1) initially taking H-functional                  starter substance or a mixture of at least two                  H-functional starter substances or suspending                  agent, removing water and/or other volatile                  compounds at increased temperature and/or reduced                  pressure, adding catalyst into the H-functional                  starter substance or to the mixture of at least                  two H-functional starter substances or to the                  suspension medium before or after drying, (2)                  activating double metal cyanide (DMC) catalyst,                  adding partial amount (based on the total amount                  of the amount of alkylene oxides used in the                  activation and copolymerization) of alkylene                  oxide into the mixture obtained from step (1),                  adding partial amount of alkylene oxide, reacting                  in the presence of carbon dioxide, waiting for                  the temperature peak and/or a pressure drop in                  the reactor which occurs due to the following                  exothermic chemical reaction, and performing                  activation for several times, and (3) adding                  alkylene oxide, carbon dioxide and optionally                  H-functional starter substance into the mixture                  obtained from the step (2), where at least one                  H-functional starter substance is added in at                  least one of steps (1) or (3), removing the                  reaction mixture obtained from the step (3) from                  the reactor, where the DMC catalyst is                  continuously metered into the reactor. Preferred                  Components: The component (K) is monocarboxylic                  acid, hydroxycarboxylic acid and vinylogous                  carboxylic acids. The component (K) includes a                  compound of formic acid, acetic acid, propionic                  acid, butyric acid, stearic acid, palmitic acid,                  oleic acid, linoleic acid, linolenic acid,                  benzoic acid, acrylic acid, adipic acid, azelaic                  acid, succinic acid, glutaric acid, isophthalic                  acid, malonic acid, oxalic acid, sebacic acid,                  terephthalic acid, malic acid, citric acid,                  glycolic acid, salicylic acid, tartaric acid,                  lactic acid, 2-hydroxybutanoic acid,                  2-hydroxyglutaric acid, mandelic acid, tartronic                  acid, glycolic acid and/or ascorbic acid,                  preferably ascorbic acid, malic acid, succinic                  acid and salicylic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyether                  carbonate polyol is polyether carbonate of                  formula                  (G1-(O-CH2-CH(-R)-O-C(=O)-)e(-O-CH2-CH(-R))f-OH)                  (I). The polycarboxylic acid is dicarboxylic acid                  (preferred) or tricarboxylic acid.e/f = 2:1-1:20.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)
MC A02-A00A; A05-E06A; A05-H01A; A10-D03; A10-D05

PN TW202037508-A
TI An emergency rescue system for in-vehicle organisms via internet.
AU YU B
   HO C L
   HE Y
   ZHUANG Z
AE YU B (YUBB-Individual)
   HO C L (HOCL-Individual)
   HE Y (HEYY-Individual)
   ZHUANG Z (ZHUA-Individual)
GA 2021590852
AB    NOVELTY - The present invention is to provide an emergency rescue system for living creatures in a vehicle via a network, which includes an in-vehicle rescue system, a vehicle-mounted device, a remote server, and a terminal device. The vehicle-mounted device has a computing module and an execution module. Group, a temperature and humidity sensor module, a carbon dioxide sensor module, a biological sensor module and a communication module, when the sensor modules detect the temperature or/and carbon dioxide data and biological Information is processed by the computing module and sent to the remote server through the communication module for storage and processing, and then sent to the terminal device. If the detected data is abnormal, the computing module of the vehicle device will send instructions to the execution module To activate the rescue system in the car, the remote server will also send an emergency message to the terminal device, which can prevent the car owners negligence from causing life-threatening danger and can grasp the situation in the car.

PN CN211648269-U
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle power generation system, has dry quenching device for cooling hot coke by inert gas, and heater for providing high temperature inert gas to heat supercritical carbon dioxide flowing through heater.
AU CHEN J
   ZHAO L
   ZHANG S
   WEI Z
AE SHANGHAI CHAOLIN POWER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020A0875B
AB    NOVELTY - The embodiment of the invention claims a supercritical CO2 Brayton cycle power generation system, comprising a supercritical CO2 Brayton cycle power generation device and a dry quenching device. wherein the supercritical CO2 Brayton cycle generating device is used for generating electricity by using supercritical CO2 as the circulating working medium; the supercritical CO2 Brayton cycle generating device comprises a heater; the dry quenching device is connected with the supercritical CO2 Brayton cycle generating device through a heater, the dry quenching device is used for cooling the hot coke by inert gas; providing high temperature inert gas for the heater, so as to heat the supercritical CO2 flowing through the heater; the waste heat generated in the coke cooling process is used for supercritical CO2 power generation, which is good for energy saving, reducing emission, promoting environmental protection and sustainable development of society.
DC Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P))

PN CN111138623-A; CN111138623-B
TI Preparing silicone-modified polyether-polycarbonate waterborne polyurethane by mixing epoxy compound and carbon dioxide, preparing silicone modified polyether-polycarbonate polyol, stirring, and reacting polyurethane prepolymer.
AU FAN H
   MA Z
   ZHANG X
   REN J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202042459S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing silicone-modified polyether-polycarbonate waterborne polyurethane, involves (a) mixing epoxy compound, carbon dioxide, initiator, catalyst A with epoxy-containing silane coupling agent, and preparing silicone modified polyether-polycarbonate polyol through reaction, where the initiator is chosen from polyhydroxy small molecules or polyhydroxy polymers, (b) stirring the organosilicon-modified polyether-polycarbonate polyol prepared in step (a), diisocyanate, polyhydroxy hydrophilic chain extender and catalyst (B), and reacting to prepare a polyurethane prepolymer with cynate ion groups at the ends, (c) reacting the polyurethane prepolymer with cynate groups at the end prepared in step (b) with an alkaline neutralizer to obtain an aqueous polyurethane prepolymer, and (d) mixing the aqueous polyurethane prepolymer prepared in step (c), water and a polyamine chain extender to prepare a silicone-modified polyether-polycarbonate aqueous polyurethane through reaction.
   USE - The method is used for preparing silicone-modified polyether-polycarbonate waterborne polyurethane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has excellent tensile strength, heat resistance and water resistance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline neutralizer in step (c) is chosen from triethylamine, ammonia, or sodium hydroxide, the molar ratio of the alkaline neutralizer to the polyhydroxy hydrophilic chain extender is 1:(0.8-1.1), the temperature of the reaction is 30-45 degrees C, and the reaction time is 20-40 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The epoxy compound in step (a) is chosen from ethylene oxide or propylene oxide. The initiator is chosen from polypropylene glycol. The epoxy group-containing silane coupling agent is chosen from KH560 (RTM: 3-glycidoxypropyltrimethoxysilane). The initiator in step (a) is chosen from polypropylene glycol with a number average molecular weight of 400 g/mol, the reaction temperature is 100 degrees C, and the molar ratio of the epoxy compound, the initiator and the epoxy-containing silane coupling agent is 1:(1.19-4.26)%:7.5%. The diisocyanate in step (b) is at least one chosen from 2,4-toluene diisocyanate, diphenylmethane diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate, dicyclohexylmethane diisocyanate, and 1,6-hexanediisocyanate, the polyhydroxy hydrophilic chain extender is chosen from 2,2-dimethylolpropionic acid and/or 2,2-dimethylolbutanoic acid, the catalyst (B) is chosen from dibutyltin dilaurate, the molar ratio of the diisocyanate, silicone-modified polyether-polycarbonate polyol and polyhydroxy hydrophilic chain extender is 1:(15-40)%:(20-40)%, the mass ratio of the diisocyanate to catalyst (B) is 1:(0.01-0.1)%, and the temperature of the reaction is 75-85 degrees C, and the reaction time is 1.5-3 hours. The polyamine chain extender in step (d) is chosen from at least one of ethylenediamine, 1,3-propanediamine, and 1,4-butanediamine, the molar ratio of the polyamine chain extender to diisocyanate is 1:(1.6-3.3), the mass ratio of the water-based polyurethane prepolymer to water is (25-60):100, the temperature of the reaction is 15-30 degrees C, and the reaction time is 1-2 hours. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the epoxy compound, the initiator and the epoxy-containing silane coupling agent in step (a) is 1:(1-5)%:(5-15)%, and the carbon dioxide pressure in the reactor is 2-5 MPa. Preferred Properties: The number average molecular weight of the polypropylene glycol is 200-1000 g/mol. Preferred Conditions: The reaction temperature in step (a) is 80-120 degrees C, and the time is 5-24 hours, where the catalyst (A) is chosen from double metal cyanide complexes, and the mass ratio of the epoxy compound to the catalyst A is 1:(0.01-0.1)%.

TI Preparation of isobutylene by catalytic cracking of tert-butylmethylphenol comprises mixing acidic molecular sieve catalyst and tert-butylmethylphenol, reacting mixture fully, and separating product and catalyst from fluidized bed reactor.
AU ZHANG S
   MA Y
   FENG Y
   LIU J
   LIU K
   LI S
   ZHANG M
AE SHANDONG JINGBO WOOD BASED MATERIAL CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202038036X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of isobutylene by catalytic cracking of tert-butylmethylphenol comprises mixing acidic molecular sieve catalyst from catalyst inlet and tert-butylmethylphenol with carrier gas in fluidized bed reactor, reacting mixture fully, and separating product and catalyst at top of fluidized bed reactor, obtaining isobutylene in gas phase and methylphenol in liquid phase.
   USE - Method for preparing isobutylene by catalytic cracking of tert-butylmethylphenol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses renewable acidic molecular sieve catalyst and makes reaction time significantly shorter while ensuring raw materials conversion rate of greater than or equal to 99.5%, product specificity of greater than or equal to 99%, and purity of greater than or equal to 99.5%. The method has no wastewater generation, and the catalyst regeneration generates trace carbon dioxide which can be incorporated into a carbon dioxide treatment device. It also has simple product separation and low production cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The carrier gas is nitrogen. The acidic molecular sieve is SH-ZSMN-21 (RTM: zeolite molecular sieve), SH-ZSMN-22 (RTM: zeolite molecular sieve), or SH-ZSMN-24 (RTM: zeolite molecular sieve).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The temperature of inlet section of tert-butylmethylphenol in the fluidized bed reactor is 40-90 degrees C. The temperature of catalyst inlet section is 90-120 degrees C. The temperature in reaction section is 150-210 degrees C. The catalyst regeneration is carried out at 500-700 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours. Preferred Component: The tert-butyl methylphenol is tert-butyl m-cresol, tert-butyl p-cresol, or tert-butyl o-cresol.

TI Separating lignin from black liquor discharged from kraft cooking wood process, by adding acid or carbon dioxide to black liquor to precipitate lignin, suspending in water and sodium hydroxide, precipitating and dehydrating precipitate.
AU WATANABE N
   KANEKO R
   NURUKI Y
   TSUJI S
   MIYAWAKI S
AE NIPPON PAPER IND CO LTD (NSES-C)
GA 2020203469
AB    NOVELTY - Method for separating lignin from black liquor discharged from process of kraft cooking wood, involves (a) adding acid and/or carbon dioxide to the black liquor to lower the pH to less than 10 to precipitate lignin and then to dehydrate the lignin, (b) suspending the lignin precipitate obtained in step (a) in water and adding sodium carbonate, (c) adding acid to the suspension obtained in step (b) to adjust the pH to less than 3, and precipitating lignin, and (d) dehydrating and washing the lignin precipitate obtained in step (c).
   USE - The method is useful for separating lignin from black liquor, where lignin is used as a thermosetting resin, a dispersant and an adhesive, and used as a phenol resin raw material or an epoxy resin raw material after reducing their molecular weight.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces clogging in the filter.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The sodium carbonate added in step (b) is 5-60 %mass based on lignin (solid content).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the dehydration is performed by a filter press device. In step (a), the air permeability of the filter cloth used in the filter press is 0.5-60 cm3/cm2/second. In step (d), the pH of the washing water used for washing is 6 or more. In step (d), the washing is performed by a filter press device. The washing is performed until the electric conductivity of the washing filtrate obtained in step (d) is 0.5 S/m or less. Preferred Components: The black liquor has a solid content of 30 %mass or less.

   ADVANTAGE - The microcellular foamed material is                biodegradable and has improved product quality. The                preparation process is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of microcellular foamed involves 80-98                pts. wt. polylactic acid, 0.5-2 pts. wt. nucleating                agent, 0.3-5 pts. wt. chain extension agent, 0.2-5                pts. wt. antioxidant, 0.3-3 pts. wt. plasticizer,                entering the stage (S1) twin-screw extruder through                the feeding port for melt plasticization, injecting                the product into a stage (S2) single-screw                extruder, performing microcellular foaming, cooling                and shaping the extruded foam sheet, and placing                the obtained foamed sheet in air at normal                temperature for 8-24 hours.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The nucleating agent                  comprises one or more of nano-organic                  montmorillonite, edible-grade talc, and                  nano-silica. PN CN110540903-A
TI Extraction of eucalyptol by drying blue Eucalyptus, crushing, sieving, soaking in water, adding into super-high pressure extracting device, sealing, raising pressure, releasing pressure instantly, and supercritical carbon dioxide extracting.
AU YANG C
AE JIANGSU ZHINENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A43728
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of eucalyptol comprises drying blue Eucalyptus, adding in a crusher to crush, sieving by 40-80-mesh sieve, soaking in water at material to liquid ratio of 1:7-9, soaking for 24 hours, adding the soaked material into super-high pressure extracting device, sealing at room temperature, raising the pressure to 200-400 MPa, releasing the pressure instantly, putting the treated material in an extraction kettle, supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extracting at 20-30 MPa, 35-45 degrees C and CO2 flow rate of 15-25 kg/hour for 2-3 hours, obtaining Eucalyptus oil with fragrance, purifying the obtained eucalyptol by ethyl ether, and removing the residual water to obtain pure eucalyptol.
   USE - The method is for extraction of eucalyptol.
   ADVANTAGE - Compared with the traditional method, the method has simple operation, sufficient color and fragrance retention, high content and good quality.

AU SCHROETER S
   KUKKALA P
   VISWANATHAN G
   MAIORANA A
   KERKAIDOU A
   SCHROTH S
AE HEXION GMBH (HEXI-Non-standard)
   HEXION GMBH (HEXI-Non-standard)
   HEXION GMBH (HEXI-Non-standard)
   HEXION GMBH (HEXI-Non-standard)
   BAKELITE GMBH (BAKE-C)
GA 2019A1205T
AB    NOVELTY - A composition comprises a binary mixture of                ethoxylated bisphenol F and propoxylated bisphenol                F, in the weight ratio of 20:80-80:20.
   USE - Composition is used for preparing polyurethane                or polyisocyanurate in form of prepolymer, foam,                insulating material, coating, paint, elastomer,                adhesive, sealant and/or composite material (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition provides polyurethane or                polyisocyanurate-based polymer product having                excellent process engineering handleability at 20                degrees C, miscibility with isocyanate component                and flame resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the composition, which involves                adding (s1) bisphenol F to a reactor, melting at                120-160 degrees C, adding alkaline medium,                tempering up to 180 degrees C, adding ethylene                carbonate, removing carbon dioxide and optionally                adding acid for neutralization, adding (s2)                bisphenol F to reactor, melting at 120-160 degrees                C, adding alkaline medium, tempering up to 180                degrees C, adding propylene carbonate, removing                carbon dioxide and optionally adding acid for                neutralization, and combining (s3) obtained                components of steps (s1) and (s2) in a                solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ethoxylated                  bisphenol F is obtained by reacting bisphenol F                  and ethylene carbonate. The propoxylated                  bisphenol F is obtained by reacting bisphenol F                  and propylene carbonate. The molar ratio                  bisphenol F and ethoxylating agent is at least                  1:2. The molar ratio bisphenol F and                  propoxylating agent is at least 1:2. The weight                  ratio of ethoxylated bisphenol F and propoxylated                  bisphenol F is 30:70-70:30. The composition                  further comprises an alkoxylated resorcinol. The                  alkoxylated resorcinol is obtained by reacting                  resorcinol with ethoxylating agent and/or                  propoxylating agent. The weight ratio of binary                  mixture and the alkoxylated resorcinol is                  80:20-20:80.

AU DONG H
AE UNIV SHAANXI TECHNOLOGY (SHTH-C)
GA 2019A0418T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing non-fired pavement brick, comprises (i) introducing vanadium-titanium magnetite ore and quicklime in mass ratio of 88.6-96.1:3.6-11.4 wt.% into a frequency-vibration stirring device and uniformly refining to obtain non-fired pavement brick precursor powder; and (ii) adding water into precursor powder, blowing carbon dioxide, uniformly stirring, placing in permeable mold, forming under high pressure and naturally drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing non-fired pavement brick from vanadium-titanium magnetite ore.
   ADVANTAGE - The pavement brick: has greater than or equal to 788.5 Mpa compressive strength, greater than or equal to 1.5 Gpa microhardness, greater than or equal to 120 ml/m3 water permeability and greater than or equal to 3.4 kg/m3 density. The method: has high component control precision, strong process stability and repeatability; and is environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The grinding medium is an alumina ball with a diameter of 28-35 mm and the ball-material ratio is 1:1. The addition amount of water is 18-25 wt.% and carbon dioxide is 240-320 ml/m3 of mixture. Preferred Method: The vibration frequency of the frequency vibration stirring device is 2 Hz and the main shaft speed is 220-340 revolutions/minute. The forming pressure of the mixture is 130-210 MPa. The drying temperature is 10-41 degrees C.

PN CN110336016-A
TI Preparing lithium aluminum manganate comprises e.g. ball milling manganese piece, adding water and ammonium chloride, reacting, washing, drying, adding industrial grade lithium carbonate, introducing carbon dioxide and thermally reacting.
AU BAO W
   PEI X
   LUO Y
   QIAN Y
   SHE S
   LIU C
   WANG F
   CHEN J
AE SINOSTEEL NANJING NEW MATERIAL INST CO (SIST-C)
   SINOSTEEL ANHUI TIANYUAN TECHNOLOGY CO L (SIST-C)
GA 201986703K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lithium aluminum manganate comprises (i) ball milling manganese piece into manganese powder, and mixing water and ammonium chloride uniformly, and reacting by air, (ii) sequentially adding aqueous ammonia, ammonium chloride mixed solution and aluminum chloride solution into the solution of step (i), reacting, washing the filter and drying to obtain an aluminum-doped trimanganese tetraoxide powder, (iii) adding industrial grade lithium carbonate into water and mixing to obtain a suspension solution, and introducing carbon dioxide into the solution at room temperature to clarify, and (iv) adding the aluminum-doped trimanganese powder obtained in (ii), stirring and adding for thermal reaction, spray-drying the mixture to obtain an amorphous lithium-manganese-aluminum-oxygen powder, and calcining the powder at a high temperature to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing lithium aluminum manganate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves capacity and cycle stability and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the particle size of the manganese powder is 200-400 meshes. In the step (i), the solid-liquid ratio of manganese powder and water is 150-300 g/l. In the step (i), the mass ratio of the ammonium chloride and manganese powder is 1:8-1:15. In the step (ii), the molar ratio of the aluminum chloride and manganese powder is 0.01:1-0.04:1, and the mass percentage of aqueous aluminum chloride solution is 10-30%. The molar ratio of ammonia to aluminum chloride is 3:1-4:1. In the step (iii), the mass ratio of water and lithium carbonate to a liquid solid product is 18:1-25:1 l/g. The molar ratio of the lithium carbonate and manganese powder is 1:2-1:4. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the reaction is performed at temperature of 30-90 degrees C for 10-18 hours. In the step (ii), the mixed solution of aqueous ammonia and ammonium chloride is added for 2 hours before and after the manganese powder is completely oxidized to trimanganese tetraoxide, and the aluminum chloride solution can be added after the mixed solution is added. The method comprises after the addition of aluminum chloride and ammonia water in the step (ii), the reaction is continued for 2-4 hours. In the step (iv), the reaction temperature is performed at 40-90 degrees C for 2-6 hours and the high-temperature baking is performed at 700-850 degrees C for 10-20 hours.

PT P
PN CN110024777-A; CN110024777-B
TI Short-term preservation of sunflower pollen comprises covering the sunflower disc in gauze bag, collecting sunflower pollen, transferring into pollen collection device, filling carbon dioxide, sealing, and transferring into refrigerator.
AU HE Z
   WANG B
   ZHU T
   LI Y
   LI X
   WANG L
   WANG S
   LIU H
   NIU H
   LI W
AE JILIN ACAD AGRIC SCI (JLAA-C)
GA 201965208F
AB    NOVELTY - Short-term preservation of sunflower pollen comprises: (i) covering the sunflower disc in 80-meshes gauze bag before shedding; (ii) disinfecting a pollen collection device, a transfer heat insulation box and a refrigerator using 75% alcohol and drying before use; (iii) collecting the sunflower pollen during sunny weather from 10 am to 1 pm, and transferring into the pollen collection device; and (iv) filling carbon dioxide in sunflower pollen-containing pollen collection device, sealing immediately, transferring the pollen collection device into the transfer heat insulation box, then transferring into the refrigerator at 4 degrees C within half an hour.
   USE - The method is useful for short-term preservation of sunflower pollen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, satisfies large breeding requirement of sunflower pollen, achieves storage of sunflower pollen for 30 days with high activity and achieves pollination maturation rate of more than 70%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (iii), collecting the sunflower pollen, then screening via 50 meshes sieve to remove anthers and insects, and immediately transferring into the pollen collection device.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The pollen collection device is a normal brown glass bottle. The pollen collecting device comprises a moisture-proof device, a blocking device and a brown glass tube on the top for storing sunflower pollen. A sealing cover is mounted on top of the brown glass tube. The moisture proof device is placed at bottom of the brown glass tube. The moisture-proof device is located at the top of the brown glass tube. A sunflower pollen preservation area is arranged between the moisture-proof device and the blocking device. The sterilized absorbent cotton ball is filled between the moisture-proof device and the sealing cover. The brown glass tube has a diameter of 1.5-3 cm and height of 5-10 cm. The sealing cover is fixedly mounted on top of the brown glass tube by plugging or screwing. A sealing ring for reinforcing the sealing effect of the brown glass tube is provided in the sealing cover. The moisture proof device comprises a support frame and a drying sheet. The support frame is an integral structure composed of a circular plate and an annular support plate located below the circular plate. Multiple small holes are distributed above the circular plate. The dried sheet is placed on a circular plate. The dried tablet comprises a highly absorbent fibrous sheet and a calcium chloride absorbent sheet at a lower end. The superabsorbent absorbent fibrous sheet is located above and facing the sunflower pollen. The dried tablet is prepared by soaking the cut circular super absorbent fiber sheet in 500000 unit/l penicillin solution for 5-10 minutes, drying, filling the paper bag with calcium chloride, and pressing into round calcium chloride absorbent sheet. The superabsorbent absorbent fibrous sheet and the calcium chloride absorbent sheet are bonded and compressed to form a dried tablet. The moisture-proof device is a moisture-proof bag. The moisture-proof bag is filled with a silica gel desiccant. The moisture-proof device is an integral structure composed of an annular baffle, an annular support sidewall and a circular baffle. The annular baffle is used for erecting at the top opening of the brown glass tube. The top end of the annular support side wall is connected to the annular baffle. The bottom end of the annular support side wall is connected to the circular baffle. Multiple vent holes are defined in the circular baffle.

TI Preparing highly active porous calcium hydroxide from limestone, comprises e.g. pulverizing limestone, then performing low temperature calcination under vacuum negative pressure, and pulverizing.
AU WANG P
   QI X
   ZHOU Y
   LI X
AE QINGDAO RONGCHUANG NEW MATERIAL ENG RES (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201939392H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing highly active porous calcium hydroxide from limestone, comprises pulverizing limestone, then performing low temperature calcination under vacuum negative pressure for 2-8 hours, stop heating and stop vacuuming, delivering air or oxygen to the furnace to obtain highly active porous calcium oxide powder, and pulverizing ultra-high flow of obtained highly active porous calcium oxide powder to 0.3-20 mu m, placing in a closed space without carbon dioxide, ultrasonically atomizing water without carbon dioxide to make the humidity of the enclosed space reach 90-95%, and carrying out flexible slow reaction of porous calcium oxide powder and water in the calcium oxide-free closed space reaction system for 6-48 hours to obtain highly active porous calcium hydroxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing highly active porous calcium hydroxide from limestone.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for highly active porous calcium hydroxide prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The limestone is crushed to 1-5 mm. The calcination temperature is 650-750 degrees C, and the degree of vacuum is -80 to -50 KPa. Preferred Method: The high activity porous calcium hydroxide has activity greater than 400, a whiteness greater than 90, and a BET greater than 25 m2/g.

PT P
PN CN109437209-A; CN109437209-B
TI Extracting silicon dioxide from natural sand, involves loading refined sand into reactor, adding hydrochloric acid, sealing, introducing carbon dioxide gas, heating, insulating, cooling, separating solid and liquid, calcining.
AU LIANG C
   WANG Y
   HUANG H
   PAN L
   XIA Y
   ZHANG W
   ZHANG J
   GAN Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 201924917M
AB    NOVELTY - The method of extracting silicon dioxide from natural sand, involves (1) reducing the particle size of sand to micron or below by mechanical ball milling, (2) loading the refined sand into the reactor, adding hydrochloric acid to the reactor, then sealing the reactor, (3) simultaneously introducing carbon dioxide gas into the sealed reactor, heating at 200-450 degrees C, insulating for 1-24 hours, (4) cooling, recovering the gas in the reactor, separating solids and liquid remaining in the reactor, washing and drying the solid residue, (5) calcining the collected solid residue at a temperature of 200-400 degrees C for 0.5-24 hours.
   USE - Method of extracting silicon dioxide from natural sand.
   ADVANTAGE - The simple and economical method enables extraction of silicon dioxide with easy available raw materials and low energy consumption, and is suitable for easy industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sand is selected from desert sand, river sand and/or sea sand. Preferred Process: The step (1) comprises mechanically ball milling at a rotation speed of 200-600 rpm for 10-120 hours. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of sand to grinding ball is 1:30-200. The mass ratio of sand to hydrochloric acid is 1:0.96-1.92. The purity of the carbon dioxide gas is not less than 95%. Preferred Conditions: The initial gas pressure of the closed reactor is 10-80 bar.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The reactor is nickel alloy reactor.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PN US2018273855-A1; US10689583-B2
TI Removing carbon dioxide (CS2) from hydrocarbon streams involves contacting a hydrocarbon stream with a clinoptilolite adsorbent or a barium exchanged chabazite containing zeolite adsorbent and adsorbing CS2 using size selective adsorption.
AU RASTELLI H
   GORAWARA J K
   FREY S J
   BOLLAPRAGADA K
AE UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 2018765467
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide (CS2) are removed from hydrocarbon streams (1) by contacting a hydrocarbon stream with a clinoptilolite adsorbent or a barium exchanged chabazite containing zeolite adsorbent and adsorbing CS2 using size selective adsorption to produce a hydrocarbon stream having a reduced CS2 content.
   USE - The method is useful for removing CS2 from hydrocarbon streams (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The advantage of using the stabilized naphtha hydrotreater effluent is to use the hot stream in order to minimize the energy needed for regeneration heating and the spent hot regeneration effluent can be directed to the combination with the hydrotreater feed upstream of the charge heater or reduce the overall energy requirement of the process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a a flow scheme with the regeneration stream that is a slip stream of the treated product stream from the adsorbent bed.
   Hydroocarbon Stream (1)
   Crude column (2)
   Straight run naphtha stream (4)
   Adsorption bed (10)
   Slip stream (16)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Processes: Alternatively, removing CS2 from hydrocarbon streams involves contacting a hydrocarbon stream with a zeolite adsorbent comprising a chabazite zeolite, an erionite zeolite, a clinoptilolite zeolite or its mixtures and adsorbing CS2 using size selective adsorption to produce a hydrocarbon stream having a reduced CS2 content. The process further involves contacting the hydrocarbon stream having a reduced CS2 content with a zeolite adsorbent or a promoted alumina adsorbent to remove impurity selected from oxygenates and other sulfur compounds. The clinoptilolite adsorbent or barium exchanged chabazite containing zeolite adsorbent is regenerated with a hydrocarbon stream that was first sent through a hydrotreater. The process also involves regenerating the zeolite adsorbent with a regeneration stream comprising a portion of the hydrocarbon stream having a reduced CS2 content, a hydrogen stream or a mixture of the hydrocarbon stream having a reduced CS2 content and hydrogen. Preferred Conditions: The clinoptilolite adsorbent or barium exchanged chabazite containing zeolite adsorbent is used in a temperature swing adsorption system where the adsorption temperature is 60-150 degrees F and the regeneration temperature is 300-550 degrees F. The regenerant stream is a hydrotreated naphtha stream (4) available at 15.5-287.8 degrees C (60-550 degrees F). The regenerant stream is at 148.9-287.8 degrees C (300-550 degrees F). It is a light hydrotreated naphtha from a naphtha splitter overhead at 15.5-287.8 degrees C (60-550 degrees F) or 93.3-204.4 degrees C (200-400 degrees F). The regeneration effluent is directed to a hydrotreater at 15.5-287.8 degrees C (60-550 degrees F). It is at 148.9-287.8 degrees C (300-550 degrees F).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The clinoptilolite adsorbent or barium exchanged chabazite containing zeolite adsorbent has exchangeable cations selected from ions of group 1A, group 2A, Group 3A, group 3B, the lanthanide series and its mixtures It is selected from natural clinoptilolite, synthetic clinoptilolite, sodium-exchanged clinoptilolite, potassium-exchanged clinoptilolite, lithium-exchanged clinoptilolite, calcium-exchanged clinoptilolite, magnesium-exchanged clinoptilolite, barium-exchanged clinoptilolite, and its mixtures. It is sodium-exchanged clinoptilolite or barium-exchanged clinoptilolite. The zeolite adsorbent is ion exchanged with barium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon stream comprises naphtha boiling range hydrocarbons and where size selective adsorption capacity of the adsorbent for CS2 is not impacted by the presence of the naphtha boiling range hydrocarbons. The hydrocarbon stream comprises additional organic sulfur compounds and size selective adsorption capacity of the adsorbent for C2 is not impacted by the presence of the additional organic sulfur compounds. The additional organic sulfur compounds comprise mercaptans, sulfides, or disulfides. The hydrocarbon stream comprises straight run naphtha from crude oil or natural gas condensate sources.

PN WO2018138689-A1; CN110352185-A; EP3573947-A1; US2021171431-A1
TI Preparation of cesium oxalate for producing e.g. disubstituted oxalate, involves contacting carbon dioxide and hydrogen with cesium carbonate, or contacting carbon monoxide and oxygen with cesium carbonate, under reaction conditions.
AU AL-JABER A
   KOROBKOV I
   PASHA F A
   AL-BAHILY K
   VIDJAYACOUMAR B
   ALBAHILY K
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 201860403D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cesium oxalate involves contacting carbon dioxide and hydrogen with cesium carbonate or contacting carbon monoxide and oxygen with cesium carbonate, under reaction conditions to form a composition comprising cesium oxalate.
   USE - Preparation of cesium oxalate for producing oxalic acid, ethylene glycol and disubstituted oxalate e.g. dimethyl oxalate (all claimed) used in pharmaceutical products, and as solvent or plasticizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently produces cesium oxalate in situ, and reduces or eliminates reliance on carbon monoxide as feed stock to produce dimethyl oxalate. The conversion of cesium oxalate to dimethyl oxalate is simple, efficient and economical since it eliminates the use of expensive noble metal catalysts such as palladium-based catalysts, and can be carried out in single-pot fashion.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of one reactor system to produce disubstituted oxalates.
   System for producing disubstituted oxalate (100)
   Reactor (102)
   Gas inlets (104,106,108,110)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reaction conditions include a temperature of 250-400 degrees C, preferably 320-330 degrees C and pressure of 1-6 MPa, preferably 3-4 MPa. The carbon dioxide is provided at a pressure of 3.0-4.0 MPa, preferably 3.5 MPa, and hydrogen is provided at a pressure of 0.05-0.5 MPa, preferably 0.1 MPa. The carbon monoxide is provided at a pressure of 1-3 MPa, preferably 2 MPa, and the oxygen is provided at a pressure of 0.05-0.5 MPa, preferably 0.1 MPa. Preferred Composition: The mole ratio of total content of carbon dioxide and hydrogen to cesium carbonate is 100:1-300:1, preferably 150:1-250: 1, or more preferably 200:1. The mole ratio of total content of carbon monoxide and oxygen to cesium carbonate is 1:1-3:1 or preferably 2:1. Preferred Process: The method further includes formation of cesium formate and cesium bicarbonate. The method involves contacting carbon dioxide with cesium carbonate at a temperature of 200-400 degrees C, preferably 300-325 degrees C for at least 1 hour to obtain a cesium carbonate/carbon dioxide mixture, contacting the mixture with hydrogen, and inhibiting formation of cesium formate. The method involves forming cesium hydroxide and converting the cesium hydroxide to cesium carbonate. The method further involves isolating the cesium oxalate from the product stream and converting the cesium oxalate to disubstituted oxalate, oxalic acid, or ethylene glycol. The cesium oxalate is generated in situ and then contacted with one or more alcohols and additional carbon dioxide under conditions to produce a disubstituted oxalate at a temperature of 100-200 degrees C, preferably 125-175 degrees C, or more preferably 150 degrees C, and pressure of 2-5 MPa, preferably 3-4 MPa, or more preferably 3.5 MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The alcohol is methanol.
DC E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN US2018214849-A1; KR2018090017-A; KR1936493-B1; US10744485-B2
TI Porous carbon material is synthesized by pyrolysis            of zinc-containing metal-organic framework, where            pyrolysis removes all of zinc for forming metal-free            porous carbon structure.
AU LEE C Y
   YEON L C
AE UNIV INCHEON IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIH-C)
   UNIV INCHEON IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIH-C)
GA 2018603221
AB    NOVELTY - Porous carbon material is synthesized by                pyrolysis of a zinc-containing metal-organic                framework, where the zinc-containing metal-organic                framework is selected from MOF-5, MOF-177 and                bioMOF-100. The pyrolysis removes all of zinc for                forming metal-free porous carbon structure. The                porous carbon materials are used for carbon dioxide                capture.
   USE - Porous carbon material.
   ADVANTAGE - The porous carbon material has improved carbon                dioxide capture capacity and selectivity than the                parent metal-organic framework.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for producing porous carbon materials from a                zinc-containing metal-organic framework, which                involves:
   (A) preparing MOF-5, MOF-177 or bioMOF-100 as                a metal-organic framework; and
   (B) removing zinc by pyrolysis of the                zinc-containing metal-organic framework for forming                metal-free porous carbon structure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graphical representation                depicting powder X-ray diffraction patterns of the                porous carbon materials. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The porous carbon                  materials have amorphous property. The                  zinc-containing metal-organic framework is MOF-5,                  and BET specific surface area of porous carbon                  materials having porous carbon structure formed                  by pyrolysis of the MOF-5 is 1978 m2g1, and                  carbon dioxide capture capacity of the porous                  carbon materials is 0.81 mmol g1. The porous                  carbon materials are used for carbon dioxide                  capture from flue gas including water vapor. The                  porous carbon materials separate carbon dioxide                  from carbon dioxide or nitrogen gas mixture under                  50% of relative humidity. The pyrolysis is                  performed at 1000 degrees C for 6 hours after the                  zinc-containing metal-organic framework is heated                  at 1000 degrees C under argon atmosphere.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E11-Q02B; E12-A10; E31-N04D; E31-N05C; J01-D; J01-E02B2; J01-E02B3

PT P
PN CN108272911-A
TI Preparing tincture useful for traumatic injury comprises e.g. taking active medicinal materials Lobelia seguinii, Kadsura oblongifolia, Claoxylon indicum, Cinnamomum cassia twig and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
AU ZENG S
   ZENG Z
   LIANG F
   ZHANG H
   CHEN S
AE GUANGDONG YILI GROUP PHARM CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI YILI GUIXI PHARM CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201860567T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing tincture comprises taking active medicinal materials including Lobelia seguinii, Kadsura oblongifolia, Claoxylon indicum, Cinnamomum cassia twig, Blumea balsamifera, radix zanthoxyli, radix aconiti, radix wikstroemia indica and Polygonum hydropiper, performing supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) fluid extraction using aqueous solution of ethanol as an cosolvent.
   USE - The tincture is useful for traumatic injury (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high extraction efficiency, shortens production cycle, has easy control parameters and good batch-to-batch identity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing tincture, comprising (i) crushing above mentioned raw materials and placing in extractor; (ii) heating CO2 to desired pressure and mixing with cosolvent to obtain mixed solvent; (iii) adding mixed solvent into extractor for extraction; (iv) reducing pressure in the separator after extraction and discharging air to obtain cosolvent liquid; and (v) adding cosolvent liquid to peppermint oil, borneol and camphor to dissolve, mixing and filtering.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The ethanol solution has 60-75% preferably, 70% ethanol content, and added amount accounts for 4.5-10% preferably, 7% of CO2 molar flow rate. Preferred Method: The method comprises extracting at 40-60 degrees C with pressure of 13-20 MPa. The extracting time is 1.5-3 hours. The method comprises (i) crushing 18.4 g Lobelia seguinii, 11 g Kadsura oblongifolia, 16.5 g Claoxylon indicum, 13.8 g Cinnamomum cassia twig, 5.5 g Blumea balsamifera, 13.8 g radix zanthoxyli, 2.80 g radix aconiti, 18.4 g radix wikstroemia indica and 3.6 g Polygonum hydropiper, passing through 60 mesh sieve and placing in the extractor; (ii) heating CO2 at 50 degrees C with 16 MPa pressure and mixing with 70% ethanol to obtain mixed solvent; (iii) adding mixed solvent into extractor for extraction at 50 degrees C with 16 MPa for 2 hours; (iv) reducing pressure in the separator after extraction and discharging air to obtain cosolvent liquid; and (v) adding cosolvent liquid to 10 ml peppermint oil, 0.7 g borneol and 2.5 g camphor to dissolve, mixing and filtering and adding 1000 ml ethanol (70%).
   ACTIVITY - Vulnerary.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.)

AE JINXI NATURAL GAS CHEM ENG CO LTD (CHNI-C)
GA 201844511A
AB    NOVELTY - Fertilizer comprises 3-5 wt.% silicon, 0.05-2 wt.% synergistic agent and rest is urea.
   USE - Fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The fertilizer can be used for base fertilizer, top dressing or as nitrogen source fertilizer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing fertilizer, which involves:
   (A) mixing ammonia and carbon-dioxide in urea synthesis system to synthesize to obtain urea aqueous solution;
   (B) preparing 60-80% sodium silicate aqueous solution;
   (C) adding to urea aqueous solution;
   (D) conveying to urea evaporation system for evaporating to obtain silicon-containing urine;
   (E) adding dissolving agent according to proportion to silicon-containing urine; and
   (F) conveying to urea granulating system for pelleting to obtain the desired fertilizer, where preparation method is based on urea production system which comprises urea synthesis system, urea evaporating system and urea granulating system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Fertilizer comprises 4 wt.% silicon, 1.5 wt.% synergistic agent and rest is urea. The synergistic agent comprises 1-2 pts. wt., preferably, 1.5 pts. wt. n-butyl thiophosphoric triamide and 1-5 pts. wt., preferably, 3 pts. wt. humic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The urea granulating system is set at front end of large grain drum granulator with nozzle.

GA 201815535X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of step hole molecular organic                frameworks (MOFs) comprises mixing metal source,                ligand and deprotonation reagent in solvent                comprising water and/or organic solvent, stirring                at 293-323 K for 0.5-2 hours, transferring mixture                into supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) reactor,                purging reactor with CO2 to increase pressure in                reactor, raising reactor for crystallization,                removing reaction solution, isolating product, and                drying at 353-423 K for 4-12 hours. The organic                solvent is ethanol and/or ethylene glycol. The                volume ratio of water and organic solvent is                1:0.5-3.
   USE - Method for preparing step hole MOFs used as                desulfurization adsorbent for fuel oil                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The MOFs have high outer specific surface area                and rich mesoporous structure, which solves                diffusion problem of macromolecular tetrasulfide.                The MOFs also have highly dispersed unsaturated                metal, which strengthens desulfurization.

The concentration of metal salt in solvent is                  0.1-0.5 mol/L.\ Preferred Method: The reactor is                  purged with CO2 to 40-100 bar. The                  crystallization is carried out at 353-473 K for                  4-24 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The ligand is trimesic                  acid, terephthalic acid, 4,4-dipyridyl, and/or                  3,4',5-biphenyltricarboxylic acid. The                  deprotonation agent is ammonia water,                  ethylenediamine, and/or N,N-dimethylformamide.                  The molar ratio of metal salt, ligand and                  deprotonation reagent is                  1-2.5:0.5-5:0.2-3.5.

AB    NOVELTY - Reducing volatile organic compounds (VOCs)                content in vinyl acetate-ethylene copolymer                emulsion, involves heating the vinyl                acetate-ethylene copolymer emulsion from defoaming                process at 60-80degrees Celsius, introducing                gaseous carbon dioxide, pressurizing to 2-6MPa, and                then decompressing to normal pressure or negative                pressure.
   USE - Method for reducing volatile organic compounds                content in vinyl acetate-ethylene copolymer                emulsion. Uses include but are not limited to                cigarettes, woods, papers, leathers, concretes,                non-woven fabric bonding, carpet back lining glue,                electrostatic flocking, paper coating, interior and                exterior wall coatings, waterproof glue, wallpaper                glue, floor glue, caulking glue, pectin, art-ware                processing, three-dimensional oil painting, air                filters, cement mortar, coatings, disposable                tableware, paper cups and food packaging.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the treatment efficiency                of the residual monomer, is effective to the                residual monomer, and can treat other impurities                except the residual monomer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the                  carbon dioxide is 60-90degrees Celsius. The                  number of carbon dioxide injections is 3-9 times,                  and the time for each injection is 20-40 minutes.                  Preferred Process: The method also includes                  heating up the vinyl acetate-ethylene copolymer                  emulsion from the defoaming process, and adding a                  reducing agent solution and an oxidizing agent                  solution from different tanks into the emulsion.                  Preferred Components: The reducing agent includes                  1-3 of sulfur compounds with a sulfur atom                  valence of -2-4, erythorbate and tartrate. The                  reducing agent comprises 1-8 of the reducing                  agents including zinc formaldehyde sulfoxylate,                  sodium erythorbate, sodium metabisulfite, sodium                  bisulfate, potassium tartrate, sodium tartrate,                  and potassium sodium tartrate. Preferred                  Compositions: The amount of the reducing agent                  solution is 0.01-0.02 wt.% reducing agent                  contained in the solution based on the vinyl                  acetate-ethylene copolymer emulsion. The                  oxidizing agent comprises 1-2 of an inorganic                  peroxide and an organic peroxide. The mass ratio                  of water and oxidant in the oxidant solution is                  5-10:0.01-0.04. The oxidant solution is used in                  an amount of 0.01-0.04 wt.% ethylene-vinyl                  acetate copolymer emulsion as the oxidant                  contained in the solution.

PN CN114426928-A
TI Culturing microalgae for inhibiting mixed bacteria involves adding microalgae seed solution and microalgae culture medium to open photobioreactor for light and dark culturing, introducing gas containing carbon dioxide into light reaction, culturing it to stationary phase, and harvesting cell.
AU ZHU H
   WANG P
   LIAO S
   LI X
   ZHANG L
   FAN Y
   SHI W
AE SINOPEC DALIAN RES PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202271591B
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing microalgae for inhibiting mixed bacteria involves adding microalgae seed solution and microalgae culture medium to open photobioreactor for alternating light and dark culturing. The gas containing carbon dioxide (CO2) is introduced into light reaction, and gas containing CO2 and NOX is introduced into dark reaction at a reaction temperature of 20-35degreesC. The dark reaction is stopped and NOX is introduced after alternate culturing for a period of time, and reaction temperature of light reaction is increased to 36-45degreesC, and cultured to the stationary phase, and the microalgal cells are harvested.
   USE - Method for culturing microalgae for inhibiting mixed bacteria.
   ADVANTAGE - The method cultures microalgae, which solves problem of contamination by mixed bacteria in open culture of microalgae through a specific culture method, does not inhibit growth of microalgae, reduces carbon fixation efficiency, and reduces the culture cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The microalgae are autotrophic single algae or mixed algae that tolerates NOX and high temperature, preferably at least one of cellulosic algae SS-B7 (Ankistrodesmus species) and Scenedesmus species TMJD3. The microalgae medium adopts one of BG11 medium, SE medium and Bold Basal medium. The microalgae seed liquid is prepared by inoculating microalgae into the microalgae culture medium at a pH of 7-9, temperature of 20-35degreesC and light cycle of 24 hours, light-dark time ratio of 14:10, and light intensity of 2000-20000Lux, culturing it in shaker and preparing microalgae seed liquid in multiple growth periods. The volume ratio of microalgae seed solution and microalgae culture medium added in photobioreactor is 1:20-1:5. The light-dark cycle is 24 hours, light-dark time ratio is 14:10-10:14, and light intensity is 2000-20000 Lux in light-dark alternate culturing. The volume content of CO2 is 5-40%, and does not contain NOX in the CO2-containing gas introduced into the photoreaction. The dark reaction is introduced into gas containing CO2 and NOX, where volume content of CO2 is 5-40%, and volume content of NOX is 0.03-0.07%. The gas containing CO2 and NOX is self-made, or it is industrial flue gas or waste gas. The industrial flue gas or waste gas is derived from at least one of sulfur recovery device incineration tail gas and catalytic cracking regeneration tail gas. The temperature of alternating light and dark cultivation is 20-35degreesC, preferably 25-30degreesC. The NOX is stopped after 2-5 days of alternating light and dark culture. The temperature for increasing the photoreaction is 36-45degreesC, preferably 40-45degreesC.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial.

TI New tin dioxide/carbon-containing catalyst used for electrocatalytic reduction of flue gas to formic acid.
AU KANG P
   CHENG Y
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2021939156
AB    NOVELTY - A tin dioxide/carbon-containing catalyst (I), is new.
   USE - The tin dioxide/carbon-containing catalyst is used for electrocatalytic reduction of flue gas to formic acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The tin oxide catalyst modified by the alcohol amine molecule through simple hydrothermal method and is used for the reaction of the electro-catalysis reducing flue gas to prepare the formic acid, and the performance is excellent. The formic acid Faraday efficiency and the partial current density under the different carbon dioxide concentrations can reach 45% and 2 mA.cm(2) or more respectively the optimal potential.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A tin dioxide/carbon-containing catalyst of formula CxH2x(OH)yNH3-y-SnO2/C (I), is new.
   x=2-6; and
   y=1-3.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the compound (I); and
   (2) application method of compound (I) comprising performing electrocatalytic reduction of flue gas to formic acid in the presence of catalyst.
   The concentration of carbon dioxide in the flue gas is 10-80%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The compound (I) is prepared by weighing soluble tin salt, dissolving in absolute ethanol to form precursor solution A, dissolving alcohol amine molecule in absolute ethanol to form precursor solution B, adding precursor solution A into a reactor, dropping precursor solution B while stirring, adding carbon powder, water bath heating to carry out reflux reaction, separating, washing, and drying in an oven above room temperature. Preferred Components: The soluble tin salt is stannous chloride, sodium stannate or tin tetrachloride. The concentration of soluble tin salt in precursor solution A is 0.0625-0.4 mol/L. The molar ratio of soluble tin salt and alcohol amine molecules is 0.625:1-3:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of alcohol amine molecule is precursor solution B is 1.3-3.2 mol/L.

PN KR2256129-B1
TI Producing metal oxide comprises e.g. performing transesterification reaction in solvent by metal oxide as catalyst, separating catalyst slurry-waste liquid, injecting carbon dioxide into catalyst slurry and waste liquid and sintering.
AU PARK Y
   KIM S
   KIM J
   HO L S
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 202158900R
AB    NOVELTY - Producing metal oxide comprises performing transesterification reaction in a solvent using a metal oxide as a catalyst; separating the catalyst slurry and waste liquid from the transesterification reaction; preparing a metal carbonate by injecting carbon dioxide into the catalyst slurry and waste liquid; and sintering the metal carbonate at 600-800 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful producing metal oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents the generation of environmental pollutants due to the reaction by separating the catalyst metal from the metal slurry and waste liquid generated by the transesterification reaction; reduces the production cost and secures economic efficiency; and has the advantage of not deteriorating activity even when reused as a catalyst for transesterification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for method for recycling transesterification catalyst comprising reusing the metal oxide prepared by above method for transesterification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide is oxides of magnesium, calcium, zinc, titanium, manganese, vanadium, beryllium, copper, zirconium, strontium, tin or barium. The ester compound is aliphatic ester, aromatic ester or cyclic carbonate. The alcohol compound is a monofunctional alcohol or diol. Preferred Method: The transesterification reaction is a reaction between an ester compound and an alcohol compound. The transesterification reaction uses 0.1-10 wt.% metal oxide as a catalyst. In the step of preparing metal carbonate, carbon dioxide is injected into the catalyst slurry and the waste liquid at room temperature or at 60-90 degrees C for 1 hour to 24 hours.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN KR2021054745-A
TI Producing porous adsorbent in e.g. selective adsorption of carbon dioxide comprises e.g. mixing metal precursor containing zinc and material containing coordination bond of organic acid in solvent and irradiating mixture with microwaves.
AU HAN S I
   GAIKWAD S
AE UNIV CHANGWON NAT IND & ACAD COOP (UVCH-C)
GA 2021534721
AB    NOVELTY - Producing porous adsorbent comprises preparing a mixture by mixing a metal precursor containing zinc, a material containing a coordination bond of an organic acid, and a substance containing a counterion of the coordination bond in a solvent; and irradiating the mixture with microwaves.
   USE - The porous adsorbent is useful in selectively adsorption of carbon dioxide (claimed), and atmospheric environment industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method shortens the synthesis time to 2-8 hours from 48 hours or more by irradiating microwaves which also improves the performance of the porous adsorbent e.g. exhibits high stability, adsorption property, and selectivity even when exposed to moisture, acid, etc. contained in the exhaust gas discharged from the actual factory, and constant crystallinity and smaller crystal size.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for porous adsorbent produced by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of irradiating the mixture with microwaves is performed for 2-8 hours at a power of 100-300 W under a pressure of 0.1-18 bar. Preferred Components: The metal precursor comprises a metal of zinc nitrate, zinc sulfate, zinc chloride, zinc acetate, zinc acetylacetone and zinc chloride. The organic acid comprises an organic acid of citric acid, pyridinedicarboxylic acid, methylfumaric acid, dihydroxyterephthalic acid, hydroxyterephthalic acid and ammonium citrate. The counterion is ion of potassium ion, sodium ion, lithium ion, hydrogen ion, rubidium ion or cesium ion. The solvent is water, ethanol, methanol, ethyl ether, toluene, acetone, isopropyl alcohol, benzene, xylene, N-methylpyrrolidone, tetrahydrofuran, nitrobenzene, N,N-dimethyl Formamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, diethyl carbonate, benzyl acetate, dimethyl glutarate, ethylacetoacetate, isobutyl isobutanoate, isobutyl acetate or m-cresol.
   EXAMPLE - 1.565 g of zinc acetate, 1.47 g citric acid and 1.17 g potassium hydroxide were dissolved in 35 ml mixed solvent of water and ethanol in a volume ratio of 1:1. Microwave of 300 W power was irradiated in the Teflon (RTM: PTFE) reactor at 90 degrees C for 2 hours. The resulting reactant was washed with ethyl ether and methanol and then dried under vacuum at 90 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain porous adsorbent.

GA 202144815Y
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon aerogel/metal organic framework (MOF)                composite material comprises carbon aerogel                framework material and MOF material, which is                loaded on the carbon aerogel framework                material.
   USE - The carbon aerogel/MOF composite material is                used for gas storage. The gas is methane                (preferred), ethane, hydrogen, nitrogen, carbon                monoxide, and carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The MOF material has better adsorption                capacity and is directly assembled into the                framework of aerogel as a guest material, after                common carbonization to obtain a block without                adhesive agent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the composite material comprising                preparing precursor of aerogel material and adding                chemical crosslinking agent to form sol solution;                adding MOF material into solvent to form MOF                suspension; and mixing the sol solution and MOF                suspension, and preparing the composite material by                conducting hydrothermal synthesis to form hydrogel,                aging and solvent replacement, and performing                supercritical drying, or freezing and                freeze-drying, and carbonizing under the protection                atmosphere.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The MOF material has                  good adsorption and storage capacity for methane,                  and has metal ions comprising copper ion (Cu2+),                  zinc ion (Zn2+), cadmium ion (Cd2+), cobalt ion                  (Co2+), nickel ion (Ni2+), zirconium ion (Zr2+),                  magnesium ion (Mg2+), iron ion (Fe2+), lanthanum                  ion (La2+) and manganese ion (Mn2+). It is                  ZIF-67, ZIF-8, HKUST-1, MIL101 and Universitetet                  i Oslo (UiO-66). The MOF material has specific                  surface area of 1000-3000 m2/g and pore volume of                  0.8-2.0 cm3/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The solvent comprises                  water, methanol, ethanol, dimethylformamide                  (DMF), ethylene glycol, glycerol, pyrrolidone,                  N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide,                  N,N-diethylformamide, pyridine, piperidine,                  furan, tetrahydrofuran, dioxane and dimethyl                  sulfoxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The precursor comprises                  resorcinol, formaldehyde and natural                  polysaccharide. The concentration of precursor is                  controlled at 1-30%. The natural polysaccharide                  comprises chitosan, konjac glucomannan, sodium                  alginate, gelatin, agarose, starch, chitin,                  lignocellulose, hemicellulose, polyvinyl alcohol                  and hydroxymethyl cellulose. The chemical                  crosslinking agent comprises                  N,N-methylene-bis(acrylamide), ethylenediamine,                  polyacrylic acid, formaldehyde, polyacrylamide                  and polyethylene glycol. It has a controlled                  concentration at 0.1-5%. The carbonization                  treatment is conducted under the atmosphere of                  nitrogen or argon gas by raising the temperature                  to 800-1000 degrees C at 3-8 degrees C/minute,                  and keeping the temperature for 2-4 hours. The                  mass ratio of MOF material to the aerogel                  framework material is 0.5-3:1.

TI Catalyst for carbon monoxide production, includes reduction acceleration component which accelerates partial reduction by hydrogen of the transition metal oxide of the transition metal element.
AU OKEMOTO A
   SATO G
   HASEGAWA Y
   HARADA A
AE NAT INST ADVANCED IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NIIT-C)
GA 202129489M
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst includes reduction acceleration component which accelerates partial reduction by hydrogen of the transition metal oxide of the transition metal element which the oxidation number which can be taken multiply exists, and contains a component and a carrier that disperses and supports the transition metal oxide and the reduction-promoting component.
   USE - Catalyst for carbon monoxide production (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst does not require a catalyst regeneration operation after the conversion reaction and has an excellent conversion rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for producing a catalyst for producing carbon monoxide, which involves immersing the carrier, taking out the soaked carrier from the impregnating solution and drying it, firing the dried carrier, and calcining the calcined carrier in a hydrogen stream, and activating by arranging; and
   (2) a method for producing carbon monoxide involves contacting carbon dioxide with a carbon monoxide production catalyst, where adding carbon dioxide and hydrogen to the carbon monoxide production catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal element of the transition metal oxide is at least one metal selected from the group consisting of vanadium (V), chromium (Cr), niobium (Nb), molybdenum (Mo), tantalum (Ta) and tungsten (W). The carrier is an inorganic porous body. The reduction promoting component is an element different from the metal element of the transition metal oxide selected from iron (Fe), cobalt (Co) nickel (Ni), copper (Cu), ruthenium (Ru), rhodium (Rh), palladium (Pd), silver (Ag), osmium (Os), iridium (Ir) and platinum (Pt). The impregnating solution is an acidic solvent. The acidic solvent is an aqueous solution of hydrogen chloride. Preferred Conditions: The content of the transition metal oxide with respect to the total mass of the catalyst for producing carbon monoxide is 1 wt.% or more.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
