PN CN112264045-A; CN112264045-B
TI Synthesis of one-dimensional composite semiconductor nanofiber for, e.g. photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, involves adding indium salt and sulfur source to solvent, adding polyvinylpyrrolidone, electrospinning, and calcining.
AU HE Y
   LIAN X
   LIU M
   XIAO G
   CHEN Z
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 2021123497
AB    NOVELTY - One-step synthesis of one-dimensional indium oxide/diindium trisulfide composite semiconductor nanofiber, involves sequentially adding a trivalent indium salt and a sulfur source to a mixed solution of absolute ethanol, ethylene glycol, and N,N-dimethylformamide, stirring, adding polyvinylpyrrolidone to the solution to obtain a precursor spinning solution, spraying and electrospinning at a receiving distance of 8-23 cm, a voltage of 8-20 kV, and a pushing speed of 0.1-0.2 mm/minute, and calcining the obtained precursor fiber in a high-temperature tubular furnace.
   USE - One-step synthesis of one-dimensional indium oxide/diindium trisulfide composite semiconductor nanofiber used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed), gas sensing, semiconductor materials, and electrocatalysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, economical and easy-to-control synthesis of nanofiber with uniform size and controllable shape, using a wide raw material source, meeting the actual production requirements.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of trivalent indium salt and sulfur source is 1:0.4-1.2. Preferred Component: The trivalent indium salt is an indium halide, preferably indium trichloride tetrahydrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sulfur source is thioacetamide, thiourea, sodium thiosulfate, and/or L-cysteine. Preferred Composition: The concentration of sulfur source in the mixed solution of absolute ethanol, ethylene glycol, and N,N-dimethylformamide is 20-60 g/L. The volume ratio of absolute ethanol, ethylene glycol, and N,N-dimethylformamide is 2:1:2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The concentration of polyvinylpyrrolidone in the precursor spinning solution is 90-110 g/L. Preferred Conditions: The precursor fiber is added to a tubular furnace, and calcined by heating from room temperature to 400-700 degrees C at 1-5 degrees C/minute in an inert gas atmosphere, for 1-3 hours.
DC A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); ; F01 (Threads and fibres - natural or artificial; spinning - including the production of mineral and carbon fibres (D01).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. PN CN112137156-A
TI Preparing traditional cigarette comprises e.g. selecting thick Eucommia bark leave, fennel stem leaves and clove leaves, high temperature extraction of carbon dioxide, placing the selected leaves into extracting container.
AU XIE G
AE HUNAN FEINAER SUCTION PROD TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202104644P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting thick Eucommia bark leaves, fennel stem leaves and clove leaves. The selected Eucommia bark leaves, fennel stem leaves and clove leave are put into an extracting container. Components are extracted by carbon dioxide high temperature technology. The auxiliary material is added with flavoring agent, humectant, combustion improver, mildew preventive and coloring agent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing tobacco substitute product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables replacing the traditional cigarette with better effect of reducing the processing cost, the utilization rate of the Eucommia bark leaves reaches 100%, the finished product not containing nicotine, the tar content is far lower than the traditional cigarette, and is the best tobacco substitute product in the market.

TI Preparing industrial solid waste-based non-burning            bricks comprises grinding furnace bottom slag,            screening, adjusting gradation, mixing the dry gel            materials including auxiliary gel materials and carbon            dioxide mineralization curing.
AU WANG T
   SONG J
   FANG M
   CEN J
   WANG Q
   GAO X
   LUO Z
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2020847864
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing industrial solid waste-based                non-burning bricks comprises (a) grinding the                furnace bottom slag, screening, then adjusting the                gradation, where the size of the sieving mesh is                4.75 mm-150 mu m in diameter, and adjusting the                gradation to more than 20% of the furnace bottom                slag distributed in the range of 250-425 microns,                and (b) completely mixing the dry gel materials                including bottom slag, cement and auxiliary gel                materials, then adding water, pressing the mixed                gel material, and then performing carbon dioxide                mineralization curing after pre-curing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing industrial                solid waste-based non-burning bricks.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses carbonation curing technology                by combining with composite gel material; and                solves the issues of single utilization path of the                bottom slag and low added value utilization                problems to obtain building materials with                excellent performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The dry gel material                  comprises 20-30 wt.% bottom slag, 20-30 wt.%                  steel slag, 10-20 wt.% fly ash, 10-20 wt.%                  calcium carbide slag, and 10-20 wt.% cement.                  Preferred Components: The auxiliary gel material                  is fly ash, steel slag, and/or calcium carbide                  slag. The cement is Portland cement, and the                  strength number is not greater than 42.5.

PT P
PN CN111548888-A
TI Roxburgh rose sparkling wine comprises roxburgh            rose, where material is fermented, and finished wine            product has specified carbon dioxide pressure.
AU GUO Z
   ZHANG Y
   ZHAO Y
   LIU Q
   MIN Z
   JIANG L
AE UNIV MOUTAI (UYMO-Non-standard)
GA 202081883E
AB    NOVELTY - Roxburgh rose sparkling wine comprises                roxburgh rose, where the raw material is fermented,                and the finished wine product has a carbon dioxide                pressure of 0.35-0.5 MPa.
   USE - As a roxburgh rose sparkling wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method retains and extracts aroma                substances of roxburgh rose, reduces sour taste and                unstable substances in roxburgh rose, increases                juice yield, reduces denaturation of active                substances, and retains fruity aroma.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing roxburgh rose sparkling wine                involving (a) screening roxburgh rose, cleaning,                cutting and removing seeds, (b) enzymatically                hydrolyzing with pectin lyase, (c) when bubbles are                generated in the fermented roxburgh rose                fermentation liquid, the roxburgh rose becomes                soft, pressing the fermented roxburgh rose liquid                to obtain a clear juice, adjusting the composition                and continuing fermenting, (d) carrying out                malolactic fermentation, (e) clarifying the                resulting base wine, and (f) carrying out secondary                fermentation in bottle, shake the bottle, removing                cork, spitting out the residue and packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  selecting fresh ripen roxburgh roses without                  mold, cutting each roxburgh rose into 4-6 pieces,                  during cutting and removing seeds, adding 90-110                  mg/l potassium bisulfite in batches based on the                  volume occupied by the roxburgh rose in the                  container. The step (d) involves adding 20-30                  mg/l pectin lyase to the roxburgh rose based on                  the volume of the roxburgh rose in the fermentor,                  and enzymolyzing for 12 hours at 10-12 degrees C.                  The step (c) involves maintaining temperature at                  14-18 degrees C, inoculating 150-250 mg/l                  activated acid-resistant Saccharomyces cerevisiae                  into the roxburgh rose, fermenting for 36-60                  hours, when the roxburgh rose becomes soft,                  squeezing the fermented liquid of roxburgh rose                  to obtain clear juice, adding sugar to the clear                  juice to adjust its total sugar content to                  160-180 g/l, if the pH is less than 2.8, reducing                  the acidity to a pH of 3-3.2, controlling the                  temperature at 14-16 degrees C, and continuing                  fermentation until the total sugar drops below 4                  g/l, and stopping fermentation. The step of (e)                  involves (e1) adding 200-300 mg/l                  polyvinylpyrrolidone to the base wine that has                  undergone malolactic fermentation and mixing                  well, (e2) after 24 hours, adding 1000-1500 mg/l                  puffed bentonite, leaving still for 5-10 days and                  separating, and (e3) testing the resulting                  product for cold stability, where if cold is                  unstable, carrying out freezing treatment and                  filtering with diatomaceous earth after 7-14 days                  at -5 degrees C. The step (f) involves after                  spitting, adding syrup to adjust the sugar                  content to obtain sparkling wine with different                  sweetness.

PN CN111525097-A; CN111525097-B
TI Preparing cathode material, comprises e.g. mixing cathode active material and oxalic acid solution with lithium source solution, drying prepared material, and sintering dried material under carbon dioxide atmosphere.
AU WANG H
AE QINXIN GROUP TIANJIN CO LTD NEW ENERGY (QINX-Non-standard)
   QINXIN GROUP TIANJIN CO LTD NEW ENERGY (QINX-Non-standard)
GA 202079531T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cathode material, comprises (i) adding cathode active material into oxalic acid solution, and stirring uniformly, (ii) adding lithium source solution into material obtained in step (i), and stirring completely, (iii) drying the material obtained in step (ii) at 200-450 degrees C, and (iv) sintering the dried material at 720-1000 degrees C under carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain final product.
   USE - The cathode material is useful in cathode sheet for lithium ion battery and lithium ion capacitor (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: coats a layer of nano-scale lithium carbonate or organic lithium on the surface of cathode active material, which reduces consumption of lithium ions in the cathode material when forming solid-electrolyte interphase film, and thus improves first Coulombic efficiency of lithium-ion battery, and achieves effect of increasing battery capacity and specific energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for cathode material prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio between cathode active material and oxalic acid is 1000:8-2. The cathode active material includes synthetic graphite, natural graphite, mesocarbon microspheres, silicon carbide, composite graphite, soft carbon and/or hard carbon. The lithium source includes lithium hydroxide, lithium carbonate, lithium oxalate, lithium fluoride, lithium ethylene dicarbonate, formyllithium, dilithium carbonate (LiCH2CH2OCO2Li) and/or lithium methyl carbonate. The mass ratio between lithium source and cathode active material is 4-9:100. Preferred Method: In step (i), the oxalic acid solution is placed at 45-50 degrees C. In step (iii), the drying is spray drying.

PN BR102019023916-A2
TI Adsorption of CO2 polymeric nanofibers for biofixation of gas in microalgal cultivation, involves developing polymeric fibers for adsorption of carbon dioxide gas,.
AU GREQUE DE MORAIS M
   VIEIRA COSTA J A
   DA SILVA VAZ B
AE UNIV FEDERAL DO RIO GRANDE FURG (URGR-C)
GA 2020537643
AB    NOVELTY - Adsorption of CO2 polymeric nanofibers for biofixation of gas in microalgal cultivation, involves developing polymeric fibers for adsorption of carbon dioxide gas (CO2), where transfer of gas to the solid surface occurs due to the adsorption phenomenon, removing gas through the cultivation of microalgae (s) and/or cyanobacteria.
   USE - Method for adsorption of CO2 polymeric nanofibers for biofixation of gas in microalgal cultivation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method eanbles to adsorption of CO2 polymeric nanofibers for biofixation of gas in microalgal cultivation, which captures CO2 due to the high porosity and surface area, adsorbs large amounts of the gas, contributes to the reduction of CO2 emissions into the atmosphere and consequent reduction of the phenomenon of the greenhouse effect and global warming, provides greater CO2 biofixation capacity, maximize availability of the gas in the culture medium and/or contact time between the gas and the microorganism, and increase the efficiency of absorption of the gas in the microalgal culture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The polymeric fibers are developed for adsorption of CO2 gas due to the high surface area in relation to the volume of these nanostructures that allow high gas adsorption capacity. The combustion gasses originated during the burning of mineral and vegetable coal, oil, petroleum products, ethanol, whole wood or in pieces and paper, cellulose and residues from the paper and cellulose industrialization, and/or from various industrial sources to reduce the emission of gas into the atmosphere. The emission of gas is reduces into the atmosphere, contributed to the reduction of the greenhouse effect and consequent heating phenomenon global. The gas in the cultivation of microalgae (s) and/or cyanobacteria (s) are desorbed for removal and/or CO2 fixation by these microorganisms. The polymeric fibers are developed with polyacrylonitrile polymer (PAN) and dimethylformamide solvent (DMF). The polymeric nanofibers of PAN/DMF are developed with the addition of nanoparticles to increase the adsorption capacity of CO2 due to increased porosity of nanofibers due to nanoparticles. The polymeric PAN/DMF nanofibers are developed without or with iron oxide nanoparticles (NPsFe2O3). The polymeric nanofibers are developed with different solvent polymers, with or without added different nanoparticles, and/or with or without other additives. The polymeric nanofibers are developed with or without NPsFe2O3 by the electrospinning technique. The polymeric nanofibers are deposited with or without photobioreactive NPsFe2O3, free in the liquid culture medium in vertical tubular bioreactors and/or reactors or horizontal or erlenmeyer or raceway or airlift or bubble column or fat plane or thin-layer for cultivating microalgae (s) and/or cyanobacteria (s). The gas is injected to the photobioreactors in the microalgae (s) and/or cyanobacteria (s) cultures. The pH of the culture medium becomes more acid. The micro algae and/or cyanobacteria includes Spirulina sp., Chlorella fusca, Chlorella vulgaris, Chlorella kessleri, Chlorella minutissimaSynechoccus nidulans, Scenedesmus actus, Scenedesmus obliquuos, Synechocystis salina, Selenastrum capricornutum, Jaaguinema geminatum and other genera and species of microalgae and/or cyanobacteria.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN110970630-A; CN110970630-B
TI Preparing copper oxide nanosheet involves            degreasing the copper raw material, followed by acid            treatment and water washing, and adding copper raw            material into mixed solution of ammonium persulfate and            sodium hydroxide.
AU CHEN G
   ZHAO W
   DU R
   LI Z
   BIN Y
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2020288122
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper oxide nanosheet involves                degreasing the copper raw material, followed by                acid treatment and water washing, adding copper raw                material into mixed solution of ammonium persulfate                and sodium hydroxide, ultrasonically processing,                filtering the filtrate, centrifuging to take a                precipitate, washing and drying to obtain a                precursor, grinding the precursor into a powder,                and then heating up to perform baking treatment to                obtain the nanosheet.
   USE - Method for preparing copper oxide nanosheet as                an electrocatalyst in electrochemical reduction                reactions (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process, low cost,                uniform morphology of the obtained product, good                repeatability, and high electrocatalytic                performance, and using the nanosheet as a catalyst                for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide,                using the reversible hydrogen electrode as the                standard, the Faraday efficiency of the carbon ion                product reduced at -0.97 volts exceeds 60%, showing                the high carbon ion product of the electrocatalyst                in carbon dioxide reduction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Preparing copper oxide                  nanosheet involves selecting copper raw material                  as one or more of copper foil, copper pipe,                  copper powder and foamed copper. Preferred                  Conditions: The degreasing treatment involves                  immersing copper raw materials in an acetone                  solution for ultrasonic treatment for 0.1-5                  hours, and 20-40 kHZ ultrasonic frequency. The                  nitric acid solution is used for acid treatment,                  and the concentration of nitric acid solution is                  0.1-1 mol/L. The mixed solution of ammonium                  persulfate and sodium hydroxide is a solution                  formed by uniformly mixing sodium persulfate                  solution and sodium hydroxide solution. In the                  mixed solution of ammonium persulfate and sodium                  hydroxide, the concentration of ammonium                  persulfate is 0.1-5 mol/L, and concentration of                  sodium hydroxide is 1-15 mol/L. The volume ratio                  of mixed solution of copper raw material and                  ammonium persulfate, and sodium hydroxide is a                  mass volume ratio of 0.01-2:1 g/mL. The                  ultrasonic frequency of ultrasonic treatment is                  20-40 kHZ, and ultrasonic treatment time is 0.1-5                  hours. The drying temperature is 60-80 degrees C,                  and the drying time is 1-8 hours. The pore size                  of sieve used for the filtration is 10 mesh. The                  temperature of roasting treatment is 100-500                  degrees C, and time is 2-4 hours.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PN US2020047082-A1; US11305212-B2
TI Extractor for separating an extract from a matrix comprises extraction vessel having internal surface which defines extraction chamber, and piston which sealably engages the internal surface of extraction vessel end.
AU JACKSON G A
   HAKE M D
AE KIINJA CORP (KIIN-Non-standard)
GA 202012710W
AB    NOVELTY - An extractor (1) comprises extraction vessel having an internal surface which defines an extraction chamber which communicates between open extraction vessel 1st and 2nd ends; 1st piston (11) adapted to sealably engage the internal surface of extraction vessel 1st end; and 2nd piston (12) adapted to sealably engage internal surface of extraction vessel 2nd end.
   USE - An extractor for separating an extract from a matrix.
   ADVANTAGE - Controller can control the flow rate, pressure and temperature of carbon dioxide to achieve and maintain pre-selected supercritical conditions for a pre-selected time under which density of supercritical carbon dioxide can be relatively high, and can remain relatively high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) producing an extractor;
   (2) separator; and
   (3) system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an exploded view of the portion of extractor assembly.
   Extractor (1)
   1st piston (11)
   2nd piston (12)
   1st piston actuator (31)
   Pressure receiving plate (70)

PT P
PN CN110721704-A; CN110721704-B
TI Preparing catalytic cloth useful for preserving fruits, preferably mango fruit, comprises e.g. adding permanganate, iron salt, cobalt salt, and oxalate to the water, stirring and uniformly mixing for hydrothermal reaction.
AU XIAO K
   YE P
   ZHU L
   CHENG Y
   WANG Z
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
   GUANGDONG HUAKAI MINGXIN TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202010609M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalytic cloth comprises (i) adding permanganate, iron salt, cobalt salt, and oxalate to the water, stirring and uniformly mixing at 170-120 degrees C for hydrothermal reaction naturally cooling to room temperature, washing and drying the solid product to obtain powdery catalyst, (ii) adding the resultant catalyst to water, dispersing uniformly by ultrasonic treatment, and adding silica solution to stirring and dispersing uniformly to obtain glue solution, and (iii) spraying the resultant glue solution on the cloth base material, and drying.
   USE - The catalytic cloth is useful for preserving fruits (claimed), preferably mango fruit.
   ADVANTAGE - The cloth: has catalytic degrading ethylene function; utilizes oxygen to degrade ethylene into carbon dioxide; reduces oxygen concentration and high-carbon dioxide; inhibits growth and reproduction of microorganisms; and extends fresh-keeping period of fruits and vegetables.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for catalytic cloth prepared by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The iron salt is potassium ferrate, ferric chloride, and/or ferric sulfate. The cobalt salt is cobalt chloride and cobalt nitrate. The oxalate is sodium oxalate, potassium oxalate, and/or ammonium oxalate. The mass ratio of permanganate, iron salt, cobalt salt, oxalate and water is (1.6-5.6): (1.6-5.6): (1.6-5.6): (1.6-5.6): (20-100). The mass ratio of the catalyst, water and silica solution is 1: (0.5-3): (1-4). The cloth-based material is PET non-woven fabric, nylon mesh and/or polyurethane sponge. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the time of the hydrothermal reaction is 7-14 hours. The process of washing and drying by soaking and stirring with distilled water, settling the product, pouring out the upper layer of water, and repeating the operation multiple times until the water after washing has no color, drying the washed product at temperature of 50-150 degrees C for 1-10 hours. In the step (ii) the silica solution is prepared by using TEOS as a precursor, ammonia as a catalyst, and ethanol as a solvent, the TEOS was first mixed with ethanol, ammonia solution was added to the mixture for stirring and heating to obtain transparent silica solution. The thickness of the glue spray is 400-500 m, and the drying means drying at a temperature of 100-150 degrees C for 3-5 hours.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN CN110665502-A
TI Monoatomic silver-based catalyst comprises active component silver and carrier and is obtained by preparing silver and manganese-aluminum-layered double oxide by co-precipitation method and performing calcination at a high temperature.
AU GUAN C
   GUO W
   LANG J
   ZHAO G
   HUANG Z
AE BEIJING HYPERION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202006266H
AB    NOVELTY - Monoatomic silver-based catalyst comprises active component silver and carrier, where the carrier is manganese-aluminum-layered double oxide (MnAl-LDO), and the active component silver is uniformly dispersed on the surface of the manganese-aluminum-layered double oxide in atomic form.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in converting formaldehyde into completely non-toxic carbon dioxide and water under normal temperature and dark conditions (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: achieves efficient removal of formaldehyde under normal temperature and dark conditions, and converts formaldehyde into completely non-toxic carbon dioxide and water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing a monoatomic silver-based catalyst comprising preparing silver and/or manganese-aluminum-layered double oxide by co-precipitation method, then performing calcination of the silver and/or manganese-aluminum-layered double oxide at a high temperature in a hydrogen atmosphere to obtain a monoatomic silver-based catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of the manganese element to the aluminum element is (2-4): 1. The loading amount of silver is 0.8-10% and the grain diameter is 0.01-1 nm. The manganese precursor is a soluble salt of manganese, the aluminum precursor is a soluble salt of aluminum, and the silver precursor is a soluble salt of silver, the total ion concentration of the mixed solution is 0.03-0.06 mol/l, and the molar ratio of the manganese precursor, aluminum precursor, and silver precursor is (2-4): 1: (0.02-0.4). The precipitant is an alkaline substance, the concentration of the precipitant solution is 0.08-0.15 mol/l, and the molar ratio of the precipitant to the metal ion (manganese ion and aluminum ion) is (1-3): 1. Preferred Method: The method for preparing silver and/or manganese-aluminum-layered double oxide, specifically comprises: adding the precipitant solution to the mixed solution containing manganese precursor, aluminum precursor, and silver precursor at room temperature and under stirring, continue to stirring until the reaction is complete, crystallizing, washing to neutrality and drying. The crystallization temperature is 70-150 degrees C, and the crystallization time degree is 8-16 hours. The calcination temperature is 300-500 degrees C, the calcination time is 1-5 hours, and the heating rate is 1-10 degrees C/minute.

PN CN110589866-A; CN110589866-B
TI Preparing aragonite superfine powder using steel residue, involves adding ammonium chloride solution to steel residue, reacting, introducing carbon dioxide to leaching solution, aging, performing vacuum filtration, rinsing and drying.
AU TONG Z
   TAN Z
   WANG J
   SUN J
   WANG W
   CHEN J
AE UNIV YANGTZE NORMAL (UYYN-C)
   UNIV YANGTZE NORMAL (UYYN-C)
GA 2019A76125
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing aragonite superfine powder using steel residue, involves (a) adding ammonium chloride solution to steel residue, reacting to obtain leaching solution of calcium chloride-ammonium chloride-ammonia-water system, and leaving still for overnight, (b) adding sucrose to obtained leaching solution, introducing carbon dioxide to the leaching solution, simultaneously opening ultrasonic waves having power of 1080-1800 W, aging for 3 hours, stopping the reaction when the pH of leaching solution is 7.1, performing vacuum filtration, rinsing and drying to obtain the product.
   USE - Method for preparing aragonite superfine powder using steel residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method shortens the production cycle, is simple, environmentally-friendly, economical, controllable and easy for industrial production, saves resources, and ensures 90% high yield product, and the obtained aragonite superfine powder has high phase purity, and is regular in particle shape, and spherical or quasi-spherical in particle size.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of calcium chloride in leaching solution is 0.4 mol/l. The concentration of ammonium chloride in leaching solution is 1mol/l. The concentration of ammonium hydroxide in leaching solution is 0.8 mol/l. The addition amount of sucrose is 2 %mass of calcium chloride in leaching solution. Preferred Conditions: The step (a) involves reacting at 40-60 degrees C. In step (b), the temperature of leaching solution during reaction process is 15-25 degrees C. The flow rate of carbon dioxide is 45 ml/minute. The step (b) involves drying at 80-125 degrees C for 1-18 hours.

PT P
PN WO2019224429-A1; FI128164-B1; IN202047055116-A; CN112166127-A; BR112020021452-A2; KR2021015858-A; EP3797125-A1; US2021206883-A1; JP2021523963-W; EP3797125-B1; CN112166127-B; RU2020142078-A
TI Controlling discharges of nitrogen compounds in            production of cellulose carbamate comprises producing            microcrystalline cellulose (MCC) from chemical pulp            produced and pulp is subjected to acid hydrolysis and            reacting MCC with urea.
AU KANTOLA J
   RAMARK H
   VALTA K
   VANHATALO K
   ROMARK H
   KANTOLA I
AE ANDRITZ OY (ANDZ-C)
   ANDRITZ OY (ANDZ-C)
   ANDRITZ OY (ANDZ-C)
   ANDRITZ OY (ANDZ-C)
GA 201999765M
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling discharges of nitrogen compounds                in the production of cellulose carbamate (CCA),                comprises producing microcrystalline cellulose from                chemical pulp produced at a pulp mill, such that                the chemical pulp is subjected to acid hydrolysis                at an elevated temperature to form microcrystalline                cellulose (MCC) and hydrolysate, and reacting the                MCC with urea to produce cellulose carbamate where                ammonia is released, and the microcrystalline                cellulose production and the cellulose carbamate                production are integrated to the pulp mill having a                flue gas system such that carbon dioxide from the                flue gases is reacted with released ammonia to                produce urea, which is used in the carbamate                production.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling                discharges of nitrogen compounds in production of                cellulose carbamate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides an efficient way to                utilize available ammonia and carbon dioxide and                thus control their emissions from the integrated                CCA, MCC plant and pulp mill.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide is                  obtained from flue gases of a lime kiln. The                  released ammonia or ammonia- containing                  wastewater from the carbamate production is used                  to neutralize hydrolysate and/or acidic                  wastewater from the MCC production. At least a                  portion of nitrogen- containing wastewater from                  the CCA production is led to an evaporation plant                  and concentrated to produce dry nitrogen                  fertilizers. The nitrogen-containing waste water                  from the CCA production is conveyed to a                  wastewater treatment plant of the pulp mill to be                  used as nutrient. The hydrolysate or acidic                  wastewater from the MCC production are used to                  neutralize wastewater from the CCA                  production.
   EXAMPLE - Bleached softwood Kraft pulp was hydrolyzed in                a reactor with sulfuric acid. Hydrolysis                circumstances were 1.5% acid dosage, 10% pulp                consistency, 30 minutes reaction time and 160                degrees C temperature. The produced                microcrystalline cellulose (MCC) was washed three                times using dilution thickening washing and finally                centrifuged to a consistency of 45%. Carbamation                was started by dosing urea to wet MCC having a dry                material content of 45%. The urea dosages were                4-10% on dry content of MCC. Then, the material was                homogenized in a screen compactor. The reaction was                completed in an oven in which the retention time                and temperature were 3.5 hours and 135 degrees C.                Final cellulose carbamate was washed and                dried.
DC A11 (Polysaccharides; natural rubber; other natural polymers (only a restricted range of (modified) natural polymers are included. Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).)

PT P
PN CN110499488-A
TI Processing corrugated heat-treatment production comprises e.g. setting corrugated roller in the special nitriding furnace, inletting ammonia gas, three kinds of carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas, nitriding, preserving the heat, and cooling.
AU CHEN Y
AE TAI METAL HEAT TREATMENT CO LTD (TAIM-Non-standard)
GA 2019A3961V
AB    NOVELTY - Processing corrugated heat-treatment production comprises (i) setting corrugated roller in the special nitriding furnace, inletting ammonia gas, three kinds of carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas, nitriding, preserving the heat for a certain time under set temperature, and cooling, (ii) the corrugated roller mounted on the intermediate machine for induction heating and quenching, quenching instant tempering furnace, and (iii) adding the corrugated roller after tempering into the deep furnace into liquid nitrogen at -200 degrees C for cryogenic treatment, discharging after preserving the heat for a certain time, corrugating, rising temperature to room temperature, and tempering in tempering furnace.
   USE - The method is useful for processing corrugated heat-treatment production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves nitriding process, avoids the phenomenon of tooth breakage in the use of corrugated rollers, improves product quality of corrugated rollers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the soft nitriding in the nitriding furnace is divided into nine stages, One stage, heating up to 150 degrees C, Second stage, keep the temperature at 150 degrees C for 30min, Three stages, heating up to 480 degrees C, Four stages, temperature 480 degrees C for 30min, Five stages, heating up to 570 degrees C, Six stages, temperature 570 degrees C for 300min, Seven stages, cooling down to 480 degrees C, Eighth stage, keep the temperature at 480 degrees C for 30min, Nine sections, Cool down to 200 degrees C. The nitrogen flow in the first stage is 15 m3/minutes, The nitrogen flow in the second stage is 5 m3/minutes, The nitrogen flow of the three stages is 5 m3/minutes, The nitrogen flow of the four sections is 5 m3/minutes, The nitrogen flow of the five stages is 15 m3/minutes, The nitrogen flow of the six stages is 5 m3/minutes, The nitrogen flow of the seventh stage is 5 m3/minutes, The nitrogen flow of the eight sections is 10 m3/minutes, The nitrogen flow of the 9th section is 0 m3/minutes. PT P
PN CN110330516-A; CN110330516-B
TI New ternary imidazole ligand zinc complex useful for energy gas purification fields, and prepared by e.g. reacting 1,3,5-tribromobenzene, 2-methylimidazole, potassium carbonate, copper(II) sulfate and zinc chloride.
AU LIU S
   LIU L
   ZHOU Y
   ZHANG F
   WANG T
AE UNIV SHANGQIU NORMAL (USQN-C)
GA 201986830D
AB    NOVELTY - Ternary imidazole ligand zinc complex (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful for energy gas purification fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process, low cost and mild reaction conditions. (I): has excellent adsorption performance for carbon dioxide, and excellent selectivity; and realizes the separation of carbon dioxide and nitrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ternary imidazole ligand zinc complex of formula (Zn3(L)2Cl6) (I) is new. The ternary imidazole ligand zinc complex crystal is monoclinic crystal system, P21/c space group, and HCA topology structure with 3-c connection network, and the unit cell parameters are: a is 16.949(12), b is 17.183(12), c is 18.588(13), alpha is 90, beta is 93.407(9), gamma is 90 degrees , and V is 5403.9 Angstrom 3.
   L = 1,3,5-tris(2-methyl-1H-imidazol-1-yl)benzene of formula (I).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises (i) mixing 1,3,5-tribromobenzene, 2-methylimidazole, potassium carbonate and copper(II) sulfate, grinding evenly, then loading into a polytetrafluoroethylene lined reactor, passing nitrogen gas, then keeping it in an oven to heating the reaction, cooling to room temperature after the reaction, washing the residue with water, filtering, extracting, filtering and rotary evaporating to obtain ligand L, (ii) adding ligand L prepared in step (i) and zinc chloride into a mixed solvent of N,N-dimethylformamide and water, then adding concentrated hydrochloric acid dropwise, and stirring until the solution is clear, and (iii) transferring the clear solution prepared in the step (ii) into a glass bottle, stamping, then placing in an oven, reacting at 80-85 degrees C, filtering, washing and drying to obtain final product. Preferred Method: The oven reaction time in step (iii) is 24 hours.

GA 201983703J
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing high-purity vaterite-type and                calcite-type calcium carbonate involves mixing and                stirring indirect carbonation reaction raw                materials and seawater at a ratio of 1.0-10.0 g/50                ml, precipitating magnesium present in the                seawater, eluting calcium present in the indirect                carbonation reaction raw material to obtain a                calcium eluate, removing magnesium which is                precipitated in the calcium eluate, injecting                carbon dioxide into the calcium eluate by removing                magnesium to obtain vaterite and calcite-type                calcium carbonate, and washing the vaterite and                calcite-type calcium carbonate with water to                increase the purity of 99% or more.
   USE - Manufacture of high-purity vaterite-type and                calcite-type calcium carbonate from general                seawater, seawater desalination concentrated water,                brine, and/or bittern (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and economical                manufacture of high-purity vaterite-type and                calcite-type calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The indirect                  carbonation reaction raw material comprises paper                  sludge ash, cement kiln dust, fuel ash (bottom                  ash), fly ash, de-inking ash, steel slag, and/or                  waste concrete, preferably calcium oxide, or                  calcium hydroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The method is                  performed using alkali such as ammonium                  hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, or potassium                  hydroxide. Preferred Property: Calcium carbonate                  has average particle diameter of 1.0-5.0 mu                  m.

PT P
PN CN110227454-A; CN110227454-B
TI Reduction catalyst useful in reducing carbon dioxide or production of carbon monoxide comprises silver nanoparticles, carbon black, and metal zinc.
AU ZHANG X
   GAO Y
   HUANG B
   WANG Z
   WANG P
   ZHENG Z
   LIU Y
   QIN X
   ZHANG Q
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 2019805661
AB    NOVELTY - Reduction catalyst comprises silver nanoparticles, carbon black, and metal zinc.
   USE - The reduction catalyst or reduction electrode is useful in reducing carbon dioxide or production of carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The reduction electrode: has higher Faraday efficiency and current density; has higher carbon monoxide selectivity and activity than pure zinc electrode and carbon silver electrode; has reduced amount of noble metal silver due to the synergistic action of zinc, carbon and silver, thus reduces the cost of the material; is suitable for industrial applications of electrochemical carbon monoxide production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for: (1) preparation of reduction catalyst comprising providing silver nanoparticles, carbon black and metal zinc with nano zinc grown in situ on the surface, mixing silver nanoparticles and carbon black in solvent system of isopropanol, water and Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer), ultrasonically obtaining ink-like carbon/silver mixture, dripping the mixture on zinc sheet having a surface of nano zinc, and drying to obtain reduction catalyst; and (2) reduction electrode comprising the reduction catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The size of the silver nanoparticles is 1-1000 nm. The mass ratio of the silver nanoparticles and carbon black is 0.001-1:1. The metal zinc is a zinc sheet, where nano zinc is grown in situ on the surface. In the preparation of reducing catalyst, the volume ratio of isopropanol, water and Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) in the solvent system is 1-20:1-10:1; the ultrasonication is carried out for 1-5 hours; the rate of dripping the mixture is 1-3 drops/second, and the thickness of carbon/silver mixture on zinc sheet is 10-10000 nm. Preferred Process: The preparation of silver nanoparticles comprises adding silver nitrate solution, trisodium citrate solution and hydrogen peroxide in water, adding sodium borohydride, vigorously stirring to obtain solution with dispersed silver nanoparticles, centrifuging, and collecting silver nanoparticles. The preparation of zinc sheet with nano zinc on the surface comprises providing zinc foil as anode and cathode, using water/ethanol solution containing salicylic acid in the double-electrode system, and oxidizing under a bias of 30 V for 1 hour.

TI Preparing plant blending oil involves taking Euphausia superba powder and vegetable oil, extracting mixture using supercritical carbon dioxide, maintaining temperature of separation kettle and extracting.
AU SONG L
   ZHAO H
   XU X
   LI X
   ZHANG J
   YANG W
   JIANG P
   LIU Y
   ZHOU D
AE UNIV DALIAN POLYTECHNIC (UYDP-C)
GA 201970779H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing plant blending oil involves (a) taking Euphausia superba powder and vegetable oil at weight ratio of 1:(0.1-1) and uniformly mixing to obtain a mixture, (b) extracting the mixture obtained in step (a) at 30-55 degrees C in 20-35 MPa using carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method, (c) maintaining the temperature of separation kettle (I) at 35-55 degrees C in 5-15 MPa, and temperature of separation kettle (II) at 30-40 degrees C in 3-10 MPa, and extracting for 30-120 minutes, where product obtained in separation kettle (I) is plant blending oil.
   USE - Method for preparing plant blending oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The blending oil is rich in phospholipids and nutrients, pure, natural and easy to absorb and utilize by human body, has soft flavor, removes odor, increases the nutritional value and commercial value of refined oil and blended oil.

Preferred Components: The vegetable oil in step (a) comprises soybean oil, peanut oil, olive oil, sunflower oil and/or corn oil. Preferred Method: The method involves (i) taking 2000 g E.superba powder having 200 mesh sieve and 10 wt.% water content, and 1000 g soybean oil and uniformly mixing to obtain a mixture, (ii) extracting the using carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method at 55 degrees C in 35 MPa, maintaining the temperature of separation kettle (I) at 55 degrees C in 15 MPa, maintaining the temperature of separation kettle (II) at 40 degrees C in 9 MPa, and extracting in 35 MPa, and separating the kettle (I) in 15 MPa, separating the kettle (II) in 9 MPa for 45 minutes, and separating the product from kettle (I) to obtain the blending oil.

GA 201966032L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing salicylic acid using microchannel continuous flow reactor comprises (i) placing preheated sodium phenolate solution and carbon dioxide in microchannel continuous flow reactor, then mixing and reacting, cooling and discharging the material liquid after gas-liquid separation, and collecting the liquid solution, and (ii) extracting the liquid solution obtained in step (i) using water, and acidifying the obtained aqueous solution to obtain crude final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing salicylic acid using microchannel continuous flow reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the gas-liquid contact area in reaction process, production efficiency and the stability of product quality; utilizes microchannel reactor having small diameter which is beneficial to the mass transfer and heat transfer of the reaction system; reduces the occurrence of side reactions during the reaction; impr; has conversion rate of sodium phenolate of 93% and product yield of not less than 91%; and is suitable for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The microchannel continuous flow reactor comprises a preheating module, a mixing module, a reaction module, a cooling module, a separation module and a product collection module.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the molar ratio between sodium phenolate and carbon dioxide is 1:1.0-50.0, the residence time is 10-900 seconds, the reaction pressure is 0.1-2.0 MPa and the reaction temperature is 100-200 degrees C. In step (ii), the acidifying process controls pH of 1-4. The step (ii) comprises recrystallizing the obtained crude salicylic acid in acidic aqueous solution, and then refining. Preferred Components: The solvent of sodium phenol solution is alcohol solvent, ether solvent, ketone solvent, ester solvent and/or aromatic hydrocarbon solvent in any ratio.

AB    NOVELTY - Production of calcium carbonate using alkali                industrial by-products and seawater, involves (i)                mixing alkali industrial by-products at a rate of                1.0-10.0 g per 100 ml of seawater such that                magnesium present in seawater is impregnated and                calcium present in the alkali industrial                by-products is eluted and making the pH of the                seawater in the range of 9.50 or more, (ii)                removing the precipitated magnesium from the                calcium eluate, (iii) adjusting the pH of the                calcium eluate to 12 or more, and (iv) introducing                carbon dioxide into the calcium eluate.
   USE - Production of calcium carbonate using seawater                and alkali industrial by-products e.g. paper sludge                ash and cement kiln dust, fuel ash, bottom ash, fly                ash, de-inking ash, steel slag and waste concrete                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical and efficient                production of calcium carbonate with improved                purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The seawater is                  chosen from general seawater, desalted                  concentrated seawater, brine, and bittern.                  Preferred Properties: The step (iv) involves                  terminating the injection of carbon dioxide when                  the pH of the eluate reaches the range of 9-11.                  The step (iii) involves controlling the average                  particle diameter of the calcium carbonate in the                  range of 1.0-10.0 mu m obtained by adjusting the                  temperature of the eluate from which the                  precipitated magnesium is removed. The                  temperature of the effluent from which the                  precipitated magnesium is removed is adjusted in                  the range of 0-100 degrees C.

PN CN112756000-A
TI Preparing sulfide semiconductor/metal nanoparticles with sulfur vacancy defects for use in photolysis of water, involves preparing suspension liquid of sulfide semiconductor nano-catalyst with sulfur vacancy defects.
AU LI P
   WANG J
   YANG S
   LIU Y
   WANG L
   QU P
AE UNIV SHANGQIU NORMAL (USQN-C)
GA 202154014H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sulfide semiconductor/metal nanoparticles with sulfur vacancy defects involves preparing a suspension liquid of a sulfide semiconductor nano-catalyst with sulfur vacancy defects, and preparing metal ions precursor solution. The suspension liquid of a sulfide semiconductor with sulfur vacancy defects and a metal ion precursor solution are mixed.
   USE - Method for preparing sulfide semiconductor/metal nanoparticles with sulfur vacancy defects for use in photodegradation, photolysis of water, and photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, and photolysis of water (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The sulfide semiconductor/metal nanoparticles preparation method is simple to operate, and does not require external energy input.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sulfide semiconductor with sulfur vacancy defect is zinc indium sulfide or indium sulfide. Preferred Process: The sulfide semiconductor with sulfur vacancy defects is prepared by mixing zinc chloride as a zinc source, indium chloride as indium source, thioacetamide as a sulfur source, and a mixed solution of ethylene glycol and water as reaction medium. The zinc indium sulfide nanocatalyst is prepared by solvothermal method. Zinc chloride tetrahydrate, indium chloride tetrahydrate and thioacetamide are mixed in a ratio of 1:2:4. The volume ratio of the ethylene glycol and water is 1:5. The mixture is stirred for 60 minutes, and transferred into a reactor for heating at 150 degrees C for 12 hours, and then cooled to room temperature naturally, centrifuged to wash the resulting precipitate, and placed in a 65 degrees C vacuum drying oven to dry.

TI Salt lake lithium carbonate extraction method involves taking salt lake brine, adding flocculant, centrifuging, taking supernatant, airing, concentrating, filtering, taking filtrate, heating, filtering, taking filtrate, and rapid cooling.
AU WANG Y
   WANG H
AE CHANGXIN ECOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY SHAANXI (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201978004D
AB    NOVELTY - A salt lake lithium carbonate extraction method comprises taking salt lake brine, adding flocculant, centrifuging, taking supernatant, airing, concentrating, filtering, taking filtrate, heating at 75-85 degrees C for 10-15 minutes, filtering, taking filtrate, rapid cooling to 10-15 degrees C by water cooling for 15-30 minutes, filtering, taking filtrate, adding alkali solution, adjusting pH value to 12, stirring for 20 minutes, filtering, introducing saturated carbon dioxide, precipitating lithium carbonate, washing with hot water, and calcining.
   USE - A salt lake lithium carbonate extraction method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high product purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The salt lake lithium carbonate extraction method comprises taking salt lake brine, adding aluminum chloride flocculant, standing for 1-1.5 hours, centrifuging at rate of 250-300 revolutions/minute for 15-20 minutes, taking supernatant, airing, concentrating, filtering, taking filtrate, heating at 85 degrees C for 10 minutes, filtering, taking filtrate, rapid cooling to 15 degrees C by water cooling for 15 minutes, filtering, taking filtrate, adding 25 wt.% ammonia water alkali solution, adjusting pH value to 12, stirring for 20 minutes, filtering, introducing saturated carbon dioxide, precipitating lithium carbonate, washing with hot water, and calcining.

AU YIN X
AE CHANGSHA ZICHEN TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019660421
AB    NOVELTY - A light calcium carbonate using calcium                carbide slag is prepared by adding water to calcium                carbide slag, mixing with oxidant or precipitant,                purifying, obtaining nontoxic and odor-free                suspended slurry, pumping industrial carbon                dioxide, precipitating to obtain calcium                bicarbonate supersaturated solution mixture                containing solid impurities, performing                solid-liquid separation, obtaining filter residue                and filtrate, taking filtrate as calcium                bicarbonate-containing supersaturated solution, and                drying.
   USE - Method for preparing light calcium carbonate                using calcium carbide slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is low-cost, environment-friendly,                energy efficient, and has good product quality and                purity of &#8805; 97%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The light calcium                  carbonate using calcium carbide slag is prepared                  by adding water to calcium carbide slag according                  to mass ratio of 20-5:1, mixing with 0.50-0.005                  wt.% oxidant comprising chlorine dioxide,                  hydrogen peroxide, sodium percarbonate or                  ferrate, or 0.20-0.002 wt.% precipitant                  comprising copper sulfate or copper chloride,                  purifying, obtaining nontoxic and odor-free                  suspended slurry, adding 0.005-0.05 wt.%                  dispersant or 0.02-0.2 wt.% carbon dioxide                  hydrate promoter, pumping industrial carbon                  dioxide under - 2&#176; C to 35&#176; C                  under 0.5-10.0 MPa for 0.2-2 hours, precipitating                  to obtain calcium bicarbonate supersaturated                  solution mixture containing solid impurities,                  performing solid-liquid separation, obtaining                  filter residue and filtrate, taking filtrate as                  calcium bicarbonate-containing supersaturated                  solution, air drying or spray drying at                  110-500&#176; C, utilizing produced carbon                  dioxide as liquefaction agent for calcium carbide                  slag hydrogenation reaction and recovered water                  for pulping water, adding 0.01-0.85 wt.% particle                  modifier or 0.002-0.2 wt.% colorant, mixing,                  grinding into 325-2000 mesh. Preferred                  Components: The particle modifier is titanate,                  silane, fatty acid salt, silicone surfactant or                  liquid wax. The colorant is organic or inorganic                  pigment.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dispersant is                  fluorocarbon surfactant or alkyl surfactant. The                  carbon dioxide hydrate promoter is alcohol ether                  or alcoholamine.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing autologous fibroblast                involves taking 2.5-3.5x 2.5-3.5 mm autologous skin                tissue, washing, cutting into pieces, uniformly                spreading autologous skin tissue in culture dish,                adding fibroblast culture medium, carrying out                primary cell culture at 37 degrees C under carbon                dioxide concentration of 5% until the cell                confluence reaches to 70-80%, removing epithelial                cells by trypsinization, terminating the digestion                with fibroblast culture medium, and subculturing                the cell.

PT P
PN CN114538471-A
TI Comprehensive utilization of sodium sulfate-sodium            chloride mixed salt comprises separation and recovery            of ammonium sulfate, separation and recovery of sodium            carbonate and ammonium chloride.
AU WANG Y
   WANG X
AE NINGBO FMR ENVIRON & TECH CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202275024H
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensive utilization of sodium                sulfate-sodium chloride mixed salt comprises                separation and recovery of ammonium sulfate,                separation and recovery of sodium carbonate and                ammonium chloride.
   USE - The method is useful for comprehensive                utilization of sodium sulfate-sodium chloride mixed                salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, and suitable for                industrial production; and has high comprehensive                utilization rate, good economic benefit, and                convenient operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Comprehensive utilization of sodium                sulfate-sodium chloride mixed salt comprises (i)                using slurry or solution of sodium sulfate-sodium                chloride mixed salt as raw material, using ammonium                bicarbonate, or a mixture of carbon dioxide/ammonia                as splitting agent, adding ammonium bicarbonate or                mixed gas of carbon dioxide/ammonia to the slurry                or solution of sodium sulfate-sodium chloride mixed                salt, stirring, using the same ion effect produced                by bicarbonate ion and ammonium ion, Na+ and Cl- in                it are forced to crystallize in the form of sodium                bicarbonate and ammonium chloride, respectively to                obtain slurry, then processing in either of the                following two ways: (1) filtering obtained slurry                to obtain primary crystallized product and a                primary crystallized mother liquor, then the                primary crystallized mother liquor is frozen,                further crystallized, and filtered to obtain                secondary crystallized product and secondary                crystallized mother liquor; (2) the obtained slurry                is frozen, further crystallized, and filtered to                obtain the mixture of primary crystallization and                secondary crystallization and secondary                crystallization mother liquor; (ii) evaporating and                concentrating secondary crystallization mother                liquor, so that 45-85% of ammonium sulfate is                crystallized out, obtaining tertiary                crystallization product and tertiary                crystallization mother liquor by filtration,                returning tertiary crystallization mother liquor to                step (i) and combining with mixed solution of                sodium sulfate and sodium chloride for                comprehensive utilization, or returns to the                dissolving solution for preparing the sodium                sulfate-sodium chloride mixed salt, after the                obtained tertiary crystals are dried, they are                directly used as agricultural ammonium sulfate                fertilizers, or a saturated ammonium sulfate                solution is used as the first refined solution, and                the tertiary crystals are added, heating, stirring,                washing, cooling, and filtering to obtain the                washed liquid and the refined ammonium sulfate                product, (iii) using saturated sodium bicarbonate                solution as second refined solution, adding primary                crystal, or the mixture of primary crystal and                secondary crystal, heating and stirring for                refining, so that ammonium chloride, ammonium                sulfate, sodium chloride and sodium sulfate are                dissolved into the solution, and filtered while hot                to obtain sodium bicarbonate filter cake and                ammonium chloride solution, solve the refined                sodium carbonate products, cooling the obtained                ammonium chloride solution, crystallizing, and                filtering to obtain ammonium chloride filter cake                and ammonium chloride crystallization mother                liquor; returning obtained ammonium chloride                crystallization mother liquor to continue to be                used as the second refined solution, and the closed                circuit is circulated until any one of the ammonium                sulfate, sodium chloride and sodium sulfate is                enriched to near saturation, and the circuit is                open, return to step (i) to combine with mixed                solution of sodium sulfate and sodium chloride for                comprehensive utilization, or return to the                dissolving solution for preparing sodium                sulfate-sodium chloride mixed salt; the obtained                ammonium chloride filter cake is directly used as a                product after drying, or a saturated ammonium                chloride solution is used as the third refined                solution, and the ammonium chloride filter cake is                added to it, heating and stirring for refining, and                cooling, filtering, the purified liquid and the                purified ammonium chloride product are obtained,                after the gained refining, returning the liquid to                continue to be used as the third refined liquid,                and the closed circuit is circulated until any one                of the ammonium sulfate, sodium bicarbonate, sodium                chloride and sodium sulfate is enriched to near                saturation, and the circuit is open, return to step                (i) to combine with the mixed solution of sodium                sulfate and sodium chloride for comprehensive                utilization, or return to the dissolving solution                for preparing sodium sulfate-sodium chloride mixed                salt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the raw                  material refers to using slurry or solution of                  sodium sulfate-sodium chloride mixed salt with a                  molar ratio of sulfate/chloride of 0.1-50 as a                  raw material, add sodium sulfate-sodium chloride                  mixed salt into water to prepare solution with                  Na+ ion concentration of 90-162 g/l, or according                  to the ratio of Na+ ion concentration of 120-180                  g/l, mix sodium sulfate-sodium chloride mixed                  salt with water to make a slurry, or directly use                  a solution containing sodium sulfate and sodium                  chloride as raw materials, and after purification                  and concentration, the Na+ ion concentration in                  it reaches 90-162 g/l; using ammonium bicarbonate                  as a resolving agent, controlling the                  ammonium/Na+ molar ratio to be 1.05-1.25:1,                  stirring ammonium bicarbonate into the slurry or                  solution of sodium sulfate-sodium chloride mixed                  salt; use mixed gas of carbon dioxide/ammonia as                  resolving agent, where the carbon dioxide/ammonia                  molar ratio is 1-1.1:1, and the ammonia/Na+ molar                  ratio is controlled to 1.05-1.25:1, bubble mixed                  gas into the slurry or solution of sodium                  sulfate-sodium chloride mixed salt, or first                  ammonia and then carbon dioxide according to the                  carbon dioxide/ammonia molar ratio of 1-1.1:1;                  after adding the resolving agent, stir at a                  temperature of 1-40&#176;C for 0.5-2.5 hours,                  using the same ion effect generated by                  bicarbonate ion and ammonia ion, forcing Na+ and                  Cl- to crystallize out in the form of sodium                  bicarbonate and ammonium chloride respectively,                  the primary crystallization product and primary                  crystallization mother liquor are obtained by                  filtration, and then the primary crystallization                  mother liquor is placed at a temperature of                  -18&#176;C to 0&#176;C for 2-6 hours of                  freezing and crystallization, filter to obtain                  secondary crystals and secondary crystallization                  mother liquor, or directly freeze the                  crystal-containing slurry obtained after                  splitting at a temperature of -18&#176;C to                  0&#176;C, and continue to crystallize for 2-6                  hours, the mixture of primary crystal and                  secondary crystal and secondary crystal mother                  liquor are obtained by filtration; in the step                  (ii), saturated ammonium sulfate solution at room                  temperature is used as the first refined                  solution, and the tertiary crystals are added to                  it according to the solid-liquid ratio of 1:0.3-3                  g/ml, stir and wash at 25-95&#176;C for 0.5-1                  hour, cool to room temperature, and filter to                  obtain the washed liquid and purified ammonium                  sulfate product, the liquid after the gained                  washing is returned to continue to be used as the                  first refined liquid, and the closed-circuit                  circulation is carried out until any one of the                  ammonium chloride, sodium chloride, sodium                  carbonate and sodium sulfate therein is enriched                  to near saturation, open circuit, return to step                  (i) and combine with the mixed solution of sodium                  sulfate and sodium chloride for comprehensive                  utilization, or return to the dissolving solution                  for preparing sodium sulfate-sodium chloride                  mixed salt; in the step (iii), saturated sodium                  bicarbonate solution at -5&#176;C to                  25&#176;C is used as the second refined                  solution, and the primary crystal or the mixture                  of primary crystal and secondary crystal is added                  into it according to the solid-liquid ratio of                  1:0.5-5 g/ml, stir at 40-64&#176;C for 0.5-1                  hour, so that ammonium chloride, ammonium                  sulfate, sodium chloride and sodium sulfate are                  dissolved into the solution, filtered while hot                  to obtain a sodium bicarbonate filter cake and a                  solution containing ammonium chloride, the sodium                  bicarbonate filter cake is washed and then                  pyrolyzed to obtain a qualified sodium carbonate                  product, the obtained solution is cooled,                  crystallized at -5&#176;C to 25&#176;C                  for 0.5-2.5 hours, and filtered to obtain the                  ammonium chloride filter cake and the ammonium                  chloride crystal mother liquor; obtained ammonium                  chloride crystallization mother liquor is                  returned to continue to be used as the second                  refined solution, and is closed-circuited until                  any one of the ammonium sulfate, sodium chloride                  and sodium sulfate therein is enriched to near                  saturation, open circuit, return to step (i) and                  combine with the mixed solution of sodium sulfate                  and sodium chloride for comprehensive                  utilization, or return to the dissolving solution                  for preparing sodium sulfate-sodium chloride                  mixed salt; the obtained ammonium chloride filter                  cake is directly used as a product after drying,                  or a saturated ammonium chloride solution at room                  temperature is used as the third refined                  solution, add ammonium chloride filter cake into                  it according to the solid-liquid ratio of                  1:0.5-2.5 g/ml, stir and refine at                  25-105&#176;C for 0.5-2.5 hours, cool to room                  temperature, purified liquid and the purified                  ammonium chloride product are obtained by                  filtration, after the gained refining, the liquid                  is returned to continue to be used as the third                  refined liquid, and the closed-circuit                  circulation is carried out until any one of the                  ammonium sulfate, sodium bicarbonate, sodium                  chloride and sodium sulfate is enriched to near                  saturation, open circuit, return to step (i) and                  combine with the mixed solution of sodium sulfate                  and sodium chloride for comprehensive                  utilization, or return to the dissolving solution                  for preparing sodium sulfate-sodium chloride                  mixed salt. In the step (ii), the carbon dioxide                  and ammonia released during the evaporation and                  concentration of the secondary crystallization                  mother liquor are collected for synthesizing                  ammonium bicarbonate, or directly introduce into                  the slurry or solution of sodium sulfate-sodium                  chloride mixed salt in step (i) as a resolving                  agent to improve the utilization rate of ammonium                  bicarbonate. In the step (iii), in the process of                  dissolving ammonium chloride, the ammonium                  bicarbonate mixed in the primary crystal and the                  mixture of the primary crystal and the secondary                  crystal is decomposed and volatilized ammonia in                  saturated sodium bicarbonate solution at                  30-60&#176;C, absorb with dilute sulfuric                  acid or ammonium hydrogen sulfate solution to                  prevent the escape of ammonia. In the step (iii),                  the washing refers to adding the sodium                  bicarbonate filter cake to water or an aqueous                  solution of saturated sodium bicarbonate at a                  solid-to-liquid ratio of 1:0.5-3.5 g/ml, and                  stirring and washing at 25-60&#176;C for                  0.5-2.5 hours, filtering to obtain washing sodium                  bicarbonate and its washing liquid, after the                  gained washing, the liquid is returned to                  continue to be used as the washing liquid of the                  sodium bicarbonate filter cake, and the closed                  circuit is circulated until any one of the                  ammonium sulfate, ammonium chloride, sodium                  chloride and sodium sulfate is enriched to near                  saturation, open circuit, combined with the mixed                  solution of sodium sulfate and sodium chloride in                  step (i) for comprehensive utilization, or used                  to prepare the dissolving solution of sodium                  sulfate-sodium chloride mixed salt. In the step                  (iii), the pyrolysis is dry pyrolysis or wet                  pyrolysis, and the gas released during the                  pyrolysis process is directly introduced into the                  slurry or solution of the sodium sulfate-sodium                  chloride mixed salt in step (i) to improve                  comprehensive utilization of raw and auxiliary                  materials, the dry pyrolysis means that the                  washed sodium bicarbonate filter cake is calcined                  at 150-300&#176;C for 0.25-1.25 hours, so                  that the sodium bicarbonate in it is converted                  into sodium carbonate; the wet pyrolysis refers                  to using a saturated sodium carbonate solution at                  25-60&#176;C as wet pyrolysis agent, and                  adding the washed sodium bicarbonate filter cake                  into it at a solid-to-liquid ratio of 1:0.5-5                  g/ml, heating and decomposing, stirring at                  65-250&#176;C for 0.5-2.5 hours to convert it                  into sodium carbonate, cooling to                  25-60&#176;C, sodium carbonate filter cake                  and post-pyrolysis liquid are obtained by                  filtration, and the obtained post-pyrolysis                  liquid is returned to continue to be used as a                  pyrolysis agent for sodium bicarbonate,                  closed-circuit circulation, until the sodium                  chloride or sodium sulfate is enriched to near                  saturation, open the circuit, introduce carbon                  dioxide to convert the sodium carbonate into                  sodium bicarbonate to crystallize out, filter,                  and the filter cake is directly used as sodium                  bicarbonate product, or returned to pyrolysis to                  produce sodium carbonate product, filtrate then                  returns to step (i) and is combined with sodium                  sulfate and sodium chloride mixed solution for                  comprehensive utilization, or is used to prepare                  the dissolving solution of sodium sulfate-sodium                  chloride mixed salt. In the process of                  comprehensive utilization of sodium                  sulfate-sodium chloride mixed salt and its                  solution, the effective utilization rates of                  sodium sulfate and sodium chloride are both                  &#8805;97%. The purity of the purified sodium                  carbonate product obtained in the process is                  98-99.2%, the purity of the purified ammonium                  sulfate product is 98.5-99.6%, and the purity of                  the purified ammonium chloride product is                  99-99.8%.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.)
MC C05-A01B; C05-C05; C05-C07; C05-C04; C05-C01; C14-T03; C14-S18
IP C01C-001/16; C01C-001/244; C01D-007/12; C05C-003/00
PD CN114538471-A   27 May 2022   C01C-001/244   202279      Chinese
