PN CN114521459-A
TI Cultivating heat-resistant, disease-resistant,            nitrogen-efficient rice by selecting Ruiliangyou 9578            rice, performing high temperature treatment,            transplanting treated seedlings with excellent growth            status, harvesting and hybridizing rice.
AU LIU J
   GAO Q
   LV X
   CONG X
AE ANHUI GUORUI SEED IND CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202276116N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for cultivating heat-resistant,                disease-resistant, nitrogen-efficient rice involves                selecting Ruiliangyou 9578 rice seeds, soaking and                sterilizing the seeds, incubating in several                incubators for hydroponic culture, dividing all                incubators into two groups, using one group as a                high temperature treatment group, and other group                as a standard temperature control group, when the                hydroponics of rice seeds in the incubator reaches                the 4-leaf stage, subjecting the high temperature                treatment group in the incubator to high                temperature treatment, continuing culturing,                applying trace liquid nitrogen fertilizer,                selecting the high temperature treated seedlings                with excellent growth status, transplanting,                harvesting after the maturity stage, hybridizing                and then self-crossing to obtain heat-resistant                varieties at the booting stage with stable genetic                traits.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating                heat-resistant, disease-resistant,                nitrogen-efficient rice.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares rice beads, which has good                performance and is suitable for planting in high                temperature and high humidity environments.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for cultivating heat-resistant,                disease-resistant, nitrogen-efficient rice involves                (a) selecting Ruiliangyou 9578 rice seeds, soaking                and sterilizing the seeds, incubating in several                incubators for hydroponic culture, dividing all                incubators into two groups and using one group as a                high temperature treatment group, and other group                as a standard temperature control group, (b) when                the hydroponics of rice seeds in the incubator                reaches the 4-leaf stage, subjecting the high                temperature treatment group in the incubator to                high temperature treatment for 3-4 days at                43degrees Celsius at 9:00-5:00 pm and 37-38degrees                Celsius at 5:00-9:00 am next day, incubating the                cultured seedlings of the standard temperature                control group in another incubator of the same                type, controlling the light intensity, light time,                humidity and carbon dioxide concentration same as                those of the high temperature treatment group,                controlling the temperature at 32-33degrees                Celsius, and continuing culturing for the same                number of days as the high temperature treatment,                (c) controlling the high temperature treatment                group in the same light intensity, light time,                humidity and carbon dioxide concentration as in                step (b), controlling the temperature at                32-33degrees Celsius, and continuing culturing for                2-3 days, (d) applying the same amount and                concentration of trace liquid nitrogen fertilizer                to the high temperature treatment group and the                standard temperature control group, and continuing                performing alternate cultivation, (e) selecting the                high temperature treated seedlings with excellent                growth status and the seedling height not less than                that in the standard temperature control group for                large-scale experimental transplanting and                cultivation in the high temperature treatment                group, where the minimum temperature of the                cultivation environment at night is not less than                31degrees Celsius and the maximum temperature                during the day is 37-38degrees Celsius, (f)                applying liquid nitrogen fertilizer 4-5 days after                the rice seedlings turn green, (g) when the rice                seedlings enter the heading stage, again applying                nitrogen fertilizer, simultaneously removing the                rice grains whose grain height is obviously shorter                than the average level, and performing pest                management, (h) when the rice seedlings enter the                milk maturity stage, again applying nitrogen                fertilizer, removing the rice grains whose ear                length is significantly shorter than the average                ear length, and performing pest management, (i)                harvesting after the maturity stage, uprooting the                rice grains, collecting the ears of rice on the                upright and sturdy rice plants with well-developed                stems of the roots, then screening the ears to                screen out the full and large rice grain size and                (j) using the rice grains obtained in step (i) as a                donor, using Yangdao No. 6 as an acceptor,                hybridizing to obtain F1 generation, backcrossing                the F1 generation with the acceptor 4-6 times to                obtain the Fn generation, self-crossing the Fn                generation for more than 2 times, screening the                stable individual plants with good comprehensive                agronomic traits, and then self-crossing to obtain                heat-resistant varieties at the booting stage with                stable genetic traits.

PN CN113155577-A
TI Preparation method for identifying specific            protein acetylation modification samples, comprises            e.g. determining acetyltransferase of protein through            exogenous experiments, preparing protein samples, and            incubating.
AU WANG J
   PENG H
   NONG A
   HUANG Y
   GAO J
AE YOUJIANG MEDICAL COLLEGE NATIONALITIES (YJMC-C)
GA 2021916692
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method for identifying specific                protein acetylated modified sample comprises e.g.                (i) selecting protein A with acetylation                modification through endogenous experiments, and                determining the acetyltransferase B of protein A                through exogenous experiments, (ii) preparing                protein samples co-transfected with plasmid A and                plasmid B and protein samples transfected with                plasmid A separately by (iia) inoculating 293T                cells in at least 20 cell culture dishes, when the                cell density reaches 78-82%, using the transfection                reagent to transfect the plasmid A alone into the                cells of a cell culture dish, simultaneously using                the transfection reagent to co-transfect the                plasmid A and the plasmid B into the cells of the                remaining cell culture dishes, and (iib) placing                the transfected cell culture dish in the incubator                and incubating for 6 hours at a constant                temperature and a constant carbon dioxide                concentration, and removing the culture medium                containing the transfection reagent-DNA                complex.
   USE - The method is useful for identifying specific                protein acetylated modified sample (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The protein enhances the success rate of                identifying specific protein acetylation                modification sites by mass spectrometry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation method for identifying specific                protein acetylated modified sample, comprises (i)                selecting protein A with acetylation modification                through endogenous experiments, and determining the                acetyltransferase B of protein A through exogenous                experiments, (ii) preparing protein samples                co-transfected with plasmid A and plasmid B and                protein samples transfected with plasmid A                separately by (iia) inoculating 293T cells in at                least 20 cell culture dishes, when the cell density                reaches 78-82%, using the transfection reagent to                transfect the plasmid A alone into the cells of a                cell culture dish, simultaneously using the                transfection reagent to co-transfect the plasmid A                and the plasmid B into the cells of the remaining                cell culture dishes, (iib) placing the transfected                cell culture dish in the incubator and incubating                for 6 hours at a constant temperature and a                constant carbon dioxide concentration, removing the                culture medium containing the transfection                reagent-DNA complex, replacing with a new culture                medium and continuously culturing for 48 hours,                (iic) collecting the above-mentioned cultured cells                in a first centrifuge tube, centrifuging the cell                pellet, adding radio-immune precipitation assay                (RIPA) lysate to the first centrifuge tube, (iid)                placing the above-mentioned first centrifuge tube                on a vertical rotating shaker to lyse for 1 hour                and then centrifuging, and transferring the cell                supernatant obtained by centrifugation into a                second centrifuge tube, adding antibody to the                second centrifuge tube, and incubating overnight on                a vertical rotating shaker, (iie) adding agarose                beads to the second centrifuge tube the next day,                and continuously incubating overnight on a vertical                rotating shaker, (iif) on the third day, after                centrifuging the complex of                antigen-antibody-agarose beads in the second                centrifuge tube, washing it for 3 times with cold                RIPA lysis solution, and blotting the liquid after                the last wash, and (iig) adding twice the                concentration of loading buffer to the obtained                complex, boiling at 100 degrees C for 5-8 minutes,                centrifuging briefly again to obtain a single                protein sample transfected with plasmid A and at                least 19 protein samples co-transfected with                plasmid A and plasmid B, (iii) randomly extracting                a protein sample from the above protein samples                co-transfected with plasmid A and plasmid B, and                completing the western blot analysis together with                the protein sample transfected with plasmid A                alone, and (iv) running sodium dodecyl                sulfate-polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis                (SDS-PAGE) gel on the remaining protein samples                co-transfected with plasmid A and plasmid B,                incubating in Coomassie brilliant blue staining                solution overnight, on the next day, decolorizing                the gel on a shaker until the background of the gel                is white, when protein bands are in the swimming                lanes, especially the target bands, are blue and                stop bleaching, according to the molecular weight                of A, cutting the band of interest and placing a                third centrifuge tube, making a label, i.e., the                sample used to identify the specific protein                acetylation modification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (iia), the                  inoculation amount of the 293T cells is 5.2x 106                  cells per cell culture dish. In the step (iia),                  the transfection reagent is EZ Trans transfection                  reagent. Preferred Method: In the step (iib), the                  constant temperature is 37 degrees C, and the                  concentration of carbon dioxide is 5%. In the                  step (iia), when the cell density reaches 80%,                  using the transfection reagent to transfect the                  plasmid A alone into the cells of a cell culture                  dish, and simultaneously using the transfection                  reagent to co-transfect the plasmid A and plasmid                  B into the rest of the cell culture dish of                  cells. In the step (i), the exogenous experiment                  comprises in 293T cells, co-transfecting plasmid                  A and plasmid B, harvesting the cells for 48                  hours, subjecting to immunoprecipitation, and                  subjecting to immunoblotting analysis.

TI Mixture of polyurethane reaction components for foam, contains polyester polyol having preset hydroxyl number and number average functionality, polyether polyol formed using e.g. ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, and sucrose, and glycerin.
AU HAHN C
   NEFZGER H
   LORENZ K
   SOTTMANN T
   TSENG S
   LUELSDORF S
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   UNIV STUTTGART (STUT-C)
GA 2021698508
AB    NOVELTY - A mixture of polyurethane reaction components comprises (i) 0-50 wt.% polyester polyols with hydroxyl number of 200-450 mg potassium hydroxide/g, and number average functionality of 2.2-4.5, (ii) 10-60 wt.% polyether polyol with hydroxyl number of 350-550 mg potassium hydroxide/g, and number average functionality of 4-6, (iii) 0-10 wt.% polyether polyol with hydroxyl number of 350-550 mg potassium hydroxide/g, (iv) 30-60 wt.% polyester polyols with hydroxyl number of 250-350 mg potassium hydroxide/g, and number average functionality of 1.9-2.1, and (v) 0-30 wt.% glycerin. The polyester polyol (i) is obtained from reaction components of (a1) dicarboxylic acid chosen from succinic acid, glutaric acid, malonic acid, malic acid, and tartaric acid, (a2) dihydroxy compound chosen from monoethylene glycol, diethylene glycol and polyethylene glycol, and (a3) polyhydroxy compound chosen from glycerol, trimethylolpropane, pentaerythritol, and sorbitol.
   USE - Mixture of polyurethane reaction components used for producing polyurethane foams for thermal insulation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mixture of polyurethane reaction components has low carbon dioxide solubility, and stably produces polyurethane foams having excellent rigidity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A mixture of polyurethane reaction components comprises (i) 0-50 wt.% polyester polyols with hydroxyl number of 200-450 mg potassium hydroxide/g, number average functionality of 2.2-4.5, and an acid number of less than 6 mg potassium hydroxide/g, (ii) 10-60 wt.% polyether polyol with hydroxyl number of 350-550 mg potassium hydroxide/g, and number average functionality of 4-6, (iii) 0-10 wt.% polyether polyol with hydroxyl number of 350-550 mg potassium hydroxide/g, and number average functionality of 4-6, (iv) 30-60 wt.% polyester polyols with hydroxyl number of 250-350 mg potassium hydroxide/g, and number average functionality of 1.9-2.1, and (v) 0-30 wt.% glycerin. The polyester polyol (i) is obtained from reaction components of (a1) dicarboxylic acid chosen from succinic acid, glutaric acid, malonic acid, malic acid, and tartaric acid, (a2) dihydroxy compound chosen from monoethylene glycol, diethylene glycol and polyethylene glycol, and (a3) polyhydroxy compound chosen from glycerol, trimethylolpropane, pentaerythritol, and sorbitol. The polyether polyol (ii) is obtained from reaction components of (b1) 40-100 wt.% ethylene oxide and 0-60 wt.% propylene oxide, and (b2) starter chosen from o-toluenediamine, sucrose, monoethylene glycol, glycerol, sorbitol, trimethylolpropane, and propylene glycol. The component (iii) is obtained from reaction component comprising (b1) propylene oxide, and (b2) starter. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a polyurethane foam, which is obtainable from the reaction products comprising (A) organic polyisocyanate, (B) mixture of polyurethane reaction components, (C) catalyst(s), (D) surfactant(s), (E) supercritical carbon dioxide, (F) optionally auxiliary agent and/or additives, and (G) optionally physical blowing agents, and has ratio of isocyanate groups from component (A) to hydroxyl groups from component (B) is 0.9:1-1.1:1; and
   (2) production of foams, which involves mixing the isocyanate-reactive polyol formulation comprising components (B)-(F), (ii) mixing the obtained premix with the component (A), (iii) introducing the reactive polyurethane mixture obtained in the step (ii) into a closed mold at pressure of 73.7-350 bar and temperature of 30.9-100 degrees C, set such that the internal volume and/or the pressure prevailing inside are changed by external influences after the mixture is introduced, (iv) allowing the reactive polyurethane mixture obtained in the step (ii) to remain in the closed mold for a period of 0.1 second or more, (v) increasing the internal volume of the closed mold and/or reducing the pressure prevailing inside the closed mold by external influence to obtain a product, and (vi) demolding the product obtained in the step (v).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The component (i) preferably has hydroxyl number of 250-400 mg potassium hydroxide/g, number average functionality of 2.8-3.2, and acid number of less than 1 mg potassium hydroxide/g. The component (ii) preferably has hydroxyl number of 400-500 mg potassium hydroxide/g, and number average functionality of 4.5-5. Preferred Components: The component (i) is preferably obtained from reaction component comprising (a1) succinic acid, (a2) dihydroxy compound chosen from monoethylene glycol, diethylene glycol, and polyethylene glycol, and (a3) glycerin. The component (ii) is obtained from reaction component comprising (b1) 100 wt.% ethylene oxide and (b2) starter chosen from sucrose, monoethylene glycol, and propylene glycol. Preferred Composition: The mixture comprises 0.2-28 wt.%, preferably 0.4-20 wt.% glycerol, and less than 0.16, preferably less than 0.12 carbon dioxide at 35 degrees C, and pressure of 120 bar.

TI Reactive steel cylinder for aquatic plant tank,            includes cylinder body having reaction chamber with            carbon dioxide production mechanism, sliding rack with            air outlet control mechanism, motor, and left and right            symmetrical medicine boxes.
AU ZHANG X
AE FUZHOU YUXINGTONG INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY (FUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2021134153
AB    NOVELTY - A reactive steel cylinder includes a cylinder                body having feeding cavity, power cavity, pressure                control cavity, air outlet control cavity, and                reaction chamber with carbon dioxide (CO2)                production mechanism; a sliding rack provided with                air outlet control mechanism; left and right                symmetrical medicine boxes fixed at upper end of                reaction chamber; a filter core fixedly arranged                between left and right medicine boxes; a feeding                shaft rotatably provided at rear end of each                medicine box; a second motor fixed on rear end of                feeding cavity and rotatably provided with second                motor shaft at front end; a gear pump fixed at                front end of feeding cavity; a water pump pipe                connected to right end of gear pump and aquatic                plant tank; and a water inlet pipe connected                between left end of gear pump and rear end of                reaction chamber.
   USE - The reactive steel cylinder is used for                aquatic plant tank.
   ADVANTAGE - The steel cylinder is simple to operate and                has simple structure; can automatically produce CO2                by putting food-grade sodium bicarbonate and citric                acid in their respective boxes, and passes the CO2                into fish tank; and can automatically control CO2                concentration in the tank to ensure uniform                introduction of CO2 into the tank and improve CO2                utilization rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A reactive steel cylinder comprises cylinder                body provided with reaction chamber, feeding                cavity, power cavity, pressure control cavity, and                air outlet control cavity; and sliding rack                provided with air outlet control mechanism. The                reaction chamber is provided with CO2 production                mechanism. A first motor is fixedly provided at                lower end of power cavity. A first motor shaft is                rotatably provided at upper end of first motor and                is sequentially fixed with cam and rotating disk                from top to bottom. An inner gear ring is fixed on                inner side of rotating disk. A first fixing block                is fixed on rear end of reaction chamber. Left and                right symmetrical first sliding grooves are                arranged in first fixing block. A first sliding                block is slidably provided on each first sliding                groove. A first spring is connected between the                first sliding block and the outer end of first                sliding groove. Left and right symmetrical second                sliding grooves are provided at lower end of                reaction chamber. A second sliding block is                slidably provided on each second sliding groove. A                second spring is connected between the second                sliding block and the outer end of second sliding                groove. The cam can push the second sliding block                to move left and right. A brush is rotatably                arranged between first and second sliding blocks. A                first gear is fixed at lower end of brush and can                be engaged with inner gear ring. Left and right                symmetrical medicine boxes are fixed at upper end                of reaction chamber. A filter core is fixedly                arranged between left and right medicine boxes. A                feeding shaft is rotatably provided at rear end of                each medicine box. A feeding column is fixed on                front end of feeding shaft. A first belt pulley is                fixed on rear end of feeding column. A second motor                is fixed on rear end of feeding cavity. A second                motor shaft is rotatably provided at front end of                second motor and is fixed with motor belt pulley. A                gear pump is fixed at front end of feeding cavity.                The rear end of gear pump is rotatably connected to                the front end of second motor shaft. A first belt                is connected between left and right first belt                pulleys and the motor belt pulley. A water pump                pipe is connected to right end of gear pump and                aquatic plant tank. A water inlet pipe is connected                between left end of gear pump and rear end of                reaction chamber.

PN US2020154727-A1; WO2020102819-A1; US10834937-B2; CA3119468-A1; EP3879991-A1; JP2022507605-W; EP3879991-A4
TI Producing coffee fraction for food and beverage            applications, such as in formulation of coffee bars and            coffee spreads, involves cleaning and drying green            coffee beans, roasting in the absence of oxygen,            cooling and transporting.
AU CELLI G B
   DE MIRANDA L F R
   DE M L F R
AE WHOLE COFFEE CO LLC (WHOL-Non-standard)
   WHOLE COFFEE CO LLC (WHOL-Non-standard)
   WHOLE COFFEE CO LLC (WHOL-Non-standard)
GA 202043524B
AB    NOVELTY - Producing coffee fraction involves cleaning                and drying green coffee beans; roasting the cleaned                and dried green coffee beans to produce roasted                coffee beans via a roasting chamber, where the                cleaned and dried green coffee beans are roasted in                the absence of oxygen at a temperature of about                100-230 degrees C at a pressure of about 1-10 bar                for about 2-60 minutes; cooling and transporting                the roasted coffee beans via a two-stage vibratory                fluid bed cooler in the absence of oxygen, where                cooling the roasted coffee beans comprises spraying                the roasted coffee beans with a solution to quench                thermolysis reactions; cryogenically dry milling                the roasted coffee beans in the absence of oxygen                to a particle size of approximately 75-100 microns                at a temperature of about - 190 to 10 degrees C;                extracting fluids from the milled, roasted coffee                beans in the absence of oxygen via a supercritical                fluid extraction (SCFE) system to produce a coffee                product.
   USE - Method for producing a coffee fraction for                food and beverage applications, such as in                formulation of coffee bars, coffee powders, coffee                spreads, coffee chunks/morsels/chips, chocolate                bars, chocolate powders, chocolate spreads,                refrigerated or frozen desserts, sugar and/or                coffee baked goods, breakfast cereals, power bars,                etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to improve quality and                safety of consumption of food and beverages                prepared with the coffee beans, tea leaves,                cannabis, and other biomaterials, provides                shelf-life extension, enhances convenience of                preparation, improves yield extension, and extends                storage and utilization without the use of chemical                preservatives or refrigeration. The classification                and selection processes improve the overall quality                of the final roasted product by providing size and                shape-conformed coffee beans for ensuring uniform                and consistent roasting. The roasting process is                performed using off-flame roasting equipment that                allows for more controlled and uniform heating for                improving roasting precision and accuracy and                providing for improved flavor in the roasted                coffee.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing coffee fraction involves cleaning                and drying green coffee beans; roasting the cleaned                and dried green coffee beans to produce roasted                coffee beans via a roasting chamber, where the                cleaned and dried green coffee beans are roasted in                the absence of oxygen at a temperature of about                100-230 degrees C at a pressure of about 1-10 bar                for about 2-60 minutes; cooling and transporting                the roasted coffee beans via a two-stage vibratory                fluid bed cooler in the absence of oxygen, where                cooling the roasted coffee beans comprises spraying                the roasted coffee beans with a solution to quench                thermolysis reactions; cryogenically dry milling                the roasted coffee beans in the absence of oxygen                to a particle size of approximately 75-100 microns                at a temperature of about - 190 to 10 degrees C;                extracting fluids from the milled, roasted coffee                beans in the absence of oxygen via a supercritical                fluid extraction (SCFE) system to produce a coffee                product, where the SCFE system comprises two or                more extraction columns, each extraction column                configured to introduce supercritical liquid carbon                dioxide to a permeation column and each permeation                column is configured to introduce the supercritical                liquid carbon dioxide to the milled, roasted coffee                beans for causing separation and extraction of the                fluids from the milled roasted coffee beans; wet                milling the coffee product in the absence of oxygen                at a temperature below about 10 degrees C to                produce a coffee powder comprising particles of                less than about 40.0 microns; and mixing the coffee                powder with one or more oils and/or fats in the                absence of oxygen at a temperature of about 10-80                degrees C and at a pressure of about 1-5 bar to                produce a coffee fraction comprising the coffee                powder particles microencapsulated in the one or                more oils and/or fats.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart for biomaterial                mass manufacturing process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The cleaned and dried                  green coffee beans are roasted in the presence of                  an inert gas. The heating media comprises an                  inert gas. The solution comprises water and 1-60                  wt.% sugar. The inert gas is nitrogen.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN110550644-A; CN110550644-B
TI Separating and extracting battery lithium carbonate, rubidium and cesium salt from lithium mica comprises crushing lithium mica, roasting, performing high-temperature evaporation and concentration, removing impurities and acid extracting.
AU LI W
   ZHANG Z
   SHEN Y
   CAI W
AE HEBEI ZHUHE GROUP XINGLONG MINING CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 2019A5193N
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and extracting battery level lithium carbonate, rubidium and cesium salt from lithium mica comprises (a) crushing lithium mica and pre-treating, (b) adding concentrated sulfuric acid and water, performing acidification, neutralization and filtering, (c) adding water and dry silica sand, (d) roasting and discharging waste gas, (e) adding water, filtering and drying, (f) cooling mother liquor 1 and 2, (g) performing secondary crystallization treatment using secondary catalytic crystallizing agent, (h) adding alkali, separating gypsum solid residue and filtering, (i) adding impurity removing catalyst, filtering and neutralizing, (j) performing high-temperature evaporation and concentration treatment, (k) adding carbonate solution, performing lithium precipitation reaction, centrifuging, (l) adding crude lithium carbonate with water, introducing carbon dioxide gas, removing impurities, filtering and drying and (m) extracting and carrying out acid extraction.
   USE - The method is useful for Separating and extracting battery level lithium carbonate, rubidium and cesium salt from lithium mica.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical; improves the overall competitiveness of lithium mica extraction; has high equipment utilization rate; is environmentally-friendly; and has low production cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating and extracting battery level lithium carbonate, rubidium and cesium salt from lithium mica comprises using lithium mica, performing acidification roasting, leaching liquid circulation, using lithium mother liquid circulation to contol the concentration of potassium, sodium, rubidium, and cesium by evaporating, concentrating, crystallizing comprising (a) crushing lithium mica into a powder, sealing, pre-treating lithium mica powder, (b) adding concentrated sulfuric acid and water, mixing to obtain a lithium mica acid water mixture, heating to obtain mother liquor 1 of sulfate solution containing lithium, potassium, sodium, rubidium and cesium, performing acidification and cooling to obtain acidified leaching material, processing hydrogen fluoride, fluoride and acid vapor generated by the acidification reaction, treating the hydrogen fluoride and fluoride and acid steam and remaining acid by neutralization and pressure filtration, performing acidification leaching process for recovering and collecting and exporting the filter residue 1, (c) adding water to the acidified leaching material to obtain an acidified leaching mixture, adding dry silica sand, mixing, placing in a drying device to obtain a dry mix and discharging the water vapor and tail gas, (d) placing the dry mix in a roasting kiln, discharging the waste gas produced by roasting, (e) adding water to the roasting material, mixing the mixture with solid-liquid mixture, filtering and separating the aqueous solid-liquid mixed solution to mother liquor 2 and filter residue 2,washing and drying to obtain dry silica sand, (f) adding mother liquor 1 and 2 in a cooling crystallization device, filtering and separating, (g) adding secondary catalytic crystallizing agent, performing cooling and secondary crystallization treatment, filtering and separating to obtain mother liquor 4 and sodium-potassium alum, (h) adding alkali to neutralize and separate gypsum solid residue adding lime milk and hydrogen peroxide solution, gypsum solid residue and mother liquor 5, filtering and separating, (i) adding impurity removing catalyst, filtering and separating to obtain mother liquid 6, returning the gypsum solid residue for neutralization, (j) placing the mother liquor 6 in an MVR high-efficiency evaporator, performing high-temperature evaporation and concentration treatment, controlling the concentration of lithium ions in the evaporative concentrated solution, recovering condensed water, (k) adding carbonate solution into the concentrated mother liquor 7, performing lithium precipitation reaction, filtering and separating, washing the crude lithium carbonate using heated water, precipitating mother liquor 8, concentrating by evaporating to an lithium oxide, centrifuging to obtain a solid and a centrifuged liquid, drying the solid to an anhydrous sodium sulfate solid and centrifuging, performing secondary lithium precipitation process, (l) adding crude lithium carbonate with water, stirring and mixing, introducing carbon dioxide gas for dissolving, decomposing under the condition of heating, removing impurities, filtering and separating, drying the filter residue and (m) extracting the mother liquor 3, preparing a high-purity cesium salt and raffinat and carrying out acid extraction to prepare high-purity rubidium salt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Separating and extracting battery level lithium carbonate, rubidium and cesium salt from lithium mica comprises using lithium mica as raw material, performing acidification roasting, leaching liquid circulation, using lithium mother liquid circulation to contol the concentration of potassium, sodium, rubidium, and cesium in the corresponding mother liquid in each process by evaporating, concentrating, crystallizing comprising (a) crushing lithium mica into a powder, sealing after stacking, conveying powder or slurry to the reactor device for pre-treating lithium mica powder, (b) adding pre-treated lithium mica powder, concentrated sulfuric acid and water in a reaction kettle device, uniformly stirring and mixing to obtain a lithium mica acid water mixture, heating the steam at normal pressure for 3-6 hours to obtain mother liquor 1 of sulfate solution containing lithium, potassium, sodium, rubidium and cesium, performing acidification to produce hydrogen fluoride and fluoride, acid vapor and remaining acid and cooling the acid to obtain acidified leaching material, processing hydrogen fluoride, fluoride and acid vapor generated by the acidification reaction into an exhaust gas recovery processing system, treating the hydrogen fluoride and fluoride produced by the acidification reaction and acid steam and remaining acid by neutralization and pressure filtration to obtain a filtrate and filter residue 1, returning the filtrate to perform acidification leaching process for recovering and collecting and exporting the filter residue 1, (c) adding water to the acidified leaching material obtained in the step (b) to obtain an acidified leaching mixture, adding dry silica sand, mixing to obtain a solid-liquid mixture, placing in a drying device for drying to obtain a dry mix and discharging the water vapor and tail gas generated during drying after treating by tail gas recovery treatment system and spray treatment, (d) placing the dry mix in a roasting kiln for roasting at 300-900 degrees C to obtain a roasting material, discharging the waste gas produced by roasting into the exhaust gas recovery treatment system for treating up to the standard, (e) adding water to the roasting material obtained in the step (d), mixing the mixture with solid-liquid mixture, controlling the mass ratio of solid-liquid, roasted material and water to 1: 1.1-1.3, filtering and separating the aqueous solid-liquid mixed solution to mother liquor 2 and filter residue 2,washing the filter residue 2 and drying to obtain dry silica sand, (f) adding mother liquor 1 and 2 in a cooling crystallization device, cooling the circulating freezing brine under continuous stirring to cool the crystals to obtain a mixed solution of precipitated aluminum alum and aluminum cesium alum, filtering and separating the mixed solution of precipitated aluminum alum and aluminum cesium alum to obtain a mother liquor 3 containing rubidium cesium alum and a mixture of rubidium cesium and potassium alum, (g) adding secondary catalytic crystallizing agent to the mother liquor 3 in the step (f), performing cooling and secondary crystallization treatment to obtain a mixed solution of aluminum-sodium alum and aluminum potassium alum, filtering and separating to obtain mother liquor 4 and sodium-potassium alum, (h) adding alkali to neutralize and separate gypsum solid residue to the mother liquor 4, adding lime milk and hydrogen peroxide solution to obtain a mother liquor mixed solution, controlling the pH value of the mother liquor mixed solution to generate a precipitate, gypsum solid residue and mother liquor 5, filtering and separating, (i) adding impurity removing catalyst to the mother liquid 5 for removing impurity, filtering and separating the gypsum solid residue to obtain mother liquid 6, returning the gypsum solid residue for neutralization by adding raw materials, (j) placing the mother liquor 6 in an MVR high-efficiency evaporator, performing high-temperature evaporation and concentration treatment to obtain an evaporative concentrated solution, controlling the concentration of lithium ions in the evaporative concentrated solution at 14-33 g/l to obtain a concentrated mother liquor 7, allowing the liquid mother liquor 7 to enter the lithium sinking and washing process, recovering the condensed water produced by the high-temperature evaporation and concentration treatment as washing water with soda ash and lithium carbonate, (k) adding carbonate solution into the concentrated mother liquor 7, performing lithium precipitation reaction to obtain mother liquor 8, filtering and separating to obtain crude lithium carbonate and mother liquor 8, washing the crude lithium carbonate using heated water, precipitating the mother liquor 8, concentrating the mother liquor 8 by evaporating to an lithium oxide concentration of 15-30 g/l in the solution, centrifuging to obtain a solid and a centrifuged liquid, drying the solid to an anhydrous sodium sulfate solid and centrifuging, performing secondary lithium precipitation process to the centrifuged liquid to obtain industrial lithium carbonate as crude lithium carbonate, (l) adding crude lithium carbonate with water, stirring and mixing to obtain a crude lithium carbonate mixed aqueous solution, introducing carbon dioxide gas for dissolving, decomposing under the condition of heating, removing impurities, filtering and separating, drying the filter residue in a dryer to obtain a battery-grade lithium carbonate and recycling the filtrate and (m)extracting the mother liquor 3 containing rubidium cesium alum and a mixture of rubidium cesium and potassium alum, preparing a high-purity cesium salt and raffinat and carrying out acid extraction using raffinate as raw material to prepare high-purity rubidium salt. The step (b) comprises controlling the mass ratio of pretreated lithium mica powder, concentrated sulfuric acid and water to 1:0.8-1.05:0.8-1.1, controlling the steam heating reaction at 80-150 degrees C, cooling to 80-100 degrees C, performing neutralization by adding lime milk or lime milk made of gypsum slag to the remaining acid at a pH of 6-9. The step (c) comprises mixing, drying, controlling the solid-liquid ratio to 0.3:1-0.5:1, controlling the drying using rotary kiln device at 100-150 degrees C for 0.4-0.6 hours, controlling the water vapor generated during drying to enter the rotary kiln exhaust gas treatment system with the exhaust gas generated during the drying process, spraying 30-38 wt.% diluted sulfuric acid and water, performing acid leaching process, absorbing secondary processing and discharging after reaching the standard. The step (e) comprises performing water leaching reaction at 70-95 degrees C for 1-3 hours, returning the silica sand as raw material, mixing and drying. The step (f) comprises primary cooling and crystallizing at 10-65 degrees C for 3-6 hours. The step (g) comprises secondary cooling and crystallizing at -15 to -25 degrees C for 3-6 hours. The step (h) comprises adding alkali for neutralizing, separating gypsum slag by adding 30-35 wt.% lime milk to mother liquor 4 at -15 to 25 degrees C and adding 20-30 wt.% hydrogen peroxide solution. The step (i) comprises adding impurity removing agent to remove impurities and separating the gypsum slag. Preferred Components: The secondary catalytic crystallization agent is aminobutyric acid, hydrochloric acid, aqueous ammonia, calcium chloride, magnesium chloride, sodium chloride, sodium phosphate and sodium nitrate. The impurity removing agent is sulfuric acid, calcium chloride, magnesium chloride, mixed solution of sodium chloride and sodium phosphate solution. The gypsum slag is calcium oxide or calcium hydroxide. The step (k) comprises separating crude lithium carbonate, adding sodium carbonate or potassium carbonate solution to the concentrated mother liqour 7 and controlling the lithium precipitation reaction at 80-95 degrees C for 40-100 minutes.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PT P
PN CN110423680-A
TI Device useful for real-time gas measurement of            biological biogas and bio methane in anaerobic            fermentation comprises anaerobic fermentation tank            connected in sequence, first gas measuring device, and            second gas measurement device.
AU NIU Q
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 201996285M
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises anaerobic fermentation tank                connected in sequence, first gas measuring device,                gas absorption device and second gas measurement                device. The anaerobic fermentor is used to produce                biogas. The anaerobic fermentation tank is                connected to the first gas measuring device for                measuring biogas by a one-way intake valve through                the pipeline A. First one-way air outlet valve is                connected to the first gas measuring device. The                first one-way air outlet valve is connected to the                air inlet of the gas absorption device via the                intake pipe. The gas absorption device is used for                absorbing carbon dioxide, water vapor, ammonia gas,                hydrogen sulfide and other alkaline biogas in the                biogas. The gas outlet of the gas absorption device                is connected to the second gas measuring device for                measuring bio-methane via the pipeline B, second                one-way air outlet valve is connected outside the                second gas measuring device.
   USE - The device is useful for real-time gas                measurement of biological biogas and bio methane in                anaerobic fermentation.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: can adapt anti-corrosion gas                metering of different flow rates, improves                corrosion resistance, and has prolonged service                life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                measuring the device comprising anaerobic fermentor                fermentation continuously produces biogas, no need                to measure gas. The first three-way solenoid valve                opens first, and the second road closes, where the                pipeline A to the pipeline D are passages, and the                biogas is from the pipeline A through the pipeline                D. The first one-way outlet valve enters the gas                absorption bottle, carbon dioxide, water vapor,                ammonia, in biogas, in gas absorption bottles,                hydrogen sulfide and other alkaline gases are                absorbed by the absorption liquid. The remaining                bio-methane, the first path of the second three-way                solenoid valve is also open. The second road is                closed i.e. the pipeline B and the pipeline F are                passages. Bio-methane is collected through line F                through a second one-way outlet valve for                collection, when it is necessary to measure gas in                biogas. The first PLC digital display controller                controls the second way of opening the first                three-way solenoid valve. The first way is closed,                and the timer inside it starts timing, i.e. the                pipeline A to the pipeline C are passages. The                biogas enters the first anti-corrosion belt U-tube                from line A through line C, and after the gas is                introduced. The liquid level at both ends of the                first anti-corrosion taped U-tube changes due to                gas accumulation, triggering the first capacitance                level sensor to collect the liquid level                difference. The first PLC digital display                controller controls the first three-way solenoid                valve to be closed first. The second way is opened                at this time, and the timer in the first PLC                digital display controller stops counting and                implement a data acquisition. The gas production                rate of real-time biogas is obtained by liquid                level elevation and multiple data acquisition, when                it is necessary to measure gas for bio methane. The                second PLC digital display controller controls the                second way of opening the second three-way solenoid                valve. The first way is closed, and the timer                inside it starts timing, i.e. the pipe B to the                pipe E are passages. The biogas enters the second                corrosion-resistant graduated U-tube from line B                through line E the gas is introduced, due to the                accumulation of gas, the liquid level at both ends                of the second anti-corrosion taped U-tube changes,                triggering the second capacitance level sensor to                collect the liquid level difference. The second PLC                digital display controller controls the second                three-way solenoid valve to be closed first. The                second way is opened, at this time, the timer in                the second PLC digital display controller stops                counting, implement a data acquisition. The gas                production rate of real biogas is obtained by                liquid level elevation and multiple data                acquisition.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the device for real-time gas measurement of                biological biogas and bio methane in anaerobic                fermentation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The gas absorption                  device is sealed gas absorption bottle. The                  absorption liquid in the gas absorption bottle is                  a mixture of a phenolphthalein indicator and a                  saturated sodium hydroxide solution. The intake                  pipe extends into the interior of the absorption                  liquid, line B is located above the liquid level                  of the absorption bottle that absorbs the liquid                  used to absorb carbon dioxide from biogas, water                  vapor, ammonia, hydrogen sulfide and other biogas                  dissolved in alkaline. The remainder is                  bio-methane. The pipeline A, pipeline B, pipeline                  C, pipeline D, pipeline E and pipeline F are                  anti-corrosion pipelines, and the material is                  preferably fluoroplastic, polypropylene and/or                  polytetrafluoroethylene. The first casing and                  second casing are both fireproof and insulated                  casings. The material is preferably polycarbonate                  and/or acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene copolymer.                  The conductive liquid filled in the U-shaped tube                  of first anti-corrosion tape and U-shaped tube of                  second anti-corrosion tape are both potassium                  chloride solution.
DC A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); T01 (Digital Computers)
MC A12-E05; A12-E13; A12-H02; A12-W11L; D05-A04A; D05-C14; D05-H09; H09-F03; J01-E01A; J01-E02B; J04-C03; J04-C04A; T01-E02B; T01-F06
IP C12M-001/107; C12M-001/34; C12M-001/36
PD CN110423680-A   08 Nov 2019   C12M-001/36   201992   Pages: 10   Chinese
AD CN110423680-A    CN10686468    29 Jul 2019

CP CN110423680-A

PN CN110076247-A
TI Method for casting automobile stamping die, involves processing mold blank, processing blank through machining center, soaking processed mold blank and protecting oxidizing solution, followed by placing mold blank in heat treatment chamber.
AU TIAN N
   JIANG Y
   ZHANG L
   JIAO A
   LI H
   ZHANG W
AE YANGZHOU SHUANGJI MACHINERY CO LTD (YANG-Non-standard)
GA 2019695279
AB    NOVELTY - An automobile stamping die casting method involves processing a mold blank, processing the blank of the mold, soaking the processed mold blank, immersing the mold blank in the mold blank and protecting the oxidizing solution, adding an alkaline water with antioxidant, immersing the mold blank in the protective oxidizing solution, arranging the venting end at both ends of the immersion tank, soaking the mold blank in the protective oxidizing solution, placing the mold blank in the heat treatment chamber, providing a transverse heating resistance wire in the heat treatment chamber, arranging a temperature sensor, heating the mold blank, preparing the information strengthening treatment liquid, mixing quartz sand and rice bran, heating the deionized water, adding deionized water, quartz sand and rice bran mixture, adding water and stirring, utilizing manual mixing, artificially stirring with wooden mixing board and arranging on the outer surface of the wooden mixing board.
   USE - Method for casting automobile stamping die.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An automobile stamping die casting method involves processing a mold blank, processing the blank of the mold through the machining center, performing the machining, controlling the cutter head by numerical control programming, processing the mold blank by the program control, soaking the processed mold blank, and immersing the mold blank in the mold blank and protecting the oxidizing solution, where the protective oxidizing solution comprises deionized water, purified water, alkaline water, and antioxidant under the ratio of deionized water to purified water is 1:5, the alkaline water is underground alkaline water, adding an alkaline water with an antioxidant and the anti-oxidant content is 1-5 kg, immersing the mold blank in the protective oxidizing solution under the immersion time for 5-10 hours, placing the protective oxidizing liquid in the immersion tank, arranging the venting end at both ends of the immersion tank, providing the rotating end with rotating blades, providing a gas delivery channel inside the gas-filling end, and the gas-injecting end releases gas under the gas contains carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and oxygen, and the ratio of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and oxygen is 1:1:3, soaking the mold blank in the protective oxidizing solution is heated, placing the mold blank in the heat treatment chamber, providing a transverse heating resistance wire in the heat treatment chamber, providing a holder between the lateral heating resistance wires, and providing the holder end with a gripping gripper and the gripper holds the mold blank, and maintaining the temperature in the heat treatment chamber at 80-120 degrees C during the heat treatment, arranging a temperature sensor around the heat treatment chamber and adjusting the temperature sensor to the heating under the power of resistance wire, heating the mold blank by subjecting the heat treatment chamber to strengthening treatment on the outer surface of the mold, preparing the information strengthening treatment liquid in the process of strengthening treatment, where the strengthening treatment liquid comprises quartz sand, rice bran, deionized water, binder, mixing the quartz sand and the rice bran, heating the deionized water and after heating, adding deionized water, adding quartz sand and rice bran mixture for five times, adding 10 kg for the first time and stirring, heating 5 kg for second time, stirring and standing, adding 5 kg for third time, allowing to stand, heating 2 kg for the fourth time, performing secondary heating, heating with hot air, blowing hot air to the outer surface of the quartz sand and rice bran mixture for a duration for 1-2 hours, after the end of hot air, adding 20 kg water for the fifth time and stirring, utilizing manual mixing, artificially stirring with wooden mixing board under the wooden mixing board is made of oak mixing board and arranging on the outer surface of the wooden mixing board of 1-2 mm fine teeth, and arranging transverse fine fiber filaments between the fine teeth.

PN CN109879297-A; CN109879297-B
TI Recovering product from mother liquid containing            ammonium chloride, sodium chloride, ammonium hydrogen            carbonate or sodium hydrogen carbonate by recovering            gas generated after deamination of mother liquid, and            recovering mother liquid.
AU LI Y
   XU B
   GAO C
   TENG L
AE HUNAN YUHUA TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2019556657
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering product from mother liquid                containing ammonium chloride, sodium chloride,                ammonium hydrogen carbonate or sodium hydrogen                carbonate involves recovering gas generated after                deamination of mother liquid, and recovering mother                liquid after deamination and obtaining ammonium                chloride and sodium chloride. Recovering gas                generated after deamination of mother liquid                involves pumping mother liquid containing ammonium                chloride, sodium chloride, ammonium hydrogen                carbonate or sodium hydrogen carbonate into                first-stage condensed water heat exchanger of                original mother liquid, heating mother liquid by                V-effect evaporating condensed water, entering                second-stage condensed water heat exchanger of                original mother liquid, heating mother liquid by                I-effect evaporation condensed water, entering into                third-stage heat exchanger of original mother                liquid, heating mother liquid, entering ammonia                steaming tower, and controlling temperature of                lower portion of ammonia steaming tower.
   USE - The method is used for recovering product from                mother liquid containing ammonium chloride, sodium                chloride, ammonium hydrogen carbonate or sodium                hydrogen carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can simultaneously recover ammonium                chloride and sodium chloride, has extremely high                recovery rate, and improves heat utilization                rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering product from mother liquid                containing ammonium chloride, sodium chloride,                ammonium hydrogen carbonate or sodium hydrogen                carbonate involves recovering gas generated after                deamination of mother liquid, and recovering mother                liquid after deamination and obtaining ammonium                chloride and sodium chloride. Recovering gas                generated after deamination of mother liquid                involves pumping mother liquid containing ammonium                chloride, sodium chloride, ammonium hydrogen                carbonate or sodium hydrogen carbonate into                first-stage condensed water heat exchanger of                original mother liquid, heating mother liquid to                35-40 degrees C by V-effect evaporating condensed                water, entering second-stage condensed water heat                exchanger of original mother liquid, heating mother                liquid to 55-60 degrees C by I-effect evaporation                condensed water, entering into third-stage heat                exchanger of original mother liquid, heating mother                liquid to 65-70 degrees C, entering ammonia                steaming tower, and controlling temperature of                lower portion of ammonia steaming tower to 105-120                degrees C. An ammonium bicarbonate is prepared                using mixture of cooled ammonia and carbon dioxide                produced during deamination process by entering                mixed gas of ammonia and carbon dioxide into                ammonia absorbing tower, absorbing by water to                generate carbon-containing ammonia water, adding                carbon-containing ammonia water into carbonizing                tower, sending into carbonizing tower, pressurizing                by compressor, generating ammonium hydrogen                carbonate crystal, concentrating reaction liquid                from carbonizing tower pressure into thickener,                entering thickening bottom liquid into centrifuge,                solid-liquid separating, and drying to obtain                food-grade ammonium bicarbonate product. Recovering                mother liquid after deamination and obtaining                ammonium chloride and sodium chloride                involves:
   (A) pumping mother liquid after deamination                from ammonia tower to II-effect evaporation tank,                heating and evaporating, controlling solution                temperature to 102-108 degrees C, discharging                mother liquid into III-effect evaporation tank,                evaporating, controlling solution temperature to                80-86 degrees C, discharging mother liquid into                IV-effect evaporation tank, evaporating,                controlling solution temperature to 64-70 degrees                C, discharging mother liquid into V-effect                evaporation tank, evaporating, controlling solution                temperature to 45-50 degrees C, pumping mother                liquid and precipitated crystals into I-effect                evaporation tank, heating and evaporating,                controlling solution temperature to 120-129 degrees                C, washing and discharging sodium chloride crystals                precipitated in I-effect evaporation, thickening by                salt thickener, dehydrating into centrifuge, and                centrifugally dehydrating to obtain product;
   (B) discharging concentrated concentrated                liquid of sodium chloride crystals in I-effect                evaporation tank to I-effect emitting tank,                flashing and concentrating, discharging mother                liquid into II-effect emitting tank downstream,                flashing and concentrating, discharging mother                liquid into III-effect emitting tank downstream,                flashing crystals, discharging mother liquid and                crystal to IV-flash crystallizing tank, flashing                crystals, pumping ammonium chloride crystal to                ammonium thickener, dehydrating ammonium thickener                bottom liquid by centrifuge to obtain wet ammonium                chloride, and drying ammonium chloride product to                obtain dry ammonium chloride product; and
   (C) sending salt thickener overflow liquid and                salt solution to salt solution barrel, pumping into                V-effect evaporation tank, entering ammonium                thickener overflow liquid and ammonium centrifuge                solution into ammonium solution barrel, and pumping                into IV-flash crystallizing tank.

PN CN109692152-A
TI Graphene-halloysite composite aerogelmaterial comprises graphene-halloysite, where the average specific surface area of the graphene-halloysite composite aerogel materialis, and both the nanoscale andmicro-scale pores.
AU LI H
   SUN C
   LIU H
   WEI D
   WANG J
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 201941954S
AB    NOVELTY - Graphene-halloysite composite aerogelmaterial comprises graphene-halloysite, where the average specific surface area of the graphene-halloysite composite aerogel materialis 600-608 m2g-1, and both the nanoscale andmicro-scale pores exist in the graphene-halloysite composite aerogel material. The nanoscale pores have an average of 10-12 nm, and the micro-scalepores have an average of 20-22 m. The preparation method includes adding 0.5-15 pts. wt. halloysite nanotubes to a mixed liquid of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, and uniformly dispersing ultrasonically to obtain an halloysite dispersion, adding 0.5-15 pts. wt. sodiump-styrenesulfonate, 0.05-2 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.5 pts. wt. initiator, 0.05-10 pts. wt. dexamethasone to a mixed liquid of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, and after stirring uniformly, adding the solution to the halloysite dispersion. The ultrasonic dispersion is uniform, and the vacuum is maintained.
   USE - Graphene-halloysite composite aerogelmaterial.
   ADVANTAGE - The graphene-halloysite composite aerogelmaterial has increases releaseing effect, increase soaking time, achieves effect of taking the medicine once and administering the medicine multiple times.The compostion has enviornmental friendly effect
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Graphene-halloysite composite aerogelmaterial comprises graphene-halloysite, where the average specific surface area of the graphene-halloysite composite aerogel materialis 600-608 m2g-1, and both the nanoscale andmicro-scale pores exist in the graphene-halloysite composite aerogel material. The nanoscale pores have an average of 10-12 nm, and the micro-scalepores have an average of 20-22 m. The preparation method includes adding 0.5-15 pts. wt. halloysite nanotubes to a mixed liquid of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, and uniformly dispersing ultrasonically to obtain an halloysite dispersion, adding 0.5-15 pts. wt. sodiump-styrenesulfonate, 0.05-2 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.5 pts. wt. initiator, 0.05-10 pts. wt. dexamethasone to a mixed liquid of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, and after stirring uniformly, adding the solution to the halloysite dispersion. The ultrasonic dispersion is uniform, the vacuum is maintained, the vacuum is maintained, and then the pressure is returned to normal pressure. After the vacuuming is repeated three times, the product is washed, dispersed in 100 pts. wt. water, heating by water bath to 70-80 degrees C to initiate polymerization, the polymerization time is 50 hours, washing and dispersing to 100 pts. wt. water to obtain the dispersion. The polydivinylsiloxane has a number average molecular weight of 500-5000, preferably 1000-3000, and a vinyl content mole percentage (ie, the ratio of the number of vinyl moles to the number of moles of the monoamino-terminated polydimethyldivinylsiloxane) a 0.1-5% amino mono-terminated polydimethyldivinylsiloxane or an amino-terminated polydimethyldivinylsiloxane. The 0.5-10 pts. wt. concentrated sulfuric acid and 0.1-15 pts. wt. graphite powder is mix, adding 0.5-15 pts. wt. potassium permanganate, placing the above mixture in an ice water bath, stirring for 12 hours, and adding 50-250 weight. The water is uniformly stirred, 350-550 pts. wt. water and 8-55 pts. wt. hydrogen peroxide are added to the above solution, stirred uniformly, and the product is washed until near neutral, and 100 pts. wt. water is added to the product, and the ultrasonic dispersion is uniform to obtaining the dispersion. The dispersions is mixed, added 0.01-2 pts. wt. initiator, 0.01-10 pts. wt. lovastatin, stirring uniformly, and then raising the temperature to 70-80 degrees C to initiate polymerization reaction for 60 hours, 0.5-15 pts. wt. ascorbic acid is added to the above reaction solution, and the reaction is continued for 10-40 hours. The product is placed in a carbon dioxide supercritical high pressure extraction apparatus, and supercritical drying is carried out at a temperature of 30-60 degrees C and a pressure of 5-15 megapascal for 1 hour with carbon dioxide as a medium to obtain a graphene-allotite composite aerogel material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Component: The initiator is dibenzoyl peroxide or azobisisobutyronitrile. Preferred Conditions: The method for preparing graphene-halloysite composite aerogelmaterial includes adding 1-10 pts. wt. halloysite nanotubes are added to a mixed solution of water and ethanol for ultrasonic dispersion for 1 hour, 1-10 pts. wt. sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.1-1 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.1 pts. wt. initiator, 0.1-5 pts. wt. dexamethasone added to a mixed solution of water and ethanol, stirred for 10-60 minutes. The above solution was added to the halloysite dispersion, ultrasonically dispersed for 30 minutes, vacuumed and kept at a vacuum for 1 hours, and prepolymerized at a temperature of 70-80 degrees C for 30-60 minutes during the polymerization in a constant temperature water bath at 40-60 degrees C for 12-24 hours, and then it is sequentially polymerized in a constant temperature water bath at 80-90 degrees C, and 100 degrees C for 2-8 hours. The method for preparing graphene-halloysite composite aerogelmaterial includes 1-5 pts. wt. concentrated sulfuric acid and 0.5-10 pts. wt. graphite powder are mixed, and 1-10 pts. wt. potassium permanganate is added, and the mixture is placed, stirred in an ice water bath and maintain the system temperature at 1-3 degrees C during the stirring process for 1-10 hours. The 100-200 pts. wt. water is added, stirred for 1-4 hours, and after cool to room temperature, 400-500 pts. wt. water and 10-50 pts. wt. hydrogen peroxide are added to the above solution, and stirred for 1-6 hours. The method for preparing graphene-halloysite composite aerogelmaterial includes 0.01-1 pts. wt. initiator, 0.05-5 pts. wt. lovastatin is added to the mixed dispersion, and the mixture is stirred for 1-10 hours and then placed at 70 degrees C, prepolymerization at -80 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, it is polymerized in a constant temperature water bath at 40-60 degrees C for 12-24 hours, followed by a constant temperature water bath at 80-100 degrees C for 2-8 hours. The mixture is placed in a hydrothermal kettle at 80-200 degrees C for 1-10 hours, and then 1-10 pts. wt. ascorbic acid is added thereto, and the reaction temperature is controlled at 20-100 degrees C, and the supercritical drying condition at a temperature of 33-55 degrees C and a pressure of 7-10 megapascal for 2-4 hours, preferably 3 hours.

PT P
PN CN108728416-A
TI Culturing chimeric antigen receptor T cells, by adding diluted blood sample to centrifuge tube containing human lymphocyte separation solution, layering, culturing, collecting suspended cells, performing cell transport and preserving cells.
AU SUI C
AE SHENYANG MEIDABO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201890677D
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T cells, by (a) separating mononuclear cells, adding diluted blood sample to centrifuge tube containing human lymphocyte separation solution, layering red blood cell layer, granulocyte layer, Ficoll (RTM: Neutral, highly branched, high-mass, hydrophilic polysaccharide)layer, single nuclear cell layer and a plasma layer, carefully pipetting plasma layer from white film layer, and all liquid above red blood cell layer into centrifuge tube and transferring to sterility testing, (b) culturing, collecting all suspended cells, adding cluster of differentiation (CD)3/CD28 magnetic beads, adding interleukin (IL)-2, incubating, adding virus, culturing, inoculating in cell count supplement medium, culturing, centrifuging, resuspending in saline, taking small amount of cell suspension for number of cells, cell viability, Gram test, and endotoxin testing, adding human albumin for injection, performing cell transport and preserving cells.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing CAR T cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for culturing chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T cells, involves (a) sterilizing surface of biosafety cabinet using 75% alcohol, opening air purifying conditioner for 30 minutes, stably running biosafety cabinet, removing cell culture medium from reagent compartment refrigerator, returning to room temperature, sterilizing peripheral blood bag and transferring to biosafety cabinet, (b) separating mononuclear cells, using sterile scissors or disposable syringe to cut blood bag plastic tube, transferring blood inside and outside blood bag into 50 ml centrifuge tube, diluting peripheral blood using phosphate buffered saline in a ratio of 1:1, mixing, slowly adding diluted blood sample into centrifuge tube containing 15 ml human lymphocyte separation solution at room temperature, performing centrifugation at 2100 revolutions per minute (rpm) and 1200 g for 20 minutes, clearly layering red blood cell layer, granulocyte layer, Ficoll (RTM: Neutral, highly branched, high-mass, hydrophilic polysaccharide)layer, single nuclear cell layer and a plasma layer, carefully pipetting plasma layer to a distance of 5 mm from white film layer, and all liquid above red blood cell layer into centrifuge tube, diluting using phosphate buffered saline (PBS), where the volume ratio of cell suspension is greater than 1:3, mixing, centrifuging at 1600 rpm and 600 g for 5 minutes, resuspending the cells in PBS, mixing, taking small number of cells, centrifuging at 1200 rpm and 300 g for 5 minutes, recovering supernatant and transferring to sterility testing, (c) culturing at 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide and humidity of 95%, adding to culture bottle according to cell count density of 2x 106 cells/ml, mixing in carbon dioxide incubator for 2 hours, shaking, tilting culture bottle at an angle of 30 degrees , pipetting into centrifuge tube, collecting all suspended cells, adjusting cell concentration to 1.2x 106 cells/ml, adding cluster of differentiation (CD)3/CD28 magnetic beads, according to ratio of cells to magnetic beads of 1:3, adding 100 U/ml interleukin (IL)-2, placing in CO2 incubator, adjusting cell density to 0.6x 106 cells/ml, adding virus, mixing uniformly in a CO2 incubator, culturing for 3 days, culturing for 5 days to remove magnetic beads, inoculating in cell count supplement medium, adjusting cell density to 0.6x 106 cells/ml with 100 U/ml IL-2, regularly observing cell growth, removing cell debris and impurities, and adding liquid culture every 2-3 days, according to state of cells and clinical treatment, the number of cells reached requirement when cultured for 9 days, and (d) according to treatment plan, taking corresponding cell suspension, culturing in remaining cell supplement medium, transferring to a centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 300 g for 5 minutes, removing supernatant, mixing with PBS, centrifuging at 300 g for 5 minutes, removing supernatant, centrifuging at 300 g/minute for 5 minutes, resuspending the cells in 20 ml saline, taking small amount of cell suspension for number of cells, cell viability, Gram test, and endotoxin testing, transferring to 100 ml saline bag, adding 5% human albumin for injection, filling in relevant records, performing cell transport and preserving cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The cell transport temperature is controlled at 4 degrees C, where transport personnel avoids distance shocks during transportation and delivered within specified time.
