PT P
PN CN108184976-A
TI Preparing low-salt low-nitrite sauced beef            involves choosing beef, cleaning and decontaminating,            trimming to right size to obtain beef pieces, weighing            certain amount of spices, washing, drying and crushing            to obtain spice powder.
AU MAI Y
   HUANGFU Y
   HAO X
   XU F
   SUN S
   MA L
   GAO X
   FU L
   ZHANG Y
   SHEN X
   LIU P
AE HENAN YISAI BEEF CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 201850497R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low-salt low-nitrite sauced beef                involves choosing beef, cleaning and                decontaminating, trimming to the right size to                obtain beef pieces. A certain amount of spices                according to need are weighed, washed and dried,                and crushed to a particle size of 90-110 mesh to                obtain a spice powder. The spice powder is placed                in a container, and the extraction of the essential                oil is carried out using a supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction device. The extraction                temperature is 40 to 50 degrees C, the extraction                pressure is 20 to 30 MPa, and the extraction time                is 1 to 2 hours. The spice essential oil is                prepared by adding essential oil with distilled                water, emulsifier is used to homogenize to form a                concentration of 5 to 7 wt.% of the essential oil                emulsion. A certain amount of essential oil                emulsion is added to the compound salt, Monascus                pigment, sodium nitrite, nisin, ascorbic                acid.
   USE - Method for preparing low-salt low-nitrite                sauced beef.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare low-salt                low-nitrite sauced beef, which has reduced sodium                chloride content by more than 20% by using a                composite salt instead of the use of pure sodium                chloride in beef sauce, reduced amount of nitrite                added by 30%, prevents the formation of                nitrosamines, further reducing the toxicity of                nitrite, has controlled flavor and extended                preservation time and has improved production                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing low-salt low-nitrite sauced beef                involves choosing beef, cleaning and                decontaminating, trimming to the right size to                obtain beef pieces. A certain amount of spices                according to need are weighed, washed and dried,                and crushed to a particle size of 90-110 mesh to                obtain a spice powder. The spice powder is placed                in a container, and the extraction of the essential                oil is carried out using a supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction device. The extraction                temperature is 40 to 50 degrees C, the extraction                pressure is 20 to 30 MPa, and the extraction time                is 1 to 2 hours. The spice essential oil is                prepared by adding essential oil with distilled                water, emulsifier is used to homogenize to form a                concentration of 5 to 7 wt.% of the essential oil                emulsion. A certain amount of essential oil                emulsion is added to the compound salt, Monascus                pigment, sodium nitrite, nisin, ascorbic acid. The                compound salt is added in an amount of 9 to 10 wt.%                of the essential oil emulsion, and the amount of                sodium nitrite added is 0.020 to 0.025 wt.% of the                mass of the essential oil emulsion. The amount of                nisin added is 0.02 to 0.03 wt.% of the mass of the                essential oil emulsion, and the amount of ascorbic                acid added is 0.2 wt.% to 0.4 wt.% of the mass of                the essential oil emulsion, which are mixed to                obtain spice essential oil. The spice essential oil                is added into the beef block with an injection                machine, the injection rate is 15 to 17 wt.% of the                mass of the beef block. The injected beef pieces                are placed in a vacuum tumbler, the rolling                temperature is 0 to 4 degrees C, the rolling vacuum                is -0.08 MPa, and the continuous rolling is 4 to 6                hours. The rolled beef pieces are fed into an                environment at a temperature of 0 to 4 degrees C                and allowed to stand for more than 12 hours. The                cured beef block is placed in a high-temperature                and high-pressure cooking pot, the heat                preservation pressure is 0.1-0.3 MPa, the                temperature is 100-110 degrees C, and the dwell                time is determined according to product                specifications. The temperature of the beef block                is reduced to 40 degrees C. The beef pieces are                cooled down to 4 degrees C or less. The cooked beef                pieces obtained are dispensed using a vacuum bag                and evacuated.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The composite salt is                  a mixture of 65 to 75 wt.% sodium chloride, 20 to                  30 wt.% potassium chloride, and 3 to 5 wt.%                  calcium chloride. Preferred Condition: The spice                  uses a variety of edible spices.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D02-A03B; D03-A01; D03-H01N; D03-H01T2B; D03-H01T5; D03-H02B2

PT P
PN US2021246420-A1
TI Isolating pluripotent human umbilical cord perivascular cells for treating e.g. cardiovascular injury, involves collecting umbilical cord from fetal tissue, collecting fetal placenta tissue, treating and washing umbilical cord with phosphate buffered saline, incubating and mixing cells with medium.
AU XIAO R
   YIE S
   LIBRACH C L
AE REPROBIOGEN INC (REPR-Non-standard)
   XIAO R (XIAO-Individual)
   YIE S (YIES-Individual)
GA 2021933570
AB    NOVELTY - Method (M1) of isolating pluripotent human umbilical cord perivascular cells for treating cardiovascular injury or heart disease, where the isolated perivascular cells are reprogrammed as pluripotent cells for differentiation into cardiomyocytes expressing cardiac troponin (cTn)I, where the pluripotent human umbilical cord perivascular cells are obtained by collecting an umbilical cord from fetal tissue, collecting fetal placenta tissue, and separating the umbilical cord from the fetal placenta tissue, treating the umbilical cord, washing the umbilical cord with phosphate buffered saline (PBS), cutting the umbilical cord, treating with collagenase, and washing the cells with PBS, and incubating the isolated perivascular cells and maintaining the isolated perivascular cells, adding trypsin-EDTA, and gene reprogramming factor, harvesting the isolated perivascular cells into a tube, separating and mixing the isolated perivascular cells with new maintenance medium.
   USE - The method is useful for (a) isolating pluripotent human umbilical cord perivascular cells for treating cardiovascular injury or heart disease, (b) isolating pluripotent human umbilical cord perivascular cells for screening drug candidate to treat cardiovascular injury or heart disease, or (c) isolating pluripotent human umbilical cord perivascular cells for preparing a cell-free composition for treating an inflammatory condition (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method (M1) of isolating pluripotent human umbilical cord perivascular cells for treating cardiovascular injury or heart disease, where the isolated perivascular cells are reprogrammed as pluripotent cells for differentiation into cardiomyocytes expressing cardiac troponin (cTn)I, where the pluripotent human umbilical cord perivascular cells are obtained by collecting an umbilical cord from fetal tissue obtained at less than 13 weeks of gestation, by collecting fetal placenta tissue by surgical aspiration, and separating the umbilical cord from the fetal placenta tissue, treating the umbilical cord to obtain isolated perivascular cells, by washing the umbilical cord with phosphate buffered saline (PBS), cutting the umbilical cord comprising an artery and two vessels into smaller cut pieces, treating the cut pieces with collagenase to obtain isolated perivascular cells, and washing the isolated perivascular cells with PBS, and incubating the isolated perivascular cells, by suspending the isolated perivascular cells in a maintenance medium comprising alpha-minimum essential medium (MEM), penicillin-streptomycin, and amphotericin, and maintaining the isolated perivascular cells by washing the isolated perivascular cells with PBS, adding trypsin-EDTA, adding at least one gene reprogramming factor chosen from octamer-binding transcription factor 4 (OCT-4), Kruppel like factor 4 (KLF-4), sex determining region Y (SRY)-box transcription factor 2 (SOX-2), GLIS family zinc finger 1 (GLIS1), and c-MYC, harvesting the isolated perivascular cells into a tube containing maintenance medium, separating the isolated perivascular cells from the maintenance medium, mixing the isolated perivascular cells with new maintenance medium, diluting the new maintenance medium containing the isolated perivascular cells with additional maintenance medium to obtain a diluted maintenance medium containing the isolated perivascular cells, maintaining the diluted maintenance medium containing the isolated perivascular cells under appropriate growth conditions of 37degreesC, 5% carbon dioxide in a maintenance medium, and changing the maintenance medium every 3-7 days, and suspending the maintained cells, where the umbilical cord is 0.5-2 cm in length and comprises an artery before cutting the umbilical cord into smaller cut pieces, where the isolated perivascular cells express one or more transcription factor associated with undifferentiated stem cells, and the transcription factor is OCT-4, SOX-2, or Nanog.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (a) a method (M2) of isolating pluripotent human umbilical cord perivascular cells for screening drug candidate to treat cardiovascular injury or heart disease; and
   (b) a method (M3) of isolating pluripotent human umbilical cord perivascular cells for preparing a cell-free composition for treating an inflammatory condition, where the isolated perivascular cells are differentiated into, or reprogrammed as pluripotent cells for differentiation into a homogeneous perivascular cell population with generational stability, where the pluripotent human umbilical cord perivascular cells are obtained by collecting an umbilical cord from fetal tissue obtained at less than 13 weeks of gestation, by collecting fetal placenta tissue by surgical aspiration, and separating the umbilical cord from the fetal placenta tissue, treating the umbilical cord to obtain isolated perivascular cells, by washing the umbilical cord with PBS, cutting the umbilical cord comprising an artery and two vessels into smaller cut pieces, treating the cut pieces with collagenase to obtain isolated perivascular cells, and washing the isolated perivascular cells with PBS, and incubating the isolated perivascular cells, by suspending the isolated perivascular cells in a maintenance medium comprising alpha-MEM, penicillin-streptomycin, and amphotericin, and maintaining the isolated perivascular cells by washing the isolated perivascular cells with PBS, adding trypsin-EDTA, optionally adding at least one gene reprogramming factor chosen from OCT-4, KLF-4, SOX-2, GLIS1, and c-MYC, harvesting the isolated perivascular cells into a tube containing maintenance medium, separating the isolated perivascular cells from the maintenance medium, mixing the isolated perivascular cells with new maintenance medium, diluting the new maintenance medium containing the isolated perivascular cells with additional maintenance medium to obtain a diluted maintenance medium containing the isolated perivascular cells, maintaining the diluted maintenance medium containing the isolated perivascular cells under appropriate growth conditions of 37degreesC, 5% carbon dioxide in a maintenance medium, and changing the maintenance medium every 3-7 days, and suspending the maintained cells, and preparing the cell-free composition from the maintenance medium comprising secretions of the isolated perivascular cells, where the umbilical cord is from 0.5-2 cm in length and comprises an artery before cutting the umbilical cord into smaller cut pieces, where the isolated perivascular cells express one or more transcription factor associated with undifferentiated stem cells, and the transcription factor is OCT-4, SOX-2, or Nanog.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method (M2) of isolating pluripotent human umbilical cord perivascular cells for screening drug candidate to treat cardiovascular injury or heart disease, where the isolated perivascular cells differentiate into cardiomyocytes expressing cTn1, where the pluripotent human umbilical cord perivascular cells are obtained by collecting an umbilical cord from fetal tissue obtained at less than 13 weeks of gestation, by collecting fetal placenta tissue by surgical aspiration, and separating the umbilical cord from the fetal placenta tissue, treating the umbilical cord to obtain isolated perivascular cells by washing the umbilical cord with PBS, cutting the umbilical cord comprising an artery and two vessels into smaller cut pieces, treating the cut pieces with collagenase to obtain isolated perivascular cells, and washing the isolated perivascular cells with PBS, and incubating the isolated perivascular cells, by suspending the isolated perivascular cells in a maintenance medium comprising alpha-MEM, penicillin-streptomycin, and amphotericin, and maintaining the isolated perivascular cells by washing the isolated perivascular cells with PBS, adding trypsin-EDTA, harvesting the isolated perivascular cells into a tube containing maintenance medium, separating the isolated perivascular cells from the maintenance medium, mixing the isolated perivascular cells with new maintenance medium, diluting the new maintenance medium containing the isolated perivascular cells with additional maintenance medium to obtain a diluted maintenance medium containing the isolated perivascular cells, maintaining the diluted maintenance medium containing the isolated perivascular cells under appropriate growth conditions of 37degreesC, 5% carbon dioxide in a maintenance medium, and changing the maintenance medium every 3-7 days, and suspending the maintained cells, where the umbilical cord is 0.5-2 cm in length and comprises an artery before cutting the umbilical cord into smaller cut pieces, where the isolated perivascular cells express one or more transcription factor associated with undifferentiated stem cells, and the transcription factor is OCT-4, SOX-2, or Nanog, where efficacy of the drug candidate is evaluated upon exposure of the isolated perivascular cells. The step of maintaining the isolated perivascular cells additionally involves freezing the isolated perivascular cells, and thawing and restoring the isolated perivascular cells to viability. The step of freezing the isolated perivascular cells involves washing the isolated perivascular cells with PBS, adding trypsin-EDTA, harvesting the isolated perivascular cells into a tube containing maintenance medium, separating the isolated perivascular cells from the maintenance medium, cooling the isolated perivascular cells to 4degreesC, mixing the isolated perivascular cells with a freezing medium at 4degreesC, where the freezing medium comprises dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO), transferring the freezing medium containing the isolated perivascular cells to vials pre-chilled to -70degreesC, storing the vials at -70degreesC for 24 hours, and storing the vials in liquid nitrogen. The step of thawing and restoring the isolated perivascular cells involves warming the vials to 37degreesC, separating the isolated perivascular cells from the freezing medium, mixing the isolated perivascular cells with maintenance medium, maintaining the maintenance medium containing the isolated perivascular cells under appropriate growth conditions of 37degreesC, 5% carbon dioxide for 24 hours, replacing the maintenance medium with new maintenance medium, and maintaining the new maintenance medium containing the material under appropriate growth conditions, the conditions being 37degreesC, 5% carbon dioxide and changing the new maintenance medium every 3-7 days. The step of maintaining the isolated perivascular cells further involves (i) aspirating the medium from the cells when the density of cells exceeds 70% of the surface of a culture dish or flask in which the cells are maintained, (ii) washing the cells with PBS, (iii) adding trypsin-EDTA to cover the cells until the cells lift off of the dish or flask, and (iv) resuspending the cells in maintenance medium. The step of maintaining the isolated perivascular cells involves adding at least one gene reprogramming factor. Preferred Biomolecule: The collagenase is type I at 1 mg/ml.
   ACTIVITY - Cardiovascular-Gen.; Vulnerary; Cardiant; Antiinflammatory.
   No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Cell therapy.
   ADMINISTRATION - The composition is administered intravenously.
   No dosage details given.

PT P
PN US2021207049-A1; US11441090-B2
TI Converting hazardous compounds to less hazardous            substances comprise e.g. providing non-auto-combustible            organic material and optionally mechanically removing            liquid, soluble components from high protein organic            material optionally applying heat to dry organic            material to reduce its moisture.
AU WILSON D D
   SMITH B M
   HOLMES C
   LARSON G L
AE AKBEV GROUP LLC (AKBE-Non-standard)
GA 202179174X
AB    NOVELTY - Converting hazardous compounds to less                hazardous substances comprise: e.g. (1a) providing                a non-auto-combustible organic material; (2a)                optionally mechanically removing liquid and soluble                components from the high protein organic material;                (3a) optionally applying heat to dry the organic                material to reduce less than or equal to 10 wt.%                its moisture; (4a) pulverizing the high protein                organic material to reduce the high protein organic                material to a particle size of less than 2 mm; (5a)                separating particles of the high protein organic                material during a combustion phase to inhibit their                cohesion into an integrated mass by spraying the                particles into a combustion chamber (28); (6a)                simultaneously injecting steam into the combustion                chamber to enhance combustion characteristics of                the high protein organic material; and (7a)                allowing protein thermal decomposition by-products                to react with nitrogen oxides (NOX) within the                combustion chamber to form water and                nitrogen.
   USE - The process is useful for: converting                hazardous compounds to less hazardous substances                (claimed); and making a fuel product from a                non-combustible high protein organic material e.g.                a biological by-product or waste material.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: is commercially available in                industry; and provides heat for a brewing processes                and other heat-required applications using a steam                boiler fueled by novel high protein organic                material as a fuel product made from the spent                grain, distillers grains and hops residues                by-products of the brewing industry; and is                economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Converting hazardous compounds to less                hazardous substances comprise: (1a) providing a                non-auto-combustible organic material, where the                organic material is a high protein organic material                having greater than or equal to 10 wt.% a protein                on a dry weight basis (DWB); (2a) optionally                mechanically removing liquid and soluble components                from the high protein organic material; (3a)                optionally applying heat to dry the organic                material to reduce less than or equal to 10 wt.%                its moisture; (4a) pulverizing the high protein                organic material to reduce the high protein organic                material to a particle size of less than 2 mm,                where the pre-combustion steps of (2a) optionally                mechanically removing liquid and soluble components                from the high protein organic material, (3a)                optionally applying heat to dry the organic                material to reduce less than or equal to 10 wt.%                its moisture and (4a) pulverizing the high protein                organic material to reduce the high protein organic                material to a particle size of less than 2 mm may                occur in any order; (5a) separating particles of                the high protein organic material during a                combustion phase to inhibit their cohesion into an                integrated mass by spraying the particles into a                combustion chamber; (6a) simultaneously injecting                steam into the combustion chamber to enhance                combustion characteristics of the high protein                organic material; (7a) allowing protein thermal                decomposition by-products to react with nitrogen                oxides (NOX) within the combustion chamber to form                water and nitrogen, where the (NOX) production of                greater than or equal to 100-300 ppm, protein                thermal decomposition by-products remaining after                combustion include ammonium, nitrogenous                hydrocarbons, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, NOX,                nitrogen free radicals, nitrogen cations and other                non-nitrogen containing free radical intermediate                combustion reactants in the combustion gasses; (8a)                controlling protein thermal decomposition                by-products including NOX production produced                during combustion within the combustion chamber;                and (9a) incinerating polyfluoro compound                impurities present within the processed                non-autocombustible high protein organic material                in the combustion chamber and/or adding and                incinerating polyfluoro compounds within the                combustion chamber, where the protein thermal                decomposition by-products functions as a reactive                species to incinerate polyfluoro compounds to                degrade hazardous polyfluoro compounds into less                hazardous substances.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                converting (p2) hazardous compounds to less                hazardous substances within a traditional                combustion chamber comprising (a4) providing an                auto-combustible organic fuel, (b4) providing a                non-auto-combustible organic material, (c4)                optionally mechanically removing liquid and soluble                components from the high protein organic material,                (d4) optionally applying heat to dry the organic                material to reduce less than or equal to 10 wt.%                its moisture, (e4) pulverizing the high protein                organic material to reduce the high protein organic                material to a particle size of less than 2 mm,                where the pre-combustion steps of (c4) optionally                mechanically removing liquid and soluble components                from the high protein organic material, (d4)                injecting the auto-combustible organic material                into a combustion chamber, (e4) spraying the                non-auto-combustible high protein organic material                into the combustion chamber through use of a                pneumatic stoker to separate particles of the high                protein organic material and inhibit their cohesion                into an integrated mass during combustion, (f4)                simultaneously injecting steam into the combustion                chamber to enhance combustion characteristics of                the high protein organic material, (g4) allowing                protein thermal decomposition by-products to react                with NOX within the combustion chamber to form                water and nitrogen, where 100 ppm to greater than                300 ppm, protein thermal decomposition by-products                remaining after combustion include ammonium,                nitrogenous hydrocarbons, carbon monoxide, carbon                dioxide, NOX, nitrogen cations, nitrogen free                radicals, and other non-nitrogen containing free                radical intermediate combustion reactants in the                combustion gasses, (i4) controlling protein thermal                decomposition by-products including NOX production,                produced during combustion within the combustion                chamber, and (j4) incinerating polyfluoro compound                impurities present within the processed                non-autocombustible high protein organic material                in the combustion chamber and optionally, adding                and incinerating additional polyfluoro compounds                within the combustion chamber as an additional or                alternative step, where the protein thermal                decomposition by-products functions as a reactive                species to incinerate polyfluoro compounds to                degrade hazardous polyfluoro compounds into less                hazardous substances.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a three schematic flow                diagram of the process.
   Hopper (16)
   Grain drying drum (18)
   Boiler (22)
   Combustion chamber (28)
   Steam generator (40)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The protein decomposition                  by-product ash resulting from the combustion of                  high protein organic materials contains greater                  than or equal to 300 ppm ammonium, nitrogenous                  hydrocarbons, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide,                  NOX, nitrogen free radicals, nitrogen cations and                  other non-nitrogen containing free radical                  intermediate combustion reactants. Pulverizing,                  pressing, applying heat to dry the high protein                  organic material particles, spraying particles                  into the combustion chamber and injecting steam                  into the combustion chamber degrades the proteins                  contained within the particles and denatures them                  by allowing nitrogen crosslinking and other                  crosslinking reactions to occur within the                  particles, allowing the particles to complete all                  of the crosslinking ability before the particles                  contact other particles. Crosslinking of the high                  protein organic material particles are prevented                  by prematurely initiating crosslinking reactions                  of the nitrogen bonds and other cross linking                  reactions while the particles are being agitated,                  where the high protein organic material particles                  no longer adhere to each other, thus arresting                  the particles tendency to adhere to each other                  within the combustion chamber via nitrogen bond                  crosslinkage and other cross-linkage reactions.                  Separating the high protein organic material by                  spraying the processed high protein organic                  material into the combustion chamber is effected                  through use of a pneumatic stoker. Spraying the                  particles of the high protein organic material                  into the combustion chamber by the pneumatic                  stoker keeps the particles separated in the                  combustion chamber long enough to allow heat                  transfer provided by the combustion process to                  quickly dry and then degrade proteins present                  within the high protein organic material and to                  prevent nitrogen crosslinking and other cross                  linking reactions between the particles that                  would have the particles adhere to each other.                  The particles of the high protein organic                  material are separated and dispersed within the                  combustion chamber and ignited and burned while                  in suspension and separated from each other                  before they land and adhere to each other. The                  process further comprises controlling the                  concentration of ammonium, nitrogenous                  hydrocarbons, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide,                  NOX, nitrogen cations, nitrogen free radicals and                  other non-nitrogen containing free radical                  intermediate combustion reactants in the                  combustion gasses within the combustion chamber.                  The concentration of protein thermal                  decomposition by-products and excess water within                  the combustion chamber is controlled to react and                  convert carbon-fluoride bonds in PFAS compounds                  to nitrogen, carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide,                  hydrogen fluoride and various inorganic fluoride                  containing salts and/or minerals based upon                  cations present in the fuel. The incineration of                  PFAS compounds within the combustion chamber                  occurs at a temperature of less than or equal to                  999degrees Celsius. The incineration of PFAS                  compounds within the combustion chamber has a                  residence time of less than or equal to 1 second                  at a temperature of 999degrees Celsius. The high                  protein organic material is a biological waste or                  by-product material, where the biological waste                  or by-product material originates from waste                  water treatment activated sludge waste and the                  process comprises (a) providing a biological                  waste material or by-product comprising a waste                  water treatment activated sludge having greater                  than or equal to 10 wt.% a protein on a dry                  weight basis (DWB), (b) removing water, moisture                  and other soluble components from the biological                  waste material or by-product, (c) drying the                  biological waste material or by-product to reduce                  less than or equal to 10 wt.% the moisture, (d)                  pulverizing the biological waste material to                  reduce the particle size to be less than 2 mm,                  (e) separating particles of the biological waste                  material or by-product during the combustion                  phase to inhibit their cohesion into an                  integrated mass by spraying the particles into                  the combustion chamber, (f) simultaneously                  injecting steam into the combustion chamber,                  where steam is injected to modify and control                  combustion reactions by reacting with nitrogen                  within proteins to form intermediate                  nitrogenous-based protein thermal combustion                  products which help to maintain regulatory                  compliance of combustion emissions, (g)                  controlling protein thermal decomposition                  by-products including NOX production, produced                  during combustion within the combustion chamber,                  where the protein thermal decomposition                  by-products functions as a reactive species to                  incinerate PFAS, organic fluoride (organo                  fluorine) compounds and non-organic mineralized                  fluorine compounds, (h) optionally, adding                  additional PFAS substances in the combustion                  chamber for further combustion and (i)                  incinerating PFAS, organic fluoride (organo                  fluorine) compounds and non-organic mineralized                  fluorine compounds within the combustion chamber,                  where the biological waste or by-product material                  contains PFAS, organic fluoride (organo fluorine)                  compounds and/or non-organic mineralized fluorine                  compounds and/or the PFAS, organic fluoride                  (organo fluorine) compounds and/or non-organic                  mineralized fluorine compounds are added to the                  biological waste or by-product material within                  the combustion chamber for incineration. The high                  protein organic material is hops residue, where                  the process comprises (a1) extracting oils and                  other compounds from the ground hops utilizing                  mechanical separation techniques or carbon                  dioxide extraction to obtain a high protein hops                  waste residue having wt.% a protein 25-30 on a                  dry weight basis (DWB), (b2) providing the hops                  waste residue, (c3) drying the hops waste                  residue, where the step of drying the hops waste                  residue comprises the application of heat, (d4)                  grinding the hops waste residue into a powder by                  pulverizing the hops waste residue to ensure that                  particles of the hops waste residue have a                  particle size of less than 2 mm, where the step                  of pulverizing the hops waste residue includes                  subjecting the hops residue to a mill, (e5)                  agitating the hops waste residue during a                  combustion phase to separate particles of the                  hops waste residue by spraying the particles into                  the combustion chamber to inhibit their cohesion                  into an integrated mass, (f6) simultaneously                  injecting steam into the combustion chamber to                  enhance the combustibility of the high protein                  organic material, (g7) controlling protein                  thermal decomposition by-products including NOX                  production, produced during combustion within the                  combustion chamber, where the protein thermal                  decomposition by-products functions as a reactive                  species to incinerate PFAS, organic fluoride                  (organo fluorine) compounds and non-organic                  mineralized fluorine compounds, (h8) optionally,                  adding additional PFAS substances in the                  combustion chamber for further combustion and                  (i9) incinerating PFAS, organic fluoride (organo                  fluorine) compounds and non-organic mineralized                  fluorine compounds within the combustion chamber,                  where the biological waste or by-product material                  contains PFAS, organic fluoride (organo fluorine)                  compounds and/or non-organic mineralized fluorine                  compounds and/or where PFAS, organic fluoride                  (organo fluorine) compounds and/or non-organic                  mineralized fluorine compounds are added to the                  biological waste or by-product material within                  the combustion chamber for incineration. The high                  protein organic material is a high protein waste                  or meal from an agricultural source of oil                  production, waste by-products and by-products                  from an oil seed pulp processing. The high                  protein organic material comprises an oil seed                  pulp waste residue, further where the process                  comprises (a3) obtaining an extracted high                  protein oil seed pulp waste residue having 35                  wt.% a protein on a dry weight basis (DWB), less                  than or equal to 10 wt.% a moisture and a                  particle size less than 2 mm, oil from the oil                  seed pulp waste residue may or may not be                  preliminarily extracted, (b3) separating and                  agitating particles of the oil seed pulp waste                  residue during the combustion phase to inhibit                  their cohesion into an integrated mass while                  simultaneously injecting steam into the                  combustion chamber, (c3) controlling protein                  thermal decomposition by-products including NOX                  production, produced during combustion within the                  combustion chamber, where the protein thermal                  decomposition by-products functions as a reactive                  species to incinerate PFAS, organic fluoride                  (organo fluorine) compounds and non-organic                  mineralized fluorine compounds, (d3) optionally,                  adding additional organic fluoride (organo                  fluorine) compounds and non-organic mineralized                  fluorine compounds in the combustion chamber for                  further combustion and (e3) incinerating PFAS,                  organic fluoride (organo fluorine) compounds and                  non-organic mineralized fluorine compounds within                  the combustion chamber, where the oil seed pulp                  waste contains PFAS, organic fluoride (organo                  fluorine) compounds and/or non-organic                  mineralized fluorine compounds. The high protein                  organic material is one of a high protein animal                  excreta or a high protein animal meat processing                  by-product or waste, where the process comprises                  obtaining a pre-processed or ''as is'' high                  protein animal excreta or high protein animal                  meat processing by-product or waste which is                  non-auto-combustible, the animal excreta has                  20-60 wt.% a protein on a dry weight basis (DWB)                  and the animal meat processing by-product or                  waste has 35-85 wt.% a protein.Preferred Components: The                  non-auto-combustible high protein organic                  material is rendered combustible without the                  addition of other combustible fuels or additives.                  The polyfluoro compound impurities and polyfluoro                  compounds comprise polyfluoroalkyl compounds and                  perfluoralkyl compounds (PFAS), organic fluoride                  (organo fluorine) compounds and non-organic                  mineralized organo fluorine compounds. The PFAS                  substances further comprise perfluorooctanoic                  acid (PFOA) or perfluorooctane sulfonate                  (PFOS).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The nitrogen cations and                  concentrations of nitrogen cations present within                  the combustion chamber after combustion of the                  high protein organic material vary upon the type                  of high protein fuel used for combustion and                  comprise at least one of calcium, sodium,                  potassium, phosphorus, silica and manganese. The                  PFAS substances: are degraded to an inorganic                  mineralized form; and are degraded to calcium                  fluoride or hydrogen fluoride.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PN WO2020152017-A1; EP3867349-A1
TI Method for automatically monitoring stability of            methane production in biogas reactors for anaerobic            fermentation of organic matter, involves determining            pCCV measured values automatically at regular and            adjustable time intervals.
AU KAZDA M
AE KAZDA M (KAZD-Individual)
   KAZDA M (KAZD-Individual)
GA 2020712661
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining the CCV                partial pressure (PCO2) in the liquid reactor                content under anaerobic measuring conditions with a                measuring system during biogas generation. The pCCV                measured values are determined automatically at                regular, adjustable time intervals, using the                determined values for process engineering                evaluation as parameters for the stability                assessment of the process of biogas and methane                formation. The sensors are used as the measuring                system, which measure the carbon-di-oxide partial                pressure. Each sensor are completely immersed in                the liquid reactor content and rinsed when the                reactor content is stirred.
   USE - Method for automatically monitoring stability                of methane production in biogas reactors for                anaerobic fermentation of organic matter such as                animal excrement, liquid manure, energy crops, such                as grain, sugar beet, maize and grass silage, or                material from the organic waste bin, bio-waste or                waste products from food production.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon-di-oxide partial pressure is a                suitable parameter to enable the process stability                of the biogas production in a reliable manner. The                process stability and efficiency of the bioreactor                are monitored by the fact that when a corresponding                value is fallen short of or exceeded pCCV values of                the plant operator are warned accordingly. The                destabilization of the reactions of the bioreactor                is prevented. The plant operator is provided by                increasing the carbon-di-oxide react to supply or                reduction of CCV supply and/or increase or                reduction of substrate supply accordingly. The                online usability of the pCCV measuring system is                used to automate the system control in general and                the substrate supply. The CCV partial pressure in                the reactor contents integrates all processes that                leads to overloading and subsequent acidification                of the biogas reactor and is suitable as a                parameter for assessing the stability of anaerobic                biological methane production. The process                stability and efficiency of methane production in                biological methanation are ensured. The process                automatically monitors the stability of the biogas                production. The procedure for assessing the                stability of the biogas process is carried out on a                laboratory and pilot scale as well as on a large                industrial scale. The trouble-free assessment of                the process stability is carried out directly in                the biogas reactor by immediately displaying the                corresponding value at an interface or on the PC                monitor. The real-time process monitoring enables                automation of biogas production. The automatic pC02                measuring system has an adjustable temporal                resolution, so that the carbon-di-oxide partial                pressure is determined in a simple manner,                depending on the time. The constantly high methane                formation rate is achieved. The determined values                provide information about the process stability of                the biogas and methane formation, so that the plant                operator immediately reacts to any significant                problems regarding the stability of the process.                The best possible utilization of the reactor volume                is ensured while maintaining process stability. The                biogas plants achieve better economic efficiency                and are prepared for the introduction of flexible                biogas generation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The measuring system used is an automated                measuring system that determines the CCV partial                pressure at regular, adjustable intervals and is                used to automate the system control, to automate                the substrate supply to the biogas reactor. The                measuring system used is suitable for online                monitoring of a bioreactor in real time, and has an                adjustable temporal resolution. The measuring                system is used through the reactor ceiling since                the existing openings in the reactor wall of the                bioreactor is introduced into the liquid reactor                content. An optimal range for the CCV partial                pressure values of a bioreactor depending on the                reactor volume, the temperature, the installation                depth of the measuring system, the organic                substance to be processed, so that when the                selected range is undershot, the substrate supply                to the bioreactor is increased and the substrate                supply is reduced when the range is exceeded. An                optimal range for the CCV partial pressure values                of a bioreactor is operated at a temperature of                about 38 degrees C to 55 degrees C, in the range of                about 80 to about 200 hPa, in the range of about 80                to about 150 hPa, in the range from about 80 to                about 130 hPa depending on the installation depth                of the measuring system, which measures the CCV                partial pressure. The upper and lower limits of the                range per meter of liquid excess are each increased                by about 10 hPa. The biogas reactors are operated                continuously, substrate supply and removal of                fermented substrate carried out at regular                intervals. The measuring system is used to                determine the carbon-di-oxide partial pressure in                the liquid reactor content of the biogas reactor                which is connected to an evaluation unit outputs                the measured values of the CCV partial pressure or                the measured values in converts the CCV partial                pressure. The methane production is a biological                methanation in the in / fy process in a                power-to-gas process, in which the organic                substance in the anaerobic reactor additionally                contains hydrogen and, when appropriate carbon                dioxide are supplied. The carbon dioxide and acidic                or low-acid substrate are metered into the liquid                reactor content as a function of the determined                values of the CCV partial pressure (PCO2). The                optimal range for the CCV partial pressure values                of a bioreactor, depending on the reactor volume,                the temperature, the installation depth of the                measuring system, the organic substance to be                processed, is selected so that when the selected                optimal pCCV values are undershot. The                carbon-di-oxide supply and substrate supply to the                bioreactor is increased and when the selected                optimal pCCV values are exceeded the CCV supply and                substrate supply to the bioreactor is                reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating                the processes involved in the anaerobic                decomposition of organic substance in a biogas                reactor using a flow diagram. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The CCV oversaturation                  of the liquid reactor content (40) is taken as                  parameter for the process stability, the pH value                  of the reactor contents (40) is measured, and the                  maximum CCV saturation state pCO2HH is calculated                  by: pCO2HH = 10 power (pKa-pH)x p(air)+p (gas                  space) +p(depth), where pCO2HH is maximum CCV                  saturation state according to the extended                  Henderson-Hasselbalch equation, p(gas space) is                  pressure in the gas space of the reactor (200)                  and p(depth) is pressure correction for the depth                  of the installation location (48) of the pCCV                  measurement system within the reactor (200). The                  CCV oversaturation of the liquid reactor content                  (40) is calculated as follows: pCO2OS = pCO2 act                  -pCO2 HH, where pCO2 HH represents the maximum                  CO2 saturation state, pCO2 act represents the                  measured pCO2 value, and the CCV partial pressure                  values in the liquid reactor content (40) of the                  bioreactor (200) are adjusted by correspondingly                  metering in carbon dioxide (60) and / or                  substrate such that relation given by 1.2x                  pPCO2HH less than pCO2 OS less than 3x pCO2 HH is                  satisfied.

TI Primary culturing of gill cells of Anodonta woodiana comprises breeding A. woodiana in sterile water, disinfecting, dissecting out gills, cutting, digesting with e.g. pronase solution, centrifuging, and culturing in culture medium mixture.
AU CHEN X
   YANG J
   LIU H
AE CAFS FRESHWATER FISHERIES RES CENT (CAFS-C)
GA 2020328716
AB    NOVELTY - Primary culturing of gill cells of Anodonta woodiana comprises temporarily breeding washed A. woodiana in sterile water, disinfecting in antibiotic solution having penicillin, streptomycin, gentamicin and kanamycin, dissecting out gills, placing in antibiotic solution, cutting, disinfecting, mixing antibiotic solution with pronase solution to form digestion solution, placing the gills to decompose the cells, filtering via sterile filter, centrifuging, collecting the cell pellet, taking cell culture medium mixture having e.g. L-15 basic medium, kanamycin, 4-hydroxyethylpiperazineethanesulfonic acid and L-glutamine, adding fetal bovine serum, adding a portion of the synthetic cell culture medium to the centrifuge tube, adjusting the cell concentration, adding cell suspension to each culture hole of the culture well plate, primary cell culturing in a carbon dioxide incubator, allowing to adhere to the wall and testing the survival rate with trypan blue staining.
   USE - The gill cells of Anodonta woodiana is useful for studying toxic effects of cadmium (claimed), preferably cytotoxic effect of cadmium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is accurate; improves the cell survival rate and the survival time; reaches the experimental research standard to successfully evaluate the cytotoxic effect of cadmium.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Primary culturing of gill cells of Anodonta woodiana comprises (a) sterilizing ultrapure water at 110-130 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, cooling to normal temperature after sterilization to obtain sterile water, (b) adding washed A. woodiana into sterile water and temporarily breeding for 24-96 hours, (c) first adding 50000-500000 IU penicillin, 0.05-0.5 g streptomycin, 0.05-5 g gentamicin and 0.03-3 g kanamycin to 900-1000 ml sterile water at 4-26 degrees C to obtain antibiotic solution, disinfecting A. woodiana in the antibiotic solution for 1-6 hours, (d) dissecting out the gills, placing the gills in antibiotic solution at 4-26 degrees C filtered through 0.2-0.3 mu m filter head, cutting the gills into 1-5 mm2 pieces and disinfecting the slices in the antibiotic solution for 1-3 hours, (e) collecting the gill pieces, taking 10-50 times the mass of gill pieces, 0.025-0.25 wt.% pronase solution of 4-26 degrees C, and 30-50 vol.% antibiotic solution, mixing the taken antibiotic solution with pronase solution to form the digestion solution, placing the gills in the solution to decompose the cells for 0.5-8 hours and adding fetal bovine serum to stop digestion, (f) using 50-100 mu m sterile filter to filter the gill cell fluid, taking into a sterile centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 1000-2000 revolutions/minute for 5-10 minutes to remove the supernatant and collect the cell pellet, (g) adding 150-300 ml L-15 basic medium, 10000-50000 IU penicillin, 0.01-0.05 g streptomycin, 0.1-1 g gentamicin, 0.01-1 g kanamycin, 1-5 g4-hydroxyethylpiperazineethanesulfonic acid and 10-100 ml L-glutamine in 300-800 ml sterile water to form a cell culture medium mixture, taking 10-20% cell culture medium mixture, adding fetal bovine serum, controlling the temperature at 15-20 degrees C to obtain synthetic cell culture medium, adding a portion of the synthetic cell culture medium to the centrifuge tube, and adjusting the cell concentration to 105-106 cells/ml cell suspension, (h) adding 0.5-1.5 ml cell suspension to each culture hole of the culture well plate, placing in a carbon dioxide incubator at 15-20 degrees C for primary cell culture, allowing to adhere to the wall after 6 hours, testing the survival rate of the cells with trypan blue staining at a concentration of 0.4-1 wt.%, replacing at least 1/2 of the artificial cell culture medium in the culture hole with the artificial cell culture medium at 15-20 degrees C, successfully obtaining cultivation time of up to 120 hours to obtain primary culture of gill cells of Anodonta woodiana with a survival rate of greater than or equal to 90%. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of the primary cultured gill cells of Anodonta woodiana in the study of toxic effects of cadmium, comprising (i) using probabilistic unit analysis, calculating the LC25 of primary cultured gill cells of cadmium(II) ions against the mussels, (ii) setting at least 5 cadmium(II) ion concentration gradient exposure groups and a blank control group using equal ratio method according to LC25, setting each cadmium(II) ion concentration gradient exposure group with 4-6 parallel groups, (iii) inoculating the cell suspension with a concentration of 105 to 106 cells/ml into a first culture well plate and a second culture well plate respectively, inoculating first culture well plate with 100-200 mu l cell suspension per well and the second culture well plate with 0.5-1.5 ml cell suspension in each well and incubating for 6-24 hours to ensure adherence of cells, (iv) using synthetic cell culture medium to prepare cadmium(II) ions mother liquor with target cadmium(II) ions concentration of 2-10 times, at the same temperature, adhering to pipette out 1/10 to 1/2 of the artificial cell culture medium in the well plate, and adherently adding the same amount of cadmium(II) ions mother liquor to adhere to the wall, to obtain 5 sets of cadmium(II) ions concentration gradient exposure groups, where the blank control group replaces the same amount of cadmium(II) ions-free synthetic cell culture medium, and the exposure time is 6-96 hours, using first culture well plate to detect cell viability, and the second culture well plate to detect antioxidant factor superoxide dismutase (SOD), antioxidant factor catalase and acid phosphatase (ACP) toxicology indicators.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The ultrapure water in the step (a) is Milli-Q grade ultrapure water. In the step (c): the antibiotic solution comprises 50000-500000 IU penicillin, 0.05-0.5 g streptomycin, 0.05-5 g gentamicin and 0.03-3 g kanamycin. In the step (e): the concentration of the pronase solution is 0.025-0.25 wt.%.

PN CN110937764-A; CN110937764-B
TI Treating early landfill leachate by using upflow            anaerobic sludge bed-sequencing batch            reactor-electrochemical oxidation (UASB-SBR-EO)            comprises passing landfill leachate raw water,            performing internal circulation and subsequent            processing.
AU WU L
   LI J
   YAN Z
AE UNIV BEIJING CIVIL ENG & ARCHITECTURE (UBCE-C)
GA 202027550N
AB    NOVELTY - Treating early landfill leachate by using                upflow anaerobic sludge bed-sequencing batch                reactor-electrochemical oxidation (UASB-SBR-EO)                comprises passing the raw water of landfill                leachate from the inlet water tank to first                compartment through outlet pipe, then pumping the                SBR nitrate in the second compartment is pumped                into UASB through an water outlet pipe and                corresponding inlet pump, performing internal                circulation through the UASB internal circulation                water pipe for flowing the liquid upwards in the                UASB reactor, stopping stirring when the                first-order derivative of the pH curve controlled                in real time changes from positive to negative,                precipitating, starting the intermediate tank inlet                pump, pumping one part into the second compartment                of the inlet water tank through the return pipe                through water outlet 1, then pumping the another                part into the electric oxidation device EO through                the EO inlet pipe through the water outlet for                performing subsequent processing.
   USE - The method is useful for treating early                landfill leachate by using upflow anaerobic sludge                bed-sequencing batch reactor-electrochemical                oxidation (UASB-SBR-EO).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: an achieve deep carbon removal,                nitrogen removal and desulfurization; is widely                used in the advanced treatment of                high-concentration organic sewage; greatly removes                degradable chemical oxygen demand; provides good                physical and chemical environment for the                subsequent anaerobic ammonia oxidation reactor; can                simultaneously remove organic matter, ammonia                nitrogen and sulfate; can strictly check through                the electro-oxidation process to deeply remove the                refractory organic matter; can obtain high                concentration in the final effluent of organic                matter, ammonia nitrogen, and sulfate, i.e. 87.511                mg/l, 18.545 mg/l, and 3065 mg/l, respectively; and                can meet the sewage discharge requirements.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating early landfill leachate by using                upflow anaerobic sludge bed-sequencing batch                reactor-electrochemical oxidation (UASB-SBR-EO)                comprises (a) passing the raw water of landfill                leachate from the inlet water tank to first                compartment through outlet pipe, then pumping the                SBR nitrate in the second compartment is pumped                into UASB through an water outlet pipe and                corresponding inlet pump, where the volume of                influent water is 10-50% the volume of UASB, (b)                starting UASB internal circulation pump after                completing UASB, performing internal circulation                through the UASB internal circulation water pipe                for flowing the liquid upwards in the UASB reactor,                then completely reacting for contacting, passing                the nitrogen, methane, and carbon dioxide generated                in the UASB reactor from bottom to top for                achieving the separation of gas, liquid and solid                under the action of three-phase separator, then                passing to lye absorption bottle through the                exhaust pipe with an exhaust valve, absorbing the                carbon dioxide in the biogas by the lye in the lye                absorption bottle, measuring nitrogen and methane,                and discharging through a gas flow meter, (c)                overflowing the supernatant of the above UASB                reactor of water, then passing into intermediate                water tank by gravity through the UASB outlet pipe,                adjusting the pH and temperature in the middle                water tank, opening the middle water tank outlet                valve, then starting the SBR inlet pump, and                pumping the water in middle water tank into the SBR                reactor through SBR inlet pipe, (d) turning on the                aeration device after completing the SBR water                inlet, passing air enter into SBR after passing                through air compressor, gas flow meter, aeration                tube, and aeration ring in the SBR process,                controlling the dissolved oxygen to 0.5-1 mg/l                through gas flow meter, monitoring the pH and                dissolved oxygen online in real time by SBR's                online pH and dissolved oxygen (DO) monitoring                equipment for determining the reaction status, then                sampling for every 4 hours, analyzing and                monitoring the water quality indicators, when the                concentration of ammonia nitrogen in the                nitrification reaction lowers to 100-150 mg/l and                the accumulation of nitrite nitrogen reaches                100-200 mg/l, stopping the aeration, then turning                on mechanical stirring device in a speed of 50-80                revolutions/minute under anoxic conditions,                removing the ammonia nitrogen, nitrite nitrogen and                sulfuric acid by nitrite-type anaerobic ammonia                oxidation and sulfate-type anaerobic ammonia                oxidation depth removal of salt, stopping stirring                when the first-order derivative of the pH curve                controlled in real time changes from positive to                negative, precipitating for 30-50 minutes, draining                for 3-5 minutes in a drainage ratio of 10-50% for                completing the operation cycle of SBR, then turning                on SBR second drain at the end of each cycle of                precipitation, starting the intermediate tank inlet                pump, and pumping the supernatant into the                intermediate tank through the SBR outlet pipe, (e)                pumping one part into the second compartment of the                inlet water tank through the return pipe through                water outlet 1, then pumping the another part into                the electric oxidation device EO through the EO                inlet pipe through the water outlet for performing                subsequent processing, where the intermediate water                tank according to the reflux ratio of the volume                ratio of 3:1-4:1, and (f) completing the water                inlet of the electro-oxidation device, passing to                EO for removal of refractory organic matter and                color, using constant current charging mode for                electrolysis, connecting direct current power                supply, then respectively connecting the anode and                cathode to the anode and cathode of EO through a                wire, where the anode is titanium-based tin(IV)                oxide coating electrode, and the coating catalyst                load is 1-5 mg/cm2, connecting to direct current                power supply, controlling the current density at                8-12 mA/cm2 for electrolysis, where the bipolar                voltage is 3.5-4.5 V, then generating a strong                oxidant hydroxy radical and ozone on the catalytic                layer of the anode surface, which will degrade the                refractory organic pollutants and reduces the                color, where the cathode uses nickel base, and                hydrogen, which is generated in the cathode during                the reaction, where the EO side is provided with a                water outlet control valve and water outlet pipe,                and discharging the system water through the EO                water outlet pipe.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. PT P
PN CN109609462-A
TI Performing three-dimensional cell pellet culture            and susceptibility test, involves forming array of            droplets on superhydrophobic micropit array chip,            adding alginate, and performing susceptibility            test.
AU CHEN X
   HAO Y
AE UNIV BEIHANG (UNBA-C)
GA 201935789Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for performing high-throughput                three-dimensional cell pellet culture and in situ                susceptibility test, involves forming an array of                droplets on superhydrophobic micropit array chip by                disinfecting superhydrophobic micro-pit chip, and                constructing droplet microarray, forming a                three-dimensional cell ball array on the                super-hydrophobic micro-pit array chip, by                culturing cell, preparing thin layer                superhydrophobic slide for rehydration, spraying                pattern forming medium solution, constructing                cellular microarray, performing rehydration                preparation, performing alignment rehydration, and                rapidly reversing the super-hydrophobic micro-pit                array chip, forming a three-dimensional cell                pellet, adding alginate to the super-hydrophobic                micro-pit array chip to fix the cell pellet in the                micro-pit, and performing in situ susceptibility                test on a super-hydrophobic micro-pit array                chip.
   USE - The method is useful for performing                high-throughput three-dimensional cell pellet                culture and in situ susceptibility test.
   ADVANTAGE - The method performs simultaneous loading of                each micro-pit of the chip by using the self-built                humidification and aligning device. The                construction of the high-flux three-dimensional                small cell micro-array is performed simply.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for performing high-throughput                three-dimensional cell pellet culture and in situ                susceptibility test, involves (a) forming an array                of droplets on superhydrophobic micropit array chip                by (i) disinfecting superhydrophobic micro-pit                chip, and (ii) constructing droplet microarray by                suspending 12 ml phosphate-buffered saline (PBS)                solution using pipette into the micro-pit of                super-hydrophobic micro-pits, drop by drop,                microscopically scanning the super-hydrophobic                micro-pit chip on the super-hydrophobic micro-pit                chip to ensure that all the micro-pits have liquids                entering, and forming mutually independent droplets                in the micro-pits, (b) forming a three-dimensional                cell ball array on the super-hydrophobic micro-pit                array chip, by (i) culturing cell by using HCC827                cells as representative of lung cancer cells and                1640 medium as lung cancer cell culture medium,                adding 10% fetal calf serum, 100 U/ml penicillin                and 100 mu g/ml streptomycin, passaging once every                2-3 days, (ii) preparing thin layer                superhydrophobic slide for rehydration, (iii)                spraying pattern forming medium solution, (iv)                constructing cellular microarray, (v) performing                rehydration preparation by firstly closing entire                humidification device, turning on the humidifier to                adjust to the maximum humidification, increasing                the relative humidity in the humidification device                to over 90%, turning the UV sterilization device on                to start the internal sterilization of the entire                humidification device, after 30 minutes, turning                off the humidifier and the UV sterilization device,                turning on the fan to blow off the excess fog,                turning off the fan after 10 minutes, and                transferring the super-hydrophobic micro-pit array                chip and the sprayed slide to the humidifier to                prepare for alignment, (vi) performing alignment                rehydration by firstly placing the                super-hydrophobic micro-pit array chip on the                aligning device's stage, removing excess cell                solution immersed in the super-hydrophobic                micro-pit array chip, forming a droplet array,                taking out the spray slide, drying the back                moisture, sucking on the tip of the alignment                device, gradually lowering the tip, gradually                bringing the spray slide and the super-hydrophobic                micro-pit array chip closer, using a charge-coupled                device (CCD) imaging system to observe the spray                pattern and the droplet dot array, adjusting the                alignment device, completely aligning the two                droplet lattices, dripping sprayed slide,                contacting the sprayed droplet array with the                droplet array in the micro-pit of the                super-hydrophobic micro-pit array chip, completing                alignment refilling, and slowly lifting the sprayed                slide, and (vii) rapidly reversing the                super-hydrophobic micro-pit array chip, placing in                a 10 cm culture dish with PBS solution, then                placing into a carbon dioxide incubator at 37                degrees C for 24 hours, and aggregating the cells                at the top of the droplet to form a                three-dimensional cell pellet, (c) adding alginate                to the super-hydrophobic micro-pit array chip to                fix the cell pellet in the micro-pit by (i)                observing and recording the cell globular state,                (ii) to the chip that has formed the                three-dimensional cell microsphere array, firstly                adding 2 wt.% aqueous sodium alginate solution and                2 wt.% calcium chloride aqueous solution,                spontaneously forming alginate, and fixing a small                ball in the micro-pit, and (iii) culturing cell                pellets by immersing the chip with lung cancer cell                culture medium, replacing the excess calcium                chloride solution, after immersing for 10 minutes,                removing excess lung cancer cell culture medium,                returning the chip to the state of droplets,                placing in a 37 degrees C, and continuously                culturing for 24 hours in a carbon dioxide                incubator, and (d) performing in situ                susceptibility test on a super-hydrophobic                micro-pit array chip, by (i) detecting cell                viability by mixing lung cancer cell culture medium                and the Alamarblue (RTM: Cell viability reagent) at                a volume ratio of 10:1 to prepare a working fluid,                using the working fluid to immerse the                superhydrophobic micro-pit array chip which has                formed cell beads, standing for 10 minutes,                allowing the excess working fluid to pump away to                restore the chip to the state of the droplet array,                placing the chip in a cassette, incubating for 30                minutes in the dark, scanning the whole chip with a                fluorescence microscope to obtain a red fluorescent                photograph of all the micro-pits on the chip, and                analyzing the fluorescence value of each micro-pit,                (ii) incubating with drug, (iii) after the drug was                added, using Alamarblue (RTM: Cell viability                reagent) to detect the cell activity by immersing                the chip in the lung cancer cell culture medium to                obtain a red fluorescence scan of the chip after                dosing, (iv) processing image, and (v) performing                data statistics and analysis by counting data of                each micro-pit of different drug concentrations,                calculating the average value and the mean standard                deviation, and normalizing average value of the                control group to obtain relationship between cell                survival rate and drug concentration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The humidification                  device comprises a glove box, a humidifier and a                  fan, and the humidifier and the fan are                  respectively placed at opposite corners of the                  glove box, and the glove box is first closed when                  used. Then turn on the humidifier for 30 minutes,                  then turn off the humidifier and turn on the fan                  for 10 minutes to blow off the mist. The glove                  box is further provided with an UV sterilizing                  device, closed together with the humidifier                  during use, for performing UV sterilization while                  humidifying. The alignment device includes a                  translational alignment platform including a                  stage, a four-way fine adjustment translation                  stage, a lifting table, a suction head, and a                  vacuum pump, and a separate stage on the right                  side. The four-way fine-tuning translation stage                  on the left side is placed on the bottom surface                  of the glove box, and the lifting platform in the                  operation table is placed on the four-way fine                  adjustment translation stage. The suction head is                  connected to the vacuum pump and fixed on the                  lifting platform, and the position of the suction                  head can be adjusted by four-way fine adjustment                  of the translation stage and the lifting table.                  The storage table is arranged under the suction                  head and fixed on the bottom surface of the glove                  box for carrying the chip. The microscopic                  imaging system includes a camera, a computer and                  a camera supporting imaging software. The                  translation alignment device and the camera are                  placed in the center of the glove box, directly                  above the storage table, and the computer is                  placed above the glove box, and the camera                  supporting imaging software is installed in the                  computer. When using, first open the microscopic                  imaging system, place the chip on the stage,                  adjust the position of the camera and the chip to                  make the image clear; then open the vacuum pump                  to adsorb the slide on the tip. The four-way                  fine-tuning translation stage and the lifting                  table realize the alignment of the droplet array                  on the slide and the alignment of the chip array,                  and realize the independent loading of each                  micro-pit on the whole chip by one alignment                  operation.
