PN CN115115470-A
TI Carbon emission management method for green data            center based on emission factor method, involves            carrying out statistical summary to calculate total            daily power consumption of one day and calculating            total daily carbon dioxide emissions.
AU XU B
AE BEIJING ZZNODE SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022C64689
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves collecting the 15-minute                granular energy consumption of various                energy-consuming facilities in the data center                through the data acquisition system and calculating                the average energy consumption of each type of                equipment through the weighted average. The                statistical summary is carried out to calculate the                total daily power consumption of one day and the                total daily carbon dioxide emissions are calculated                by the total daily power consumption carbon                emission factor. The total daily carbon dioxide                emissions are multiplied by 365 to obtain the                annual carbon dioxide emissions. The various energy                consumption facilities are divided into four                categories. The first category is IT equipment, the                second category is refrigeration system equipment,                the third category is power supply and distribution                system equipment and the fourth category is other                equipment.
   USE - Carbon emission management method for green                data center based on emission factor method.
   ADVANTAGE - The data center carbon emissions are                effectively quantified. The statistical costs are                reduced and statistical efficiency and accuracy is                improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic diagram of                the energy consumption structure of a typical data                center. (Drawing includes non-English language                text)

PN CN114790422-A
TI Carbon dioxide culture box for health detection of            public places comprises culture box main body equipped            with box door, and culture box main body equipped with            mounting box.
AU XU J

PN CN114053967-A
TI Control method for calcium reactor providing calcium reverse water, involves measuring carbonate ion concentration value in solution to be tested in water tank, and adjusting input amount of carbon dioxide gas in calcium reactor.
AU CHEN S
AE CORAL BAY TECHNOLOGY LTD (CORA-Non-standard)
GA 2022370350
AB    NOVELTY - The control method comprises measuring carbonate ion concentration value in solution to be tested in water tank; and adjusting input amount of carbon dioxide gas in calcium reactor according to measurement result and judging condition. When judging that carbonate ion concentration value is lower than concentration default value, the input amount of carbon dioxide gas is adjusted to input amount of previous set value and increased by number of times, and the calcium reverse water is inputted into solution to be tested until carbonate ion concentration value is the same as concentration default value. If carbonate ion concentration value is higher than concentration default value, the input amount of carbon dioxide gas is adjusted to input amount of previous set value and decreased by number of times, and the input of calcium reverse water into the solution to be tested is stopped or reduced until carbonate ion concentration value is the same as the concentration default value.
   USE - The control method is used for calcium reactor providing calcium reverse water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can improve control precision and use convenience.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a calcium reaction control device.

PN CN111646617-A
TI Combined method for electrolysis and photocatalyst to treat sewage, involves decomposing organic matter in sewage into carbon dioxide and water, discharging carbon dioxide, and mixing and discharging generated water with sewage.
AU YANG B
AE ANHUI HUIGUANG OPTOELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202091505T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using grid structure to remove surface suspended matter and impurities in sewage containing multiple heavy metal ions and organic matter. The sliding structure set up at the top of the filter tank is used to remove oily impurities in the sewage. The acid-base value of the treated sewage is measured with a PH meter, and an acid regulator is added to the sewage. The acidified sewage is led into the electrolytic cell. The electrodes in the electrolytic cell reduce the metal ions in the sewage to elemental metals under the action of the power source, and the reduced elemental metals are recovered. The sewage treated by the electrolysis method is passed into the photocatalyst reaction tank, and the photocatalyst forms holes and free electrons on the surface under the light. The organic matter in the sewage is decomposed into carbon dioxide and water, and the carbon dioxide is directly discharged, and the generated water is mixed and discharged with the sewage.
   USE - Combined method for electrolysis and photocatalyst to treat sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively saves costs, and has simple operation, fast speed, high reaction efficiency and strong applicability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The acid regulator is a hydrochloric acid solution.

PT P
PN CN110756197-A; CN110756197-B
TI Synthesis of gold-doped nickel core-shell-type nanocatalyst for reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide, involves dissolving nickel precursor salt and gold precursor salt in oleylamine, heating, dispersing in solvent, and calcining.
AU SU D
   ZHANG X
   WU Z
   YANG B
   LIU W
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202014102W
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of gold-doped nickel core-shell-type nanocatalyst involves dissolving (i) a nickel precursor salt and a gold precursor salt in oleylamine, placing (ii) the obtained solution in an inert atmosphere, heating (iii) the solution at 200-300 degrees C, maintaining for certain time period, and cooling to room temperature, washing (iv) the obtained suspension with organic small molecule solvents, and collecting the obtained nanoparticles, dispersing (v) the nanoparticles in an organic solvent, impregnating and supporting the corresponding supports, and calcining the obtained powder in a reducing atmosphere to obtain a supported gold-doped nickel core-shell nanocatalyst, or calcining the nanoparticles in a reducing atmosphere.
   USE - Synthesis of gold-doped nickel core-shell-type nanocatalyst used for reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide by reverse water-gas shift reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables synthesis of gold-doped nickel core-shell-type nanocatalyst, which has high stability and excellent catalytic activity, and enables reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide with high carbon monoxide selectivity of close to 100%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of gold-doped nickel core-shell-type nanocatalyst in reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide by reverse water-gas shift reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The nickel precursor salt comprises nickel acetylacetonate, nickel chloride, nickel acetate, nickel nitrate, nickel sulfate, and/or their corresponding hydrates. The gold precursor salt comprises chloroauric acid tetrahydrate, chloroauric acid trihydrate, chloroauric acid dihydrate, chloroauric acid monohydrate, gold acetate, and/or gold trichloride. The molar concentration of nickel precursor salt in oleylamine is 50-200 mmol with respect to 1 l of oleylamine, preferably 80-120 mmol with respect to 1 l of oleylamine. The atomic ratio of nickel with respect to gold in oleylamine is 20:1 to 1:1, preferably 6:1 to 8:1. The inert atmosphere gas comprises nitrogen, argon and/or helium. The support comprises silica, alumina, titania, cerium oxide, molybdenum oxide, graphene, and/or graphene oxide, and the usage amount of carrier is 1-10 %mass, preferably 3-6 %mass. The reducing atmosphere includes hydrogen and a gas comprising more than 5 vol.% hydrogen as a main component, and remainder of nitrogen, argon and/or helium. The atomic ratio of nickel with respect to gold in the catalyst is 20:1 to 1:1. The configuration of catalyst comprises a core-shell-type structure comprising nickel as a core, and gold as a shell layer. Preferred Properties: The size of the core is 10-30 nm, and the thickness of the shell layer is 1-10 nm. Preferred Process: In the process (iii), the heating is performed at a heating rate of 10-20 degrees C/minute, preferably 10-12 degrees C/minute, and maintaining for 0.5-1.5 hours, preferably 1-1.5 hours. The calcining is performed at 150-600 degrees C, preferably 300-400 degrees C for 1-5 hours, preferably 1.5-2 hours. The reverse water-gas shift reaction conditions of carbon dioxide and hydrogen as reaction raw materials, and reaction temperature of 300-500 degrees C, preferably 300-400 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The organic small molecule solvent and organic solvent used in the process (v) comprise acetone, n-hexane, toluene, absolute ethanol, dimethylformamide, acetonitrile, and/or isopropyl alcohol.

PN CN110396701-A; CN110396701-B
TI Catalyst useful for preparing formic acid by high-efficiency electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, catalyst is prepared by preparing tin(IV) sulfide nanosheet precursor by chemical vapor deposition process, and calcining.
AU LIU G
   JIANG L
   XU Y
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 201993528U
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst, is claimed. The preparation of catalyst comprises (1) preparing tin(IV) sulfide nanosheet precursor by chemical vapor deposition process; (2) calcining the tin(IV) sulfide nanosheet precursor in atmosphere furnace to obtain white porous tin(IV) oxide nanosheet; and (3) placing the white porous tin(IV) oxide nanosheets in atmosphere furnace, and annealing in mixed atmosphere of argon and hydrogen to obtain black porous tin(IV) oxide nanosheets.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for preparing formic acid by high-efficiency electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: can exhibits excellent performance in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid (the Faraday conversion efficiency of carbon dioxide to formic acid at overpotential of 0.51 V reaches 92.4%; the Faraday conversion efficiency of formic acid can be stabilized at 90 plus minus 2% in the voltage range of -0.60 to -1.10 V (relative to the reversible hydrogen electrode, RHE); and current density of 11.4 plus minus 0.3 mA/cm2 and Faraday conversion of formic acid of 90 plus minus 0.5% can be obtained for at least 10 hours at -1.10 V (relative to the reversible hydrogen electrode, RHE)).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), carrying the chemical vapor deposition in atmosphere furnace, the raw material is high-purity sulfur powder and tin tetrachloride pentahydrate, the mass ratio is 2/1, and substrate is conductive glass (FTO) or carbon paper (CP). In the step (1), the precursor for preparing the tin(IV) sulfide nanosheet has growth temperature of 400-450 degrees C, growth time of 5-20 minutes, and the substrate is placed downstream of the tin tetrachloride pentahydrate powder. In the step (2), the calcination temperature of the tin(IV) sulfide nanosheet precursor in the atmosphere furnace is 450-500 degrees C, the heating rate is 1-5 degrees C/minute, and the heat preservation time is 60-120 minutes. In the step (3), the flow rates of argon and hydrogen in the mixed atmosphere are 60 sccm and 10 sccm, the annealing temperature is 400-450 degrees C, the heating rate is 5-10 degrees C/minute, and the heat preservation time is 30-60 minutes.

PN CN108993180-A; CN108993180-B
TI New grafted polyionic liquid polyimide membrane grafted polyionic liquid material useful for various gas separation e.g. carbon dioxide/nitrogen separation, and carbon dioxide separation from natural gas in natural gas purification.
AU YAN X
   GAO Y
   HE G
   RUAN X
   DAI Y
   ZHENG W
   PAN Y
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2018A3676H
AB    NOVELTY - Grafted polyionic liquid polyimide membrane grafted polyionic liquid material (A) i.e. 2-methyl-6-(4-(2-oxo-3-p-tolylindolin-3-yl)phenyl)-2,6-diazaspiro(3.3)heptane-1,3,5,7-tetraone polymer (I) or (II) is new.
   USE - (A) is useful for various gas separation e.g. carbon dioxide/nitrogen separation, and carbon dioxide separation from natural gas in natural gas purification.
   ADVANTAGE - (A): has mechanical stability and gas separation performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Grafted polyionic liquid polyimide membrane grafted polyionic liquid material (A) i.e. 2-methyl-6-(4-(2-oxo-3-p-tolylindolin-3-yl)phenyl)-2,6-diazaspiro(3.3)heptane-1,3,5,7-tetraone polymer of formulae (I) or (II) is new.
   A = (perfluoropropane-2,2-diyl)dibenzene-4,4',5,5'-tetrayl, propane-2,2-diyldibenzen-4,4',5,5'-tetrayl, biphenyl-4,4',5,5'-tetramin-4,4',5,5'-tetrayl, oxydibenzen-4,4',5,5'-tetrayl, diphenylmethanol-4,4',5,5'-tetrayl or benzophenone-4,4',5,5'-tetrayl;
   x = 0.1-0.8; and
   R = methyl, propyl, benzyl or propanol.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (A).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (A), comprises (i) dissolving indoline-2,3-dione in trifluoromethanesulfonic acid, then adding aniline in ice bath, removing the ice bath, reacting at room temperature for 8 hours to obtain solution, then neutralizing the solution A, extracting by dichloromethane, separating the diamine monomer by gel chromatography under the action of developing agent A and then evaporating under reduced pressure, vacuuming and drying to obtain diamine monomer, where the molar ratio of indoline-2,3-dione to aniline is 1:4, the molar ratio of trifluoromethanesulfonic acid to indoline-2,3-dione is 15-20:1, the solution A is sodium hydroxide and potassium hydroxide solutions, and the developing agent A is a solution of dichloromethane and methanol in a molar ratio of 10:1, (ii) dissolving diamine monomer in solvent A, then adding dianhydride monomer and stirring at room temperature for 10 minutes, then heating to 180 degrees C and continuously stirring for 8 hours, cooling, precipitating using excess precipitating agent A, washing, vacuum drying to obtain polymer, where the solvent A is m-cresol, phenol or nitrobenzene, the precipitating agent A is methanol, ethanol or acetonitrile, and the molar ratio of dianhydride monomer to diamine monomer is 1:1, (iii) dissolving the polyimide polymer in anhydrous solvent B and stirring under inert atmosphere at room temperature, then injecting the halogenated substance into the reaction solution and sealing, heating to 80 degrees C and stirring for 30 hours, cooling to room temperature, precipitating the resulting polymer using excess precipitating agent B, washing, and then vacuum drying to obtain grafted poly(ionic liquid) (PIL) material, where the precipitating agent B is ethyl ether or acetone, the molar ratio of halogenated substance to polyimide polymer is 1.8:1, the solvent B is dimethylformamide, dimethylacetamide (DMAc) or N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP), the halogenated substance is iodomethyl, iodoethyl, benzyl bromide or 3-bromo-1-propanol, and (iv) dissolving lithium bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide (LiTFSI) in solvent B at room temperature, then adding to solvent B containing PIL, continuously stirring at room temperature for 18 hours, precipitating the obtained solution using excess precipitating agent B, washing, and then vacuum drying to obtain (A), where the fluorine-functionalized polyionic liquid material is dissolved in the solvent C, forming a casting solution, and casting the film into a film, the solvent C is DMAc or NMP; the molar ratio of LiTFSI to fluorine functionalized polyionic liquid material is 2:1, and ratio of the casting solution is 3-10%. Preferred Method: In step (i), the drying conditions: the temperature is 80-120 degrees C and the time is more than 12 hours. In step (ii), the drying conditions: the temperature is 30-60 degrees C and the time is more than 12 hours. In step (iii), the drying conditions: the temperature is 40-80 degrees C and the time is more than 8 hours. In step (iv), the drying conditions: the temperature is 40-80 degrees C and the time is more than 24 hours. The drying temperature of the film formation by casting method is 50-80 degrees C, and the time is 24-48 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Manufacture of methane involves performing methanation of gas containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen in presence of catalyst and subjecting remaining to methanation to adjust chemical equilibrium state based on variation of reactive gas.
AU SUZUKI T
   KATAGIRI Y
   SASAKI T
AE HITACHI LTD (HITA-C)
GA 2018677330
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of methane involves performing methanation (m1) of a gas containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen in presence of a methanation catalyst, subjecting remaining product to methanation (m2), such that process (m1) approximate a chemical equilibrium state according to the variation of the reactive gas which flows in into reaction process (m1), and carrying out bypass process which bypasses a portion of reactive gas which flows in into reaction process (p1) and generating flow of resultant into reaction process (m2).
   USE - Manufacture of methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of methane with controllable inlet gas flow volume and output characteristics by effectively recovering carbon dioxide generated in coal gasification, iron manufacture and thermal power generation. The deterioration of the reactor and the catalyst is suppressed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for methane production apparatus, which consists of a methanation reaction device (3), a methanation reaction device (4), and a bypass channel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a structure of the methane production apparatus.
   Methanation reaction device (3,4)
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (6)
   Hydrogen flowmeter (7)
   Compressor (8)
   Mixed gas flowmeter (10)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrogen supplied to process (m1) is hydrogen generated by electrolysis of water using electricity acquired by renewable energy. The methanation catalyst comprises rhenium/manganese-type catalyst, rhodium-type catalyst, nickel-type catalyst, palladium-type catalyst and platinum-type catalyst supported on alumina.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The manufacture of methane further involves gas cooling and/or gas heating process performed between process (m1) and process (m2).
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN WO2018149709-A1
TI Method for removal of oxygen from industrial gas feed, involves heating feed gas, optionally removing siloxanes and silanols, oxidizing feed gas, removing sulfur dioxide and/or hydrogen chloride, injecting reactant(s), and cleaning.
AU EGEBLAD K
   JAKOBSSON N B
   TRANE-RESTRUP R
   ZEUTHEN J H
   STUMMANN T D
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 201865847B
AB    NOVELTY - Removal of oxygen involves heating the feed gas containing volatile organic compounds, sulfur compounds and/or chlorine compounds and optionally siloxanes and silanols, optionally removing siloxanes and silanols from the heated feed gas, oxidizing any or all of the volatile organic compounds to carbon dioxide in the heated gas, oxidizing sulfur-containing compounds to sulfur dioxide and/or oxidizing the chlorine-containing compounds to carbon dioxide and hydrogen chloride, removing sulfur dioxide and/or hydrochloric acid in a wet caustic or hydrogen peroxide scrubber or in a dry scrubber using a caustic sorbent, injecting reactant(s), carrying out a selective catalytic oxygen conversion in suitable reactor(s), and cleaning the resulting oxygen-depleted gas. The reactant(s) is chosen from hydrogen, carbon monoxide, ammonia, urea, methanol, ethanol and dimethyl-ether (DME).
   USE - Method for removal of oxygen from industrial gas feed e.g. landfill gas, digester gas or industrial carbon dioxide off-gas comprising hydrogen sulfide, organic sulfur compounds, carbon dioxide, water, methane, volatile oxygen compounds, optionally siloxanes and silanols (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses a monolithic catalyst which decreases the power consumption for transport of the landfill gas through the cleaning section.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst for the oxygen conversion comprises metal chosen from vanadium, tungsten, chromium, copper, manganese, molybdenum, platinum, palladium, rhodium, and ruthenium in metallic or metal oxide form supported on a carrier chosen from alumina, titania, silica and ceria. Preferred Property: The feed gas is heated to 150-450 degrees C.

PT P
PN WO2018120576-A1; CN108262055-A; EP3593901-A1; EP3593901-A4; CN108262055-B; US2022118430-A1
TI Catalyst for preparing hydrocarbons by one-step            hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, comprises nano-metal            oxide and multi-stage porous zeolite molecular            sieve.
AU SUN Y
   DANG S
   GAO P
   BU X
   LIU Z
   WANG H
   ZHONG L
   QIU M
   SHI Z
   GANG P
   BO X
   DAN Z
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
   SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
   SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
   CHINESE ACAD SCI SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES (CAAR-C)
GA 201852557Y
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises 10-90 %mass nano-metal                oxide and 10-90 %mass multi-stage porous zeolite                molecular sieve.
   USE - Catalyst used for preparing hydrocarbons by                one-step hydrogenation of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent catalytic                performance, reaction stability and high                selectivity for target products including 80% or                less of 2-4C hydrocarbon, 80% of 5C or more                hydrocarbon, and 65% or less aromatic                hydrocarbons.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of catalyst, which involves                dissolving metal salt in a solution to prepare                metal salt solution, dissolving precipitant in a                solution to prepare precipitant solution, dripping                precipitant solution into metal salt solution to                form precipitation mother liquor, aging and washing                precipitation mother liquor, drying and roasting to                obtain metal oxide, mixing metal oxide with                multi-stage porous molecular sieve; and
   (2) usage of catalyst in preparation of                hydrocarbons, which involves hydrogenating carbon                dioxide at 250-600 degrees C for 1-10 hours under                inert gas atmosphere of argon and nitrogen or                reducing gas atmosphere of hydrogen, reacting at                pressure of 0.3-5.0 MPa and temperature of 300-450                degrees C, volume space velocity of 1000-12000                hour-1, and hydrogen/carbon dioxide molar ratio of                1-5.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nano-metal oxide                  is magnesium oxide, zinc oxide, aluminum oxide,                  chromium oxide, yttrium oxide, lanthanum oxide,                  gallium oxide, indium oxide, ferrous oxide,                  manganese oxide, titanium dioxide, zirconium                  dioxide, tin dioxide and/or cerium oxide. The                  multi-stage porous zeolite molecule is screened                  with yttrium, ZSM-5, ZSM-22, ( beta ), MCM-22 and                  SAPO-34 molecular sieves. The metal salt is                  selected from hydrochloride, nitrate, bromide,                  acetate, oxalate and/or ammonium salt. The                  solution is one or two of water and alcohol. The                  concentration of metal salt solution is 0.1-1                  mol/L. The precipitant is selected from aqueous                  ammonia, ammonium carbonate, sodium carbonate,                  sodium hydroxide and sodium acetate. The                  concentration of precipitant solution is 0.1-2                  mol/L. The reaction is carried out at 10-40                  degrees C. The pH of precipitation mother liquor                  is 7-11. Aging is carried out at 60-100 degrees C                  for 0.1-10 hours. Drying is carried out at 60-120                  degrees C for 6-24 hours. Roasting is carried out                  at 250-600 degrees C for 1-8 hours. Preferred                  Process: Mixing is either mechanical mixing,                  grinding mixing or ball milling mixing. The                  mechanical mixing is carried out by tableting and                  sieving the metal oxide with multi-stage porous                  molecular sieve to obtain nano-particles of 20-80                  mesh, and loading into a glass container to                  enable uniform mixing by shaking. The grinding                  mixing is carried out by grinding metal oxide and                  multi-stage porous molecular sieve in an agate                  mortar for 0.1-4 hours, pressing and sieving. The                  ball milling mixing is carried out by                  ball-milling metal oxide and multi-stage porous                  molecular sieve in a ball mill for 0.1-24 hours,                  pressing and sieving.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC E10-J02D; E11-D02; E11-F03; E12-A10; E31-P02A; E31-U; E34-B01; E34-C01; E34-E01; E34-E02A; E35; H04-E05; H04-E08; H04-F02E; H04-F05; H04-F06; N01-B; N01-C02; N02-A01; N03-A01; N03-A02A; N03-B01A; N03-B02; N03-D01; N03-E; N03-F01; N03-G01; N03-G03; N03-G04; N06-A; N06-C09; N06-E01; N07-B02; N07-D03

PN CN108018122-A
TI Extracting peony essential oil involves picking fresh peony flower, taking supercritical carbon dioxide fluid as extraction solvent, extracting to prepare peony extract, molecular distilling, cooling, and adding auxiliary material.
AU XIE Z
AE GUIZHOU LONGLI ZEYANG ECOLOGICAL TECHNOL (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 201838511T
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting peony essential oil involves picking fresh peony flower, taking supercritical carbon dioxide fluid as extraction solvent, extracting to prepare peony extract, molecular distilling, cooling, and adding auxiliary material. The auxiliary material is mixture of purslane extracting liquid and honey.
   USE - Method for extracting peony essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has no solvent pollution, naturally separates product from carbon dioxide, keeps peony natural flavor, and improves effective component extraction rate and product quality. The oil prevents allergy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting peony essential oil involves picking fresh peony flower, taking supercritical carbon dioxide fluid as extraction solvent, extracting at 50 degrees C for 3 hours at extraction pressure of 25 MPa, separator I pressure of 16 MPa at 54 degrees C and separator II pressure of 8 MPa at 19 degrees C to prepare peony extract, molecular distilling at 120-130 degrees C at feeding temperature of 50-70 degrees C, vacuum degree of 35-55 Pa, peony extract rate of 200-300 ml/hour and film scraping speed of 370-390 revolutions/minute (rpm), cooling to 23 degrees C, and adding auxiliary material. The auxiliary material is mixture of purslane extracting liquid and honey.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weight ratio of purslane extracting liquid and honey is 1:0.9-1.1.

PT P
PN CN108247179-A; CN108247179-B
TI Detecting I-shaped groove carbon dioxide welding            deviation comprises e.g. collecting the image of the            molten pool, calculating the average gray value,            setting different thresholds and calculating difference            between center coordinates.
AU ZHU Y
   GAO S
   WU Z
   LI K
   LI Y
AE UNIV TAIYUAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNTK-C)
   UNIV TAIYUAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNTK-C)
GA 201854597F
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting I-shaped groove carbon dioxide                welding deviation comprises e.g. (i) collecting the                image of the molten pool; (iii) calculating the                average gray value AVG of the ROI image when the                average gray value AVG of the ROI image is greater                than or equal to to 50, discarding image, and                returning to step (i) and when the average gray                value AVG of the ROI image is less than 50 carrying                out next step; (iv) setting different thresholds T0                and dividing the ROI image into different                background regions C1 and target regions C2; and                (v) scanning the contour of front area of the                molten pool and the contour of the welding wire                from bottom to top, scanning to bottommost vertex                of the contour of front area of molten pool,                marking point as center coordinate of welding seam,                scanning contour of welding wire and calculating                difference between center coordinates of welding                seam and center coordinate of welding gun and                converting proportional relationship of distance                values into welding deviation according to the                pixel.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting I-shaped                groove carbon dioxide welding deviation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the welding deviation                detecting precision and effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detecting I-shaped groove carbon dioxide                welding deviation comprises (i) collecting the                image of the molten pool after the welding is                stabilized by image acquisition system; (ii)                selecting ROI area of the acquired image as the ROI                image, where ROI area comprises only the front area                of the molten pool and the area where welding wire                is located; (iii) calculating the average gray                value AVG of the ROI image when the average gray                value AVG of the ROI image is greater than or equal                to to 50, discarding image, and returning to step                (i) and when the average gray value AVG of the ROI                image is less than 50 carrying out next step; (iv)                setting different thresholds T0, dividing the ROI                image into different background regions C1 and                target regions C2, where inter-class variance and                intra-class variance between the background region                and the target region of the ROI image is                calculated to exhibit the maximum inter-class                variance, where threshold value in the intra-class                variance ratio beta is the optimal threshold Th                which is binarized according to the optimal                threshold Th, and extracting contour of the front                region of the molten pool and the contour of the                wire; and (v) scanning the contour of the front                area of the molten pool and the contour of the                welding wire from bottom to top, scanning to the                bottommost vertex of the contour of the front area                of the molten pool, marking the point as the center                coordinate of the welding seam, scanning the                contour of the welding wire when the horizontal                distance is equal to the actual diameter of the                wire, recording coordinates of the wire contour at                the middle point of the row as the center                coordinates of the welding torch, calculating                difference between the center coordinates of the                welding seam and the center coordinate of the                welding gun and converting proportional                relationship of the distance values into welding                deviation according to the pixel. An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is also included for I-shaped groove carbon                dioxide welding deviation detecting device,                comprising a welding system, image acquisition                system, a voltage and current acquisition system, a                composite filter system and a control unit, where                image acquisition system is installed in front of                the T-axis of the robot of the welding system, and                the installation direction is consistent with the                welding advance direction and sending to the                control unit, the composite filter system is                disposed at a front end of the image acquisition                system; the voltage and current acquisition system                is to detect a welding voltage and current value of                the welding system, and send the same to the                control unit, the control unit is configured to                control the image acquisition system to perform                image acquisition according to the measured values                of the voltage and current acquisition system,                control unit is further configured to perform                calculation processing on the image information                collected by the image acquisition system to obtain                welding deviation, the calculation processing step                of the control unit comprises selecting an ROI area                of the acquired image as an ROI image, where ROI                area comprises pre-melting area and a region where                the welding wire is located, calculating an average                gray value AVG of the ROI image when the ROI image                average gray value AVG is greater than or equal to                50, the image is discarded when the average gray                value AVG of the ROI image is less than 50, setting                different thresholds T0, ROI image is divided into                different background regions C1 and target regions                C2, and the inter-class variance and intra-class                variance between the background region and the                target region of the ROI image are calculated to                appear the largest inter-class, the threshold value                of the variance and the intra-class variance ratio                beta is the optimal threshold Th which is binarized                according to the optimal threshold Th, extracts the                contour of the front region of the molten pool and                the contour of the welding wire, the contour of the                front region of the molten pool and the welding                wire Performing a scan from bottom to top, scanning                to the bottommost vertex of the contour of the                front region of the molten pool, marking the point                as the center coordinate of the welded seam;                scanning the wire when the horizontal distance of                the contour of the wire is equal to the actual                diameter of the wire coordinates of the contour at                the middle point of the line are recorded as the                center coordinates of the welding torch, the                difference between the center coordinates of the                welding seam and the center coordinate of the                welding gun is calculated, and converted into the                welding deviation according to the proportional                relationship between the pixel and the actual                distance value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) further                  comprises detecting the welding voltage and                  current value of the welding system, and                  binarizing the welding voltage of the welding                  system when the welding voltage is lower than                  10V, converting into a low level when the voltage                  is higher than 10V converting into a high level                  and when the voltage value after the binarization                  processing is at a low level for 0.001 seconds                  and controlling image acquisition system to delay                  the image acquisition by 0.003 seconds. The step                  (iii) comprises calculating total average gray                  value AVG of the ROI image which represents the                  probability of occurrence of the gray level i, fi                  represents the number of all pixels whose gray                  level is i, and M and N respectively represents                  horizontal and vertical pixels of the image                  number and L represents gray level. The step (iv)                  comprises calculating formulas of the inter-class                  variance and the intra-class variance between the                  background region and the target region of the                  ROI image where omega 1 represents the                  probability of occurrence of the C1 portion of                  the background region, omega 2 represents the                  probability of occurrence of the portion of the                  target region C2, and mu 1 represents the                  background region, where average gray level of                  the C1 portion, mu 2 represents the average gray                  level of the portion of the target region C2,                  delta 1 represents the partial gray scale                  variance of the background region C1, delta 2                  represents the partial gray scale variance of the                  target region C2, and f(x, y) represents the                  image IMx N gray value at (x, y). The formula of                  the binarization process in step (iv) as                  specified.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The voltage and                  current acquisition system includes a Hall sensor                  and a peripheral circuit, the image acquisition                  system comprises CMOS industrial camera and an                  external camera trigger circuit, and the external                  camera trigger circuit is used for                  analog-to-digital conversion of the voltage                  collected by the voltage and current acquisition                  system and after the output voltage of the                  peripheral circuit is low level and lasts for                  0.001 seconds and CMOS industrial camera is                  triggered to delay the image acquisition by 0.003                  seconds. The control unit is coupled to the                  welding system for transmitting the calculated                  welding deviation to the welding system to                  achieve automatic correction of the welding                  process. The welding system comprises arc welding                  robot, a welding power source, and a welding                  stroke control table and composite filter system                  comprises 630 plus minus 10 nm filter, 10%                  dimming piece, and a splash-proof glass plate for                  welding.

A processing unit                (15) removes the carbon dioxide gas from the                carrier gas before oxidizing the sample. The                processing unit has a binder (16) for binding the                carbon dioxide gas from the carrier gas. A                high-temperature furnace (17) evaporates and/or                oxidizes sample at a high temperature. An injection                unit (18) injects the sample into high-temperature                furnace. A condensation unit (19) condenses the                water vapor produced during the evaporation and/or                oxidation of sample to form a condensate. The                condensation unit has an outlet (20) for condensate                to a humidification unit (21). The humidification                unit humidifies the carrier gas by the condensate.                The analysis unit determines the carbon content of                the sample based on oxidation of sample formed                carbon dioxide gas.

   ADVANTAGE - The binder is easily moistened. The vessel of                the humidification unit is provided with drain so                that excess condensate is drained from the vessel.                The overflowing of the vessel is avoided.

   ELLIS B R
   ZHANG D
AE UNIV MICHIGAN (UNMI-C)
GA 2021B61504
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves carbonating a fly ash including greater than or equal to 25 wt.% calcium oxide and having a water content of 12% to 18% by weight of water by exposing the fly ash to a first gas stream including carbon dioxide, so that the fly ash has greater than or equal to 4% by weight of carbon dioxide uptake on a dry basis to form a carbonated fly ash. A steel slag including greater than or equal to 40 wt.% calcium oxide and having a water content of 12% to 18% by weight of water is carbonated by exposing the steel slag to a second gas stream including carbon dioxide, so that the steel slag has greater than or equal to 5% by weight of carbon dioxide uptake on a dry basis to form a carbonated steel slag. The carbonated fly ash and the carbonated steel slag are suitable for use as engineered cementitious composite precursors in an engineered cementitious composite composition that further comprises Portland cement, a polymeric fiber, and a superplasticizer.

PT P
PN IN202111030753-A; IN391874-B
TI Preparing nickel-iron bimodal catalyst for syngas production from carbon dioxide reforming of methane by preparing solution of metal nitrates of nickel, iron, lanthanum and aluminum, adding metal salt solution, and co-precipitating.
AU SHAH M
   MONDAL P
AE INDIAN INST TECHNOLOGY ROORKEE (INTE-Non-standard)
GA 2021B1116W
AB    NOVELTY - A nickel (Ni)-iron (Fe) bimodal catalyst (NiFe/La2O3-Al2O3) for syngas production from carbon dioxide reforming of methane is prepared by (a) preparing 0.4 M solution of metal nitrates of Ni, Fe, lanthanum (La), and aluminum (Al) in water, (b) preparing a precipitating solution of 1 M sodium hydroxide (NaOH) and 0.5 M sodium carbonate (Na2CO3) solution in water, (c) adding metal salt solution at the rate of 2 mL/minute into a flask that contains 50 mL water at 65 degrees C under continuous stirring of 600 rpm, (d) co-precipitating the metal salt solution by simultaneous addition of precipitating agent through a pump to maintain the solution pH at 10, (e) filtering precipitates and washing thoroughly with double distilled water, and (f) drying the catalyst material at 105 degrees C for 24 hours and calcining at 800 degrees C for 6 hours in airflow to obtain Ni-Fe/La2O3-Al2O3 catalyst.
   USE - Preparation of nickel (Ni)-iron (Fe) bimodal catalyst (NiFe/La2O3-Al2O3) for syngas production from carbon dioxide reforming of methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high activity and long-term stability to mitigate the catalyst deactivation problem for dry reforming of methane (DRM) technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for process for dry reforming of methane with carbon dioxide using Ni-Fe/La2O3-Al2O3 catalyst which involves reducing the catalyst at 10-20% hydrogen (H2)/argon (Ar) mixture at 800 degrees C for 1-2 hours and contacting the reduced catalyst with the feed gas mixture of methane, carbon dioxide, and nitrogen which have a molar ratio of 1:1:2 with a space velocity of 1x 104-2.4x 105 mL/g-hour over the catalyst at 500-800 degrees C at atmospheric pressure to obtain syngas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amount of Ni is 5-18 wt.%. The amount of Fe in the catalyst is 2-10 wt.%. The amount of La in the catalyst is 5-25 wt.%. The Ni-Fe/La2O3-Al2O3 catalyst is derived from hydrotalcite-like structure by oxidation of precursor in the air at 800 degrees C. The conversion of methane is 60-90%, and conversion of carbon dioxide is 90-98% without any noticeable deactivation until 100 hours. The synthesis gas is obtained from methane and carbon dioxide with hydrogen (H2) to carbon monoxide (CO) ratio of 0.6-0.9. Preferred Parameters: The Ni-Fe/La2O3-Al2O3 catalyst have a bimodal pore size distribution, primary pore centered at 4 nm and secondary pore centered at 11 nm and the average pore diameter is 9 nm.

PN WO2020012268-A1; US2021284616-A1; IN202117001128-A; US11453653-B2
TI Production of ethylene oxide used as raw material,            involves reacting ethylene with gas comprising oxygen,            processing reactor effluent to produce product stream            containing ethylene oxide, and flowing recycle gas            stream.
AU DUAN J
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 2020065504
AB    NOVELTY - Production of ethylene oxide involves reacting                (201) ethylene with a gas comprising oxygen in a                reactor to produce a reactor effluent comprising                ethylene oxide, carbon dioxide, unreacted ethylene,                oxygen, methane, and argon, processing (202) the                reactor effluent to produce (a) a product stream                comprising 5-15 wt.% ethylene oxide, (b) a                reabsorber overhead stream comprising 20-50 wt.%                ethylene, 10-30 wt.% methane, 1-10 wt.% argon, and                20-50 wt.% carbon dioxide, and (c) a carbonate                flash gas stream comprising 10-50 wt.% carbon                dioxide, 5-45 wt.% ethylene, 5-10 wt.% methane, and                40-60% water, combining (205) the reabsorber                overhead stream and the carbonate flash gas stream                to produce a reclaim gas stream, and removing                carbon dioxide and argon from the reclaim gas                stream, by a membrane separation unit to form a                recycle gas stream comprising ethylene and methane                and a gas stream comprising the removed carbon                dioxide and the removed argon, and flowing the                recycle gas stream to the reactor.
   USE - Production of ethylene oxide used as raw                material for producing ethylene glycol. Uses                include but are not limited to monoethylene glycol                such as antifreeze and coolant for engine and                intermediate for producing polyester fiber and                plastic bottle, diethylene glycol for producing                polyurethane, plasticizer, and organic solvent,                triethylene glycol used as plasticizer and                moisture-retaining agent, and polyethylene glycols                used in manufacture of perfume, cosmetic,                lubricant, and plasticizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of ethylene                oxide with high-purity.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of production                of ethylene oxide.
   Reacting ethylene with gas (201)
   Processing reactor effluent (202)
   Processing aqueous stream (203)
   Processing ethylene oxide absorber overhead                stream (204)
   Combining reabsorber overhead stream                (205)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The reacting step is                  performed in the presence of catalyst comprising                  silver supported on alumina, silica, or                  silica-alumina. The reacting step is performed at                  reaction temperature of 200-300 degrees C, and                  reaction pressure of 10-35 bar. The method                  further involves processing the reactor effluent                  in ethylene oxide absorber to produce (A) aqueous                  stream comprising 2-5 wt.% ethylene oxide, 2-5                  wt.% monoethylene glycol and (B) ethylene oxide                  absorber overhead stream comprising 2-15 wt.%                  carbon dioxide, 20-35 wt.% ethylene, and 30-45                  wt.% methane, processing (203) the aqueous stream                  to produce product stream comprising 5-15 wt.%                  ethylene oxide, reabsorber overhead stream                  comprising 20-50 wt.% ethylene, 10-30 wt.%                  methane, 1-10 wt.% argon, and 20-50 wt.% carbon                  dioxide, and processing (204) the ethylene oxide                  absorber overhead stream in processing unit to                  produce carbonate flash gas stream comprising                  10-50 wt.% carbon dioxide, 5-45 wt.% ethylene,                  5-10 wt.% methane, and 40-60 wt.% water, and                  combining process involves flowing the reabsorber                  overhead stream and the carbonate flash gas                  stream to form a combined stream, removing water                  from the combined stream in a knockout drum to                  form de-watered stream, and compressing the                  de-watered stream to form the reclaim gas stream.                  In the step (203), the aqueous stream is                  processed in a reabsorber comprising packed                  column comprising water absorbent. The method is                  performed using separation unit comprising one or                  more membrane module, housing pressure vessel,                  and compressor. Preferred Components: The                  processing unit comprises carbon dioxide absorber                  configured to separate the ethylene oxide                  absorber overhead stream to form a recycle stream                  (I) comprising primarily ethylene and methane,                  collectively and a carbon dioxide rich stream                  comprising primarily carbon dioxide, ethylene,                  methane, and water, flasher in fluid                  communication with the carbon dioxide absorber,                  in which flasher is configured to separate the                  carbon dioxide rich stream to produce the                  carbonate flash gas stream and a residual stream,                  and carbon dioxide stripper in fluid                  communication with the flasher and the carbon                  dioxide absorber, in which carbon dioxide                  stripper is configured to process the residual                  stream and to produce a lean carbonate solution                  to flow to the carbon dioxide absorber. The                  recycle stream (I) is recycled to the reactor.                  The recycle gas stream is flowed to the carbon                  dioxide absorber, and/or ethylene oxide absorber.                  The membrane module comprises cellulose                  acetate/diacetate/triacetate membrane, polyimide                  membrane, perfluoro polymer membrane, polysulfone                  membrane, polycarbonate membrane, or                  poly(phenylene oxide) membrane. The product                  stream is fed to ethylene glycol production                  reactor. Preferred Properties: The reclaim gas                  stream has pressure of 15-50 bar after                  compressing. Preferred Composition: The product                  stream further comprises 85-95 wt.% water, and                  less than 1 wt.% carbon dioxide, and ethylene.                  The product stream is further flowed to                  purification column to form a high-purity                  ethylene oxide product comprising 95 wt.% or more                  ethylene oxide.

PT P
PN US2019386105-A1; WO2019246224-A1; US11211458-B2
TI Device used to convert electromagnetic radiation into charges that are collected and used to promote chemical reactions comprising rare earth ion containing material containing rare earth ions within e.g. crystallites and/or nanoparticles.
AU GRIMES C A
   KREISLER K
   GRIMES C
AE FLUX PHOTON CORP (FLUX-Non-standard)
   FLUX PHOTON CORP (FLUX-Non-standard)
GA 2019A5980D
AB    NOVELTY - Device (10) for conversion of electromagnetic radiation into charges that are collected and used to promote chemical reactions comprising a rare earth ion containing material, is claimed. The rare earth ion containing material comprises rare earth ions within crystallites, quantum-dots, and/or nanoparticles. The rare earth ion containing material is intercalated within a one-dimensional nanoarchitecture.
   USE - The device is useful for: conversion of electromagnetic radiation into charges that are collected and promoting chemical reactions; and photocatalytically converting carbon dioxide into reaction products comprising hydrocarbons and hydrocarbon-containing products, hydrogen and hydrogen-containing products, carbon monoxide and/or carbon-containing products (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device: has ability to convert broad-spectrum electromagnetic radiation, including microwave, millimeter wave, infrared, visible, and UV, into charge carriers; utilizes sunlight for transformation of carbon dioxide and water vapor to hydrocarbon fuels e.g. methane, ethane, or even higher order hydrocarbons in which the solar fuels reduce atmospheric carbon dioxide emissions; and provides a viable means for the storage and transport of solar energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) photocatalytically converting carbon dioxide into reaction products comprising hydrocarbons and hydrocarbon-containing products, hydrogen and hydrogen-containing products, carbon monoxide and/or carbon-containing products, comprising exposing a reactant gas comprising carbon dioxide to the device and electromagnetic radiation to generate the reaction products; and
   (2) photocatalytically converting a first gas into reaction products comprising other gases, comprising exposing a reactant gas comprised at least in part of the first gas to the device and electromagnetic radiation to generate the reaction products.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic representation of the device.
   Device (10)
   Nanoparticles containing rare earth ions (13)
   Nanowire (14)
   Electrically insulating (organic or inorganic) shell (15)
   Substrate (16)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crystallites, quantum dots, or nanoparticles containing the rare earth ions (13) are bound within an organic host matrix comprising organic ligands bearing aromatic chromophores. The rare earth ions of the rare earth ion containing material are bound within an inorganic host matrix, where the matrix comprises halides, oxides, oxyhalides, or oxysulfides. The radiation absorbed by the rare earth ion containing material possesses a wavelength of 0.01 mu m to 300 cm. The crystallites, quantum dots, or nanoparticles are comprised of following elements lanthanum, cerium, praseodymium, neodymium, promethium, samarium, europium, gadolinium, terbium, dysprosium, holmium, erbium, thulium, ytterbium, lutetium, scandium and/or yttrium. The one dimensional material nanoarchitecture is an ordered disordered array of charge-transporting architectural features including nanowires, nanotubes, nanorods and/or nanofeathers. The one-dimensional nanoarchitecture comprises multiple elements including nanowires, nanorods, nanotubes or nanofeathers, where the feature to feature spacing of the elements is less than 4 mu m and length of the elements is more than 50 nm to less than 100 mm. The one-dimensional material nanoarchitecture is made of semiconductors. The charge generation capabilities of the rare earth ion containing crystallites, quantum dots, or nanoparticles are adjusted through appropriate elemental doping. The rare earth ion containing nanoparticles or quantum dots are intercalated within the one-dimensional material nanoarchitecture using sol-gel, potential-assisted sol-gel, electrodeposition, photodeposition, centrifuge-assisted deposition, microemulsion, spin-coating, atomic layer deposition, hydrothermal synthesis, microwave-assisted hydrothermal synthesis, or dip-coating techniques. The crystallites, quantum dots, or nanoparticles containing rare earth ions are in contact with compounds including graphene, graphene oxide, or boron nitride, by which charge separation is facilitated. The crystallites, quantum dots, or nanoparticles of at least one co-catalyst is deposited on at least one surface of the device, where the co-catalyst is graphene, boron nitride, silver, arsenic, gold, bismuth, cadmium, cobalt, copper, cupric oxide, cuprous oxide, iron, gallium, germanium, indium, iridium, niobium, lead, palladium, platinum, rhodium, antimony, silicon, tin, tantalum, thallium, tungsten and/or zinc. The rare earth ion containing crystallites, quantum dots, or nanoparticles contain nonmetals including boron, carbon, potassium, calcium, sodium, fluorine, iodine, phosphorus and/or sulfur. The electromagnetic radiation comprises UV, visible, infrared radiation, millimeter wave and/or microwave. The rare earth ion containing photocatalytic device is in the form of a flow-through membrane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The one-dimensional structure is: a p-type semiconductor, and in contact with an underlying p-type semiconductor substrate such that holes within the one-dimensional structure may pass into the substrate and thus react with gas molecules at a distance from the one-dimensional structures; or a n-type semiconductor, and in contact with an underlying n-type semiconductor substrate such that holes within the one-dimensional structure may pass into the substrate and thus react with gas molecules at a distance from the one-dimensional structures. The substrate is a conductor.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. TI Supercritical carbon dioxide power plant comprises a recuperator, where an end is arranged between the pump, first heat exchanger and a second heat exchanger and first transfer line is connected to the first heat exchanger.
AU SUNG H C
   KIM S H
   CHA S H
   KIMSANGHYUN
AE DOOSAN HEAVY IND & CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
GA 201941451Y
AB    NOVELTY - The supercritical carbon dioxide power plant comprises a recuperator (130). An end is arranged between the pump, first heat exchanger (120) and a second heat exchanger (125). First transfer line (140) is connected to the first heat exchanger. First control valve (150) is installed at the first transfer line. First pressure sensor (160) is measured the pressure of the working fluid. Second transfer line (141) is branched from the first transfer line. Second control valve (151) is formed in the second transfer line. Second pressure sensor (161) is measured the pressure of the working fluid. Third transfer line (142) is connected to the recuperator. Fourth transfer line (143) is supplied the working fluid. A turbine (180) is produced electricity by using the working fluid passing the second heat exchanger. A return line (145) is connected to the pump. A condenser (185) is formed in the return line. A control unit (190) is controlled the operation of the pump.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The power consumption required for compression and circulation of the fluid is minimized with the miniaturization of the instrument.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a control method for supercritical carbon dioxide power plant.

AB    NOVELTY - The factory (1) has multiple growing stands                (20) that are separately installed within a culture                layer (7) segmented through a layer compartment                supporting frame (10). Multiple planting beds (30)                are formed with a planting layer that is                partitioned off within each growing stand. A                nutrient solution cyclic supply part circularly                provides nutrient solution required for plant                growth. An inter-layer air circulation part forms                inter-layer circular air current between culture                layers segmented through a bottom surface of the                layer compartment supporting frame. An                artificiality illuminating unit irradiates                predetermined light quantity in plant planted to a                material bed through an artificial light source                equipped in a material layer. A carbon dioxide gas                supply part is installed in an upper side of the                material layer for closely supplying carbon dioxide                gas to a plant.

The                factory realizes carbon dioxide densification and                light quantity enlargement so as to increase air                velocity to control photosynthetic active                radiation, so that replacement of the plant can be                facilitated, and cost for manufacturing the plant                can be reduced.

PN CN108915810-A; CN208650933-U
TI Working substance exchanging device for non-rotating device part ultra-critical carbon dioxide system, has first main pipe connected with second main pipe, and high-pressure storage tank inlet pipe provided with check valve.
AU HAN W
   WANG Y
   YAO M
   LI H
   YANG Y
   WU S
   ZHANG Y
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 201898323W
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a heat source ultra-critical carbon dioxide system (1) whose first outlet is connected with an inlet of a high-pressure turbine through a first main pipe (2-1). An outlet of the high-pressure turbine is connected with a first inlet of the heat source ultra-critical carbon dioxide system through a second main pipe (2-2). An outlet of a low-pressure turbine is connected with an inlet of a high-temperature regenerator (3) by a fourth main pipe (2-4). Outlets of a low-temperature regenerator (4) are divided into two branches that are connected in a parallel manner. An outlet of a main compressor (17) is connected with the low-temperature regenerator by a fifth main pipe. A sixth main pipe (2-3) is connected with a seventh main pipe (2-4). A high-pressure storage tank inlet pipe (14) is provided with a check valve (15-3).
   USE - Working substance exchanging device for a non-rotating device part ultra-critical carbon dioxide system.
   ADVANTAGE - The device reduces unfavorable difficulty of replacing dead air in the high-temperature regenerator, the low-temperature regenerator, a pre-cooler and a heat source, and reaches purity of the working medium in the non-rotating device with 99.9%.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a working substance exchanging device.
   Heat source ultra-critical carbon dioxide system (1)
   Main pipes (2-1-2-4)
   High-temperature regenerator (3)
   Low-temperature regenerator (4)
   High-pressure storage tank inlet pipe (14)
   Check valve (15-3)
   Main compressor (17)
DC Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P))
MC Q51-C
IP F01K-025/10; F01K-007/32

PT P
PN CN108030013-A
TI Selenium-enriched fruit powder, prepared by e.g. peeling and rinsing fresh apple, soaking in saline water, washing brine-soaked apple, soaking apple in lemon juice and placing apple in supercritical carbon dioxide fluid treatment system.
AU LI X
   LI G
AE DANYANG SANMU FRUIT CO LTD (DANY-Non-standard)
GA 201839534U
AB    NOVELTY - Selenium-enriched fruit powder, is claimed. The preparation of fruit powder comprises e.g. peeling and rinsing fresh apples, soaking in saline water, and using clear water, washing the brine-soaked apple, soaking the apple in lemon juice, placing the apple in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid treatment system, extracting the water from the apple by using the supercritical fluid of carbon dioxide to obtain dried apple, and placing the dried apple in a grinder and grinding into a powder to obtain the apple powder, taking juicy fresh lemon and adding ginger juice and honey.
   USE - Used as selenium-enriched fruit powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The fruit powder has tooth whitening function; and facilitates absorption while drinking.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Selenium-enriched fruit powder, is claimed. The preparation of fruit powder comprises (i) peeling and rinsing fresh apples, soaking in 3% saline water, and using clear water, washing the brine-soaked apple, soaking the apple in lemon juice, placing the apple in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid treatment system, extracting the water from the apple by using the supercritical fluid of carbon dioxide to obtain dried apple, and placing the dried apple in a grinder and grinding into a powder to obtain the apple powder; (ii) taking juicy fresh lemon, adding 15 wt.% ginger juice and 15 wt.% honey, and obtaining lemon powder in a solid state after low-temperature concentration of lemon juice, grinding into a powder by a grinder to obtain lemon powder; and (iii) taking apple powder, lemon powder, selenium yeast powder, baking soda powder, mixing uniformly and dispersing by a high-speed disperser.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: In the step (iii), components are: 60-100 pts. wt. apple powder, 1-3 pts. wt. lemon powder, 10-15 pts. wt. selenium yeast powder and 1-3 pts. wt. baking soda flour. Preferred Method: In the step (i), soaking the salt water for 3-5 minutes. The soaking time of the lemon juice is 3-5 minutes.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D03-H01G1; D03-P; D05-A04D
IP A23L-019/00; A23L-033/105; A23L-033/16; A23L-005/20
PD CN108030013-A   15 May 2018   A23L-019/00   201838   Pages: 5   Chinese

      CN102813117-A   LIAO Y (LIAO-Individual)   LIAO Y
      CN106962842-A   INST AGRO FOOD SCI & TECHNOLOGY CHINESE (CAGS)   BI J, ZHOU M, CHEN Q, LIU X, XUE Y, WU X, LV J
