PT P
PN WO2022157309-A1
TI Kit for improving food shelf life, comprises            sheet-like element component having coating layer            comprising particulate filler comprising            surface-reacted calcium carbonate, polymeric binder,            and oxygen scavenger, substrate layer, and alkaline            component comprising base.
AU MONNARD F W
   ROECKER B
   WALLER S
   WEIHS J P
   YILDIRIM S
   MENARD R
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 202295617J
AB    NOVELTY - Kit comprises (A) a sheet-like element                component having (A1) a coating layer comprising: a                particulate filler, where the particulate filler                comprises a surface-reacted calcium carbonate in an                amount of at least 50 wt.%, based on the total                amount of the particulate filler, and the                surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction                product of natural ground calcium carbonate or                precipitated calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide                and one or more hydronium ion donors, where the                carbon dioxide is formed in situ by the hydronium                ion donors treatment and/or is supplied from an                external source; a polymeric binder; and at least                one oxygen scavenger in an amount of 25-70 wt.%,                based on the total dry weight of the coating layer,                where the at least one oxygen scavenger is a                compound having at least one phenyl ring bearing at                least two phenolic hydroxyl groups, and (A2) a                substrate layer, and (B) an alkaline component                comprising a base.
   USE - The kit is used for improving food shelf life.                The kit or the activated sheet-like element is used                in a food packaging for a packaged food, and for                prolonging food shelf life (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The activated sheet-like element scavenges                oxygen and, thus, prevents or retards food spoilage                and/or increases the shelf life of the foodstuff.                The sheet-like element can be easily dispensed and                provided at the point of use. The kit can provide a                food-safe oxygen scavenger, which efficiently                reduces the amount of oxygen in a food packaging,                preferably also at low relative humidity and/or in                the presence of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Kit for improving food shelf life, comprises                (A) a sheet-like element component having (A1) a                coating layer comprising: a particulate filler in                an amount of 25-70 wt.%, based on the total dry                weight of the coating layer, where the particulate                filler comprises a surface-reacted calcium                carbonate in an amount of at least 50 wt.%, based                on the total amount of the particulate filler, and                the surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction                product of natural ground calcium carbonate or                precipitated calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide                and one or more hydronium ion donors, where the                carbon dioxide is formed in situ by the hydronium                ion donors treatment and/or is supplied from an                external source, and the surface-reacted calcium                carbonate has a specific surface area of 20-200                m2/g, as measured by the Brunauer-Emmett-Teller                (BET) method; a polymeric binder in an amount of                5-25 wt.%, based on the total dry weight of the                coating layer; and at least one oxygen scavenger in                an amount of 25-70 wt.%, based on the total dry                weight of the coating layer, where the at least one                oxygen scavenger is a compound having at least one                phenyl ring bearing at least two phenolic hydroxyl                groups and at least one group R, and two of the at                least two phenolic hydroxyl groups are arranged on                the at least one phenyl ring in an ortho or para                fashion relative to each other, and where R is                chosen from a hydrogen atom, a hydroxyl group, an                alkoxy group, an aryloxy group, an amino group, an                alkyl group, an aryl group and a -Y-R1 group,                preferably where R is a -Y-R1 group, where Y is                chosen from a direct bond, a linear or branched                alkylene group having 1-6C, and a -CH=CH- group,                preferably Y is a direct bond, and R1 is an                alkoxycarbonyl group, an aryloxycarboxyl group or                an essentially fully deprotonated carboxyl group,                and (A2) a substrate layer, and (B) an alkaline                component comprising a base having a pKb value of 6                or lower. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   an activated sheet-like element formed from                the kit by adding the alkaline component to the                coating layer of the sheet-like element component,                where the activated sheet-like element comprises                reaction products of the at least one oxygen                scavenger with the base, where the alkaline                component is added in an amount such that the base                is added in an amount of at least 0.01 molar                equivalents, preferably at least 0.1 molar                equivalents, based on the molar amount of the                oxygen scavenger, and/or the alkaline component is                added in an amount of 10-70 wt.%, preferably 35-60                wt.%, based on the total weight of the coating                layer;
   a process for the manufacture of a kit for                improving food shelf life;
   a process for the manufacture of a sheet-like                element component;
   a process for activating the sheet-like                element of the kit, comprising (j) mixing the                alkaline component with water to obtain an aqueous                alkaline component comprising the base and water,                and (k) applying the aqueous alkaline component                onto at least a portion of the surface of the                coating layer, where the alkaline component is                added in an amount such that the base is added in                an amount of at least 0.01 molar equivalents,                preferably at least 0.1 molar equivalents, based on                the molar amount of the oxygen scavenger, and/or                the alkaline component is added in an amount of                10-70 wt.%, preferably 35-60 wt.%, based on the                total weight of the coating layer, and/or                application step (k) is performed by inkjet                printing, spraying, coating, and/or dripping;
   a supply device comprising the activated                sheet-like element, where the supply device                protects the activated sheet-like element from                oxygen and preferably comprises a roll, a stack, a                magazine, or a packaging, such as a box;
   a food packaging comprising the activated                sheet-like element, where the coating layer is                present within the food packaging; and
   a packaged food comprising a food and a food                packaging having an activated sheet-like element,                where the coating layer of the activated sheet-like                element is present within the food packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The food packing                  comprises a food or foodstuff. The food is chosen                  from liquid and solid food, preferably which is                  an oxygen sensitive food, including raw and                  processed meat, poultry, beef, pork, ham,                  sausage, dried meat, raw and processed fish,                  dairy products, bakery products, snacks, nuts and                  oil seeds, vegetables, sweets, ready-to-eat foods                  and beverages, especially orange juice.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The particulate                  filler comprises the surface-reacted calcium                  carbonate in an amount of at least 70 wt.%,                  preferably at least 90 wt.%, based on the total                  weight of the at least one particulate filler,                  and the particulate filler consists of the                  surface-reacted calcium carbonate, and where any                  optionally present further particulate filler                  material is chosen from dolomite, ground calcium                  carbonate, precipitated calcium carbonate,                  magnesium hydroxide, talc, gypsum, titanium                  dioxide, kaolin, silicate, mica, barium sulphate,                  calcined clay, non-calcined (hydrous) clay,                  bentonite and their mixtures, preferably chosen                  from ground calcium carbonate, precipitated                  calcium carbonate and their mixtures, where the                  particulate filler consists of the optionally                  present further particulate filler material and                  the surface-reacted calcium carbonate. The                  alkaline component comprises a base chosen from                  hydroxide bases, carbonate bases, ammonia bases                  and their mixtures, chosen from lithium                  hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide,                  lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium                  carbonate, caesium carbonate and their mixtures,                  preferably chosen from sodium hydroxide,                  potassium carbonate and sodium carbonate. The                  alkaline component is an aqueous alkaline                  component comprising the base and water, where                  the pH of the aqueous alkaline component is at                  least 8, preferably at least 12, and/or the                  aqueous alkaline component comprises the base in                  an amount of 1-75 wt.%, more preferably 10-35                  wt.%, based on the total weight of the aqueous                  alkaline component. The basic compound of step                  (b2) is chosen from carbonate bases, hydroxide                  bases, bicarbonate bases, amine bases and their                  mixtures, chosen from sodium carbonate, sodium                  bicarbonate, sodium hydroxide, potassium                  carbonate, potassium bicarbonate, potassium                  hydroxide, calcium carbonate, calcium                  bicarbonate, calcium hydroxide, magnesium                  carbonate, magnesium bicarbonate, magnesium                  hydroxide, ammonia, and mixtures, preferably                  calcium carbonate. Preferred Properties: The                  surface-reacted calcium carbonate has a specific                  surface area of 50-120 m2/g, as measured by the                  BET method, and/or a total intra particle                  intruded specific pore volume of 0.1-2.5 cm3/g,                  preferably 0.6-1.8 cm3/g, as measured by mercury                  intrusion porosimetry.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxygen scavenger                  is chosen from phenolic acid derivatives bearing                  at least two phenolic hydroxyl groups arranged                  ortho or para relative to each other, cinnamic                  acid derivatives bearing at least two phenolic                  hydroxyl groups arranged ortho or para relative                  to each other, and their mixtures, where the at                  least one oxygen scavenger is chosen from gallic                  acid derivatives, digallic acid derivatives,                  protocatechuic acid derivatives, caffeic acid                  derivatives, 5-hydroxyferulic acid derivatives,                  gentisic acid derivatives, orsellinic acid                  derivatives, chebulic acid derivatives,                  phloroglucinol carboxylic acid derivatives,                  chicoric acid derivatives, and their mixtures,                  preferably a gallic acid derivative, and where                  the acid derivatives are chosen from alkyl                  esters, aryl esters and essentially fully                  deprotonated acids of the respective acid, and                  the at least one oxygen scavenger is an                  essentially fully deprotonated gallic acid,                  and/or the at least one oxygen scavenger                  comprising an essentially fully deprotonated                  carboxyl group comprises a cation chosen from                  ammonium, sodium, lithium, potassium, cesium,                  magnesium, calcium and their mixtures, preferably                  a calcium cation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The kit further                  comprise: a supply device comprising the                  sheet-like element component, where the supply                  device preferably comprises a roll or a magazine;                  or a food packaging comprising the sheet-like                  element component, where the coating layer is                  present within the food packaging. The sheet-like                  element component further comprises one or more                  adhesive layers, being located on the substrate                  layer on the opposite side of the coating layer                  and/or between the individual substrate layers,                  where the adhesive layer is chosen from                  adhesives, sealants, rubber coatings,                  pressure-sensitive layers and their mixtures,                  and/or one or more primer layers, being located                  between the substrate layer and the coating                  layer, and/or one or more oxygen-permeable                  covering layers to cover the coating layer,                  preferably chosen from oxygen-permeable film                  layers, fibrous material layers and nonwoven                  fabric layers, and/or one or more protective                  layers to temporarily seal the coating layer,                  and/or the adhesive layer, preferably chosen from                  polyethylene, polypropylene and/or coated paper.                  The sheet-like element component comprises a                  coating layer: having a total intruded specific                  pore volume of 0.1-1.5 cm3/g, preferably 0.1-1                  cm3/g, as measured by mercury intrusion                  porosimetry; and/or having a total intra particle                  intruded specific pore volume of 0.05-1 cm3/g,                  preferably 0.1-0.4 cm3/g, as measured by mercury                  intrusion porosimetry; and/or having a total                  inter particle intruded specific pore volume of                  0.05-0.5 cm3/g, preferably 0.1-0.3 cm3/g, as                  measured by mercury intrusion porosimetry; and/or                  having a total occlusion intruded specific pore                  volume of 0.05-0.4 cm3/g, preferably 0.1-0.2                  cm3/g, as measured by mercury intrusion                  porosimetry; and/or being present on the                  substrate layer in an amount of 1-200 g/m2,                  preferably 10-120 g/m2. The polymeric binder is                  chosen from polyacrylic acid and its salts and                  derivatives, starch, proteins, styrene butadiene                  latices, polyvinyl alcohol, polyvinyl acetate and                  their mixtures, preferably chosen from                  polyacrylic acid and its salts and derivatives                  and their mixtures. The substrate layer comprises                  one or more individual substrate layers chosen                  from polymer material layers, preferably made                  from polyethylene, polypropylene, polyethylene                  terephthalate, polylactic acid, polyhydroxy                  butyrate, polyethylene-2, 5-furandicarboxylate,                  polystyrene or their mixtures, fibrous material                  layers, preferably made from cellulose acetate,                  viscose, polypropylene, polyethylene                  terephthalate, polylactic acid, or their                  mixtures, paper layers, cardboard layers, textile                  layers, nonwoven layers, layers made from                  bio-based materials, wood layers, bamboo layers,                  metal foil layers, aluminum layers, print                  receptive coating layers, and their mixtures,                  where the one or more individual substrate layers                  optionally have been subjected to a corona                  treatment, and the one or more individual                  substrate layers is chosen from polymer material                  layers. Preferred Composition: The coating layer                  comprises the polymeric binder in an amount of                  10-20 wt.%, based on the total dry weight of the                  coating layer, and/or the particulate filler in                  an amount of 30-60 wt.%, based on the total dry                  weight of the coating layer, and/or the oxygen                  scavenger in an amount of 30-60 wt.%, based on                  the total dry weight of the coating layer.                  Preferred Method: The process for the manufacture                  of a kit for improving food shelf life, comprises                  (a) providing a particulate filler comprising a                  surface-reacted calcium carbonate in an amount of                  at least 50 wt.%, based on the total amount of                  the particulate filler, where the surface-reacted                  calcium carbonate is a reaction product of                  natural ground calcium carbonate or precipitated                  calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide and one or                  more hydronium ion donors, where the carbon                  dioxide is formed in situ by the hydronium ion                  donors treatment and/or is supplied from an                  external source, and where the surface-reacted                  calcium carbonate has a specific surface area of                  20-200 m2/g, preferably 50-120 m2/g, as measured                  by the BET method, (b) providing at least one                  oxygen scavenger being a compound having at least                  one phenyl ring bearing at least two phenolic                  hydroxyl groups mentioned above and at least one                  group R mentioned above, (c) providing a                  polymeric binder, (d) providing a substrate layer                  comprising one or more individual substrate                  layers or a food packaging comprising the                  substrate layer, (e) mixing, in the order set out                  in it, the oxygen scavenger of step (b), the                  particulate filler of step (a) and the polymeric                  binder of step (c) to obtain a coating                  composition, (f) applying the coating composition                  of step (e) onto the substrate layer of step (d)                  to obtain a sheet-like element precursor, (g)                  drying the sheet-like element precursor obtained                  in step (f) to obtain a sheet-like element                  component, (h) providing an alkaline component                  comprising a base having a pKb value of 6 or                  lower, and optionally (i) mixing the alkaline                  component of step (h) with water to obtain an                  aqueous alkaline component comprising the base                  and water, where the pH of the aqueous alkaline                  component is at least 8, preferably at least 12                  and/or the aqueous alkaline component comprises                  the base in an amount of 1-75 wt.%, preferably                  10-35 wt.%, based on the total weight of the                  aqueous alkaline component. The process for the                  manufacture of a sheet-like element component,                  comprises (a1) providing a particulate filler                  comprising a surface-reacted calcium carbonate in                  an amount of at least 50 wt.%, based on the total                  amount of the particulate filler, where the                  surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction                  product of natural ground calcium carbonate or                  precipitated calcium carbonate with carbon                  dioxide and one or more hydronium ion donors,                  where the carbon dioxide is formed in situ by the                  hydronium ion donors treatment and/or is supplied                  from an external source, and the surface-reacted                  calcium carbonate has a specific surface area of                  20-200 m2/g, preferably 50 to 120 m2/g, as                  measured by the BET method, (b1) providing at PT P
PN CN111100880-A
TI Preparing methane by using magnetosomes to reduce carbon dioxide by microbial electric fermentation comprises e.g. extracting and purifying magnetosomes from Magnetospirillum in logarithmic growth phase and using two graphite rod electrodes.
AU CHENG J
   ZHOU J
   LIU J
   YANG W
   CEN K
   WANG Z
   ZHANG Y
   ZHOU Z
   HE Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202040358L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing methane by using magnetosomes to reduce carbon dioxide by microbial electric fermentation comprises (i) extracting and purifying magnetosomes from Magnetospirillum in logarithmic growth phase, (ii) using microbial electrical fermentation system which uses three-electrode system, and its working volume is 50 ml, using two graphite rod electrodes as cathode and anode, and silver/silver chloride electrode as reference electrode, taking 1 g small molecule organic acid salt as carbon source of electric fermentation system, adding to system together with anaerobic activated sludge as the inoculated bacteria, where mass ratio of total volatile solids of anaerobic activated sludge and small molecule organic acid salt is 2:1, adjusting pH value of mixture in system to 7.5, adding magnetosome as an exogenous conductive additive to system, and controlling amount added to make concentration in system to 100 mg/l, and (iii) sealing microbial electro-fermentation system.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing methane by using magnetosomes to reduce carbon dioxide by microbial electric fermentation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases the concentration of biomethane; reduces the cost of subsequent biogas purification; optimizes the electron transfer performance of the system; increases the content of extracellular polymer with redox activity; and enriches the microorganisms responsible for reducing carbon dioxide to produce methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing methane by using magnetosomes to reduce carbon dioxide by microbial electric fermentation comprises (i) extracting and purifying magnetosomes from Magnetospirillum in the logarithmic growth phase, (ii) using microbial electrical fermentation system which uses three-electrode system, and its working volume is 50 ml, using two graphite rod electrodes as cathode and anode, and silver/silver chloride electrode as reference electrode, taking 1 g small molecule organic acid salt as carbon source of electric fermentation system, adding to system together with anaerobic activated sludge as the inoculated bacteria, where the mass ratio of total volatile solids of anaerobic activated sludge and small molecule organic acid salt is 2:1, adjusting the pH value of mixture in system to 7.5, then adding magnetosome as an exogenous conductive additive to the system, and controlling amount added to make the concentration in the system to 100 mg/l, and (iii) sealing microbial electro-fermentation system, passing pure nitrogen for 10 minutes to remove air, connecting system to an electrochemical workstation, applying potential of -0.6 V to -0.8 V at 37 degrees C, starting operation, electrolyzing small molecule organic acid salt at the graphite rod anode to produce carbon dioxide, removing carbon dioxide at the graphite rod cathode to produce methane, and using gas chromatograph to test methane product concentration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), extracting the magnetosomes, purifying from Magnetospirillum by ultrasonic extraction comprising culturing Magnetospirillum to logarithmic growth phase, centrifuging the bacteria and resuspending in phosphate buffer solution, ultrasonically crushing the cells in an ice bath environment, controlling power to 100 W, crushing for 10 seconds in an interval of 10 seconds, repeating for 50 times, separating the magnetosomes by adsorption with a magnet and washing for 5 times with phosphate buffer to obtain purified magnetosomes. In the step (ii), adjusting the pH of the mixture with a sodium hydroxide and hydrochloric acid solution with a molar concentration of 6 mol/l. Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the small molecule organic acid salt is acetate, propionate or butyrate. The anaerobic activated sludge is taken from stable-running electro-fermentation methanogenic reactor and is rich in electro-active bacteria and methane producing bacteria. The diameter of the graphite rod electrode is 0.5 cm and the length is 7.5 cm.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.)

PT P
PN CN109921060-A; CN109921060-B
TI Storing electricity and syngas based on solid            oxide battery comprises introducing air during the            power generation of solid oxide cells into the cathode            and the anode, switching the cathode gas to carbon            dioxide or water.
AU HAN M
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 2019570709
AB    NOVELTY - Storing electricity and syngas based on solid                oxide battery comprises (i) introducing air during                the power generation of solid oxide cells into the                cathode and the anode with hydrocarbon fuel, the                anode off-gas is carbon dioxide and water when the                hydrocarbon fuel is fully converted, the anode                off-gas is carbon monoxide or hydrogen fuel when                the hydrocarbon fuel is partially converted, (ii)                switching the cathode gas to carbon dioxide or                water during the electrolysis of the solid oxide                battery, and the anode still passing into the                hydrocarbon fuel, controlling the hydrocarbon fuel                flow according to the electrolysis current, and                both the cathode and the anode generates carbon                monoxide or hydrogen fuel, (iii) adjusting the                ratio of cathode carbon dioxide and water during                the electrolysis process, converting the tail gas                into syngas of different components and mixing with                the syngas produced by the anode to generate syngas                with different hydrogen/carbon monoxide                ratio.
   USE - The syngas is useful in the anode gas for                power generation and high-quality chemicals                produced by Fischer-Tropsch (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The syngas: generated by whole generate                electricity/electrolytic process is an important                raw material for the chemical industry; storage                method is different from the traditional reversible                fuel cell operation method; can realize generate                electricity/electrolytic switch, at the same time,                effectively reduces the electrolysis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Storing electricity and syngas based on solid                oxide battery comprises (i) introducing air during                the power generation of solid oxide cells into the                cathode and the anode with hydrocarbon fuel, the                anode off-gas is carbon dioxide and water when the                hydrocarbon fuel is fully converted, the anode                off-gas is carbon monoxide or hydrogen fuel when                the hydrocarbon fuel is partially converted, (ii)                switching the cathode gas to carbon dioxide or                water during the electrolysis of the solid oxide                battery, and the anode still passing into the                hydrocarbon fuel, controlling the hydrocarbon fuel                flow according to the electrolysis current, and                both the cathode and the anode generates carbon                monoxide or hydrogen fuel, (iii) adjusting the                ratio of cathode carbon dioxide and water during                the electrolysis process, converting the tail gas                into syngas of different components and mixing with                the syngas produced by the anode to generate syngas                with different hydrogen/carbon monoxide ratio,                which can be used in the chemical industry e.g. the                subsequent Fischer-Tropsch synthesis to realize the                efficient utilization of the syngas. An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is also included for solid oxide cell-based                power storage and system for preparing synthetic                gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows flow chart of storing                electricity and syngas based on a solid oxide                battery (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solid oxide                  battery anode is a conventional nickel /                  Yttria-stabilized zirconia composite ceramic                  material modified by carbon deposition, or                  carbon-resistant materials. The solid oxide                  battery cathode has good stability during storage                  and discharge, and has good cycle stability,                  represented by lanthanum-strontium-iron complex                  of La1-xSrxFeO3- delta ,                  lanthanum-strontium-manganese complex of                  La1-xSrxMnO3- delta ,                  lanthanum-strontium-chromium-manganese complex of                  La1-xSrxCr1-yMnyO3- delta ,                  barium-strontium-chromium-iron complex of                  Ba1-xSrxCo1-yFeyO3- delta , and                  lanthanum-strontium-iron complex of                  La1-xSrxCo1-yFeyO3- delta . The anode carbon                  hydrogen fuel comprises a solid fuel e.g. coal,                  biomass etc, liquid fuel e.g. ethanol, petroleum                  etc. and a gas fuel e.g. methane, liquefied                  petroleum gas. The electrolysis mode cathode gas                  is mixture of proportion of carbon dioxide or                  water. Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide and                  water vapor generating during power generation                  can be used in the cathode gas of the                  electrolysis process, and the electrolysis                  process is realizing together with CO2 or water                  vapor obtained by other routes.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. PT P
PN US11167242-B1
TI Performing desulfurization and hydrogen recovery from sour gas influent stream e.g. methane, carbon dioxide gas and hydrogen sulfide, by gas-liquid reactor column structured to facilitate reaction of sour gas influent stream with aqueous bromine in hydrobromic acid and sulfuric acid solution.
AU PARKER R Z
   PARKER M L
AE PARKER R Z (PARK-Individual)
   PARKER M L (PARK-Individual)
   CHEMERGY INC (CHEM-Non-standard)
GA 2021C73704
AB    NOVELTY - Process (P1) for performing desulfurization and hydrogen recovery from a sour gas influent stream including an amount of methane and/or ethane and/or carbon dioxide gas and an undesirable amount of hydrogen sulfide, involves a gas-liquid reactor column structured to facilitate reaction of the sour gas influent stream with an amount of aqueous bromine in a hydrobromic acid and sulfuric acid solution, where the amount of bromine is presented to the sour gas influent stream at a temperature not greater than 400C, the reactor structured to produce a gaseous effluent stream comprising substantially all of the amount of methane and/or ethane and/or carbon dioxide gas with some trace amount of bromine and/or hydrogen bromide.
   USE - The process is useful for performing desulfurization and hydrogen recovery from a sour gas influent stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The process removes undesirable amounts of hydrogen sulfide from a sour gas feed stream through reaction with bromine/hydrogen bromide in a reactor, and produces substantially clean hydrocarbon gas effluent, and solid sulfur/sulfuric acid/hydrobromic acid side streams. The process produces recoverable amounts of hydrogen gas in a cost-effective manner and eliminates the need for spinning reserve and allows greater sourcing from intermittent renewable power.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process (P1) for performing desulfurization and hydrogen recovery from a sour gas influent stream including an amount of methane and/or ethane and/or carbon dioxide gas and an undesirable amount of hydrogen sulfide, involves a gas-liquid reactor column structured to facilitate reaction of the sour gas influent stream with an amount of aqueous bromine in a hydrobromic acid and sulfuric acid solution, where the amount of bromine is presented to the sour gas influent stream at a temperature not greater than 400C, the reactor structured to produce a gaseous effluent stream comprising substantially all of the amount of methane and/or ethane and/or carbon dioxide gas with some trace amount of bromine and/or hydrogen bromide, the reactor structured to produce liquid effluent stream comprising an aqueous solution of hydrobromic acid, sulfuric acid, and/or solid sulfur with some trace amount of aqueous bromine, a filter i.e. used to remove solid sulfur and/or sulfuric acid from the reactor liquid effluent stream, a first absorber structured to separate bromine, hydrogen bromide and/or water vapor from the methane and/or ethane and/or carbon dioxide gas in the gaseous effluent stream at a temperature and pressure less than the reactor, the first absorber further structured to discharge a first amount of a dilute aqueous bromine and hydrobromic acid solution, an electrolyzer structured to electrolytically disassociate the filtered dilute aqueous bromine, hydrobromic acid and sulfuric acid liquid effluent stream solution into an aqueous solution of more concentrated bromine and less concentrated hydrobromic acid in same sulfuric acid, and the electrolyzer further structured to produce an amount of hydrogen gas and water vapor through dissociation of the hydrobromic acid solution at a temperature not greater than 150C and a pressure not greater than1000 psi, a second absorber structured to separate bromine and dilute hydrogen bromide from the hydrogen gas produced through dissociation of the hydrobromic acid solution at a temperature not greater than 150C and a pressure not greater than 1000 psi, and the second absorber further structured to discharge a second amount of a dilute aqueous hydrobromic acid solution.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process (P2) for performing desulfurization and hydrogen recovery from a sour gas influent stream including an amount of methane gas and an undesirable amount of hydrogen sulfide, which involves a reactor column structured to facilitate reaction of the sour gas influent stream with 1-20 wt.% bromine in a 5-48 wt.% hydrobromic acid solution, where the amount of bromine is presented to the sour gas influent stream at a temperature not greater than 400C, the reactor structured to produce a gaseous effluent stream comprising substantially all of the amount of methane gas, the reactor structured to produce an aqueous effluent stream comprising an aqueous solution of hydrobromic acid, sulfuric acid, and/or solid sulfur, a first absorber structured to separate carbon dioxide gas trace bromine and hydrogen bromide and water vapor from the methane gas in the gaseous effluent stream, the first absorber further structured to discharge a first amount of a dilute aqueous bromine and hydrobromic acid solution, an electrolyzer structured to electrolytically disassociate hydrobromic acid solution into an aqueous solution of more dilute hydrobromic acid and more concentrated bromine, the electrolyzer further structured to produce an amount of hydrogen gas and water vapor through dissociation of the hydrobromic acid solution, the electrolyzer comprises proton exchange membrane cells, where the proton exchange membrane cells are disposed in a series configuration in an electrolyzer stack, a second absorber structured to separate bromine and hydrogen bromide from the hydrogen gas produced through dissociation of the hydrobromic acid solution, and the second absorber further structured to discharge a second amount of a dilute aqueous hydrobromic acid solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Device: The reactor and/or first absorber comprises a packed column made of glass, ceramic, polyvinyl chloride (PVC) and/or polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF) materials. The first and/or second absorber includes an amount of a solid absorbent material to remove any trace amount of hydrogen sulfide, bromine and/or hydrogen bromide. The electrolyzer comprises at least one proton exchange membrane cell operated at up to 2.250 Amps/m2 current density. The electrolyzer comprises several proton exchange membrane cells. The proton exchange membrane cells are disposed in a series configuration in an electrolyzer stack.Preferred Process: The process (P1) further involves a cooler structured to reduce the temperature of the hydrogen gas from the second absorber to reduce the vapor pressure of any trace chemicals. The process (P1) further involves a dryer structured to remove the water vapor from the hydrogen gas in the effluent of the second absorber.Preferred Component: The stoichiometric ratio of bromine in the liquid solution introduced into the reactor to hydrogen sulfide in the sour gas influent stream is close to a 1:1 ratio to encourage the production of solid sulfur. The stoichiometric ratio of bromine in the liquid solution introduced into the reactor to hydrogen sulfide in the sour gas influent stream is close to a 1:4 ratio to encourage the production of sulfuric acid.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of initial bench scale process, bench scale hydrogen sulfide removal from sour-gas and bench scale electrolysis of hydrobromic acid that do not support the claims.

PT P
PN CN112293152-A; CN112293152-B
TI Increasing yield of Agaricus bisporus by filling            fermentation process recovery tank with water, soaking            wheat straw, replenishing water, piling into            high-pressure fermentation bin, fermenting and sowing            Agaricus bisporus.
AU JUN J
   HUANG J
   WANG Q
   XIAO T
   SONG X
   ZHANG J
   CHEN H
   GAO Z
AE SHANGHAI AGRIC SCI ACAD (SHNY-C)
GA 202116561S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for increasing yield of Agaricus                bisporus involves (a) weighing 800-1000 pts. wt.                wheat straw, 400-500 pts. wt. high-quality                nitrogen-content chicken manure, 80-100 pts. wt.                gypsum and 60-70 pts. wt. peanut meal, (b) filling                fermentation process recovery tank with water,                soaking 800-1000 pts. wt. weighed wheat straw, (c)                obtaining mixture to be fermented after mixing, and                replenishing water during the mixing process, (d)                piling the mixture to be fermented into a                high-pressure fermentation bin to obtain a                fermentation compost, (e) performing secondary                fermentation, (f) sowing A.bisporus evenly in the                secondary fermentation compost to obtain fermented                compost and (g) managing cultivation house of                mushroom.
   USE - The method is useful for increasing the yield                of A.bisporus.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for increasing yield of Agaricus                bisporus by three fermentation involves (a)                collecting chicken manure, detecting nitrogen                content of chicken manure, if the nitrogen content                of the chicken manure is less than 2%, adding a                nitrogen source to the chicken manure until                nitrogen content reaches 2-4% to obtain                high-quality nitrogen content chicken manure, and                weighing 800-1000 pts. wt. wheat straw, 400-500                pts. wt. high-quality nitrogen content chicken                manure, 80-100 pts. wt. gypsum and 60-70 pts. wt.                peanut meal, (b) filling fermentation process                recovery tank with water, soaking the 800-1000 pts.                wt. weighed wheat straw in tank until no bubbles                are produced, forming pile, and stacking until the                temperature in the pile rises to 50-60 degrees C to                obtain pre-wet wheat straw, (c) transferring                400-500 pts. wt. high-quality nitrogen content                chicken manure, 80-100 pts. wt. gypsum and 60-70                pts. wt. peanut meal to crushing and mixing machine                together with pre-wet wheat straw for crushing and                mixing to obtain mixture to be fermented after                mixing, and replenishing water during mixing                process to ensure that water content of mixture is                70-80%, where average length of wheat straw in                mixture to be fermented is 5-6 cm, (d) piling the                mixture to be fermented into a high-pressure                fermentation bin with a height of 4.5-6.5 m, and                fermenting at 77-83 degrees C until pile weight is                reduced by 20-25% to obtain a fermentation compost,                (e) performing secondary fermentation by (i) piling                the primary fermentation compost into the secondary                fermentation bin to the pile height of 2.5-3.2m,                and fermenting at 45 degrees C for 13-14 hours,                (ii) increasing temperature in secondary                fermentation bin from 45 degrees C to 56-60 degrees                C at a heating rate of 1.3-1.5 degrees C/hour,                (iii) fermenting at 56-60 degrees C for 8-10 hours,                (iv) controlling air temperature in secondary                fermentation bin to 45 degrees C, (v) fermenting at                46-49 degrees C until ammonia concentration in air                in secondary fermentation chamber is lower than 5                ppm and (vi) controlling air temperature in                secondary fermentation bin to room temperature and                cooling pile naturally until temperature of pile is                25-26 degrees C to obtain secondary fermentation                compost, (f) sowing A.bisporus evenly in secondary                fermentation compost at a stacking temperature of                25-26 degrees C with a seeding rate of 7-10 l/time                , and fermenting at 25-26 degrees C for 16-19 days                to obtain fermented compost and (g) managing                cultivation house by (i) stirring 3-6 ml/kg liquid                microbial fertilizer into fermented compost to                obtain compost, spreading compost on bed frame with                a thickness of 18 cm, covering with turf soil to a                thickness of 6-7 cm, filling bed frame with culture                material at 23-24 degrees C, where the liquid                microbial fertilizer comprises 2x 108 cells/ml                Bacillus amyloliquefaciens, (ii) spraying 2-6 l/m2                aqueous solution of sodium hypochlorite for 3 times                on culture material at 23-24 degrees C, on third                day, spraying 2 l/m2 sodium hypochlorite aqueous                solution for 3 times at 23-24 degrees C, on fourth                day, spraying 2 l/m2 sodium hypochlorite aqueous                solution twice at 25-26 degrees C, and on fifth                day, spraying 20-24 l/m2 sodium hypochlorite                aqueous solution at 25-26 degrees C, (iii)                controlling air temperature at 17 degrees C,                controlling carbon dioxide concentration at                3000-5000 ppm, and reducing carbon dioxide                concentration to 1200 ppm at a rate of 300-500                ppm/day during cooling process, (iv) culturing at                17 degrees C under carbon dioxide concentration of                1200 ppm and air humidity of 88%, (v) turning when                temperature of the culture material is 17.5-18                degrees C and carbon dioxide concentration is 1500                ppm, (vi) culturing at 17 degrees C under carbon                dioxide concentration of 1200 ppm and air humidity                of 86% to harvest A.bisporus, (vii) turning at                17.5-18 degrees C under carbon dioxide                concentration of 1500 ppm and (viii) culturing at                17 degrees C under carbon dioxide concentration of                1200 ppm and air humidity of 84-85% to harvest                A.bisporus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves                  using ground water or tap water to fill                  fermentation process recovery tank. The step (d)                  involves controlling fan to ensure that internal                  air oxygen content of pile is not less than 8%                  during fermentation at 77-83 degrees C until the                  weight loss of the pile is 20-25%, adding water                  to ensure that the water content of the stock is                  not less than 70%, at beginning of the                  fermentation process, turning fan on for 5                  minutes, turning off for 5 minutes, turning,                  adding water at same time to ensure that moisture                  content of the pile is not less than 70%,                  continuing fermentation, turning fan on for 5                  minutes and off for 17 minutes, after 3 days of                  fermentation, turning, adding water to ensure                  that the moisture content of the pile is not less                  than 70%, continuing fermentation, turning on fan                  for 5 minutes, turning off for 17 minutes, after                  5 days of fermentation, turning, adding water to                  ensure that moisture content of the pile is not                  less than 70%, and continuing to ferment until                  the pile weight is reduced by 20-25%. The step                  (g) (i) involves adding gypsum, lime and                  formaldehyde to peat soil, where the mass ratio                  of peat soil and gypsum is 300:1, the mass ratio                  of peat soil and lime is 300:1.5 and the mass                  ratio of peat soil and formaldehyde is 300:1.5.                  Preferred Components: In step (a), the nitrogen                  source is urea or ammonium sulfate. The aqueous                  sodium hypochlorite solution comprises 300-450                  ppm sodium hypochlorite. In step (f), the                  A.bisporus strain is a species strain of                  A15.

PN CN111548826-A; CN111548826-B
TI Transforming biomass gas boiler with MCFC circulating loop fuel cell-biomass gas-pulverized coal coupling and ammonia synthesis comprises using straw gasification furnace, burning, transporting combustion products and fermentation gas.
AU CHE P
   WEI L
   LIANG B
   CHENG Y
   TAN S
   XU H
   LIU Y
AE HARBIN BOILER FACTORY CO LTD (HRBE-C)
GA 202081884M
AB    NOVELTY - Transforming biomass gas boiler with MCFC circulating loop fuel cell-biomass gas-pulverized coal coupling and ammonia synthesis comprises using straw gasification furnace, adding straw gasification gas into gas pipeline, burning in biomass gas boiler, transporting combustion products, high-temperature steam-water mixture and separated mixed gas produced after water removal, using straw fermentation tank to ferment with straw as raw material, transporting nitrogen to ammonia synthesis tower for industrial ammonia synthesis, integrating the electric energy produced by the fuel cell into the grid, realizing the grid-connected coupling and assisting the power generation of the pulverized coal boiler in the thermal power plant.
   USE - The method is useful for transforming biomass gas boiler with MCFC circulating loop fuel cell-biomass gas-pulverized coal coupling and ammonia synthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: alleviates the coupling failure and the inability to adjust the peak caused by the untimely supply of straw; and is suitable for the transformation of biomass gas boilers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Transforming biomass gas boiler with MCFC circulating loop fuel cell-biomass gas-pulverized coal coupling and ammonia synthesis comprises using the straw gasification furnace to produce straw gasification gas with straw as raw material, where the straw gasification gas includes nitrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and water, adding the straw gasification gas into the gas pipeline, burning in the biomass gas boiler through the burner nozzle, sequentially transporting the combustion products through the horizontal flue, tail flue and mixed flue gas downcomer of the biomass gas boiler to remove water, transporting the high-temperature steam-water mixture produced after dewatering to the heating pipe for heating or sending to the header on the water wall of the power plant's pulverized coal boiler as boiler feed water, transporting the separated mixed gas produced after water removal to the mixed gas collecting box, where the separation gas mixture includes nitrogen, carbon dioxide and the balance of inert gas, transporting the high-temperature steam-water mixture in the main steam pipeline of the biomass gas boiler to the header on the water wall of the pulverized coal boiler of the power plant, using straw fermentation tank to ferment with straw as raw material, where the main component of fermentation gas produced by fermentation is methane, transporting the fermentation gas to the carbon monoxide shift furnace by allowing to react to obtain carbon monoxide and hydrogen, where the reaction in the carbon monoxide shift furnace is 2CH4+O2=2CO+4H2, sending part of the carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas produced by the carbon monoxide shift furnace to the pressure adsorption device for reduced pressure adsorption, transporting the carbon monoxide obtained by decompression adsorption to the gas pipeline of the biomass gas boiler as fuel, transporting the pure high-temperature hydrogen obtained by vacuum adsorption to the ammonia synthesis tower for industrial ammonia synthesis, sending the other part of carbon monoxide and hydrogen produced by the carbon monoxide shift furnace to the anode of the fuel cell, where the anode reaction of the fuel cell is2H2+2CO32--4e-=2H2O+2CO2 and CO-2e-+2H2O=CO32-+4H+, the anode tail gas of the fuel cell produces a large amount of mixed gas of high temperature water and high temperature carbon dioxide after removing water from the mixed gas of high temperature water and high temperature carbon dioxide, transporting the high-temperature steam-water mixture produced after dewatering to the heating pipe for heating or sending to the header on the water wall of the pulverized coal boiler of the power plant as the boiler feed water, adding the high temperature carbon dioxide generated after water removal to the mixed gas collection box, adding the compressed air in the compressed air storage tank to the mixed air collecting box, mixing the high-temperature carbon dioxide in the mixing gas collecting box, delivering the separated mixed gas and compressed air to the cathode of the fuel cell as the cathode reactant, where the MCFC circulation loop fuel cell is made up of a fuel cell stack, a low-temperature molten salt storage tank and a high-temperature molten salt storage tank, the fuel cell stack is made up of multiple molten carbonate fuel cells, the high temperature molten salt electrolyte input port of the molten carbonate fuel cell is connected with the high temperature molten salt storage tank outlet, connecting the high temperature molten salt electrolyte discharge port of the molten carbonate fuel cell with the inlet of the low temperature molten salt storage tank, connecting the inlet of the high-temperature molten salt storage tank with the outlet of the low-temperature molten salt storage tank through the molten potassium carbonate loop pipeline, setting the middle part of the molten potassium carbonate loop pipeline in the biomass gas boiler, where the exhaust gas produced by the fuel cell cathode is pure, high temperature and high pressure nitrogen, transporting nitrogen to ammonia synthesis tower for industrial ammonia synthesis, integrating the electric energy produced by the fuel cell into the grid, realizing the grid-connected coupling and assisting the power generation of the pulverized coal boiler in the thermal power plant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction temperature in the process of hydrogen and nitrogen synthesizing ammonia in the ammonia synthesis tower is 500 degrees C, the pressure is 30 MPa and iron contact coal catalysis is adopted.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements.    USE - Method for preparing fertilizer composition.

PT P
PN CN113201484-A; CN113201484-B
TI Processing bovine in vitro fertilization embryo culture and cryopreservation comprises e.g. collecting of oocyte and carrying out in vitro maturation, taking cattle ovaries from the slaughter site, extracting, picking and thawing.
AU HAN Y
   WANG X
   WANG N
   HAO S
   ZHAO M
   GUO C
   XU X
AE TIANJIN BOYU LIMU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
   TIANJIN LIMU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202192599M
AB    NOVELTY - Processing bovine in vitro fertilization embryo culture and cryopreservation comprises e.g. (i) collecting of oocyte and carrying out in vitro maturation, (a) taking cattle ovaries from the slaughter site, extracting 2-8 mm follicles on the surface, and collecting the precipitates, picking out the cumulus-oocyte complex of oocytes, placing in the transport culture medium containing 4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic acid (HEPES) at 38.8 degrees C without carbon dioxide supply, and transporting back to the laboratory within 24 hours, and(b) washing the COCs obtained from the above in vitro or live collection in the oocyte maturation medium once, (ii) washing mature COCs for once in the fertilization culture medium, then transferring to the fertilization culture medium, and placing in the incubator, taking frozen fine tube from liquid nitrogen and thawing in a 37 degrees C water bath, and(iii) preserving and thawing of embryos in vitro fertilization.
   USE - The method is useful for processing bovine in vitro fertilization embryo culture and cryopreservation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the efficiency of bovine in vitro fertilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing bovine in vitro fertilization embryo culture and cryopreservation comprises (i) collecting of oocyte and carrying out in vitro maturation, (a) taking cattle ovaries from the slaughter site, extracting 2-8 mm follicles on the surface, and collecting the precipitates, picking out the cumulus-oocyte complex (COCs) of oocytes that contain at least 3 layers of cumulus cells under a stereo microscope, placing in the transport culture medium containing 4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic acid (HEPES) at 38.8 degrees C without carbon dioxide supply, and transporting back to the laboratory within 24 hours, and(b) washing the COCs obtained from the above in vitro or live collection in the oocyte maturation medium once, and then transferring to a maturation medium for 22-24 hours, where the culture conditions are 38.8 degrees C, 5.5-6.5% carbon dioxide under saturated humidity, (ii) washing mature COCs for once in the fertilization culture medium, then transferring to the fertilization culture medium, and placing in the incubator, taking frozen fine tube from liquid nitrogen and thawing in a 37 degrees C water bath, cutting both ends of the thin tube aseptically, pouring the semen into a 15 ml centrifuge tube containing semen preparation culture solution, centrifuging at 328x g for twice, 5 minutes each time, discarding the supernatant, adding 300 mu l semen preparation culture solution to the above centrifuge tube, re-suspending the sperm pellet, taking specific sperm suspension for sperm count, adding the calculated volume of sperm suspension to the fertilization culture droplet filled with oocytes, placing the culture plate into the incubator, and incubating the sperm and eggs for 16-20 hours, where the culture conditions are 38.8 degrees C, 5.5-6.5% carbon dioxide under saturated humidity, and(iii) preserving and thawing of embryos in vitro fertilization, removing the granular cells around the embryo with a peeling needle, placing into embryo culture medium for culture, recording this time as the first day of embryo culture, where the culture conditions are 38.8 degrees C, 6% oxygen, 88% nitrogen under saturated humidity, recording the cleavage rate on the 3rd day, recording blastocyst rate on the 7th day, and counting the blastocyst hatching rate on the 9th day, and evaluating the quality, washing the available embryos in the preservation solution for 3 times, equilibrating in the balance solution for 10 minutes, transferring to the freezing solution, and loading the embryos according to the 5-stage loading method, making the mark and placing in the program cooling device and decreasing to -35 degrees C at a rate of 0.5 degrees C/minutes, then quickly taking out the embryo straw and placing in liquid nitrogen for cryopreservation, when you need to use cryopreserved embryos, removing the thin tube from the liquid nitrogen, staying in the air for 5 seconds and placing in a 37 degrees C water bath until all the ice crystals have melted, cutting the thin tube, quickly transferring the embryo into the thawing solution, insulating at room temperature for 5 minutes, the embryos are cultured in droplets of embryo culture medium to complete the thawing process. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for freezing solution for freezing and thawing of bovine in vitro fertilization blastocysts, comprising phosphate-buffered saline and 20 wt.% FBS, 1.8 mol/l ethylene glycol, 0.25 mol/l sucrose, 0.3 wt.% dextran 20 and 5 mmol/l acetyl cysteine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the composition of the freezing solution includes phosphate-buffered saline, 20 wt.% fetal bovine serum (FBS), 1.8 mol/l ethylene glycol, 0.25 mol/l sucrose, 0.3 wt.% dextran 20 and 5 mmol/l acetylcysteine. The composition of the thawing solution includes phosphate-buffered saline and 20% fetal bovine serum, 0.25 mol/l sucrose, 0.3 wt.% dextran 20 and 5 mmol/l acetylcysteine. In the step (a), the transportation culture fluid includes 50 mg/l glycine, 25 mg/l L-alanine, 70 mg/l L-arginine hydrochloride, 30 mg/l L-aspartic acid, 26 mg/l L-cystine dihydrochloride, 75 mg/l L-glutamic acid, 100 mg/l L-glutamine, 21.88 mg/l L-histidine hydrochloride monohydrate, 10 mg/l L-hydroxyproline, 40 mg/l L-isoleucine, 60 mg/l L-leucine, 70 mg/l L-lysine hydrochloride, 15 mg/l L-methionine, 25 mg/l L-phenylalanine, 40 mg/l L-Proline, 25 mg/l L-serine, 30 mg/l L-threonine, 10 mg/l L-tryptophan, 58 mg/l L-tyrosine disodium salt dihydrate, 25 mg/l L-valine, 0.05 mg/l ascorbic acid, 0.01 mg/l biotin, 0.5 mg/l choline chloride, 0.01 mg/l D-calcium pantothenate, 0.01 mg/l folic acid, 0.01 mg/l menadione, 0.025 mg/l niacinamide, 0.025 mg/l niacin, 0.05 mg/l P-aminobenzoic acid, 0.025 mg/l pyridoxal hydrochloride, 0.025 mg/l pyridoxine hydrochloride, 0.01 mg/l riboflavin, 0.01 mg/l thiamine hydrochloride, 0.1 mg/l vitamin A acetate, 0.1 mg/l calciferol as vitamin D2, 0.01 mg/l alpha -tocopherol phosphate sodium salt, 0.05 mg/l inositol, 200 mg/l anhydrous calcium chloride, 0.7 mg/l iron nitrate nonahydrate, 97.67 mg/l anhydrous magnesium sulfate, 400 mg/l potassium chloride, 6800 mg/l sodium chloride, 140 mg/l sodium dihydrogen phosphate monohydrate, 10 mg/l adenine sulfate, 0.2 mg/l 5'-adenosine phosphate, 1 mg/l adenosine triphosphate, 0.2 mg/l cholesterol, 1000 mg/l glucose, 0.5 mg/l deoxyribose, 0.05 mg/l reduced glutathione, 0.3 mg/l guanine hydrochloride, 0.354 mg/l sodium hypoxanthine, 20 mg/l phenol red, 0.5 mg/l ribose, 50 mg/l sodium acetate, 0.3 mg/l thymine, 20 mg/l Tween-80 (RTM: Polysorbate 80), 0.3 mg/l uracil, 0.3 mg/l xanthine sodium, 0.01 IU/ml FSH, 0.01 IU/ml LH, 1 mu g/ml E2, 50 ng/ml EGF, 100 ng/ml IGF, 10 wt.% gentamicin, 55 mu g/ml sodium pyruvate, 1.2 mM/l cysteine, 3 mg/ml bovine serum albumin (BSA) and 10 mM/l 4-(2-hydroxyethyl)piperazine-1-ethanesulfonic acid (HEPES). In the step (i), the transportation culture solution further includes taurine and zinc gluconate. The concentration of taurine in the transportation culture solution is 30-50 mg/l, for example, 40 mg/l or 45 mg/l, the concentration of zinc gluconate in the transportation culture solution is 1-3 mg/l, for example, 2 mg/l. In the step (ii), the fertilization medium includes 112 mM sodium chloride, 4.02 mM potassium chloride, 2.25 mM calcium chloride dihydrate, 0.52 mM magnesium chloride hexahydrate, 0.83 mM potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 37 mM sodium bicarbonate, 1.25 mM sodium pyruvate, 10 mu g/ml heparin, 4 mg/ml bovine serum albumin, 100 U/ml penicillin, 100 mu g/ml streptomycin, 10 mu mol/l alpha -lipoic acid, 25 mu g/ml folic acid, and 3 mg/ml sodium glutamate aqueous solution. In the step (ii), the semen preparation culture medium includes 112 mM sodium chloride, 4.02 mM potassium chloride, 2.25 mM calcium chloride dihydrate, 0.52 mM magnesium chloride hexahydrate, 0.83 mM potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 37 mM sodium bicarbonate, 1.25 mM sodium pyruvate, 10 mu g/ml heparin, 4 mg/ml bovine serum albumin (BSA),10 mM caffeine, 100 U/ml penicillin, 100 mu g/ml streptomycin, 10 mu mol/l alpha -lipoic acid, 25 mu g/ml folic acid, and 3 mg/ml sodium glutamate aqueous solution. In the step (iii), the embryo culture fluid includes 109.5 mM sodium chloride, 3.1 mM potassium chloride, 26.2 mM sodium bicarbonate, 0.8 mM magnesium chloride hexahydrate, 1.19 mM potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 0.4 mM sodium pyruvate, 1.5 mM glucose, 5 mM calcium galactonate, 2.5 v/v.% fetal bovine serum (FBS), 1 mM L-glutamine, 2 v/v.% essential amino acids, 1 v/v.% non-essential amino acids, 3 mM glutathione, 0.04 w/v.% sodium citrate, 0.02 w/v.% maltose aqueous solution. The essential amino acids includes 6.32 g L-arginine hydrochloride, 1.564 g L-cystine dihydrochloride, 2.1 g L-histidine hydrochloride monohydrate, 2.625 g L-isoleucine, 2.62 g L-leucine, 3.625 g L-lysine hydrochloride, 0.755 g L-methionine, 1.65 g L-phenylalanine, 2.38 g L-threonine, 0.51 g L-tryptophan, 1.8 g L-tyrosine and 2.34 g L-valine. The non-essential amino acids includes 0.89 g L-alanine, 1.5 g L-asparagine monohydrate, 1.33 g L-aspartic acid, 1.47 g L-glutamic acid, 0.75 g glycine, 1.15 g L-proline and1.05 g L-serine. The BY basic culture fluid is an aqueous solution includes 180-220 mg/l, preferably 200 mg/l calcium chloride, 0.70-0.75 mg/l, preferably 0.72 mg/l ferric nitrate nonahydrate, 380-420 mg/l, preferably 400 mg/l potassium chloride, 90-100 mg/l, preferably 97.7 mg/l magnesium sulfate, 6500-7000 mg/l, preferably 6800 mg/l sodium chloride, 130-150 mg/l, preferably 140 mg/l sodium dihydrogen phosphate monohydrate, 2000-2500 mg/l, preferably 2200 mg/l sodium bicarbonate, 40-60 mg/l, preferably 50 mg/l sodium acetate, 20-30 mg/l, preferably 25 mg/l L-alanine, 60-80 mg/l, preferably 70 mg/l L-arginine hydrochloride, 25-35 mg/l, preferably 30 mg/l L-aspartic acid, 0.10-0.12 mg/l, preferably 0.11 mg/l L-cysteine hydrochloride monohydrate, 20-30 mg/l, preferably 26 mg/l L-cystine dihydrochloride, 70-80 mg/l, preferably 75 mg/l L-glutamic acid, 40-60 mg/l, preferably 50 mg/l glycine, 20-25 mg/l, preferably 21.88 mg/l L-histidine hydrochloride monohydrate, 8-12 mg/l, preferably 10 mg/l L-hydroxyproline, 15-25 mg/l, preferably 20 mg/l L-isoleucine, 50-70 mg/l, preferably 60 mg/l L-leucine, 60-80 mg/l, preferably 70 mg/l L-lysine hydrochloride, 10-20 mg/l, preferably 15 mg/l L-methionine, 20-30 mg/l, preferably 25 mg/l L-phenylalanine, 30-50 mg/l, preferably 40 mg/l L-proline, 20-30 mg/l, preferably 25 mg/l L-serine, 25-35 mg/l, preferably 30 mg/l L-threonine, 8-12 mg/l, preferably 10 mg/l L-tryptophan, 55-60 mg/l, preferably 57.66 mg/l L-tyrosine disodium dihydrate, 20-30 mg/l, preferably 25 mg/l L-valine, 0.04-0.06 mg/l, preferably 0.05 mg/l ascorbic acid, 0.008-0.012 mg/l, preferably 0.01 mg/l alpha -D-tocopherol phosphate, 0.008-0.012, preferably 0.01 mg/l mg/l biotin, 0.08-0.12 mg/l, preferably 0.1 mg/l calcidiol, 0.008-0.012 mg/l, preferably 0.01 mg/l D-calcium pantothenate, 0.4-0.6 mg/l, preferably 0.5 mg/l choline chloride, 0.008-0.012 mg/l, preferably 0.01 mg/l folic acid, 0.04-0.06 mg/l, preferably 0.05 mg/l inositol, 0.015-0.025 mg/l, preferably 0.019 mg/l menadione trihydrate sodium bisulfite, 0.02-0.03 mg/l, preferably 0.025 mg/l niacin, 0.02-0.03 mg/l, preferably 0.025 mg/l niacinamide, 0.04-0.06 mg/l, preferably 0.05 mg/l p-aminobenzoic acid, 0.04-0.06 mg/l, preferably 0.05 mg/l pyridoxine hydrochloride, 0.008-0.012 mg/l, preferably 0.01mg/l riboflavin, 0.008-0.012 mg/l, preferably 0.01 mg/l thiamine hydrochloride, 0.1-0.2 mg/l, preferably 0.14 mg/l vitamin A acetate, 8-12 mg/l, preferably 10 mg/l adenine sulfate, 0.15-0.25 mg/l, preferably 0.2 mg/l adenine, 0.8-1.2 mg/l, preferably 1 mg/l adenosine triphosphate disodium, 0.15-0.25 mg/l, preferably 0.2 mg/l cholesterol, 0.4-0.6 mg/l, preferably 0.5 mg/l 2-deoxy-D-ribose, 800-1200 mg/l, preferably 1000 mg/l D-glucose, 0.04-0.06 mg/l, preferably 0.05 mg/l glutathione, 0.25-0.35 mg/l, preferably 0.3 mg/l guanine hydrochloride, 0.3-0.4 mg/l, preferably 0.354 mg/l hypoxanthine sodium, 0.4-0.6 mg/l, preferably 0.5 mg/l ribose, 0.25-0.35 mg/l, preferably 0.3 mg/l thymosin, 4-6 mg/l, preferably 5 mg/l Tween-80 (RTM: Polysorbate 80), 0.25-0.35 mg/l, preferably 0.3 mg/l uracil, 0.3-0.4 mg/l, preferably 0.34 mg/l xanthine sodium, and 8-12 mg/l, preferably 10 mg/l phenol red. In the step (i), the BY basic culture solution further includes sodium selenite having concentration of 0.2-0.3 mg/l for example 0.25 mg/l, copper sulfate having concentration of which is 0.05-0.1 mg/l as an anhydrate, i.e. 0.075 mg/l. Preferred Method: The pH of the phosphate-buffered saline is 7 and prepared by taking 9.39 g dipotassium hydrogen phosphate and 3.5 g potassium dihydrogen phosphate, adding water to make 1000 ml, filtering, and sterilizing at 115 degrees C for 30 minute. In the step (b), the maturation medium is a BY basic medium supplemented with 100 ml/l fetal bovine serum, 10 mu g/ml follicular stimulating hormone (FSH), 10 mu g/ml luteinizing hormone (LH), 1 mu g/ml estradiol (E2), and 20 ng/ml epidermal growth factor (EGF).

PT P
PN CN112892530-A; CN112892530-B
TI Atomic-grade dispersed noble metal three-way            catalyst useful in exhaust emission purification            treatment of gasoline vehicles comprise e.g. active            precious metals composed of modified alumina, catalytic            promoter including rare earth oxide.
AU WANG C
   ZHAO Y
   FENG F
   YANG D
   XIA W
   HE X
   REN D
   LU B
AE KUNMING SINO PLATINUM METALS CATALYST CO (KUNM-Non-standard)
GA 202167122C
AB    NOVELTY - Atomic-grade dispersed noble metal three-way                catalyst comprises active precious metals,                supporting materials, and catalytic promoter. The                active precious metal includes 0.1-1.5 wt.%                platinum, 0.1-2.5 wt.% palladium, and/or 0.02-0.8                wt.% rhodium. The active precious metal is                atomically dispersed on the surface of the                supporting material or the catalytic promoter, and                metal dispersion is 130-100%. The active precious                metal supporting material comprises 25.2-95 wt.%                modified alumina. The catalytic promoter includes                0.2-70 wt.% barium compound, zirconium oxide, and                rare earth oxide. The preparation of active                precious metal comprises (1) controlling the pH                value of the slurry between 1-9.5, during the                process of adding precious metal precursors, (2)                heat-treating the catalyst in an oxidizing gas                stream.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in the exhaust emission                purification treatment of gasoline vehicles, gas                vehicles, and hybrid vehicles (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: is economical, and                environmentally friendly. The method: is easy to                carry out; and realizes noble metal efficient                utilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Atomic-grade dispersed noble metal three-way                catalyst comprises active precious metals,                supporting materials, and catalytic promoter. The                active precious metal includes 0.1-1.5 wt.%                platinum, 0.1-2.5 wt.% palladium, and/or 0.02-0.8                wt.% rhodium. The active precious metal is                atomically dispersed on the surface of the                supporting material or the catalytic promoter, and                metal dispersion is 130-100%. The active precious                metal supporting material comprises 25.2-95 wt.%                modified alumina. The catalytic promoter includes                0.2-70 wt.% barium compound, zirconium oxide, and                rare earth oxide. The preparation of active                precious metal comprises (1) controlling the pH                value of the slurry is 1-9.5, during the process of                adding precious metal precursors, (2) heat-treating                the catalyst in an oxidizing gas stream, where the                heat treatment temperature is 400-850 degrees C,                the heat treatment time is more than 10 minutes,                where the oxidizing gas stream is oxygen, water                vapor, and carbon dioxide, the oxygen content of                carbon dioxide and water vapor does not exceed 25%,                the content of carbon dioxide does not exceed 5%,                and using nitrogen. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also                included for preparing atomic-grade dispersed noble                metal three-way catalyst comprising (1) preparing                oxide carrier slurry, uniformly dispersing the                modified alumina, catalytic auxiliary agent in                de-ionized water, preparing into oxide carrier                slurry, or the modified alumina, catalytic agent                respectively uniformly dispersed in the de-ionized                water, separately preparing into oxide carrier                slurry, or uniformly dispersing modified aluminum                oxide, zirconium oxide and rare earth compound in                the de-ionized water, separately preparing into                oxide carrier slurry, or dispersing the modified                aluminum oxide and barium compound, zirconium oxide                and rare earth compound or both composite oxide in                de-ionized water, preparing into oxide carrier                slurry, or uniformly dispersing modified alumina                and cerium oxide, the catalytic agent in the                de-ionized water, separately preparing into oxide                carrier slurry, after fully stirring, measuring                oxide carrier slurry pH value, adding acid or                alkali solution to adjust the pH value to 1-9.5,                (2) adding the active noble metal, dispersing the                noble metal precursor solution at a rate of 20                drops to 300 drops per minute to the prepared oxide                carrier slurry, fully stirring, selecting acid or                alkali solution to adjust the pH value to 1-9.5,                continuously fully stirring and directly drying,                grinding to obtain powder material, directly                coating on the honeycomb carrier, drying to obtain                the integral catalyst, adding catalytic auxiliary                agent, fully stirring, drying, grinding to obtain                powder material, coating the honeycomb-shaped                carrier to obtain the integral catalyst, after                adding catalytic auxiliary agent, adding modified                alumina and fully stirring, drying, grinding to                obtain powder material, coating the honeycomb                carrier to obtain the integral catalyst, after                adding modified alumina, coating the obtained                powder material on the honeycomb carrier after                pulping again, drying, (3) placing the powder                material or integral catalyst in calcining furnace,                introducing oxygen, water vapor, carbon dioxide,                where the oxygen and water vapor content are not                more than 25 %, continuously passing the powder                material or integral catalyst, continuously                introducing oxygen and water vapor in the calcining                furnace, using the nitrogen as the balance gas, the                residence time of the powder material or the                integral catalyst in the constant temperature area                is more than 10 minutes by adjusting the conveying                speed of the conveying belt, performing thermal                treatment of the oxidizing gas flow, dispersing                active noble metal platinum, palladium, rhodium                component on the modified alumina or catalytic                agent surface to obtain the atom-grade dispersed                noble metal three-effect catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The active precious                  metal is dispersed on the surface of the                  supporting material or the catalytic promoter in                  the form of a single atom or 2-5 atom clusters to                  ensure that the metal dispersion degree is                  30-100%. The barium compound comprises barium                  oxide, barium nitrate, barium acetate, barium                  oxalate, barium hydroxide, barium carbonate,                  barium sulfate, and/or barium chloride. The rare                  earth oxide comprises scandium oxide, yttrium                  oxide, lanthanum oxide, cerium oxide,                  praseodymium oxide, neodymium oxide, samarium                  oxide, and/or europium oxide. The modified                  alumina, zirconia, and rare earth oxides are                  single metal oxides, or binary, ternary,                  quaternary, five-membered, six-membered,                  seven-membered, and eight-membered metal                  composite oxides. The precious metal precursor is                  any water-soluble precious metal compound.                  Preferred Method: The modified alumina is alumina                  modified by organic acids or organic bases or                  surfactants, and completely removing during the                  heat treatment process, or modifying alumina by                  the catalytic promoter. In the process, adjusting                  oxygen and carbon dioxide content in the                  oxidizing gas stream by supplementing industrial                  pure gas on the basis of compressed air carrying                  water vapor, adjusting water vapor content in the                  oxidizing gas stream by the flow rate of the                  carrier gas stream or the temperature of the                  vaporization tank, or accurately controlling the                  liquid water feed volume by a liquid phase mass                  flow meter.
