PN CN114192149-A
TI Preparing catalyst useful for producing methanol by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. dissolving zinc source and aluminum source in water, dissolving copper source, zinc source, aluminum source and zirconium source in water, reacting, continuously pressurizing and aging.
AU KANG X
AE SHANGHAI FANGMIN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202245045J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst comprises e.g. (1) dissolving the zinc source and aluminum source in water to form first salt solution, dissolving the copper source, zinc source, aluminum source and zirconium source in water to form second salt solution, dissolving the alkaline substance in water, and forming the first alkaline solution and second alkaline solution with different concentrations, and (2) introducing the first salt solution and first alkaline solution into the first homogenizing pump, reacting, and obtaining the first precipitate, subjecting the second salt solution and second alkaline solution into the second homogenizing pump, and reacting to obtain second precipitate, conveying the obtained first precipitate and second precipitate to the reactor at the same time, and stirring under the carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain third precipitate.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for producing methanol by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process, greatly improves low-temperature activity of the synthesis gas methanol catalyst, reaches carbon dioxide conversion rate to not greater than 31%, preferably 34%, and selectivity of methanol to not greater than 75.5%, preferably 83%, and has good industrial application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing catalyst comprises (1) dissolving the zinc source and aluminum source in water to form first salt solution, dissolving the copper source, zinc source, aluminum source and zirconium source in water to form second salt solution, dissolving the alkaline substance in water, and forming the first alkaline solution and second alkaline solution with different concentrations, (2) introducing the first salt solution and first alkaline solution into the first homogenizing pump, reacting, and obtaining the first precipitate, subjecting the second salt solution and second alkaline solution into the second homogenizing pump, and reacting to obtain second precipitate, conveying the obtained first precipitate and second precipitate to the reactor at the same time, and stirring under the carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain third precipitate, (3) continuously pressurizing and aging the third precipitate under the carbon dioxide atmosphere, separating solid and liquid to obtain catalyst precursor, and (4) calcining the catalyst precursor to obtain calcined product, mixing and molding the calcined product with graphite to obtain synthesis gas methanol catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the molar ratio of zinc ion to aluminum ion in the first salt solution is 4:1-1:4. In the step (1), the alkaline substance comprises sodium carbonate, where the concentration of the first alkaline solution is 2-5 wt.%, and the concentration of the second alkaline solution is 5-10 wt.%. In the step (2), independently pre-heating the first salt solution, first alkaline solution, second salt solution and second alkaline solution, passing into the homogenizing pump, where the temperature of the preheating is independently 60-80degrees Celsius. In the step (2), the pH of the first precipitate is 7-8, and the pH of the second precipitate is 7-8. In the step (2), the reactor further comprises deionized water, where the temperature of the deionized water in the reactor is 60-80degrees Celsius. In the step (3), the pressurizing and aging temperature is 60-80degrees Celsius, the pressurizing and aging pressure is 0.2-0.8 MPa, the pressurizing and aging time is 30-120 minutes. In the step (4), washing the catalyst precursor, drying, calcining, where the standard of the washing is as follows: the washing filtrate does not contain sodium ion and/or potassium ion, the drying temperature is 80-140degrees Celsius, and the drying time is 4-8 hours. In the step (4), the calcining temperature is 300-400degrees Celsius, the calcining time is 4-8 hours, where the calcining product comprises 62-72 wt.% copper oxide, 20-30 wt.% zinc oxide, 3-10 wt.% aluminum oxide and 1-3 wt.% zirconium dioxide, the adding amount of graphite is 3% of the total mass of the calcined product.

PN CN113945663-A
TI Determination of dissolved organic carbon isotopes            in water by total organic carbon-iron-manganese-mass            spectrometry, involves passing water samples into the            IRMS (iron-manganese-mass spectrometry) to stabilize            it, isotope mass spectrometer to test dissolved organic            carbon isotope in water samples.
AU SHEN K
   YANG H
   YIN X
   SU R
AE MIN NATURAL RESOURCES OCEANOGRAPHY THIRD (CNDG-C)
GA 2022191948
AB    NOVELTY - Determination of dissolved organic carbon                isotopes in water by total organic                carbon-iron-manganese-mass spectrometry, which                involves: i. filtering the collected water samples                with a filter membrane to remove particulate                organic carbon and insoluble impurities; ii.                acidifying the water sample obtained in step 1),                and adjusting the pH of the water sample to be less                than 2; iii.vibrating the water sample obtained in                step 2) ultrasonically to remove the dissolved                inorganic carbon in the water sample; iv. adding                the water sample obtained in step 3) into the total                organic carbon (TOC) automatic sample injection                tray, feeding oxygen, and continue to bubbling to                remove dissolved carbon dioxide and volatile                organic carbon in the sample; v. Through the TOC                sampling system, draw the water sample in step 4)                and directly injecting it into the high-temperature                oxidation tube, and combusting the dissolved                organic carbon in the water sample.
   USE - Determination of dissolved organic carbon                isotopes in water by total organic                carbon-iron-manganese-mass spectrometry.
   ADVANTAGE - Determination of dissolved organic carbon                isotopes in water by total organic                carbon-iron-manganese-mass spectrometry has simple                pretreatment process, does not need sample                freeze-drying, directly injects the acidified water                sample into the oxidation tube, and converts                dissolved organic carbon in the water body into                carbon dioxide under the optimal oxidation                efficiency by adjusting the temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Determination of dissolved organic carbon                isotopes in water by total organic                carbon-iron-manganese-mass spectrometry, which                involves: i. filtering the collected water samples                with a filter membrane to remove particulate                organic carbon and insoluble impurities; ii.                acidifying the water sample obtained in step 1),                and adjusting the pH of the water sample to be less                than 2; iii.vibrating the water sample obtained in                step 2) ultrasonically to remove the dissolved                inorganic carbon in the water sample; iv. adding                the water sample obtained in step 3) into the total                organic carbon (TOC) automatic sample injection                tray, feeding oxygen, and continue to bubbling to                remove dissolved carbon dioxide and volatile                organic carbon in the sample; v. Through the TOC                sampling system, draw the water sample in step 4)                and directly injecting it into the high-temperature                oxidation tube, and combusting the dissolved                organic carbon in the water sample with oxygen at                high temperature under the action of the platinum                (Pt) catalyst to generate carbon dioxide (CO2); vi.                under the carrier gas purging, the generated carbon                dioxide (CO2) is firstly passed through a                semiconductor refrigerator and a chemical                dehydration trap to remove the moisture in the                sample gas, and then, after being frozen and                enriched by liquid nitrogen, separating and                purifying by a chromatographic column, passing into                the IRMS (iron-manganese-mass spectrometry) to                stabilize it, isotope mass spectrometer to test                dissolved organic carbon isotope                deta13carbondissolved organic carbon (DOC) in water                samples; vii. accurately weighing the standard                samples of glucose, phthalic acid, potassium                acetate, sodium humate and caffeine, packing the                samples in tin cups, and measuring the deta13carbon                value of the solid standard samples by elemental                analyzer-stable isotope mass spectrometer; and                viii. preparing DOC standard solution of glucose,                phthalic acid, potassium acetate, sodium humate and                caffeine, repeat steps 1-6, and testing the                dissolved organic carbon isotope deta13carbonDOC of                the DOC standard solution.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a effect of different acid                pretreatment time on the delta13Carbon value of                lake water and karst water. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Acid added to the                  water sample is hydrochloric acid, catalyst is a                  platinum catalyst. Preferred Method: In step 3),                  the shaking is shaking at room temperature for                  15-20 minutes, then standing at room temperature,                  and reacting for three days. In step 4), the flow                  rate of oxygen introduced is 5 milliliter per                  minutes, and the continuous bubbling time is                  15-20 minutes. In step 5), the oxidation                  temperature of the high temperature oxidation                  tube is 850 degreesC. In step 5), the flow rate                  of oxygen gas introduced into the high                  temperature oxidation tube is 10 milliliter per                  minutes, and the oxygen supply time is 20                  seconds. In step 6), the carrier gas is                  high-purity helium gas, and the flow rate is 80                  milliliter per liter.

General compositions.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation);

PN CN113694747-A
TI Preparing polyimide mixed substrate membrane useful for carbon dioxide separation, comprises e.g. mixing zirconium chloride, 2-bromoterephthalic acid and dimethylformamide, ultrasonically treating to ensure that all reactants are completely dissolved, and drying overnight under vacuum.
AU WANG X
AE SHENZHEN SIDAHUI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021E1921U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyimide mixed substrate membrane comprises e.g. (a) mixing zirconium chloride, 2-bromoterephthalic acid and dimethylformamide, ultrasonically treating to ensure that all reactants are completely dissolved to obtain mixture, pouring terephthalic acid and dimethylformamide into the mixture, performing ultrasonic treatment, allowing to stand, washing the precipitated particles with dimethylformamide and methanol, centrifuging to remove unreacted impurities, and drying overnight under vacuum at 60degrees Celsius to obtain bromine-modified zirconium metal organic framework, and (b) preparing polyethylene glycol grafted zirconium metal organic framework.
   USE - The polyimide mixed substrate membrane is useful for carbon dioxide separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves problem of obvious non-selective gaps between the metal organic frame and polyimide layer, improves dispersibility of the zirconium metal organic frame in the base membrane, and enhances separation of carbon dioxide by the membrane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing polyimide mixed substrate membrane comprises (a) mixing zirconium chloride, 2-bromoterephthalic acid and dimethylformamide, ultrasonically treating to ensure that all reactants are completely dissolved to obtain mixture, pouring terephthalic acid and dimethylformamide into the mixture, performing ultrasonic treatment, allowing to stand, washing the precipitated particles with dimethylformamide and methanol, centrifuging to remove unreacted impurities, and drying overnight under vacuum at 60degrees Celsius to obtain bromine-modified zirconium metal organic framework, (b) preparing polyethylene glycol grafted zirconium metal organic framework as follows: (b1) dissolving polyethylene glycol, catalyst silver oxide and potassium iodide in dichloromethane, adding p-toluenesulfonyl chloride, magnetically stirring at room temperature for 2-3 hours, and filtering product A, dissolving in 25-28 wt.% concentrated aqueous ammonia, adding ammonium chloride, stirring magnetically at room temperature for three days, extracting with dichloromethane, evaporating to remove the solvent to obtain polyethylene glycol with hydroxy group at one end and amino group (HO-PEGNH2) at another end, and (b2) ultrasonically dispersing the bromine-modified zirconium metal organic framework in ethanol solvent, adding HO-PEG-NH2, evenly stirring, carrying out substitution reaction in nitrogen atmosphere, completing the reaction, filtering, washing with acetone, and drying to obtain polyethylene glycol grafted zirconium metal organic framework, (c) preparing modified polyimide as follows: (c1) adding 3,5-diaminobenzoic acid, 4,4'-diaminodiphenyl ether and dimethylacetamide into reaction flask in nitrogen atmosphere, stirring uniformly, adding dimethylacetamide solution containing pyromellitic dianhydride into the mixed solution to perform synthesis reaction, completing the reaction, and obtaining the side chain carboxyl-containing polyamic acid precursor, and (c2) dissolving the side chain carboxyl group-containing polyamic acid precursor in dimethylacetamide, diluting to solution with a mass fraction of 3-8%, and processing to imidization reaction, completing the reaction, washing with ethanol, and drying to obtain polyimide containing carboxyl group in the side chain, and (d) preparing polyimide mixed substrate membrane as follows: (d1) dissolving carboxyl group-containing polyimide in the side chain in anhydrous N,N-dimethylformamide, ultrasonically dispersing, adding polyethylene glycol grafted zirconium metal organic framework, reacting under heating and stirring conditions, centrifuging, and washing to obtain zirconium metal organic framework-polyethylene glycol grafted polyimide composite material, and (d2) dissolving zirconium metal organic framework-polyethylene glycol grafted polyimide composite material in dimethylacetamide solvent, ultrasonically treating for 1-3 hours, pouring the suspension on glass mold, evaporating, and drying the membrane to obtain polyimide mixed substrate membrane for carbon dioxide separation.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for polyimide mixed substrate membrane for carbon dioxide separation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (a), the mass ratio of zirconium chloride, 2-bromoterephthalic acid and dimethylformamide in the mixture is 1:1-2:50-60. In the step (a), the mass ratio of phthalic acid and dimethylformamide added is 1:1-100. In the step (a), the ultrasonic treatment time of the mixture is 20-120 minutes, and allowing to stand at condition of ultrasonically heating and allowing to stand at 70-80degrees Celsius for 12-24 hours. In the step (b1), the mass ratio of polyethylene glycol, p-toluene sulfonyl chloride, silver oxide and potassium iodide is 10:1-1.5:1-2:1-1.5. In the step (b1), the mass ratio of product A and ammonium chloride is 1:2-4. In the step (b2), the mass ratio between the bromine-modified zirconium metal organic framework and HO-PEG-NH2 is 10:50-100, and the reaction temperature of the grafting reaction is 10-30degrees Celsius, and the reaction time is 1-5 hours. In the step (c1), the mass ratio of 3,5-diaminobenzoic acid, 4,4'-diaminodiphenyl ether and pyromellitic dianhydride is 8-20:60-80:100, and the reaction temperature of the synthesis reaction is 10-30degrees Celsius, and the reaction time is 18-36 hours. In the step (c2), the reaction temperature of the imidization reaction is 90-200degrees Celsius, and the reaction time is 18-36 hours. In the step (d1), the weight ratio of the polyethylene glycol grafted zirconium metal organic framework and the polyimide containing carboxyl group in the side chain is 1:10-100, heating and stirring the reaction at 50-100degrees Celsius for 3- 6 hours. In the step (d2), carrying out evaporation process at 40-60degrees Celsius for 12-24 hours, and drying at 80-120degrees Celsius for 12-28 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - An apparatus (100) consists of a supply unit                (A) for supplying a raw material gas comprising                methane and carbon dioxide, a synthetic gas                generation unit (103) for generating a synthesis                gas comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen while                heating a catalytic structure (c1), a supply unit                (B) for supplying synthesis gas discharged from the                synthesis gas generation unit, a lower                olefin-containing gas generation unit for                generating the lower olefin containing gas                containing propylene from carbon monoxide and                hydrogen contained in the synthesis gas supplied                from the supply unit (B) while heating the catalyst                structure (c2), and a detection unit (u1) for                detecting propylene in the lower olefin-containing                gas discharged from the lower olefin-containing gas                generation unit. The catalytic structure (c1) is                provided with a carrier (cc1) having a porous                structure comprising a zeolite-type compound and                metal microparticles (m1) arranged within the                carrier (cc1).
   USE - Apparatus used for producing lower                olefin-containing gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus has suppressed deterioration of                the catalytic activity, increased selectivity of                the hydrocarbon, and is capable of efficiently                producing lower olefin-containing gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An apparatus (100) consists of a supply unit                (A) for supplying a raw material gas comprising                methane and carbon dioxide, a synthetic gas                generation unit (103) for generating a synthesis                gas comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen while                heating a catalytic structure (c1), a supply unit                (B) for supplying synthesis gas discharged from the                synthesis gas generation unit, a lower                olefin-containing gas generation unit for                generating the lower olefin containing gas                containing propylene from carbon monoxide and                hydrogen contained in the synthesis gas supplied                from the supply unit (B) while heating the catalyst                structure (c2), and a detection unit (u1) for                detecting propylene in the lower olefin-containing                gas discharged from the lower olefin-containing gas                generation unit. The catalytic structure (c1) is                provided with a carrier (cc1) having a porous                structure comprising a zeolite-type compound and                metal microparticles (m1) arranged within the                carrier (cc1). The carrier (cc1) has a passage (p1)                communicating with the outside of the carrier                (cc1). The metal microparticles (m1) are located in                the passage (p1). The catalytic structure (c2) is                provided with a carrier (cc2) and including a                porous structure comprising zeolite-type compound,                and metal microparticles (m2) arranged within the                carrier (cc2). The carrier (cc2) has a passage (p2)                communicating with the outside of the carrier                (cc1). The portion of the passage (p2) has an                average inner diameter of 0.95 nm or less. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of                lower olefin-containing gas, which involves (S1)                supplying a raw material gas containing the methane                and the carbon dioxide to the catalyst structure                (c1), (S2) generating a synthesis gas containing                the carbon monoxide and hydrogen from the methane                and carbon dioxide contained in the raw material                gas while heating the catalyst structure (c1), (S3)                supplying the synthesis gas obtained in the step                (S2) to the catalyst structure (c2), (S4)                generating a lower olefin-containing gas containing                propylene from carbon monoxide and hydrogen                contained in the synthesis gas while heating the                catalyst structure (c2), and (S5) detecting                propylene in the lower olefin-containing gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of main                component comprising the apparatus for producing                lower olefin-containing gas.

TI Device for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide, has anode chamber comprising anode made of carbon cloth, cathode chamber, intermediate proton exchange membrane, and electrode material placed in anode and cathode chambers.
AU BIAN B
   WANG C
   LI H
   DAI W
   BIAN F
   XU G

   ZHANG X
AE XI'AN MODERN CHEM RES INST (CHNI-C)
GA 201897144L
AB    NOVELTY - An electrochemical reduction device has an anode chamber comprising anode made of carbon cloth coated with 0.1-0.8 mg/cm2 platinum/carbon or iridium oxide/carbon catalyst and anolyte, a cathode chamber, an intermediate proton exchange membrane, and a corresponding electrode material that is placed in the anode chamber and cathode chamber. The electrode material is connected with an external electrochemical workstation to form a loop. The cathode metal chamber is filled with a porous metal hollow fiber cathode, and an external carbon dioxide gas bag that is connected through an air guiding tube to provide an inorganic carbon source for growth of microorganisms on the cathode. The cathode chamber comprises catholyte comprising ammonium chloride, magnesium sulfate, sodium chloride, calcium chloride, potassium chloride, monopotassium phosphate, dipotassium hydrogen phosphate trihydrate, sodium bicarbonate, and trace elements.
   USE - Device for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide, and production of electrode material and microbial electrolysis cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is operated at low cost, can be used for a long period of time, and has high energy utilization rate. The three-dimensional cathode material has large specific surface area, facilitating direct transmission of carbon dioxide that is beneficial to the growth of the cathode microorganism.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An electrochemical reduction device has an anode chamber comprising anode made of carbon cloth coated with 0.1-0.8 mg/cm2 platinum/carbon or iridium oxide/carbon catalyst and anolyte, a cathode chamber, an intermediate proton exchange membrane, and a corresponding electrode material that is placed in the anode chamber and cathode chamber. The electrode material is connected with an external electrochemical workstation to form a loop. The anolyte composition comprises is 0.3-1.5 g/l ammonium chloride, 0.3-0.8 g/l magnesium sulfate, 0.5-2.5 g/l sodium chloride, 0.01-0.2 g/l calcium chloride, 0-0.1 g/l potassium chloride, 0.1-0.25 g/l monopotassium phosphate, 0-0.35 g/l dipotassium hydrogen phosphate trihydrate, and solvent including water. The loop comprises 0.3-0.8 g/l magnesium sulfate, 0.5-2.5 g/l sodium chloride, 0.01-0.2 g/l calcium chloride, 0-0.1 g/l potassium chloride, 0.1-0.25 g/l potassium phosphate, 0-0.35 g/l dipotassium hydrogen phosphate trihydrate and solvent including water. The cathode metal chamber is filled with a porous metal hollow fiber cathode, and an external carbon dioxide gas bag that is connected through an air guiding tube to provide an inorganic carbon source for growth of microorganisms on the cathode. The cathode chamber comprises catholyte comprising 0.3-1.5 g/l ammonium chloride, 0.3-0.8 g/l magnesium sulfate, 0.5-2.5 g/l sodium chloride, 0.01-0.2 g/l calcium chloride, 0-0.1 g/l potassium chloride, 0.1-0.25 g/l monopotassium phosphate, 0-0.35 g/l dipotassium hydrogen phosphate trihydrate, 1-4 g/l sodium bicarbonate, and 10 ml/l trace elements. The trace elements include 1.5 g/l ferric chloride, 10 ml/l 7.7M hydrochloric acid, 0.006 g/l cobalt chloride hexahydrate, 0.036 g/l manganese chloride tetrahydrate, 0.024 g/l zinc chloride, 0.002 g/l boric acid, 0.19 g/l sodium manganate, 0.1 g/l nickel chloride hexahydrate, and 0.07 g/l copper chloride dihydrate. The catholyte has pH value of 6.5-8.5.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. PT P
PN CN108277207-A; CN108277207-B
TI Online amplification of chicken infectious bursal disease virus useful for preparing vaccinating agent, by performing three-stage online amplification culture using microcarriers, infecting cells and setting virus adsorption parameters.
AU LI Z
   YIN S
   WU H
   JIANG D
   REN Z
AE GUANGZHOU QIZHI BIOLOGICAL ENG EQUIP CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201856088W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for online amplification of chicken infectious bursal disease virus, involves (a) preparing seed cells by taking adherent cells for spinner culture to obtain seed cells, (b) when number of cells reaches 0.65-1.3x 1010, performing three-stage online amplification culture by transferring the seed cells or monolayer cells to bioreactor, adding microcarriers and setting dissolved oxygen to 40-50, pH to 7.2-7.3, temperature to 32-37 degrees C, speed to 30-100 revolutions per minute (rpm), oxygen to 0.2-0.4 MPa/(l/minute)/minute, carbon dioxide to 0.2-0.6 MPa/(l/minute)/minute, and air to 0.2-0.4 MPa/(l/minute)/minute, (c) washing the carrier with phosphate buffered saline, removing culture liquid and serum, adding the virus solution and washed cell liquid to a tank containing bovine serum free Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium/F12 culture solution, infecting cells and setting virus adsorption parameters and (d) setting virus culture parameters and harvesting virus solution.
   USE - The method is useful for online amplification of chicken infectious bursal disease virus for preparing vaccinating agent for chicken infectious bursal disease virus (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for online amplification of chicken infectious bursal disease virus, involves (a) preparing seed cells by taking adherent cells for spinner culture to obtain seed cells, (b) when number of cells reaches 0.65-1.3x 1010, performing three-stage online amplification culture by transferring the seed cells or monolayer cells to a bioreactor, adding 3-5 g/l microcarriers, where total volume of culture solution is 65-75% of the bioreactor, setting dissolved oxygen to 40-50, pH to 7.2-7.3, temperature to 32-37 degrees C, speed to 30-100 revolutions per minute (rpm), oxygen to 0.2-0.4 MPa/(l/minute)/minute, carbon dioxide to 0.2-0.6 MPa/(l/minute)/minute, and air to 0.2-0.4 MPa/(l/minute)/minute, and after 3-4 hours, adjusting cell culture parameters such as dissolved oxygen to 40-60, pH to 7-7.5, temperature to 35-38 degrees C, speed to 30-80 rpm, oxygen to 0.2-0.4 MPa/(l/minute)/minute, carbon dioxide to 0.2-0.6 MPa/(l/minute)/minute, air to 0.2-0.4 MPa/(l/minute)/minute and perfusion flow rate to 25-65% of total volume of bioreactor, (c) when cell density reaches 2.58-4.73x 108 cells/g microcarrier, washing the carrier with phosphate buffered saline (PBS), removing culture liquid and serum, adding the virus solution and washed cell liquid to a tank containing bovine serum free Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium (DMEM)/F12 culture solution, infecting cells, and setting virus adsorption parameters: dissolved oxygen to 30-40, pH to 7.4-7.8, temperature to 33-37 degrees C, speed to 30-50 rpm, oxygen to 0.2-0.3 MPa/(l/minute)/minute, carbon dioxide to 0.2-0.4 MPa/(l/minute)/minute and air to 0.2-0.4 MPa/(l/minute)/minute, and (d) after 1-2 hours, setting virus culture parameters: dissolved oxygen to 30-50, pH to 7.35-7.4, temperature to 33-37 degrees C, speed to 40-60 rpm, oxygen to 0.2-0.3 MPa/(l/minute)/minute, carbon dioxide to 0.2-0.4 MPa/(l/minute)/minute and air to 0.2-0.4 MPa/(l/minute)/minute, and when 70-85% of cytopathic lesions appeared, harvesting virus solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The adherent cells are chosen from chicken embryo DF-1 cells and MDCK cells. The bioreactor is bubble-free aerated bioreactor having 2 l, 5 l, 7 l, 25 l, 75 l, 200 l and 500 l capacity. In step (b), the culture solution comprises 90-99% DMEM/F12 medium, 1-10% bovine serum and 0.001-0.02% antibiotics and has pH of 6.5-8. The monolayer cells are obtained by trypsin digestion by washing the carrier with sterile PBS, removing culture fluid and serum, adjusting pH to 7.6-7.8, and temperature to 35-37 degrees C, adding 0.01-0.025% trypsin, stirring for 3-5 minutes, and adding 2-10% culture solution to terminate digestion. The microcarrier is cross-linked dextran prepared by swelling dextran with water, adding solid sodium hydroxide and allyl-2,3-epoxypropyl ether, heating upto 50-60 degrees C, pressing, sequentially washing with water-ethanol-water to obtain activated dextran microspheres, adding water and anhydrous sodium acetate, stirring uniformly, adding bromine water, reacting the mixture for 10 minutes, adding sodium formate to terminate reaction, continuously stirring for 30 minutes, filtering to obtain dextran gel, adding 0.1 g/ml lysine or polylysine solution, adjusting pH to 8.5-9, reacting the mixture at 45 degrees C for 24 hours, immersing in tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane (tris) buffer having pH of 8 and acetic acid buffer having pH of 4 for 5 times, immersing in a 0.9% sodium chloride solution for 1 time, dehydrating using acetone, and thoroughly drying in an oven at 70 degrees C. In step (c), inoculation amount of virus is 0.001-0.1%.
   ACTIVITY - Virucide.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Vaccine.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN114766367-A
TI Culturing kiwifruit tissue involves inoculating            kiwifruit tissue culture seedling into a rooting            medium, and performing rooting culture in a culture            container under light conditions to obtain kiwifruit            seedlings, where carbon dioxide is introduced into            culture container during the rooting culture.
AU GAO Z
   ZHANG A
   LIU Y
   WANG Z
   LIU Z
AE UNIV NORTHWEST A & F (UNAF-C)
GA 202298569C
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing kiwifruit tissue involves                inoculating kiwifruit tissue culture seedling into                a rooting medium, and performing rooting culture in                a culture container under light conditions to                obtain kiwifruit seedlings. The rooting medium does                not contain sugar. The carbon dioxide is introduced                into the culture container during the rooting                culture.
   USE - Method for culturing kiwifruit tissue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method cultures kiwifruit tissue, which                adopts photoautotrophic micro-propagation                technology, makes full use of rapidity of plant                tissue culture technology and integrity of                environmental engineering regulation, uses carbon                dioxide to induce tissue culture seedling for                photosynthesis that solve problem of long rooting                cycle and low efficiency of traditional tissue                culture, reduces production cost, ensures that                supply of carbon dioxide enables the tissue culture                seedlings to perform photoautotrophy, which changes                previous heterotrophic culture method powered by                sucrose so that growth of tissue culture seedlings                is close to the natural state, which is more                favorable for tissue culture seedlings to adapt to                field environment, and does not require time for                transplanting and domestication, solves problem of                high pollution rate, difficult rooting, long                rooting cycle, weak photosynthetic ability and poor                quality of seedlings of kiwifruit tissue culture                seedlings, and promotes industrialized production                of kiwifruit seedling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide is                  introduced on the third day of the rooting                  culture, where flow rate of carbon dioxide is                  0.8-1.2L/minute on 3rd to 7th days of the rooting                  culture, and flow rate of carbon dioxide is                  1.8-2.2L/minute on 8th day of the rooting                  culture. The carbon dioxide concentration in the                  environment of the rooting culture is maintained                  at 800-1200&#956;mol/mol from the third day                  of culture. The introduction frequency of carbon                  dioxide is once every 15-30 minutes, and                  introduction time of each time is 3-8 minutes.                  The illumination is 8 hours and darkness is 16                  hours every day, and the illumination intensity                  is lower than 1000lx on 0th to 7th days of the                  rooting culture, and light intensity is 2500-3000                  lx every day with 14 hours of light and 10 hours                  of darkness on 8th day of the rooting culture.                  The kiwifruit tissue culture seedlings are                  cultivated by placing kiwifruit stem section in                  the explant medium to carry out explant culture,                  where length of stem section sprouted axillary                  buds and axillary buds is more than 2cm. The                  axillary buds are transplanted into a                  proliferation medium for proliferation culture to                  obtain kiwifruit tissue culture seedlings. The                  explant culture medium takes water as a solvent,                  and contains 4.43g/L Murashige and Skoog (MS),                  30g/L sucrose, 0.2mg/L 1-naphthaleneacetic acid                  (NAA) and 6-7g/L agar. The light intensity of                  explant culture is 2500-3000lx and temperature of                  the explant culture is 22-26&#176;C. The                  proliferation medium uses water as a solvent, and                  contains 4.43g/L MS, 30g/L sucrose, 0.1-0.6mg/L                  NAA, 0.1mg/L 6-benzylaminopurine (6-BA) and                  6-7g/L agar. The light intensity of the                  proliferation culture is 2500-3000lx and                  temperature of the proliferation culture is                  22-26&#176;C. The rooting medium comprises a                  vermiculite substrate and a nutrient solution,                  and humidity of the rooting medium is                  &#8805;70%. The nutrient solution takes                  &#189; MS as basic nutrient solution, and                  further comprises 0.5-2.0mg/L indole-3-butyric                  acid.

The first coating film is coated on the surface of                the inner core particle. The second coating film is                coated on the surface of the first coating                film.

   ADVANTAGE - The fracturing and supporting agent: utilizes                amine groups and carbon dioxide molecule to form                carbon-nitrogen (CN) covalent bonds to achieve the                purpose of adsorbing carbon dioxide; seals the part                of carbon dioxide in the ground after fracturing;                meets the policy direction of carbon                neutralization; and improves the greenhouse                effect.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine group                  compound is polyetherimide and/or ethanolamine. The first resin                  is phenolic resin, furan resin, urea-formaldehyde                  resin and/or epoxy resin. The second resin is                  epoxy resin and/or epichlorohydrin. In the step                  (i), the mass ratio of the first resin and the                  inner core particles is 1:25-2:25. In the step                  (ii), the mass ratio of second resin and amine                  group compound is 1:1-1:20.The content of the sum                  of the mass of the second resin and amine group                  compound in first solution is 5-20 wt.%.Preferred Method: The step (i) further                  comprises (ia) grinding the inner core particles                  to 20-40 meshes, and (ib) heating the ground core                  particles to a temperature higher than the                  melting point of first resin, coating first resin                  on the surface of the core particles, cooling to                  80-105&#176;C, and spraying the curing agent                  solution for cooling to 20-80&#176;C to                  obtain primary film-coated particles. The step                  (ii) further comprises soaking primary                  film-coated particles in first solution and                  curing to obtain the fracturing and supporting                  agent, preferably soaking for 6-8 hours, where                  curing at 80-105&#176;C for 2-3 hours.

PN CN212776769-U
TI Dry ice storage system, has tank body formed with containing cavity for containing carbon dioxide gas, and controller for controlling on/off of compressor start-stop control branch of compressor according to pressure value detected by pressure detector.
AU ZHANG Y
   LIAO Y
   ZHANG G
   LI T
   LU G
   CAO W
   CHEN M
AE LONGYAN TOBACCO IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 202132277N
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a dry ice storage system, relating to the field of tobacco machinery, for optimizing the structure of dry ice storage system, reducing the waste of carbon dioxide. The dry ice storage system comprises a tank body, a compressor, a pressure detector and a controller. The tank body comprises a containing cavity for containing carbon dioxide gas. the compressor is connected with the containing cavity, and is configured to compress the carbon dioxide gas in the containing cavity. the pressure detector is installed on the tank body, and is configured to detect the gas pressure in the containing cavity. the controller is in communication connection with the pressure detector; the controller is configured to control conduction and disconnection of the first compressor start-stop control branch of the compressor according to the pressure value detected by the pressure detector. the compressor is started under the condition that the first compressor start-stop control branch is conducted; the compressor is stopped when the first compressor start-stop control branch is disconnected. The technical solution of the invention optimizes the structure of the dry ice storage system, realizes the real-time starting compressor.

TI Regenerating a liquid absorbent for absorbing            carbon dioxide from a gas stream, involves contacting            the liquid absorbent with a hydrophobic medium, and            liquid absorbent comprises at least one amine and            degradation product.
AU PUXTY G
   WEBSTER-GARDINER M
   CONWAY W
   YANG Q
   BENNETT R
   FERON P
   PROULXTY G
   BENNET R
   PHILON P
AE COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
   COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
   COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
   COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
GA 2020A7064J
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating a liquid absorbent involves                contacting the liquid absorbent with a hydrophobic                medium. The liquid absorbent comprises at least one                amine (I) and degradation product comprising at                least one imine (II), and selectively extracted the                degradation product into or through the hydrophobic                medium.
   USE - Method for regenerating a liquid absorbent for                absorbing carbon dioxide from a gas stream                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to regenerated a liquid                absorbent with improved stability against                degradation and/or amenability to regeneration, and                still provide satisfactory gas absorption                capability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Regenerating a liquid absorbent involves                contacting the liquid absorbent with a hydrophobic                medium. The liquid absorbent comprises at least one                amine of formula (I) and degradation product                comprising at least one imine of formula (II), and                selectively extracted the degradation product into                or through the hydrophobic medium.
   Ar'=is independently an aromatic group;                and
   R=independently selected from hydrogen or an                organyl group.
   . An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                method for absorbing carbon dioxide from a gas                stream containing carbon dioxide, which involves                contacting the gas stream with a liquid absorbent                comprising at least one amine of formula (I), where                the amine of formula (I) degrades to form a                degradation product comprising at least one imine                of formula (II), and regenerating the liquid                absorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrophobic medium                  is an organic solvent. The organic solvent is                  selected from the group consisting of aromatic                  hydrocarbon solvents, oxygen-containing solvents                  and halogenated solvents. The liquid absorbent                  comprises at least one additional base selected                  from tertiary amines, hindered amines, carbonate                  salts, amino acid salts and mixtures. The                  aliphatic amine is selected from unhindered                  primary or secondary amines and alkanolamines.                  Preferred Conditions: The regenerating a liquid                  absorbent process further comprising separating                  the liquid absorbent from the hydrophobic medium                  and recycling the liquid absorbent to a liquid                  absorbent stream in a gas absorption process, and                  further comprising converting the aldehyde to                  form a second amount of regenerated amine of                  formula (I) by reductive amination. The liquid                  absorbent is an aqueous composition comprising at                  least 10 wt.% water. Preferred Process: The                  reductive amination involves reacting the                  aldehyde with hydroxylamine to form an oxime, and                  reducing the oxime to form the second amount of                  regenerated amine.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110551317-A
TI Preparing organic amine-loaded silicon-based aerogel in carbon dioxide adsorption application, mixing aminopropyltrimethoxysilane, ethanol and water, hydrolyzing, adding tetraethyl orthosilicate, freezing to obtain alcohol gel and drying.
AU YANG X
   CHEN H
   HAN M
   CHEN X
   LIN Z
AE UNIV GUANGDONG TECHNOLOGY (UGTE-C)
GA 2019A5791G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing an organic amine-loaded silicon-based aerogel, involves (i) mixing aminopropyltrimethoxysilane, ethanol and water and hydrolyzing at 20-40 degrees C for 12-14 hours, (ii) adding tetraethyl orthosilicate to the system of step (i) after completing the hydrolysis process, stirring at 20-40 degrees C for 3-5 minutes to obtain a sol and freezing at -10 to -18 degrees C for 12-18 hours to obtain an alcohol gel, and (iii) leaving still the alcohol gel at 20-40 degrees C for 12-18 hours, controlling the temperature gradients at 55-65 degrees C, 75-85 degrees C and 115-125 degrees C and drying for 2-4 hours to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing organic amine-loaded silicon-based aerogel used in carbon dioxide adsorption application (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of organic amine-loaded silicon-based aerogel is a nano-material with a three-dimensional network structure, has the characteristics of light weight, low density, high specific surface area, high porosity, uniform pore size distribution and surface chemical customization, and is used as a carbon dioxide adsorbent with excellent performance and captures carbon dioxide in the environment with high efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of aminopropyltrimethoxysilane, tetraethyl orthosilicate and ethanol is (1-2.6):(4-5):(27-33), preferably 1.5:27.48:24.48. The molar ratio of aminopropyltrimethoxysilane and water is (1-2.6):(24-41). Preferred Conditions: The hydrolysis reaction in step (i) is carried out at 25 degrees C. The stirring reaction in step (ii) is carried out at 25 degrees C. The leaving still treatment in step (iii) is carried out at 25 degrees C. The temperature gradient in step (ii) is controlled at 60 degrees C, 80 degrees C and 120 degrees C.

TI Absorbent composition for carbon dioxide capture and reducing concentration of carbon dioxide in process stream comprises amino-siloxane.
AU PERRY R J
AE GE OIL & GAS INC (GENE-C)
   GE OIL & GAS INC (GENE-C)
   GE OIL & GAS INC (GENE-C)
GA 2018819820
AB    NOVELTY - An absorbent composition comprises amino-siloxane.
   USE - An absorbent composition for carbon dioxide capture and reducing concentration of carbon dioxide in process stream.
   ADVANTAGE - Absorbent compositions display at least satisfactory carbond dioxide uptake and maintained a substantially liquid, flowable state after reaction. Amino-siloxane compositions have low volatility, high thermal stability, and a high net capacity for absorption capability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An absorbent composition comprises amino-siloxane of formula R3-Si(R1)2-O-(Si(R1)2-O)n-Si(R1)2-R2-NH-CH2CH2-X.
   R1=1-6C aliphatic or aromatic radical;
   R2=2-10C aliphatic or aromatic radical;
   R3=1-18C aliphatic or aromatic radical or R4;
   R4=structure of formula -R2-NH-CH2CH2-X;
   X=electron donating group; and
   n= greater than or equal to 1.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) absorbent composition comprising amino-siloxane of formula R3-(Si(O-Si(R1)2-R2-NH-CH2CH2X)2-O)w-(Si(R1)(O-Si(R1)2-R2-NH-CH2CH2X))y-(Si(R1)2O)z-Si(R1)2-R2-NH-CH2CH2X; and
   (2) absorbent composition comprising amino-siloxane of formula (V).
   R3=1-18C aliphatic or aromatic radical or R4;
   R4=structure of formula -O-Si(R1)2-R2-NH-CH2CH2X or -R2-NHCH2CH2X in formula (V);
   n=1-6;
   w=0-5;
   y=0-10;
   z=0-10; and
   w+y+z= greater than or equal to 1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: Absorbent composition is free of co-solvent with amino-siloxane. Preferred Process: Amino-siloxane forms a reaction product with carbon dioxide at 20-70 degrees C in which reaction product is liquid.
   Preferred Definitions:
   X=R5O-, R5S-, (R5)2N- or (R5)2P-, preferably CH3O-, C2H5O-, (C2H5)2N-, (C2H5)2P- or C2H5S-;
   R5=1-8C aliphatic or aromatic radical;
   R2=3C aliphatic radical in linear propyl form or branched isopropyl form; and
   n= greater than or equal to 3.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN108502868-A
TI Preparing oxygen-riched carbon nano-tube involves using carbon dioxide hydrogenation by taking carbon dioxide and hydrogen as raw material gas in certain volume ratio, and selected catalyst is nickel-iron/alumina catalyst.
AU JIANG C
   SUN S
   CHU W
   HU J
   LI J
   DENG J
   YING J
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 201872400K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing oxygen-riched carbon nano-tube involves using carbon dioxide hydrogenation by taking carbon dioxide and hydrogen as a raw material gas in a certain volume ratio, and selected catalyst is nickel-iron/alumina (Ni-Fe/Al2O3) catalyst, spreading 100 milligram catalyst in two porcelain boats, placing porcelain boat in middle of horizontal quartz tube, firstly passing 30 milliliter per minute nitrogen, draining air of the reactor, and then passing nitrogen , after 20 minutes, and switching to hydrogen with flow rate of 50 milliliter per minute. The gas is switched while reactor started to heat from room temperature at 400-600 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, again switched hydrogen to nitrogen, continued to raised temperature to 500-800 degrees C. The first stage of exhaust gas is used for reaction for 120-360 minutes, after reaction is finished.
   USE - Method for preparing oxygen-riched carbon nano-tube.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare oxygen-riched carbon nano-tube which has high research value and industrial value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The system is taken out in nitrogen atmosphere and then cooled to room temperature. The porcelain boat is taken out, and nano-carbon is weighed for product quality, and calculated carbon yield and heating rate of all heating processes which is 2-10 degrees C per minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The volume ratio of hydrogen and carbondioxide raw material gas is 4.5:1, where volume flow rate of carbon dioxide is 10 millilite per minute. The reducing temperature is 500 degrees C and reducing duration is 60 minutes. The heating speed of the heating process is 5 degrees C per minute. Preferred Process: The preparation of oxygen-riched carbon nano-tube involves using nickel-iron/alumina (Ni-Fe/Al2O3) catalyst, and perorming citric acid combustion method by adding nickel ion, ferrous ion in beaker which is filled with 500 milliliter secondary de-ionized water, then using a preservative film to sealed beaker opening under action of magnetic force stirrer and stirring at room temperature for 30 minutes, followed by adding citric acid according to a certain proportion of metal cation molar sum and citric acid with mol amount, after stirring process. The obtained solution is placed in magnetic stirrer for 120 minutes to make citric acid and active metal ion complex, putting beaker in an ultrasonic cleaner for ultrasonic 30 minutes, after stirring is finished with ultrasonic power of 60 watt, after finishing ultrasonic, followed by putting solution at 80 degrees C in water bath stirring at constant temperature, until solution after stirring to gel state together beaker and putting in a blast drying box at 120 degrees C for 12 hours, drying citric acid combustion, recycling combustion of sample substance in the bubble to cellular sponge, collecting sample, grinding into powder using an agate mortar, and then treating under static air at 350-550 degrees C for 3-5 hours. Preferred Condition: The mass ratio of nickel ion, ferrous ion, aluminum ion is 40:4:56. The molar weight of metal ion and citric acid is 1:1. The roasting temperature is 350 degrees C, 450 degrees C and 550 degrees C according to different roasting temperature, where each sample is named as NF-350, NF-450 and NF-550. The catalyst precursor roasting duration under different roasting temperature is 5 hours.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC J04-E01; J04-E04; J04-F02B
IP C01B-032/162

AU LEE G H
   JEON J Y

AE KOREA LAND & HOUSING CORP (KOLA-Non-standard)
GA 201846093P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining whether smoke density exceeds a smoke density threshold value over predetermined first period, and determining that fire occurs in a dwelling if carbon dioxide density exceeds a predetermined carbon dioxide density threshold value or fine dust concentration exceeds a predetermined fine dust concentration threshold value. A point of rising rate in which the smoke density exceeds the smoke density threshold value, is determined. Determination is made that the fire occurs in the dwelling if the rising rate exceeds predetermined fine dust concentration rising rate in predetermined second period that is longer than the first period.
   USE - Method for providing fire alarms in a dwelling.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables minimizing fault alarms in the dwellings based on the carbon dioxide density and the calculated fine dust concentration on the automatic ventilation system so as to enhance fire detection reliability by controlling the rising rate of carbon dioxide density and fine dust concentration, while minimizing installation fee.

   USE - Non-alkyl tin oxygen cluster compound used in                electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The synthesis method of the cluster compound                has simple process, easy repetition, cost-effective                raw materials and easy production. The non-alkyl                tin oxide has good stability and electrocatalytic                carbon dioxide reduction performance, and better                applied in the fields of environmental                protection.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Non-alkyl tin oxygen cluster compound has                molecular formula of C48H56Cl12N32O20Sn14                (excluding free molecules), where Mr is 3488.30,                space group is P-421c, cell parameter a and b are                18.0 Angstrom, and c is 6.7 Angstrom, alpha, beta,                and gamma are 90degrees, and V is 5448.9                Angstrom3.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for synthesizing a non-alkyl tin oxide cluster                compound, which involves: mixing tin salt and                nitrogen heterocyclic ligand, and carrying out                solvothermal reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The crystalline state                  of the tin oxygen cluster is tetragonal. The                  crystalline form of the tin oxide cluster has a                  cluster core size of left and right. The                  crystalline form of the tin oxide cluster has a                  symmetrical structure. The the tin salt is tin                  tetrachloride pentahydrate, stannous chloride                  dihydrate and tin sulfate, preferably tin                  tetrachloride pentahydrate. The nitrogen                  heterocyclic ligand is pyrazole, imidazole,                  pyrazine, piperazine and benzimidazole,                  preferrably pyrazole. Preferred Compositions: The                  molar ratio of the tin salt and nitrogen                  heterocycle is 1:(15-33). Preferred Conditions:                  The temperature of the solvothermal reaction is                  40-160degrees Celsius, preferably 60-100degrees                  Celsius, and more preferably 80degrees Celsius.                  The solvothermal reaction time is 24-240 hours,                  preferably 48-150 hours, more preferably 72 hours                  or 120 hours. Preferred Process: The tin salt and                  nitrogen heterocyclic ligand are uniformly mixed,                  and heated to perform solvothermal reaction to                  obtain a mixture. The mixture is separated after                  the reaction, cleaned obtained solid phase with                  alcohol, and dried to prepare the stannoxy                  cluster compound.

TI Catalyst for converting carbon dioxide to methanol            by hydrogenation, comprises mesoporous indium oxide,            and catalyst supported on mesoporous indium oxide,            where porous structure of mesoporous indium oxide            comprises mesopores and micropores.
AU GOAG T Y
   BAE J W
   CHOUNG J W
   HONG C H
   HWAN H
   JEONG J
   GOAG T
   BAE J
   HONG C
AE UNIV SUNGKYUNKWAN RES & BUSINESS FOUND (USKK-C)

   HYUNDAI MOTOR CO LTD (HYMR-C)

   UNIV SUNGKYUNKWAN RES & BUSINESS FOUND (USKK-C)

   KIA MOTORS CORP (KIAK-C)
GA 2022016252
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises mesoporous indium oxide,                and a catalyst supported on the mesoporous indium                oxide, where a porous structure of the mesoporous                indium oxide comprises Ia3d symmetry, and comprises                mesopores and micropores interconnecting the                mesopores.
   USE - Catalyst for converting carbon dioxide to                methanol by hydrogenation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has increased reactivity,                catalytic activity and specific surface area,                reduced deactivation, high stability for a long                time, excellent carbon dioxide conversion rate and                methanol selectivity, and reaction stability at                high temperature catalytic reaction                condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the catalyst, which involves:
   a. supporting a precursor of indium oxide on                mesoporous silica template;
   b. preparing mesoporous indium oxide by                separating the mesoporous silica template from the                precursor of the indium oxide; and
   c. supporting the catalyst on the mesoporous                indium oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises                  preparing a first solution comprising the                  precursor of the indium oxide, contacting the                  first solution with the mesoporous silica                  template, first drying the first solution and the                  mesoporous silica template on which the precursor                  of the indium oxide is supported at                  20-30degreesC, second drying at 60-90degreesC,                  and firing at 300-550degreesC. The step (b)                  comprises adding the mesoporous silica template                  on which the precursor of the indium oxide is                  supported into an alkaline aqueous solution, and                  reacting at 70-90degreesC for 2-4 hours. The step                  (c) comprises preparing a second solution                  comprising a secondary precursor of the catalyst,                  contacting the second solution with the                  mesoporous indium oxide, drying the second                  solution and the mesoporous indium oxide on which                  the precursor of the catalyst is supported at                  60-90degreesC and firing at                  300-550degreesC.Preferred Components: The mesoporous indium                  oxide has a specific surface area of greater than                  40 m2/g, a pore volume of greater than 0.05                  cm3/g, a particle size of 15-20 nm and a size of                  2-20 nm. The mesoporous indium oxide is prepared                  using a mesoporous silica template. The catalyst                  is formed of1-9 wt.% palladium, and 1-5 wt.%                  cerium oxide and/or gallium oxide. The first                  solution contains 5-10 wt.% indium oxide                  precursor.
   EXAMPLE - 0.5 g m-Indium(III) oxide was provided and                dried in an oven at 80degreesC for 30 minutes.                0.011 g Palladium(II) nitrate hydrate was provided                and dissolved in 0.1 ml distilled water. After it                was dissolved, it was extracted with a pipette,                dripped dropwise into a dried mesoporous indium                oxide, and rubbed with a spoon. The operation was                repeated until the solution in the pipette was used                up. After the dry support was finished, it was put                in the oven at 80degreesC and dried overnight. The                solution was fired at 300degreesC for 3 hours to                prepare a catalyst palladium (I)- Indium(III)                oxide.

AU LI J

   WANG Y
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 202207970Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mixed matrix film based on intercalation type montmorillonite/copper-tetrahydro-3-tetrachloro-2 composite material involves using montmorillonite, trihydrate copper nitrate, trimesic acid as raw material, in-situ synthesizing metal organic framework material copper-tetrahydro-3-tetrachloro-2 between the montmorillonite layers, then using 3-amino propyl triethoxy silane to perform amination modification to the montmorillonite of copper-tetrahydro-3-tetrachloro-2 intercalation, then adding the aminated nano material to the polyvinyl amine solution, uniformly mixing to prepare casting solution and coating on the surface of the microporous filtering film to obtain product.
   USE - Method for preparing mixed matrix film based on intercalation type montmorillonite/copper-tetrahydro-3-tetrachloro-2 composite material used in carbon dioxide separation and purification (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mixed matrix film has high carbon dioxide flux and selectivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The microporous filtering film is polysulfone, polyether sulfone, polyvinylidene fluoride, polytetrafluoroethylene, cellulose acetate in any one of the interception molecular weight range of the microporous filtering film is 1000-50000. Preferred Conditions: The method involves preparing intercalation type montmorillonite/copper-tetrahydro-3-tetrachloro-2 composite material in the montmorillonite dispersion, adding trihydrate copper nitrate and trimesic acid in-situ synthesis intercalation type montmorillonite/copper-tetrahydro-3-tetrachloro-2 composite material, adding the montmorillonite into the de-ionized water to prepare the dispersion liquid with mass percentage content of 0.5-10 %, using ultrasonic dispersion machine at the temperature of 15-60degrees Celsius, the ultrasonic frequency is 30-60 kiloHertz, ultrasonic power is 400-800 W, ultrasonically dispersing for 20-40 minutes, then heating to dissolve to obtain montmorillonite dispersion in 80?oC water bath, naming as solution A, adding copper nitrate trihydrate in the solution A, the temperature is 20-80degrees Celsius, the rotating speed is 100-500 r/minutes, mechanically stirring for 1-12 hours until the solution is mixed uniformly, adding the trimesic acid into ethanol, the temperature is 20-80degrees Celsius, the rotating speed is 100-500 r/minutes mechanically stirring for 20-360 minutes to dissolve completely, adding the solution C into the solution B, where the temperature is 20-80degrees Celsius, rotating speed is 100-500 r/minutes, mechanically stirring for 20-360 minutes, transferring the solution to stainless steel high pressure reaction kettle for reacting for 8-24 hours at 100-200degrees Celsius, after finishing reaction, naturally settling the product, removing supernatant, putting in 50-200degrees Celsius, vacuum oven to drying for 8-24 hours to obtain the blue powder, namely intercalation type montmorillonite/copper-tetrahydro-3-tetrachloro-2 composite material, where the intercalation type montmorillonite/copper-tetrahydro-3-tetrachloro-2 composite material of aminated modified specific method includes adding the product D into the mixed solvent of ethanol and water, ultrasonically dispersing for 60-720 minutes at the temperature of 15-60degrees Celsius and the ultrasonic frequency of 20-60 kiloHertz, and then the temperature is 20-80degrees Celsius, mechanically stirring for 60-720 minutes at the rotating speed of 100-500 r/minutes, naming as solution E, dropping 3-amino propyl triethoxy silane in the solution E, ultrasonically dispersing for 60-80 minutes at 25-80degrees Celsius, ultrasonic frequency of 30-60 kiloHertz, then condensing and refluxing for 8-24 hours at 40-70degrees Celsius in the water bath, naturally settling the product, removing the supernatant, putting in 80-150degrees Celsius vacuum oven to dry for 12-24 hours to obtain light blue powder, namely aminated modified intercalated montmorillonite/copper-tetrahydro-3-tetrachloro-2 composite material, named as product F, preparing the mixed matrix film based on intercalated montmorillonite/copper-tetrahydro-3-tetrachloro-2 composite material, adding the product F into the polyvinyl amine solution with the concentration of 1-10 wt.%, where the product F accounts for 0.1-15 wt.% mass percentage content of the polyvinylamine, preparing into uniform casting film liquid, coating the product G on the surface of the microporous filtering film by coating method, controlling the thickness of the wet film to 1-150 microns, putting the mixed substrate film in the blast drying box with the relative humidity of 30-80% and the temperature of 25-80degrees Celsius for 8-24 hours. The mass ratio of montmorillonite and trihydrate copper nitrate is 1:(0.01-3), the content of trimesic acid in ethanol solution is (0.0 1-0.5) g/100mL, the mol ratio of trimesic acid and trihydrate copper nitrate is 1:(1.0-10). The volume percentage content of ethanol in the mixed solvent of ethanol/water is 60-98%, the content of product D in the mixed solvent is (0.5-5) g/100mL and the mass ratio of product D and 3-amino propyl triethoxy silane is 1:(0.1-10). The mixed matrix film is used for testing carbon dioxide/nitrogen mixed gas separation performance, the film is fixed in the film pool, the effective film area is 1-12.56 cm2, the feeding side gas test pressure range is 0.1-1.3 megaPascal, the pressure of the permeable side is 0.1 megaPascal, the test temperature is 10-80degrees Celsius, controlling the flow rate of the feed gas is 20-90 sccm, the downstream side of the film cavity adopts hydrogen as the purge gas purge, the purge gas flow rate is controlled at 25-35 sccm, using gas chromatography to detect the downstream side component, the gas performance test is carried out in the humidifying state, the feeding side gas comprises the humidity of the feeding gas and the blowing gas is adjusted by the humidifier to ensure the relative humidity of the feeding gas reaches 0-100%.

PN CN112210113-A
TI Preparing polypropylene foam material comprises foaming blend obtained by blending polypropylene and polydimethylsiloxane under carbon dioxide conditions, and adding small molecule co-foaming agent during the foaming process.
AU HU D
   ZHAO L
   QIANG W
   LIU T
   XU Z
   GAO X
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 202108146J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polypropylene foam material comprises foaming blend obtained by blending polypropylene and polydimethylsiloxane under carbon dioxide conditions, and adding small molecule co-foaming agent during the foaming process, where the small molecule co-foaming agent is n-pentane, acetone or water, and the foaming temperature is 146-165 degrees C, and the added amount of the small molecule co-foaming agent is 4-6 wt.% of the mass of carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polypropylene foam material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process; and widens the upper limit of the foaming temperature of polypropylene, so that polypropylene can be foamed at higher temperatures. The foam material has high foaming ratio, small pore size and high cell density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for polypropylene foam material prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The molecular weight of the polypropylene is 400,000 to 600,000 g/mol, and/or before the blending, the polypropylene is compressed into a sheet, and/or the number average molecular weight of the polydimethylsiloxane is 3500-4500 g/mol, and/or the mass ratio of the polypropylene to the polydimethylsiloxane is (90-110):1, and/or the blending temperature is 160-200 degrees C, and/or the blending time is 20-30 minutes, and/or the blending is performed in a Hack mixer, and/or the torque speed of the blending is 80-100 revolutions per minute, and/or the foaming temperature is 156-165 degrees C, and/or the added amount of the small molecule co-foaming agent is 5 wt.% of the mass of carbon dioxide, and/or the foaming pressure is 15-20 MPa. The number average molecular weight of the polydimethylsiloxane is 4000 g/mol, and/or the mass ratio of the polypropylene to the polydimethylsiloxane is 100:1, and/or the blending temperature is 170, 180 or 190 degrees C, and/or the blending time is 25 or 28 minutes, and/or the foaming temperature is 156, 160, 162 or 165 degrees C, and/or the foaming pressure is 15, 18 or 20 MPa. The polypropylene foam material has the following characteristics: the cell density is 3.21x 109 to 3.95x 1010 cells/cm3, the cell expansion ratio is 12-25 times and the average cell diameter is 9-50 mu m. The cell density is 3.38x 109, 5.51x 109, 9.39x 109, 1.25x 1010 or 2.55x 1010 cells/cm3. The cell expansion ratio is 12.57, 15.65, 19.30, 19.64 or 21.16 times. The average cell diameter is 11.16, 12.52, 41.21 or 43.53 mu m. Preferred Method: The foaming comprises adding the small molecule co-foaming agent, purging with carbon dioxide, heating to the foaming temperature, keeping the temperature, filling with high-pressure carbon dioxide, and setting the pressure to saturate the sheet. The number of times of the purging is three, and/or the heating rate is a constant rate, preferably 5-10 degrees C/minute, and/or the heat preservation time is 20-60 minutes, and/or the saturation time is 1-2 hours, and/or after the sheet is saturated, the following steps are further included: pressure relief, cooling and setting. The heating rate is 7, 9 or 10 degrees C/minutes, and/or the time for the heat preservation is 30, 40 or 50 minutes, and/or the time for the saturation is 1.5 hours, and/or after saturating the sheet, the following steps are further included: pressure relief, during cooling and shaping, the pressure relief rate is 300-700 MPa/s, preferably 400-600 MPa/s, e.g. 500 MPa/s. The preparation method of the polypropylene foam material specifically comprises (a) mixing polypropylene raw material and polydimethylsiloxane with number average molecular weight of 4000 g/mol in a mass ratio of 100:1, then blending at 180 degrees C for 20 minutes, and obtaining polypropylene/polydimethylsiloxane blend, (b) foaming under carbon dioxide conditions, adding small molecule co-foaming agent acetone during the foaming process, where the addition amount is 5 wt.% of the carbon dioxide mass under the experimental temperature and pressure, purging with carbon dioxide, heating to 156 degrees C, after holding for half an hour, filling with high pressure carbon dioxide, where the pressure is controlled to 15 MPa, saturating the sheet for 1.5 hours to the equilibrium of dissolution and diffusion, then quickly releasing the pressure, discharging the high-pressure carbon dioxide from the container within 1 second, and cooling and shaping.

Testing.)

AU YANG J
   LI X

GA 202018135W
AB    NOVELTY - Mononuclear nickel coordination compound includes an organic ligand 1,2-bis(dicyclohexylphosphoryl)ethane and a nickel ion with a chlorine bridge.

   ADVANTAGE - The mononuclear nickel coordination compound has easy implementation, high product purity and yield, and good application prospect in the field of photocatalytic conversion of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing mononuclear nickel complex, which involves:
   (A) mixing 1,2-bis(dicyclohexylphosphino)ethane and bistriphenylphosphine nickel chloride in acetonitrile with stirring;
   (B) passing argon into the obtained mixed solution for 6-24 hours with stirring to obtain a yellow-brown precipitate;
   (C) dissolving the obtained yellow-brown precipitate with a mixed solution of acetonitrile and ethanol; and
   (D) slowly volatilizing to obtain the yellow-green needle-like crystals.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The nickel ion is a divalent nickel ion. The crystal of the mononuclear nickel complex belongs to a triclinic system. The 1,2-bis(dicyclohexylphosphyl)ethane and bistriphenylphosphine nickel chloride are provided in a molar ratio of 1:0.5-1:1. The reaction is performed at 50-80 degrees C.

TI Preparation of pseudoboehmite by preparing sodium metaaluminate solution, adding aluminum sulfate, flowing into reaction kettle, stirring, controlling pH, reacting, inletting carbon dioxide gas, aging, washing, filtering and drying.

AE HUNAN HUIJING NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202007555W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of pseudoboehmite comprises preparing sodium metaaluminate solution, adding aluminum sulfate, flowing into reaction kettle, stirring, controlling pH of reaction slurry to greater than or equal to 13, reacting at 20-50 degrees C, adding reaction slurry into another gas-liquid reaction kettle, inletting carbon dioxide gas, controlling volume ratio of air and carbon dioxide at 1: 0.2-0.5, reacting at 20-50 degrees C for 5-40 minutes, aging, washing, filtering and drying to obtain dry powder.

Preferred Components: The exchange medium is inorganic ammonium salt, preferably ammonium bicarbonate.

PN WO2019236752-A1; US2021238752-A1
TI Preparation of hypochlorous acid solution used as e.g. disinfectant in hospitals, involves electrolyzing salt solution comprising purified water and pure sodium chloride, and introducing carbon dioxide gas and sulfamic acid into solution.
AU CUNNINGHAM E M
   EPSTEIN M J
AE CUNNINGHAM E M (CUNN-Individual)
   EPSTEIN M J (EPST-Individual)
   EPSTEIN M J (EPST-Individual)
   CUNNINGHAM E M (CUNN-Individual)
GA 2019A4766E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of hypochlorous acid solution involves electrolyzing a salt solution comprising purified water and pure sodium chloride, and introducing carbon dioxide gas and sulfamic acid into acid solution.
   USE - Preparation of hypochlorous acid solution (claimed) used as disinfectant in hospitals, schools, nursing homes and military facilities.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares shelf-stable hypochlorous acid solution having excellent disinfection performance while minimizing the time required and damages to personnel, equipment and product, and does not affect the efficacy of the hypochlorous acid due to the addition of sulfamic acid and carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for liquid solution, which comprises hypochlorous acid as the predominant chlorine species. The solution has a pH of 6.0-7.0 and is shelf-stable for at least one year.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves preparing solution by adding purified water and pure sodium chloride. The method involves purifying water by at least one process chosen from steam distillation, micro filtration, reverse osmosis and ozonization. The electrolyzing process involves applying 30V direct current to the salt solution at a maximum current level of 30 amps. Preferred Properties: The hypochlorous acid solution has a pH of 6.0-7.0, preferably 4.5-5.5 after introducing carbon dioxide gas and sulfamic acid. Preferred Composition: The hypochlorous acid solution comprises at least 99% pure sodium chloride and is free of additives. The salt solution comprises sodium chloride at a concentration of 10-28 g/L of purified water. The hypochlorous acid solution comprises sulfamic acid at a concentration of 0.3 g per 1000 mg/L or more, preferably 1.0 g per 1000 mg/L free available chlorine. The liquid solution comprises 250-5000 mg/L, preferably 1000 mg/L free available chlorine. Preferred Condition: The electrolyzing process is carried out at a solution temperature of 45-80 degrees C, preferably 54-60 degrees C.
