TI Preparation of selective oxygen permeable film used as used as separator for metal-air battery by mixing e.g. metal organic framework material ZIF-8 and dopamine hydrochloride, oxidation self-polymerizing, and adding polymethyl methacrylate.
AU LU H
   LIU J
AE YIHANGSHIDAI BEIJING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YIHA-Non-standard)
GA 201998085E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of selective oxygen permeable film comprises mixing metal organic framework material (ZIF-8), dopamine hydrochloride, alkaline substance and water, performing oxidation self-polymerization reaction, adding polymethyl methacrylate, adding to organic solvent, and stirring to obtain paste; and three-dimensional print on glass, soaking to water to automatically separate film from glass plate, and collecting film.
   USE - The method is for preparation of selective oxygen permeable film used as used as separator for metal-air battery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The film is easy-to-process, can prevent water vapor in air from poisoning the battery, and has preferential adsorption performance for carbon dioxide, ability to adsorb oxygen that is significantly lower than carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide blocking and oxygen permeability functions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of ZIF-8 and dopamine hydrochloride is 0.4-0.6:1.5-1.6. The ZIF-8 has diameter of 326-476 nm and pore diameter of 0.3-0.4 nm. The alkaline substance is trimethylolaminomethane, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide or ammonia; and has pH of 8-8.5. The mass ratio of powder product and polymethyl methacrylate is 0.4-0.6:1.7-1.9. The film has thickness of 10-20 mu m. Preferred Method: The oxidation self-polymerization is done at room temperature for 12-24 hours. The method further comprises subjecting oxidized self-polymerized product to centrifugation-ultrasonic water washing for 3 times, and drying.

TI Preparation of cyclic carbonate by coupling carbon dioxide with epoxide using quaternary phosphonium eutectic ionic liquid comprising quaternary phosphonium bromide salt, and N-hydroxysuccinimide as catalyst.
AU LIU M
   LIU F
   GU Y
   ZHAO P
   WANG Y
   PING R
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 201960513N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cyclic carbonate by coupling carbon dioxide with epoxide using quaternary phosphonium eutectic ionic liquid comprising quaternary phosphonium bromide salt as hydrogen bond acceptor component, and N-hydroxysuccinimide as hydrogen bond donor component, as catalyst.
   USE - Preparation of cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The quaternary phosphonium eutectic ionic liquid used in preparation of cyclic carbonate is prepared economically by simple method using easily available raw materials, and has high catalytic performance, excellent recycling performance, and selectively catalyzes cyclic carbonate under normal temperature, normal pressure, with no metal, and solvent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of cyclic carbonate by coupling carbon dioxide with epoxide using quaternary phosphonium eutectic ionic liquid as catalyst comprises quaternary phosphonium bromide salt as hydrogen bond acceptor component, and N-hydroxysuccinimide as hydrogen bond donor component. The quaternary phosphonium eutectic ionic liquid is of formula (I).
   R1-R4 = phenyl, benzyl or 8C or les alkyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The quaternary phosphonium bromide salt is any one of benzyl triphenyl phosphonium chloride of formula (XI), allyl triphenyl phosphonium bromide of formula (XII), methyl triphenyl phosphonium bromide of formula (XIII), and tetrabutylphosphonium bromide of formula (XIV), preferably tetrabutylphosphonium bromide. Preferred Compositions: The amount of the catalyst is 2-8%, preferably 4-6% of the molar amount of the epoxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The epoxide used in the preparation is any one of formula (II), (III), (IV), (V), (VI), (VII), (VIII), (IX), and (X). Preferred Conditions: The quaternary phosphonium bromide salt and an N-hydroxysuccinimide (NHS) are stirred at 50 degrees C for 0.5 hour in molar ratio of 1:2. The cycloaddition reaction is carried out for 2-24 hours at temperature of 25-60 degrees C, preferably 25 degrees C and the reaction pressure of 0.1-1 MPa, preferably 0.1MPa.

TI Method for treating strawberry seedling, involves utilizing carbon dioxide, selecting strawberry seedlings, pouring plug seedling substrate with sufficient moisture, followed by putting into warehouse and sealing for treatment.
AU YU H
   YUAN H
   GUAN L
   ZHAO M
   WANG J
   XIA J
AE JIANGSU AGRIC SCI INST (JSAS-C)
GA 201942441S
AB    NOVELTY - A strawberry seedling treating method involves utilizing carbon dioxide, selecting strawberry seedlings with 2 leaves and 1 heart and seedling age of more than 45 days and pouring the plug seedling substrate with sufficient moisture before treatment. The seedlings are put into the warehouse in the afternoon, set the warehouse carbon dioxide concentration to 55 plus minus 5%, the temperature to 35 plus minus 2 degrees C, sealed for treatment for 36-40 hours and moved out in the morning of the third day and routinely managed the water, fertilizer, light and plants with reference to seedlings.
   USE - Method for treating strawberry seedling.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables treating the strawberry seedling with effectively improving the quality of the seedlings from the seedling source, better quality health without insect pest mite production and economic benefit and reducing the labor cost.

PT P
PN CN109652820-A; CN109652820-B
TI Preparing electrode useful for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. treating the metal substrate for removing impurities, (ii) soaking the metal substrate treated in soluble salt solution.
AU QIU Y
   ZHANG H
   ZHANG T
   XU W
   LI X
   SU P
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201939344D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing electrode comprises (i) treating the metal substrate for removing impurities, (ii) soaking the metal substrate treated in the step (i) in soluble salt solution containing surfactant as additive to carry out hydrothermal reaction to obtain metal electrode having dense oxide layer on the surface, and (iii) subjecting the metal electrode having dense oxide layer on the surface to electrochemical reduction treatment to obtain metal electrode having high catalytic selectivity to formic acid.
   USE - The electrode is useful for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple operation and strong reproducibility; The electrode: is thin and dense; can effectively hinder the participation of the base metal in the ERC reaction; and controls the efficient conversion of the electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to the target product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal substrate is copper, zinc, tin, bismuth or indium, the metal mass content is not less than 99 wt.%, and the base shape is sheet shape. The step (i) treatment for removing impurities, including chemical treatment or electrochemical treatment, where the chemical treatment is immersed in a hydrochloric acid solution having a mass concentration of 1-38% for 5-60 minutes, and after being cleaned, it is blown dry with an inert gas. The electrochemical treatment is a phosphoric acid solution having a mass concentration of 50-87% as treatment liquid. The treatment potential is 0.2-3 V higher than the standard electrode potential of the treated metal, the optimum high potential range is 0.5-2 V, the treatment time is 5-30 minutes, and the treatment temperature is room temperature. The soluble salt solution of the step (ii) is halide, sulfate, nitrate or acetate. The metal ions in the soluble salt solution are the same as the metal ions in the substrate. The concentration of ions of the metal ions in the substrate in the soluble salt solution is 0.1-10 mM, and the optimal ion concentration is 0.5-3 mM. The additive is water-soluble cationic surfactant and/or nonionic surfactant. The concentration of the additive in the soluble salt solution is 0.2-10 mM, and the optimal concentration is 0.5-5 mM. The cationic surfactant is a compound containing a quaternary amino group, and the nonionic surfactant is a polyethylene glycol type surfactant. The compound containing quaternary amino group is behenyl trimethyl ammonium chloride, octadecyl alkyl trimethyl ammonium chloride, octadecyl alkyl trimethyl ammonium bromide, hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide, hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium chloride, hexadecyl ammonium bromide, tetradecyl alkyl trimethyl ammonium chloride, tetradecyl alkyl trimethyl ammonium bromide, tetramethyl ammonium bromide or trimethyl ammonium chloride. The polyethylene glycol type surfactant is lauryl alcohol, oleyl alcohol or Triton-100. In the step (ii), the hydrothermal reaction conditions are: the reaction temperature is controlled at 120-190 degrees C, the optimum temperature range is 150-180 degrees C. The reaction time is 3-30 hours, and the optimal reaction time is 10-18 hours. The mass purity of sodium hydrogencarbonate or potassium hydrogencarbonate is not less than 99%. The thickness of the surface oxide layer of the metal electrode is 5-100 mu m, and the diameter of the oxide particles in the oxide layer is 30-500 nm. Preferred Method: The method comprises subjecting metal electrode having a dense oxide layer on the surface in the step (iii) to electrochemical treatment under the condition that the electrolyte is aqueous solution of sodium hydrogencarbonate and/or potassium hydrogencarbonate having concentration of 0.05-0.5 M saturated with nitrogen, where the treatment current density is -0.5--10 mA cm-2, the optimum treatment current density is -1--5 mA cm-2, the electrochemical treatment time is 20-180 minutes, and the optimal treatment time is 30-60 minutes.

The 3,6-bis(carbazolyl)-1,2,4,5-tetrazine is treated in oxidation coupling reaction to obtain tetrazine ring polycarbazole.
   USE - Method for preparing tetrazine ring poly-carbazole is used for carbon dioxide adsorption and energy storage (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare tetrazine ring poly-carbazole that has excellent physical and chemical stability and higher specific surface area, pore structure and carbon dioxide adsorption value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The 3,6'-bis (carbazolyl)-1,2,4,5-tetrazine and crosslinking agent is reacted under action of Lewis acid catalyst in Friedel-Crafts reaction. The 3,6-bis (carbazolyl)-1,2,4,5-tetrazine and initiator is reacted under action of ferric chloride catalytic in oxidative coupling reaction. Preferred Components: The alkali is strong alkali, preferably potassium hydroxide and sodium hydroxide. The lewis acid catalyst is ferric chloride, zinc chloride, aluminum chloride and boron fluoride. The initiator is dibenzoyl peroxide, azo-bis-iso-butyrynitrile, potassium persulfate and azobisisoheptanenitril. Preferred Conditions: The stirring time is 0.5-5, preferably 1-2 hours. The reaction time is 4-12, preferably 6-10 hours. The ratio of tetrazine and oxazole salt is 1:0.5-5, preferably 1:2-3. The ratio of 1,2,4,5-tetrazine to crosslinker is from 1:1-12, preferably 1:3-6.

PT P
PN PL420167-A1; PL234252-B1
TI Method for preparing stable carbon dioxide sorbent based on potassium-modified titanium dioxide, involves mixing dried anatase titanium dioxide with potassium hydroxide solution, followed by washing and heating.
AU MORAWSKI W A
   KAPICA-KOZAR J
   PIROG E
   WANAG A M
   KUSIAK-NAJMAN E
   KAPICAKOZAR J
AE UNIV ZACHODNIOPOMORSKI TECHNOLOGICZNY (UZAC-C)
   UNIV ZACHODNIOPOMORSKI TECHNOLOGICZNY (UZAC-C)
GA 201862336X
AB    NOVELTY - A stable carbon dioxide sorbent preparing method involves mixing dried anatase titanium dioxide with 10 M potassium hydroxide solution in a weight ratio of 1:20 until a homogeneous suspension is obtained. The suspension is heated to 140 degrees C, cooled to room temperature and washed several times with 0.1 M hydrochloric acid solution under constant stirring until the pH is strongly acidic to obtain washed material. The washed material is washed with distilled water until neutral pH, dried in an oven in an air atmosphere at 350 degrees C for 2 hours and milled to obtain milled material. The milled material is heated to 140 degrees C for 12, 24 or 48 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing stable carbon dioxide sorbent based on a potassium-modified titanium dioxide.

AU BAE S T
   PARK Y
   PARK S J
   HEO Y J
   BAE J
   PIAO Y
   PIAO X
   XU N
   BAE S
   PARK Y J
   PARK S
   HEOYOUNG-JUNG
AE HYUNDAI MOTOR CO LTD (HYMR-C)
   UNIV INHA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (INHI-C)
   UNIV INHA IND-ACAD INST COOP (INHI-C)
   HYUNDAI MOTOR CO LTD (HYMR-C)
   UNIV INHA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (INHI-C)
   UNIV INHA INST IND-ACAD COOP (INHI-C)
GA 201800999U
AB    NOVELTY - Activated carbon is manufactured by (S10) preparing activated carbon precursors, (S20) carbonizing activated carbon precursors by performing heat treatment, (S30) equalizing carbonized activated carbon precursors by grinding, and (S40) activating the activated carbon precursors by introducing a mixture gas including carbon dioxide and steam into the ground activated carbon precursors and performing heat treatment.
   USE - Manufacture of activated carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method may form a large number of micropores and may introduce metal oxide particles, which may improve selective carbon dioxide adsorptive capacity of the activated carbon.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of a method for manufacturing activated carbon.
   Preparation of activated carbon precursors (S10)
   Carbonization of activated carbon precursors (S20)
   Grinding of carbonized activated carbon precursors (S30)
   Activation of carbonized activated carbon precursors (S40)
   Reaction of carbonized activated carbon precursors with metal salt and reducing agent (S50)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The method further includes, after activation step, introducing metal oxide particles on a surface of activated carbon by mixing and reacting activated carbon, metal salt, and reducing agent in a solvent. The method further includes, after equalizing step, selecting the equalized activated carbon precursors by using a sieve having a size of 100-250 um. The method further includes, after introducing metal oxide particles, performing heat treatment at 250-350 degrees C in an air atmosphere. Equalizing is performed using a ball mill, and particle size of balls used for the ball mill is 1-20 mm. Preferred Compositions: During activation, the mixed gas includes 10-50 vol.% carbon dioxide and 50-90 vol.% steam. Metal salt includes nickel, manganese, cobalt, zinc, yttrium, copper, and/or iron. Metal salt includes manganese nitrate, magnesium nitrate, nickel sulfate, and/or copper nitrate. Volume of activated carbon precursors is 0.1-40 vol.% for a total of 100 vol.% of the balls used for the activated carbon precursors and the ball mill. Preferred Conditions: Activation temperature is 700-1000 degrees C. Activation is performed while introducing the mixture gas into the activated carbon precursors at a speed of 1-100 ml/hour. Activation is performed under a pressure of less than or equal to 5 bar, preferably 1-5 hours. Introduction of metal oxide particles is performed at 80-120 degrees C. Processing speed of the ball mill is 100-500 rpm. Processing time of the ball mill is 30 minutes to 5 hours. Carbonizing is performed at 700-1000 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Reducing agent includes formaldehyde (HCHO), sodium borohydride, and/or lithium aluminum hydride. Solvent includes a polyol solvent selected from ethylene glycol, diethylene glycol, polyethylene glycol, 1,2-propanediol, and/or dodecanediol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: In the preparation of the activated carbon precursors, the activated carbon precursor includes poly(vinylidene fluoride), poly(vinylidene fluoride-co-hexafluoropropylene), poly(vinylidene chloride-co-acrylonitrile), poly(vinylidene chloride-co-acrylonitrile-co-methyl methacrylate), poly(vinylidene chloride-co-vinyl chloride), and/or poly(vinylidene chloride-co-methyl acrylate).

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Method for measuring carbon sink based on tree            year wheel, involves obtaining annual ring width of            each year according to crossed and fixed year result,            and calculating carbon collection according to width of            annual ring.
AU ZHANG L
   ZOU S
   YIN S
   CAO Z
AE CAO Z (CAOZ-Individual)
GA 2022C3566T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves drilling an annual wheel                sample core of a tree. An annual wheel core is                pre-processed. A year is crossed after                pre-processing the year wheel core. annual ring                width of each year is obtained according to a                crossed and fixed year result. Carbon collection is                calculated according to the width of the annual                ring.
   USE - Method for measuring a carbon sink based on                tree year wheel for planting trees by planting                trees, recovering vegetation and absorbing carbon                dioxide in the atmosphere, activity or                mechanism.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables measuring the carbon with                high precision under the condition of not causing                the tree to cause irreversible damage. The method                avoids cutting trees and other operations, avoids                damage to trees and environment. The sampling of                the tree-like core is convenient and the                operability is strong. The pre-processing the                sample core can improve the measuring precision and                reduce the error. The fixed year precision improves                the precision of the carbon sink measurement. After                the year, the carbon remittance is calculated                according to the annual wheel, the calculation is                simple, convenient and accurate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a                method for measuring a carbon sink based on tree                year wheel (Drawing includes Non-English language                text).

AU CHO S
   NAKAZAWA T
   TRIPATHI A
   BHATIA M
   DESHPANDE N A
   KARHADE O G
AE INTEL CORP (ITLC-C)
GA 2022A95542
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing a microelectronic structure                comprises forming a cavity at a face of a                substrate, forming a first conductive contact at a                bottom of the cavity, forming a second conductive                contact of the substrate outside the cavity, and                depositing the first solder on the first conductive                contact. The second solder is deposited on the                second conductive contact, where the first and                second solder has different material compositions.                A bridge component is placed in the cavity, where                the bridge component includes a first face and an                opposing second face. The bridge component includes                a second conductive contact at the second face of                the bridge component. The second conductive contact                of the bridge component is coupled to the first                conductive contact of the substrate by the first                solder. A microelectronic component is placed on                the face of the substrate, where the                microelectronic component has a first face and an                opposing second face.
   USE - Method for manufacturing a microelectronic                structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method manufactures the microelectronic                structure simply and cost-effectively by avoiding                expensive and complex manufacturing operations                typically required for high-density interconnect                structures (e.g., ultraviolet or carbon dioxide                laser machining, first-level interconnect plating,                etc.) and thus may speed development of new                structures and reduce cost. The microelectronic                structures and assemblies may achieve interconnect                densities as high or higher than conventional                approaches.

PN CN114849669-A
TI Preparing complex material for waste water            treatment comprises crushing the coconut fiber into            coconut fiber powder, then ultrasonically dispersing            the coconut fiber powder, centrifuging and taking the            precipitate to obtain the coupling polymer,            transferring the foaming product and polymerizing.
AU GONG Q
   KONG H
   WANG H
AE TAICANG BAICHUAN WATER TREATMENT EQUIP (TAIC-Non-standard)
GA 2022A4236M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing complex material for waste water                treatment comprises (i) firstly crushing the                coconut fiber into coconut fiber powder, then                ultrasonically dispersing the coconut fiber powder                in the urea-sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, then                adding soybean meal and epoxy chloropropane,                carrying out ultrasonic oscillation reaction,                centrifuging and taking the precipitate to obtain                the coupling polymer, (ii) then placing the                coupling polymer in a sealed high pressure kettle,                using supercritical carbon dioxide to carry out                foaming treatment to obtain the foaming product,                and (iii) finally transferring the foaming product                to the chemical vapor deposition chamber,                introducing tertiary butyl hydrogen peroxide and                2-(perfluorooctyl)-ethyl methacrylate, steam of                dimethyl dichlorosilane, and polymerizing on the                surface of the foamed product to realize reaction                deposition to obtain the complex material for waste                water treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing complex                material for waste water treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The complex material has low cost, it can                quickly absorb oil spill and has no toxicity,                therefore, compared with the front two types of                processing method, it has economic benefit,                efficiency and environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                complex material for waste water treatment prepared                by above-mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: In the step (i), the                  mass ratio of the coconut fiber powder, soybean                  meal and epoxy chloropropane is 1:2-3:2-3. In the                  step (i), the dosage of the urea-sodium hydroxide                  aqueous solution is 5-8 times of coconut fiber                  powder, the urea-sodium hydroxide aqueous                  solution is obtained by adding 10-12 pts. wt.                  urea and 5-6 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide into 100                  pts. wt. water, and stirring to completely                  dissolve to obtain the product.Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  technical conditions of the ultrasonic                  oscillation reaction are as follows: 500-700 W                  ultrasonic oscillation for 3-5 hours. In the step                  (ii), the specific method of the foaming                  treatment is as follows: firstly maintaining the                  pressure for 10-15 minutes at 175-185&#8451;,                  18-30 MPa carbon dioxide pressure, cooling to                  150-160&#8451; for 5 minutes, and then                  deflating. In the step (iii), placing the foaming                  product on the sample table at the bottom of the                  chemical vapor deposition chamber, the heating                  wire is arranged at the position of 30-40 mm                  above the sample table, controlling the                  temperature of the sample table at                  5-9&#8451;, the heating wire temperature is                  200-220&#8451;, and the pressure in the                  chemical vapor deposition chamber is 2-5 Pa. In                  step (iii), the steam of tert-butyl hydroperoxide                  and 2- (perfluorooctyl)-ethyl methacrylate, and                  dimethyl dichlorosilane, and their intake flow                  sequentially 1 ml/minute, 0.05-0.08 ml/minute and                  0.1-0.3 ml/minute. The steam is obtain by water                  bath, where volatilizing tert-butyl hydroperoxide                  at 25&#8451; in water bath, volatilizing                  2-(perfluorooctyl)-ethyl methacrylate, dimethyl                  dichlorosilane at 80&#8451; in water bath. In                  the step (iii), the reaction deposition time is                  80-100 minutes.

PN CN114437875-A
TI Water-based cleaning agent for structural            components of laser amplifying device in irradiation            working environment, includes dibasic acid ester            solvent, methoxy methyl butanol, nonionic surfactant,            emulsifier, and deionized water.
AU PANG X
   XIA Z
   ZHU J
   WANG L
AE CAS SHANGHAI INST OPTICS & FINE MECHANIC (CAOF-C)
GA 2022659790
AB    NOVELTY - Water-based cleaning agent is prepared from 2                pts. wt. dibasic acid ester (DBE) solvent, 2 pts.                wt. 3-methoxy-3-methyl-1-butanol, 5 pts. wt.                nonionic surfactant, 1 pt. wt. emulsifier, and 10                pts. wt. deionized water.
   USE - Water-based cleaning agent for structural                components of a laser amplifying device in an                irradiation working environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The water-based cleaning agent saves a lot of                water resources, achieves the same cleaning effect,                and improves the working efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for cleaning laser amplifying device structural                member under an irradiation working environment,                which involves spray cleaning with supercritical                carbon dioxide mixed with a volatile hydrocarbon                cleaning agent, mixing the water-based cleaning                agent for the structural units of the laser                amplifying device in the irradiation working                environment with pure water, configuring into a                cleaning mixture, and performing ultrasonic initial                cleaning, pure water spray washing, spray water                backflow, spray water backflow to replace the mixed                solution used by the ultrasonic cleaning solution,                ultrasonic cleaning with pure water, spraying and                rinsing the pure water again to obtain a clean                amplifier structure, recycling the spray water to                replace the pure water used for the pure water                ultrasonic rewashing, and drying the structural                components of the amplifier in a vacuum oven.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nonionic                  surfactant is selected from polyethylene glycol                  trimethyl nonyl ether, octylphenol polymer                  oxyethylene ether, nonylphenol polyoxyethylene                  ether, fatty acid polyoxyethylene ester, fatty                  acid methyl ester ethoxylate, ethylene oxide                  adduct of polypropylene glycol, sorbitan ester,                  and sucrose fatty acid ester. The emulsifier is                  selected from Tween-80 (RTM: polyoxyethylene                  sorbitan monooleate), Span-80 (RTM: biodegradable                  surfactant), carbomer, sodium dodecylbenzene                  sulfonate, sodium dodecyl sulfate, sodium dodecyl                  sulfonate, hexadecane, sodium benzene sulfonate,                  sodium hexadecyl sulfonate, sodium hexadecyl                  sulfate, sodium octadecyl benzene sulfonate, and                  sodium octadecyl sulfonate. The DBE solvent                  includes butanediol dimethyl glutarate, dimethyl                  glutarate, and dimethyl adipate. The silane                  coupling agent is an amino group-containing                  silane coupling agent selected from                  &#947;-aminopropyl trimethoxysilane,                  &#947;-aminopropyltriethoxysilane,                  N-&#946;(aminoethyl)-&#947;-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane,                  N-&#946;(aminoethyl)-&#947;-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane                  ethoxysilane,                  N-&#946;(aminoethyl)-&#947;-aminopropylmethyldimethoxysilane,                  N-&#946;(aminoethyl)-&#947;-aminopropylmethyldiethoxysilane,                  and diethoxysilane                  ethylenetriaminopropyltrimethoxysilane. The                  external environment is a dust-free environment                  not lower than ten thousand.Preferred Conditions: The modified aluminum                  oxide nano-powder is obtained by dissolving                  aluminum nitrate in water, adding sodium citrate,                  ultrasonically dispersing to 60-80&#8451;,                  evaporating the solvent to obtain a sol,                  increasing temperature to 170-190&#8451; to                  form a dry coagulation glue, and igniting the dry                  gel to obtain alumina nano-powder. The obtained                  alumina nano-powder is added into an ethanol                  solution, ultrasonically dispersed, added a                  silane coupling agent at 60-80&#8451; for 1-3                  hours, and centrifuged and dried to obtain                  modified alumina nano-powder.

GA 202260747A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves synchronously capturing red, green and blue (RGB) images of garbage, and collecting a high-spectrum image information of garbage by using a two-dimensional industrial camera.    YANG C

   XIE Z
   LI Z
   YANG W
   WEI G
   ZOU C
   LI J
   WANG X
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 202250283W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using a four-stage mass spectrum to analyze research area natural gas sample. Helium gas content in the natural gas sample measured by the four-stage mass spectrum, is obtained. A gas chromatography is used to analyze the research area natural gas sample. The helium gas content is taken after weighted average as the helium gas content in the research area natural gas sample. The helium gas content in the natural gas sample measured by the four-stage mass spectrum and the helium gas content in the natural gas sample measured by the gas phase chromatography are weighted and averaged. The helium gas content after weighted average is taken as the helium gas content in the research area natural gas sample.

The high-temperature                lubricating material and the zero-dimensional                nanomaterial are located on the surface of the                carbon film. The high temperature lubricating                material is hexagonal boron nitride, graphite,                fluorinated graphite, antimony-impregnated                graphite, and/or tungsten diselenide. The carbon                film is diamond-like carbon film (DLC),                polymer-like carbon film (PLC), onion-like carbon                film (OLC), and/or graphitic carbon film                (GLC).
   USE - The composite material is useful in vacuum and                inert environment i.e. inert gas atmosphere, carbon                dioxide atmosphere and nitrogen atmosphere (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material has good robustness and                low friction coefficient of 0.0004 and meets the                lubrication requirements of materials or components                that have extremely high requirements for lubricity                under special working conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the composite material, comprising (i)                uniformly dispersing the high temperature                lubricating material and the zero-dimensional                nanomaterial in a solvent; (ii) dropping the                mixture obtained in step (1) on the surface of the                carbon film, and waiting for the solvent to be                completely volatilized in a vacuum environment; and                (iii) placing the carbon film treated in a vacuum                environment for 1-10 hours.

PN US2021370357-A1
TI Removing microorganisms or biofilm growth and mineral deposits in membrane system, involves contacting membrane with peroxyformic acid composition, removing microbial growth and dissolving mineral deposits on membrane, and reducing concentration of hydrogen peroxide in composition.
AU POWER C
   SCHACHT P F
   STAUB R
   BUNDERS C
   LI J
AE ECOLAB USA INC (ECON-C)
GA 2021D90984
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing microorganisms and mineral deposits in a membrane system involves contacting the membrane with a peroxyformic acid composition comprising peroxyformic acid, formic acid and hydrogen peroxide, removing microbial growth and dissolving mineral deposits on the membrane, and reducing the concentration of the hydrogen peroxide in the peroxyformic acid composition, where the peroxyformic acid composition is membrane compatible and does not damage the membrane as measured by a decrease in flux of the membrane.
   USE - The method is useful for removing microorganisms or biofilm growth and mineral deposits in a membrane system or reducing and preventing biofilms and mitigation of mineral build-up on the membranes or killing and removing biofilms and other soils. The microorganisms are Gram positive bacteria (e.g. Staphylococcus aureus, Bacillus subtilis, and Clostridia species), and Gram negative bacteria (e.g. Escherichia coli, Pseudomonas species, Klebsiella pneumoniae, Legionella pneumophila, Enterobacter species, Serratia species, Desulfovibrio species, and Desulfotomaculum species).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition is biodegradable, and decomposes into non-hazardous products that leave no toxic traces on the treated membranes (due to rapid degradation into water, carbon dioxide and formic acid) and efficiently kills biofilms and other soils along with inorganic scale on membranes without damaging or negatively interfering with the membranes treated.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The concentration of the hydrogen peroxide in the peroxyformic acid composition is reduced using an enzyme, where the enzyme is a catalase or a peroxidase enzyme. The concentration of hydrogen peroxide is 5 weight/weight% or less, preferably 0-0.001 weight/weight%. The concentration of hydrogen peroxide is maintained for 1 hour. The peroxyformic acid composition is generated in situ. The peroxyformic acid composition is generated in situ by contacting the formic acid and the hydrogen peroxide to generate the peroxyformic acid. The formic acid and the hydrogen peroxide are contacted in the absence of 2-22C carboxylic acid and the peroxyformic acid is the only peracid in the peroxyformic acid composition. The formic acid and hydrogen peroxide are contacted in the presence of 2-22C carboxylic acid, and the peroxyformic acid composition comprises peroxyformic acid and 2-22C peroxycarboxylic acid, where the 2-22C carboxylic acid is acetic acid, octanoic acid, and or sulfonated oleic acid and the 2-22C peroxycarboxylic acid is peroxyacetic acid, peroxyoctanoic acid, and/or peroxysulfonated oleic acid. The step of contacting the formic acid and the hydrogen peroxide is performed in the presence of a catalyst, where the catalyst is a mineral acid and/or an enzyme catalyst, where the mineral acid is sulfuric acid, methanesulfonic acid, nitric acid, phosphoric acid, pyrophosphoric acid, polyphosphoric acid, or phosphonic acid.Preferred Composition: The peroxyformic acid composition further comprises an additional oxygen source, where the additional oxygen source comprises a percarbonate, perborate, and/or persulfate.Preferred Component: The membrane is a reverse osmosis membrane, a nanofiltration membrane, an ultrafiltration membrane, or a microfiltration membrane. The membrane comprises cellulose, cellulose acetate, nitrocellulose, polysulfone, polyethersulfone, fully aromatic polyamide, polyvinylidene fluoride, polytetrafluoroethylene, polyacrylonitrile, polypropylene, carbon, alpha-aluminum oxide, zirconium oxide, ceramic and/or stainless steel.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of removal of biofilm tested to determine efficacy of biofilm removal and kill rates of Pseudomonas aeruginosa and mesophilic bacterial endospores present in raw milk are capable of surviving pasteurization and other adverse conditions encountered during dairy product production that do not support the claims.

PN CN113553767-A
TI Method for constructing greenhouse crop photosynthetic rate prediction model, involves performing normalization operation on sample data to obtain training sample group, and obtaining greenhouse crop photosynthetic model when training batch reaches preset condition.
AU ZHANG N
   ZHANG D
   WANG Y
   TAN D
   YUAN Q
   LIU T
AE UNIV SHENYANG AGRIC (USAG-C)
GA 2021C32557
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining sample data. Normalization operation is performed on the sample data to obtain a training sample group. A photosynthetic rate prediction model is constructed based on a second-order oscillatory particle swarm optimization-least square support vector machine (SOPSO-LSSVM) model. The photosynthetic rate prediction model is trained by using the training sample group. The training sample group is input into an LSSVM Model for training. A predicted value of a photosynthesis rate in a current training batch is obtained. An optimal parameter value of the current training batch is obtained according to the predicted value through a SOPSO algorithm. Parameters in the LSSVM model are updated by using the optimal parameter value. A greenhouse crop photosynthetic rate prediction model is obtained when the training batch reaches a preset condition.
   USE - Method for constructing a greenhouse crop photosynthetic rate prediction model based on SOPSO-LSSVM.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables accurately predicting the photosynthetic rate, carbon dioxide concentration and photosynthetic photon flux density (PPFD) conditions under different temperatures, and providing better model base for greenhouse environment optimization control with higher prediction precision and robustness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for constructing a greenhouse crop photosynthetic rate prediction model based on SOPSO-LSSVM.

   ADVANTAGE - The nickel-based alloy flux-cored wire has                excellent vertical welding operability even in 100%                carbon dioxide shielding gas atmosphere,                high-temperature crack resistance, and arc                stability, and reduced generation of slag burning                and pit on bead surface.

PN KR2021090949-A
TI Oxygen separation filter for supplying high-concentration oxygen for vehicle air conditioner, has screen filter layer, which is provided with silk mesh, where fine dust filter layer is made of fiber layer.
AU KIM T H
AE HUTUM CO LTD (HUTU-Non-standard)
GA 202186704J
AB    NOVELTY - The oxygen separation filter has a screen filter layer that is provided with a silk mesh. A fine dust filter layer is made of a fiber layer to filter fine dust. An ultrafine filter layer is provided with microfibers to filter residual contaminants contained in the air that is passed through the fine dust filter layer. A nano filter layer is comprised with pores through which only oxygen in the air is passed through the ultrafine filter layer. An activated carbon filter layer is configured to filter residual contaminants and moisture contained in the air passed through the nano-filter layer. A protective filter layer is configured to protect an activated elastic filter layer that is laminated with a ceramic material and a sintered alloy.
   USE - Oxygen separation filter for supplying high-concentration oxygen for vehicle air conditioner.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxygen separation filter maintains comfortable indoor air quality even without opening a window by separating oxygen and supplying it to the interior of the vehicle. The increase in the carbon dioxide concentration due to the breathing of the occupant is prevented. PN CN113060264-A; CN113060264-B
TI Underwater vehicle thermoelectric life support propulsion system, comprises air circulation mechanism includes thermoelectric generator and storage tank to store liquid oxygen/liquid hydrogen/LNG.
AU CAO W
   HE H
AE UNIV JIMEI (UYMJ-C)
GA 202180478H
AB    NOVELTY - Underwater vehicle thermoelectric life support propulsion system comprises an air circulation mechanism. The air circulation mechanism includes a thermoelectric generator, a storage tank, a first electrolytic cell, a mixer, a first heat exchanger, a second heat exchanger, and a separator. The storage tank is used to store liquid oxygen/liquid hydrogen/LNG. The process of breathing oxygen supply includes seawater is used as a heat source to connect hot end of low temperature zone of thermoelectric generator for heat exchange. The liquid oxygen/liquid hydrogen/LNG flowing out of storage tank is used as a cold source to connect to cold end of low-temperature zone of thermoelectric generator for heat exchange, and the resulting power generation current flows into the first electrolytic cell. The oxidation reaction occurs on anode of first electrolytic cell to generate oxygen and decarburized air to flow through mixer to form fresh air in cabin to provide oxygen for breathing.
   USE - Used as underwater vehicle thermoelectric life support propulsion system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can simplify the structure and composition of the underwater vehicle; reduce the cost; utilizes free energy to realize oxygen supply and carbon dioxide removal, and has simple operation, safety and reliability.

PN KR2266802-B1
TI Cement-based inorganic binder composition for renewable raw materials, involves selecting ferrous and non-ferrous materials for binder mixture containing sand dust and fly ash, pulverizing, and mixing performance improvement admixture.
AU HAN S H
AE HAN S H (HANS-Individual)
GA 202171308Y
AB    NOVELTY - Environmentally-friendly cement-based inorganic binder composition is prepared by selecting ferrous and non-ferrous materials using a non-ferrous separator using a vortex and magnetic force for a binder mixture in which 1-20 wt.% stone dust, 10-40 wt.% fly ash, and 50-80 wt.% flooring material are mixed, pulverizing non-ferrous materials of the selected binder mixture, and mixing 0.1-10 pts. wt. performance improvement admixture with respect to 100 pts. wt. of non-ferrous materials of the pulverized binder mixture. The performance improvement admixture comprises 100 pts. wt. of calcium sulfoaluminate, 80-100 pts. wt. anhydrite, 50-70 pts. wt. sodium sulfate, 1-20 pts. wt. zinc oxide, 1-20 pts. wt. silicoaluminophosphate, 5-10 pts. wt. tungsten disulfide and 0.1-10 pts. wt. biocellulose powder.
   USE - Cement-based environmentally-friendly inorganic binder composition for renewable raw materials for manufacturing building materials such as bricks and concrete for structures or road paving.
   ADVANTAGE - The cement-based inorganic binder composition is environmentally-friendly, and provides building materials with excellent physical properties such as strength, durability and fast hardness, and prevents secondary soil contamination by heavy metals such as high energy consumption, carbon dioxide emission, and hexavalent chromium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Process: The cement-based environmentally-friendly inorganic binder composition is obtained by preparing a culture solution by heating coconut liquid at 100-110 degrees C for 20-40 minutes to obtain bio-cellulose powder, adding 0.01-1 wt.% sucrose and 0.01-1 wt.% ammonium lactate, inoculating the culture medium with 10-20 wt.% Acetobacter xylinum and culturing for 5-15 days at 28-35 degrees C to produce biocellulose, cutting the bio-cellulose to a thickness of 2-4 mm and washing the oil fraction of the bio-cellulose with 0.1-10 wt.% sodium hydroxide solution, removing moisture to 15% or less of the remaining moisture by compressing the cut bio-cellulose, drying the bio-cellulose from which the moisture has been removed with hot air at 60-99 degrees C for 4-5 hours to prepare a dried bio-cellulose having a residual moisture content of 10% or less, and pulverizing the dried bio-cellulose to a diameter of 0.1-1 mm. The preparation of the culture solution involves adding silicon nitride nanofibers to 10-30 wt.% of the mass of the coconut solution. The silicon nitride nanofibers are prepared by gas phase reaction of silicon chloride and ammonia to prepare silicon diimide, pyrolyzing the prepared silicon diimide to prepare amorphous silicon nitride, and heat-treating the prepared amorphous silicon nitride in a non-oxidizing atmosphere to prepare a crystalline silicon nitride nanofiber. The cement-based environmentally-friendly inorganic binder composition is obtained by synthesizing a template solution (S1) by dissolving silicoaluminophosphate in a block copolymer of polyethylene oxide and polypropylene oxide in an organic solvent containing alcohol, synthesizing a template solution (S2) by dissolving piperidine and tetramethylammonium chloride (TMACl) in distilled water, preparing a hydrogel by mixing 100 pts. wt. mixed template solution obtained by mixing the template solution (S1) and the template solution (S2) in a weight ratio of 1:(2-5), and 20-50 pts. wt. mixture comprising 50-80 wt.% sodium silicate, 15-45 wt.% pseudo-boehmite and 1-10 wt.% alkylphosphonic acid compound of formula (I), forming a solid product by stirring the prepared hydrogel at 30-90 degrees C for 2-24 hours and then stirring for 5-48 hours at 150-200 degrees C to hydrothermally react, and calcining the obtained solid product at 800-1000 degrees C at a temperature increase rate of 0.2-2 degrees C/minute for 1-5 hours and cooling.R1 = 1-4C linear or branched alkyl;andx = 0 or 1.Preferred Components: The stone powder is generated from the manufacturing process of stone aggregate by crushing the rock of the stone mountain. The fly ash and floor ash are incineration ash generated after incineration at thermal power plants, local government incinerators and private waste incinerators. Among the incineration ash, only those that satisfy environmental standards for heavy metals are selected and prepared. Preferred Composition: The stone powder comprises 50-70 wt.% silicon oxide, 0.1-5 wt.% calcium oxide, 15-30 wt.% aluminum oxide, 0.1-5 wt.% magnesium oxide, 0.1-5 wt.% sodium oxide, 1-10 wt.% potassium oxide, 1-10 wt.% iron oxide, and remaining amount of unburned powder. The fly ash and the flooring material comprises 20-40 wt.% silicon oxide, 10-30 wt.% calcium oxide, 5-15 wt.% aluminum oxide, 1-10 wt.% magnesium oxide, 1-10 wt.% sodium oxide, 1-10 wt.% potassium oxide, 1-10 wt.% iron oxide and remaining of unburned powder.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the beverage comprising sweetener, dietary fiber, sodium bicarbonate, sour agent, potassium sorbate, essence and portion of purified water, mixing, and dissolving to obtain pre-mixed liquid; mixing and diluting the mixed material liquid with the remaining purified water; and orderly sterilizing and filtering, filling carbon dioxide, and sterile cold filling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acidity regulator is citric acid, malic acid, tartaric acid, sodium gluconate and/or lactic acid. The essence is liquid essence and/or solid essence. The beverage further comprises carbon dioxide gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The dietary fiber is resistant dextrin, polydextrose, inulin, xylooligosaccharide and/or isomaltooligosaccharide.

PN CN112569916-A; CN112569916-B
TI Regenerating waste activated carbon comprises e.g.            performing vacuum thermal desorption of waste activated            carbon, then washing waste activated carbon desorbed by            vacuum heat to obtain wet activated carbon and            drying.
AU NING J
   HE X
AE CHANGSHA GONGYANYUAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTE (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202137228X
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating waste activated carbon comprises                performing vacuum thermal desorption of the waste                activated carbon until the desorption rate of                volatile organic compounds (VOCs) in the waste                activated carbon reaches more than 90%, then                washing the waste activated carbon desorbed by                vacuum heat to obtain wet activated carbon, after                the wet activated carbon is dried, roasting in a                protective atmosphere, then carbonizing in an                atmosphere with an oxygen content of less than or                equal to 1%, then activating in a mixed atmosphere                of water vapor and carbon dioxide, cooling to                obtain regenerated activated carbon, where the                vacuum thermal desorption process is as follows:                first heating to 30 degrees C, then increasing 5                degrees C every 8-12 seconds to the final                desorption temperature, the final desorption                temperature is less than or equal to 95 degrees C,                preferably 85-91 degrees C, and keeping the                temperature until the desorption equilibrium is                reached at the final desorption temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for regenerating waste                activated carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the temperature required                for desorption regeneration; produces less waste                gas; does not cause serious pollution to the                atmosphere; makes the overall reaction temperature                of the whole method lower; and increases the                adsorption capacity of the produced product and the                number of reuses.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The vacuum degree of                  the vacuum thermal desorption is controlled at                  18-20 kPa. The protective atmosphere is nitrogen.                  The atmosphere with oxygen content less than or                  equal to 1% is air that consumes oxygen through                  natural gas combustion. In the mixed atmosphere                  of water vapor and carbon dioxide, the volume                  ratio of water vapor and carbon dioxide is 2-3:1.                  Preferred Method: The process of drying the wet                  activated carbon is to first dry the wet                  activated carbon at room temperature to a                  moisture content of 40-50%, then placing it at                  100-150 degrees C and heating to constant weight.                  The calcination temperature is 150-400 degrees C                  for 1-2 hours, and the heating rate is 7-9                  degrees C/minutes. The temperature of the                  carbonization treatment is 400-500 degrees C for                  5-10 hours, and the heating rate is 7-9 degrees                  C/minutes. The temperature of the activation                  treatment is 800-900 degrees C, and the time of                  the activation treatment is 5-10 hours. The                  cooling method is air cooling, and the cooling                  time is 4-6 hours.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN RU2744623-C1
TI Valve for positive crancase ventilation with air purification has positive crancase ventilation valves with air purification.
AU LITVINOVA N A
AE UNIV TYUMEN IND (UTYU-C)
GA 202138582X
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the field of ventilation, in particular to positive crancase ventilation valves with air purification. It is designed to supply air indoors and effectively clean it. The device contains sequentially arranged filters inside a plastic head of a rectangular shape measuring 200 by 200 mm with slots, installed in a plastic pipe with an outer diameter of 100 mm: the first and second filters - with sorption loads of shungite (fractions of 1 mm) and zeolite (fractions of 1-3 mm), the thickness of each layer of the sorbent is 20 mm, and the third filter - photocatalytic, consisting of a thin porous plate of titanium dioxide (TiO2) and two UV lamps with a wavelength of 365 nm (UV-A range), with a power of 6 W each, arranged vertically in a row. Filters with sorption loading of shungite (fractions of 1 mm) and with sorption loading of zeolite (fractions of 1-3 mm) adsorb solid and gaseous impurities and then the supply air passes through a photocatalytic filter, where the gaseous substances between a thin porous plate of titanium dioxide (TiO2) and two UV lamps are oxidized to safe element compounds (carbon dioxide and gaseous water-water vapor). Further, high quality air after cleaning in the valve is supplied indoors.
   USE - Ventilation technology.
   ADVANTAGE - Improved quality of cleaning the supply air supplied indoors from gaseous inorganic and organic substances.

PN CN112481015-A
TI Preparing refined snake oil comprises e.g. taking snake fat petals, soaking in isotonic aqueous solution containing citric acid and sodium chloride, rinsing to obtain pretreated snake fat petals, adding sulfhydryl antioxidant and stirring.
AU ZHOU H
   CHANG L
   ZHOU J
   DU Z
   WU S
AE KARLIE COSMETICS CO LTD (KARL-Non-standard)
GA 2021294647
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing refined snake oil comprises (i) taking the snake fat petals, soaking them in a 10-20 times isotonic aqueous solution containing citric acid and sodium chloride for 20-60 minutes, and rinsing with deionized water for 2-6 times to obtain the pretreated snake fat petals; (ii) adding the pretreated snake fat flaps in step (i) into the stirring tank, adding the sulfhydryl antioxidant, stirring and homogenizing to obtain liquefied snake fat; (iii) centrifuging the liquefied snake fat in step (ii) through a tube centrifuge to obtain crude snake oil; (iv) stirring the crude snake oil obtained in step (iii) and the added adsorbent and centrifuging in a tubular centrifuge to obtain decolorized and deodorized snake oil; and (v) extracting the snake oil obtained in step (iv) and separating by supercritical carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing refined snake oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts the selection of specific sulfhydryl antioxidant, which can reduce the peroxide value of snake oil; has remarkable high temperature stability; does not use organic solvents, utilizes homogeneous wall breaking, releases snake oil fully from fat cells, and combines high-speed tubular centrifugation to improve the yield of low-temperature extraction oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass percentage concentration of citric acid in the isotonic aqueous solution is 0.05-0.5%, mass percentage concentration of sodium chloride is 0.6-0.8% and the weight of sulfhydryl antioxidant is 0.5-5 wt.% of snake fat petals, and the weight of adsorbent is 2-10 wt.% snake fat petals. The sulfhydryl antioxidant comprises at least one of cysteine, acetylcysteine, glutathione, mercaptopropionic acid or lipoic acid, preferably cysteine. The adsorbent is alumina or silica gel. The adsorbent is silica gel with a particle size of 5-30 mu m and a pore size of 5-20 nm. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the method comprises adding sulfhydryl antioxidant, stirring for 1-2 hours at 30-45 degrees C, and homogenizing for 10-30 minutes at 2000-8000 revolutions/minute through high-speed shearing machine. In the step (iv), stirring at 30-45 degrees C for 30-120 minutes at 50-500 revolutions/minute. In the step (iii) and (iv), the conditions for tubular centrifugal separation are the speed is 12000-18000 revolutions/minute. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and separation conditions are the extraction temperature is 30-40 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 28-40 Mpa, the flow rate is 20-60 liter/h, the pressure of the separation kettle is 8-25 Mpa, and the separation temperature is 30-40 degrees C.

PT P
PN WO2021019466-A1
TI Preparing lettuce (Lactuca sativa) in hydroponic culture involves using treated waste water that comes from urban, domestic or agro-industrial waste, and treating wastewater in two stages, in which carrying out basic chemical precipitation.
AU CARVALHO M
   REGATO M
   ALMEIDA M
   PEREIRA N
   SANTOS M
   CORREIA T
   AFONSO A
   LUZ S
AE INST POLITECNICO BEJA (POLI-Non-standard)
GA 2021129331
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lettuce (Lactuca sativa) in hydroponic culture involves using treated waste water that comes from urban, domestic or agro-industrial waste. The wastewater is treated in 2 stages, in which a basic chemical precipitation is carried out, which is followed by natural neutralization of the supernatant from by injection of atmospheric carbon dioxide (CO2), and application of the supernatant to lettuce (Lactuca sativa) in hydroponics, as a nutritive solution. The basic precipitation is carried out by adding alkalizing agents, such as calcium hydroxide, industrial hydrated lime or quicklime (CaO), to residual water at treat, until it reaches a pH value greater than 9.5. The natural neutralization step of the supernatant is carried out for the treatment of residual water, be done by injection of atmospheric CO2 , carried out by distributors submerged in a recarbonation chamber, until the supernatant of the residual water reaches a value pH ranging from 5.5-8.0.
   USE - Method for preparing lettuce (Lactuca sativa) in hydroponic culture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to remove high yield of undesirable compounds and provides advantageous application to hydroponic system cultures, and this process also has the advantages of being easy to implement and apply and to be ecologically sustainable, and eliminates the use of agricultural fertilizers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The chemical precipitation is carried out with manual agitation, in longitudinal and lateral movements with turning forming an angle of about 90 degrees in relation to the ground. The application of the supernatant resulting from the treatment of domestic or urban wastewater, is applied directly to lettuce (Lactuca sativa) in hydroponics. The adjustment of the pH and EC values is carried out by adding water, namely drinking water, groundwater or irrigation water. The irrigation water is added in a proportion of about 1: 4 of agro-industrial waste water treated for irrigation. The treated agro-industrial wastewater comes from cellars, mills or cheese factories. The perlite and rock wool are used as substrate. The lettuce is grown in hydroponic growing systems under artificial lighting conditions. The artificial lighting is carried out by using full spectrum or solar spectrum light emitting diode (LED) lamps.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).); P27 (Shop, household, furnishings (A47F, G, H).)
MC C04-A08G2; C04-D02; C05-A01; C05-A03A2; C05-B02C; C14-S18; C14-T04; D04-A01B; P13-A99; P13-E01; P13-E02; P27-A01; P27-B99

TI Producing metal organic framework (MOF) useful in            composition as adsorbent or catalyst for inhibiting            hydrolysis in moisture-containing medium, comprises            e.g. modifying unsaturated metal sites in MOF with            nucleophilic compound.
AU HWANG Y K
   HONG D Y
   YOUNG C G
   KWAK J S
   CHO G H
   UHWANG L
   CHANG J S
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 202104969P
AB    NOVELTY - Producing metal organic framework (MOF) with                moisture stability, comprises (a) modifying some or                all of the unsaturated metal sites in the MOF with                nucleophilic compound to block coordinatevely                unsaturated metals or defect sites from moisture,                and (b) additionally supporting a compound for                performing adsorption or catalytic reaction on the                modified MOF.
   USE - The MOF is useful in composition as adsorbent                or catalyst for inhibiting hydrolysis in wet                conditions of moisture-containing medium, and                adsorbing toxic gas, radioactive gas, carbon                dioxide, semiconductor waste gas and secondary                battery waste liquid (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The MOF blocks access to water molecules. The                method selectively modifies part or all of                unsaturated metal sites or defect sites which are                easily hydrolyzed due to high reactivity by                introducing nucleophilic compound to MOF by                modification after synthesis, improves stability by                blocking, and effectively adsorbs harmful                substances i.e. organic iodine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) MOF with improved moisture stability                compared to MOF before modification in which some                or all of the unsaturated metal sites in the MOF                are modified with nucleophilic compound to block                hydrolysis at the unsaturated metal or defect                sites; and
   (2) MOF having improved moisture stability                produced by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) is carried                  out by selectively modifying nucleophilic                  compound smaller than the pore size of the MOF                  which is target of modification at the                  unsaturated metal site which is the Lewis acid                  site of MOF. The step (b) comprises (i) placing                  the modified MOF and the compound to be supported                  in an oven, or (ii) supporting the MOF modified                  by a vapor deposition method; adding a compound                  for adsorbing or performing catalytic reaction                  while partially removing the nucleophilic                  compound supported on the modified MOF. Preferred                  Components: The MOF has defect sites which act as                  Lewis acids during MOF synthesis in step (a). The                  compounds for modification includes 1-12C                  alkylamine, 1-12C haloalkylamine, 1-4C                  haloalkyl-aryl-1-4 alkylamine, amine based                  compound of formula (NHR1-CnH2n-NHR2) (I), thiol                  based compound of formula (SH-CaH2a-SH) (II) and                  phosphorus based compound of formula                  (R3-5P-CcH2cN-PR6-8) (III). The compound is                  additionally supported by triethylenediamine or                  quinuclidine. The MOF: has increased                  hydrophobicity compared to MOF before                  modification due to modification with                  nucleophilic compounds; and is preferentially                  modified within mesopores rather than micropores                  by nucleophilic compounds in the case of                  mesoporous MOF. The MOF has an amino group                  substituted on the ligand before modification.                  The MOF before modification is functional product                  having reactive functional group, preferably                  NU-1000, MIL-100, MIL-101, extended MOF-74,                  PCN-222, DUT-32, Al-benzene dicarboxylate (BDC),                  Fe-BDC, Cr-BDC, V-BDC, Al-BTC, Cr-BTC, Fe-BTC,                  Al-BDC, Cr-BDC, Zr-BPDC, CuBTC(L1), where L1 is                  5-nitro-1,3-dicarboxylate,                  5-cyanide-1,3-dicarboxylate,                  5-hydroxyl-1,3-dicarboxylate, or                  pyridine-3,5-dicarboxylate, UiO-67, UiO-68,                  MOF-808, DUT-37, HKUST-1                  (Cu3(benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate (btc))2, MOF-14                  (Cu3(benzene-1,3,5-tribenzoate (btb)2,                  DUT-34(Cu3(btb)2), DUT-23((Cu2(bipy))3(btb)4),                  MOF-14, DUT-34, DUT-33, MOF-74(CPO-27), UMCM-1,                  UMCM-2, DUT- 6(MOF-205), UMCM-3, UMCM-4, UMCM-5,                  MOF-210, DUT-32( Zn4O(bpdc)(btctb)4/3, where                  btctb is                  4,4',4''-(benzene-1,3,5-triyltris(carbonylimino))trisbenzoate,                  where bpdc is 4,4'-biphenylendicarboxylate),                  DUT-6, PCN-21, DUT- 68, MOP-1, DUT-49, UMCM-1,                  DUT-7, DUT-28(Co22(BTB)12(NO3)8( DEF)x(H2O)y),                  where x and y are 0-50, NU-109, NU-110, DUT-13                  (Zn4O-(BenzTB)3/2, where BenzTB is                  N,N,N',N'-benzidinetetrabenzoate, PCN-69,                  HKUST-1, MIL-101(Cr) and/or UMCM-1. The                  nucleophilic compound is amylamine, heptylamine,                  4-(trifluoromethyl)benzylamine, and/or                  2,2,3,3,4,4,4-heptafluorobutylamine. The MOF                  inhibits hydrolysis in wet conditions with                  relative humidity of greater than or equal to                  0.01% in a moisture-containing medium.n, a and b = 1-12; andR1-R8 = H, 1-12C alkylene.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN112079583-A
TI Preparing building material by collecting dust and fine particles generated in process of crushing waste concrete and separating components into recycled aggregates, grinding into powder, homogenizing obtained powder and curing raw embryo.
AU HE Y
   WANG P
   LV L
   CHEN X
   LIU Z
   WANG F
   HU S
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 2020C7909J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing building material based on the rapid carbonization of regenerated cementitious materials, involves (a) collecting dust and fine particles generated in the process of crushing waste concrete and separating the components into recycled aggregates, and grinding into powder with a Blaine specific surface area greater than or equal to 300 m2/kg, (b) homogenizing the obtained powder, calcining at 900-1100 degrees C for 2-3 hours, cooling, grinding and homogenizing to obtain a regenerated cementitious material, (c) re-grinding, evenly mixing the regenerated cementitious material with water and pressing the product into a green embryo, where the Blaine specific surface area of the calcined powder is greater than or equal to 300 m2/kg, and (d) curing the raw embryo of the product in a carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing building materials based on the rapid carbonization of regenerated cementitious materials (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The building material has high strength and low free calcium oxide content, and solves the problem of low strength and high content of free calcium oxide on product durability when recycled cementing materials prepared from waste concrete are used as cementing materials or concrete admixtures.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The powder in step (a) involves 83-88 %mass cement stone powder, 9-11 %mass quartz sand powder and 3-8 %mass limestone powder. Preferred Method: The heating rate during the calcination process is 5-10 degrees C/minute. The step (b) involves calcining at 950-1100 degrees C for 2-3 hours at a heating rate of 6-9 degrees C/minute. The specific surface area of dust and fine particles after grinding is 300-420 m2/kg. The Blaine specific surface area of the calcined powder after grinding is 300-450 m2/kg. The method involves uniformly mixing the reclaimed cementitious material with water in a water-to-solid ratio of 0.15-0.25. The pressure used in the compression molding process is 10-20 MPa. The carbon dioxide concentration is 95-99.9%. The carbon dioxide gas partial pressure is 0.1-0.4 MPa. The curing time is 8-24 hours.

The promoter is                lanthanum oxide, magnesium oxide, copper oxide                and/or manganese oxide.
   USE - Low-temperature methanation catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has lower reaction temperature,                lower energy consumption, simple operation, high                catalytic activity, long service life in low                temperature environment, and effective catalytic                conversion of carbon monoxide and carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the catalyst comprising dissolving                soluble nickel salt, soluble aluminum salt and                soluble salt of promoter metal element in deionized                water, and adding N-vinylamide non-ionic polymer to                obtain precipitation solution containing 0.5-5                mol/L nickel; dissolving soluble sodium salt in                deionized water to obtain precipitation agent                containing 1-3 mol/L sodium; and mixing diatomite                with deionized water, water bath heating to 50-90                degrees C, adding precipitation solution and                precipitation agent, keeping pH to 9-11, aging to                50-90 degrees C for 1-3 hours, suction filtering                repeatedly, washing until pH is 6-8, drying filter                cake to 80-150 degrees C for 1-10 hours, and                calcining to 300-700 degrees C for 1-10                hours.

The soluble nickel salt is nickel nitrate or                  nickel acetate. The soluble aluminum salt is                  aluminum nitrate. The soluble salt of promoter                  metal element is nitrate. The soluble sodium salt                  is sodium carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The N-vinylamide                  non-ionic polymer is 0.01-0.8 wt.%                  polyvinylpyrrolidone.

   USE - As biomass carbon evolution electrocatalyst                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The biomass carbon evolution electrocatalyst                is simple and low-effective, and provides excellent                catalytic activity than that of the noble metal                ruthenium(IV) oxide catalyst and excellent                stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                preparation method of the biomass carbon evolution                electrocatalyst, comprising roasting a pure cotton                in a carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain a porous                biomass carbon-based material, formulating a mixed                solution containing metal ions and                sulfur-containing ligands, subjecting the porous                biomass carbon-based material into the mixed                solution such that metal ions and adsorbing                sulfur-containing ligands in the mixed solution in                the porous biomass carbon-based material, and                roasting the porous biomass carbon-based material                adsorbed with the metal ions and the                sulfur-containing ligand in a nitrogen atmosphere                to obtain a biomass carbon evolution                electrocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The specific surface                  area of the biomass carbon oxygen evolution                  electrocatalyst is 1796 plus minus 2 m2/g.                  Preferred Method: In the step of roasting the                  pure cotton in a carbon dioxide atmosphere to                  obtain a porous biomass carbon-based material,                  the porous biomass carbon-based material has a                  mesoporous and microporous structure, the                  specific surface area is 1226 plus minus 2 m2/g,                  and the conditions for roasting in a carbon                  dioxide atmosphere are heating at 900 degrees C                  at 5 degrees C/minute for 2 hours. The                  preparation method of the mixed solution                  comprises weighing a certain amount of ferric                  nitrate, nickel nitrate and thiourea in a glass                  bottle, and adding deionized water, where the                  concentration of ferric nitrate is 200-1000 ppm,                  the concentration of nickel nitrate is 100-2000                  ppm, the concentration of thiourea is 10-40 g/l,                  and the mixture is uniformly stirred to obtain a                  mixed solution. In the step of subjecting the                  porous biomass carbon-based material with the                  mixed solution, the porous biomass carbon-based                  material is soaked in the mixed solution and                  subjected to oscillation treatment for 5 hours,                  where the ratio of the weight of the porous                  biomass carbon-based material to the volume of                  the mixed solution is 20 mg:20 ml. In the step of                  roasting the porous biomass carbon-based material                  adsorbed with the metal ion and the                  sulfur-containing ligand in a nitrogen atmosphere                  to obtain a biomass carbon evolution                  electrocatalyst, the condition of roasting in a                  nitrogen atmosphere is heating to 800-1000                  degrees C at 5oC/minute for 1 hour.

   BAI S
   DU Y
   MA Z
AE UNIV XINJIANG (UXIJ-C)
GA 2020636507
AB    NOVELTY - Optimizing geopolymer for carbonation of                refined slag comprises (i) grinding and sieving                required amount of refined slag, and mixing with                water and stirring, conveying carbon dioxide for                carbonation reaction to obtain carbonized refining                slag slurry, where the weight ratio of refining                slag and water is 1:4.5-5.5, (ii) separating                solid-liquid and drying of the carbonized refining                slag slurry to obtain carbonized refining slag,                (iii) taking the 22-26 pts. wt. sodium silicate,                mixing and stirring 38-42 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide                and 58-62 pts. wt. water to obtain alkaline liquid                activator, (iv) adding 65-75 pts. wt. carbonized                refining slag and 200 pts. wt. fly ash to the                alkaline liquid activator, mixing and stirring to                obtain geopolymer with optimized carbonation of                refining slag.
   USE - The geopolymer is useful for in preparing                building material, wall material and the adsorption                material field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids the problem of high                alkalinity and free calcium oxide in the refining                slag of industrial waste, which causes it to be                used as building materials, water conservancy                projects, problems of poor stability and poor                stability of raw materials i.e. road engineering;                realizes the reuse of industrial waste; has good                compactness and good mechanical properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  particle size of the refined slag is 38-88 mu m                  and/or the stirring speed is 600-700                  revolutions/minute (rpm). The carbon dioxide                  ventilation is 450-500 ml/minute. The carbonation                  reaction temperature is 18-22 degrees C. In the                  step (ii), the drying temperature is 103-108                  degrees C, and the drying time is 1.8-2.2                  hours.
