PN WO2020048729-A1; EP3847143-A1; US2021340446-A1; US11459507-B2
TI Converting plastics to olefins comprises            gasification of plastics to synthesis gas by reaction            of plastics with pure oxygen, catalytic converting            synthesis gas to methane, catalytic converting methane            into olefins and separating olefins.
AU MICHELETTI F
   COPPINI V
AE MET GAS PROCESSING TECHNOLOGIES SPA (METG-Non-standard)
   NEXTCHEM SPA (NEXT-Non-standard)
   NEXTCHEM SPA (NEXT-Non-standard)
   NEXTCHEM SPA (NEXT-Non-standard)
GA 2020207548
AB    NOVELTY - Converting plastics to olefins, comprises: (A)                gasification of the plastics to synthesis gas by                reaction of the plastics with pure oxygen, where                the pure oxygen is used in a quantity which, added                to the content of oxygen possibly present in the                plastics and measured with ASTM D5291-A, forms a                content of total oxygen of 40-65 mol.% with respect                to the stoichiometric oxygen required for the                converting all the carbon contained in the plastics                to carbon dioxide; (B) catalytic converting the                synthesis gas produced in the step (A) to methane                in at least three successive steps, in at least the                first two of which hydrogen having a degree of                purity of greater than or equal to 95%, measured                with ASTM D7941M-14, is added to the synthesis gas;                (C) catalytic converting the methane produced in                the step (B) into olefins by the oxidative coupling                reaction of methane; and (D) separating the olefins                produced in the step (C) from other compounds                present in the reaction mixture of the step                (C).
   USE - The process: is useful for converting plastics                to olefins.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: provides the monomers for the                production of virgin polyolefins, i.e. polymers                that have a higher economic value than that of                recycled plastic; maximizes efficiency when carbon                content in the plastic used is high; and is                recyclable.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The synthesis gas                  produced in the step (A), which is sent to the                  step (B), and has a molar ratio hydrogen/carbon                  monoxide of greater than 1 before the addition of                  hydrogen in the step (B). The gasification step                  (A) of the plastics is carried out in a reactor                  in which the plastics are introduced in the                  presence of a stream of carbon dioxide. The step                  (B) is: carried out by recycling a part of the                  gaseous mixture produced in the first of the at                  least three subsequent steps to the first                  reactor, where the recycle ratio of the recycled                  part varies of 4-20 wt.%, preferably 8-18 wt.%;                  carried out with a molar ratio hydrogen/carbon                  comprised of 1.2-3, preferably 1.5-2.5; and                  carried out in four successive steps, in at least                  the first three of which hydrogen is added to the                  synthesis gas. The step (C) is: carried out using                  as a raw material a mixture containing at least                  85% molar of methane produced in the previous                  step (B). In the step (C), a gaseous mixture is                  produced comprising the olefins and carbon                  dioxide, in that part of the carbon dioxide is                  also sent to the gasification step (A) and used                  to produce a nitrogen-free atmosphere in the                  plastics which is introduced in the step                  (A).
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Method for ablating optical fiber, involves            forming discrete craters that are spaced apart from            each other in array which extends along longitudinal            axis of optical fiber and about circumference of            optical fiber.
AU ZHENG W
   JEBALI M A
   ZHU G
AE AFL TELECOM LLC (AFLT-C)
GA 202010198B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves generating a laser beam                for multiple discrete time periods. The laser beam                impacts and ablates the optical fiber during each                discrete time period. Each discrete impact of the                laser beam during the discrete time periods is at a                different location on a surface (16) of a cladding                (14). The ablation of the optical fiber during the                discrete time periods forms multiple discrete                craters (30) that are spaced apart from each other                in an array which extends along a longitudinal axis                (20) of the optical fiber and about a circumference                of the optical fiber. The discrete craters extends                only into the cladding and not extend into a core                (12). The laser beam is a carbon dioxide laser                beam.
   USE - Method for ablating optical fiber.
   ADVANTAGE - The use of a matrix of holes or discrete                craters reduce the risk of linear stress                concentrations. The parameters of the discrete                craters can be varied and customized per                application to increase cladding light absorption.                The individual craters can be made deeper to                increase the power stripping efficiency without a                strong impact on the tensile strength of the                fiber.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                ablated end fiber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the perspective view of the                ablated optical fiber.
   Core (12)
   Cladding (14)
   Surface (16)
   Longitudinal axis (20)
   Discrete crater (30)

AE CHITKARA INNOVATION INCUBATOR FOUND (CHIT-Non-standard)
GA 202002351Y
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an insulating layer (L2) configured on a substrate layer (L1). A sensing layer (L3) changes in presence of a target gas in ambient air, where the sensing layer includes a resistance characteristic. The sensing layer adsorbs the target gas on a surface, and causes resistance change across the surface. An electrode (L4) is arranged in a metal-semiconductor-metal pattern. The electrode provides an electrical connection, and measures change in resistance, and confirms the presence of target gas. The sensing layer is coated on an insulating layer of the substrate, where the substrate is formed as silicon-wafer, and the insulating layer is formed as silicon di oxide.
   USE - Device for detecting poisonous gases e.g. carbon monoxide, hydrogen sulfide, nitrogen oxides, sulfur oxides, carbon dioxide and ammonia (all claimed), released from domestic and industrial areas.
   ADVANTAGE - The device detects the gases that are poisonous to environment in accurate, rapid and efficient manner.

   (1) a method of manufacturing a gas detecting device
   (2) a method for detecting a target gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a device for detecting poisonous gases.
   Substrate layer (L1)
   Insulating layer (L2)
   Sensing layer (L3)

PT P
PN CN110551318-A; CN110551318-B
TI Preparation of graphene-based heat and moisture exchange composite film used in membrane type heat exchange system, involves depositing graphene quantum dots and metal organic framework on polyester microporous membrane, and drying.
AU ZHANG Y
   DUAN Y
   ZHAO Z
AE SHANGHAI LEVSON NANOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A5791F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of graphene-based heat and moisture exchange composite film involves (i) subjecting a natural flake graphite ethanol slurry or an expandable graphite ethanol slurry to a surfactant modification treatment by a condensation reflux method, (ii) ultrasonically treating the prepared slurry by supercritical carbon dioxide fluid method at 35-45 degrees C and pressure of 20-30 MPa for 20-26 hours and centrifuging the supernatant to obtain graphene quantum dots having particle size of 10-75 nm, (iii) immersing the microporous polyester film in the graphene quantum dot slurry, (iv) introducing the metal-organic framework precursor synthesis solution to the graphene quantum dot slurry, and depositing graphene quantum dots and metal organic framework in situ on the polyester microporous membrane after 24 hours, and (v) drying the polyester microporous membrane using 80-120 W microwave dryer for 10-20 minutes.
   USE - Preparation of graphene-based heat and moisture exchange composite film used as core material of membrane type heat exchange system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces composite film having excellent heat and moisture transmission properties, mechanical properties, and chemical stability. The heat exchange system has actively developed as an energy saving and environmentally protective device which effectively improves indoor air quality and reduces air-conditioning energy consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The condensation reflux method involves adding 1-7% natural graphite or expandable graphite into ethanol for dispersing uniformly at high speed, condensing and refluxing at 60-80 degrees C for 7-9 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surfactant comprises one of sodium lauryl sulfate or polyvinylpyrrolidone or polyethylene glycol p-octylphenyl ether. The metal organic framework is one of zeolitic imidazolate framework-8 (ZIF-8) or zeolitic imidazolate framework-90 (ZIF-90). The ZIF-8 precursor synthesis solution comprises methanol as solvent and 0.01-0.03 mol/L zinc nitrate and 2-methylimidazole (in total), and the ZIF-90 precursor synthesis solution comprises tert-butanol as solvent and 0.04-0.10 mol/L zinc nitrate and imidazole-2-formaldehyde (in total).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The microporous polyester film is one of polyvinylidene fluoride film, polyethersulfone film, or PTFE film, and has pore diameter of 50-200 mu m.

PT P
PN CN110537659-A
TI Sulfur-free drying of edible and medicinal fungi by washing fungi, soaking in inhibitor solution, steaming, drying, cooling under ozone, adding to film-forming liquid, baking, packaging, vacuumizing and adding non-contact water absorbent.
AU WANG R
   CAO S
   JI N
   MA F
   MA C
   BA L
   WU W
   LEI J
AE UNIV GUIYANG (UGUY-C)
GA 2019A51811
AB    NOVELTY - Sulfur-free drying method for edible and medicinal fungi, involves (i) washing the edible and medicinal fungi with water, soaking in an inhibitor solution for 25-30 minutes, removing, and draining the water, (ii) rapidly steaming the edible and medicinal fungi for 10 -80 minutes by adopting gas mixed water, (iii) drying the steamed fungi, passing the fungi through hot air or infrared ray until the water content is lower than 10%, and placing the fungi in an ozone environment to cool to below 40 degrees C, and (iv) adding the cooled fungi to a film-forming liquid, dipping for 2-3 seconds, baking in a vacuum drying machine for 6-8 minutes, cooling, filling into a packaging bag made of a high barrier film material, vacuumizing, filling it with more than 98% high purity carbon dioxide, and adding a non-contact water absorbent to the packaging bag.
   USE - As sulfur-free drying method for edible and medicinal fungi.
   ADVANTAGE - The drying method effectively reduces the loss of nutritional components in edible and medicinal fungi, and increases the flavor of edible and medicinal fungi. The dried fungi has a long storage time and excellent quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The concentration of the ozone environment is 2.5-3.5 mu l/l. The baking is carried out at a degree of vacuum of 0 MPa to -0.1 MPa at 60-65 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The gas is a mixed gas of chlorine dioxide and carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The inhibitor solution comprises 6-8 pts. wt. loquat leaves, 1-2 pts. wt. herba houttuyniae, 3-4 pts. wt. radix paeoniae alba, 6-8 pts. wt. cortex lycii radicis, 1-2 pts. wt. common selfheal spike, 6-8 pts. wt. Camellia oleifera shell, 0.3-0.5 pt. wt. betaine and 0.1-0.2 pt. wt. calcium chloride. The film-forming liquid comprises 4-6 pts. wt. dextrin, 3-4 pts. wt. chitosan, 6-7 pts. wt. Zanthoxylum bungeanum, 1-2 pts. wt. galangal, 1-2 pts. wt. Litsea cubeba, 0.1-0.2 pt. wt. cloves and 0.5-1 pt. wt. cinnamon. Preferred Method: The inhibitor solution is prepared by pulverizing loquat leaves, herba houttuyniae and radix paeoniae alba, adding 5-6 times water, ultrasonically extraction at 30-40 degrees C for 25-30 minutes, filtering and concentrating the filtrate under reduced pressure to a paste to obtain extract (A), mixing and pulverizing cortex lycii radicis, common selfheal spike, and C.oleifera shell, adding 8-10 times 80% ethanol, performing reflux extraction for 35-40 minutes, filtering, and recovering the ethanol in the solution under reduced pressure to obtain extract (B), mixing the extract (A), extract (B) and calcium chloride and diluting 500-600 times to obtain the inhibitor solution. The film-forming liquid is prepared by pulverizing Z.bungeanum, galangal, L.cubeba, cloves and cinnamon, adding 15-20 times absolute ethanol, extracting in a water bath at 45-55 degrees C for 4-5 hours, and recovering ethanol under reduced pressure to obtain essential oils, mixing the essential oil, dextrin and chitosan, adding water with a mass of 500 times, and placing in a high-speed shear emulsifier for shearing at a speed of 10000 rpm for 5 minutes to obtain the film-forming liquid.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

   BIOSENSE WEBSTER ISRAEL LTD (BIWE-C)
GA 2019862026
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves producing an elastic filler material comprising foam (23), by mixing a carbonate with a hydrolyzed silicone. A flexible covering (22) configured for implantation within a breast of a human subject is filled with the elastic filler material. The foam is homogenized by spinning the foam during or after mixing the hydrolyzed silicone and the carbonate. The carbonate is mixed by mixing sodium bicarbonate. The carbonate is mixed with the hydrolyzed silicone by mixing the hydrolyzed silicone and the carbonate with a catalyst. The elastic filler material is produced by producing a foam of silicone gel mixed with carbon dioxide bubbles.
   USE - Method for manufacturing breast implant.
   ADVANTAGE - The cushion and weight of the implant are properly adjusted so as to maintain the cushion at a level similar to that of cellular cells of the human body and to shorten a recovery time. Plural grooves or through-holes are provided on a surface of the implant, and the body fluid are smoothly flowed through the grooves or through-holes to improve the affinity of the implant against the cellular cells. Therefore, the availability of light breast implants is increased.

TI Fire fighting method based on battery partition management of automobile, involves injecting fire-fighting medium in to battery charging chamber, when second smoke value and battery temperature exceeds corresponding limit value.
AU REN Y
   LI J
   ZHANG C
   DONG C
AE LIFAN IND GROUP CO LTD (LIFG-C)
   LIFAN IND GROUP CO LTD (LIFG-C)
GA 201997332T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves setting two smoke sensor in battery charging interval to respectively obtain corresponding first smoke value and second smoke value of battery charging chamber. The temperature of battery, first smoke value and second smoke value in battery charging chamber are monitored. The fire-fighting medium is injected to the battery charging chamber, when any one of battery charging chamber first smoke value, second smoke value and battery temperature exceeds the limit value. The difference between first smoke value and second smoke value is less than preset value, and then fire-fighting medium is injected in to the battery charging chamber, when second smoke value and battery temperature exceeds the corresponding limit value. The fire fighting medium comprises fire liquid and fire gas that is carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Fire fighting method based on battery partition management of automobile.
   ADVANTAGE - The fire treatment is performed before battery capable of developing into fire, the fire effect is better and the economic loss is reduced. The occurrence of fault is avoided, and the reliability of the fire fighting system is improved.

   VIKENMAN J

AB    NOVELTY - Disinfecting water and/or components contacting with process water in evaporative cooling systems, cooling towers and wet scrubbers, comprises providing at least one UV irradiation device at least in components intended for collecting the process water, preferably at least one UV radiator is provided in at least one process water sump, where the UV emitters have electrical or electronic ignition devices, and UV-irradiating the process water with the aid of the UV irradiation devices. The process water at least one preferably oxidized by UV radiation oxidizing agent and/or disinfectant is added and/or the process water at least one lime-carbon dioxide balance affecting chemical is added.
   USE - The method is useful for disinfecting water and/or components contacting with process water in evaporative cooling systems, cooling towers and wet scrubbers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can be carried out efficiently irrespective of the large dimensions of the systems.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for evaporative cooling system, cooling tower or wet scrubber, comprising at least one UV irradiation device in at least one provided for collecting process water component, preferably a process water tank, where a UV radiator provided in the UV irradiation device has at least one electrical or electronic ignition device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The ignition devices are arranged on the holder of the UV lamps or integrated in this holder. The UV irradiation devices have a protective structure, preferably a cage-like protective structure, where the protective structure comprises at least two brackets which span a UV radiator provided in a UV irradiation device in the transverse direction and in the longitudinal direction of the UV radiation emitter is arranged at a distance. At least one UV irradiation device is immersed at least partially, preferably completely, in the process water, at least during the UV irradiation. At least one UV irradiation device is provided at least during the UV irradiation on the surface of the process water. The hydrogen peroxide is added as oxidizing agent. At least one complexing agent, preferably citric acid or a phosphonate, is added as a chemical affecting lime-carbonic acid balance. The addition of an oxidizing agent and/or disinfectant and/or a chemical influencing lime-carbonic acid balance takes place at least once a day, preferably between once a day and ten times a day, preferably between once a day and five times a day.

PT P
PN CN110116024-A; CN110116024-B
TI Heterogeneous three-dimensional bivalent copper based metal-organic framework catalyst for catalyzing cycloaddition reaction.
AU ZHAO B
   JIANG X
   HOU S
AE UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
   UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
GA 201971596S
AB    NOVELTY - A heterogeneous three-dimensional bivalent copper based metal-organic framework catalyst, is claimed.
   USE - Heterogeneous three-dimensional bivalent copper based metal-organic framework catalyst for catalyzing cycloaddition reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high porosity and large surface area, and is prepared by a simple process using inexpensive and easily available raw materials. The catalyst has excellent catalytic activity can still be maintained at a high level even after repeated recycling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of heterogeneous three-dimensional bivalent copper based metal-organic framework catalyst, which involves (1) mixing organic ligand L (5-aminonicotinic acid) with copper nitrate pentahydrate and cuprous iodide in a mixed solvent of dimethylacetamide (N,N-dimethylacetamide) and acetonitrile under closed conditions, and crystallizing metal organic framework material using solvothermal reaction by adding a small amount of formic acid. Mixing organic ligand L (5-aminonicotinic acid) with copper nitrate pentahydrate and cuprous iodide at a molar ratio of 4:2:1. Each 0.054 mmol of copper nitrate pentahydrate corresponds to 3-4 mL of N,N-dimethylacetamide, 2-3 mL of acetonitrile and 20-40 mu L of anhydrous formic acid, and solvothermal reaction temperature is 120 degrees C for 72 hours; and
   (2) use of heterogeneous three-dimensional bivalent copper based metal-organic framework catalyst for catalyzing a cycloaddition reaction of an alkyne compound with carbon dioxide to form a 2-oxazolidinone compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The heterogeneous three-dimensional bivalent copper based metal-organic framework catalyst is a hexagonal system belonging to space group of P6/m space group, and having unit cell parameters a of 24.4751 (4), b of 24.4751 (4), c of 23.8686 (4), alpha of 90 degrees , beta of 90 degrees , and gamma of 120 degrees . The metal center of the frame contains two different valence states of copper ions such as monovalent copper and divalent copper, each of the six monovalent copper is connected to five iodine ions, forming a football-type (Cu6I5) cation cluster, copper(I) iodide extends outward through the pyridine nitrogen atom on the ligand, at the same time, every two divalent copper bridges with eight carboxy oxygen from four ligands to form a typical Paddle-wheel dual-core copper structure. The bivalent copper ends are coordinated with two oxygen atoms from the dimethylacetamide molecule, each divalent copper is a five-coordination coordination mode, and eventually constitute a three-dimensional long-range orderly structure. In catalyst structure, along the c-axis, (Cu6I5) clusters are connected to Paddle-wheel dual-core copper and ligands to form holes which contain nitrate ions that maintain charge balance and a large amount of free solvent molecules. Preferred Condition: The crystal of the metal organic framework material is cooling naturally to room temperature or lowering to room temperature at a rate of 1.25 degrees C/hour, centrifuge washing with N,N-dimethylacetamide and acetonitrile solution, activating with acetonitrile for 2 days, changing the new acetonitrile solution every 8 hours, and drying at 60 degrees C for 6 hours.

PN RU2696739-C1
TI METHOD OF STIMULATING OIL AND GAS FORMATION BY PUMPING LIQUEFIED GAS COMPOSITION.
AU TOROPETSKII K V
   BORISOV G A
   PONOMARENKO D V
   ZHIRNOV R A
AE G4 GROUP CO LTD (GFOU-Non-standard)
GA 201974782V
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to oil and gas industry and can be used for stimulation of oil and gas formation. Method involves pumping a composition from liquefied gases to a bottom-hole formation zone by forming a composition of three streams containing a propane-butane mixture, natural gas or associated petroleum gas and nitrogen, which are mixed before pumping. Note here that flow control is performed to vary gas composition, injection rate and volume thereof. According to invention, fourth flow of liquefied carbon dioxide is added, and control of all flows is carried out additionally with possibility to control temperature of gas composition so, that the composition maintains liquid aggregate state on the entire flow from the flow mixing point to the well bottom. Flow control is carried out in a certain sequence. First, a buffer zone is formed by adding an additional stream of a hydrate inhibitor to four main streams. Then, four main streams are fed into the formation until formation of a composition with temperature below the reservoir, which after levelling of its temperature with the reservoir provides growth of intra-bed pressure with excess pressure of formation fracturing. After that, supply of four main flows is continued until formation of fluid column in the well, which provides its killing.
   USE - Oil and gas industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result consists in improvement of economic efficiency of hydrocarbon production.
5 cl, 2 dwg

PN WO2019145089-A1; CN111655750-A; US2020354503-A1; EP3743455-A1
TI Organic aerogel obtained by reacting isocyanate compound and thiol compound having specified functionalities in presence of solvent, used in thermal insulating material or acoustic material, and as thermal insulating for storage of cryogens.
AU MIGUEL GARCIA I
   DEL SAZ-OROZCO RODRIGUEZ B
   DE SANTO I
   SAKALYTE A
   TORRES CANO E
   SALHI F
   MIGUEL G I
   DEL SAZ-OROZCO R B
   TORRES C E
AE HENKEL AG & CO KGAA (HENK-C)
   HENKEL IP & HOLDING GMBH (HENK-C)
   HENKEL IP & HOLDING GMBH (HENK-C)
   HENKEL AG & CO KGAA (HENK-C)
   HENKEL IP & HOLDING GMBH (HENK-C)
   HENKEL AG & CO KGAA (HENK-C)
   HENKEL IP & HOLDING GMBH (HENK-C)
GA 2019675575
AB    NOVELTY - Organic aerogel obtained by reacting an isocyanate compound having a functionality greater than or equal to 2 and a thiol compound having a functionality greater than or equal to 2 in the presence of a solvent is claimed.
   USE - The organic aerogel is: useful in a thermal insulating material or an acoustic material; and as a thermal insulating for the storage of cryogens (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The organic aerogel: has a solid content of 4-40%, based on initial weight of the solution, preferably 4-20%; a thermal conductivity less than 60 mW/m.K, preferably less than 40 mW/m.K; is light weight; has low thermal conductivity, low shrinkage and high mechanical properties; and exhibits improved hydrophobicity, while maintaining good mechanical properties and being safe to use and not dusty.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the organic aerogel comprising (1) dissolving a thiol compound into a solvent and adding an isocyanate compound and mixing, (2) adding a catalyst if present and mixing, (3) letting the mixture to stand in order to form a gel, (4) washing the gel with a solvent, and (5) drying the gel by supercritical drying or ambient drying, where optionally the carbon dioxide from the supercritical drying is recycled.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the method, temperature from room temperature to 100 degrees C is applied at step (3) to form a gel, preferably temperature from room temperature to 45 degrees C is applied. Preferred Components: The isocyanate compound and the thiol compound are reacted in the presence of a catalyst. The isocyanate compound has a functionality of 2-6, preferably 2-3. The thiol compound has a functionality of 2-6, preferably 2-4. The isocyanate compound is an aromatic isocyanate compound or an aliphatic isocyanate compound of formula (OCN-R1-NCO) (I), (R1(NCO)3) (II), (OCN-(R1)n-NCO) (III), (N(OCN-CH2-(CH2)n-CH2-NH-C(=O))3) (IV), (OCN-(CH2)q-N(C(=O)-NH-(CH2)q-NCO)2 (V), and 1,4-diisocyanato-benzene of formula (VI), preferably 1,1'-methylenebis(4-isocyanatobenzene), triphenylmethane- 4,4',4''-triisocyanate, 1,3,5-tris(6-isocyanatohexyl)-1,3,5-triazin-2,4,6-trione, N,N,N'-tris(6-isocyanatohexyl)dicarbonimidic diamide, 5-(5-(3,5-bis(3-isocyanatotolyl)-2,4,6- trioxo-1,3,5-triazinan-1-yl)tolyl)-1-(3-(3-(3-(3,5-bis(3-isocyanatotolyl)-2,4,6-trioxo-1,3,5-triazinan-1 -yl)tolyl)-5-(3-isocyanatotolyl)-2,4,6-trioxo-1,3,5-triazinan-1-yl)tolyl)-3-(3-isocyanatotolyl)-1,3,5-triazinane-2,4,6-trione, 1-(3,5-bis(isocyanatomethyl)-3,5-dimethylcyclohexyl)-3-(3-(isocyanatomethyl)-3,5,5-trimethylcyclohexyl)-5-(4-(3-(3- (isocyanatomethyl)-3,5,5-trimethylcyclohexyl)-5-(3-isocyanatotolyl)-2,4,6-trioxo-1,3,5-triazinan-1-yl)tolyl)-1,3,5-triazinane-2,4,6-trione, and/or di-3-isocyanatotoluidino-3-ethyl-3-((3-isocyanatotoluidinooxycarbonyl)methyl)glutarate. The thiol compound is of formula e.g. (R5-CH2-C(CH2-R6)(CH2-R7)-CH2-R8), (R5-CH2-C(CH2-R6)(CH2-R7)-N(R9)2), (R5-CH2-C(CH2-R6)(CH2-R7)-CH2-O-CH2-C(CH2-R10)(CH2-R11)-CH2-R12) and R13-(CH2)s-R14), preferably dipentaerythritol hexakis(3-mercaptopropionate), 4,4'-bis(mercaptomethyl)biphenyl, 1,3,5-tris(3-mercaptobutyloxethyl)-1,3,5-triazine-2,4,6(1H,3H,5H)-trione, pentaerythritol tetrakis (3-mercaptobutylate), trimethylolpropane tris(3-mercaptobutyrate) and/or pentaerythritol tetrakis (3-mercaptobutylate). In the aerogel, ratio of thiol groups to isocyanate groups is 10:1-1:10, preferably 4:1-1:4. The catalyst is alkyl amines, aromatic amines, imidazole derivatives, aza compounds, guanidine derivatives and/or amidines, preferably catalyst is tertiary amine consisting of triazabicyclodecene, dimethylbenzylamine, triethylamine and/or 1,4-diazabicyclo(2.2.2)octane. The aerogel further comprises a reinforcement consisting of fibers, particles, non-woven and woven fiber fabrics, chopped strand mats, honeycombs and/or three dimensional structures.R1 = 1-30C alkyl, 3-30C cycloalkyl, aryl, 7-30C alkylaryl, 3-30C heterocycloalkyl, 1-30C heteroalkyl (all optionally substituted), single bonded -O-, -S-, -C(O)-, - S(O)2- and/or -S(PO3)-;n = 1-30;X1 = H, halo or linear or branched 1-6C alkyl, attached on their respective phenyl ring at the 2-position, 3-position or 4-position, and their respective isomers;R2 = 1-30C alkyl, 3-30C cycloalkyl, aryl, 7-30C alkylaryl, 3-30C heterocycloalkyl, 1-30C heteroalkyl (all optionally substituted), single bonded -O-, -S-, -C(O)-, -S(O)2- and/or -S(PO3)-;q = 1-6;R5-R12 = -O-CO-(CH2)rSH, -O-CO-(CH2)rCHSHCH3 and/or -(CH2)rCH3;R9 = -(CH2)r-R5;r = 1-6;R13, R14 = -O-CO-(CH2)t-SH and/or O-CO-(CH2)t-CH(SH)CH3;t = 1-6; and = 1-10.
   EXAMPLE - Solution was prepared with 20 wt.% of solid content, an equivalent ratio isocyanate/thiol of 1:1, without catalyst and acetone as a solvent. For the preparation of a sample of 30 ml 2.57 g of dipentaerythritol hexakis(3-mercaptopropionate) was dissolved in 15.1 g of acetone and subsequently 2.46 g of 1,1'-methylenebis(4-isocyanatobenzene) was added. The mixture was stirred manually and left at 45 degrees C for 48 hours. The resulting gel was washed three times with acetone every 24 hours, and using a volume of solvent three times the volume of the gel for each step. Subsequently the gel was dried via carbon dioxide supercritical drying. Thus the organic aerogel was obtained.

PN WO2019118117-A1; CA3085881-A1; CN111479908-A; SG11202005407-A1; EP3724301-A1; US2020369975-A1; US2022073836-A1
TI Lubricating composition used to lubricate mechanical device i.e. internal combustion engine, comprises an oil of lubricating viscosity, and a detergent comprising a polyolefin-substituted hydroxy-aromatic carboxylic acid or its salt.
AU DELBRIDGE E E
   MOSIER P E
   MIATT P
AE LUBRIZOL CORP (LUBR-C)
   LUBRIZOL CORP (LUBR-C)
   LUBRIZOL CORP (LUBR-C)
GA 201951797F
AB    NOVELTY - Lubricating composition comprises: (a) an oil of lubricating viscosity; and (b) a detergent comprising a polyolefin-substituted hydroxy-aromatic carboxylic acid or its salt, where the polyolefin is derived from a branched alkene having at least 4C atoms and the polyolefin has a number average molecular weight of 150-800.
   USE - The composition is useful for lubricating a mechanical device (claimed) including internal combustion engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has appropriate oil solubility which may also provide at least one other benefit to a lubricating composition including anti-wear performance, oxidation performance, and/or viscosity performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) lubricating (M1) a mechanical device comprising supplying the lubricating composition to the device; and
   (2) forming (M2) the lubricating composition, comprising (i) forming a polyolefin-substituted hydroxy-aromatic carboxylic acid, where the polyolefin is derived from a branched alkene having at least 4C atoms and the polyolefin has a number average molecular weight of 150-800, to form a substrate, (ii) optionally, reacting the substrate with a metal base in the presence of carbon dioxide to form a metal salt, and (iii) combining the polyolefin-substituted hydroxy-aromatic carboxylic acid salt thereof with an oil of lubricating viscosity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The detergent is a metal salt, where the metal in the salt comprises calcium, and the metal is overbased.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The composition further comprises at least one of detergents, antioxidants, dispersants, antiwear agents, friction modifiers and/or corrosion inhibitors. Preferred Components: The polyolefin-substituted hydroxy-aromatic carboxylic acid is of formula (I), preferably an alkylsalicylate of formula (II). The carboxylic acid is a hydroxybenzoic acid.R1 = polyolefin;R2 = H or 1-40C hydrocarbyl, preferably second polyolefin derived from a branched alkene having at least 4C atoms and the polyolefin has a number average molecular weight of 150-800; andR = polyolefin.The branched alkene comprises isobutylene. The polyolefin: includes a chain derived from at least 4, preferably up to 18 branched alkene units; and has a number average molecular weight of 150-400. The detergent is free of 8C and higher unbranched alkyl chains. The polyolefin: contains 8-50C atoms, preferably up to 30C atoms; and is a polyisobutylene having a number average molecular weight of up to 600, up to 500 or 300-400. The composition comprises at least 0.01 wt.%, preferably at least 2 wt.% or up to 20 wt.%, preferably up to 2.5 wt.% of polyolefin-substituted hydroxy-aromatic carboxylic acid. The oil of lubricating viscosity: comprises at least one of an API Group I, II, III, IV, and V base oil; is at least 10 wt.% of the lubricating composition; and is at least 30 wt.% or up to 95 wt.% of the lubricating composition. In the method (M2), the mechanical device: comprises an engine or driveline device; comprises a heavy duty diesel or marine diesel engine; is a hydraulic system, turbine system or circulating oil system; and is a transmission.
   EXAMPLE - Typical lubricating composition comprised of: 0.01-2 wt.% polyolefin-substituted hydroxy- aromatic carboxylic acid or its salt; 0-4 wt.% antioxidant; 0-2 wt.% dispersant; 0-5 wt.% detergent; 0-5 wt.% anti-wearagent; 0-3 wt.% friction modifier; 0-10 wt.% viscosity modifier; 0-1.3 wt.% any other performance additive (antifoam/demulsifier/pour point depressant); 0-0.1 wt.% metal deactivator; 0-0.2 wt.% rust inhibitor; 0-3 wt.% extreme pressure agent; and rest of oil of lubricating viscosity.

PN CN109762106-A; CN109762106-B
TI Preparing hydrogen sulfide molecularly imprinted polymer removing hydrogen sulfide comprises e.g. providing water as a template molecule instead of hydrogen sulfide, performing bulk polymerization, or suspension polymerization.
AU WANG R
   HUANG Y
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 201946433D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrogen sulfide molecularly imprinted polymer comprises providing water as a template molecule instead of hydrogen sulfide, performing bulk polymerization, suspension polymerization, emulsion polymerization, precipitation polymerization or surface blot polymerization to obtain molecularly imprinted polymers.
   USE - The hydrogen sulfide molecularly imprinted polymer is useful for removing hydrogen sulfide (claimed) and separating vulcanized hydrogen and carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing hydrogen sulfide molecularly imprinted polymer having sandwich structure comprising providing metal organic skeleton modified with heteropoly acid as a carrier, water as a template molecule, monomer, crosslinking agent and porogen, carrying out crosslinking polymerization on the surface of the carrier, preparing hydrogen sulfide molecularly imprinted polymer having a sandwich structure, where the monomer contains a double bond and a functional group, and the functional group is carboxy group, amide group, ester group or pyridine group;
   (2) hydrogen sulfide molecularly imprinted polymer;
   (3) removing hydrogen sulfide comprising providing hydrogen sulfide molecularly imprinted polymer as a desulfurizing agent, passing hydrogen sulfide-containing gas through desulfurizing agent to remove hydrogen sulfide from the gas; and
   (4) regenerating a hydrogen sulfide molecularly imprinted polymer comprising purging hydrogen sulfide molecularly imprinted polymer obtained by removing hydrogen sulfide by air to obtain regenerated hydrogen sulfide molecularly imprinted polymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal organic skeleton is prepared from terephthalic acid and zirconium tetrachloride. Preferred Method: The heteropoly acid modified metal organic skeleton is prepared by adding phthalic acid, zirconium tetrachloride and heteropolyacid in hydrochloric acid and dimethylformamide for solvothermal reaction, where the mass ratio of zirconium tetrachloride to phthalic acid is 0.05-10:0.05-10, the mass ratio of zirconium tetrachloride to heteropoly acid is 0.05-10:0.05-10, the addition ratio of zirconium tetrachloride, hydrochloric acid and dimethylformamide is 0.05-10:0.1-10:1-100 (g:ml:ml). The solvothermal reaction temperature is 100-300 degrees C and the reaction time is 12-48 hours. In the process, after adding water and the monomer, adding carrier, stirring to obtain suspension, adding crosslinking agent and initiator, passing through nitrogen gas, sealing mixture, reacting to obtain precipitate, washing and drying. The nitrogen flow rate is 10-200 ml/minute, the time is 5-60 minute, the reaction time is 12-48 hours, the reaction temperature is 0-75 degrees C, the input ratio of water, monomer, porogen, carrier, and crosslinking agent is 0.01-10:0.05-10:0.1-100:0.1-5:0.01-10 (ml:g:ml:g:ml), the porogen is methanol, dimethyl sulfoxide, dimethylformamide, acetonitrile, ethyl acetate or acetone, the monomer is methacrylic acid, acrylamide, methyl methacrylate or 4-vinylpyridine, the crosslinking agent is tetraethoxysilane, phenyltriethoxysilane, ethylene glycol dimethacrylate or triallyl isocyanurate, the initiator is 2,2'-azobis(2,4-dimethylvaleronitrile), azobisisobutyronitrile, benzoyl peroxide or potassium persulfate.

PN CN109382096-A; CN109382096-B
TI Preparing ruthenium-based catalyst, comprises e.g. extruding carrier precursor, obtaining a molded product, subjecting molded product to steam treatment in atmosphere of steam and supporting active metal component ruthenium on support.
AU SUN X
   HOU C
   XIA G
   WU Y
   JIN C
   YAN Z
   LI M
   WU H
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)
GA 201920841D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ruthenium-based catalyst, comprises (a) extruding carrier precursor, obtaining a molded product, subjecting the molded product to steam treatment in an atmosphere of steam to obtain a carrier; and (b) supporting the active metal component ruthenium on the support obtained in the step (a), where the method of molding the extruded strip conditions of steam treatment and the type of carrier precursor include average pore diameter of the obtained carrier is increased compared to the average pore diameter of the carrier precursor, BET low temperature nitrogen adsorption, and amount of increase Delta n is at least Delta n is n2-n1, n1 is the average pore diameter of the carrier precursor and n2 is the average pore diameter of the obtained carrier.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ruthenium-based catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method of is simple and easy, catalyst prepared has good physicochemical parameters, and has excellent performance when applied to Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction, and has high 5C hydrocarbon selectivity and high carbon-monoxide conversion and high stability with low carbon dioxide and methane selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) ruthenium-based catalyst, prepared as mentioned above; and
   (2) Fischer-Tropsch synthesis method, comprising contacting syngas with catalyst under Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction condition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amount of increase Delta n is as specified. Carrier precursor is pseudoboehmite having an average pore diameter of 70-109 Angstrom , and pseudoboehmite composed of sulfur-free compounds. Preferred Method: The method of extruding the carrier precursor comprises mixing carrier precursor, water, 2-4 pts. wt. extrusion agent and 0.6-4 pts. wt. peptizing agent to obtain mixture, extruding in a extruder, where the mass ratio of water to carrier precursor is 0.5-1.8:1, preferably 0.55-1.5:1, and extruder is a screw extruder; or mixing carrier precursor, 1-5 pts. wt. surfactant, water, co-extruding agent and peptizing agent to obtain a material C, extruding material C in extruder, where the mass ratio of material C to carrier precursor is 0.5-1.8:1, preferably 0.55-1.5:1, carrier precursor, the surfactant, the water, the extrusion agent and the peptizing agent are mixed in such a manner so that the carrier precursor, the extrusion agent , the peptizing agent and optionally water are mixed to obtain the material A, and the surfactant is used, mixing with water and/or alcohol to obtain material B, mixing material A and material B to obtain the material C, and surfactant is a cationic surfactant, an amphoteric surfactant, a nonionic surfactant or a high molecular substance having surfactant characteristics. The carrier precursor is mixed with a surfactant, and then the extrusion molding is performed, and the obtained molded product is directly subjected to steam treatment in a steam atmosphere without drying. The conditions for the extrusion molding include an extrusion temperature of 40-90 degrees C, preferably 45-80 degrees C. The steam atmosphere has a steam content of 50-100 vol.%. The steam -containing atmosphere contains steam and air, and the content of steam is 50-90vol.%, preferably 60-80 vol.%. The conditions of steam treatment include temperature of 400-700 degrees C, preferably 500-650 degrees C, a time of 1-10 hours, preferably 2-6 hours, and a volumetric air space velocity of 300-800/hour. The method further comprises drying the molded product, and then performing steam treatment in an atmosphere containing water vapor, where drying includes drying the shaped body within 0-10 hours after extrusion, preferably within 0-5 hours and drying conditions include temperature of 140-260 degrees C and time is 1-5 hours. In the step (b), the amount of support and active metal component ruthenium added is ruthenium content based on the total weight of the catalyst is 0.1-15 wt.%, preferably 0.2-12 wt.%, where the method further comprises, after step (a), introducing an auxiliary agent into the catalyst, the auxiliary agent is lanthanum, zirconium, cerium, silicon, tungsten, molybdenum, copper, manganese, rhenium, rhodium, lead, osmium, iridium, platinum, silver or gold, and after step (a), prior to step (b), an auxiliary agent is introduced into the catalyst amount of is based on the oxide is 0.01-10 wt.% based on the total weight of the catalyst.

TI Device for storing or transporting harvested fresh fish in e.g. ocean shipping freighter, has cathode inlet being in communication with inside of tote, where cathode consumes oxygen inside tote and extracts oxygen from inside of tote.
AU BELL L D
   SCHANZER D G
   WESTCOTT B J
AE GLOBAL FRESH FOODS (GLOB-Non-standard)
   GLOBAL FRESH FOODS (GLOB-Non-standard)
GA 2019109715
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a sealable tote for storing or transporting an oxidatively degradable foodstuff. A fuel cell/hydrogen (104) includes a cathode having a cathode inlet in gaseous communication with an inside of the tote. The cathode consumes oxygen inside the tote and extracts oxygen from the inside of the tote. A hydrogen source is in gaseous communication with the fuel cell anode. A carbon dioxide remover comprises hydrated lime, activated carbon, lithium hydroxide or metal oxides. The fuel cell reacts hydrogen from the hydrogen source with oxygen located in the tote.
   USE - Device for storing or transporting oxidatively degradable foodstuff i.e. harvested fresh fish, in a shipping freighter e.g. ocean shipping freighter, trucking shipping freighter such as tractor-trailer, railway car and airplane of transporting cargo load. Can also be used for a meat, poultry, bakery goods, fruits, grains and vegetables.
   ADVANTAGE - The device allows an oxygen remover to operate throughout duration of the transport and/or storage when oxygen is present to maintain the reduced oxygen environment within a packaging module, thus maintaining freshness of the oxidatively-degradable material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system comprising packaging modules in a container.
   Packaging modules (100)
   Cartons of Fish (102)
   Fuel cell/hydrogen (104)
   Freight container (200)

GA 2019104645

PT P
PN CN109128564-A
TI Welding method for producing automobile portion, involves preparing set of welding fixtures, using each of upper and lower electrodes for production and welding of portion, cleaning product surface to remove oil and rust and fixing welding.
AU QIN Q
   ZHANG W
   CHEN S
AE CHUZHOU YUEZHONG AUTO PARTS CO LTD (CHUZ-Non-standard)
GA 201904907G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining composition of the metal to be welded on an analyzer by a sampling unit, and determining carbon content of the metal and the content of different metal elements. A slow cooling method is carried out after pre-weld welding for the metal with high carbon content. The cooling rate, the hardening tendency of the near seam area and the welding residual stress are reduced, and the plasticity of the welded joint is improved. Multiple weld units of the same type are selected into three equal portions for welding test, and three welding methods of carbon-dioxide gas shielded welding, electric resistance welding and laser welding are tested. The best welding method is determined by a series of tests after observation and welding. A set of welding fixtures are prepared. Each of the upper and lower electrodes is used for the production and welding of portion. Each product is surface cleaned to remove oil and rust. The welding is fixed by a robot.
   USE - Welding method for producing automobile portion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has reliable design and verifies effectively and conveniently, is accurately determined through an experimental method, and the welding precision is improved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: In process (S1), the steel having excellent welding performance comprises the low-carbon steel having carbon content of less than 0.25, a low-alloy steel having alloy element content of 1-3 and carbon content of less than 0.2, and a stainless steel having alloy element content of more than 3 and carbon content of less than 0.18, a general welding steel comprises medium-carbon steel having alloy element content of less than 1 and carbon content of 0.25-0.35, a low-alloy steel having alloy element content of less than 3 and carbon content of 0.3, and a stainless steel having alloy element content of 13-25 and carbon content of less than 0.18, a steel having poor weldability comprises a medium-carbon steel having alloy element content of less than 1 and carbon content of 0.35-0.45, a low-alloy steel having alloy element content of 1-3 and carbon content of 0.3-0.4, and a stainless steel having alloy element content of 13 and carbon content of 0.2, and a steel having poor welding performance comprises a medium and high carbon steel having alloy element content of less than 1 and carbon content of more than 0.45, a low-alloy steel having alloy element content of 1-3 and carbon content of more than 0.4, and a stainless steel having alloy element content of 13 and carbon content of 0.3-0.4.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of chromium-doped cobalt powder involves preparing a cobalt chloride solution having cobalt concentration of 80-140 g/L and ammonium hydrogencarbonate solution having an ammonia concentration of 160-230 g/L, adding chromium chloride hexahydrate to the cobalt chloride solution, uniformly stirring to obtain a doped cobalt chloride solution, separately degreasing the ammonium hydrogencarbonate solution and doped cobalt chloride solution, reacting the both degreased solutions, controlling the pH at 7.0-7.3 to obtain a doped cobalt carbonate, filtering the doped cobalt carbonate, washing with 30-50 degrees C water for 20-30 minutes three times, drying and crushing at 80-120 degrees C and negative pressure to obtain a chromium-doped cobalt carbonate powder, reducing the chromium-doped cobalt carbonate powder at 300-400 degrees C under mixed gas of hydrogen and nitrogen for 5-10 hours, cooling for 4-6 hours, storing in a nitrogen or carbon dioxide atmosphere for 12-24 hours, passivating and crushing.
   USE - Preparation of chromium-doped cobalt powder for producing cemented carbide.
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides chromium-doped cobalt powder with excellent dispersibility and uniform particle diameter, which effectively improves the physical properties such as toughness, corrosiveness and wear resistance of the cemented carbide, thereby improving the performance of the alloy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of chromium in the doped cobalt chloride solution is 0.1-0.5 g/L.

PN CN109092312-A; CN109092312-B
TI Preparing copper-based catalyst by hydrothermal method e.g. useful in synthesis of methanol comprises e.g. adding copper salt, zinc salt and acetylacetone to reaction vessel, reacting under stirring condition, filtering, washing and drying.
AU GAO W
   ZHANG Y
   WANG H
   XUE Y
   NA W
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 201902549L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper-based catalyst by hydrothermal method comprises (1) adding copper salt, zinc salt and acetylacetone to the reaction vessel, and refluxing and reacting at a temperature of 50-80 degrees C for 1-4 hours under stirring condition to obtain first solid precipitate, filtering, washing and drying to obtain acetylacetonate precursor; and (2) placing the acetylacetonate precursor in a hydrothermal reaction kettle, and adding sodium hydroxide solution, carrying out hydrothermal reaction at a pH of 9-11 and at temperature of 110-180 degrees C for 12-24 hours to obtain the second solid precipitation, filtering, washing, and drying to obtain the copper-based catalyst.
   USE - The copper-based catalyst is useful in synthesis of methanol for hydrogenation of carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can utilizes the coordination reaction of acetylacetone and the decomposition of acetylacetonate, and can prepare a copper-based catalyst containing low-cost copper Copper(I) oxide and copper(II) oxide by simple complex precipitation and hydrothermal reaction, without complicated equipment, and has easy operation and is easy to control.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for copper-based catalyst, is prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the mol ratio of copper in the copper salt to zinc in the zinc salt is (0.125-1.25):1. In the step (1), the copper salt is copper nitrate, copper chloride or copper sulfate, and the zinc salt is zinc nitrate, zinc chloride or zinc sulfate.

AE WUHAN LINQUAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 201902953B
AB    NOVELTY - An ionic liquid electrolyte comprises lithium-titanium-aluminum-phosphate composite.
   USE - Ionic liquid electrolyte for use in a lithium-air battery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ionic liquid electrolyte does not volatilize, undergo change in composition, absorb moisture and react with electrode, such that effectively preventing corrosion of lithium metal anode, has better stability, allows lithium-air battery to work at higher temperatures and improves safety of lithium-air battery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An ionic liquid electrolyte of lithium-air battery comprises lithium-titanium-aluminum-phosphate composite (Li (1.3-1.6)Ti (1.4-1.7)Al (0.4-0.6)(PO4) 3). INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:

   (2) a lithium-air battery comprising the ionic liquid electrolyte.

AU FUJII C

The laser beam going to the surface member is scanned two-dimensionally. The detected intensity of the wavelength is displayed as a two-dimensional image on a display (50).

PN US10102728-B1; EP3416145-A1; EP3731199-A1; EP3416145-B1
TI Method for operating smoke detector, involves computing adjusted smoke concentration by adding smoke concentration to product of rate of increase of smoke and time constant, and comparing adjusted smoke concentration to threshold.
AU BAJAJ K K
   KORCHAK A
AE GOOGLE LLC (GOOG-C)
   GOOGLE LLC (GOOG-C)
GA 201879549H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring a voltage signal in response to an electromagnetic signal emitted by an illuminator. A smoke concentration is determined using a voltage signal. A rate of increase of smoke is calculated. An adjusted smoke concentration is computed by adding a smoke concentration to a product of a rate of increase of smoke and a time constant. An adjusted smoke concentration is compared to a threshold. A warning alarm is generated in response to a finding that the adjusted smoke concentration exceeds the threshold. The rate of increase of smoke is calculated in response to a determination that a warning zone criterion is unmet. The rate of increase of smoke is calculated in response to a determination that a heads-up zone criterion is unmet.
   USE - Method for operating a smoke detector.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables facilitating smoke rate of rise and the carbon dioxide rate of increase to be evaluated in the smoke detector in parallel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a smoke detector.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a method for operating a smoke detector.
   Step for computing smoke rate (825)
   Step for determining adjusted smoke concentration (826)
   Step for comparing adjusted smoke concentration (827)
   Step for determining whether smoke concentration is adjusted (828)

PT P
PN CN108654654-A; CN108654654-B
TI Precipitated iron Fischer-Tropsch catalyst comprises iron, copper, potassium, silicon and phosphorus.
AU GUO X
   LV Y
   MEN Z
AE SHENHUA GROUP CORP LTD (SHGR-C)
   NAT INST CLEAN & LOW CARBON ENERGY (CGUO-C)
   CHN ENERGY INVESTMENT GROUP CO LTD (SHGR-C)
GA 2018837221
AB    NOVELTY - Precipitated iron Fischer-Tropsch catalyst comprises 100 pts. wt. iron, 1-5 pts. wt. copper, 0.5-5 pts. wt. potassium, 5-40 pts. wt. silicon and 0.01-15 pts. wt. phosphorus.
   USE - Used as precipitated iron Fischer-Tropsch catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst improves the mechanical strength, improves the abrasion resistance, and improves the carbon utilization rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the precipitated iron Fischer-Tropsch catalyst, comprising (i) co-precipitating a mixture containing an iron source, a copper source and a phosphorus source, and filtering and washing to obtain a filter cake, and (ii) mixing the filter cake with a silicon source and a potassium source into a slurry, and drying and calcining; and
   (2) Fischer-Tropsch synthesis, comprising contacting a synthesis gas with a precipitated iron Fischer-Tropsch catalyst in a reactor to carry out a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction, and synthesizing a feed amount of the syngas relative to 1 g precipitated iron Fischer-Tropsch catalyst 3000-8000 ml/minute, the composition of the syngas is hydrogen: carbon monoxide = (1-3):1, the synthesis reaction temperature is 220-270 degrees C, the synthesis reaction pressure is 0.5-3 MPa, The reactor is a slurry bed reactor or a fixed bed reactor. In the method, the carbon dioxide selectivity is 8-25%, the methane selectivity is 0.5-4%, and the carbon utilization rate is 4-6.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises 0.5-10 pts. wt. phosphorus. The catalyst further comprises 0.01-5 pts. wt. aluminum. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the mixed solution further contains an aluminum source. The method comprises (a) adding the mixed solution containing iron source and copper source for coprecipitation, and obtaining filter cake accessory-I, (b) contacting the filter cake by filtering and washing, silicon source, potassium source, phosphorus source and water to obtain slurry for pulping, drying, and roasting. In step (b), the process of the pulping in adding an aluminum source. The method comprises (i) adding the mixed solution containing iron source and copper source to the coprecipitation, and then filtering and washing the filter cake, (ii) mixing the filter cake with silicon source, potassium source are mixed into slurry, drying to obtain dry product, (iii-a) contacting the dried product with phosphorus after drying II and roasting to obtain the precipitated iron Fischer-Tropsch catalyst or (iii-b) the dried product to II, roasting the roasted product obtained by impregnating phosphorus, obtaining precipitated iron Fischer-Tropsch catalyst III by the drying and roasting III. In step (iii-a) in the process of adding aluminum source, or the immersion in step (iii-b) in the process of adding aluminum source.
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

   CAO L
   PENG L

   MIDEA GROUP CO LTD (MEDE-C)
GA 201879401Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves detecting an indoor                environment by an air conditioner in real time                through a camera according to a number of human                bodies. Rotating speed rate of a fresh air fan is                determined. The fresh air fan is controlled to                operate according to the rotating speed rate. A                table is generated according to the number of the                human bodies. The number of the human bodies is                determined corresponding to speed gear position of                the fresh air fan. Predetermined formula is                calculated. A rotating speed value of the fresh air                fan is determined based on the number of the human                bodies. The human bodies are controlled when a                human body quantity value is greater than a                predetermined number value.
   USE - Method for controlling fresh wind of an air                conditioner (claimed).

AU WU Y
AE CHANGSHA SIMAI FOOD DEV CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201878632Y

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Flaxseed blend oil comprises 100-300 pts. wt. flaxseed, 30-60 pts. wt. walnut, 60-80 pts. wt. Camellia seeds, 30-55 pts. wt. shrimp skin, 50-75 pts. wt. carrot and 20-55 pts. wt. squid. The preparation comprises drying flaxseed, walnut and Camellia seed are dried to 10% moisture content, crushing to 20 mesh, cleaning carrots, slicing, boiling in water for 3-5 minutes, draining surface moisture, crushing to 40-60 mesh to obtain dried carrot powder, removing internal organs in squid, cleaning blood, extracting in boiling water for 3-5 minutes, draining, placing in microwave, crushing to 40-60 mesh to obtain dried squid, cleaning shrimp skin, soaking in water for one day, boiling for 10-20 minutes, draining surface water, drying to 10% moisture content, crushing through 40-60 mesh sieve, placing in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, extracting, sieving residue to 40-60 mesh, heating at 150-210 degrees C under vacuum conditions, sieving to 40-60 mesh, leaching for 15-35 minutes, centrifuging and filtering.

Camellia seeds, 30-55 pts. wt., preferably 45 pts. wt. shrimp skin, 50-75 pts. wt., preferably 60 pts. wt. carrot and 20-55 pts. wt., preferably 40 pts. wt. squid.

PN CN108486049-A; CN108486049-B
TI Increasing the proliferation rate of bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells comprises e.g. superimposing the human bone marrow on the upper layer of an equal volume of lymphocyte separation liquid, centrifuging and inoculating the culture flask.
AU TIAN H
AE NANJING QIANNIANJIAN STEM CELLS GENETIC (NANJ-Non-standard)
   JILIN AOWEIZI BIOMEDICAL ENG CO LTD (JILI-Non-standard)
GA 2018712706
AB    NOVELTY - Increasing the proliferation rate of bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells comprises (i) superimposing the human bone marrow on the upper layer of an equal volume of lymphocyte separation liquid, centrifuging at 4 degrees C under 2000 revolutions/minute for 20 minutes to absorb the cloud-type mononuclear cell layer at the interface of the separation liquid and the plasma, centrifuging at 1000 revolutions/minute after suspension with serum-free IMDM,, inoculating the culture flask at a density of 2x105/cm2, changing the solution after 48 hours, washing away the adhered cells, changing the adherent cells for every 3 days and digesting the cells with trypsin when the cell fusion degree is more than 80% and (ii) inoculating the passage cells at a density of 2x104 /cm2 in a culture flask containing serum-free IMDM, culturing at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide for 48 hours and culturing for 24-72 hours by adding a miR-196a expression inhibitor.
   USE - The method is useful for increasing the proliferation rate of bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method helps to obtain large number of bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells for surgical treatment e.g. stem cell transplantation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The miR-196a expression inhibitor is a palmetto base D. Preferred Method: The passage cells of passages 3-5 are used to induce proliferation. The miR-196a expression inhibitor is added to the IMDM medium to promotes the proliferation of bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells.

TI Apparatus for performing biomass pyrolysis to produce bio-oil, has heater for heating biomass feedstock introduced into fluidized bed reactor such that organic vapors are produced, and inlet for introducing stream of fluid into reactor.
AU YELVINGTON P E
   ZASTROW D J
   SCHWARTZ N R
AE MAINSTREAM ENG CORP (MAIN-Non-standard)
GA 201854116F
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a bubbling fluidized bed reactor (11) including a fluidized bed medium. An inlet introduces biomass feedstock into the reactor and into the fluidized bed medium. A heater i.e. heat exchanger, heats the biomass feedstock introduced into the reactor such that organic vapors and char particles (18) are produced. An outlet is arranged such that the produced vapors and char particles are elutriated as reactor effluent from the fluidized bed medium in the reactor. Another inlet introduces stream of temperature-lowering quench fluid (16) into the reactor downstream of the fluidized bed medium, where the quench fluid is formed as gas such as recycled process gas, steam, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, Hydrogen, synthesis gas, light hydrocarbon, and inert gas.
   USE - Apparatus for performing biomass pyrolysis to produce bio-oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus can maximize liquid yield of bio-oil in fast pyrolysis of biomass or carbonaceous material and quench product vapors to avoid secondary cracking reactions. The apparatus can recycle process gas to the bubbling fluidized bed reactor as the quench fluid.

A filter (101), a liquid pre-heater (102), a gas pre-heater (103), and a heater (104) are sequentially arranged on the pipeline between the sewage collection unit and the reactor.
   USE - Municipal sewage treatment system.

TI Fabrication of nanoporous metal structure used as            catalyst in fuel cell, involves providing piece of            alloy comprising metal(s) comprising gold, palladium or            platinum and another metal, and dealloying alloy piece            by removing another metal.
AU XU Y
   YIU P M
   SHEK C H
AE UNIV HONG KONG CITY (UYOO-C)
GA 2018412067
AB    NOVELTY - Fabrication of a nanoporous metal structure                involves providing a piece of alloy (A1) comprising                metal(s) (M1) and metal(s) (M2), and dealloying the                alloy (A1) piece to form a nanoporous metal                substrate by chemically removing at least some of                the metal (M2) from the alloy (A1) piece. The metal                (M1) comprises gold, palladium or platinum.
   USE - Fabrication of nanoporous metal structure e.g.                nanoporous metal supported palladium catalyst used                in fuel cell e.g. direct methanol fuel cell (all                claimed), direct ethanol fuel cell and proton                exchange membrane fuel cell, used as energy                conversion device to convert chemical energy into                electricity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently provides nanoporous                metal structure with excellent porosity and                catalytic activity, and is economical since it uses                non-platinum electrocatalyst such as palladium                having excellent stability and catalytic                performance, with gold or less expensive                three-dimensional transition metals such as iron,                cobalt, nickel and copper. The use of gold as                catalyst helps to eliminate the poisoning                intermediates formed on the surface, which can                oxidize carbon monoxide-like intermediate species                to release active sites by producing carbon                dioxide, and increase active sites.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining                the method for fabricating nanoporous metal                structure and nanoporous metal supported palladium                catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The alloy (A1) is                  dealloyed by using iron(III) chloride solution.                  The method uses sulfuric acid as an electrolyte                  to dissolve at least some of cobalt from the                  alloy (A2) layer.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The alloy (A1) piece                  preferably comprises gold-copper-silicon                  (Au55Cu25Si20) or palladium-copper-germanium                  (Pd40Cu20Ge40). The metal (M2) comprises copper.                  The method deposits a layer of alloy (A2)                  comprising palladium and metal (M3) comprising                  cobalt, on the metal substrate by using palladium                  cobalt (Pd0.5Co0.5) (atomic%) as target in argon                  atmosphere. Preferred Properties: The alloy (A1)                  piece is in the form of ribbon having thickness                  of 50 mu m. The alloy (A2) layer has a thickness                  of 10-100 nm.

PN CN207323875-U
TI Fire head mouth and nose mask, has cover body whose middle part is formed with containing cavity and body cavity, and ventilation chamber connected to end of expiratory pipe, where rear part of cover body is formed with ventilation cavity.
AU XU J
AE GUANGZHOU KAIAN FIRE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201837757F
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a fire fighting equipment, especially a special fire head for mouth and nose mask, comprising a cover body, nasal cavity, containing cavity, expiratory tube and a filter pipe, middle of the cover body provided with a containing cavity for containing the human mouth and nose. the cover cavity gradually becomes thinner to the peripheral edge, in the rear part of the cover body form air cavity, the containing cavity lower end connected ventilation chamber, said air chamber rear end provided with a filter pipe; said ventilation cavity lower end is equipped with a respiration pipe; gradually become thinner to the peripheral edge of the cover body from the nasal cavity. its function is to make the centre of the cover body can have enough strength to keep the shape, fully human mouth and nose, the periphery is provided with strong deformation ability to adapt to the change of various human face model using the expiring pipe and filter pipe respectively connected with exhalation valve and canister, the oxidase in the canister can be carbon monoxide is oxidized into carbon dioxide, which greatly reduces the probability of carbon monoxide poisoning when fire escape.
DC P35 (Life-saving, fire-fighting (A62).)
MC P35-A03E1; P35-A03E5

PN FR3058210-A1; WO2018078254-A1; EP3532790-A1
TI Heat exchanger e.g. gas cooler, for use on low pressure line, of air-conditioning system of car, has heat exchange channel beams in which coolant is circulated and made of anisotropy material, where beams are separated from each other.
AU DE PELSEMAEKER G
   RAGONDET D
   BELLENFANT A
   RENAULT V
   SPORNA D
AE VALEO SYSTEMES THERMIQUES (VALO-C)
   VALEO SYSTEMES THERMIQUES (VALO-C)
GA 2018339451
AB    NOVELTY - The exchanger has heat exchange channel beams (5) in which a coolant i.e. carbon-dioxide is circulated and made of an anisotropy material. The heat exchange channel beams are separated from each other by a distance and a median width for representing bursting pressure of the heat exchange channel beams, where the bursting pressure is selected higher than 300 Bars. The coolant is cooled in a supercritical state. The heat exchange channel beams are provided with an oval cross section and a body that comprises a plane face.
   USE - Heat exchanger e.g. evaporator, gas cooler, electric battery cooler and water cooler, for use on a low pressure line and a high pressure line (all claimed) of an air-conditioning system of a car.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat exchange channel beams in which the coolant is circulated and made of the anisotropy material, where the heat exchange channel beams are separated from each other by the distance and the median width for representing bursting pressure of the heat exchange channel beams, thus achieving better mechanical resistance of the exchanger.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a heat exchanger.
   Heat exchange elements (4)
   Heat exchange channel beams (5)
   Flat face (8)
   Edge (9)

PT P
PN US2018118631-A1; US10301228-B2
TI Multiple porous carbonaceous particle useful for            enhancing growth of crops e.g. tomato, lettuce and            celery, and soil health, and in remediation of ground            water, storm water runoff and soil, has specific            hydrophobicity index.
AU BONTCHEV R P
   KIM H S
   WILSON R W
   BELCHER R W
   CHEYNE C
   MANZER L E
   JARAND M L
   WAN H
   MALYALA R
AE COOL PLANET ENERGY SYSTEMS INC (COOL-Non-standard)
GA 2018339069
AB    NOVELTY - Multiple porous carbonaceous particles have                hydrophobicity index of 5 or less when tested by                molarity of ethanol drop (MED) method, and exhibits                weight loss of 1% or more when performing the                Bontchev-Cheyne test at 43-60 degrees C, where the                particles in the batch meet EU feed limit for                dioxins less than or equal to 0.75 ng/kg                WHO-polychlorinated dibenzo-p-dioxins/furans-toxic                equivalents/kg (WHO-PCDD/F-TEQ/kg).
   USE - The multiple porous carbonaceous particles are                useful for producing biochar, which is used for                removing carbon dioxide from atmosphere, enhancing                growth of crops e.g. tomato, lettuce and celery,                and soil health. The carbonaceous particles are                also is used in remediation of ground water, storm                water runoff, soil to remove toxic substances e.g.                heavy metals, mine and mercury, and as cattle or                poultry feed additive.
   ADVANTAGE - The non-toxic carbonaceous particle is                produced in a simple, rapid, labor-intensive and                cost-effective manner, has improved water retention                capability and nutrients holding ability for long                period of time, and is suitable for large scale                production. The multiple porous carbonaceous                particles minimize root damage and soil compaction,                and improves plant growth with high yield and                quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Component: The additive is a soil                  enhancing agent chosen from water, water                  solutions of salts, inorganic and organic liquids                  of different polarities, liquid organic compounds                  or combinations of organic compounds and                  solvents, mineral and organic oils, slurries and                  suspensions, supercritical liquids, fertilizers,                  plant growth promoting rhizobacteria, free-living                  and nodule-forming nitrogen fixing bacteria,                  organic decomposers, nitrifying bacteria,                  phosphate solubilizing bacteria, biocontrol                  agents, bioremediation agents, saprotrophic                  fungi, ectomycorrhizae, and                  endomycorrhizae.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Product: The particles comprise at                  least 55 wt.% carbon, and 5 wt.% or less volatile                  organic compounds. The ratio of macropore volume                  and total pore volume in the particles is greater                  than or equal to 50%. The particles have a bulk                  density of 0.1-0.6 g/cm3, a cation exchange                  capacity of greater than or equal to 5                  milliequ/l, an anion exchange capacity of greater                  than or equal to 5 milliequ/l, and a water                  holding capacity of 30 wt.% or greater, where at                  least 75% (measured by weight or volume)                  particles in the batch having a particle size of                  less than or equal to 10 mm. The particles have                  at least 70 vol.% pores having 50000 nm or less,                  which is measured using mercury porosimetry                  testing. Preferred Method: The particles are                  treated by rapidly infusing the additive into                  pores of the particles using vacuum infusion                  treatment, and/or surfactant infusion treatment.                  The particle is treated by infusing an infiltrate                  into at least 10 vol.% pores of the                  particles.
   ACTIVITY - Plant growth stimulant. Test details are                described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)
MC C04-B01C3; C04-D02; C04-F09; C04-F10A; C05-C06; C14-E11; C14-M01D; C14-T; C14-U01; D05-H08A; H09-F03

TI Electrolytic-degreasing method for removing oil component adhered to surface of rolled-steel belt, involves removing contaminated material which adhered to surface of rolled-steel belt with shock wave accompanying destruction.
AU OGAWA K
AE FUDAUCHI KOGYO CO LTD (FUDA-Non-standard)
   FUDAUCHI KOGYO KK (FUDA-Non-standard)
   FUDAUCHI KOGYO CO LTD (FUDA-Non-standard)
GA 201821630A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves carrying out cathode polarization of a rolled-steel belt (20) by arranging and electrifying the rolled-steel belt and an anode electrode (11) in alkaline electrolyzed water. A hydrogen bubble is formed in a surface of the rolled-steel belt, and destroyed in a surface vicinity of rolled-steel belt. The contaminated material which adhered to the surface of the rolled-steel belt with the shock wave accompanying the destruction is removed. The anode electrode is comprised of an electrode catalyst layer which contains iridium oxide.
   USE - Electrolytic-degreasing method for removing contaminated materials such as oil component adhered to surface of rolled-steel belt using alkaline electrolyzed water.
   ADVANTAGE - The contaminant adhering to the surface is removed effectively. The reattachment to the belt surface of the removed oil component is not occurred, since the oil component removed from the surface of the belt can be disassembled into carbon and carbon dioxide in alkaline electrolyzed water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an electrolytic-degreasing apparatus for removing contaminated materials such as oil component adhered to surface of rolled-steel belt using alkaline electrolyzed water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the electrolytic-degreasing apparatus.
   Electrolytic-degreasing apparatus (1)
   Degreasing tank (10)
   Anode electrode (11)
