PN CN107722526-A
TI Degradable plastic comprises degradable resin, glycerol, plant fiber starch, polylactic acid, compound coupling agent, glycerin, fluorescent whitening agent, ultraviolet absorbent, and filling agent, which has short degradation period.
AU MA Y
AE GUANGXI YINYING BIOMASS ENERGY TECHNOLOG (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018171533
AB    NOVELTY - Degradable plastic comprises 40-60 pts. wt. degradable resin, 8-20 pts. wt. glycerol, 7-13 pts. wt. plant fiber starch, 5-9 pts. wt. polylactic acid, 5-8 pts. wt. compound coupling agent, 5-9 pts. wt. glycerin, 2-5 pts. wt. fluorescent whitening agent, 5-8 pts. wt. ultraviolet absorbent, and 3-7 pts. wt. filling agent.
   USE - Degradable plastic.
   ADVANTAGE - The degradable plastic has low cost raw material, simple production process, easy realization of large-scale industrial production, greatly reduces the production cost, has a short degradation period, is completely degradable into carbon dioxide and water after being discarded, no longer produces white pollution, purifies the environment, and ensures food hygiene and people's health.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a degradable plastic, which involves:
   (A) taking the raw material, where the degradable resin, glycerol and plant fiber starch are fed into a kneader and kneaded for 20-30 minutes to obtain a kneaded material;
   (B) transferring the kneaded material to the mixer, adding polylactic acid, compound coupling agent, glycerin, fluorescent whitening agent, ultraviolet absorbent and filling agent sequentially, and mixing uniformly to obatin a mixture; and
   (C) feeding the mixture to a single-screw extruder for granulation and controlling the temperature to 140-170 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The degradable plastic comprises 50 pts. wt. degradable resin, 15 pts. wt. glycerol, 10 pts. wt. plant fiber starch, 7 pts. wt. polylactic acid, 6 pts. wt. compound coupling agent, 7 pts. wt. glycerin, 3 pts. wt. fluorescent whitening agent, 7 pts. wt. ultraviolet absorbent, and 4 pts. wt. filling agent. The degradable resin comprises 32-40 pts. wt., preferably 36 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol, 20-25 pts. wt., preferably 22 pts. wt. n-butyraldehyde, 20-30 pts. wt., preferably 25 pts. wt. hydrochloric acid, and 1-2 pts. wt., preferably 1.5 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate.

TI System for allowing gas to flow into liquid e.g. water in container, has container having bottom with gas inlet and locking mechanism ensures flow of gas into liquid only when container is properly covered by lid respectively.
AU ASIS L
AE ASIS L (ASIS-Individual)
   ASIS L (ASIS-Individual)
   ASIS L (ASIS-Individual)
   ASIS L (ASIS-Individual)
GA 201813777Y
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a container having a bottom with a gas inlet. A lid covers the container. The gas providing mechanism allows flowing gas through gas inlet into the liquid when contained in the container. The locking mechanism ensures flow of gas into the liquid only when the container is properly covered by the lid. The gas is carbon dioxide. The container and lid has a screw thread allowing proper covering of the container by screwing the lid over an opening of the container. The lid comprises an orifice allows flow of excess gas from the container.
   USE - System for allowing gas to flow into liquid e.g. water in container and bottle supplied with the soda makers.
   ADVANTAGE - The second plug releases excess gas from the container to allow removal of the lid after gas is allowed to flow into the liquid contained in the container. The second plug prevents exit of foam from the container during the release of excess gas pressure. Thus leakage of liquid from the container is prevented.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system for allowing gas to flow into liquid.

GA 201813408Q

The piston is fixed or moved during the grinding process.
   USE - Method for machining a piston of a combustion engine.

PN WO2022225979-A1
TI Porous sheet product used in separator of storage            batteries, is obtained by providing extrusion mixture            comprising polymeric binder and superabsorbent polymer,            milling superabsorbent polymer, and melt-extruding            extrusion mixture to form sheet material in film            form.
AU YEN W W
AE YEN W W (YENW-Individual)
GA 2022D30087
AB    NOVELTY - A porous sheet product is obtained by (i)                providing an extrusion mixture comprising a                polymeric binder and a superabsorbent polymer which                is in particle form and having a particle size,                (ii) milling the superabsorbent polymer such that                the particle size of the polymer before and after                milling is reduced by a ratio of at least 5:1,                (iii) melt-extruding the extrusion mixture to form                a sheet material in film form, and (iv) cooling the                sheet material.
   USE - Porous sheet product. Uses include but are not                limited to separator for storage batteries, reverse                osmosis/diffusion barrier in ultrafiltration of                colloidal substance, disposable garments,                geo-membrane, breathable non-woven protective                scrim, diaper, gloves, wound dressing artificial                skin and transport from perspiration barrier for                pathogens and liquid chemicals, packaging materials                for bread, cheese, meat, sausage, chicken and fish                shrink bag, beef and pork display tray liner,                etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The porous sheet product provides battery                having high energy density and specific energy, and                has excellent electrolytic conduction and                dimensional stability. The product provides                moisture and/or flavor transfer to foods and/or to                promote adhesion, and helps to achieve equilibrium                of moisture, oxygen, and carbon dioxide levels to                prolong product shelf life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                production of the porous sheet product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The extrusion mixture                  further comprises an inorganic oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The thermoplastic                  polymer constitutes 1-80 vol.% extrusion mixture.                  The superabsorbent polymer, before swelling or                  milling is added to the extrusion mixture and                  constitutes 0.1-40 vol.% extrusion mixture.                  Preferred Process: The swelling step further                  involves exposing the superabsorbent polymer to a                  swelling agent, where the superabsorbent polymer                  absorbs at least a portion of the swelling agent                  containing at least one of water and 1-3C alcohol                  or diol, and exposing the superabsorbent polymer                  to 0.1-60 vol.% infused agent, where the                  superabsorbent polymer absorbs at least a portion                  of the infused agent which is at least one                  selected from humectants, antistatic additives,                  ionic conductivity additives, color additives,                  flavorings, medications, and indicators. The                  porous sheet product is obtained by providing a                  liquid coating mixture which is a homogeneous                  one-phase solution comprising superabsorbent                  polymer, polymer binder and swelling agent, and                  the liquid coating mixture comprises a                  combination of a swelled superabsorbent polymer                  and non-swelled superabsorbent polymer, coating                  the liquid coating mixture to a porous substrate,                  and removing at least a portion of the swelling                  agent from the coated porous substrate, and                  causing the particle size of the swelled                  superabsorbent polymer to shrink and creating a                  layer of porosity. Preferred Components: The                  milling step is performed while the                  superabsorbent polymer is present in the                  extrusion mixture. The polymeric binder comprises                  a thermoplastic polymer which forms an open-                  celled matrix in the porous sheet product. The                  thermoplastic polymer comprises at least one                  compound selected from polyolefins,                  fluoropolymers, polyamides, polyethylene                  terephthalate, polyacrylics, polyvinyl acetate,                  polyvinyl alcohol, cellulosics, hydroxypropyl                  cellulose, polyacrylonitrile, polystyrene,                  polyurethane, polycarbonate, polysulfone and                  polyimide, or the thermoplastic polymer comprises                  at least one compound selected from elastomers of                  polyamide, polyolefin, polyether,                  polyisobutylene, polybutadiene, polystyrene,                  atactic polypropylene, polyurethane,                  ethylene-propylene rubber, ethylene-vinyl acetate                  copolymer, polyvinyl chloride, oxidized                  polyethylene, coumarone-indene resins, and                  terpene resins. The superabsorbent polymer                  comprises at least one compound selected from                  cross-linked polyacrylates, acrylic acid                  polymers, methacrylates, polyacrylamides,                  carboxymethyl celluloses, polyvinyl alcohol                  copolymers, polyethylene oxides, cellulose,                  starch-grafted copolyacrylates or                  polyacrylamides, ethylene maleic anhydride                  copolymers, and their copolymers. The extrusion                  mixture further comprises 1-80 vol.%                  compatibilizing agent which promotes uniform                  mixing of the polymeric binder and superabsorbent                  polymer at extrusion temperature. The                  superabsorbent polymer is a water-absorbent                  superabsorbent polymer, and the polymer binder is                  a water-soluble polymer binder or oil-phase                  polymer binder. The liquid coating mixture                  comprises an oil phase comprising cyclic olefin                  and polyisobutylene in mineral spirits and a                  water phase comprising water-swelled                  superabsorbent polymer, and a single-phase                  homogeneous water solution comprising water as                  the swelling agent and polyvinyl alcohol as the                  polymer binder. Preferred Properties: The                  superabsorbent polymer has an initial, un-milled                  and a dry size of 20-5 mm. The porous sheet                  product has a thickness of 0.1-80 mils, and a                  pore size of at least 0.01 &#956; m. The                  ratio of unbound swelling agent to bound swelling                  agent is 0.3:1-1.5:1. The liquid coating mixture                  has a surface tension of 30 mN/m or less, and a                  viscosity of 100-5000 cps. The ratio of the pores                  of porous sheet product to the superabsorbent                  polymer is at least 5:1.

TI Method for producing multilayer ceramic electronic            component e.g. laminated ceramic capacitor, involves            colliding dry ice fine particles with average particle            size on cut surface where internal electrode layer is            exposed, and removing foreign matter on cut            surface.
AU SATO H
AE KYOCERA CORP (KYOC-C)
GA 2022C4410E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves cutting a mother laminate                in which multiple dielectric ceramics (4) and                internal electrode layers (5) are alternately                laminated. Dry ice fine particles (19) are collided                with an average particle size of 200 &#956; m                or less on the cut surface (35) where the internal                electrode layer is exposed. The foreign matter (22)                on the cut surface is removed.
   USE - Method for producing multilayer ceramic                electronic component e.g. laminated ceramic                capacitor.
   ADVANTAGE - The dry ice fine particles used for dry ice                cleaning and polishing are finally released into                the air as carbon-dioxide gas, so that a drying                process is not required and waste water or waste                liquid treatment is not required. Since dry ice                particles is softer than ordinary abrasives, thus                effective in cleaning and polishing with less                damage to relatively soft surfaces such as internal                electrode exposed surfaces composed of ceramic                powder or metal particles and a resin binder. Since                fine particles are used, thus fine cleaning and                polishing is performed.

   5Internal electrode layer

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The paint composition                  further comprises 1-5 pts. wt. vinyl acetate.                  Preferred Method: The functional additive is                  prepared by (a) mixing 35-70 pts. wt. alumina                  sulfate, 3-15 pts. wt. acetic acid, and 25-55                  pts. wt. purified water to prepare a first slurry                  solution, (b) preparing a second slurry solution                  by mixing 65-80 pts. wt. first slurry solution                  and 20-35 pts. wt. activated clay and stirring at                  2500-4500 rotation/minute for 1-10 minutes, and                  (c) mixing 1-5 pts. wt. functional enhancer                  comprising surfactin, 70-80 pts. wt. the second                  slurry solution, 10-15 pts. wt. magnesium                  silicate, 5-10 pts. wt. saponin and 3-5 pts. wt.                  grape seed extract. The functional enhancer is                  produced by (i) preparing a first mixture by                  mixing 10-40 pts. wt. surfactin, 25-50 pts. wt.                  silica powder, and 30-70 pts. wt. purified water,                  (ii) preparing a second mixture by mixing 70-80                  pts. wt. the first mixture, 5-15 pts. wt. sodium                  trimeta phosphate, and 10-15 pts. wt. cordierite,                  and (iii) mixing 80-90 pts. wt. the second                  mixture, 3-5 pts. wt. chloroplatinic acid, and                  1-5 pts. wt. dibutyl hydroxytoluene. Preferred                  Components: The aqueous-based finishing paint                  comprises 5-25 pts. wt. binder containing acrylic                  emulsion resin, 20-60 pts. wt. filler including                  limestone, titanium dioxide, and/or talc, 20-40                  pts. wt. solvent containing purified water, and                  0.1-5 pts. wt. additive including                  ethanediol.
   EXAMPLE - 16 g Calcium chloride, 16 g sodium chloride,                16 g magnesium chloride, 16 g potassium acetate, 16                g urea, and 20 g the functional additive (prepared                by mixing 50 g alumina sulfate and 50 g activated                clay) were mixed to prepare cryoprotectant.

   USE - The composition is useful for making a solid dosage form for loading a vaccine (claimed) for corona virus disease 2019 (COVID-19).
   ADVANTAGE - The vaccine compositions have improved stability and ease of administration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a capsule comprising the composition;
   (2) a solid dosage form for delivery of a vaccine composition by oral, sublingual, or buccal administration of the vaccine composition, the solid dosage form comprising an aragonite composition comprising aragonite particles, where the aragonite particles are impregnated with carbon dioxide (CO2); and a biocompatible polymer and a disintegrating agent mixed with the aragonite composition; where the solid dosage form further comprises the vaccine composition, and where the solid dosage form is a powder, a tablet, or a capsule; and
   (3) making a solid dosage form for loading a vaccine, which involves providing an aragonite composition comprising aragonite particles impregnated with CO2; mixing the aragonite composition with a biocompatible polymer and a disintegrating agent to form the solid dosage form; and adding the vaccine composition to the solid dosage form.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The adenovirus vector is derived from a type 5 adenovirus, and where the adenovirus has deletions in the E1, E2b, and E3 regions. The heterologous protein comes from a virus. It comes from a virus selected from SARS-CoV-2 (preferred), MERS-CoV, SARS-CoV, HCoV- NL63, HCoV-229E, HCoV-OC43, HKU1, and influenza virus. The heterologous protein is a spike (S) protein, a nucleocapsid (N) protein, or a membrane (M) protein. It is 80-100% identical to SEQ ID NO: 1-4. The filler comprises or lacks one or more compounds selected from lactose, sucrose, magnesium stearate, glucose, mannitol, sorbitol, starch, dextrose, maltodextrin, maltitol, and plant cellulose. The composition comprises one or more compounds selected from sodium chloride, potassium chloride, sodium citrate, sodium phosphate, sucrose, dimethylglycine, glycine, methyl sulfonylmethane, and yeast lysate. The capsule is enteric coated. It comprises alginate. The solid dosage form further comprises at least one excipient and is formulated to form a lozenge. The powder, the tablet, and the capsule disintegrate in an aqueous solution in less than or equal to 30 s. The vaccine composition comprises a recombinant viral expression construct encoding a viral protein or fragment. The viral protein or fragment corresponds to a coronavirus protein or fragment. Coronavirus protein or fragment is SARS-CoV2 virus binding protein, where the SARS-CoV2 viral binding protein comprises a recombinant ACE2 protein. The recombinant ACE2 protein has greater than or equal to 85% sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 5. It comprises the sequence of SEQ ID NO: 6. Sequences not defined here may be defined at ftp://ftp.wipo.int/pub/published_pct_sequences/publication. The recombinant ACE2 protein comprises at least one mutation selected from Thr27Phe, Thr27Trp, Thr27Tyr, Asp30Glu, His34Glu, His34Phe, His34Lys, His34Met, His34Trp, His34Tyr, Asp38Glu, Asp38Met, Asp38Trp, Gln24Leu, Asp30Leu, His34Ala, and Asp355Leu. The vaccine composition comprises an adenovirus expression construct. The solid dosage form is a powder, a tablet, or a capsule. Preferred Composition: The adenovirus vector has 0.5-5% or less than or equal to 5% or less than or equal to 3% residual moisture content. Preferred Parameter: The aragonite particles have an average particle size of between 100 nm to 1 mm. Preferred Method: Mixing the aragonite composition with the biocompatible polymer and a disintegrating agent involves hot melt extrusion. The solid dosage form is a tablet and where the method further involves compacting the solid dosage form.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbonite mineral has an orthorhombic lattice. It is selected from aragonite (preferred), cerrusite, strontianite, witherite, and rutherfordine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The biocompatible polymer is selected from polylactic acid (PLA) (preferred), polyethylene, polystyrene, polyvinylchloride, polyamide 66 (nylon), polycaprolactame, polycaprolactone, acrylic polymers, acrylonitrile butadiene styrene, polybenzimidazole, polycarbonate, polyphenylene oxide/sulfide, polypopylene, teflon, polylactic acid, aliphatic polyester such as polyhydroxybutyrate, poly-3-hydroxybutyrate (P3HB), polyhydroxy valerate, polyhydroxybutyratepolyhydroxyvalerate copolymer, poly(3 -hydroxybutyrate-co-3-hydroxy valerate), polyglyconate, poly(dioxanone) and mixtures. The disintegrating agent is selected from a starch, modified cellulose gums, insoluble crosslinked polyvinylpyrrolidones, starch glycolates, micro crystalline cellulose, pregelatinized starch, sodium carboxymethyl starch, low substituted hydroxypropyl cellulose, homopolymers of N-vinyl-2-pyrrolidone, alkyl-, hydroxyalkyl-, carboxyalkyl-cellulose esters, alginates, and microcrystalline cellulose and its polymorphic forms. It is pea starch. Preferred Composition: The aragonite composition and the biocompatible polymer are in a weight ratio of 95:5 to 5 to 95.
   ACTIVITY - Immunostimulant; Virucide. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Vaccine.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration can be through intravenous, oral, parenteral, intra-arterial, cutaneous, subcutaneous, intramuscular, topical, intracranial, intraorbital, ophthalmic, intravitreal, intraventricular, intracapsular, intraspinal, intraci sternal, intraperitoneal, intranasal, aerosol, central nervous system (CNS) administration, and administration by suppository (for vaginal or rectal delivery).    USE - The method is useful for directly using high alkali red mud for preparing cement clinker and in solid waste material recycling treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to directly use high alkali red mud as raw material for preparing cement raw material, thus reduces the technical process of using red mud and production cost of cement; adopts addition of calcium sulfate which solves the problem that minerals cannot be formed or the amount of formation is low due to excessive alkali content in red mud, and eliminates the adverse effects of alkali; and the cement clinker calcining temperature is low, which reduces the production energy consumption and carbon dioxide emission, simultaneously solves the application problem of red mud due to high alkali content; thus the method is suitable for large-scale application of red mud.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for method for synthesizing iron aluminate cement clinker by using high alkali red mud.

TI Preparing nano calcium carbonate useful for anti-flow hanging poly vinyl chloride plasticizing paste comprises e.g. introducing lime milk, adding crystal control agent to perform carbonation reaction, pre-treating suspension with phosphoric acid compound and performing hydraulic filtering.
AU HUANG X
   RONG Q
   ZHU Y
   MO X
AE GUANGXI HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202223065L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano calcium carbonate, comprises (i) introducing lime milk with a specific gravity of 1.050-1.090 into a bubbling carbonization tower, adding crystal control agent and carbon dioxide to perform carbonation reaction under high speed stirring, stopping reaction when pH of carbonization reaction system is not greater than 7 and obtaining nano-calcium carbonate suspension; (ii) pre-treating the suspension with phosphoric acid compound by adding amount of the phosphoric acid compound which is 0.4-0.9 % of mass of suspension having concentration 4-9 % and rate of 6-12 ml/minutes; (iii) pre-processing suspension using fatty acid saponification liquid, polyhexamethylene glycol, fatty acid methyl ester sodium sulfonate, alkyl phenol polyoxyethylene ether phosphate monoester compound to prepare surfactant for surface modification to obtain modified nanocalcium carbonate suspension; and (iv) performing modified nanocalcium carbonate suspension hydraulic filtering, drying and sieving.
   USE - The calcium carbonate is useful for anti-flow hanging poly vinyl chloride plasticizing paste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can make the nano calcium carbonate filling PVC speed-increasing paste with good flow hanging performance, fluidity, viscoelasticity, dispersibility, impact resistance and stability; and ensures automobile chassis filler.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crystal form control agent is composed of sucrose and sodium polyacrylate, the adding amount is 1-2 % of the mass of the calcium carbonate dry basis in the suspension. The phosphoric acid compound is phosphoric acid, sodium hexametaphosphate, octadecyl carbon alcohol phosphate, polyphosphoric acid, metaphosphoric acid and/or sodium tripolyphosphate. The phosphoric acid compound is composed of sodium hexametahposphate and octadecanol phosphate with mass ratio of 2: 1. The adding amount of the surfactant agent is 4-5 % of the mass of the calcium carbonate dry basis in the suspension. The surfactant on a dry basis of calcium carbonate comprises 2-3 wt.% fatty acid saponification solution, 0.5-1 wt.% polyethylene glycol, 0.5-1 wt.% sodium fatty acid methyl ester sulfonate and 0.5-1 wt.% alkylphenol polyoxyethylene ether phosphoric acid monoester.Preferred Method: The carbonation reaction is the temperature is 25-35 degrees Celsius, the concentration of carbon dioxide is 30 -40 % of the kiln gas, the gas flow rate is 2-6 m3/hours under the condition of reaction. The surface modification is processed for 40-50 minutes at the stirring speed of 2000- 3000 revolutions/minute and the temperature of 70-90 degrees Celsius. The drying is drying for 6-8 hours in the oven of 80-80 degrees Celsius.

PN CN113816371-A
TI Preparation method of expandable graphite,            involves adding oxidant into natural crystalline flake            graphite, washing with deionized water, cleaning            oxidant, drying, adding ammonium hydrogen carbonate,            and weighing prepared graphite oxide.
AU WANG W
   LIU H
   LI Y
   CHEN T
   LI X
   WEN G
   REN D
AE TRIUMPH GRAPHITE CARBON MATERIAL CO LTD (TRIU-Non-standard)
GA 202205509T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves adding oxidant to natural                flake graphite, and oxidizing, fully washing with                deionized water for 3-5 times, washing the oxidant,                and drying at 35-65degrees Celsius for 2.5-3.5 h.                The ammonium bicarbonate is added to deionized                water at a constant temperature, and continuously                stirred until insolubility occurs, and configured a                supersaturated ammonium bicarbonate solution. The                graphite oxide is weighed, put into supersaturated                ammonium bicarbonate solution. The volume ratio of                graphite oxide to ammonium bicarbonate solution is                controlled to be 1:2-5, put into an airtight                container filled with carbon dioxide, mix well, and                sonicate for 5-60 min in ice-water bath. The dried                material is placed into the sieve, shaked gently to                separate the graphite oxide and the unintercalated                ammonium bicarbonate, and sealed it at 18~10degrees                Celsius at a low temperature immediately after the                separation is completed. The intercalated graphite                is expanded at a temperature of 300-900degrees                Celsius. to obtain expanded graphite.
   USE - Method useful for preparing expandable                graphite, which is useful in energy conservation                and environmental protection, new energy materials,                new generation of information technology, high-end                equipment manufacturing, biological medical                treatment, aerospace and other strategic emerging                industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is mild, safe, non-toxic and                pollution-free, and meets the high-end field use                requirement.

PN DE102020003426-A1; WO2021245134-A1
TI Additive manufacturing of workpiece e.g. tool steels under inert gas, comprises assembling workpiece from sequence of workpiece contours, feeding composition to workpiece contour and subjecting to heat treatment.
AU KAMPFFMEYER D
   HILDEBRANDT B
AE MESSER GROUP GMBH (MESG-C)
GA 2021D99759
AB    NOVELTY - Additive manufacturing of a workpiece under inert gas, comprises assembling a workpiece (3) from a sequence of workpiece contours (4), each of which is produced by melting a metallic material in powder or wire form by applying an energy beam (8), manufacturing the workpiece contours under the action of a protective gas with a composition that varies during the manufacture of the workpiece, utilizing a gas mixture including an inert gas and carbon dioxide as a protective gas in the production of each workpiece contour to generate zones with different metallurgical properties within the workpiece, feeding the composition to the workpiece contour and subjecting to heat treatment during and/or after production process.
   USE - The method is useful for additive manufacturing of workpieces e.g. tool steels and high-speed steels.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces workpiece with different zones of different metallurgical properties that is simple and quick to carry out in terms of the outlay on equipment and the use of work and time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for additive manufacturing of workpieces under inert gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device for additive manufacturing of workpieces under inert gas.
   Workpiece (3)
   Workpiece contour (4)
   Energy beam (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metallic material is ferritic or martensitic steel. The protective gas includes more than 1 vol.%, preferably 2-25 vol.% carbon dioxide. The inert gas includes argon, helium and/or nitrogen. Preferred Method: The additive manufacturing process is laser powder bed fusion process, laser metal deposition process or wire arc additive manufacturing process.

TI Reversible solid oxide battery system comprises solid oxide cell unit for selectively performing electrochemical power generation or water electrolysis, where fuel supply unit is provided for supplying fuel for driving fuel cell mode.
AU GYU K
   CHOI W J
   SONG H H
AE GWANGJU INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (GWAN-C)
   UNIV SEOUL NAT R & DB FOUND (USEO-C)
GA 2021E0368W
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has a solid oxide cell (SOC) unit (10) for selectively performing electrochemical power generation or water electrolysis process. A fuel supply unit (20) supplies fuel to drive a fuel cell mode to the SOC unit. An oxidizing unit oxidizes exhaust gas discharged from a SOC unit and discharges the oxidized exhaust gas as high-temperature gas. A thermal battery unit receives the discharged exhaust gas from an oxidation unit (30) and stores heat using a heat storage material. A water electrolyzed water storage unit (50) is utilized for storing water for supplying water vapour required for driving a water-electrolysis mode.
   USE - Reversible solid oxide battery system.
   ADVANTAGE - The efficiency of driving in fuel cell mode or water electrolysis mode is maximized. The environmental pollution is prevented by minimizing or preventing exhaust gas by converting the gas generated in fuel tank mode into electrolyzed water and carbon dioxide and separately storing the gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a functional block diagram of a thermal battery-linked reversible solid oxide battery system.(Drawing includes non-English language texts)

   SOC unit (10)
   Fuel supply unit (20)
   Oxidation unit (30)
   Water electrolyzed water storage unit (50)

AE PNEUTEC BV (PNEU-Non-standard)
GA 2021D5263U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves feeding raw material to an extruder and liquefying and compressing and possibly degassing in the extruder. The raw material and/or the hose (17) are rinsed with fluid before and/or after the extrusion. The raw material is rinsed with the fluid before being fed into the extruder (8). The hose is flushed from the inside and/or outside with the fluid when it emerges from the die (11). The hose is additionally rinsed with the fluid from the inside and/or outside in a cooling bath (15). The raw material and/or the hose are flushed with a gaseous fluid. The fluid is an extremely dry or moist gas. The fluid is air, nitrogen, carbon dioxide, water vapor, reaction gasses such as ozone, a noble gas, or a mixture of the aforementioned gases. The fluid is a liquid, in particular, purified and/or distilled water or another liquid solvent.
   USE - Method for producing a plastic hose (claimed).

PN CN113664409-A
TI Rubidium and cesium fluoroaluminate brazing agent comprises rubidium, cesium, aluminum, fluorine, lithium, potassium, sodium, calcium, barium, oxygen and other impurities.
AU CHEN K
   ZUO Q
   PENG Q
AE JIANGXI DONGPENG NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D7117H
AB    NOVELTY - Rubidium and cesium fluoroaluminate brazing agent comprises 45-50 wt.% rubidium (Rb), 9-10 wt.% cesium (Cs), 10-13 wt.% aluminum (Al), 27-35 wt.% fluorine (F), 0.01 wt.% lithium (Li), 0.01 wt.% potassium (K), 0.05 wt.% sodium (Na), 0.01 wt.% calcium (Ca), 0.005 wt.% barium (Ba), oxygen and other impurities.
   USE - Rubidium and cesium fluoroaluminate brazing agent
   ADVANTAGE - The rubidium and cesium fluoroaluminate brazing agent has excellent welding performance, and reduces production cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing rubidium-cesium fluoroaluminate aluminum brazing agent, which involves:
   a. mixing potassium, rubidium and cesium with vanadium extracted from lepidolite;
   b. adding lime to convert the potassium, rubidium and cesium mixed vanadium into potassium, rubidium and cesium sulfate solution;
   c. extracting to extract rubidium and cesium in sulfate solution together;
   d. removing potassium and other impurities, performing carbon dioxide back extraction to obtain rubidium-cesium carbonate solution;
   e. performing concentration crystallization to obtain rubidium-cesium mixed carbonate, adding rubidium-cesium mixed carbonate and analytically pure aluminum hydroxide into a reaction kettle in a certain ratio;
   f. adding water or mother liquor to stir uniformly, adding hydrofluoric acid step by step, controlling proper reaction temperature, obtaining rubidium-cesium fluoroaluminate crystals; and
   g. performing centrifugal separation, drying and crushing to obtain qualified rubidium-aluminium-acid cesium product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The potassium, rubidium and cesium mixed alum is reacted with lime to convert mixed alum into sulfate solution of potassium, rubidium and cesium, carried out filter pressing on converted materials, washed residues with pure water, used washing liquid for conversion reaction, and carried out filter pressing to obtain a pure carbonate solution of potassium, rubidium and cesium. The sulfate solution of potassium, rubidium and cesium is co-extracted by using extracting agent, and performed back extraction by using carbon dioxide to obtain carbonate solution of rubidium and cesium, concentrated and crystallized solution to obtain mixed carbonate solid of rubidium and cesium. The rubidium and cesium mixed carbonate and analytically pure aluminum hydroxide are added into reaction kettle according to proper proportion, added pure water or mother liquor, and uniformly stirred. The hydrofluoric acid is added into reaction kettle, and continuing stirred until qualified rubidium-cesium fluoroaluminate product is generated after the reaction reaches the end point.
DC M23 (Soldering; welding - including brazing, flame cutting and scarfing, cutting and welding rods, soldering and unsoldering apparatus and solder compositions (B23K).); P55 (Soldering, welding metal (B23K).)
MC M23-A01; P55-D03; P55-B02
IP B23K-035/26; B23K-035/40

PN CN113648853-A
TI Preparing composite forward osmosis membrane for e.g. seawater desalination, by soaking electrospun nanofiber supporting layer with intermediate layer in calcium chloride solution, washing, soaking in sodium carbonate solution, washing and preparing polyamide selective separation layer.
AU LIU H
   YU L
   CHEN D
   YU Y
AE UNIV JINAN (UJIN-C)
GA 2021E1414Y
AB    NOVELTY - A preparation method of composite forward osmosis membrane, involves soaking an electrospun nanofiber supporting layer with a graphene oxide intermediate layer into calcium chloride solution, moistening and washing with water, soaking in sodium carbonate solution, moistening and washing in water, preparing polyamide selective separation layer on a surface of electrospinning film substrate loaded with graphene oxide nano-intermediate layer loaded with calcium carbonate through interfacial polymerization of m-phenylenediamine and trimesoyl chloride, and drying to obtain a final product.
   USE - Preparation method of composite forward osmosis membrane for use in seawater desalination, drinking water treatment and wastewater recycling applications (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method loads calcium carbonate particles loaded on the electrospinning nanofiber support layer and its surface graphene oxide intermediate thin layer, generates carbon dioxide nanobubbles in situ with the help of hydrogen (H+) ions generated in the interfacial polymerization process, and regulates the structure and properties of the polyamide separation layer to prepare forward osmosis membrane materials with high permeation flux and high rejection rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) the composite forward osmosis membrane; and
   (2) the application of the composite forward osmosis membrane in seawater desalination, drinking water treatment and wastewater recycling applications.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of calcium chloride is 0.05-0.2 mmol. The concentration of the sodium carbonate solution is 0.05-0.2 mmol. Preferred Process: The electrospinning nanofiber substrate is prepared by dissolving polyacrylonitrile powder in dimethylformamide, and uniformly mixing to obtain uniform electrospinning film casting liquid, and electrostatically spinning. Preferred Conditions: The soaking time of the electrospun nanofiber supporting layer with the graphene oxide intermediate layer in the calcium chloride solution and sodium carbonate solution is 0.5-2 minutes.

   ADVANTAGE - The composite particles have improved thermal                insulation properties. During the production of the                composite particles, some of the airgel particles                are destroyed, but the remaining undestroyed                proportion is sufficient to significantly improve                the thermal insulating properties of the carrier                material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) composite particles prepared by the above                method;
   (2) dry building material mix containing                composite particles;
   (3) plaster mortar, preferably insulating                plaster obtained from a dry building material mix                and/or containing composite particles; and
   (4) shaped body, preferably thermal insulation                board obtained from the dry building material mix                and/or containing composite particles.

   USE - Used as high resilience and antibacterial foam material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material improves the thermal stability of the antibacterial agent in the TPU foam material; solves the problem of poor dispersion of graphene or chitosan single-component antibacterial agent in the matrix resin; ensures problem of agglomeration affecting material properties is prone to occur; reduces the influence of the addition of inorganic antibacterial agents on the elastic properties of foamed materials; and can effectively maintain and improve the elastic properties of the material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing foam material comprising (i) taking TPU material, nylon elastomer, antibacterial agent, stearic acid, crosslinking agent, and blending agent; (ii) adding TPU material, nylon elastomer, antibacterial agent, stearic acid, crosslinking agent, and blending agent to the high-speed mixer in sequence, and mixing at high speed to obtain a premix; (iii) adding premix to the twin-screw extruder, and go through the processes of supercritical carbon dioxide injection, shear mixing, pressure-reducing extrusion, heating foaming, cooling and shaping, etc., to obtain high resilience and antibacterial TPU foam material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The antibacterial agent is modified graphene/chitosan intercalated montmorillonite. The coupling agent is a silane coupling agent, an aluminate coupling agent or titanic acid ester coupling agents. The small molecular amine pre-intercalation agent is ethylenediamine, hexamethylenediamine, aniline or diethylenetriamine. The blending agent is diethyl 2-oxomalonate and ethylene glycol diethyl ether diamine tetraacetic acid in a mass ratio of 2:3 and the crosslinking agent is dicumyl peroxide.Preferred Method: The modified graphene/chitosan intercalated montmorillonite antibacterial agent is prepared by (i) obtaining graphene oxide by the modified Hummers method, taking 10-20 g graphene oxide and adding into three-necked flask containing 100 ml of ethanol, adding 0.5-1 g coupling agent while stirring, reacting for 2-4 hours at -60degrees Celsius filtering with suction, and drying to obtain modified graphene oxide; (ii) taking 3-5 g chitosan, adding 200 ml deionized water and stirring, slowly dropping into 30-50 g of 10-20% acetic acid solution, and stirring uniformly for 3-5 hours prepared chitosan solution, taking 3-5g of the modified graphene and slowly adding into chitosan solution, stirring uniformly to obtain a uniformly mixed modified graphene/chitosan solution, adding the solution in a vacuum oven at 85degrees Celsius vacuum drying is performed to obtain a modified graphene/chitosan material; (iii) taking 10-20 g montmorillonite and add 600 ml deionized water, stirring and dispersing uniformly at room temperature, slowly adding 20-40 g small molecular amine while stirring pre-intercalation agent, fully reacting for 3-6 hours and then ultrasonically treating for 60-100 minutes to obtain a pre-intercalated montmorillonite suspension, taking 3-5 g of the modified graphene/chitosan material, adding into pre-intercalated montmorillonite suspension, heating to to 60-80degrees Celsius and stirring rapidly for 12-24 hours to obtain the graphene/chitosan intercalated montmorillonite suspension, which is filtered, washed and dried to obtain the graphene/chitosan intercalated montmorillonite antibacterial agent .
DC A11 (Polysaccharides; natural rubber; other natural polymers (only a restricted range of (modified) natural polymers are included. Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. TI Preparing nitrogen-doped nano carbon ball with            ultrahigh specific surface area used for e.g. carbon            capture research, involves dissolving 3-aminophenol in            mixed solvent of water and ethanol, performing            hydrothermal treatment, adding carbon precursor,            washing product with water and drying.
AU YAN N
   XU J
   CUI H
   SHI J
AE JIANGXI ACAD SCI APPLIED CHEM INST (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021C4475S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing nitrogen-doped                nano-carbon ball with ultra-high specific surface                area involves (a) dissolving 3-aminophenol in a                mixed solvent of water and ethanol, dropping                aqueous ammonia, stirring and reacting at                30&#8451;, adding formaldehyde solution,                performing hydrothermal treatment at 100&#8451;                and water washing the reaction product, drying to                obtain spherical nano-polymer particles, (b)                placing the spherical nano-polymer in a tubular                furnace, carbonizing under the protection of                nitrogen to obtain a carbon precursor, (c) adding                the carbon precursor into potassium bicarbonate                aqueous solution, stirring, and drying, and (d)                placing the product in a tube furnace, performing                high-temperature activation treatment under the                protective of nitrogen, cooling to room                temperature, using hydrochloric acid to process the                obtained product, washing the product with water                and drying. The method utilizes nitrogen as inert                gas.
   USE - Preparation method of nitrogen-doped nano                carbon ball with ultrahigh specific surface area                used for carbon capture research, super capacitor,                and lithium sulfur battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared carbon material has uniform and                regular spherical structure. The method uses                potassium bicarbonate with lower safety and lower                corrosivity as activating agent. The carbon ball                material has excellent carbon dioxide adsorption                performance.

PN IN201911051776-A
TI Cell-based assay using house dust mite (HDM) in rat mast cell line for measuring biomarkers relevant in pulmonary inflammation by stimulating cells with different concentrations of HDM, treating supernatants, and quenching reactions.
AU SINGH R K
AE UNIV AMITY (UYAM-Non-standard)
GA 2021B9923H
AB    NOVELTY - Cell-based assay using house dust mite (HDM) in rat mast cell line for measuring biomarkers relevant in pulmonary inflammation involves: (a) seeding RBL-2H3 cells at density of 105 cells/well and culturing overnight in 24-well plate in complete cell growth medium including 15% heat-inactivated fetal bovine serum (FBS) in a carbon dioxide (CO2) incubator at 37 degrees C; (b) washing attached RBL-2H3 cells twice and resuspended in phosphate-buffered solution (PBS); (c) stimulating cells with different concentrations of HDM (1-100 mu g/ml in PBS) for 60 minutes; (d) collecting the supernatants; (e) treating 25-50 mu l supernatants from each well with 50-100 mu l of 1 mM p-nitrophenyl N-acetyl- beta -D-glucosamine in 0.05 M citrate buffer (pH of 4.5) for 1 hour at 37 degrees C; and (f) quenching reactions by addition of 50-100 mu l of 0.05M sodium carbonate buffer (pH of 10.0). The intensity of color development is measured by reading absorbance of each sample at 405 nm in a multimode micro plate reader.
   USE - The cell-based assay, using house dust mite in rat mast cell line, is useful for measuring biomarkers relevant in pulmonary inflammation (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: Preferably, to control the total release of beta -hexosaminidase, the cells are completely lysed with 0.5-1% Triton X-100 after the collection of supernatants.

PT P
PN KR2021099240-A; KR2345326-B1
TI Manufacturing carbon-supported alloy nanoparticle catalyst e.g. oxygen reduction reaction, comprises e.g. stirring water-soluble support-deposited alloy nanoparticle-anhydrous polar solvent and heat-treating dried carbon-supported catalyst.
AU YOO S J
   UK L D
   JANG I
   YOUNG P H
   LEE S Y
   PARK H S
   KIM J
   CHANG J H
   KIM H J
AE KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
GA 2021944815
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing carbon-supported alloy nanoparticle catalyst, comprises (a) depositing nanoparticles of the alloy on a water-soluble support; (b) adding the water-soluble support-deposited alloy nanoparticles to an anhydrous polar solvent in which carbon is dispersed and stirring to obtain a dispersion containing an alloy nanoparticle catalyst supported on carbon; (c) washing and filtering the dispersion containing the alloy nanoparticle catalyst supported on carbon to obtain a solid phase of the alloy nanoparticle catalyst supported on carbon; (d) drying the solid phase of the alloy nanoparticle catalyst supported on carbon; and (e) heat-treating the alloy nanoparticle catalyst supported on dried carbon.
   USE - The carbon-supported alloy nanoparticle catalyst is useful in oxygen reduction reaction (claimed), battery, polymer electrolyte membrane fuel cell (PEMFC), phosphate fuel cell (PAFC) and alkaline fuel cell (AEMFC), and water electrolysis catalyst for hydrogen generation reaction, and electrochemical catalyst, preferably carbon-dioxide reduction catalyst, artificial photosynthesis catalyst and an electrochemical synthesis catalyst, and fuel cell for mobile and home use including notebook computers, portable electronic devices, automobiles and buses.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes relatively small amount of chemicals, thus it is environmentally friendly and economical; has simple process; and maintains good electrode catalyst activity even during long-term operation or high-temperature operation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises vacuum drying the water-soluble support before step (a). The water-soluble support is a sugar powder comprising glucose, sucrose or fructose, preferably glucose; a water-soluble metal salt powder containing sodium chloride, potassium chloride or sodium hydrogen carbonate; and/or a water-soluble polymer powder containing poly(vinyl alcohol) (PVA) or polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP). The alloy is at least three alloys of cobalt, platinum, gold, palladium, silver, rhodium, iridium, ruthenium, ruthenium, nickel, iron, copper, manganese, vanadium, chromium, yttrium, lanthanum, cerium, zirconium, titanium, tantalum and/or osmium, preferably platinum-cobalt-vanadium alloy. The anhydrous polar solvent is anhydrous ethanol. The deposition step is performed through sputtering using argon as sputtering gas, with the pressure in the sputtering chamber of 5-15 mTorr and is carried out for 5-20 hours under the condition of sputtering intensity of 10-100 W. In the step (c), the washing and filtration are performed using water as a washing solution and a filtrate. The average size of the alloy nanoparticles is 1-10 nm. In the step (e), the heat treatment in carried out in gas atmosphere or vacuum atmosphere of argon, nitrogen, ammonia, hydrogen or helium, at 600-1000 degrees C for 0.5-4 hours.
   EXAMPLE - 30 mg of carbon carrier (Vulcan XC-72R (RTM: Conductive carbon black)) was placed in absolute ethanol, and sonicated for 60 minutes to evenly disperse. 10 g of platinum-cobalt-vanadium alloy nanoparticles were added to absolute ethanol in which carbon was dispersed, and 10 g glucose was stirred at room temperature for 12 hours. Catalyst was filtered using filter paper after the solution was stirred, and the process of washing off the remaining glucose using 2 l distilled water was repeated multiple times. Then, the catalyst was dried in a vacuum oven at 60 degrees C for 2 hours or more to completely evaporate moisture. The dried catalyst was placed in a zirconia crucible, and heat-treated at 800 degrees C for 2 hours in a hydrogen atmosphere using a high-temperature tube furnace. At this time, in order to prevent the inflow of oxygen gas, air and hydrogen gas were flowed at a rate of 0.5 ml/minute. After the heat treatment, carbon-supported platinum-cobalt-vanadium alloy nanoparticle catalyst was obtained.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); A95 (Transport - including vehicle parts, tyres and armaments.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); ; L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. PT P
PN CN113181944-A
TI Preparing nickel-based non-metal doped catalyst useful e.g. in electrochemical catalysis field, includes taking e.g. nickel salt in reactor, placing cleaned nickel foam (NF), reacting, drying, and pyrolyzing nickel hydroxide/NF precursor.
AU CHENG D
   LIU N
   CAO D
   WU D
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 202190085T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nickel-based non-metal doped catalyst, comprises (i) ultrasonically cleaning nickel foam (NF) cutting sheet in hydrochloric acid with a concentration of 5-20 wt.% for 5-10 minutes, ultrasonically cleaning in water for 5-10 minutes, ultrasonically cleaning in ethanol for 5-10 minutes to remove NF surface impurity, taking out, vacuum drying to obtain cleaned NF, (ii) taking nickel salt, organic alcohol and N,N-dimethyl formamide as raw material, ultrasonically stirring, transferring to a polytetrafluoroethylene reactor, placing the cleaned NF in the reactor, sealing, heating to react, cooling to normal temperature, using ultra-pure water and absolute ethyl alcohol to wash, and vacuum drying to obtain nickel hydroxide (Ni(OH)2)/NF precursor, and (iii) heating to pyrolyze the Ni(OH)2/NF precursor under an oxygen atmosphere to obtain the nickel oxide (NiO)/NF.
   USE - The prepared catalyst is useful in electrochemical catalysis field for oxygen evolution reaction, hydrogen evolution reaction, oxygen reduction reaction, electrocatalysis, carbon dioxide reduction, zinc air battery and lithium battery, and industrial catalysis field for treating industrial wastewater and exhaust gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple operation and mild preparation condition, utilizes uses sulfur source, nitrogen source and transition metal raw materials, and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises vacuum drying in a vacuum oven at 30-70 degrees C for 2-10 hours, and maintaining vacuum degree when drying in vacuum oven at 0.08-0.12 Mpa. The step (ii) comprises ultrasonically stirring for 5-15 minutes until the nickel salt is completely dissolved in the mixed solution of organic alcohol and DMF, and forming a clear green solution. The step (ii) comprises heating to react at 120-180 degrees C for 9-13 hours. The step (i) comprises vacuum drying in the vacuum oven at 20-60 degrees C for 10-24 hours, and maintaining vacuum degree when drying in vacuum oven at 0.08-0.12 Mpa. The step (iii) (iv) and (v) comprises heating to pyrolyze at 300-450 degrees C under a heating rate of 1-10 degrees C/minute, preferably 3-5 degrees C/minute. The method further comprises (iv) placing the NiO/NF precursor obtained in step (i) in sulfur source in a tubular furnace, and heating to pyrolyze under an inert gas atmosphere to obtain nickel disulfide (NiS2)/NF; or (v) placing the NiO/NF precursor obtained in step (i) in nitrogen source in the tubular furnace, and heating to pyrolyze under the inert gas atmosphere to obtain nickel nitride (Ni3N)/NF. Preferred Components: In the step (i), the thickness of the NF cutting sheet is 0.5-1 mm, and the width and length size of the NF cutting sheet is 1.8-2.2x 2.8-3.2 cm, the porosity is 80-90 %. In the step (ii), the nickel salt is nickel nitrate hexahydrate, nickel chloride hexahydrate or nickel sulfate hexahydrate, preferably nickel nitrate hexahydrate, the organic alcohol is methanol, ethanol and isopropanol, preferably methanol and isopropanol, the addition amount of nickel is 30-200 mg, preferably 50-120 mg, the addition amount of the organic alcohol is 10-20 ml, and the addition amount of dimethylformamide (DMF) is 0.2-2 ml, preferably 0.3-1 ml. In the step (iv), the sulfur source is sublimed sulfur, thiourea, thioacetamide or thioformamide, and the inert atmosphere is nitrogen, helium gas or argon gas, and the addition amount of sulfur source is 0.2-2 g. In the step (v), the N source is urea or melamine, and the nitrogen source is 2-3 g.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. PT P
PN WO2021142528-A1; CA3163078-A1
TI Producing biofuel involves providing biogas from            biogas sources, where biogas comprises methane and            provided in mobile vessels, removing and depressurizing            biogas from each of mobile vessels, and generating heat            or power by combusting gas.
AU FOODY P J
   DECHMAN J
AE IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
GA 202183140D
AB    NOVELTY - Producing biofuel involves providing biogas                from biogas sources, where biogas comprises methane                and provided in mobile vessels, each mobile vessel                is pressurized to 2000 pounds per square in gage                (psig) (13.79 megapascal (MPa)), removing and                depressurizing biogas from each of the mobile                vessels, generating heat, power, or combination by                combusting gas comprising methane from biogas                removed and depressurized from the mobile vessels,                and producing biofuel or biofuel intermediate in                production process that includes treating                feedstock, where production process includes use of                heat, power, or combination generated. The quantity                of biogas used to produce heat, power, or                combination used is sufficient to reduce carbon                intensity of biofuel or biofuel intermediate by 5                gCChe/MJ, and change in pressure provided by                depressurizing is used to provide work, cooling,                increased pressure for production process, or                combination.
   USE - Method for producing biofuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to remove hydrogen sulfide                (H2S), which improves carbon dioxide/methane                separation if present during the partial                purification, good balance between upgrading cost                and compressibility, allows more biogas to be                stored, and reduces carbon intensity, net                electricity and/or methane usage, utility                requirements, process energy requirements per unit                of biofuel, costs associated with heating the                cooled biogas, and life cycle greenhouse gas                emissions of the biofuel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The biofuel or biofuel                  intermediate produced is ethanol. The feedstock                  is com grain. The feedstock comprises                  lignocellulosic feedstock. The biogas in the one                  or more mobile vessels is raw biogas or partially                  purified biogas. The biogas is derived from swine                  manure or dairy manure. Preferred Conditions: The                  ethanol recovery is acheived by distillation. The                  process is an integrated process that produces                  ethanol and renewable natural gas, and where                  there is heat exchanged between ethanol                  production process and renewable natural gas                  production process. The biogas used has a carbon                  intensity that does not exceed -100 gCChe/MJ and                  is produced from manure. The biogas provides more                  than 25% of methane used for generating heat,                  power, or a combination within the fuel                  production process. The biogas production process                  uses minimum 3000 million british thermal                  units/day of manure derived biogas. Preferred                  Process: The production process involves treating                  feedstock to produce one or more sugars, adding                  fermentation organism to mixture comprising one                  or more sugars and fermenting one or more sugars                  to produce ethanol, and recovering the ethanol.                  The method for treating feedstock to produce one                  or more sugars involves milling feedstock and                  adding amylase enzyme to milled feedstock to                  hydrolyze starch. The production process produces                  distiller s dried grain or distiller s dried                  grain with solubles. The method for treating                  lignocellulosic feedstock to produce one or more                  sugars involves pretreating feedstock at elevated                  temperature to produce pretreated slurry, and                  adding cellulase enzyme to pretreated slurry to                  hydrolyze cellulose. The change in pressure                  produces cooled biogas, which is used to cool                  circulating fluid directed to condenser, cool                  distiller s dried grains or distiller s dried                  grain with solubles. The change in pressure                  provided by depressurizing is used to provide                  work for the production process, which involves                  passing biogas removed from one or more mobile                  vessels through a turbo expander. The                  depressurizing is conducted so as to provide                  biogas at a pressure of minimum 150 psig (1.03                  MPa), which is provided to biogas upgrading unit                  comprising a membrane unit or scrubbing unit                  without substantial reduction in pressure, where                  membrane unit or scrubbing unit is configured to                  remove carbon dioxide. The method for upgrading                  raw or partially purified biogas removed prior to                  generating heat, power, or combination involves                  collecting carbon dioxide produced from upgrading                  and providing collected carbon dioxide as portion                  of a carbon capture, usage, and storage approach                  to reduce carbon intensity of biofuel. The                  process for producing one or more biofuels                  involves treating feedstock to produce one or                  more sugars, adding fermentation organism to                  mixture comprising one or more sugars and                  fermenting one or more sugars to produce ethanol,                  recovering the ethanol, removing and                  depressurizing biogas from one or more mobile                  vessels pressurized to minimum 2000 psig (13.79                  MPa), where removed and depressurized biogas                  comprises methane, generating heat, power, or a                  combination from portion of methane, and using                  heat, power, or combination for reducing a carbon                  intensity of the ethanol.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D17 (Sugar and starch industry (C07H, C13).); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PN CN112591773-A
TI Preparing battery-grade lithium carbonate by e.g.            pre-processing the waste power lithium battery, heat            treating waste battery powder in box-type atmosphere            furnace, water leaching waste battery powder,            filtering, and precipitating lithium.
AU LI L
   HU J
   WANG J
   CHEN T
   TANG B
   ZHANG M
   CHAI G
   WU H
   HE Y
AE JINCHUAN GROUP CO LTD (JCJT-C)
   LANZHOU JINCHUAN SCI PARK CO LTD (LANZ-Non-standard)
GA 202136107D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing battery-grade lithium carbonate                comprises (a) pre-processing the waste power                lithium battery to obtain waste battery powder; (b)                heat treating the waste battery powder in a                box-type atmosphere furnace, (c) water leaching the                waste battery powder obtained in step (b), and                filtering to obtain a lithium-containing solution                and leaching residue; (d) adding sodium carbonate                to the lithium-containing solution to precipitate                lithium, and filtering to obtain crude lithium                carbonate, (e) slurrying the crude lithium                carbonate obtained in step (d) and passing into                carbon dioxide to obtain a lithium bicarbonate                solution; and (f) crystallizing the lithium                bicarbonate solution by ultrasound to obtain a                battery-grade lithium carbonate product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                battery-grade lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the multiple separation of                impurities in the crude lithium carbonate, reduces                the extraction used for deep purification of                lithium-containing solution, ion exchange and other                steps, the lithium yield reaches more than 90%,                obtains battery level lithium carbonate product has                uniform granularity distribution, regular                appearance, and is easy to realize large-scale                industrial application.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the method for preparing battery-grade lithium                carbonate (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Method: In the step (a),                  pre-processing is charging the waste power                  lithium battery, crushing, sorting, and sieving                  under the protection of inert gas and a closed                  atmosphere to obtain waste battery powder. In the                  step (c), the waste battery powder obtained in                  step (b) has a solid-to-liquid ratio of 1:3-8,                  the leaching temperature is 20-90&#176; C,                  and the leaching time is 30-180 minutes for water                  leaching. In the step (d), the added amount of                  sodium carbonate is 1-1.5 times the theoretical                  amount, the precipitation temperature is                  90-98&#176; C, and the precipitation time is                  20-120 minutes. In the step (e), pure water is                  added to the crude lithium carbonate for                  slurrying, and the liquid-to-solid ratio of pure                  water to crude lithium carbonate is 1-8:1; the                  flow rate of carbon dioxide introduced is 0.2-1                  l/minutes, and the time is 60-180 minutes. In the                  step (f), the ultrasonic crystallization                  temperature is 60-73&#176; C, the ultrasonic                  frequency is 20-33 KHZ, and the ultrasonic power                  is 30-90 W. Preferred Components: In the step                  (a), the waste power lithium battery includes                  nickel cobalt manganese ternary lithium battery,                  nickel cobalt aluminum battery, lithium cobalt                  oxide battery, lithium manganese oxide battery,                  and lithium iron phosphate battery. The inert gas                  is any one of nitrogen, argon or carbon dioxide.                  In the step (a), the particle size of the waste                  battery powder is 5-30&#956; m. In the step                  (b), the heat treatment temperature is                  100-700&#176; C, and the time is 20-120                  minutes, adding the additive including any one of                  sodium bisulfate and potassium bisulfate, and the                  mass ratio of the additive to the waste battery                  powder is 0.5-1.5:1.

   YABLONSKY G

GA 202124327M
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst structure (100) comprises: a                catalytic material (102); and a metal material                (104) on the catalytic material, where an interface                between the metal material and the catalytic                material comprises bonds between the metal material                and the catalytic material, and the metal material                comprises particle sizes of 1.5-3 nm.
   USE - The catalyst structure is useful for                mitigating catalyst deactivation, and carbon                monoxide disproportionation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst structure exhibits high                selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (a) mitigating (M1) catalyst deactivation,                comprising providing the catalyst structure in a                reactor chamber, flowing at least one gas into the                reactor chamber, reacting the gas with the catalyst                structure to produce at least one product and at                least one undesired chemical species, and                accumulating the undesired chemical species on the                metal material without accumulating the undesired                chemical species on the catalytic material;                and
   (b) carbon monoxide disproportionation (M2),                comprising forming the catalyst structure,                introducing carbon monoxide into a reactor chamber                comprising the catalyst structure, reacting the                carbon monoxide with the catalyst structure to                produce carbon dioxide and solid carbon, and                collecting the solid carbon on the metal material                of the catalyst structure while active sites of the                catalytic material remain available to adsorb the                carbon monoxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of                fabrication of a catalyst.
   Catalyst structure (100)
   Catalytic material (102)
   Metal material (104)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalytic material                  comprises molybdenum carbide, titanium carbide,                  vanadium carbide, zirconium carbide, hafnium                  carbide, niobium carbide, tantalum carbide (TaCx                  (where x is 0.4-1)), an oxide of molybdenum,                  titanium, vanadium, zirconium, hafnium, niobium                  or tantalum and/or a mixed metal oxide. The metal                  material comprises platinum, iron, cobalt,                  nickel, molybdenum, copper, potassium, palladium,                  rhodium, ruthenium and/or iridium. The particle                  sizes of the metal material comprise particle                  sizes are 2-2.7 nm. The metal material is                  formulated to preferentially bond to                  carbon-containing compounds. The catalytic                  material comprises at least one active site on a                  surface of it. The active site comprises an                  active molybdenum site.Preferred Method: The step of reacting the                  gas with the catalyst structure comprises:                  reacting the gas with the catalyst structure to                  produce at least one carbon-containing compound;                  and producing at least one of carbon nanofibers,                  carbon nanotubes, carbon filaments or carbon                  whiskers. The method (M1) further comprises                  oxidizing the undesired chemical species while                  the metal material remains on the catalytic                  material. The step of oxidizing the undesired                  chemical species comprises: oxidizing the                  undesired chemical species in parallel with                  reacting the gas with the catalyst structure; and                  oxidizing the undesired chemical species in                  sequence with reacting the gas with the catalyst                  structure. The step of forming the catalyst                  structure comprises: forming the catalyst                  structure comprising molybdenum carbide and                  platinum; and forming the metal material on the                  catalytic material by atomic layer deposition.                  The step of collecting the solid carbon on the                  metal material of the catalyst structure while                  active sites of the catalytic material remain                  available to adsorb the carbon monoxide comprises                  adsorbing oxygen from the carbon monoxide to the                  catalytic material, diffusing carbon from the                  carbon monoxide across a surface of the catalyst                  structure, and collecting the carbon on the metal                  material. The step of collecting the solid carbon                  on the metal material of the catalyst structure                  comprises preferentially bonding the metal                  material to carbon, where the step of                  preferentially bonding the metal material to                  carbon comprises growing the carbon in a                  direction of growth away from a surface of the                  catalytic material. The method (M2) further                  comprises oxidizing the solid carbon.
   EXAMPLE - Platinum/molybdenum carbide samples were                prepared with 50 and 100 atomic layer deposition                cycles which included 1.07 wt.% platinum and 4.4                wt.% platinum respectively. The material was exsitu                pretreated by flowing a gas mixture of 15% methane                in hydrogen gas at a flow rate of 150 ml/minute and                a temperature of 590?oC for 4 hours to reduce the                molybdenum carbide surface that may have been                oxidized in air, to obtain catalyst                structure.

PN CN112371176-A
TI Catalyst useful for treatment of cyanide-containing waste water, prepared by dispersing chitosan in deionized water, adding chloroauric acid solution, filtering, adding e.g. ammonium molybdate, heating and obtaining catalyst.
AU FENG J
   GAO X
   WANG M
AE FENG J (FENG-Individual)
GA 202120328D
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst is prepared by dispersing chitosan in deionized water, adding 1 wt.% chloroauric acid solution to the chitosan mixture, stirring the mixture at room temperature for 10 minutes, adding chloroauric acid to the mixed solution under constant stirring for 1.5 times mass of ascorbic acid, after continuing to stir for 2 hours, filtering and washing to obtain monoatomic gold-loaded chitosan, dispersing the monoatomic gold-loaded chitosan in ethanol, adding ammonium molybdate and sodium sulfide, increasing the temperature to 60-80 degrees C, and stirring for 3-5 hours to obtain a mixture, transferring the mixture to a muffle furnace, firstly heating to 200 degrees C, pre-sintering for 1 hour, heating at 600-800 degrees C at a rate of 3 degrees C/minute, calcining for 3-5 hours, and cooling to at room temperature, and obtaining gold/molybdenum disulfide/chitosan catalyst.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for treatment of cyanide-containing waste water (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst provides energy for ammonium molybdate catalysis by titanium dioxide absorption solar energy, promotes ammonium molybdate to effectively catalyze cyanohydrin reaction with oxygen to generate carbon dioxide and nitrogen, absorbs heavy metal ion in the waste water, and is prepared simply and suitable for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of chitosan and chloroauric acid is 5-10:2. The mass-volume ratio of chitosan and deionized water is 1 g:(5-10) ml. The mass ratio of chitosan loaded with monoatomic gold, ammonium molybdate and sodium sulfide is 5-10:3:3-4. The mass-volume ratio of chitosan loaded with monoatomic gold and ethanol is 1 g:3-5 ml.
