TI Method for baking photoresist layer on substrate,            involves flowing reactive gas species, exposing            substrate to reactive gas species in process chamber            and baking photoresist layer while substrate is exposed            to reactive gas species.
AU WEIDMAN T W
   WU C
   NARDI K L
   VOLOSSKIY B
   GU K L
   GOO K L
AE LAM RES CORP (LRES-C)
   LAM RES CORP (LRES-C)
   LAM RES CORP (LRES-C)
GA 202100960C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves receiving the substrate                (201) in a process chamber. The substrate is                comprised with the photoresist layer. The                photoresist layer is comprised with a                metal-contained with photoresist material. A                reactive gas species are flown into the process                chamber from a gas source through a gas delivery                line. The substrate is exposed to the reactive gas                species in the process chamber. The photoresist                layer is baked while the substrate is exposed to                the reactive gas species. The reactive gas species                is comprised with a gas selected from the group                consisted of water, hydrogen, oxygen, ozone,                hydrogen peroxide, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide,                ammonia, nitrous oxide, nitric oxide, alcohol,                acetyl acetone, formic acid, oxalyl chloride,                pyridine, carboxylic acid, and amine.

The better                control over the processing atmosphere is provided,                and additional safety with respect to potentially                hazardous reactive chemicals is provided.

TI Vacuum cracking method for power battery, involves            performing pyrolysis under oxygen atmosphere to obtain            pyrolysis gas, and non-pyrolyzed substance composed of            anode material powder, cathode material powder, or            copper-iron-aluminum oxide.
AU YU H
   XIE Y
   WU B
AE GUANGDONG BRUNP CYCLING TECHNOLOGY CO (NDCT-C)
   HUNAN BRUNP RECYCLING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NDCT-C)

   GUANGDONG BRUNP RECYCLING TECHNOLOGY CO (NDCT-C)

   HUNAN BRUNP EV RECYCLING CO LTD (NDCT-C)
   GUANGDONG BRUNP RECYCLING TECHNOLOGY CO (NDCT-C)
GA 2021031698
AB    NOVELTY - Vacuum cracking method for power battery                involves feeding waste power battery from feeding                hopper, and then entering roller press for rolling                treatment to obtain crushed aggregates; conveying                crushed material to crushing device (700) for                pre-heating, then heating, under inert atmosphere                or vacuum, cracking to obtain pyrolysis gas, solid                cracking product and non-cleavable substance;                conveying solid cracking product and non-cleavable                substance to pyrolysis device (500); performing                pyrolysis under oxygen atmosphere to obtain                pyrolysis gas and non-pyrolyzed substance, where                main component of pyrolysis gas is carbon dioxide                and water vapor; non-pyrogenic substance is mainly                anode material powder, cathode material powder,                copper powder, iron powder, aluminum powder and                copper-iron-aluminum oxide.
   USE - Vacuum cracking method for power battery                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The vacuum cracking method avoids harm of                traditional pyrolysis process to generate dioxin,                completely decomposing coke, avoids traditional                single cracking process by-product to increase acid                and alkali consumption of subsequent process,                increases solid waste slag amount, and increases                waste water treatment difficulty.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                internal front view of cylinder of cracking device                of power battery.
   Pyrolysis device (500)
   Second air inlet (520)
   Crushing device (700)
   Feeding device (800)
   Discharge device (900)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cracked gas is                  mixed gas of 3-12C olefins and alkanes, pyrolyzed                  fuel. The positive electrode material powder is                  one of lithium nickel cobalt manganate, lithium                  iron phosphate or lithium manganate. The negative                  electrode material powder is one of graphite or                  lithium titanate.

PN IN202041039034-A
TI Anti-fungal herbal composition useful for treating denture and oral stomatitis caused by Candida species, comprises single plant extract or more than one plant extract optionally along with natural and/or additives.
AU GOWDA D V
   MEENAKSHI S
   GUJJARI A K
AE JSS ACAD HIGHER EDUCATION & RES (JSSH-Non-standard)
GA 202101987J
AB    NOVELTY - Anti-fungal herbal composition comprises (1) a single plant extract or more than one plant extract optionally along with one or more natural and/or additives or (2) a floral bud extract of Syzygium aromaticum and bark extract of Cinnamomum zeylanicum at a ratio of 1:8 or 1:16.
   USE - The anti-fungal herbal composition is useful for treating denture and oral stomatitis caused by Candida sp..
   ADVANTAGE - The anti-fungal herbal composition is cost-effective, environmentally-friendly, and safe to administer, exhibits synergistic effect, and treats stomatitis without adverse or effects on the gums and teeth.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing composition, which involves (a) obtaining plant material of S.aromaticum and C.zeylanicum, (b) drying and powdering the plant material obtained in step (a) to obtain a coarse powder material, (c) extracting the powdered plant material of step (b) using hydro distillation or super critical extraction methodology without any co-solvents and carbon dioxide as a super critical fluid (SCF) to obtain pale brown extract extracts of S.aromaticum and C.zeylanicum, and (d) mixing and formulating the plant extract of step (c) with suitable excipients to obtain the emulgel formulation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The ratio of 1:8 is a hydro distillate extract and 1:16 is a supercritical fluid extract of S.aromaticum and C.zeylanicum. The extracts of S.aromaticum and C.zeylanicum are having aromatic odor, pungent taste, pale brown in color, soluble in organic solvents and contains phytochemicals like alkaloids, terpenes, flavonoids, glycosides, tannins, aldehydes including cinnamaldehyde and phenols. The efficacy of herbal extracts obtained from clove or cinnamon by super critical fluid (SCF) extraction is almost similar to the one extracted by hydro distillation procedure. Preferred Composition: The composition is active against the fungi Candida albicans having MIC values of 64+512 mu g/ml for 1:8 hydro distillate ratio of the extract, 64+1024 mu g/ml for 1:16 supercritical fluid extract ratio of S.aromaticum and C.zeylanicum and maximum ZOI value for C.zeylanicum is 32 plus minus 0.12 mm and 17 plus minus 0.15 mm for S.aromaticum. The composition is formulated into an emulgel formulation using almond gum at a concentration of 1-2 wt.% along with excipients including Tween (RTM: Polysorbate 80) 80 and labrasol at a ratio of 1:1, Carbopol (RTM: Cross-linked polyacrylic acid polymer) 934 at a concentration of 0.3-0.8 wt.% and polyethylene glycol (PEG) 400 at a concentration of 12-15 wt.%, methyl paraben at a concentration of 0.18 wt.%, propyl paraben at a concentration of 0.02 wt.%, triethanolamine and water quantity sufficient up to 100 wt.%. The emulgel formulation is obtained by (a) dispersing 1-2 weight/volume% almond gum in purified water and allowing it to swell for 24 hours to obtain gel, (b) obtaining extract phase plant extract by mixing at a ratio of 1:8 or 1:16 of extract phase with 1:1 ratio of Tween (RTM: Polysorbate 80) 80 and labrasol followed by stirring until the extract phase becomes clear solution, and (c) adding the extract phase to the gel obtained in step (a) followed by adding 0.3-0.8 weight/volume% Carbopol (RTM: Cross-linked polyacrylic acid polymer) and 13% PEG 400 followed by addition of preservatives, triethanolamine and water quantity sufficient with vigorous stirring at 1100 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 15 minutes to obtain a uniform emulgel formulation.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Fungicide. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - The composition is administered orally. No dosage details given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN112094423-A; CN112094423-B
TI Liquid crystal flame-retardant foam material for preparing flame-retardant foam material, prepared by reacting aromatic diphenol monomer, aromatic diacid monomer and aromatic monomer having terminal hydroxyl and carboxyl groups and treating.
AU GUAN Q
   GU A
   TANG Y
   LIANG G
   YUAN L
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 2020C9593C
AB    NOVELTY - Liquid crystal flame-retardant foam material is prepared by (i) taking aromatic diphenol monomer, aromatic diacid monomer, and aromatic monomers containing terminal hydroxyl and carboxyl groups as raw material, reacting in presence of active terminal group catalyst and acetic acid compound under protection of nitrogen at 120-140 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, increasing the temperature to 280-325 degrees C at a rate of 0.5-1.5 degrees C/minute, and reacting for 1-3 hours to obtain a solid material, and (ii) treating the solid material at 320-380 degrees C for 20-60 minutes under a vacuum of 10-90 kPa to obtain the product.
   USE - The foam material is useful for preparing flame-retardant foam material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses the residual by-product acetic acid during the condensation polymerization reaction of liquid crystal polymer as a foaming agent, decomposes and releases carbon dioxide at high temperature to form a microporous structure and at the same time the matrix undergoes a curing reaction to form a crosslinked network structure, does not need to add flame retardant to achieve flame-retardant effect, and reaches the UL-94 V0 flame-retardant level, and is environmentally-friendly and simple.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aromatic diphenol monomer comprises 1,4-hydroquinone, 1,3-resorcinol, 4,4'-biphenol, 1,1'-biphenyl-3,4'-diphenol, 1,1'-biphenol benzene-3,3'-diphenol, 1,6-naphthalenediol, 1,7-naphthalenediol, 2,6-naphthalenediol, and/or 2,7-naphthalenediol. The aromatic diacid monomer comprises 1,4-terephthalic acid, 1,3-isophthalic acid, 4,4'-biphthalic acid, 1,1'-biphenyl-3,4'-dicarboxylic acid, 1,1'-biphenyl-3,3'-dicarboxylic acid, 1,6-naphthalenedicarboxylic acid, 1,7-naphthalenedicarboxylic acid, 2,6-naphthalenedicarboxylic acid, and/or 2,7-naphthalenedicarboxylic acid. The aromatic monomer containing terminal hydroxyl and carboxyl group comprises 3-hydroxybenzoic acid, 4'-hydroxybiphenyl-3-carboxylic acid, and/or 7-hydroxy-2-naphthoic acid. The active end group comprises 3-aminophenylacetylene, N-(4-carboxyphenyl)-4-phenylethynylphthalimide, N-(4-Phenyl acetate)-4-phenylethynyl phthalimide, N-(3-carboxyphenyl)-4-phenylethynyl phthalimide, and/or N-(3-acetate phenol ester)-4-phenylethynyl phthalimide. The catalyst comprises potassium acetate, sodium acetate, zinc acetate, dibutyltin oxide, stannous octoate, or dibutyltin laurate. The acetic acid compound is acetic anhydride. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of aromatic diphenol monomer, aromatic diacid monomer, aromatic monomer containing terminal hydroxyl group and terminal carboxyl group, active terminal group, catalyst and acetic acid compound is 1:1:(0.5-2):(0.03-0.45):(0.5-5)%:(1.5-4). Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the solid material is ground into a powder, and treated at 320-380 degrees C for 20-60 minutes under a vacuum of 10-90 kPa to obtain a liquid crystal flame-retardant foam material.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); U11 (Semiconductor Materials and Processes)

AU ZHANG H
   DONG H
   SU B
   MA R
AE UNIV FOURTH MILITARY MEDICAL (UYFM-C)
GA 202099861X
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model relates to the field of medical appliance, specifically to a lime tank. The soda lime pot comprises a lime pot main body and a guide pipe. the lime tank main body is provided with an air inlet end and a bottom wall; the guide pipe is set in the lime tank main body; one end of the guide pipe is propped against the bottom wall; the other end of the guide pipe is protruded out of the air inlet end; and the pipe wall of the guide pipe is provided with a plurality of through holes. by setting the guide pipe, the gas can enter into the lime tank main body from the guide pipe, and is filled between the bottom wall of the whole lime tank main body and the air inlet end of the lime tank main body. so that all the sodium (calcium) lime in the whole lime tank main body can fully react with the gas, which effectively avoids the problem of carbon dioxide and sodium (calcium) lime " free binding ", which greatly improves the utilization rate of sodium calcium lime in the whole sodium calcium lime tank.

TI Foamable, intumescent multi-component composition useful e.g. in moldings for cable and pipe penetrations in walls, comprises aliphatic isocyanate compound, isocyanate-reactive reactive component, fire protection additive and propellant.
AU PAETOW M
   MILLER J
AE HILTI AG (HILT-C)
   HILTI AG (HILT-C)
   HILTI AG (HILT-C)
GA 202079454Y
AB    NOVELTY - Foamable, intumescent multi-component composition comprises at least one aliphatic isocyanate compound having average cyanate functionality of greater than or equal to 1, at least one isocyanate-reactive reactive component having compounds with at least two amino groups and/or polyol, at least one fire protection additive which forms intumescent layer, and propellant comprising at least one compound for releasing carbon dioxide through reaction. The individual components of the propellant are separated from one another in a reaction-inhibiting manner before the use of the multicomponent composition and at least one aliphatic isocyanate compound is separated from the reactive component which is reactive towards isocyanate groups.
   USE - The composition is useful in moldings for foaming openings, cable and pipe penetrations in walls, floors and/or ceilings, joints between ceilings and wall parts, between wall openings and structural parts to be installed, i.e. window and door frames, between ceilings and Walls and between external walls and curtain walls of buildings for fire protection.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has excellent application properties, improved expansion factor and flexibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for moldings obtained from the composition, the respective components mixed with one another and the mixture to be foamed in a mold.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition further comprises catalyst for reaction between isocyanate compound and isocyanate-reactive reactive component; foam stabilizer; plasticizers, inorganic fillers and/or further additives. Preferred Components: The propellant is water. The reactive component which is reactive towards isocyanate compounds is polyamines, polyether polyamines and/or polyaspartic acid esters and at least one polyaspartic acid ester of formula (I). The reactive component which is reactive towards isocyanate compounds further comprises at least one polyol. The equivalent ratio of isocyanate groups of the isocyanate compound to the isocyanate-reactive reactive component is 0.3 and 1.7. The intumescent fire protection additive is graphite intercalation compounds and/or expandable silicate material and ash crust stabilizer.R1 and R2 = organic radicals which are inert to isocyanate groups;R3 and R4 = H or organic radicals which are inert towards isocyanate groups;X = n-valent organic radical which is inert towards isocyanate groups; andn = greater than or equal to 2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyol includes polyester polyol, polyether polyol, hydroxylated polyurethane and/or alkanes having at least two hydroxy groups per molecule.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   SINGH V P
AE CGC TECH CAMPUS (CGCT-Non-standard)
GA 2020648988
AB    NOVELTY - The system for fresh air regulation in air-conditioned room (13) with air-conditioner (7) comprises a first fan (3) covered with first protection cover (2), for injecting outside air in, operably connected with a controller (9). Mounting pipe (4) at one end of which the first fan is connected and at the other end, a filter cover (5) is connected. Filter cover with one end connected to mounting pipe and the other end placed on the evaporator (6) of the air-conditioner (7). A second fan (10) suitably placed in air-conditioned room and operably connected with the controller, for ejecting inside air out.

PT P
PN CN111359600-A; CN111359600-B
TI Waste water and waste gas pollutant treatment ball loaded with composite modified nano titanium dioxide comprises active microporous balls, composite modified nano titanium dioxide, and plant-extracted photosensitizer or strong oxidant.
AU SU B
   SU W
AE BEIJING JINXIU NEW TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202067251H
AB    NOVELTY - Waste water and waste gas pollutant treatment ball loaded with composite modified nano titanium dioxide comprises active microporous balls, composite modified nano titanium dioxide, and plant-extracted photosensitizer or strong oxidant. The composite modified nano-titanium dioxide and plant extract photosensitizer or strong oxidant are sequentially loaded on the active microporous spheres.
   USE - Used as waste water and waste gas pollutant treatment ball loaded with composite modified nano titanium dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The ball: has high-capacity active groups; treats exhaust gas; can be placed in the exhaust gas discharge channel; exhausts pollutants in the exhaust gas react with active groups with higher energy; and transforms into carbon dioxide, water, and other substances.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the wastewater and waste gas pollutant treatment balls loaded with composite modified nano-titanium dioxide, comprising (i) performing surface modification of microporous spheres to make active microporous spheres, (ii) preparing composite modified nano-titanium dioxide solution, (iii) immersing the active microporous spheres in the composite modified nano-titanium dioxide sol for a predetermined time, and drying and roasting to obtain composite modified nano-titanium dioxide hollow spheres, and (iv) immersing the composite modified nano-titanium dioxide hollow spheres in a plant extract photosensitizer or strong oxidizer, taking out, dand rying for a predetermined time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The active microporous balls comprise hollow glass microspheres, floating beads, hollow microspheres, vitrified microbeads, sink beads, glass fiber balls, nickel balls, silica balls, alumina balls, zeolites, molecular sieves, silica gel balls, activated carbon balls, ceramic ball, cement ball, or sand ball. The strong oxidant comprises hydrogen peroxide, sodium chlorate, sodium perchlorate, calcium hypochlorite, sodium hypochlorite, sodium percarbonate, iron permanganate, potassium permanganate, potassium ferrate, sodium thiosulfate, potassium dichromate, trichloroisocyanuric acid, sodium acetate, lead dioxide, cobalt trifluoride, sodium ferrate, periodic acid, sodium bismuth, sodium persulfate, potassium persulfate, chlorine dioxide, and/or dichloroisocyanuric acid and its salts. The acid in the acid solution comprises nitric acid, sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, boric acid, formic acid, tartaric acid, citric acid, tannic acid, acetic acid, oxalic acid, malic acid, malic acid, succinic acid, punicic acid, vanillic acid, benzoic acid, salicylic acid, coumaric acid, ferulic acid, syringic acid, citric acid, caffeic acid, benzoic acid, stearic acid, oleic acid, linoleic acid, and/or linolenic acid. The diameter of the active microporous sphere is 0.5-19 mm, the pore size distribution is 20 nm to 20 mu m, the ignition loss less than or equal to 6, the water content at room temperature is less than or equal to 18 wt.%, the bulk density is 0.75 g/ml, the specific surface area is greater than or equal to 220 m2/g, the pore volume is greater than or equal to 0.65 ml/g, and crushing strength is greater than or equal to 8-100 N/piece. The coupling agent comprises silane coupling agent, titanate coupling agent, aluminate coupling agent, organic chromium complex coupling agent or phosphate coupling agent. The rare earth salt comprises nickel nitrate, cerium nitrate, yttrium nitrate, ytterbium nitrate, erbium nitrate, cerium ammonium nitrate, lanthanum nitrate, lanthanum cerium nitrate, neodymium nitrate, europium nitrate, bismuth chloride, praseodymium chloride, indium chloride, chlorine cerium chloride, lanthanum chloride, yttrium chloride, gadolinium chloride, thulium amide, samarium chloride, cerium acetate, lanthanum acetate, dysprosium acetate, holmium acetate, erbium acetate, thulium acetate, lanthanum carbonate, lanthanum praseodymium carbonate, carbonic acid lanthanum cerium praseodymium, neodymium praseodymium carbonate, cerium carbonate and/or lanthanum cerium carbonate. The reducing agent is unsymmetrical dimethyl hydrazine, hydrazine carbonate, hydrazine sulfate, hydrazine nitrate, dihydrazine carbonate, disulfide hydrazide, semicarbazide, isoniazid, perhenhydrazine, hydrazine hydrate, nitrofurazone, sodium borohydride, borohydride Potassium, ammonium borohydride, lithium borohydride, manganese borohydride, sodium cyanoborohydride, lithium aluminum hydride, carbohydrazide, modified lignin, thiourea dioxide, steam ethanol, gaseous sulfur dioxide, sodium thiosulfate, dimethylacetamide , ethylenediamine, EDTA, hydroiodic acid, vitamins, glucose, chitosan, gallic acid, L-glutathione, amino acids, aluminum powder, sodium citrate, yeast enzyme, nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide phosphate, pectin, flavonoids, ascorbic acid, apigenin, and/or luteolin. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the surface modification of the microporous ball comprises (ia) washing and drying the microporous balls, placing in 8-20% acid solution, immersing for 5-25 minutes, washing immersed active microporous balls with water, and drying to obtain acid-treated microporous balls, and (ib) soaking the acid-treated microporous spheres in an aluminum salt solution, washing with water, then adding pH3-4 hydrolysis coupling agent alcohol solution at 60-80 degrees C for a constant temperature reaction for a predetermined time, washing with toluene, and vacuum-drying at -65 degrees C to obtain surface-modified active microporous spheres. In the step (ib), the aluminum salt solution is soaked and washed with water, the acid solution is added dropwise to pH 3-4, hydrolyzed at room temperature for 30-90 minutes to obtain a hydrolytic coupling agent solution, and 50-150 g/l active microporous hollow spheres are added to the hydrolysis coupling agent solution for constant temperature reaction, where the volume ratio of the coupling agent to the alcohol used in the process of obtaining the hydrolysis coupling agent in the alcohol solution is 20-100 ml/l. The composite modified nano-titanium dioxide sol is a rare earth doped titanium dioxide composite sol, which is specifically prepared by (iia) adding n-butyl titanate dropwise to the mixture of absolute ethanol and glacial acetic acid under stirring conditions to form solution A, where the volume ratio of n-butyl titanate, absolute ethanol and glacial acetic acid is 8:40:11-20:25:4, and (iib) adding 20-100 mg/l rare earth salt to ionized water, uniformly stirring, adding 5-100 ml/l nitric acid, and stirring and mixing with the disperser to obtain solution B, and (iic) dripping the solution B to the solution A under high-speed stirring, and completely stirring to form a transparent sol. The composite modified nano-titanium dioxide sol is a graphene oxide composite titanium dioxide composite sol, which is specifically prepared by (iia) dispersing 100-500 mg/l graphene oxide in deionized water, ultrasonically dispersing, and homogenizing obtain a graphene oxide solution, and dissolving tetrabutyl titanate in isopropanol to obtain mixture A, where the volume ratio of tetrabutyl titanate and isopropanol is 1:10-1:30, and (iib) dripping the graphene oxide solution to the mixed solution A while stirring to obtain the mixed solution B, dripping the volume ratio of the mixed solution A: the graphene solution is 1:1-1:3, dripping 10-50 mg/l reducing agent to the mixed solution B under stirring conditions, and refluxing and continuously stirring to obtain a graphene composite titanium dioxide composite sol. The composite modified nano-titanium dioxide sol is a semiconductor coupled titanium dioxide composite material sol, which is specifically prepared by (iia) ultrasonically dispersing nano-titanium dioxide in absolute ethanol at 20-200 g/l to obtain mixed solution C, and (iib) dripping metal salt solution to the mixed solution C to obtain the mixture D, then reacting the sodium and potassium with the metal salt, calcining to form the metal oxide, sulfide or selenide, slowly dripping salt solution into the mixed solution D, stirring and reacting, and homogenizing to obtain a semiconductor modified titanium dioxide composite sol. In the step (iv), the composite modified nano-titanium dioxide hollow spheres is soaked in the plant extract photosensitizer at 50-100 g/l for 20-30 hours, vacuum-drying to obtain the wastewater and waste gas pollutant treatment of the composite modified nano-titanium dioxide ball, or immersing the loaded composite modified nano-titanium dioxide hollow spheres in a strong oxidant dipping solution with a mass fraction of 1-10 wt.%, where the mass ratio of the loaded composite modified nano-titanium dioxide hollow spheres and the strong oxidant dipping solution is 1:2-1:4, soaking and stirring for 2-5 hours, statically soaking for 20-30 hours, and vacuum-drying to obtain wastewater and waste gas pollutant treatment balls loaded with composite modified nano-titanium dioxide. In the step (iii), the active microporous spheres is soaked in the sol for 5-50 hours, taking out, drying at 60-80 degrees C for 2-4 hours, and heating to 300-600 degrees C at a rate of 10 degrees C/minute in an air atmosphere for 4 hours to obtain composite modified nano-titanium dioxide hollow sphere.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The nano metal particles comprise platinum, gold, silver, palladium, copper, nitrogen, rhodium, iridium, ruthenium or osmium, and the particle size of the nano metal particles is less than 100 nm. The metal in the metal salt solution is zinc, cadmium, tungsten, tin, lead or iron. The metal oxide formed by calcination is zinc oxide, tungsten trioxide, tin dioxide, lead sulfide, or iron oxide. The sulfide is cadmium sulfide, and the selenide is cadmium selenide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The plant extract photosensitizer comprises porphyrin, chlorophyll, xanthene, erythrosine B, eosin, humic acid, quinoline, anthocyanin, anthocyanin, fluorescein, thionine, phthalocyanine, porphyrine, and/or rose Bengal.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The stabilizer comprises polyvinylpyrrolidone, poly-2-vinylpyrrolidone, polyvinyl alcohol, poly-2-hydroxypropyl methacrylate, polymethyl methacrylate, poly-4-vinylpyrrolidone, poly-N-vinylformamide, poly N-vinyl acetamide, poly N-vinyl isobutyramide, polystyrene sulfonic acid, sodium polystyrene sulfonate, polyacrylic acid, sodium polyacrylate, poly-2-acrylamide-2-methyl-propyl sulfonic acid , polystyrene-b-poly 4-vinylpyrrolidone, polystyrene-b-polymethyl methacrylate, polystyrene-b-polyethylene oxide, polystyrene-b-poly-2-vinylpyrrolidone, polystyrene- g-polyacrylic acid, poly-2-vinylpyrrolidone-b-polybutadiene, polyvinylpyrrolidone-b-polyethylene oxide, polyethylene glycol-b-polymethyl methacrylate, polyacrylic acid-b-polyacrylamide, polyacrylic acid-b-polyhydroxyethyl acrylate, polyoxyethylene-b-polyethyleneimine, branched polyoxyethylene-b-polyethyleneimine, polymethylmethacrylate-g-polyacrylic acid, polydiallyldimethylammonium chloride, poly-2-hydroxy-3-methacrylate trimethylammonium chloride, polyacrylamide, chitosan, or polyaniline.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC A12-W11J; A12-W14; D04-A01B; D04-A01F2; D04-A01J; D04-A01P1; E05-B01; E06-A01; E06-A03B; E06-D18; E06-F04; E07-A01; E07-A02B; E07-D04C; E07-D13B; E10-A13B2; E10-B01C1; E10-B01E; E10-C02A; E10-C02F; E10-C03; E10-C04C; E10-C04H; E10-C04J2U; E10-C04L2; E10-D03C1; E10-G02F1; E31-B03D; E31-C; E31-E; E31-F05; E31-G; E31-H05; E31-N04C; E31-Q02; E31-U01; E34-C03; E34-E01; E34-E02A; E34-E02B; E35; J04-E09

AU LIU J
   ZHENG J
AE BEIJING GUAVA TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202058187U
AB    NOVELTY - A benzene derivative high-efficiency catalyst                (I) is new.
   USE - Benzene derivative high-efficiency                catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The product efficiently catalyzes formic acid                dehydrogenation and reduction reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A benzene derivative high-efficiency catalyst                of formula (I) is new.
   M=metal atom selected from one of iridium,                rhodium, ruthenium, iron, cobalt, silver,                palladium, nickel and gold;
   Y'=NO3-, ClO4-, BF4-, SO42-, SbF6-, PF6-, Cl-                and acetyl;
   X=Cl, Br, I, F, and/or OH, and neutral ligand                selected from water, methanol, ethanol and/or                tetrahydrofuran;
   Z=H, Me and Et;
   n=1 or 2;
   R=electron-donating group from 3-6 positions                on the pyridine ring, selected from MeO, R'O, Me2N                or R'2N;
   R'=alkyl, cycloalkyl, and aromatic                groups;
   R=optional electron-withdrawing group                including OR1 or NR1R2; and
   R1-R2=alkyl, cycloalkyl and aromatic groups of                formula (i).
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) method for catalyzing formic acid                dehydrogenation using benzene derivative                high-efficiency catalyst comprising adding benzene                derivative high-efficiency catalyst aqueous                solution and pure formic acid or formic acid                solution to reaction device, reacting, and                collecting generated hydrogen and carbon dioxide;                and
   (2) method for catalyzing formic acid                reduction reaction using benzene derivative                high-efficiency catalyst comprising adding benzene                derivative high-efficiency catalyst aqueous                solution, pure formic acid or mixture of formic                acid solution and aldehyde solution to reaction                device, reacting, and purifying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Methods: The method for catalyzing                  formic acid dehydrogenation using benzene                  derivative high-efficiency catalyst comprises                  adding 0.001-5.0 mol/L benzene derivative                  high-efficiency catalyst aqueous solution and                  pure formic acid or 0.001-28 mol/L formic acid                  solution to reaction device, reacting at 0-100                  degrees C at pH value of 0-14 for 0.1 minute to                  24 hours, and collecting generated hydrogen and                  carbon dioxide. The method for catalyzing formic                  acid reduction reaction using benzene derivative                  high-efficiency catalyst comprises adding                  0.001-5.0 mol/L benzene derivative                  high-efficiency catalyst aqueous solution, pure                  formic acid or mixture of 1:1-10:1 molar ratio of                  0.001-25 mol/L formic acid solution and 0.001-10                  mol/L aldehyde solution to reaction device,                  reacting at 0-100 degrees C at pH value of 0-14                  for 0.1 minute to 24 hours, and purifying.                  Preferred Components: The aldehyde is fatty                  aldehyde or aromatic aldehyde.
   Preferred Definitions:
   M=iridium.

PN CN111185167-A; CN111185167-B
TI Platinum-based catalyst useful for purifying            carbon monoxide, comprises carrier titanium dioxide,            platinum, ceric oxide, tungsten trioxide and/or            molybdenum trioxide.
AU ZHU T
   LIU X
   LIU F
   FENG C
AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 202046605B
AB    NOVELTY - Platinum-based catalyst comprises a carrier, a                main active component, a first auxiliary active                component and a second auxiliary active component.                The carrier is titanium dioxide, the main active                component is platinum, the first auxiliary active                component is ceric oxide, and second co-active                component is tungsten trioxide and/or molybdenum                trioxide.
   USE - The platinum-based catalyst is useful for                purifying carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: is economical; and has strong                universality, good stability, high carbon monoxide                catalytic oxidation efficacy, high selectivity to                carbon dioxide and sulfur resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing platinum-based catalyst,                comprising (i) soaking titanium dioxide in the                cerium source solution and roasting to obtain ceric                oxide/ titanium dioxide, (ii) soaking the ceric                oxide/titanium dioxide obtained in step (i) in the                source solution of the second auxiliary active                component and calcining to obtain titanium dioxide                modified with the first auxiliary active component                and the second auxiliary active component, and                (iii) soaking the product in step (ii) in a                platinum source solution and calcining; and
   (2) application of platinum-based catalyst,                comprising utilizing platinum-based catalyst used                for carbon monoxide catalytic oxidation treatment                of industrial flue gas, preferably, the                concentration of carbon monoxide in the industrial                flue gas is 2000-9000 ppm and the concentration of                sulfur dioxide is 0-500 ppm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the platinum-based                  catalyst, the first co-active component is                  located on the surface of the support, the second                  auxiliary active component is located on the                  surface of the first auxiliary active component                  and the main active component is located on the                  surface of the second auxiliary active component,                  preferably the titanium dioxide is anatase                  titanium dioxide. The mass percentage content of                  the first co-active component in the                  platinum-based catalyst is 2-10 wt.%, preferably                  3-7 wt.%, the mass percentage content of the                  second co-active component in the platinum-based                  catalyst is 1-10 wt.%, preferably 3-7 wt.% and                  the mass percentage content of the main active                  component in the platinum-based catalyst is                  0.05-0.5 wt.%, preferably 0.25-0.4 wt.%,                  preferably based on the mass of the                  platinum-based catalyst being 100 wt.%. The                  cerium source in step (i) includes cerium nitrate                  and/or ammonium cerium nitrate, preferably, the                  immersion time in step (i) is 2-24 hours,                  preferably after immersion in step (i) before                  roasting carrying out drying at 80-120 degrees C                  for 2-5 hours and preferably, the calcination                  temperature in step (i) is 450-600 degrees C,                  preferably the calcination time is 2-10 hours.                  The source of the second auxiliary active                  component in step (ii) comprises a tungsten                  source and/or a molybdenum source, preferably the                  tungsten source includes ammonium metatungstate                  and/or ammonium paratungstate, preferably the                  molybdenum source includes ammonium molybdate                  and/or ammonium paramolybdate, preferably, the                  immersion time in step (ii) is 2-24 hours,                  preferably after immersion in step (ii) and                  before roasting carrying out drying at 80-120                  degrees C for 2-5 hours, preferably the                  calcination temperature in step (ii) is 450-600                  degrees C and preferably the roasting time in                  step (ii) is 2-10 hours. The platinum source in                  step (iii) comprises at least two of platinum                  nitrate, platinum chloride or chloroplatinic                  acid, preferably the immersion time in step (iii)                  is 2-24 hours, preferably after immersion in step                  (iii), and before roasting carrying out drying at                  80-120 degrees C for 2-5 hours, preferably the                  calcination temperature in step (iii) is 450-600                  degrees C, preferably, the clacining time in step                  (iii) is 2-10 hours. Preferred Method: The                  preparation method of platinum-based catalyst is                  a dipping method, preferably a step dipping                  method. The preparation method of platinum-based                  catalyst preferably comprises (a) preparing                  cerium source solution, adding anatase titanium                  dioxide to impregnate for 2-24 hours, drying at                  80-120 degrees C for 2-5 hours and then calcining                  at 450-600 degrees C for 2-10 hours to obtain                  ceric oxide/ titanium dioxide; (b) preparing a                  solution of tungsten source and/or molybdenum                  source, adding ceric oxide/ titanium dioxide of                  step (a) to impregnate for 2-24 hours, drying at                  80-120 degrees C for 2-5 hours, and then                  calcining at 450-600 degrees C for 2-10 hours to                  obtain titanium dioxide modified by the first                  auxiliary active component and the second                  auxiliary active component; and (c) preparing a                  platinum source solution, adding the product in                  step (b) for 2-24 hours, drying at 80-120 degrees                  C for 2-5 hours and then calcining at 450-600                  degrees C for 2-10 hours.

PN KR2112796-B1
TI Oxynitriding press mold material surface by heating useful in industrial site, comprises e.g. removing oil and foreign substances on surface of object by processing press mold component for die casting, and removing processed chromium film.
AU CHUNG J
AE SHINKEE IND CO LTD (SHIN-Non-standard)
GA 202043792G
AB    NOVELTY - Oxynitriding press mold material surface by heating comprises (a) removing oil and foreign substances on surface of object by processing press mold component for die casting, and removing processed chromium film on the surface of the object using sanding, (b) treating removed chromium film on the surface of the object, and loading the object into nitriding furnace chamber, (c) injecting ammonia and nitrogen in equal ratio into the nitriding furnace chamber, (d) raising the temperature to 520 degrees C for 120 minutes, and maintaining the temperature for 300 minutes, (d) injecting 0.8 wt.% carbon dioxide into the nitriding furnace chamber, maintaining temperature at 520 degrees C for 300 minutes, transferring gas containing nitrogen and oxygen in weight ratio of 8:2, oxynitriding at 520 degrees C for 20 minutes to form iron(III) oxide film layer, and (e) cooling at less than or equal to 100 degrees C for 120 minutes, and taking the object out of the nitriding furnace chamber.
   USE - The oxynitriding press mold material surface by heating is useful in industrial site.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxynitriding press mold material has surface strength, abrasion resistance, heat resistance, and high hardness of surface.

AB    NOVELTY - The separation unit (10) has a main air compression system that is configured for receiving a stream of incoming feed air and producing a compressed air stream (24). An adsorption based pre-purifier unit (28) is configured for removing water vapor, carbon dioxide, nitrous oxide, and hydrocarbons. A distillation column system (70) has a higher pressure column and a lower pressure column (74) linked in a heat transfer relationship through a condenser-reboiler. The distillation column system includes an argon column arrangement coupled with the lower pressure column. The argon column arrangement has an argon column and an argon condenser (78). The air separation unit main portion is configured to produce nitrogen products, the gaseous oxygen product stream, and the crude argon stream and the argon recovery in the crude argon stream is greater than 30 percent of the argon contained in the compressed air stream.
   USE - Air separation unit for nitrogen production.
   ADVANTAGE - The higher nitrogen recovery improves the cost efficiency of the nitrogen producing air separation plant in terms of lower capital costs and lower operating costs.

GA 2020359156
AB    NOVELTY - A bio-based polycarbonate ester comprises 80%                or more bio-based carbon which is derived from                biomass according to ASTM D6866, and at least one                hexahydrofuro(3,2-b)furan-containing repeating                units (I)-(III).
   USE - Bio-based polycarbonate ester used for forming                molded article (claimed). Uses include but are not                limited to injection molded article, extrusion                molded article, blow molded article, and profile                extrusion molded article.
   ADVANTAGE - The bio-based polycarbonate ester is                environmentally-friendly and human-friendly, and                has excellent biodegradability. The manufacture of                these paleontological polycarbonate esters has the                advantage of continuously utilizing a vast amount                of biological resources and reducing carbon dioxide                emissions to prevent global warming.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A bio-based polycarbonate ester comprises 80%                or more bio-based carbon which is derived from                biomass according to ASTM D6866, and at least one                hexahydrofuro(3,2-b)furan-containing repeating                units of formulae (I)-(III). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM                is included for manufacture of bio-based                polycarbonate ester, which involves performing melt                polycondensation of 1,4:3,6-dianhydrohexitol with                at least one of dicarbonate compounds of formulae                (IV)-(VI).
   R1 = 1-18C alkyl, 6-18C aryl, 4-20C                cycloalkyl, 1-18C alkoxy, 4-20C cycloalkoxy, 6-18C                aryloxy, 1-18C alkysulfonyl, 4-20C                cycloalkylsulfonyl, 6-18C arylsulfonyl or ester                substituents.

GA 202032907W
AB    NOVELTY - Aqueous gas conversion reaction catalyst comprises 40-80 mol.% copper, 15-50 mol.% zinc and 1-13 mol.% aluminum, where aluminum rich layer is present on the surface layer of the catalyst particles.
   USE - The aqueous gas conversion reaction catalyst is useful for producing hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is easy to operate at 250-350 degrees C, and has high catalytic activity, excellent performance even in high temperature conditions due to exothermic reaction, and stable performance even under low water vapor/carbon dioxide ratio conditions and excellent hydrothermal durability, so that deactivation does not occur even when exposed to high temperature water vapor for prolonged time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for manufacturing the catalyst, involving (a) injecting a metal precursor solution comprising copper and zinc precursor into a precipitant solution, and coprecipitating copper and zinc to produce a copper-zinc coprecipitate, (b) injecting aluminum precursor solution into a solution comprising the copper-zinc coprecipitate, and arranging aluminum on the copper-zinc coprecipitate surface to prepare a copper, zinc and aluminum catalyst precursor having the aluminum rich layer on the surface, and (c) calcining the copper, zinc and aluminum catalyst precursor to prepare the catalyst; and
   (2) a method for producing hydrogen, involving performing an aqueous gas conversion reaction using the above-mentioned catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The aluminum rich layer is 0.1-10% of the radius of the catalyst particles from the surface layer of the catalyst particles. Preferred Method: The step (a) involves performing the copper and zinc coprecipitation by aging the mixture comprising the copper precursor, metal precursor solution comprising zinc precursor, and precipitant solution at 20-80 degrees C for 30-180 minutes. The step (b) involves injecting the aluminum precursor solution after recovering, and decreasing pH to 0.05-0.2 during aging, and after the copper-zinc coprecipitate is changed from an amorphous form to a crystalline form during aging. The aluminum precipitation step is performed by injecting aluminum precursor solution into solution containing copper-zinc coprecipitate, and aging at 20-80 degrees C for 15-60 minutes. The method involves (i) filtering, and washing the solution containing the copper, zinc and aluminum catalyst precursor obtained in the aluminum precipitation step, and drying the obtained catalyst precursor in an oven at 100-300 degrees C for 22-24 hours, and (ii) performing a reduction reaction under a hydrogen atmosphere at 280-500 degrees C. Preferred Conditions: The metal precursor solution containing copper precursor and zinc precursor is injected into the precipitant solution until the pH is 5-8. The precipitant solution has the temperature of 20-80 degrees C, pH of 6-9 and concentration of 0.01-1.2 M. The aluminum precursor solution is injected until the pH reaches to 5-7. The calcining temperature is 300-500 degrees C. The aqueous gas conversion reaction is carried out at 200-450 degrees C, where the molar ratio of water vapor/carbon monoxide is 1-3. Preferred Composition: The metal precursor solution containing the copper precursor and zinc precursor has a concentration of 0.01-1.5 M, and the molar ratio of copper ions to zinc ions is 50:50-80:20. The concentration of aluminum precursor solution is 0.01-1.5 M. Preferred Components: The copper precursor, zinc precursor and aluminum precursor are salts comprising anion comprising nitrate ion, sulfate ion, acetate ion, formate, chloride ion and/or iodide ion. The precipitating agent comprising alkali metal, carbonate or bicarbonate of lithium, sodium, potassium, ammonium, sodium hydroxide and/or ammonium hydroxide.

A rotor blade and a side channel shape can be adapted to the gasses to be conveyed.

PN US2019352571-A1; WO2019221882-A1
TI Method for performing fluidized coking on feed, involves removing first portion of partially gasified solid particles from gasifier, and passing second portion of partially gasified solid particles from gasifier to reactor.
AU LE T
   RAICH B A
   DU B
   DUBOIS W E
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 201997400E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing an oxygen-containing stream and steam into a gasifier. A portion of the solid particles comprising deposited coke is passed from a reactor to the gasifier. The portion of the solid particles comprising deposited coke is exposed to gasification conditions in presence of a portion of the steam, oxygen from the oxygen-containing stream, and potassium carbonate, and calcium oxide to form partially gasified solid particles and a gas phase product comprising hertz, carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide. First portion of the partially gasified solid particles is removed from the gasifier. Second portion of the partially gasified solid particles is passed from the gasifier to the reactor.
   USE - Method for performing fluidized coking on a feed.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing or minimizing gasifier slag formation while providing a syngas-containing product. The method enables improving cycle length of an integrated coker and gasifier, thus reducing formation of a corrosive slag.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for performing fluidized coking.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a fluidized bed coking system including a coker, a heater, and a gasifier that performs coking and catalytic gasification.
   Reactor section (10)
   Heater section (11)
   Gasifier section (12)
   Lines (14-17, 21-26, 31)
   Heater cyclone system (27)

PN CN110464759-A
TI Composition used to prevent and treat diabetes            comprises radix bupleuri, radix astragali, smoked plum,            spina date seed, Desmodium, root of kudzu vine, rhizoma            coptidis, Magnolia officinalis, radix scutellariae,            liquorice, and raspberry.
AU ZHANG H
   RUI T
   SHI L
   DU R
AE UNIV JIANGSU AFFILIATED HOSPITAL (UYJS-C)
GA 2019990878
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises 10-15 pts. wt. radix                bupleuri, 8-12 pts. wt. radix astragali, 8-12 pts.                wt. smoked plum, 8-12 pts. wt. spina date seed, 5-8                pts. wt. Desmodium, 2-4 pts. wt. root of kudzu                vine, 3-5 pts. wt. rhizoma coptidis, 4-6 pts. wt.                Magnolia officinalis, 2-4 pts. wt. radix                scutellariae, 3-5 pts. wt. liquorice, and 4-6 pts.                wt. raspberry.
   USE - The composition is useful for preventing and                treating diabetes.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: is composed pure traditional                Chinese medicine components; and does not cause                side effects to the body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the composition comprising (a) mixing                radix astragali, spina date seed, Desmodium, root                of kudzu vine, rhizoma coptidis and liquorice,                cooking with water, extracting for three times,                combining the filtrates, and concentrating the                filtrates to a relative density of 1.20-1.40 to                obtain component A, (b) mixing radix bupleuri,                Magnolia officinalis, and radix scutellariae, and                using carbon dioxide supercritical extraction for                extracting the mixture and collecting the extract                to obtain component B, (c) mixing smoked plum and                raspberry, and crushing to 300-500 mesh to obtain                component C, and (d) adding component B and                component C to component A, mixing and drying at                50-55 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The composition                  comprises 12 pts. wt. radix bupleuri, 10 pts. wt.                  radix astragali, 10 pts. wt. smoked plum, 10 pts.                  wt. spina date seed, 6 pts. wt. Desmodium, 3 pts.                  wt. root of kudzu vine, 4 pts. wt. rhizoma                  coptidis, 4 pts. wt. Magnolia officinalis, 3 pts.                  wt. radix scutellariae, 4 pts. wt. liquorice, and                  5 pts. wt. raspberry. Preferred Method: The                  cooking in the step (a) is specifically carried                  out by adding water (10-15 times) to the                  component mixture, decocting for 1 hour,                  filtering, then adding water (15-20 times) to the                  filter residue, decocting for 2 hours, filtering,                  adding water (10-15 times) to the filter residue,                  decocting for 0.5 hour, filtering, collecting the                  filtrate for three times after filtering to                  obtain total filtrate. The supercritical                  extraction is carried out by (i) mixing radix                  bupleuri, Magnolia officinalis, and radix                  scutellariae having a water content of less than                  5%, and crushing to 100-200 mesh to obtain a                  medicine powder, (ii) mixing the medicine powder                  with cosolvent at a material-liquid ratio of                  1:2-3 g/ml, and mixing at room temperature for                  0.5-1 hours to obtain a wet powder, and (iii)                  adding the wet powder into supercritical carbon                  dioxide extraction kettle, adjusting the                  extraction temperature to 35-40 degrees C,                  extraction pressure to 18-22 MPa, carbon dioxide                  flow rate to 35-50 g/minute, and cosolvent flow                  rate to 1-1.5 g/minute, and extracting for 1-25                  hours, and collecting the extract at the outlet                  of the collection kettle to obtain component B.                  The step (iii) comprises extracting at 38 degrees                  C under the pressure of 20 MPa, carbon dioxide                  flow rate of 40 g/minute, and cosolvent flow rate                  of 1.2 g/minute for 2 hours. Preferred                  Components: The cosolvent is absolute                  ethanol.
   ACTIVITY - Antidiabetic.

The several ventilation holes are formed on the end faces of the catalytic cake and the heat storage cake. The catalytic cake and the heat storage cake are stacked and distributed at intervals. A fan (6) and a methanol nozzle (7) are connected to a bottom of a heat exchange barrel (1). The methanol nozzle sprays out atomized methanol, and is blown into the heat exchange barrel with the air stream to contact the catalytic cake. TI Field programmable gate array (FPGA) based grain depot monitoring system, has ZigBee cordinator that is provided to communicate with ZigBee terminal node module through ZigBee data transmission protocol respectively.
AU ZHANG R
   ZHOU Z
   SHI C
   LIU Y
AE UNIV EAST CHINA NORMAL (UYEN-C)
GA 2019973098
AB    NOVELTY - The system comprises a ZigBee networking communication module, a power voltage regulator module, an FPGA controller, a wireless fidelity (wifi) module, an air temperature and humidity sensor, smoke sensor, carbon dioxide concentration sensor, combustible gas sensor, infrared pyroelectric sensor and flame sensor. The ZigBee networking communication module has a ZigBee terminal node module and a ZigBee coordinator. The output end of the voltage regulator module is connected to power input end of FPGA controller. The output pins corresponding to Zigbee terminal node module are combined with an air temperature and humidity sensor, grain temperature and humidity transmitter, smoke sensor, the concentration sensor, combustible gas sensor, an infrared pyroelectric sensor, and a flame sensor. AE SCHAEFFLER TECHNOLOGIES AG & CO KG (ISCH-C)
GA 2019699869
AB    NOVELTY - The lever (10) has a rod-shaped permanent magnet (44) coaxially surrounded by a coil (46). A cup-shaped coupling element (42) is arranged in a direction of the coil to move the coupling element to a decoupling state (A) by applying a first actuating current to the coil. The coupling element is moved away from the coil to move the coupling element to a coupling state by applying second actuating current to the coil when the coil is de-energized. The decoupling state is maintained permanently by a first ferromagnetic holding element (74). The coupling state is maintained by a second ferromagnetic holding element (76).
   USE - Switchable drag lever for a valve train of an internal combustion engine for motor vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The coupling element is moved away from the coil to move the coupling element to the coupling state by applying the actuating current to the coil so as to reduce fuel consumption and carbon dioxide emissions of the lever and ensure reliable shifting of the drag lever.

PN CN110052274-A
TI Controlled synthesis method of multi-stage pore            metal oxide catalyst by adding bacterial cellulose pore            former to mixed metal salt solution, and changing            dosage ratio of bacterial cellulose and sugar            content.
AU DOU B
   ZHAO R
   HAO Q
   YAN N
   ZHAO C
   YANG Q
   ZHANG Q
AE UNIV TIANJIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYTC-C)
GA 2019670210
AB    NOVELTY - Controlled synthesis method of multi-stage                pore metal oxide catalyst comprises adding                bacterial cellulose pore former to mixed metal salt                solution by sol-gel method; and changing dosage                ratio of bacterial cellulose and sugar content to                adjust pore size distribution of oxide to obtain                multi-stage pore oxide catalyst.
   USE - The method is for controlled synthesis of                multi-stage pore metal oxide catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The bacterial cellulose and sugar used are                widely available, cheap and easy to obtain. They                are calcined and decomposed to produce carbon                dioxide and water via non-polluting production                process. The bacterial cellulose pore former can be                produced on large scale. It does not require                modification. The method has simple synthesis                process, low energy consumption, has simple steps,                no modification, realizes direct multi-stage pore                structure through roasting, and produces                multi-stage pore metal oxide catalyst with                adjustable pore size, which is beneficial to mass                transfer and diffusion of reactants and products,                and has large specific surface area. The                multi-stage pore metal oxide catalyst has pore size                distribution that can be regulated by changing                dosage ratio of bacterial cellulose and sugar                content, and simultaneously with existing                stenciling agents (microporous, mesoporous,                macroporous templating agent).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: The bacterial cellulose                  pore-forming agent is cultured or commercial                  Acetobacter xylinum, Achromobacter, aerobacteria,                  Agrobacterium, Alcaligenes, Acetobacter oxytocus,                  Acetobacter, Acetobacter pasteurianus, mixed                  solution of bacterial cellulose microorganism                  Gluconobacter cerinus, Agrobacterium tumfaciens,                  Rhizobium, Pseudomonas fluorescens, Sarcocystis,                  bacterial cellulose synthesized by microorganisms                  of genus Ganoderma lucidum or Campylobacter                  jejuni, or bacterial cellulose and oxalic acid,                  citric acid, and mixed solution of another                  organic solvent or organic solutions.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method preferably                  involves dissolving different metal salts at                  certain mol ratio in 40-70 ml solvent at 50-80                  degrees C to completely dissolved; homogenizing                  bacterial cellulose at 30-40 MPa to obtain                  uniform bacterial cellulose viscous liquid;                  adding bacterial cellulose viscous liquid (at                  different saccharide ratio) to metal salt                  solution to form sol; heating and stirring at                  60-90 degrees C for 2-5 hours to form viscous                  colloid; aging viscous colloid at room                  temperature for 0-48 hours; drying in oven at                  80-110 degrees C for 0-12 hours; and calcining at                  350-550 degrees C in air for 2-8 hours. Preferred                  Component: The metal salt solution comprises                  nitrate or acetate of copper, cobalt, iron, zinc,                  manganese, chromium, nickel, vanadium, niobium,                  tantalum, zirconium, hafnium, tantalum, other                  transition metals including sodium or potassium,                  or acetate or nitrate of main group dissolved in                  solution formed by solvent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The added sugar is                  glucose, fructose, sucrose, lactose, maltose,                  starch, and/or dextrin, and is added at 0-25% of                  bacterial cellulose.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN RU2694965-C1
TI APPARATUS FOR SIMULATING CONDITIONS FOR DECOMPOSITION OF COAL DRESSING SLUGS AND LOW-QUALITY COALS BY MICROBIOLOGICAL ACTION.
AU DYAGILEV D V
   SHAVRIN V A
AE UNIV KEMEROVO (UYKE-Non-standard)
GA 201971423X
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to biotechnology, in particular to equipment bioreactor system, which during operation provides modeling conditions for efficient production of liquid and gaseous products from coal-beneficiation sludge and low-quality coals with provision of preliminary treatment, which makes coal dressing slugs and low-quality coals susceptible to microbiological action. Apparatus for simulating conditions for decomposition of coal-beneficiation sludge and low-quality coals by microbiological exposure comprises a temperature-controlled housing with a reactor cover. There are an illumination ultraviolet lamp, temperature humidity sensors in housing. Plant has a reactor gas circuit, which includes a circulating pump, a receiver and a manifold with nozzles for mounting sensors, connection of a receiver, an inlet valve of gas sensors for gaseous hydrocarbons, methane, carbon dioxide, carbon oxide, hydrogen sulphide. There is a Baly tube in the reactor housing. Baly tube has a polymer mesh with a layer of cotton disc located above the bottom of the Baly tube for placing a sample on it. At that, the housing is made with the possibility of supplying to the Baly tube with the nutrient solution and solution containing microorganisms peristaltic pumps placed in it, from the bulbs with the specified speed. Lid has a window made of quartz glass, and above it a lighting ultraviolet lamp is installed.
   USE - Biotechnology.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention provides implementation of technical result consisting in improvement of efficiency of investigations.

PN CN110016216-A; US2020339804-A1; CN110016216-B
TI Polyglycolic acid composite packing material comprises polyglycolic acid, opening agent, slip agent, toughening agent, and water blocking agent.
AU AN J
AE RIBER CHINA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TEC (RIBE-Non-standard)
   REBERET CHINA CO LTD (REBE-Non-standard)
GA 2019641522
AB    NOVELTY - Polyglycolic acid composite packing material comprises 80-100 pts. wt. polyglycolic acid, 0.2-1 pts. wt. opening agent, 0.05-2 pts. wt. slip agent, 1-7 pts. wt. toughening agent, and 0.5-11 pts. wt. water blocking agent.
   USE - Used as polyglycolic acid composite packing material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: completely under goes degradable; has good microbial degradability and hydrolysis; decomposes into water and carbon dioxide; is environmentally friendly, safe, non-toxic; has no potential health threats to animals and humans, good mechanical properties; meets the various application needs of packaging materials. The method: adds inexpensive and environmentally friendly fillers without affecting mechanical properties; is economical and simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing polyglycolic acid composite packing material comprising (i) pulverizing polyglycolic acid, polycaprolactone and poly(L-lactide-co-caprolactone) to obtain particles having an average particle diameter of 50-100 mu m, and uniformly mixing to form a mixed main ingredient, (ii) adding opening agent, slip agent, toughening agent, water blocking agent, pentaerythritol diphosphite, chitosan, starch and reinforcing fibers, grinding, pulverizing, and uniformly mixing to form mixed auxiliary, and (iii) mixing the mixed main material and mixed auxiliary material uniformly, molding at 160-190 degrees C for 1-8 minutes at a pressure of 5-20 Mpa to obtain 0.3-4 mm thin plate, or performing extrusion at 180-220 degrees C through an extruder to form film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The material further comprises 45-60 pts. wt. polycaprolactone, 2-5 pts. wt. poly(L-lactide-co-caprolactone), 0.03-0.2 pts. wt. pentaerythritol diphosphite, 2-6 pts. wt. chitosan, 15-35 pts. wt. starch and 1-5 pts. wt. reinforcing fiber. Preferred Method: The method comprises performing ultrasonic treatment during molding and extruding process, ultrasonic processing power of 400-800W, and the frequency of 50-150 KHz. Preferred Components: The poly(L-lactide-co-caprolactone) has average molecular weight of 200000-500000, where the amounby of caprolactone is 20-25 wt.%. The opening agent comprises flake graphite, talc, diatomaceous earth or silica. The toughening agent is DuPont Biomax Strong (RTM: Ethylene copolymer designed to modify polylactic acid) or composite toughening agent comprises nanocalcite, nano talc and subnano fatty acid rare earth salt in mass ratio of 30-50:10-20:1-5. The water-blocking agent comprises animal water-blocking agent or a plant-based water-blocking agent, where the animal water-blocking agent comprising beeswax, tallow, whale oil, wool wax or spermaceti, and the plant water-blocking agent comprising palm wax, peanut oil, castor wax, palmitic acid, soybean oil, epoxy soybean oil, bayberry wax, jojoba oil or hydrogenated vegetable oil. The slip agent comprises oleic acid amide, stearic acid amide or erucamide. The reinforcing fiber comprises wood fiber, hemp fiber, cotton fiber or bamboo fiber.

PT P
PN CN109970056-A; CN109970056-B
TI Preparing biomass-based ordered microporous carbon material used as electrode material for supercapacitor, involves mixing biomass particles and iron(III) chloride powder, microwave reacting, activating, stirring with hydrochloric acid.
AU LIU D
   SU R
   ZHAO X
   HAO Z
   XU B
   GAO W
   YAN X
   LIU L
   LIANG D
AE UNIV JILIN AGRIC (UJIL-C)
GA 201960528G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing biomass-based ordered microporous carbon material, involves (1) pulverizing and sieving the biomass, performing deliming treatment, (2) mixing obtained biomass particles and iron(III) chloride powder, magnetically stirring at 60-80 degrees C for 200-300 rpm, under the liquid immersion, magnetically stirring until the water is completely evaporated, (3) placing obtained mixed particles in a quartz reactor, reacting at microwave input power of 1000 W and frequency of 2450 MHz, controlling nitrogen flow rate at 100 ml/minute, pyrolyzing in a tube furnace at a temperature increase rate of 8-10 degrees C/minute and under nitrogen atmosphere at a rate of 300-400 ml/minute, insulating for 40-60 minutes, when the pyrolysis temperature reaches 800-900 degrees C to obtain the precursor, (4) stop introducing nitrogen, activating to 800-900 degrees C at a rate of 300-400 ml/minute under carbon dioxide atmosphere, magnetically stirring with hydrochloric acid and washing to neutral with deionized water.
   USE - Preparing biomass-based ordered microporous carbon material used as electrode material for supercapacitor (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical preparation of biomass-based ordered microporous carbon material with low porosity, high specific surface area, highly ordered carbon framework, high mass transfer, high conductivity, and prepares supercapacitor with excellent capacitance performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The biomass is agricultural and forestry waste. The agricultural and forestry waste is selected from straw, rice husk, fuelwood, bark or peanut shell.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The biomass particles have a particle size of 250-380 mu m. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of iron(III) chloride powder to biomass particles is 1:10, 2:10 or 3:10. The concentration of hydrochloric acid is 0.2 mol/L. Preferred Conditions: The microwave irradiation process is carried out for 1.5, 5 or 30 minutes. The activation to burnout rate is 16.3%, 29.4% or 41.4%. The step (4) comprises magnetically stirring at a rate of 300 rpm.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); V01 (Resistors and Capacitors)

PN WO2019053484-A1; US2020217976-A1; EP3685190-A1; BR112020005265-A2; EP3685190-B1; US11320550-B2
TI Method for processing a four-dimensional (4D) seismic signal relative to a subsoil which includes a zone subject to extraction and/or injection, by processing 4D seismic signal based on determined noise model of 4D seismic signal.
AU HUBANS C
   SHCHUKINA N
AE TOTAL SA (PETF-C)
   TOTAL SA (PETF-C)
   TOTAL SA (PETF-C)
   TOTAL ENERGIES SE (PETF-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
GA 201926160K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves identifying a portion of the 4D seismic signal corresponding to a zone of the subsoil distinct from the zone subject to extraction and/or injection. The method also involves determining a noise model of the 4D seismic signal based on the identified portion of the 4D seismic signal, and processing the 4D seismic signal based on the noise model.
   USE - Method for processing a four-dimensional (4D) seismic signal relative to a subsoil which includes a zone subject to extraction and/or injection, such as a zone subject to production of oil and/or gas or a zone subject to injection of carbon dioxide and/or of drill cuttings.
   ADVANTAGE - A relatively accurate noise model can be determined by determining noise model based on a zone of the subsoil distinct from the zone subject to extraction and/or injection. The seismic acquisition area coverage can be kept relatively small, for example restrained to the area corresponding to the zone subject to production plus the siding area corresponding to the input zone.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a client computer system.
   Internal communication bus (1000)
   Network adapter (1050)
   Haptic device (1090)
   Video RAM (1100)
   Graphics processing unit (1110)

TI Composite structure SSZ-13-silicalite-1 molecular            sieve useful in removing nitrogen oxide in            ammonia-selective catalytic reduction reaction,            methanol to olefin reaction and selective adsorption            comprises a peripheral area and a central area.
AU SONG X
   AI L
   JIAO Y
   PAN G
AE SHANDONG SINOCERA FUNCTIONAL MATERIAL CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201922703J
AB    NOVELTY - Composite structure SSZ-13-silicalite-1                molecular sieve comprises a peripheral area and a                central area, where the central area of the silicon                species and aluminum species are uniformly                distributed, the peripheral area with a silicon                species but without aluminum species, preferably,                calculated by XRD characteristic peak area, the                central area and the peripheral area ratio is                (4-9):1.
   USE - The SSZ-13-silicalite-1 molecular sieve is                useful in removing nitrogen oxide in                ammonia-selective catalytic reduction reaction,                methanol to olefin reaction and selective                adsorption and separation process of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The molecular sieve has wider crystallization                phase, improved hydrothermal stability, exhibits                excellent catalytic performance in                ammonia-selective catalytic reduction reaction,                especially after hydrothermal treatment, still                keeps good catalytic performance. The method is                simple and reliable and easy to realize.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing composite structure                SSZ-13-silicalite-1 molecular sieve comprising                mixing silicon source, template agent, water and                SSZ-13 molecular sieve raw powder according to the                ratio, mixing uniformly to obtain the starting gel,                and crystallizing; and
   (2) copper-SSZ-13 silicalite-1 molecular                sieve, obtained by using                SSZ-13-silicalite-1-molecular sieve for copper                source ion exchange, then calcining.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mol ratio of                  silicon source, template agent and water is                  1:(0.05-0.15):(10-40), preferably                  1:(0.08-0.12):(15-30). Silicon source is solid                  silica gel, white carbon black, ethyl                  orthosilicate, sodium silicate and/or silica sol;                  the template agent is tetrapropyl ammonium                  hydroxide, tetrapropyl ammonium bromide,                  n-butylamine, ethanol, triethylamine and/or                  quadrol. When preparing the initial gel, further                  adding silicalite-1 molecular sieve crystal seed                  and/or alkali source, and/or the silicon source,                  alkali source, the mol ratio of template agent                  and water is 1:(0.05-0.12):(0.05-0.15):(10-40),                  preferably 1:(0.08-0.10):(0.08-0.12):(15-30);                  and/or, the mass ratio of the silicalite-1                  molecular sieve seed crystal to the silicon                  source is (0.01-0.10):1, preferably                  (0.01-0.05):1. Preferred Method: The                  crystallization temperature is 100-200 degrees C,                  preferably 140-180 degrees C. After the                  crystallization is finished, after-treating the                  obtained product, comprising a solid-liquid                  separating, washing, drying and calcining,                  preferably, the calcining temperature is 550-650                  degrees C. Copper source is copper sulfate,                  copper nitrate or copper acetate, and/or the ion                  exchange temperature is 35-85 degrees C, and/or                  the pH of the working liquid of the ion exchange                  is 3.5-6.5.
