PN CN109329275-A
TI Preserving Chinese medicinal decoction pieces by drying medicinal pieces, irradiating with cobalt-60-gamma rays, vacuum-sealing treated pieces and irradiating again and sealing pieces in container comprising e.g. nitrogen and oxygen.
AU GUO Z
   XIONG Q
   WANG Z
AE JIANGXI ZHIHENG HALL CHINESE HERBAL MEDI (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201917923Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preserving a traditional Chinese medicinal decoction pieces involves (a) drying the medicinal pieces, stacking vertically, packing pieces in a carton, irradiating with cobalt-60- gamma rays at 1-1.5 kGy, inserting a rotating shaft in the middle of the carton and rotating the shaft perpendicularly to a line connecting a center of an irradiating source and a center of the rotating shaft, where (i) the vertical stacking thickness of the medicinal pieces is equal to thickness of the dose area, (ii) the dose area thickness is determined by distance between the center of the rotating shaft and the center of the irradiation source, and (iii) when the distance is farther, the thickness of the dose area becomes larger, (b) vacuum-sealing the treated pieces, performing a secondary irradiation treatment at a dose of 2-2.5 kGy, and (c) putting the irradiated pieces in a closed container filled with nitrogen, 72% carbon dioxide gas, 25% oxygen and 3% mixed gas, sealing and storing.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving a traditional Chinese medicinal decoction pieces.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts auto-transmission in the dose area to compensate the upper and lower surfaces to ensure uniformity and improves preservation effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves drying the pieces until to reach a water content of 4-7%, carrying out irradiation treatment under high pressure environment for 0.5-6 hours, and rotating the shaft at a speed of 5-10 rpm. The step (b) involves irradiation treatment performed in a same manned as performed in the step (a) for 0.5-4 hours. The dose area thickness is calculated by Monte Carlo method using a Monte Carlo N-Particle (MCNP) program.

GA 2018A3936J

The pipeline is guided to near the bottom of the cavern, so that above the cavern forms a collecting space for gas, in which the vent line opens. TI Method for purifying and complete burning coal in            coal-fired electrical utility boilers, involves            providing oxygen to mix with gasified form of fluidized            hydrocarbons, and igniting mixed hydrocarbons and            oxygen to achieve complete burning.
AU SU S
   GONG Y
AE SU S (SUSS-Individual)
   SU S (SUSS-Individual)
   SU S (SUSS-Individual)
   GONG Y (GONG-Individual)
GA 2018913230
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves heating a coal in a solid                form to a gasified form without air including                various hydrocarbons and harmful substances to a                predetermined range of temperature in a coal                gasifier (110). A reaction chamber is provided with                oxygen and connected with the coal gasifier. The                harmful substances are separated from the                hydrocarbons. The gasified coal is condensed from                the reaction chamber to collect the hydrocarbons in                a fluidized phase. The fluidized hydrocarbons are                heated to become a gasified form. The oxygen is                provided to mix with the gasified form of the                fluidized hydrocarbons. Mixed hydrocarbons and the                oxygen are ignited to achieve complete                burning.
   USE - Method for purifying and complete burning a                coal in coal-fired electrical utility boilers for                generating electricity for residential and                commercial applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables performing coal gasified                process so as to avoid producing the harmful                substances in exhausted gas, thus gasifying and                burning the coal, hence releasing less carbon                dioxide. The method enables separating the mixture                by fractional distillation through the                hydrocarbons, so that harmful hydrosulfate acid and                hydro-phosphoric acid are eliminated in a                separation zone.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a coal                purification and gasification system.
   110Coal gasifier
   111Air inlet
   113Outlet pipe Gas filter
   114Coal inlet
   116Motor

TI Cultivating Morchella in vegetable greenhouse by            culturing Morchella hyphae and fruiting body,            intercropping with wheat, furrowing soil, opening            drainage ditch, spreading culture material, evenly            sowing Morchella and spraying water.
AU FU D
   WU L
AE JIANGSU XINYE ECOLOGICAL AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018933815
AB    NOVELTY - Method for cultivating Morchella in vegetable                greenhouse involves culturing Morchella hyphae at                3-28 degrees C and humidity of 30-70%, preferably                not greater than 70% under darkness or low light                conditions and suitable ventilation, culturing the                Morchella fruiting body at 10-22 degrees C and                humidity of 75-95%, preferably 80-90% under                illumination and not greater than 0.3% carbon                dioxide in the mid-late October, intercropping with                plants such as wheat to improve yield, covering the                greenhouse with shade net, furrowing the soil such                that the furrow has width of 100 cm and depth of 15                cm, where the length depends on the terrain and                distance between the furrow is 30-50 cm, opening a                drainage ditch around the furrow, spreading 1 cm                culture material layer on the furrow, crushing and                evenly sowing the Morchella, covering with 2 cm                thick soil and spraying water, covering with                bacteriostatic cream, where placing 1200 Morchella                cultured bags/acre.
   USE - The method is useful for planting Morchella in                a vegetable greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilize the factors of the                adaptation of the Morchellato adapt to the                environment during the idle period of the                greenhouse and increases the mass production of                Morchella to promote the agricultural                development.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  culturing the hyphae at 18-22 degrees C and                  humidity of 60-65% and fruiting body at 15-18                  degrees C.

TI Ceria- and zirconia-supported vanadium oxide            catalyst for dehydrating methanol into dimethyl ether            is prepared by calcining solution of ceria powder,            zirconia powder, vanadium compound, and water,            pelletizing, and grinding calcined powder.
AU INOKAWA H
   ZAMAN S F
   DAOUS M A
   AL-ZAHRANI A
   PETROV L
AE UNIV KING ABDULAZIZ (UYKI-Non-standard)
GA 201888222V
AB    NOVELTY - A ceria (CeO2)- and zirconia (ZrO2)-supported                vanadium oxide catalyst comprises 0.1-10 wt.%                vanadium, 65-80 wt.% CeO2, and 20-32 wt.% ZrO2,                where catalyst is in the form of microparticles                with diameters of 50-200 mu m.
   USE - A ceria (CeO2)- and zirconia (ZrO2)-supported                vanadium oxide catalyst for dehydrating methanol                into dimethyl ether (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst reaction may enable the methanol                oxidative reaction to continue at a stable reaction                rate for hours.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) production of CeO2- and ZrO2-supported                vanadium oxide catalyst which involves mixing CeO2                powder, ZrO2 powder, vanadium compound, and water                to form solution, drying the solution to produce a                dried catalyst precursor, heating the dried                catalyst precursor at 450-700 degrees C for 2-8                hours to produce calcined powder, pelletizing, and                grinding calcined powder to produce CeO2- and                ZrO2-supported vanadium oxide catalyst; and
   (2) dehydration of methanol into dimethyl                ether which involves feeding gas stream comprising                oxygen (O2) and methanol to fixed bed flow reactor                comprising the catalyst at 200-300 degrees C to                produce dimethyl ether and greater than or equal to                1 side product selected from hydrogen gas, carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide, water, and/or                formaldehyde.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The catalyst has                  surface area of 80-150 m2/g. Preferred                  Compositions: The catalyst has Ce:Zr molar ratio                  of 1.4:1-3.5:1. Vanadium oxide is vanadium                  pentoxide (V2O5). The solution has vanadium                  concentration of 0.05-1 M. Vanadium compound is                  selected from ammonium metavanadate, potassium                  metavanadate, sodium metavanadate, bismuth                  vanadate, vanadium oxytrichloride, vanadium                  pentafluoride, or vanadium oxide, preferably                  ammonium metavanadate. Gas stream has O2:methanol                  molar ratio of 0.2:1-0.6:1. Preferred Condition:                  Gas stream is fed to fixed bed flow reactor at a                  gas hourly space velocity of                  25000-35000/hour.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: Selectivity percentage                  for dimethyl ether from conversion of methanol is                  at greater than or equal to 90%. Percentage yield                  of dimethyl ether, relative to a mol.% of                  methanol that is converted, is greater than or                  equal to 30%.

PN CN108786392-A
TI Removing mercury from an oxyfuel-burning flue gas and a mercury-containing compound comprises e.g. purging fuel with pure oxygen, recycling partially burning flue gas, and subjecting recycled flue gas for low temperature plasma treatment.
AU WEI D
   YANG X
   HAN J
   YU F
   QIN L
AE GUANGZHOU HUARUN THERMOELECTRICITY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV WUHAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UWSC-C)
GA 201893355Q
AB    NOVELTY - Removing mercury from an oxyfuel-burning flue gas and a mercury-containing compound comprises (i) purging fuel with pure oxygen, recycling partially burning flue gas, where flue gas comprises 73-97 vol.% carbon dioxide, 1-26 vol.% water, 1-5 vol.% oxygen, and 0.02-0.05 vol.% hydrochloric acid, (ii) subjecting recycled flue gas for low temperature plasma treatment with activated carbon powder in plasma reactor into highly active atomic or ionic state, and forming active functional group on the activated carbon to realize adsorption of combustion fumes mercury, (iii) forming fluidized bed of mercury removal sorbent, delivering mercury removal adsorbent to the mercury-containing combustion flue gas, attaching to the flue gas filtering device, forming fluidized bed to collect and remove mercury from combustion flue gas, and (iv) renewing adsorbent on the fluidized bed and updating mercury removal adsorbent on the filtration unit after every 120 minutes.
   USE - The method is useful for removing mercury from an oxyfuel-burning flue gas and a mercury-containing compound.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMA are also included for:
   (1) preparing combustion flue gas mercury removal adsorbent comprising providing the combustion flue gas, activated carbon powder, mixing before entering plasma reactor to form mercury removal adsorbent; and
   (2) mercury removal sorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The flue gas comprises 95-97 vol.% carbon dioxide, 1-3 vol.% water, 1-2 vol.% oxygen, 0.1-0.25 vol.% sulfur oxide and 0.02-0.05 vol.% hydrochloric acid. The combustion flue gas further comprises fly ash having 150-300 mu m particle size and 0.35-0.45 mg/m3. Preferred Method: The temperature of the combustion fumes is 100-300 degrees C. In the step (ii), the amount of activated carbon powder is 1-2 g/m3. The reaction conditions of the combustion flue gas and activated carbon powder in the plasma reactor are: the output voltage is 10-40 kV preferably 30-35 kV, the frequency is 5 kHz, and residence time is 0.05-0.1 seconds.

TI Non-metallic porous organic composition used for separating gases, comprises core and shell non-chemically bonded to core, both comprising nitrogen-containing molecules where at least one of molecules in core differ from molecules in shell.
AU DU Y
   CORCORAN E W
   COOPER A I
   HASELL T M
   JIANG S
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 201848518Y
AB    NOVELTY - A non-metallic porous organic composition comprises a core comprising nitrogen-containing molecules, and a shell comprising nitrogen-containing molecules provided around the core and being non-chemically bonded to the core. At least one of the core nitrogen-containing molecules is different from at least one of the shell nitrogen-containing molecules.
   USE - Non-metallic porous organic composition used for separating gases to remove contaminants e.g. nitrogen, hydrogen, carbon dioxide, methane, xenon and krypton, from gas mixture (all claimed) e.g. natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The non-metallic porous organic composition has excellent selectivity, capacity and hydrophobicity, and can be efficiently synthesized in core-shell configuration. The nitrogen-containing molecules are soluble in solvents, and can efficiently form shell and/or core/shell interface with no cracks and hence provides the composition with improved selectivity and/or resistance to fouling when used in gas separation processes in order to provide gases with excellent purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of composition, which involves sequentially adding solution of the nitrogen-containing molecules and a solvent to the core molecules to create a product, and evaporating the product; and
   (2) gas separation, which involves contacting gas mixture comprising contaminant(s) with the composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrogen-containing molecules comprise one or more moieties chosen from moiety of formulae (I)-(IV). The core and/or the shell comprise a combination of nitrogen-containing molecules comprising R enantiomer, S enantiomer, or racemate. The core comprises racemic moiety (I) in which R1-R6 are group (iii) and R7-R10 are hydrogen and the shell comprises racemic moiety (I) in which R1-R6 are group (iii) and R7-R10 are hydroxy, the core comprises racemic moiety (I) in which R1-R6 are group (iii) and R7-R10 are hydroxy and the shell comprises racemic moiety (I) in which R1-R6 are group (iii) and R7-R10 are hydrogen, the core comprises racemic moiety (I) in which R1-R6 are group (iii) and R7-R10 are hydrogen and the shell comprises racemic moiety (II) in which R1-R6 are group (iii) and R7-R10 are hydrogen, or the core comprises racemic moiety (II) in which R1-R6 are group (iii) and R7-R10 are hydrogen and the shell comprises racemic moiety (I) in which R1-R6 are group (iii) and R7-R10 are hydrogen.R1-R6 = group chosen from groups of formulae (i)-(iv), preferably group chosen from group (ii)-(iv) and groups of formulae (v)-(ix);Z1-Z4 = H, 1-4C alkyl, or optionally substituted aryl;R7-R10 = H or hydroxy;R11-R18 = H;andor R11+R12,R13+R14,R15+R16,R17+R18 = optionally substituted 1-4C alkylene.(i) In moiety (II) or (IV), R1-R6 are group (ii) or (iii) and in moiety (III), R5 and R6 are group (iii). Preferred Properties: The shell and/or core/shell interface has no cracks. The composition has adsorptive loading ratio of carbon dioxide to methane of 1.2 or more. The nitrogen-containing molecules have an octahedral, spherical, or tetragonal shape. The composition is in the form of particles having average particle diameter of 10 nm-10000 mu m. The weight ratio of core to shell is 1:10-1:2. Preferred Process: The process includes pressure swing adsorption, temperature swing adsorption, partial pressure swing or displacement purge adsorption, partial temperature swing adsorption, rapid cycle pressure swing adsorption, rapid cycle thermal swing adsorption, rapid cycle partial pressure swing or displacement purge adsorption or rapid cycle partial temperature swing adsorption.

PT P
PN RU2652212-C1
TI Hollow fiber membrane used in gas separation devices with increased strength.
AU KULINICH M YU
AE TEKON MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGIES CO LTD (TEKO-Non-standard)
GA 201841972R
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to a technology for producing hollow fibers based on polymers as well as membranes obtained from such hollow fibers that can be used in gas separation devices for separating, purifying and concentrating gas mixtures, in particular, containing helium, hydrogen, hydrogen sulphide, mercaptans, carbon dioxide and/or hydrocarbons. Hollow fiber membrane is obtained by passing a molding solution containing from 21 to 35 wt. % of a polyetherimide, 40 to 65 wt. % of a solvent and from 1 to 25 wt. % of a non-solvent, through a spinneret with simultaneous feeding into the internal cavity of an inner coagulant membrane to form a hollow fiber, and on the external surface - external coagulant. Membrane is passed through a bath with a coagulant and a washing bath, after which the membrane is wound onto a receiving wheel rotating at a linear fiber reception rate of 3 to 60 m/min, then dried and a hollow fiber membrane with an anisotropic fiber structure with an outer diameter is received from 90 to 800 microns, the internal fiber diameter from 30 to 400 microns, wall thickness from 30 to 385 microns, volume porosity from 30 to 80 percent. Hollow fiber membrane is designed to operate at a gas pressure of up to 120 atm and a temperature of up to 80 degrees C.
   USE - Natural and chemical threads and fibers.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention makes it possible to obtain a hollow fiber membrane with increased strength.
3 cl, 3 dwg

   ADVANTAGE - The pyrolysis gas generated from the waste clay pyrolysis furnace is entered into calciner for providing the high temperature conditions required for calcium-based absorbent regeneration. The operating cost of reduction system is reduced. The deactivated calcium-based absorbent discharged from the calciner is served as raw material for production of cement. Hence, the emission of carbon dioxide from the cement plant is reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon emission reduction system.
   Carbonation furnace (1)
   Calciner (2)
   Rotary kiln (5)
   Cooler (6)
   Pulverizer (7)
   Cyclone portions (8-1,8-2)
   Heat exchangers (9-1,9-2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The absorbent comprises 85 %mass calcium carbonate, 5 %mass pyrolytic clay residue and 10 %mass cement.

PN US2018088462-A1
TI Composition used in manufacture of sub-micron architectural material used in device, comprises hybrid organic-inorganic polymer resin comprising metal-containing photopolymerizable functional group.
AU VYATSKIKH A
   DELALANDE S J
   GREER J R
AE CALIFORNIA INST OF TECHNOLOGY (CALY-C)
GA 2018246682
AB    NOVELTY - A composition comprises hybrid organic-inorganic polymer resin comprising metal-containing photopolymerizable functional groups (I).
   USE - Composition used in manufacture of sub-micron architectural material used in device e.g. electrode, photocell, filter, circuit, water purification device and nanocage (all claimed). Can also be used in photocatalytic reactors for household water disinfection, applications in photocatalytic hydrogen production, carbon dioxide conversion, and tissue engineering.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition produces complex three-dimensional structures with sub-micron architecture, by safe, efficient and economical process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A composition comprises a hybrid organic-inorganic polymer resin comprising metal-containing photopolymerizable functional group of formula: M(power(n+))(-R'OC-R)n (I).
   M=metal, metal ion, metalloid, metal alloy, metal oxide, metal nitride, inorganic, inorganic-organic hybrid and/or metal-inorganic composite material;
   R=2-10C terminal alkene;
   R'=N, O, F, S or Cl;and
   n=1, 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of sub-micron architectural material, which involves patterning the composition in the presence of a photoinitiator using single or two photon lithography technique to polymerize the polymer resin and generate the sub-micron architectural material having desired characteristic dimension of about 5 nm to 5 micron; and
   (2) device, which comprises the sub-micron architectural material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic component of the polymeric resin is chosen from titanium dioxide, aluminum oxide, zirconium dioxide, silicon carbide, silicon dioxide, cerium (IV) oxide, and zinc oxide. The composition comprises a photoinitiator or a photoinitiator and a monomer capable of forming a polymer with the hybrid organic-inorganic polymer. The sub-micron architectural material comprises a metal, a metalloid and/or an inorganic structure. The device comprises titania. The hybrid organic-inorganic polymer resin preferably of formula: R-COR'-M2+R'OC-R (I). Preferred Properties: The inorganic structure having a dimension across an axis of a metal, a metalloid and/or a inorganic strut, beam or joint of less than 1 micron.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The method further involves removing non-polymerized resin, and pyrolyzing the sub-micron architectural material to remove organic material. The pyrolyzing comprises a two-step pyrolysis technique to remove organic material followed by removing oxygen.
   Preferred Definitions:
   M = Li+, Na+, K+, Rb+, Cs+, Be2+, Mg2+, Ca2+, Sr2+, Ba2+, Sc3+, Sc2+, Sc+, Y3+, Y2+, Y+, Ti4+, Ti3+, Ti2+, Zr4+, Zr3+, Zr2+, Hf4+, Hf3+, V5+, V4+, V3+, V2+, Nb5+, Nb4+, Nb3+, Nb2+, Ta5+, Ta4+, Ta3+, Ta2+, Cr6+, Cr5+, Cr4+, Cr3+, Cr2+, Cr+, Cr, Mo6+, Mo5+, Mo4+, Mo3+, Mo2+, Mo+, Mo, W6+, W5+, W4+, W3+, W2+, W+, W, Mn7+, Mn6+, Mn5+, Mn4+, Mn3+, Mn2+, Mn+, Re7+, Re6+, Re5+, Re4+, Re3+, Re2+, Re+, Re, Fe6+, Fe4+, Fe3+, Fe2+, Fe+, Fe, Ru8+, Ru7+, Ru6+, Ru4+, Ru3+, Ru2+, Os8+, Os7+, Os6+, Os5+, Os4+, Os3+, Os2+, Os+, Os, Co5+, Co4+, Co3+, Co2+, Co+, Rh6+, Rh5+, Rh4+, Rh3+, Rh2+, Rh+, Ir6+, Ir5+, Ir4+, Ir3+, Ir2+, Ir+, Ir, Ni3+, Ni2+, Ni+, Ni, Pd6+, Pd4+, Pd2+, Pd+, Pd, Pt6+, Pt5+, Pt4+, Pt3+, Pt2+, Pt+, Cu4+, Cu3+, Cu2+, Cu+, Ag3+, Ag2+, Ag+, Au5+, Au4+, Au3+, Au2+, Au+, Zn2+, Zn+, Zn, Cd2+, Cd+, Hg4+, Hg2+, Hg+, B3+, B2+, B+, Al3+, Al2+, Al+, Ga3+, Ga2+, Ga+, In3+, In2+, In+, Tl3+, Tl+, Si4+, Si+, Si2+, Si+, Ge4+, Ge3+, Ge2+, Ge+, Ge, Sn4+, Sn2+, Pb4+, Pb2+, As5+, As3+, As2+, As+, Sb5+, Sb3+, Bi5+, Bi3+, Te6+, Te5+, Te4+, Te2+, La+, La2+, Ce4+, Ce3+, Ce2+, Pr4+, Pr3+, Pr2+, Nd3+, Nd2+, Sm3+, Sm2+, Eu3+, Eu2+, Gd3+, Gd2+, Gd+, Tb4+, Tb3+, Tb2+, Tb+, Db3+, Db2+, Ho3+, Er3+, Tm4+, Tm3+, Tm2+, Yb3+, Yb2+, and/or Lu3+, Au-Ni-TiO2, Ni-Co-TiO2, Ni-Zn-Al2O3, and/or Ni-B-TiO2.

PN CN107693679-A
TI Formulation for treating psoriasis, comprises fungus strain and traditional Chinese medicine extracting solution comprising fleece-flower root, radix rehmanniae, Rehmannia root, radix asparagi, Liriope spicata, medlar and Angelica.
AU LEI Z
AE HEBEI ZHENRUI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 201816104P
AB    NOVELTY - Formulation comprises 70-80 pts. wt. traditional Chinese medicine extracting solution, 20-30 pts. wt. fungus strain, where traditional Chinese medicine extracting solution comprises 4-16 pts. wt. fleece-flower root, 3-11 pts. wt. radix rehmanniae, 2-8 pts. wt. prepared Rehmannia root, 3-11 pts. wt. radix asparagi, 3-15 pts. wt. Liriope spicata, 2-8 pts. wt. medlar, 4-14 pts. wt. glossy privet fruit, 3-15 pts. wt. Schisandra berry, 2-14 pts. wt. Achyranthes, 4-16 pts. wt. Angelica and 3-15 pts. wt. black bean.
   USE - Formulation for treating psoriasis (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing formulation, which involves:
   (A) taking fleece-flower root, radix rehmanniae, prepared rehmannia root, radix asparagi, Liriope spicata, medlar, glossy privet fruit, Schisandra berry, Achyranthes, Angelica and black bean;
   (B) removing impurities by rinsing;
   (C) performing rough crushing and fine crushing of traditional Chinese medicine;
   (D) grinding;
   (E) performing ultrasonic processing;
   (F) performing low-temperature carbon dioxide supercritical extraction to obtain traditional Chinese medicine extracting solution;
   (G) preparing fungus strain by adding bacteria flora, Saccharomycetes flora strain and lactic acid bacteria strain for fusion to obtain clarifying black tea fungus strain; and
   (H) putting traditional Chinese medicine extracting liquid with fungus strain mixed fermentation to obtain the desired formulation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Compositions: Formulation comprises 75 pts. wt. traditional Chinese medicine extracting solution, 25 pts. wt. fungus strain, where traditional Chinese medicine extracting solution comprises 10 pts. wt. fleece-flower root, 8 pts. wt. radix rehmanniae, 7 pts. wt. prepared Rehmannia root, 9 pts. wt. radix asparagi, 12 pts. wt. Liriope spicata, 6 pts. wt. medlar, 9 pts. wt. glossy privet fruit, 12 pts. wt. Schisandra berry, 8 pts. wt. Achyranthes, 10 pts. wt. Angelica and 9 pts. wt. black bean. The culture medium while preparing acetic acid bacterial strain comprises 1 g yeast extract, 1 g glucose and 100 ml water. The culture medium while preparing microzyme bacterial strain comprises 1 g yeast extract, 1 g bacteria peptone, 0.5 g sodium chloride and 100 ml water. The culture medium while preparing lactic acid bacterial strain comprises 1 g beef extract, 1 g yeast extract, 1 g peptone, 2 g glucose, 0.5 g sodium acetate, 0.005 g magnesium sulfate and 100 ml water. The culturing liquid also comprises 90 g white granulated sugar, 10 g tea and 900 ml water. Preferred Process: The fungus strain is prepared by selecting Acetobacter xylinum, Acetobacter, Gluconobacter oxydans, ketone-producing Acetobacter, weak oxidizing bacteria, glucose Acetobacter, Acetobacter aceti and strain of Acetobacter pasteurianus, placing in culture medium to culture to obtain the 8 strain of acetic bacteria, preparing microzyme bacterial strain by selecting Saccharomyces cerevisiae, non-displaying yeast, lude yeast, Schizosaccharomyces pombe, Candida tropicalis, Candida krusei, Debaryomyces yeast, wine yeast and kloeckera yeast, taking all strains in culture medium to culture to obtain microzyme group, preparing lactic acid bacterial strain by selecting Lactobacillus bulgaricus and other strains of lactic acid bacteria, putting in culture medium to obtain 4 tube strain of lactic acid bacteria, inoculating 8 strain of bacteria flora, 4 strains of microzyme group, 10 strains of lactic acid bacteria on sterile super clean operating table access in nutrient solution and ventilating culture and static culture to obtain fungus strain. The lactic acid bacterial group strain and yeast strain are firstly added to fusion culture and lactic acid bacterial group strain is added after magnetic stirring for 48 hours and then added to culture fluid. Preferred Conditions: The time for ventilating culture is 48 hours and static culturing time is 72 hours in culture liquid.
   ACTIVITY - Antipsoriatic.

PN US2018044157-A1; WO2018031844-A1; US9919909-B2; EP3497334-A1; CN109844317-A; HK40007876-A0; CN109844317-B
TI Pump and controller system for post-mix beverage dispenser system, has micro controller to stop pump motor when pressure falls below specific minimum pressure level and remains below minimum pressure level for specific time interval.
AU COHEN A
   KIMBERLIN R R
AE COHEN A (COHE-Individual)
   KIMBERLIN R R (KIMB-Individual)
   STANDEX INT CORP (STAN-Non-standard)
   ARBEL AGENCIES LTD (ARBE-Non-standard)
   COHEN A (COHE-Individual)
   KIMBERLIN R R (KIMB-Individual)
   STANDEX INT CORP (STAN-Non-standard)
   ARBEL AGENCIES LTD (ARBE-Non-standard)
   ARBEL AGENCIES LTD (ARBE-Non-standard)
GA 201813556U
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a pump housing (12) comprising an internal pumping chamber, an inlet port (14), an outlet port (16), and a sensor port, and the ports are in communication with pumping chamber. A programmable micro controller is capable of starting and stopping a pump motor (22). The micro controller is programmed to immediately stop the pump motor when the pressure exceeds predetermined maximum pressure level and, falls below predetermined minimum pressure level and remains below minimum pressure level for a predetermined time interval. The pressure transducer comprises a ceramic piezo disc.
   USE - Pump and controller system for post-mix beverage dispenser system (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The problem of syrup flavors getting absorbed by the rubber components and subsequently leaching out into other beverage syrups is eliminated. The significant confined space asphyxiation hazard presented by carbon dioxide leaks is eliminated. The drive shaft is magnetically coupled to the pumping mechanism, thus eliminating the need for an additional seal. The pump is prevented from running for an extended time in a low pressure condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a post-mix beverage dispenser.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front perspective view of a pump and controller system.
   Pump housing (12)
   Inlet port (14)
   Outlet port (16)
   Pump motor (22)

TI Method for treating substrate, involves supplying mixture liquid, removing mixture liquid from substrate by supplying supercritical fluid to substrate after supplying of mixture liquid, and dissolving mixture liquid in supercritical fluid.
AU KIM H
   LEE Y H
   JIN X
   LEE Y
AE SEMES CO LTD (SMES-C)
   SEMES CO LTD (SMES-C)
GA 201809092T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves supplying mixture liquid obtained by mixing an additive with an organic solvent onto a substrate. The mixture liquid is removed from the substrate by supplying supercritical fluid to the substrate after the supplying of the mixture liquid, and the mixture liquid is dissolved in the supercritical fluid, where an additive has a surface tension that is lower than that of the organic solvent and a boiling point that is lower than that of the organic solvent and a solubility of the additive for the organic solvent is higher than that of hexane.
   USE - Method for treating a substrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures that initially supplied fluid can be prevented from being supplied to the substrate while not reaching threshold pressure or threshold temperature, and blocking unit prevents the fluid supplied from a lower supply port from being directly supplied to a non-treatment surface of the substrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The supercritical fluid is carbon dioxide. The organic solvent is isopropyl alcohol. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an apparatus for treating a substrate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of an apparatus for cleaning a substrate in a first process chamber.
   Substrate treating apparatus (300)
   Outer recovery vessel (326)
   Body (342)
   Support shaft (386)
   Driver (388)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The additive includes fluid pertaining to one group consisting of fluorinated alcohol, alcohol, fluorinated ether, ether, fluorinated ketone, and ketone.

TI Expanded polystyrene recovery method comprises            taking expanded polystyrene, coagulating, dissolving in            solvent, purifying via filtration and separation            process, and carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide            extraction.
AU LEE G
   LIU D
   FANG H
   SONG Y
   LI K
   LI J
AE NANO & ADVANCED MATERIALS INST LTD (NAMN-C)
GA 2018103742
AB    NOVELTY - An expanded polystyrene recovery method                comprises taking expanded polystyrene, coagulating,                dissolving in solvent, purifying via filtration and                separation process, and carrying out supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction to obtain recovered                polystyrene.
   USE - Expanded polystyrene recovery method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is low-cost, and has recovered                polystyrene increased quality and purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is                  limonene, dipentene, isopropyltoluene, anisole,                  cinnamaldehyde, phellandrene, linalool, pinene,                  terpinene or terpineol. Preferred Method: The                  expanded polystyrene recovery method further                  comprises adding 1-50 wt.%, preferably 1-25 wt.%                  additive comprising ethanol, methanol,                  isopropanol, pentane or hexane. The expanded                  polystyrene recovery method comprises taking                  expanded polystyrene, coagulating at 20-80                  degrees C, dissolving in solvent, purifying via                  filtration and separation process, and carrying                  out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at                  40-80 degrees C under 5-50 MPa at carbon dioxide                  gas flow rate of 1-50 L/hour to obtain recovered                  polystyrene.

PT P
PN CN112191255-A
TI Catalyst prepared by mixing, stirring, grinding and extruding chromium-copper-manganese-oxygen mixed/composite oxide, aluminum hydroxide and kaolin to form ball shape, drying and roasting to obtain the product.
AU HUANG Q
   XU D
   CHEN K
AE NANJING RONGZHONG ENVIRONMENTAL ENG RES (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021069608
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst prepared by mixing, stirring, grinding and extruding the active component including chromium-copper-manganese-oxygen (Cr-Cu-Mn-O) mixed/composite oxide and carrier including aluminum hydroxide and kaolin to form ball shape, drying and roasting to obtain the product, is claimed.
   USE - Used as catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst can achieve complete catalytic oxidation of Chlorinate volatile organic compounds to carbon dioxide, water, hydrogen chloride and/or chlorine gas under medium and low temperature conditions without obvious chlorine poisoning; has the characteristics of high catalytic oxidation activity, large specific surface area and good thermal stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composite oxide is a copper-manganese composite oxide (Cu1.4Mn1.6O4). The mixed oxide is a mixture of chromium trioxide (Cr2O3) and manganese oxide (MnOx) in a ratio of 1:1. The mixed/composite oxide is a mixture of two mixed oxides of chromium trioxide (Cr2O3) and manganese oxide (MnOx) and a copper-manganese composite oxide (Cu1.4Mn1.6O4). The carrier comprises aluminum hydroxide and kaolin powder in a mass ratio of 1:1. The catalyst is mainly used for thermal catalytic oxidation of chlorine-containing volatile organic compound. Preferred Method: The preparation method comprises (a) preparing manganese nitrate solution and solid copper nitrate, adding distilled water, stirring to completely dissolve, adding solid chromium nitrate and citric acid, stirring to completely dissolve, where the ratio of copper, manganese and chromium is 7: 14: 6 and the ratio of manganese and citric acid is 15: 1, placing the above solution in a water bath at 80 degrees C for steaming to obtain a sol, natural cooling to form a gel, drying at 80 degrees C for 6 hours, calcining at 350-550 degrees C for 4 hours, grinding to obtain powdered chromium-copper-manganese-oxygen mixed/composite oxide and (b) taking the chromium-copper-manganese-oxygen mixed/composite oxide powder, aluminum hydroxide and kaolin powder in a ratio of 1-4: 2: 2, mixing and stirring, adding de-ionized water, crushing, extruding to form ball shape, drying at 80 degrees C for 2 hours and calcining at 300 degrees C for 2 hours to obtain the catalyst ball with diameter of 5mm.

TI Preparing 2-4C olefins comprises introducing feed            stream comprising hydrogen gas and carbon containing            gas into reaction zone of reactor, and converting feed            stream into product stream comprising 2-4C olefins in            reaction zone.
AU CHOJECKI A
   KIRILIN A
   MALEK A
   DEWILDE J F
   SANTOS CASTRO V P
   YANCEY D F
   ANDREWS K C
   SANTOS C V P
   CASTRO V P S
   MAREK A
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 202059425L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2-4C olefins comprises: introducing                a feed stream comprising hydrogen gas and a                carbon-containing gas comprising carbon monoxide                and/or carbon dioxide into a reaction zone of a                reactor; and converting the feed stream into a                product stream comprising 2-4C olefins in the                reaction zone in the presence of a hybrid catalyst,                where the hybrid catalyst comprises a metal oxide                catalyst component comprising gallium oxide and                phase pure zirconia and a microporous catalyst                component.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing 2-4C                olefins.
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides 2-4C olefins with high                yield on stream stability of the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The phase pure zirconia                  comprises crystalline phase pure zirconia. The                  phase pure zirconia comprises monoclinic phase                  pure zirconia. The phase pure zirconia has a                  Brunauer-Emmett-Teller surface area that is                  greater than or equal to 40 m2/g, preferably 100                  m2/g. The metal oxide catalyst component                  comprises greater than 0.0 gallium/100 g phase                  pure zirconia to 30 g, preferably 15 g                  gallium/100 g of phase pure zirconia. The                  microporous catalyst component comprises an 8                  membered ring structure. The microporous catalyst                  component comprises mixture of gallium and                  zirconium oxides without microporous catalyst                  component (SAPO-34). Pressure within the reaction                  zone during the converting is at least 1 bar (100                  kPa). Gas hour space velocity (GHSV) within the                  reaction zone during the converting is                  1200-12000/hour. The metal oxide catalyst                  component is formed by an impregnation method.                  Preferred Components: The metal oxide catalyst                  component comprises 1-99 wt.%, preferably 60-90                  wt.% the hybrid catalyst.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Method for regulating Krafft temperature of            surfactant for e.g. oil recovery, involves adding acid            to surfactant to lower Krafft temperature of surfactant            and adding alkali to surfactant to restore or raise            Krafft temperature of surfactant.
AU LIU X
   CAI S
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
   UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
GA 2019961922
AB    NOVELTY - Method for regulating Krafft temperature of                surfactant involves adding acid to aqueous solution                of surfactant to lower Krafft temperature of                surfactant by adjusting pH and adding alkali to                aqueous solution of the surfactant to restore or                raise the Krafft temperature of the                surfactant.
   USE - Method for regulating Krafft temperature of                surfactant used for washing, oil recovery and                mineral flotation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, easy to operate and is                environmentally-friendly. The method has excellent                reversibility especially when the Krafft                temperature of the surfactant is adjusted by using                carbon dioxide/nitrogen and ability to recycle                multiple times.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The tertiary amine is                  one of N,N-dimethylethanolamine, triethanolamine                  and N,N,N',N'-tetramethylethylenediamine. The                  surfactant is alkyl selenyl sulfonate or alkyl                  selenium propyl sulfate. The surfactant is of                  formula: H3C(CH2)nCH2Se(CH2)3SOmM+.m = 3 or 4;n = 8-14;andM+ = sodium ion or potassium ion.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  the tertiary amine and the surfactant is 2.5-5:1.                  Preferred Process: The method preferably involves                  adding acid or introducing carbon dioxide into                  surfactant solution and adding alkali or passing                  nitrogen gas into acid treated surfactant to                  restore the Krafft temperature of surfactant and                  repeating the cycle. The preparation of aqueous                  solution of a surfactant involves using 1-10                  %mass tertiary amine and adjusting pH to 1-6 by                  adding acid or passing carbon dioxide where molar                  amount of carbon dioxide introduced is equal to                  the molar amount of the tertiary amine group.                  Preferred Condition: The pH of aqueous solution                  of the surfactant after adding alkali is                  10-11.

   (1) method for producing alanine involves culturing the transformant using carbon dioxide substantially as a sole carbon source;
   (2) modified alanine dehydrogenase obtained by adding polypeptide comprising a 11 amino acid sequence (SEQ ID NO: 37) fully defined in the specification to N terminal of alanine dehydrogenase; and
   (3) modified alanine dehydrogenase gene obtained by adding polynucleotide comprising a 33 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 38) fully defined in the specification to 5' end of alanine dehydrogenase gene.

Preferred Biomolecule: The alanine dehydrogenase gene has (a) a DNA comprising one of 4(1038-1134) base pair sequences (SEQ ID NOs: 29, 30, 31, or 32) all fully defined in the specification, (b) a DNA comprising a base sequence having 90% or more sequence identity to SEQ ID NOs: 29, 30, 31, or 32 and a DNA encoding a polypeptide having alanine dehydrogenase activity, (c) a DNA which hybridizes under stringent conditions with a DNA comprising a base sequence complementary to SEQ ID NOs: 29, 30, 31, or 32 and which encodes a polypeptide having alanine dehydrogenase activity, (d) a DNA encoding a polypeptide comprising one of 4 (345-377) amino acid sequences (SEQ ID NOs: 33, 34, 35, or 36) all fully defined in the specification, (e) a DNA comprising an amino acid sequence having 90% which one or more amino acids are deleted, substituted, or added in the amino acid sequences of SEQ ID NOs: 33, 34, 35 or 36 and having alanine dehydrogenase activity, (g) DNA encoding a polypeptide comprising an amino acid sequence in which the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 37 is added to the N-terminus of the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NOs: 33, 34, 35, or 36, (h) a DNA encoding a polypeptide having an amino acid sequence in which the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 37 is added to the N terminus of the amino acid sequence having 90% or more identity to SEQ ID NOs: 33, 34, 35, or 36, and polypeptide having alanine dehydrogenase activity, (i) a DNA encoding a polypeptide having alanine dehydrogenase activity and comprising an amino acid sequence in which amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 37 is added to the N-terminus of the amino acid sequences of SEQ ID NOs: 33, 34, 35 or 36 in which one or more amino acids is deleted, substituted or added, (j) a DNA comprising a nucleotide sequence having base pair sequences of SEQ ID NOs: 38 added to 5' end of the base pair sequences of SEQ ID NOs: 29, 30, 31, or 32, (k) a DNA encoding a polypeptide having alanine dehydrogenase activity and comprising a nucleotide sequence obtained by adding nucleotide sequence of SEQ ID NO: 38 to the 5' end of a nucleotide sequence having 90% or more sequence identity to SEQ ID NOs: 29, 30, 31, or 32, and (l) a DNA encoding a polypeptide having alanine dehydrogenase activity and comprising nucleotide sequence obtained by adding nucleotide sequence of SEQ ID NO: 38 to the 5' end of the nucleotide sequence of DNA comprising base sequence complementary to SEQ ID NOs: 29, 30, 31, or 32 hybridizing with DNA under stringent conditions. PT P
PN CN114028433-A
TI Extracting active ingredients of plant, comprises preprocessing plant raw material by calcium chloride solution, quickly freezing, grinding, adding ethanol in grinding liquid, ultrasonically centrifuging, concentrating and drying supernatant.
AU LIU X
AE TIBET MEIDUO WUYU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIBE-Non-standard)
GA 202236094J
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting active ingredients of plant, comprises preprocessing plant raw material by calcium chloride solution, quickly freezing the pre-processed plant raw material for 2-3 minutes at -100degrees Celsius to -120degrees Celsius, grinding the quick-frozen plant raw material in environment less than 0degrees Celsius to obtain grinding liquid, adding ethanol in grinding liquid, ultrasonically centrifuging to obtain supernatant fluid, concentrating and drying the supernatant while carbon dioxide is continuously flowing into the supernatant.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting active ingredients of plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the extracting quantity of the active component and improves the quality of the extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The concentration of the calcium chloride solution is 1.85-1.9 g/ml, preferably 1.87 g/ml.Preferred Method: The step of preprocessing the plant raw material with calcium chloride solution comprises soaking plant raw material in calcium chloride solution with concentration of 1.5-2.2 g/ml for 2-3 minutes, fishing out and drying. The step of ultrasonically centrifuging supernatant after adding ethanol to the grinding liquid comprises (1) adding 40 vol.% ethanol solution into grinding liquid according to weight ratio of 1:20-30, ultrasonicating for 10-15 minutes under the condition of 200-400W, and centrifuging to obtain suspension and supernatant; and (2) adding 60 vol.% ethanol solution into turbid liquid according to weight ratio of 1:10-15, ultrasonicating for 10-15 minutes under the condition of 200-400 W, and centrifuging to obtain suspension and supernatant, where the supernatant obtained in step (1) and supernatant obtained in step (2) are mixed. The duration of grinding the quick-frozen plant raw material is 2-3 minutes, preferably 3 minutes.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)

PN JP2021153524-A
TI Producing organic acid e.g. malic acid, by culturing cyanobacterium in which expression of malate dehydrogenase gene is enhanced or cyanobacterium in which malate dehydrogenase activity is enhanced in culture to produce organic acid, and collecting organic acid from cyanobacterium and/or culture.
AU OSANAI T
   IIJIMA H
   WATANABE A
AE UNIV MEIJI (UMIJ-C)
GA 2021B4883J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing organic acid involves culturing cyanobacterium in which expression of malate dehydrogenase gene is enhanced or cyanobacterium in which malate dehydrogenase activity is enhanced in a culture to produce an organic acid, and collecting the organic acid from the cyanobacterium and/or the culture.
   USE - The method is useful for producing organic acid chosen from succinic acid, fumaric acid and malic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces organic acid in a cost-effective and environmentally-friendly manner with high efficiency, and directly recycles carbon dioxide and light energy by photosynthesis.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves culturing the cyanobacterium in a culture by culturing the cyanobacterium aerobically and then anaerobically. The method involves during the anaerobic culture, reducing the concentration of the organic acid in the culture produced by the cyanobacterium. The value of the amount of organic acid produced per dry weight of the cyanobacterium (the weight of the organic acid produced in the culture in the anaerobic culture (g)/the dry weight of the anaerobic-cultured cyanobacterium (g)x 100) satisfies (a) the amount of succinic acid produced per dry weight of the cyanobacterium being 7% or more, (b) the amount of fumaric acid produced per dry weight of the cyanobacterium being 5% or more, and (c) the amount of malic acid produced per dry weight of the cyanobacterium being 6% or more. The anaerobic culture is performed under dark conditions. Preferred Biomolecule: The cyanobacterium has a malate dehydrogenase gene in which the coding region and/or the expression-regulating region are rearranged. The cyanobacterium has a sequence containing a nucleic acid sequence of a malate dehydrogenase gene encoding malate dehydrogenase and a nucleic acid sequence of a promoter of a psbA gene operably linked to the nucleic acid sequence. The malate dehydrogenase gene is a gene encoding a protein chosen from (a) a protein having a 324 amino acid sequence (SEQ ID NO: 1) fully defined in the specification, (b) a protein having an amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 1 in which one or more amino acids are substituted, deleted, inserted, or added and malate dehydrogenase activity, and (c) a protein having an amino acid sequence having 80% or more sequence identity with the above-mentioned amino acid sequence and having malate dehydrogenase activity. The concentration of cyanobacterium specified by optical density (OD)730 of the culture at the end of the aerobic culture is 5 or more. The cyanobacterium is in a non-diapause state at the start of the anaerobic culture. The cyanobacterium is Synechocystis.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details about preparation of citrate-proton symporter (citH) overexpression strain, expression analysis of citH, manufacture of organic acid and measurement of the organic acid content of the cell outside (inside of a culture solution) that do not support the claims.

The concentration of the hbFGF is 45-55 ng/ml, preferably 50 ng/ml. The concentration of the hVEGF is 45-55 ng/ml, preferably 50 ng/ml. PN CN111719165-A; CN111719165-B
TI Preparing bismuth nanosheets by electrochemical stripping method comprises electrochemical stripping metal bismuth block as cathode in sodium iodide electrolyte solution under the protection of electricity and nitrogen.
AU ZHANG Y
   WANG D
   ZHU Y
   PAN C
   LOU Y
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
GA 2020984369
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bismuth nanosheets by electrochemical stripping method comprises electrochemical stripping metal bismuth block as cathode in sodium iodide electrolyte solution under the protection of electricity and nitrogen to obtain final product.
   USE - The bismuth nanosheet is useful in electrocatalytic reducing carbon dioxide to formic acid reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The bismuth nanosheet: has high purity, Faraday efficiency in electrocatalytic reduction of 94%, current density of more than 50 mA/cm2 at a low electrolytic potential (-0.5V vs. RHE), more uniform morphology and size and more surface active sites, simple operation, low energy consumption, very economical and practical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for bismuth nanosheets prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) taking H-Cell as a reaction vessel, adding sodium iodide solution as supporting electrolyte solution into both the cathode and anode electrolytic cells of H-Cell, (ii) taking metal bismuth block as he cathode, and platinum sheet electrode as anode, performing electric reaction under the protection of nitrogen, and (iii) separating the obtained precipitate in the cathodic electrolytic cell in step (ii), washing, and vacuum drying to obtain bismuth nanosheets. In step (ii), nitrogen before electric reaction is passed to the catholyte solution for 15-30 minutes to drive off the dissolved oxygen in the solution. The power-on condition in step (ii) is that the DC regulated power supply provides a voltage of 10-30 V. The time of the electric reaction in step (ii) is 2-5 hours. The drying process in step (iii) is performed by drying in an electric vacuum oven at 60-80 degrees C for 4-6 hours. Preferred Components: The concentration of the sodium iodide solution in step (i) is 0.5-1 mol/l. The metal bismuth block in step (ii) has size of 3-5 mm3, and purity of more than 99.99%.

PT P
PN CN111495424-A
TI Preparing graphene composite material comprises e.g. modifying titanium carbonitride with gamma-methacryloxypropyltrimethoxysilane, mixing and reacting modified titanium carbonitride, modified graphene and modified silica gel.
AU SHI Y
AE HENAN ZHICHUAN ENERGY SAVING & ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECH (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 202078101C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene composite material comprises modifying titanium carbonitride with gamma -methacryloxypropyltrimethoxysilane to obtain modified titanium carbonitride, then modifying silicon and boron co-doped graphene with 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane to obtain modified graphene, mixing and reacting modified titanium carbonitride, modified graphene and modified silica gel to obtain graphene composite material. The modified silica gel is obtained by modifying silica gel with 3-aminopropyltrihydroxysilane.
   USE - The graphene composite material is useful for removing formaldehyde (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The graphene composite material: can catalyze and oxidize formaldehyde into harmless carbon dioxide and water at room temperature, and realizes the complete removal of formaldehyde in a short time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for graphene composite material obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The titanium carbonitride oxide is obtained by heating titanium carbonitride in inert gas containing oxygen, where mass-volume ratio of titanium carbonitride, oxygen and inert gas is 1:1:6-7 g/l, inert gas is helium or argon, and the heating temperature is 1000-1100 degrees C and heating time is 7-8 hours. The preparation method of modified titanium carbonitride comprises adding titanium oxycarbonitride into the toluene solution of gamma -methacryloxypropyltrimethoxysilane for 12 hours, and then filtering to obtain final product. The molar ratio between titanium carbonitride and gamma -methacryloxypropyltrimethoxysilane is 1:1.2-1.5; and mass concentration of gamma -methacryloxypropyltrimethoxysilane in the toluene solution of gamma -methacryloxypropyltrimethoxysilane is 10-12%. The preparation method of silicon and boron co-doped graphene comprises adding nano silicon powder and boric acid into the graphene oxide dispersion, heating at 600-650 degrees C for 8-9 hours under inert atmosphere and ultrasonic oscillation conditions, and post-processing to obtain multi-doped graphene. The molar ratio between nano silicon powder, boric acid and graphene oxide contained in the graphene oxide dispersion is 0.08-0.09:0.03-0.04:1; and graphene oxide dispersion is obtained by adding graphene oxide into 8-10 times the weight of water, and ultrasonically oscillating for 30-40 minutes. The specific method of reduction comprises adding hydrazine hydrate, stirring and heating at 60-80 degrees C for 2-3 hours, then cooling to room temperature, where amount of hydrazine hydrate is 1.3-1.5 times the weight of graphene oxide. The preparation method of modified graphene comprises adding silicon and boron co-doped graphene into the toluene solution of 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane and soaking for 12 hours, and then filtering to obtain final product, where molar ratio between silicon and boron co-doped graphene and 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane is 1:1.2-1.5, and mass concentration of 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane in the toluene solution of 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane is 10-12%. The preparation method of the modified silica gel comprises activating silica gel first to obtain activated silica gel, and then modifying activated silica gel by 3-aminopropyltrihydroxysilane, centrifuging, and washing with water to obtain modified silica gel. The specific method of modification comprises adding activated silica gel into the toluene solution of 3-aminopropyltrihydroxysilane, soaking for 18-24 hours, filtering, washing, and drying; mass volume ratio between activated silica gel and toluene solution of 3-aminopropyltrihydroxysilane is 1:8-10 g/ml, and mass concentration of 3-aminopropyltrihydroxysilane in the toluene solution of 3-aminopropyltrihydroxysilane is 15-20%. The specific method of mixed reaction comprises adding 1 pts. wt. modified titanium carbonitride, 4-6 pts. wt. modified graphene and 45-55 pts. wt. modified silica gel into 80-90 pts. wt. dimethyl sulfoxide, performing ultrasonic vibration of 500-800 W for 5-8 hours, centrifuging and drying.

PT P
PN CN111468116-A; CN111468116-B; US2021346872-A1
TI Preparing brown coal coke loaded nano cobalt            composite catalyst comprises e.g. crushing and            screening original brown coal coke, adding cobalt            precursor and lignite particles to solvent, mixing,            impregnating and drying.
AU ZHAO C
   LI F
   CHEN W
   JIN W
   QI Y
   YANG S
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
   UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
   UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 202074593L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing brown coal coke loaded nano cobalt                composite catalyst comprises (1) crushing and                screening original brown coal coke to obtain                lignite particles, (2) adding cobalt precursor and                lignite particles to solvent, mixing, impregnating                and drying to obtain a first solid substance, (3)                subjecting first solid material to high-temperature                pyrolysis treatment to obtain lignite coke-loaded                nano-cobalt composite catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing brown coal                coke loaded nano cobalt composite catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has hierarchical pore structure,                high specific surface area, controllable particle                size, good low-temperature carbon dioxide                methanation catalytic activity, and simple                preparation process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (2), adding                  cobalt precursor and the lignite particles to                  solvent and mixing, and dipping and drying to                  obtain first solid substance, which includes:                  adding lignite particles to the solvent,                  ultrasonically dispersing, and adding cobalt                  precursor, continue ultrasonic dispersion,                  stirring mixed solution obtained by ultrasonic                  dispersion at a certain temperature until solvent                  is fully volatilizing, and drying to obtain first                  solid substance. The mixing liquid obtained by                  ultrasonic dispersion is continuously stirring at                  a certain temperature until the solvent is fully                  volatilizing, including continuously stirring                  mixed liquid obtained by ultrasonic dispersion at                  room temperature until solvent is fully                  volatilizing, or the mixed solution obtained by                  ultrasonic dispersion is first stirring for a                  certain period of time under a water bath                  condition of 30-60 degrees C, and stirring at                  room temperature until solvent is fully                  volatilizing. In the step (2), before adding                  cobalt precursor and the lignite particles to                  solvent and mixing, includes: subjecting lignite                  particles to acid treatment. The acid treatment                  includes adding lignite particles to a nitric                  acid solution, or a sulfuric acid solution, or a                  hydrochloric acid solution, and stirring in a                  water bath at room temperature to 55 degrees C,                  where the water bath stirring time is /~2 hours.                  In the step (2), the feed ratio of cobalt in the                  cobalt precursor to the lignite particles is 5-15                  wt.%. In the step (2), the cobalt precursor is                  cobalt salt or cobalt salt solution, the cobalt                  salt is cobalt nitrate, cobalt carbonate, or                  cobalt acetate, the solute in cobalt salt                  solution is cobalt nitrate, cobalt carbonate, or                  cobalt acetate, and the solvent in cobalt salt                  solution is ethanol, water, or a mixture of                  ethanol and water, and the solvent is water,                  ethanol, or an aqueous solution of ethanol. In                  the step (3), subjecting first solid substance to                  high-temperature pyrolysis treatment to obtain                  lignite coke-loaded nano-cobalt composite                  catalyst includes placing first solid substance                  in an inert atmosphere at a temperature of 0.5-10                  degrees C/minutes, heating to 700-900 degrees C,                  and maintaining for 1-6 hours to obtain lignite                  coke-loaded nano-cobalt composite catalyst. The                  specific surface area of the lignite coke-loaded                  nano-cobalt composite catalyst is 350-470 m2/g,                  and the loading amount of the nano-cobalt in the                  lignite coke-loaded nano cobalt composite                  catalyst is 9-27 wt.%, and the size of the                  nano-cobalt is 1-15 nm. Preferred Components: The                  inert atmosphere is argon, nitrogen, or a mixed                  gas of argon and nitrogen.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN110559383-A
TI Composition useful for treating gynecological inflammation, is prepared using e.g. evening primrose, frankincense, myrrh, fructus cnidii, Phellodendron, dried alum, borneol, Smilax bockii warb, pomegranate, catechu, borax and pearl.
AU CHEN Y
AE CHEN Y (CHEN-Individual)
GA 2019A6760E
AB    NOVELTY - Composition is prepared using evening primrose, frankincense, myrrh, fructus cnidii, radix sophorae flavescentis, Phellodendron, dried alum, borneol, Smilax bockii warb, pomegranate, catechu, borax and pearl.
   USE - The composition in the form of oral dripping pill, injection or suppository is useful for treating gynecological inflammation and inhibiting Staphylococcus aureus, Escherichia coli, mildew and Candida albicans.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing the composition, which involves (a) grinding the above-mentioned raw materials into powder and extracting the powder by performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method and distillation to obtain mixed extract, (b) formulating the mixed extract into suitable dosage form.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The composition is prepared using 6-17 pts. wt., preferably 10 pts. wt. evening primrose, 3-6 pts. wt., preferably 5 pts. wt. frankincense, 4-9 pts. wt., preferably 5 pts. wt. myrrh, 8-15 pts. wt., preferably 10 pts. wt. fructus cnidii, 3-11 pts. wt., preferably 10 pts. wt. radix sophorae flavescentis, 4-11 pts. wt., preferably 10 pts. wt. Phellodendron, 1-6 pts. wt., preferably 2 pts. wt. dried alum, 0.5-3 pts. wt., preferably 1 pts. wt. borneol, 12-19 pts. wt., preferably 15 pts. wt. Smilax bockii warb, 14-19 pts. wt., preferably 15 pts. wt. pomegranate, 2-6 pts. wt., preferably 5 pts. wt. catechu, 0.5-2.5 pts. wt., preferably 2 pts. wt. borax and 0.5-3 pts. wt., preferably 2 pts. wt. pearl. Preferred Method: The step (a) involves adding the powder into a supercritical fluid extraction tank and extracting at a critical extraction fluid flow rate of 2.5-2.8 l/minute, 25-28 MPa and 38-39 degrees C for 65-75 minutes to obtain a mixed extract. The step (b) involves uniformly mixing semi-synthetic fatty acid glyceride having a weight ratio of 1:1.5 to mixed extract in a weight ratio of 1:5, heating and melting the mixture in a water bath, evenly stirring the mixture, adding the mixture into a plug mold while it is hot, cooling for complete solidification and removing the overflow portion to obtain an suppository. The step (a) involves mixing evening primrose, frankincense, myrrh, fructus cnidii and matrine, crushing, adding 15-20 times water to the crushed product into a volatile oil extractor, distilling by passing steam for 4.5 hours, collecting the primary distillation liquid, re-distilling for 3.5 hours to obtain the mixed distilled liquid (A), adding sodium chloride to adjust isotonicity, then adding Tween (RTM: Polysorbate) or meglumine, evenly stirring the mixture, refrigerating to obtain the mixed distilled liquid (B), mixing Phellodendron, dried alum, borneol, smilax bockii warb, pomegranate, catechu, borax and pearl, crushing, adding 15-20 times water to the crushed product into a volatile oil extractor, distilling by passing steam for 4.5 hours, collecting the primary distillation liquid, re-distilling for 3.5 hours to obtain the mixed distilled liquid (C), adding sodium chloride to adjust isotonicity, then adding Tween (RTM: Polysorbate) or meglumine, evenly stirring the mixture and refrigerating to obtain the mixed distilled liquid (D). The step (b) involves evenly stirring the distilled liquids (A) and (C) to obtain distilled liquid (E), uniformly mixing distilled liquid (E) with drip pill matrix polyethylene glycol 6000 in a weight ratio of 1:3.5, heating and melting at 90 degrees C, dripping into methyl silicone oil as the coolant, collecting the dripping pill, and absorbing the coolant to obtain the oral dripping pill. The step (b) involves evenly stirring the distilled liquids (B) and (D) to obtain distilled liquid (F), adding water for injection to the distilled liquid (F), adding 9 g sodium chloride and 1-5 ml Tween (RTM: Polysorbate) or 5-15 g meglumine to 1000 ml mixture, evenly stirring the mixture, cooling in a refrigerator, centrifuging, filtering the supernatant using a microporous filter membrane and sterilizing to obtain the injection.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Gynecological; Antibacterial; Fungicide.

TI Preparation of activated carbon used for active carbon material comprises dipping blue carbon powder in activator aqueous solution, drying, introducing nitrogen gas and water vapor, activating, cooling, washing, filtering, and drying.
AU YAN L
   WANG Y
   LI J
AE UNIV YULIN (UNYL-C)
GA 2019919784
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of activated carbon comprises impregnating blue carbon powder in an aqueous solution of activator and drying; placing in a heating furnace, simultaneously introducing nitrogen gas and water vapor, activating, lowering furnace temperature to 90-120 degrees C, and taking out activated material; and washing, vacuum filtering, and drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing activated carbon for active carbon material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The activated carbon has large specific surface area, high micropore content, good carbon dioxide adsorption performance, methylene blue adsorption value of 690 mg/g, and large specific surface area of 1361.48 m2/g.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of activator and blue carbon powder is 2.5-3.5:1. The activator is potassium hydroxide. Preferred Method: The activation is done at 750-850 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours. The washing is done by using 0.05-0.15 mol/L hydrochloric acid aqueous solution, boiling for 15-30 minutes, and washing with distilled water until neutral. The drying is done at 90-130 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The activated carbon material has aperture of 2-15 nm and contains hydroxyl and carbonyl groups.

GA 201974670M
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises catalytic material containing copper, and a carrier containing cerium oxide. The catalyst material comprises at least one promoter (A) chosen from alkali metals and alkaline earth metals, and a cocatalyst (II) comprising a lanthanide metal.
   USE - Catalyst used in production of oxychlorination of hydrocarbon (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has increases the selectivity even at low temperature, and suppresses generation of by-products e.g. carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of oxychlorinated compound of hydrocarbon, which involves preparing carrier comprising cerium oxide and supporting the active material including copper, promoter (A) and promoter (B) on the carrier.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The process is carried out in fixed bed reactor, fluidized bed reactor or circulating fluidized bed reactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst comprises 5-25 wt.% catalytic material. The catalyst comprises 10-2000 pts. wt. promoter (A) and (B) with respect to 100 pts. wt. of copper. Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises cocatalyst (I) is chosen from sodium, lithium, potassium, magnesium and calcium. The cocatalyst (II) is chosen from yttria and rare earth elements. Preferred Properties: The carrier has specific surface area of 50-250 m2/g. The catalyst has diameter of 0.1-1.0 mm. The method is performed at temperature of 450-550 degrees C, pressure of 0.5-3 atm, and space velocity of 2000-20000 hour-1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process is carried out under catalyst for oxychlorinating hydrocarbon.

TI METHOD OF PRODUCING NANOPARTICLES OF MANGANESE OXIDES AND AEROGELS BASED THEREON AND AEROGEL OBTAINED BY THIS METHOD.
AU ZEFIROV
   ELMANOVICH
   GALLYAMOV
AE SKOLKOVO SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (SKOL-Non-standard)
   UNIV MOSC LOMONOSOV (MOSU-C)
GA 201968567E
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to synthesis of nanoparticles of manganese oxides and manganese oxide aerogels. Method involves dissolving an organometallic precursor of manganese in supercritical carbon dioxide in a high-pressure reactor with addition of pure oxygen as an oxidising agent. Further, the organometallic precursor of manganese is directly decomposed in a high-pressure reactor to form powdered nanoparticles of manganese oxide and manganese oxide aerogels. Disclosed is a manganese oxide aerogel having an average particle size not exceeding 10 nm, a specific surface area of not less than 70 m2/g and density not exceeding 7 mg/ml.
   USE - Technological processes.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention enables to obtain separate nanoparticles in the form of powder and monolithic aerogels of manganese oxide without solvent replacement and drying.

PN CN109437128-A
TI Preparation of two-dimensional boron nitride nanosheet supercritical includes using amino sodium and sodium borohydride as reaction precursors, heating, washing, mixing, performing ultrasonic dispersion, reacting and drying.
AU MIAO Z
   TIAN L
AE UNIV YANCHENG TEACHERS (UAET-C)
GA 201924919S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of two-dimensional boron nitride nanosheet supercritical comprises using amino sodium and sodium borohydride as reaction precursors, placing in stainless steel reaction tank, heating reaction product with anhydrous ethanol, washing concentrated hydrochloric acid and deionized water, drying in vacuum oven, mixing boron nitride powder and polyvinylpyrrolidone in anhydrous ethanol and water mixed solution, performing ultrasonic dispersion, transferring to supercritical high-pressure reaction tank, injecting supercritical carbon dioxide, reacting, performing ultrasonic dispersion, performing centrifugal separation to remove large-size boron nitride particles and drying.
   USE - Method of preparing two-dimensional boron nitride nanosheet supercritical.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple preparation and low cost and is easy to industrialize.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of sodium amide and sodium borohydride is 3:2. The mass ratio of boron nitride powder and polyvinylpyrrolidone is 1:4.

PT P
PN CN109438724-A
TI Extracting lignin from lingo cellulose useful in the field of food industry, medicinal industry or bio-environmental industries comprises e.g. ball milling the pulverized and dried lingo cellulosic raw material, adding and evaporating.
AU ZHANG J
   HUANG T
   YANG S
   DING S
   LI M
   CHEN D
   ZHENG X
   LI Z
   MAO B
AE ZHEJIANG HUAKANG PHARM CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
   JIAOZUO HUAKANG SUGAR ALCOHOL TECHNOLOGY (JIAO-Non-standard)
GA 201924879J
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting lignin from lingo cellulose comprises ball milling the pulverized and dried lingo cellulosic raw material together with the pearl powder, adding an extracting agent to the ball milled mixture for extraction, controlling the extraction pressure from 10-30 Mpa by adding supercritical carbon dioxide before and during the extraction, and evaporating the extract to obtain lignin crystals.
   USE - The lignin is useful in the field of food industry, medicinal industry or bio-environmental industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally friendly, economical, suitable for cleaning of biomass material, and has high pure lignin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The lingo cellulosic material comprises corn stover, corn cob, agricultural and forestry waste, sugar cane, wheat, and/or sweet sorghum. The extracting agent is a mixed solvent of Tween 80 and water, or mixed solvent of polyethylene glycol and water. In the mixed solvent of Tween 80 and water, the mass ratio of Tween 80 and water is: 1:1-1:9, and the mass ratio in the mixed solvent of polyethylene glycol and water is 1:5-1:10. Preferred Method: (1) the lingo cellulosic raw material is pulverized and dried by using lingo cellulose as raw material, (2) the lingo cellulosic raw material is mixed with pearl powder, the pulverized lingo cellulosic raw material is mixed with the pearl powder according to the quality, and the ratio of lingo cellulosic raw material and pearl powder is 1:1-1:5, and mixture of lingo cellulose and pearl powder is obtained, (3) the mixture of lingo cellulose and pearl powder is ball-milled using ball mill to obtain a ball milled powder of lingo cellulose and pearl powder, where the particle diameter of the lingo cellulose is maintained at 200-400 meshes, (4) taking 50 g of resultant powder into the extraction kettle, (5) preparing the Tween 80 and water extractant in the mass ratio of 1:1-1:9, (6) heating the extraction kettle to control the temperature of the extraction kettle at 40-60 degrees C, (7) passing supercritical carbon dioxide to the extraction kettle to control the pressure of the extraction kettle from 10-30Mpa, (8) the extractant configured in step (v) added to the extraction kettle, the flow rate of the extracting agent is 0.1-0.5ml/min, for 0.5-2 hours, and the static extraction time is 0.5-2 hours, (9) the dynamic extraction is performed, the flow rate of the extracting agent is 0.1-0.5 ml/minutes, the dynamic extraction time is 1-3 hours, and supercritical carbon dioxide is continuously introduced into the extraction kettle, and the extraction kettle is added to the extractant for 2-5 times, and the pressure of the extraction kettle is controlled at 10-30 Mpa, (10) the resultant extract is subjected to rotary evaporation, the temperature is controlled at 50-70 degrees C, the degree of vacuum is controlled at 0.09-0.1 Mpa, and dried to obtain lignin crystals, preferably (11) the corn stalk is used as raw material, the corn stalk raw material is pulverized and dried, (12) the pulverized corn stalk raw material and pearl powder is mixed according to the quality, the ratio of corn stalk raw material and pearl powder is 1:1 and obtaining a mixture of corn stalk and pearl powder, (13) the mixture of corn stalk and pearl powder was ball milled by ball mill, the ball milling time was 12 hours to obtain ball milled powder of corn stalk and pearl powder, where the particle size of the corn stalk was maintained at 200 meshes, (14) the 50 g corn stover and pearl ball milling powder is placed into the extraction kettle, (15) the Tween 80 and water extractant is preparewd in the mass ratio of 1:1, (16) the extraction kettle is heating at 40 degrees C, (17) passing supercritical carbon dioxide to the extraction kettle to control the pressure of the extraction kettle to 10 MPa, (18) the resultant extractant is added to the extraction vessel, the flow rate of the extracting agent is 0.1 ml/minutes, the introduction time is 0.5 hour, and the static extraction time is 0.5 hour, (19) the dynamic extraction is further performed, the flow rate of the extracting agent is 0.1 ml/minutes, the dynamic extraction time is 1 hour, the supercritical carbon dioxide is continuously introduced into the extraction vessel, and the extracting agent is added to the extraction vessel twice, and controlling the pressure of the extraction kettle to 10Mpa, (20) the resultant extract is subjected to rotary evaporation, the temperature was controlled at 50 degrees C, the degree of vacuum was controlled at 0.09 Mpa, and dried to obtain lignin crystals.
