AE CHANGSHA MINING & METALLURGY INST (CHMM-C)
GA 2019211775
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of carbon-coated high-nickel ternary anode material involves mixing nanocarbon material dispersion and ternary anode material, and uniformly dispersing to obtain mixed slurry, and drying the mixed slurry.
   USE - Preparation method of carbon-coated high-nickel ternary anode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of carbon-coated high-nickel ternary anode material with excellent hydrophobic characteristics and improved cycle performance, which effectively limits the reaction of water and carbon dioxide in the air with the matrix high nickel cathode material, and suppresses the corrosion of the high-nickel ternary positive electrode material of the substrate during the charging and discharging process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of nanocarbon material is 0.01-5 %mass of the high-nickel ternary anode material. The concentration of the nanocarbon material dispersion is 0.01-5 wt.%. Preferred Component: The nanocarbon material comprises graphene oxide and graphene, carbon nanotubes, Ketjen Black (RTM: electroconductive carbon black), and fullerene. The high-nickel ternary anode material comprises lithium-nickel-metal oxide of formula: LiNixM1-xO2.x = greater than or equal to 0.6;andM = doped element comprising Co, Mn, Al, and/or Mg.Preferred Process: The nanocarbon material dispersion is obtained by dissolving nanocarbon material and solvent into a suspension, and ultrasonically dispersing for 1-20 hours. Preferred Conditions: The drying is vacuum drying or blowing drying, the drying temperature is 50-250 degrees C, and the drying time is 1-30 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is glycol, isopropanol, N,N-dimethylformamide, pyridine, tetrahydrofuran, dimethyl sulfoxide, N-methyl pyrrolidone, acetone, cyclohexane, and/or butanone.

PN CN108912803-A
TI Preparing graphene radiating paste placing graphite powder, sealing the reaction mill, reacting, reducing, mixing solvent, ultrasonically dispersing, grinding and dispersing.
AU XIANG Z
   NIU M
   ZHAO S
   YANG F
   ZHOU Z
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 201898395P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene radiating paste comprises graphene, dispersant, solvent, binder and additive, where the method comprises placing graphite powder, carbon dioxide in a reaction mill equipment, sealing the reaction mill equipment, starting the reaction mill equipment, reacting to obtain functionalized graphene, reducing the functionalized graphene to graphene, mixing solvent, graphene and dispersant for 0.1-6 hours, ultrasonically dispersing for 0.5-10 hours to obtain a graphene dispersion, mixing the graphene dispersion, binder and additives, grinding and dispersing for 0-12 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing graphene radiating paste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical, environment-friendly and efficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for graphene radiating paste.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The graphene radiating paste comprises 1-40 pts. wt. graphene, 0.5-5 pts. wt. dispersant, 50-150 pts. wt. solvent, 40-100 pts. wt. binder and 1-10 pts. wt. auxiliary agent. The reaction grinding apparatus comprises planetary ball mills, horizontal ball mill, sand mill and rod ball mill. The total graphite powder filling amount is 1/8-2/3 of the total volume of the reaction grinding tank. The graphite powder is natural crystalline flake graphite, earthy graphite, bulk graphite and/or colloidal graphite. The dispersant comprises cyclodextrin, N-methylpyrrolidone, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, monoalkenyl butyl imide, oleic acid, sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate and/or cetyltrimethylammonium bromide. The solvent comprises water, alcohol solvent, ester solvent, ketone solvent, ether solvent, benzene, dimethyl sulfoxide, ethylenediamine and/or xylene. The binder comprises rosin, epoxy resin, phenolic resin, acrylic resin, synthetic cellulose, silicone resin and/or polyester resins. The auxiliary agent comprises diluent, defoamer, stabilizer, leveling agent, preservative, crosslinker, film former and/or tackifier. Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide gas passed into the reaction mill equipment, filling pressure is 0.1-25 MPa, continuously grinding the reaction at a speed of 100-5000 rotations/minute for 3-72 hours. The method of reducing functionalized graphene to graphene comprises direct reducing agent reduction method, microwave assisted reduction method, ultraviolet radiation reduction method, chemical reduction method, electrochemical reduction and/or thermal reduction.

PN MX2017009467-A1
TI Increasing durability of construction material involves improving properties of Portland cement based materials (slurry, grout, mortar, concrete) by decreasing percentage of empty deposits or capillary pores that transport liquids and gases.
AU TORRES ACOSTA A A
   GUZMAN E M A
   CHAVEZ GARCIA H L
   MOLINA W M
AE UNIV MARISTA QUERETARO AC (UYMA-Non-standard)
   UNIV MICHOACANA SAN NICOLAS HIDALGO (UYMI-Non-standard)
GA 2018A55210
AB    NOVELTY - Increasing the durability of construction materials involves improving the properties of Portland cement based materials (slurry, grout, mortar, concrete) by decreasing the percentage of empty deposits or capillary pores that transport liquids and gasses within these materials, decreasing the entry of aggressive agents such as chloride ion and carbon dioxide or sulfur gas, and sulfates from the exposure environment, and working as a decorosion inhibitor of reinforcing steel in Portland cement base materials (grout, mortar, concrete) by forming more dense and compact oxides on the surface of reinforcing or prestressing steel.
   USE - Method for increasing durability of construction materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method for increasing the durability of construction materials involves mixing the mucilage of dehydrated prickly pear with cement and then mixing this product with the other components that form the grout, mortar, concrete, or mixing the mucilage of colloidal prickly pear to the mixing water of the material (slurry, grount, mortar, concrete) to later mix this product with the other components, or coating the deconstruction steel with a layer of mucilage in prickly pear colloid. The mucilage of pear is produced by soaking the colloid of pear, cutting into small portions of any geometric forms such as cubes, prisms, long strips, immersing in water for up to 24 hours or cooking in water for up to 1 hour in water and cladode in a ratio of 1: 1 in both cases, and comprises 1.5-95 wt.% water mixture. Preferred Conditions: The mucilage concentrations of dehydrated prickly pear cactus mucilage comprise 0.5-2 wt.% cement mixture.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).); Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3))

TI Repairing old concrete column comprises taking fresh concrete, adding Streptomyces microflavus bacteria powder and calcium lactate or calcium acetate, injecting fresh concrete in template, spraying pure water, and curing.
AU YU X
   YU C
AE UNIV WENZHOU (UNWZ-C)
GA 201826381P
AB    NOVELTY - Repairing old concrete column comprises (i) taking fresh concrete, and adding Streptomyces microflavus bacteria powder and calcium lactate or calcium acetate, (ii) mounting a casting template on the concrete column, and injecting the fresh concrete into the template, (iii) spraying pure water once in every 6-8 hours at the junction of fresh concrete and old concrete column connection, and injecting carbon dioxide gas into the joint for 1 hour under 0.1 MPa pressure, and (iv) curing the concrete until solidified.
   USE - The method is useful for repairing old concrete column.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improve the bonding strength of old and fresh concrete.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The content of Streptomyces microflavus microflavus 1-3 g per kilogram of fresh concrete. The content of calcium lactate or calcium acetate is 1-3 g per kg fresh concrete.

The upper air hole and the lower aerated watering holes are provided with plug. The injection hole is provided with a sealing door. The template is hooped with a transverse rod, two ends of the transverse rod are provided with a through hole. The pull rod passes through the hole of the front transverse rod and the back transverse to the pull connection. Each pair of opposite distribution of the template is provided with at least two opposite-pulls bolt is clamped and fixed with the transverse rod along the height direction. The template is matched with column surface and the template is sealed by rubber ring and rubber reinforcing. The template is set on the position of the transverse rod and horizontally distributed with at least two clamping blocks with clamping groove, and the transverse rod is embedded in the neck.

   USE - Composition agent used as a stimulant.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition agent improves activity of the blast furnace slag fine powder, minimizes the amount of the ordinary cement which is most widely used in construction work, reduces production cost by reducing cement usage, and saves natural resource, energy consumption and environment from carbon dioxide emission.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The composition agent also comprises 0.5-200 pts. wt. circulating fluidized bed boiler fly ash, 0.5-100 pts. wt. pulverized coal boiler fly ash, 0.5-200 pts. wt. gypsum, and 0.5-200 pts. wt. cement. The pulverized coal boiler fly ash is discharged from a pulverized combustion dust collector of a thermal power plant having 35-60 wt.% silicon dioxide. The gypsum comprises a mixture of natural gypsum, flue gas desulfurization gypsum, and petro coke desulfurization gypsum. The cement comprises a primary type cement, a secondary type cement, calcium sulfur aluminate, a blast furnace slag cement, and a fly ash cement or their mixtures.

PT P
PN WO2022180586-A1; IN202121007954-A
TI New chimeric antigen receptor T cell product            comprising third generation chimeric antigen receptor            gene comprising the nucleotide sequences, used to treat            cancer.
AU NATESAN S
AE NATESAN S (NATE-Individual)
GA 2022B2426A
AB    NOVELTY - Chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T cell product                (P1) comprising third generation chimeric antigen                receptor gene CD19-CD28-41BB-CD3z comprising the                nucleotide sequences of single chain variable                region of anti-CD19 antibody, signaling domain of                CD28, signalling domain of 41BB, transmembrane                region CD8a, CD8aHinge region, and CD3z signaling                domain along with leader and linker sequences,                where in the CAR gene nucleotide sequence is as per                SEQ ID NO: 1 (comprising a fully defined sequence                of 1602 nucleotides as given in the specification),                is new.
   USE - The CAR T cell product is useful for treating                cancer in a human subject, cloning of CD19 CAR                sequence in the lentiviral vector, preparing                lenti-CD19 CAR viral vector, and transfecting 293T                cells for generating lentiviral particles                containing CAR gene (all claimed). Test details are                described but no results given.
   ADVANTAGE - The CAR T cell product regulates cellular                activity via defined signaling pathways by                generating second messengers or functioning as                effectors by responding to such messengers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   CAR T cell product (P2);
   CAR T cell product (P3);
   composition, comprising the CAR T cell product                with carrier, diluent or excipient, and optionally                at least one active polypeptide and/or                compound;
   treating cancer in a human subject, comprising                administering CAR T cell product;
   cloning of CD19 CAR sequence in the lentiviral                vector, comprising cloning of purified CAR gene                product into a cloning vector, used to transform                the competent cells, spreading the transformed                competent cells into a LB (Luria broth) agar plate                containing ampicillin, incubating the plates in an                incubator at 37&#176; C overnight, growing the                large number of colonies in the agar plate in the                LB broth, screening of the clones using polymerase                chain reaction and identifying the clones positive                for the desired insert, purifying the plasmids from                the clones of interest, analyzing the sequence of                CAR clones by sequencing shows presence of                mutations either in the form of deletion or                insertions, where the desired clone was selected                for further cloning in to the lentiviral vector,                cloning the CAR gene into the lentiviral vector, a                double digest of cloning vector and the lentiviral                vector, where loading them on to agarose gel and                purifying from the gel, setting up ligation                reaction using the linearized lentiviral vector and                the CAR gene in the presence of T4 ligase,                transforming the competent cells using the ligation                mixture by spreading over the LB agar plate and                keeping in an incubator overnight, growing the                clones observed on the plate in LB broth medium,                and confirming the presence of gene of interest was                by polymerase chain reaction and confirming that                all the clones had insert of the expected size and                selecting the clone having intact reading frame and                no mutation;
   prepairing lenti-CD19 CAR viral vector,                comprising confirming the sequence of all the three                clones containing CAR gene sequences by sequencing,                first clone, lentiviral vector with third                generation CAR gene containing both CD28 and 41BB                (CD19-CD28-41BB-CD3z), second clone, lentiviral                vector with second generation CAR gene containing                CD28 single domain (CD19-CD28-CD3z), and third                clone, lentiviral vector with second generation CAR                gene containing 41BB signaling domain                (CD19-41BB-CD3z), preparing the Midiprep of all the                clones and used that for transfection using 293T                cells initially, preparing the maxiprep of the                lentiviral transfer vectors containing CD19-CAR and                packaging vectors using the endotoxin free maxiprep                plasmid preparation kit, growing the transformed                bacteria harboring the clone in 700 ml LB broth and                pelleting the cells by centrifuging the broth                culture at 4000 revolutions/minute for 15 minutes,                centrifuging, suspending the cell pellet in the                resuspension buffer by vortexing, recovering the                plasmid following extraction, dissolving the DNA                pellet in 1.2 ml TE buffer, and observing the                concentration of plasmids in Midiprep was much                lower compared to the Maxiprep as expected, several                aliquots of each plasmid can be made and stored at                -20&#176; C; and
   transfecting 293T cells for generating                lentiviral particles containing CAR gene,                comprising using 293 T cell line for transfection                for preparing lentiviral particles, culturing the                293 T cells in RPMI medium containing 10% fetal                bovine serum and L-glutamine without antibiotics,                using the purified plasmids for the transfection of                293T cells, transfecting the purified lentiviral                transfer vector containing CAR gene along with the                packaging vectors into 293T cells, seeding the 293T                cells on to a T25 culture flask in 5 ml RPMI medium                with 10% FBS without antibiotics one the day prior                to the transfection, where the flask contains                monolayer confluence of 60-70% next day and it is                used for transfection, diluting the plasmids in 0.5                ml of Opti-MEM medium in tube-1 and the                transfection reagent was diluted in another 0.5 ml                of Opti-MEM medium in tube-2, allowing the plasmids                to stand for 30 minutes at room temperature, where                both the plasmid (tube-1) and transfection reagent                (tube-2) content are mixed together and allowed to                stand for 45 minutes, taking out the 293T cell                flask from the incubator and removing the old media                and discarding, washing the monolayer with 2ml of                Opti-MEM medium gently, adding fresh 1 ml Opti-MEM,                adding the plasmid-transfection reagent complex to                the flask gently and mixing uniformly gently for                uniform distribution of the complex, incubating the                flask at 37&#176; C incubator with 5% carbon                dioxide, adding 2 ml of fresh Opti-MEM medium after                8 hours of incubation and keeping back the flask in                to the carbon dioxide incubator for overnight                incubation, adding 4 ml RPMI medium containing 20%                FBS without antibiotics on next day and incubating                back in to the carbon dioxide incubator and                observing the cells everyday under the inverted                microscope, harvesting the supernatant containing                viral particles after 72 hours following                transfection, and determining the concentration of                viral particles by measuring the p24 protein using                standard commercially available kit, and aliquoting                and storing the lentiviral particles at                -80&#176; C freezer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation: No preparation method is given.                  Preferred Components: The CAR T cell product (P2)                  comprising second generation CAR gene                  CD19-CD28-CD3z comprising the nucleotide                  sequences of single chain variable region of                  anti-CD19 antibody, signaling domain of CD28,                  transmembrane region CD8a, CD8a Hinge region, and                  CD3z signaling domain along with leader and                  linker sequences, where in the CAR gene                  nucleotide sequence is as per SEQ ID NO: 2                  (comprising a fully defined sequence of 1476                  nucleotides as given in the specification). The                  CAR T cell product (P3) comprising second                  generation CAR gene CD19- 41BB-CD3z comprising                  the nucleotide sequences of single chain variable                  region of anti-CD19 antibody, signaling domain of                  41BB, transmembrane region CD8a, CD8aHinge                  region, and CD3z signaling domain along with                  leader and linker sequences, where in the CAR                  gene nucleotide sequence is as per SEQ ID NO: 3                  (comprising a fully defined sequence of 1479                  nucleotides as given in the specification). The                  antibody portion or the signalling domains can be                  switched by restriction digestion using                  restriction enzymes. The CAR gene is the third                  generation CD19-CD28-41BB-CD3z CAR gene sequence                  comprising DNA encoding a chimeric antigen                  receptor (CAR) protein which is at least 90%                  identical to SEQ ID NO: 1. The second generation                  CAR gene sequence selected from CD19-CD28-CD3z                  and CD19-41BB-CD3z sequences comprising DNA                  encoding a chimeric antigen receptor (CAR)                  protein which is at least 90% identical to SEQ ID                  NO: 2 and SEQ ID NO: 3 (comprising a fully                  defined sequence of 1479 nucleotides as given in                  the specification), respectively. The CAR T cell                  products are created by polymerase chain reaction                  and restriction enzyme which cuts at the critical                  junction without compromising the functional                  integrity of CAR gene. The restriction enzyme                  includes Hpa 1, which is inserted between the                  hinge region and single chain antibody sequence,                  and Bam HI, which is located at the junction                  between CD3z signaling domain and co- stimulatory                  domain or combinations thereof or along with                  suitable restriction enzymes to cut the vector                  sequences to replace the single chain variable                  chain of antibody sequence or signaling domain                  sequences. The CAR T cell product allows                  replacing the antibody domain or the signaling                  domain with an alternative antibody domain or                  alternative signaling domain without affecting                  the functional integrity of the vector by                  restriction digestion. The alternative antibody                  domain includes anti-B7-H3, BCMA, carbonic                  anhydrase IX (CAIX), cluster of differentiation                  123 (CD123), CD133, CD138, CD147, CD19, CD22,                  CD30, CD33, CD38, CD4, CD5, CD7, CD70, CEA,                  epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR), and                  vascular endothelial growth factor receptor 2                  (VGFR2) antibody sequences. The alternative                  signaling domain are selected from ICOS, OX40 and                  Fc&#951; R1&#947; . The CAR T cell                  product is used for parenteral or local                  administration in the patient. The CAR gene                  sequence is cloned into a DNA expression vector,                  lentiviral vector, adenoviral vector,                  adeno-associated viral vector, and transposon                  vector. The CAR T cell product is an engineered                  cell comprising DNA encoding a chimeric antigen                  receptor (CAR) protein comprises an amino acid                  sequence which is at least 90% identical to SEQ                  ID NO: 4 (comprising a fully defined sequence of                  sequence of 533 amino acids as given in the                  specification), SEQ ID NO: 5 (comprising a fully                  defined sequence of sequence of 491 amino acids                  as given in the specification), or SEQ ID NO: 6                  (comprising a fully defined sequence of sequence                  of 492 amino acids as given in the                  specification), respectively. The DNA encoding                  the CAR is integrated into the genome of the cell                  or remain as an extra chromosomal DNA. The CAR T                  cell product is manufactured using the soluble                  anti-CD3 and anti-CD28 antibodies based                  activation of T cell without utilizing anti-CD3                  bead or anti-CD28 bead. The CAR T cell product is                  manufactured using cells of peripheral blood                  mononuclear cells without any need for                  purification of T cells, where the T cells are                  activated using anti-CD3 and anti-CD28 antibodies                  and addition of interleukin, and then infecting                  using CAR gene containing lentiviral particles to                  generate CAR T cells. The CAR T cell product is                  manufactured using T cells recovered from the in                  vitro cultures of macrophages, nurse macrophages,                  and lympho-myeloid niches following activation                  and/or addition of interleukin and infecting them                  with CAR gene containing lentiviral particles to                  generate CAR T cells. The interleukin includes                  interleukin-2, interleukin-15, interleukin-12,                  interleukin-18, interleukin- 21 and                  interleukin-7.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Cell therapy.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of CAR T cell product is by                parenteral route. No dosage details given.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of biological                methods that do not support the claims.

TI Storing post-harvesting tangerine oranges, by picking and transporting tangerine oranges, performing chlorosis process, selecting fruit in grading production line, soaking fruit in preservative, grading, packing and quality testing product.
AU HE X
   YANG C
   SUN J
   LI J
   HUANG R
   ZHAO K
AE GUANGXI YOUNGSHI FRESH FRUIT CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   CHINESE ACAD TROPICAL AGRIC SCI AGRO-PRO (CTAG-C)
   GUANGXI YOUNGSHI FRESH FRUIT CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A3918S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for storing post-harvesting tangerine oranges, involves picking and placing tangerine oranges under the sunshade net in time, and the thickness of the tangerine fruit pile is not greater than 10 cm, lightly placing the harvested tangerine fruit into a plastic transfer basket, loading the plastic transfer basket into a transport vehicle, transporting into the factory, removing the plastic turnover basket from the transport vehicle, and then selecting the tangerine fruit, removing rot fruit, pest fruit and wind damaged fruit, performing chlorosis process, selecting fruit in the grading production line, soaking fruit in preservative in preserving drug pond, grading the fruit size and the fruits of the same batch with relatively better color is sorted out as the display fruit of each piece, performing packing process and quality testing packed product.
   USE - The method is useful for storing post-harvesting tangerine oranges.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures safe and effective preservation the tangerine orange.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for storing post-harvesting tangerine oranges, involves (a) picking and placing tangerine oranges under the sunshade net in time, and the thickness of the tangerine fruit pile is not greater than 10 cm, (b) lightly placing the harvested tangerine fruit into a plastic transfer basket, loading the plastic transfer basket into a transport vehicle, transporting into the factory, (c) removing the plastic turnover basket from the transport vehicle, and then selecting selecting the tangerine fruit, removing rot fruit, pest fruit and wind damaged fruit, (d) performing chlorosis process by (i) performing storage chlorosis treatment by placing tangerine oranges which is mature flesh and still green tangerine oranges, n a chlorotic tank and added with ethylene for chlorosis treatment to control the chlorotic library temperature to plus minus 25 degrees C, relative humidity is plus minus 90%, and processing for 24-30 hours, the air circulation treatment is used to process the cycle once every plus minus 3 minutes, the air replacement cycle is processed once every 1 hour, and the volume fraction of carbon dioxide in the chlorotic reservoir is controlled to be less than 3%, (ii) performing medium-term chlorotic treatment by after the chlorosis treatment is completed, control ethylene concentration and flow rate, and control the chlorotic library temperature to plus minus 25 degrees C, the concentration of ethylene is controlled to plus minus 2 mu l/l, and the flow rate is 4-9 ml/minutes, the relative humidity is plus minus 92%, and the treatment is 30-32 hours, the treatment process is air circulation treatment for one cycle every plus minus 6 minutes, and the air replacement cycle is processed once every 1.5 hours, controlling the volume fraction of carbon dioxide in the chlorotic pond less than 6% and (iii) performing late chlorotic treatment by after the completion of the mid-term chlorosis treatment, control the ethylene concentration and the flow rate, controlling the chlorotic library temperature is plus minus 26 degrees C, the concentration of ethylene is controlled to plus minus 2 mu l/l, the flow rate is 1-3 ml/minute and the relative humidity is plus minus 93%, it is treated for 10-12 hours and air circulation treatment is processed once every plus minus 8min, and the air replacement cycle is processed once every 2 hours, controlling the volume fraction of carbon dioxide in the chlorotic library to be less than 9%, after the treatment is completed, it can be discharged after 3-5 minutes of cold shock treatment, (e) selecting fruit in the grading production line by in the promotion transfer phase, selecting the defective fruit, and selecting the residual fruit position to have sufficient light, the selected defective fruits is divided into saleable sub-fruits and directly scrapped sub-fruits, removing rotten fruit to avoid contamination of other fruits by the bacteria attached to the rotten fruit, which results in shortened shelf life, (f) soaking fruit in the preserving drug pond, where the preserving drug pond is formed by preservative mixed s with 5 times the volume of water, and the preservative is prepared using 5-15 pts. wt. Aloe extract, 10-15 pts. wt. Amaranth extract, 8-16 pts. wt. Platycodon grandiflorum extract, 10-20 pts. wt. dandelion extract, 30-40 pts. wt. Citronella essential oil, 10-20 pts. wt. lemon essential oil, and 5-8 pts. wt. film forming agent, (g) drying soaked fruit at a 35-36 degrees C, grading the fruit size and the fruits of the same batch with relatively better color is sorted out as the display fruit of each piece, (h) performing packing process by placing fruit in strict accordance with the order, and take it lightly, it is strictly forbidden to forcibly store the fruit and place it reasonably, fruits that have been sorted out and have a better appearance color is used as display fruits, and at the same time, the unsuccessful fruit is picked out, (i) quality testing packed product by finding the diseased fruit in the packed product and picking product according to the export standard, the number of failing fruits in each finished product not greater than 3%, according to the domestic sales standard, and the number of failing fruits in each finished product pack not greater than 5% and (j) before loading, the cabinet of the vehicle or the cold chain container must be inspected, and the cabinet must be flat, clean, without odor and water content, if shipped in a cold chain container, it is required to close the door and open the cold air for at least 30 minutes before loading, and when loading the goods, barbaric loading is forbidden, the goods must be stable and palletized, and the movement will be reduced during the journey.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Process: The film forming agent is prepared by mixing 10-15 parts propolis, 10-15 parts guar gum, and 3-5 parts konjac glucomannan into a high-pressure heat preservation tank, adding high pressure heat preservation tank into the high pressure steam pot and adjusting the vacuum of the high pressure steam pot, performing high pressure steam treatment at 0.71 MPa, the steam will be sprayed on the outer surface of each of the high-pressure insulation tanks at a temperature of 110-125 degrees C, after 5-10 minutes of high pressure steam treatment, removing the high pressure insulation tank, the upper cover of the high-pressure heat preservation tank is opened in time, and the modified mixed film-forming agent is obtained by immediately adding 3-5 parts olive oil and grinding content at a high speed of 100-150 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 1-3 minutes. The Citronella essential oil is prepared by washing Citronella and removing roots, placing Citronella in the mantle for preservation, the temperature in the mantle is 2-4 degrees C, the humidity is 60-70%, the oxygen content is 2%, and the preservation is 12-13 days, then, removing stem of the Citronella, and the water is added to the distillation tank in a ratio of 2:1, closing tank opening, then opening the steam, the pressure in the distillation tank is adjusted to 0.4 MPa, and the temperature in the distillation tank is controlled to 75-85 degrees C and continue distilling content for 40 minutes, then adjust the pressure in the distillation tank again, controlling the temperature in the distillation tank to 100-105 degrees C, continue distilling content for 40 minutes, and starting the stirring paddle while stirring to stir, then applying ultrasonic microwave assisted treatment, first adjusting the microwave power to 600-700W for 4-6 minutes, then stopping the microwave treatment for 1 minute, then adjusting the microwave power to 200-300W for 3-5 minutes, then stopping the microwave treatment for 1 minute, then adjusting the microwave power to 60-70W for 1-3 minutes to stop the microwave treatment, then adjusting pressure in the distillation tank, controlling the temperature in the distillation tank to 80-85 degrees C, distilling for 40 minutes, subjecting content to oil-water separator for extracting the Citronella essential oil. The preservative is prepared by weighing and mixing Aloe extract, Amaranth extract, P.grandiflorum extract and dandelion extract, adding mixture to the magnetic dryer, starting the magnetic dryer and pre-treating the mixture magnetically for 4-5 minutes, then add the film forming agent, then adding 5-6 times deionized water and mixing the content, then heating content for 15 minutes with constant stirring at 45 degrees C, then, steam drying and concentrating content, until the original volume is obtained 1.15 times, then adding Citronella essential oil and lemon essential oil and uniformly stirring the content and then ultrasonic treating content for 20-25 seconds, spray drying content, where the ultrasonic wave has a power of 450-550 Hz and the particle size of the preservative after the spray drying treatment is less than 1 mu m. Preferred Components: The Aloe extract contains 12.36-15.68 mg/g Aloe polysaccharide. The Amaranth extract contains 15.39-28.34 mu g/ml oxalic acid. The Platycodon grandiflorum extract contains 8.29-12.30 mg/g saponin. The dandelion extract contains 10.26-19.25 mg/g flavonoids and 3.25-8.20 mg/g total phenols.

TI Desalination cell for providing drinking water from saline water resources, comprises electrode containing sodium, lithium or potassium-containing compound, configured to reduce calcium carbonate formation and/or carbon dioxide gas formation during operation of the desalination cell.
AU TUFFILE C
   METZGER M
   HELLSTROM S
   KUPPAN S
   KORNBLUTH M
   KIM S
   KING S
AE BOSCH GMBH ROBERT (BOSC-C)
   BOSCH GMBH ROBERT (BOSC-C)
   BOSCH GMBH ROBERT (BOSC-C)
GA 202251598W
AB    NOVELTY - A desalination cell comprises electrode containing at least one sodium, lithium or potassium-containing compound The material is configured to reduce calcium carbonate formation and/or carbon dioxide gas formation during operation of the desalination cell.
The electrode of desalination cell comprises a Prussian blue compound (A) and a Prussian blue compound (B). The Prussian blue compounds (A, B) collectively operate in collective voltage window during operation of desalination cell, such that voltage window of Prussian blue compound (A) is less than collective voltage window and voltage window of Prussian blue compound (B) is less than collective voltage window. The Prussian blue compound (A) decomposes into decomposition phase mixture (a) during operation of desalination cell and Prussian blue compound (B) decomposes into decomposition phase mixture (b) during operation of desalination cell. The decomposition phase mixtures (a, b) are different from each other.
   USE - The desalination cell is used to provide drinking water from saline water resources.
   ADVANTAGE - The presence of Prussian blue compounds in the desalination cell electrodes, enables to reduce calcium carbonate formation and/or carbon dioxide gas formation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A desalination cell comprises an electrode containing a material having at least one compound of the formula:
   Ax MIy MIIz (CN)6 (or) Fe4 (M(CN)6)3,
   In formula Ax MIy MIIz (CN)6, A is sodium, lithium or potassium K, 0 is less than or equal to x less than or equal to 2, MI is a metal (I), MII is a metal (II), 1 is less than or equal to y and z is less than or equal to 2. The material is configured to reduce calcium carbonate formation and/or carbon dioxide gas formation during operation of the desalination cell. In formula Fe4 (M(CN)6)3, M is a metal.
   The electrode of the desalination cell comprises a Prussian blue compound (A) and a Prussian blue compound (B) which is different from compound (A). The Prussian blue compounds (A, B) collectively operate in a collective voltage window during operation of the desalination cell, such that voltage window of the Prussian blue compound (A) is less than the collective voltage window and the voltage window of the Prussian blue compound (B) is less than the collective voltage window. The Prussian blue compound (A) decomposes into a decomposition phase mixture (a) during operation of the desalination cell and the Prussian blue compound (B) decomposes into a decomposition phase mixture (b) during operation of the desalination cell. The decomposition phase mixtures (a, b) are different from each other.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graph plotting reaction data for reactions between iron cobaltous cyanide compound (FeCo2 (CN)6) and calcium bicarbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the desalination cell, the metal (I) and metal (II) of the electrode material, are iron, manganese, cobalt, scandium, titanium, chromium, or zinc, respectively. The metal (I) is different from the metal (II). The amount of the metal (II) in the electrode material, is 5-50 wt. percent. The metal (II) is nickel, and/or vanadium.5 to 50 wt.% of the metal (I) and/or metals (II) is substituted with one or more 4d-series elements and/or one or more 5d-series elements. In compound of formula Ax MIy MIIz (CN)6:x is equal to 0, the metal (I) is iron and the metal (II) is cobalt or manganese. The ratio of z to y is 2:1. The compound is iron cobalt cyano compound of formula FeCo2 (CN)6.When the ratio of z to y is 1.6:1, at least one compound is FeMn1.6 (CN)6. The compound of electrode material, is Fe4 (Co(CN)6)3 or Fe4 (Mn(CN)6)3. The Prussian blue compounds (A, B) are independently selected from compound of formula: A x M I y M II z (CN) 6 and Fe4 (M(CN)6)3. The collective voltage window in the desalination cell, is 0.0 - 1.4 volts.

TI Preparing lutein oil suspension comprise e.g.            adding marigold extract into low alcohol solution,            heating, adding potassium hydroxide-alcohol solution,            saponifying, washing saponified product, centrifuging,            mixing resultant lutein crystals with antioxidant,            adding vegetable oil, stirring, preheating.

   ZHU T
   LI Y
   DING Q
   MA Y
AE YUNNAN BORUI BIO-TECH CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 202202794C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lutein oil suspension, comprise e.g.                (i) taking marigold extract with total carotenoids                of greater than 160 g/kg, adding 10-30 wt.% low                alcohol solution, heating at 50-70degrees Celsius,                keeping constant temperature to form mixture of                marigold extract-alcohol solution, adding potassium                hydroxide-alcohol solution into marigold                extract-alcohol solution, saponifying, washing the                saponified product with alcohol aqueous solutions,                and centrifuging to obtain lutein crystals and                drying lutein crystals, and (ii) mixing lutein                crystals with antioxidants, adding vegetable oil,                stirring to obtain lutein premix, turn on the                carbon oxide supercritical equipment, preheating,                pouring the lutein premix from the top of the                extraction kettle, closing the lid of the                extraction kettle, heating, maintaining the                constant temperature and pressure, sampling the                product through the discharge port at the lower end                of the extraction kettle, then recovering part of                the carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing lutein oil                suspension.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple production process and                low labor cost; is suitable for industrial                production; and has high product stability and high                product content.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing lutein oil suspension, comprise (i)                selecting marigold extract with total carotenoids                of greater than 160 g/kg, adding 10-30 wt.% low                alcohol solution, heating at 50-70degrees Celsius,                keeping constant temperature to form mixture of                marigold extract-alcohol solution, adding potassium                hydroxide-alcohol solution into marigold                extract-alcohol solution, and saponifying at                constant temperature to obtain saponified product,                washing the saponified product with alcohol aqueous                solutions of different concentrations, and                centrifuging to obtain lutein crystals and drying                lutein crystals for subsequent use, and (ii) mixing                lutein crystals with a corresponding amount of                antioxidants, adding vegetable oil in a ratio of                250:1-40:1 in terms of the quality of vegetable oil                and lutein crystals, and stirring to obtain lutein                premix, turn on the carbon oxide supercritical                equipment, preheating for 10-30 minutes, pouring                the lutein premix from the top of the extraction                kettle, closing the lid of the extraction kettle,                heating at 50-70degrees Celsius, when the pressure                in the kettle reaches 10-30 MPa, turn off the                carbon dioxide flow rate, and maintaining the                constant temperature and pressure for 3-6 hours,                sampling the product through the discharge port at                the lower end of the extraction kettle, If the                product is qualified, then recovering part of the                carbon dioxide, when the pressure is 2-5 MPa, then                open the lower discharge port, and releasing the                product lutein oil suspension, the carbon dioxide                flow rate is 5-50 kg/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the                  alcohol in the alcohol solution is 1-4C lower                  alcohol. The amount of the alcohol aqueous                  solution is 5-8 times the amount of the                  saponified product.Preferred Method: The step (i), comprises                  adding 40-60% potassium hydroxide-alcohol                  solution into the marigold extract-alcohol                  solution, and carrying out saponification at a                  constant temperature for 3-6 hours, monitoring                  the end point of saponification on thin layer                  chrotagraphy plate to obtain saponified product,                  the alkali solution is an aqueous solution of                  alkali metal hydroxide, the temperature during                  the saponification process is 50-70degrees                  Celsius, and the mass of the marigold extract per                  1 ml of alcohol solution is 1-7 g. In step (i),                  the washing saponification comprises firstly                  washing the saponification using an alcohol                  aqueous solution with a concentration of 10-50%,                  then secondly washing using 50-70% alcohol                  aqueous solution and then third washing using                  70-90% alcohol aqueous solution, washing until                  the alcoholic aqueous solution that flows out is                  yellow, the alcohol in the alcohol aqueous                  solution is 1-4C lower alcohol, and the                  temperature during washing is normal temperature                  washing. In step (i), the drying step comprises                  vacuum drying the lutein crystals, the                  temperature is 45-60degrees Celsius, the vacuum                  degree is 0.05-0.08 MPa, and the vacuum drying                  time is 1-5 days. In step (ii), if a high-content                  lutein oil suspension is prepared, the fluidity                  of the lutein premix is poor at normal                  temperature and pressure, then it can be prepared                  in the following way: turn on the carbon dioxide                  supercritical equipment, preheating for 10-30                  minutes, continuously introducing the carbon                  dioxide into the extraction kettle from the                  bottom of the extraction kettle at a constant                  flow rate using a high-pressure pump, heating at                  50-70degrees Celsius, when the pressure in the                  kettle reaches 10-30 MPa, then the flow rate of                  carbon dioxide is 5-50 kg/minute, turn off the                  carbon dioxide flow rate, heating the lutein                  premix at 50-70degrees Celsius at the same time,                  and pumping the lutein premix into the extraction                  kettle at a constant rate through the entrainer                  pump, and the constant flow rate of the pump is                  25-100 ml/minute, and the constant temperature                  and pressure are maintained for 3-6 hours.
   ADMINISTRATION - oleanthin oil suspension

PT P
PN CN113751050-A
TI Preparing graphitic carbon nitride/iodine composite photocatalyst useful for reducing carbon dioxide in gas-solid phase, comprises e.g. preparing ultra-thin graphitic carbon nitride nanosheets, preparing iodine, dispersing in deionized water, ultrasonically oscillating, filtering, and freeze-drying.
AU CAO S
   LIU H
   WANG W
AE UNIV HEBEI (UYHB-C)
GA 2022057083
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphitic carbon nitride/iodine composite photocatalyst comprises compositing ultra-thin graphitic carbon nitride nanosheets (g-C3N4) with iodine using ultrasonic exfoliation to form photocatalytic material combined with van der Waals forces, where the introduction of iodine improves the carrier separation efficiency on the carbon nitride surface and enhances the carbon dioxide reduction ability. Specifically comprises e.g. (1) placing urea in crucible with lid, adding into muffle furnace for calcining, washing off impurities with ethanol and deionized water, and drying to obtain ultra-thin g-C3N4, transferring the ultra-thin g-C3N4 to an uncovered porcelain crucible, placing in muffle furnace for calcining, cooling to room temperature, and grinding into powder to obtain ultra-thin g-C3N4.
   USE - The graphitic carbon nitride/iodine composite photocatalyst is useful for reducing carbon dioxide in the gas-solid phase.
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst has large specific surface area, rich catalytic active sites, strong visible light absorption properties, and photogenic carrier separation and transfer capabilities, and has strong photocatalytic activity i.e., 7.76 times the catalytic performance of pure gC3N4. The method has simple process and low cost, and is easy to realize large-scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing graphitic carbon nitride/iodine composite photocatalyst comprises compositing ultra-thin graphitic carbon nitride nanosheets (g-C3N4) with iodine using ultrasonic exfoliation to form photocatalytic material combined with van der Waals forces, where the introduction of iodine improves the carrier separation efficiency on the carbon nitride surface and enhances the carbon dioxide reduction ability. Specifically comprises (1) placing urea in crucible with lid, adding into muffle furnace for calcining, washing off impurities with ethanol and deionized water, and drying to obtain ultra-thin g-C3N4, transferring the ultra-thin g-C3N4 to an uncovered porcelain crucible, placing in muffle furnace for calcining, cooling to room temperature, and grinding into powder to obtain ultra-thin g-C3N4, (2) grinding the iodine block into iodine powder, dispersing in deionized water, ultrasonic peeling by an ultrasonic cleaning machine for certain period of time, dispersing the iodine in deionized water, and separating the supernatant to obtain homo dispersed iodine solution, (3) dispersing ultra-thin g-C3N4 nanosheets in deionized water, ultrasonication to form uniform mixture, dropping prepared iodine into the ultra-thin g-C3N4 mixture, and ultrasonically oscillating, and (4) stirring the mixed solution of iodine and g-C3N4, suction filtering with a filter membrane, and placing in freeze dryer for freeze-drying to obtain light red graphitic carbon nitride/iodine composite photocatalyst.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating the method for preparing graphitic carbon nitride/iodine composite photocatalyst (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the calcining is secondary calcining, the dosage of the urea is 20g, subjecting the crucible with cover for first calcining, setting the heating speed to 5degrees Celsius/minutes, heating to 550degrees Celsius, thermally insulating for 2 hours, subjecting the crucible without cover for second calcining, setting the heating speed to 5degrees Celsius/minutes, heating to 500degrees Celsius, and thermally insulating for 2 hours. In the step (2), the mass of the iodine powder is 5 g, dispersing in 1 L deionized water, where the ultrasonic power is 700W and the time is 4 hours, centrifuging twice at 3000 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes. In the step (3), the amount of ultra-thin g-C3N4 dispersed is 0.1g, the dosage of the de-ionized water is 20 ml, ultrasonically mixing time is 2 hours, and magnetically stirring for 12 hours. In the step (4), the suction filtering membrane is 0.15 microm aqueous filter membrane, and the freeze-drying time is 12 hours.

PN CN110085817-A
TI Preparing active bamboo charcoal/sulfur composite material useful in anode anode material for lithium sulfur primary battery comprises e.g. cutting dried bamboo into particles and adding into tube furnace.
AU ZHANG Y
   DU J
   LI P
   PENG F
AE SHENZHEN GAONENGDA BATTERY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201969063R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing active bamboo charcoal/sulfur composite material comprises e.g. cutting dried bamboo into particles, adding into tube furnace under argon protection, increasing the temperature, then heating, then incubating at this temperature, closing argon gas inlet valve, opening the carbon dioxide gas inlet valve to introduce carbon dioxide gas, increasing the temperature, maintaining temperature at 0.5-3 hours to obtain active bamboo charcoal having high conductivity and porous structure; and (ii) ball-milling the activated bamboo charcoal and nano-sulfur powder in a ball mill for 2-4 hours, placing the mixture obtained by the ball milling in reaction vessel with polytetrafluoroethylene substrate as substrate, heating, holding the reaction time for 10-20 hours, the reaction temperature is 100-300 degrees C to obtain active bamboo charcoal/sulfur composite lithium sulfur battery anode material and the mass ratio of active bamboo charcoal to nano sulfur powder is 1:1-5.
   USE - The composite material is useful in anode anode material for lithium sulfur primary battery, preferably lithium sulfur primary battery is button type CR2025 half battery (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material solves the poor conductivity of anode material of the lithium-sulfur primary battery, serious volume expansion and low sulfur loading, improves the discharge efficiency of the lithium-sulfur primary battery as a whole, overcomes the safety performance and energy density shortcomings of the existing battery capacity in the current lithium battery technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing active bamboo charcoal/sulfur composite material comprises (i) cutting the dried bamboo into particles having a size of 0.1-2 mm, adding into a tube furnace under argon protection, increasing the temperature to 200-400 degrees C at a heating rate of 5-20 degrees C/minute, then heating up to 400-700 degrees C at a heating rate of 1-10 degrees C/minutes, then incubating at this temperature for 1-5 hours, closing argon gas inlet valve, opening the carbon dioxide gas inlet valve to introduce carbon dioxide gas, increasing the temperature to 700-1200 degrees C at a temperature rising rate of 5-20 degrees C, maintaining the temperature at 0.5-3 hours to obtain active bamboo charcoal having high conductivity and porous structure; and (ii) ball-milling the activated bamboo charcoal and nano-sulfur powder in a ball mill for 2-4 hours, placing the mixture obtained by the ball milling in reaction vessel with polytetrafluoroethylene substrate as substrate, heating, holding the reaction time for 10-20 hours, the reaction temperature is 100-300 degrees C to obtain active bamboo charcoal/sulfur composite lithium sulfur battery anode material and the mass ratio of active bamboo charcoal to nano sulfur powder is 1:1-5.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The bamboo is moso bamboo, bamboo or bamboo.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inlet rate of the argon gas and the carbon dioxide gas in the tubular furnace is 10-200 ml/minute. The ball mill has a rotation speed of 100-500 rotations/minute.

PN CN109725238-A
TI Transformer online monitoring method, involves predicting early failure of transformer by monitoring composition and content of fault gas dissolved in transformer oil, followed by issuing fault indication of arc in oil and paper.
AU HAN Z
   FENG X
   DING Y
   ZHOU Y
   SHANG X
   LI C
   ZHOU X
AE JIAXING POWER SUPPLY CO STATE GRID ZHEJI (SGCC-C)
GA 201942783Y
AB    NOVELTY - A transformer online monitoring method involves predicting an early failure of the transformer by monitoring a composition and content of a fault gas dissolved in a transformer oil, when detected components of the fault gas dissolved in the transformer oil, dissolving the fault gas in the transformer oil, issuing a fault message indicating partial discharge of the oil-paper insulation, when detected the components of the fault gas dissolved in the transformer oil, dissolving the fault gas in the transformer oil, issuing a fault indication of spark discharge in the oil, when detected the components of the fault gas dissolved in the transformer oil, dissolving the fault gas in the transformer oil, issuing a fault indication of the arc in the issuing a fault indication of spark discharge in the oil, when detected the components of the fault gas dissolved in the transformer oil are hydrogen, acetylene, methane, ethylene and ethane.
   USE - Transformer online monitoring method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables online monitoring transformer by performing timely maintenance of the transformer and prolonging service life of the transformer and preventing an occurrence of catastrophic accidents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A transformer online monitoring method involves predicting an early failure of the transformer by monitoring a composition and content of a fault gas dissolved in a transformer oil in real time, when it is detected components of the fault gas dissolved in the transformer oil are hydrogen, methane, carbon monoxide, ethylene, ethane and carbon dioxide, dissolving the fault gas in the transformer oil of hydrogen, methane, carbon monoxide, ethylene, ethane and carbon dioxide, when the set value is reached, issuing a fault message indicating partial discharge of the oil-paper insulation, when detected the components of the fault gas dissolved in the transformer oil are hydrogen and acetylene, dissolving the content of the fault gas in the transformer oil of hydrogen and acetylene reaches a set value, issuing a fault indication of spark discharge in the oil, when detected the components of the fault gas dissolved in the transformer oil are hydrogen, acetylene, methane, ethylene and ethane, dissolving the fault gas in the transformer oil of hydrogen, acetylene, methane, ethylene and ethane reach the set value, issuing a fault indication of the arc in the oil, when detected the components of the fault gas dissolved in the transformer oil are hydrogen, acetylene, methane, ethylene, ethane, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, dissolving the fault gas in the transformer oil of hydrogen, acetylene, methane, ethylene, ethane, carbon monoxide, reaching the carbon dioxide content to the set value and issuing a fault indication of the arc in the oil and paper.

AU YEUL K S
AE URIM ECHO LTD (URIM-Non-standard)
GA 201940664M
AB    NOVELTY - Buoy comprises a plasma reaction part provided at one side of the buoy and provided with an inflow portion for introducing water molecules, a first flow path for introducing and transferring water molecules from the inflow portion, a spraying unit for directly converting the water molecules flowing into the first flow path into water molecules and fine particles and injecting the water molecules into the plasma reaction part, a plasma generating unit for generating oxygen and hydroxide plasma by discharging water molecules and fine particles from the plasma reaction part, a carbon dioxide storage part for storing and discharging carbon dioxide for reacting with the hydroxy plasma, a fluorescent material coating portion for coating fluorescent material on the inner wall of the plasma reaction part at a predetermined interval, and a transparent window provided to identify the fluorescent material inside the plasma reaction part.
   USE - Used as buoy.
   ADVANTAGE - The buoy has fluorescent display- and ultraviolet shielding functions, strong alkaline resistance, and high durability, and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Buoy comprises a plasma reaction part provided at one side of the buoy and provided with an inflow portion for introducing water molecules, a first flow path for introducing and transferring water molecules from the inflow portion, a spraying unit for directly converting the water molecules flowing into the first flow path into water molecules and fine particles and injecting the water molecules into the plasma reaction part, a plasma generating unit for generating oxygen and hydroxide plasma by discharging water molecules and fine particles from the plasma reaction part, a carbon dioxide storage part for storing and discharging carbon dioxide for reacting with the hydroxy plasma, a fluorescent material coating portion for coating fluorescent material on the inner wall of the plasma reaction part at a predetermined interval, and a transparent window provided to identify the fluorescent material inside the plasma reaction part. The spraying unit includes a heater part for heating water molecules flowing from the first flow path, and a nozzle unit for discharging the water molecules heated by the heater part into fine particles inside the plasma reaction part. The fluorescent material coating portion includes fluorescent materials having different fluorescent colors depending on pH. The fluorescent material is poly(N-phenylmaleimide) synthesized by using N-phenylmaleimide as monomer. The buoy is coated with buoy coating composition. The buoy coating composition comprises agalmatolite, polyurea coating agent, ethylene vinyl acetate, a UV absorber and fluorescent material. The polyurea coating agent includes prepolymer, curing agent and antifoaming agent. The fluorescent material is polymaleimide-based polymer.

PN CN108714962-A
TI Preparing carbon-fixed concrete prefabricated            portion using waste concrete involves using crushing            equipment for preliminary crushing pretreatment,            milling and sieving treatment of waste concrete to            obtain desired coarse aggregate.
AU WANG S
   JIANG H
   LU L
   YIN H
   ZHANG Z
AE XUZHOU INST TECHNOLOGY (XUZH-C)
GA 201886358J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon-fixed concrete prefabricated                portion using waste concrete involves using                crushing equipment for preliminary crushing                pretreatment, milling and sieving treatment of the                waste concrete to obtain the desired coarse                aggregate and fine aggregate. The industrial waste                gas is first subjected to dust removal treatment                through a dust filter. The dedusted industrial                waste gas is mixed with normal temperature air, so                that the temperature of the mixed industrial waste                gas is 30-40 degrees C. The ratio of the cement is                determined according to the performance parameters                required for the concrete precast. The water,                coarse aggregate, fine aggregate, cement are mixed                in mixing vessel to form a concrete mixture. The                mixing and stirring time is 10-15 minutes, and the                industrial waste gas is continuously injected into                the mixing container during the mixing process                until the end of the mixing and stirring, and the                concrete mixture is carbonized with carbon dioxide                in the industrial waste gas.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon-fixed concrete                prefabricated portion using waste concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare carbon-fixed                concrete prefabricated portion which reduces                content of carbon dioxide in the industrial waste                gas, ensure required strength of the concrete                preform, and realize resource utilization of the                waste concrete.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The mixing and stirring time is 10-15 minutes,                and the industrial waste gas is continuously                injected into the mixing container during the                mixing process until the end of the mixing and                stirring, and the concrete mixture is carbonized                with carbon dioxide in the industrial waste gas,                and industrial waste gas after carbonization                reaction is collected. The concrete mixture and                steel bars are placed in the preform mold and                ensured required vibrating quality of the concrete.                The preform mold is put into the closed concrete                carbon fixation chamber, and then injected into the                concrete carbon fixation chamber after the                industrial waste gas. The humidity in the concrete                carbon fixation chamber is 35-65% through the                humidifier so that the carbon dioxide reaction                between the concrete mixture and the industrial                waste gas continues to be carbonized, and the                industrial waste gas after the carbonization                reaction is collected, and the concrete is in the                preform mold. The curing time is not less than 28                days. The industrial waste gas after the                carbonization reaction is collected and mixed with                the normal temperature air and the industrial waste                gas which is dedusted, so that the temperature of                the mixed industrial waste gas is 30-40 degrees C,                and injected into the concrete carbon fixation                chamber. The cycle is repeated until the final                curing time is reached after the concrete curing                time is reached, and the prepared concrete preform                is taken out from the preform mold.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The concrete carbon                  fixation chamber is a transparent rubber tank,                  and the volume is 3-5 times of the volume of the                  preform mold. The reinforcing bars of the                  concrete prefabricated parts are rustproof steel                  bars or ordinary steel bars which is subjected to                  rust prevention pretreatment. The cement is                  ordinary Portland cement.

PN CN114807014-A
TI Relieving pancreatic cancer immunosuppressive            microenvironment involves growing mouse luciferase            stable pancreatic cancer Panc02 cells in Dulbecco's            Modified Eagle medium, adding fetal bovine serum,            penicillin and streptomycin, and adding carbon dioxide            to cells for culturing in incubator.
AU CHEN L
   LUO J
   ZENG Y
   SHI Z
   ZHANG T
   WANG X
   HUANG P
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2022A21779
AB    NOVELTY - Relieving pancreatic cancer immunosuppressive                microenvironment involves growing the mouse                luciferase stable pancreatic cancer Panc02 cells in                Dulbecco's Modified Eagle medium (DMEM), adding 10%                fetal bovine serum (FBS), 1 wt.% penicillin and                streptomycin, and adding 5% carbon dioxide at                37&#8451; to the cells for culturing in an                incubator. The luciferase stable pancreatic cancer                Panc02 cells are injected into pancreas of a model                mouse, in-situ Panc02 mouse pancreatic cancer model                is established, in-situ tumor growth is monitored                by injecting fluorescein at each time point and                imaging through an IVIS(RTM: in vivo imaging                system) system after exposing for 5 seconds on the                sixth day after implantation, and mice with tumors                are separated.
   USE - Method for relieving pancreatic cancer                immunosuppressive microenvironment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to utilize ultrasonic                cavitation reshape the gene expression profile of                tumor tissues, down-regulate a plurality of immune                checkpoint molecules including forkhead box P3                (FOXP3), cluster of differentiation 274 (CD274),                cytotoxic T-lymphocyte-associated protein 4 (CTLA4)                and some co-stimulatory molecules, and relieve                inhibitory tumor immune microenvironment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Relieving pancreatic cancer immunosuppressive                microenvironment involves growing the mouse                luciferase stable pancreatic cancer Panc02 cells in                Dulbecco's Modified Eagle medium (DMEM), adding 10%                fetal bovine serum (FBS), 1 wt.% penicillin and                streptomycin, and adding 5% carbon dioxide at                37&#8451; to the cells for culturing in an                incubator. The luciferase stable pancreatic cancer                Panc02 cells are injected into pancreas of a model                mouse, in-situ Panc02 mouse pancreatic cancer model                is established, in-situ tumor growth is monitored                by injecting fluorescein at each time point and                imaging through an IVIS(RTM: in vivo imaging                system) system after exposing for 5 seconds on the                sixth day after implantation, and mice with tumors                are separated. The mouse pancreatic cancer Panc02                cells are inoculated in DMEM growth medium, 10% FBS                is added, 1% penicillin and streptomycin are added,                and cells are cultured at 37&#8451;                concentration of 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator.                The Panc02 cells is injected into right back of                mouse to construct a distant tumor model. An 100                microliter (&#956;L) phospholipid-coated sulfur                hexafluoride microbubbles (Sonovue (RTM:                second-generation ultrasound contrast agent),                Bracco, Italy) is injected into the tail vein, and                immediately perform ultrasonic irradiation (1.0                megahertz (MHz), 0.5 watt per square centimeter                (W/cm2), 20% duty cycle) on pancreatic cancer in                situ tumors for 5 minutes. The Panc02 tumors of                different treatments are collected for                transcriptome analysis, and high-quality reads that                passed the Illumina quality filter are retained for                sequence analysis and mapped to GRCm38.

AU WANG L
   LIN W
   WANG P
   TIAN H
   SUN M
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)

GA 202137275J
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises 20-70 wt.% clay, 10-50 wt.% inorganic oxide, 18-60 wt.% zeolite beta (BEA) structure molecular sieve, and 0.1-10 wt.% noble metal; where the noble metal is at least one of gold, silver, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, platinum, iridium and osmium.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for producing high propylene-to-ethylene ratio product in the catalytic conversion of gasoline (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst can realize the catalytic conversion of gasoline and efficient utilization of carbon dioxide under mild conditions, and the yield ratio of propylene and ethylene is high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for
   (1) a method for preparing the catalyst for producing high propylene-to-ethylene ratio product comprising either mixing and pulping the noble metal salt and/or load type noble metal, zeolite beta structure molecular sieve, clay, inorganic oxide and/or inorganic oxide precursor, spray drying, and then baking, preferably, the method further comprises introducing auxiliary agent into the catalyst, the introduction mode of the auxiliary agent comprises the load type noble metal containing auxiliary agent, zeolite beta structure molecular sieve, clay, inorganic oxide and/or inorganic oxide precursor mixing and pulping or (1) loading at least part of the noble metal on the zeolite beta structure molecular sieve, obtaining zeolite beta structure molecular sieve containing noble metal, (2) using the zeolite beta structure molecular sieve containing noble metal, optionally the remaining part of noble metal salt and/or load type noble metal, clay and inorganic oxide and/or inorganic oxide precursor mixing and pulping, spray drying, then baking, preferably, the method further comprises introducing auxiliary agent into the catalyst, the method further comprises in the step (1), introducing auxiliary agent into the catalyst or loading at least part of noble metal and auxiliary agent on the Zeolite beta structure molecular sieve, obtaining zeolite beta structure molecular sieve containing noble metal and auxiliary agent; and
   (2) a method for catalytic conversion of gasoline, comprising the gasoline, carbon dioxide and the catalyst for producing high propylene ethylene product and contact reaction, preferably, the condition of the contact reaction comprises: the temperature is 400-850 degrees C, the pressure is 0.1-2 MPa, the weight ratio of the carbon dioxide and gasoline is 0.22-3:1, gasoline mass space velocity is 0.3-10 hour-1, more preferably, the conditions of the contact reaction include: the temperature is 500-650 degrees C, the pressure is 0.1-0.3 MPa, the weight ratio of the carbon dioxide and the gasoline is 0.25-2:1, and the gasoline mass space velocity is 0.5-5 h-1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The noble metal is at least one of gold, ruthenium, palladium and iridium. The zeolite beta structure molecular sieve is at least one of beta molecular sieve, SSZ-33 molecular sieve and SSZ-26 molecular sieve. The clay is at least one of kaolin, montmorillonite, diatomite, attapulgite, sepiolite, halloysite, hydrotalcite, bentonite and rectorite. The inorganic oxide is the group consisting of at least one of silicon oxide, alumina, alumina-silica, zirconia, titanium oxide, boron oxide, amorphous silica-alumina, aluminum phosphate, tungsten oxide, tungsten oxide-zirconium oxide, molybdenum oxide, molybdenum oxide-zirconium oxide, molybdenum oxide-titanium oxide, tungsten oxide-titanium oxide, tin oxide, zinc oxide, copper oxide, nickel oxide, cobalt oxide, vanadium oxide and niobium oxide. In the catalyst, Based on the total weight, the content of the clay is 30-60 wt.%, preferably 30-55 wt.%, the content of the inorganic oxide is 10-50 wt.%, preferably 10-45 wt.%, the content of the molecular sieve with Zeolite beta structure is 19-55 wt.%, preferably is 23-45 wt.% counted by element, and the content of the noble metal is 0.5-4 wt.%, preferably is 1.2-3.2 wt.%. Based on the total weight of the load-type noble metal, the content of the noble metal is 5-20 wt.%. Preferred Composition: The catalyst further comprises an auxiliary agent including the group consisting of IIA, IIIA, IVA, VA, lanthanides, scandium, yttrium, hafnium, tantalum, chromium, magnesium, rhenium, iron and cadmium, preferably at least one of calcium, iron, gallium, indium, bismuth, lanthanum and manganese, more preferably the auxiliary agent is gallium, preferably, based on the total weight of the catalyst, the content of the auxiliary agent is 0.5-5 wt.% based on the oxide. Preferred Method: The method for preparing the catalyst for producing high propylene-to-ethylene ratio product specifically comprises (1) mixing and pulping the noble metal salt and/or load type noble metal, part of clay and part of inorganic oxide and/or inorganic oxide precursor, spray drying to obtain the first solid product, (2) the Zeolite beta structure molecular sieve, the rest part of clay, the rest part of inorganic oxide and/or inorganic oxide precursor mixing and pulping, spray drying to obtain the second solid product, (3) mixing first the solid product and the second solid product, and then baking, preferably, the clay in step (1) accounts for 1-15 % of the total weight of the clay, Preferably, step (1) the inorganic oxide and/or inorganic oxide precursor accounts for the inorganic oxide and/or inorganic oxide precursor total weight of the total weight of 1-20%, preferably, the method further comprises in the step (1), introducing auxiliary agent into the catalyst, step (1) comprises: the auxiliary agent precursor, noble metal salt and/or load type noble metal, part of clay and part of inorganic oxide and/or inorganic oxide precursor mixing and pulping or (I) using the Zeolite beta structure molecular sieve, clay, precursor of inorganic oxide and/or inorganic oxide precursor and optionally partially noble metal salt and/or load type noble metal mixing and pulping, spray drying to obtain the first solid product, then baking, (II) loading the noble metal onto the first solid product, preferably, the method further comprises introducing auxiliary agent into the catalyst, the method further comprises in the step (II), introducing auxiliary agent into the catalyst, further preferably, step (II) comprises: loading noble metal and the additive onto the first solid product. The baking condition comprises: the baking is carried out under the air atmosphere, inert atmosphere or water vapor atmosphere, the baking temperature is 400-800 degrees C, and the baking time is 0.5-8 hours.
