The                good culture bag is put into the autoclave together                with the turnover basket for high temperature and                high pressure sterilization. The deflated culture                bag is inoculated with the maitake mushroom culture                strain in the aseptic inoculation room using a                fully automatic inoculation machine, and is                automatically sealed with a sealing machine. The                planted culture bag and the turnover basket are                transferred from the inoculation room to the                culture rack in the culture room at 20-26 degrees                C, with air humidity of 60-65%, carbon dioxide                concentration below 3500ppm, and dark light.
   USE - Method for cultivating organic Tricholoma                matsutake using soybean curb residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the yield by more than                30%, completely satisfies the industrial                production, produces organic, safe, no pesticide                residue, no mercury, provides a cadmium-free fuzz                product, which provides safe and nutritious health                food for human. The method is efficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cultivating organic Tricholoma matsutake tofu                residue in factories, comprises adding fresh,                mildew-free broad-leaved wood chips, corn cobs,                wheat bran and bean curd dregs into the mixer and                stirring, and adding 60-65 wt.% water, adjusting                the pH value to 5.5-6.5. The nutrient base is put                into the bacteria bag into the turnover basket. The                good culture bag is put into the autoclave together                with the turnover basket for high temperature and                high pressure sterilization. The deflated culture                bag is inoculated with the maitake mushroom culture                strain in the aseptic inoculation room using a                fully automatic inoculation machine, and is                automatically sealed with a sealing machine. The                planted culture bag and the turnover basket are                transferred from the inoculation room to the                culture rack in the culture room at 20-26 degrees                C, with air humidity of 60-65%, carbon dioxide                concentration below 3500ppm, and dark light. The                cultivating bag and the turnover basket are                transferred to the germination room at 16-23                degrees C, the humidity is above 90%, the carbon                dioxide concentration is below 2000ppm, and the                light is above 300lx to make mushrooms tidy and                concentrated. The temperature is controlled at                16-22?C, the humidity is above 90%, the carbon                dioxide concentration is below 1500ppm, and the                light is above 300lx, so that the maitake mushroom                can grow up quickly and neatly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Compositions: The culture medium                  comprises 30-60 wt.% of broad-leaved wood chips,                  10-50 wt.% corn cobs, 1-15 wt.% wheat bran, and                  1-20 wt.% bean curd residue. The weight of the                  medium in each culture bag is 1-2.3 kg, and the                  inoculation amount of the maitake mushroom                  species is 1 kg. Preferred Conditions: The                  cultivation time in a dark light environment is                  35-60 days, the germination time is 7-15 days,                  and the fruiting time is 7-15 days.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PN CN112293542-A
TI Processing coffee fruit by washing coffee fruits,            sterilizing, drying, classifying, adding lactic acid            bacteria, sealing, fermenting in oxygen-free            environment, heating for esterification reaction,            drying and baking coffee fruits.
AU TAO Z
   MA J
   WANG M
   XIA S
AE UNIV PUER (UYPU-Non-standard)
GA 202113714J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for processing coffee fruit by                anaerobic fermentation involves selecting and                removing bad, overripe and underripe fruits of the                picked coffee fruits, washing the selected coffee                fruits with water, subjecting the cleaned coffee                fruits to steam sterilization, drying the whole                coffee fruit, classifying the air-dried coffee                fruit using a screening machine, adding the coffee                fruits of the same category into a stainless steel                fermentation tank with sealing function, adding                carbon dioxide to the stainless steel fermentation                tank to exhaust air, sealing the stainless steel                fermentation tank, fermenting the coffee fruit                under a carbon dioxide environment, adding lactic                acid bacteria into the fermentation tank, sealing                the stainless steel fermentation tank, continuing                fermenting in an oxygen-free environment, heating                the fermentation tank for esterification reaction,                naturally drying and baking coffee fruits.
   USE - The method is useful for processing coffee                fruit.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces non-controllable factors of                environment to the fermentation process, increases                roundness and sweetness of coffee beans, since                fermentation is carried out for a long time in an                anaerobic environment, avoids uncomfortable dryness                and acetic acid, is convenient to control variables                such as pectin residue, temperature and time and                processes coffee fruit which has slight sweet and                sour feeling and perfect mellowness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for processing coffee fruit by                anaerobic fermentation involves (a) selecting and                removing bad, overripe and underripe fruits of the                picked coffee fruits, and washing the selected                coffee fruits with water, (b) subjecting the                cleaned coffee fruits obtained in step (a) to steam                sterilization to obtain sterile coffee fruits, (c)                drying the whole coffee fruit obtained in step (b)                by blower wind field and performing fruit quality                density preliminary selection, (d) classifying the                air-dried coffee fruit obtained in the step (c)                using a screening machine for fruit with consistent                size and mass density, (e) adding the coffee fruits                of the same category obtained in step (d) into a                stainless steel fermentation tank with sealing                function, adding carbon dioxide to the stainless                steel fermentation tank to exhaust air, sealing the                stainless steel fermentation tank and fermenting                the coffee fruit under a carbon dioxide                environment, (f) adding lactic acid bacteria into                the fermentation tank, sealing the stainless steel                fermentation tank and continuing fermenting the                coffee fruit in an oxygen-free environment, (g)                removing the sealing of the fermentation tank and                heating the fermentation tank to cause the lactic                acid and ethanol in the fermentation tank to                undergo an esterification reaction, (h) naturally                drying the fermented coffee fruit obtained in step                (g) and then shelling and (i) baking the coffee                fruits obtained in step (h) to obtain target coffee                beans.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves                  performing steam sterilization at 70-110 degrees                  C for 10-200 seconds. The step (e) involves                  fermenting at 20-24 degrees C for 5-7 days, where                  the air pressure in the fermentation tank is                  maintained at a normal pressure environment, and                  adding catalyst ion exchange resin into the                  fermentation tank. The step (f) involves                  performing anaerobic fermentation at 36-45                  degrees C for 2-3 days while maintaining the pH                  value of the liquid in the fermentation tank at                  6-8 and air pressure at a normal pressure                  environment. The step (g) involves heating at                  75-110 degrees C for 1-2 hours under atmospheric                  pressure.

PN WO2020205702-A1; AU2020256133-A1; CA3134833-A1; CN113614094-A; KR2021146975-A; EP3947397-A1; US2022169663-A1; JP2022527963-W
TI Metal-organic framework material used for            sequestering e.g. hydrocarbons, comprises metal centers            and multidentate organic ligand comprising reaction            product of vicinal dicarbonyl compound and            amine-substituted salicylic acid.
AU FALKOWSKI J M
   ABDULKARIM M S
   ABNEY C W
   ABDUL-KARIM-MERRIES
   ABNICA-TER-W
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 202098015L
AB    NOVELTY - A metal-organic framework material comprises                several metal centers and a multidentate organic                ligand coordinated via at least two binding sites                to the several metal centers to define at least                partially crystalline network structure having                several internal pores. The multidentate organic                ligand comprises a reaction product of a vicinal                dicarbonyl compound and an amine-substituted                salicylic acid. The multidentate organic ligand                comprises a binding site (a) and a binding site (b)                that are bridged together with a binding site (c).                Each binding sites (a) and (b) comprises a                salicylate moiety. The binding site (c) comprises a                diimine moiety.
   USE - Metal-organic framework material used for                sequestering hydrocarbons such as ethylene and                ethane, and carbon dioxide (all claimed) from fluid                recovered from upstream processing unit such as                methanol producing unit, steam power plant, steam                generator, combustor, oxy-fuel combustor, and ion                transport membrane. Can also be used for gas                storage, gas and liquid separations, isomer                separation and resolution, waste treatment, and                catalysis applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The metal-organic framework material has                excellent tailored properties, and structural and                morphological properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) manufacture of the metal-organic framework                material, which involves combining a metal source                with the multidentate organic ligand and reacting                the metal source with the multidentate organic                ligand under conditions effective to form the                metal-organic framework material having at least                partially crystalline network structure with                several internal pores. The crystalline network                structure comprises several metal centers, which                are coordinated to at least the binding site (a)                and the binding site (b). Each binding sites (a)                and (b) comprises metal (m1). The metal center                coordinated to the binding site (a) comprises the                metal (m1) and the metal center coordinated to the                binding site (b) comprising a metal (m2);
   (2) sequestering of carbon dioxide, which                involves providing the metal-organic framework                material comprising several metal centers                coordinated via at least two binding sites to                5,5'-(((1E,2E)-ethane-1,2-diylidene)bis(azaneylylidene))-bis(2-hydroxybenzoic                acid) to define at least partially crystalline                network structure, contacting the metal-organic                framework material with a fluid comprising carbon                dioxide and sequestering at least a portion of the                carbon dioxide from the fluid into the                metal-organic framework material; and
   (3) sequestering of hydrocarbons, which                involves providing the metal-organic framework                material comprising several metal centers                coordinated via at least two binding sites to                5,5'-(((1E,2E)-ethane-1,2-                diylidene)bis(azaneylylidene))-bis(2-hydroxybenzoic                acid) to define at least partially crystalline                network structure, contacting the metal-organic                framework material with a fluid comprising at least                one of hydrocarbons and sequestering at least a                portion of the at least one hydrocarbons from the                fluid into the metal-organic framework                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The portion of several                  metal centers comprises a divalent metal. The                  binding site (a) is coordinated to a divalent                  metal center (c1) and the binding site (b) is                  coordinated to a divalent metal center (c2). The                  divalent metal center (c1) and the divalent metal                  center (c2) comprises the same divalent metal.                  The divalent metal is an alkaline earth metal,                  preferably magnesium. The metal center (c2)                  comprises a divalent metal differing from the at                  least one metal center (c1) coordinated to the                  binding site (a) and the binding site (b). The                  metal-organic framework material lacks a metal                  center at the binding site (c3) or is                  incompletely occupied with a metal center at the                  binding site (c3). The metal-organic framework                  material comprises a metal center at the binding                  site (c3). The binding sites (a), (b) and (c) are                  coordinated to the same type of metal center. The                  portion of the several metal centers comprises                  magnesium, manganese, chromium, iron, copper,                  cobalt, or nickel. The metal source comprises at                  least one of manganese, chromium, iron, copper,                  cobalt, nickel, palladium, platinum, gold,                  silver, iridium, osmium, ruthenium, and rhodium.                  Preferred Process: The manufacture of the                  metal-organic framework material further involves                  introducing the metal center to the at least                  partially crystalline network structure at the                  binding site (c) by exchanging the metal (m1) or                  metal (m2) coordinated at the binding site (c)                  for different metal and filling an empty binding                  site (c) with different metal.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine-substituted                  salicylic acid is 5-aminosalicylic acid. The                  vicinal dicarbonyl compound is glyoxal. The                  multidentate organic ligand is                  5,5'-(((1E,2E)-ethane-1,2-diylidene)bis-(azaneylylidene))bis(2-hydroxybenzoic                  acid). The hydrocarbons comprise two or more                  different hydrocarbons, in which hydrocarbon (H1)                  is sequestered into the metal-organic framework                  material over a hydrocarbon (H2).

PN CN109054449-A; CN109054449-B
TI Preparation of nano yttrium oxide by mixing peanut cellulose with sulfuric acid, reacting, centrifugally separating, filtering, drying, adding aminating agent, adjusting pH, adding yttrium, introducing carbon dioxide, heating and calcining.
AU ZHOU R
   LIU X
   ZHU D
AE ZHOU R (ZHOU-Individual)
   GANZHOU QICHANG NEW MATERIAL LTD CO (GANZ-Non-standard)
GA 201900887T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nano yttrium oxide involves mixing peanut cellulose with sulfuric acid, stirring and reacting, mixing peanut cellulose mixture with water, centrifugally separating, removing supernatant, dialyzing and freeze-drying; mixing nanocellulose with water, adding sodium bromide, 2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidine-N-oxide reagent and sodium hypochlorite solution, adjusting pH, reacting, adding sodium hydroxide solution, reacting, filtering and drying; arranging modified nanocellulose and N-hydroxysuccinimide, adding 1-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)-3-ethyl-carbodiimide hydrochloride and aminating agent, adjusting pH, reacting, filtering, washing and drying; weighing alcohol solvent, isopropanol, fatty acid, polyallylamine, organic aluminum and organic modified nanocellulose, adding yttrium, and mixing; and introducing carbon dioxide, heating, stirring and reacting, filtering, drying, calcining, mixing nano yttrium oxide and sodium hydroxide solution, soaking, filtering and drying.
   USE - Method for preparing nano yttrium oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The nano yttrium oxide has narrow grain size distribution, small size and excellent dispersibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of nano yttrium oxide comprises:
   (A) mixing peanut cellulose with sulfuric acid at mass ratio of 1:30-1:40, stirring and reacting to obtain peanut cellulose mixture, mixing peanut cellulose mixture with water at mass ratio of 1:8-1:10, centrifugally separating, removing supernatant, repeating centrifugal separation, removing supernatant turbid liquid, dialyzing to neutral pH and freeze-drying to obtain pretreated nanocellulose;
   (B) mixing nanocellulose with water at mass ratio of 1:80-1:100, adding 0.1-0.2 times by mass sodium bromide, 3-4 times by mass 2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidine-N-oxide reagent and 1-2 times by mass sodium hypochlorite solution, adjusting pH to neutral, stirring and reacting, adding sodium hydroxide solution, keeping pH at 7.0-7.5, reacting, filtering and drying to obtain modified nanocellulose;
   (C) arranging modified nanocellulose and N-hydroxysuccinimide at mass ratio of 1:2-1:3, adding 1-2 times by mass 1-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)-3-ethyl-carbodiimide hydrochloride and 1-2 times by mass aminating agent, adjusting pH to 6.0-6.3, stirring and reacting, filtering, washing and drying to obtain organic modified nanocellulose;
   (D) weighing 120-150 pts. wt. alcohol solvent, 38-50 pts. wt. isopropanol, 80-90 pts. wt. fatty acid, 20-30 pts. wt. polyallylamine, 20-28 pts. wt. organic aluminum and 30-40 pts. wt. organic modified nanocellulose, adding yttrium, and stirring and mixing to obtain mixed material; and
   (E) introducing carbon dioxide into mixed material for 2-10 minutes, heating, stirring and reacting, continuously introducing carbon dioxide for 15-20 minutes, filtering, drying to obtain blank, calcining to obtain nano yttrium oxide mixture, mixing nano yttrium oxide and sodium hydroxide solution at mass ratio of 1:10-1:12, soaking, filtering and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aminating agent is ethylenediamine, diethylenetriamine or triethylenetetramine. The alcohol solvent is ethanol, propanol or ethylene glycol. The fatty acid is oleic acid, linoleic acid or linseed oil. The organic aluminum is aluminum isopropoxide or aluminum ethylate.

TI Inducing cytokine-induced killer cells, by adding            placental-derived mononuclear cell suspension to not            inactivated cord blood plasma, adding inactivated cord            blood plasma, culturing, adding GT-T551 medium and            culturing mixture.
AU ZHANG R
   ZHU F
   WANG H
AE CRUILIFE STEM CELL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CRUI-Non-standard)
GA 2018948057
AB    NOVELTY - Inducing cytokine-induced killer (CIK) cells                by placenta-derived mononuclear cell, by (a)                dividing cord blood plasma into two parts,                cryopreserving first cord blood plasma after                inactivation, storing the second cord blood plasma                that is not inactivated at 4 degrees C, adding the                not inactivated cord blood plasma to cell culture                flask, culturing and washing with phosphate                buffered saline (PBS) and GT-T551 medium, (b)                obtaining placental-derived mononuclear cells and                preparing placenta-derived mononuclear cell                suspension using GT-T551 medium, and (c) adding the                cell suspension to cell culture flask in step (a),                simultaneously adding inactivated cord blood plasma                and interferon- gamma , culturing, adding GT-T551                medium, adding interleukin (IL)-1, cluster of                differentiation (CD)3 monoclonal antibody, IL-2 and                cord blood plasma, culturing, on fourth day, adding                GT-T551 medium, adding IL-2, culturing, adding                GT-T551 culture solution, incubating, centrifuging                and collecting precipitate.
   USE - The method is useful for inducing CIK cells by                placenta-derived mononuclear cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method expands amplification number and                improves survival rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for inducing cytokine-induced killer                (CIK) cells by placenta-derived mononuclear cell,                involves (a) dividing cord blood plasma into two                parts, cryopreserving first cord blood plasma at                -20 degrees C after inactivation, storing the                second cord blood plasma that is not inactivated at                4 degrees C, adding the not inactivated cord blood                plasma to cell culture flask, culturing overnight                at 4 degrees C, or at 37 degrees C for 3 hours and                washing once with 10 ml phosphate buffered saline                (PBS) and 10 ml GT-T551 medium, (b) obtaining                placental-derived mononuclear cells and preparing                placenta-derived mononuclear cell suspension having                a cell concentration of 1-2x 106 cells/ml using                GT-T551 medium, and (c) adding the cell suspension                to cell culture flask in step (a), simultaneously                adding 10% inactivated cord blood plasma and 1000                IU/ml interferon- gamma , culturing in 5% carbon                dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 24 hours,                adding GT-T551 medium, adding 100 IU/ml interleukin                (IL)-1, 50 ng/ml cluster of differentiation (CD)3                monoclonal antibody, 300 IU/ml IL-2 and 10% cord                blood plasma, culturing in 5% carbon dioxide                incubator at 37 degrees C, on fourth day, adding 20                ml GT-T551 medium, adding 300 IU/ml IL-2, culturing                in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C,                adding GT-T551 culture solution once every 2 or 3                days, supplementing IL-2 until concentration                reaches 300 IU/ml, incubating in 5% carbon dioxide                incubator at 37 degrees C, on 15th day, collecting                all culture medium and cells in culture flask,                centrifuging at 500 g/minute for 10 minutes, and                collecting precipitate as CIK cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The cord blood plasma is                  prepared by using a sterile blood collection bag                  containing anticoagulant, puncturing umbilical                  vein, collecting cord blood, centrifuging the                  cord blood at 3000 revolutions per minute (rpm)                  for 15 minutes, removing precipitate, and                  recovering supernatant. The placenta-derived                  mononuclear cells are obtained by (a) washing the                  placental tissue with PBS containing 1%                  penicillin-streptomycin double antibody, and                  repeating many times to remove blood clots and                  other impurities from surface of placenta tissue,                  (b) cutting the placenta tissue into several                  tissue pieces having a volume of 1-5 mm3, adding                  the tissue blocks into 200 mesh screen, rinsing                  and filtering with PBS containing 1%                  penicillin-streptomycin double antibody and                  collecting filtrate, (c) centrifuging the                  filtrate at 1800 rpm for 10 minutes, removing                  supernatant, resuspending in PBS containing 1%                  penicillin-streptomycin double antibody, slowly                  adding to human peripheral blood lymphocyte                  separation solution in volume ratio of 4:3, and                  centrifuging at 800 g for 15 minutes, (d)                  dividing liquid in centrifuge tube into 4 layers,                  recovering middle white layer cells, resuspending                  in GT-551 medium, and centrifuging at 600 g for                  10 minutes, (e) removing supernatant, collecting                  cells, resuspending in GT-551 medium, and                  centrifuging at 500 g for 10 minutes, and (f)                  removing supernatant, collecting cells, and                  resuspending in CIK complete medium. The                  anticoagulant is heparin or citrate phosphate                  dextrose adenine (CPDA)-1.

PN CN108067239-A; CN108067239-B
TI Preparing large particle copper-bismuth catalyst comprises e.g. preparing acidic solution containing copper salt and cerium salt, preparing precipitant solution, adding bottom water into the reaction kettle, heating and spray-drying.
AU ZHANG Y
   QIAO K
   DUAN R
   BAO H
   FU Q
   WANG L
   HUO W
   ZHANG B
AE SINOPEC FUSHUN RES INST PETROLEUM & PETR (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC FUSHUN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
GA 201843884L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing large particle copper-bismuth catalyst comprises e.g. (i) preparing acidic solution containing copper salt and cerium salt, (ii) preparing precipitant solution, (iii) adding bottom water into the reaction kettle and heating to the reaction temperature by heating, (iv) adopting parallel flow method, adding acidic solution of the step (i) and the precipitant solution of the step (ii) in a dropwise into the reaction vessel, introducing gas carbon dioxide from the bottom of the reactor, (v) aging, (vi) continuously reacting and passing the carbon dioxide gas from the bottom of the reactor, (vii) when the remaining acidic solution is 1/5-1/10 of the total amount of the acidic solution obtained in the step (i), increasing the flow rate of water into the reaction system, where the flow rate is 2-4 times of the total flow rate of the acidic solution and the alkaline solution, (viii) aging, washing and filtering, and (ix) spray-drying to obtain large particle copper-bismuth catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing large particle copper-bismuth catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces product has excellent wear resistance, high activity stability and yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing large particle copper-bismuth catalyst comprises (i) preparing acidic solution containing copper salt and cerium salt, (ii) preparing precipitant solution, (iii) adding bottom water into the reaction kettle and heating to the reaction temperature by heating, (iv) adopting parallel flow method, adding acidic solution of the step (i) and the precipitant solution of the step (ii) in a dropwise into the reaction vessel, introducing gas carbon dioxide from the bottom of the reactor, (v) when the remaining acidic solution is 4/5-9/10 of the total amount of the acidic solution obtained in the step (i), stopping the reaction and aging, (vi) continuously reacting and passing the carbon dioxide gas from the bottom of the reactor, when the remaining acidic solution is 1/2-3/5 of the total amount of the acidic solution obtained in the step (i), adding deionized water into the reaction system, where the flow rate is 0.5-1.5 times of the total flow rate of the acidic solution and the alkaline solution, (vii) when the remaining acidic solution is 1/5-1/10 of the total amount of the acidic solution obtained in the step (i), increasing the flow rate of water into the reaction system, where the flow rate is 2-4 times of the total flow rate of the acidic solution and the alkaline solution, (viii) aging, washing and filtering, and (ix) adding deionized water containing potassium nitrate and sodium hexametaphosphate into the filter cake, stirring uniformly, and spray-drying to obtain large particle copper-bismuth catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the copper salt is copper sulfate, copper nitrate, copper acetate or copper chloride. The molar concentration of copper salt in the acidic solution is controlled at 0.6-3 mol/l. The barium salt is barium nitrate, barium sulfate or barium acetate. The molar concentration of cerium salt in the acidic solution is controlled at 0.01-0.05 mol/l. The pH of the acidic solution is 0-2. In the step (ii), the precipitating agent is sodium carbonate, sodium hydroxide, potassium carbonate, potassium hydroxide, aqueous ammonia, and sodium hydrogencarbonate. The molar concentration of the precipitating agent is 0.1-3 mol/l. In the step (vi), the pH of the reaction is controlled at 5-8, and the reaction temperature is controlled at 30-80 degrees C, volume of carbon dioxide introduced into the reactor is 20-60 wt.%, and the flow rate is 150-1000 ml/minutes. In the step (vi), the pH of the reaction is controlled to 5-8. The reaction temperature is controlled at 30-80 degrees C. The volume of carbon dioxide introduced into the reactor is 20-60%. The flow rate is 150-1000 ml/minutes. In the step (viii), aging time is 0.5-4 hours. The content of copper oxide is 30-80 wt.%. The content of cerium oxide is 1-10 wt.%. The catalyst has particle size is 10-30 mu m at 93 wt.% above. The reaction temperature at 80-100 degrees C. The reaction pressure is 0.5-1.5 MPa. The flow rate of acetylene is 50-150 ml/minutes. The concentration of formaldehyde aqueous solution is 4-25 wt.%. The mass ratio of the catalyst and the formaldehyde aqueous solution added is 1:3-1:10. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), adding bottom water into the reaction kettle, adding 0.15-0.4 times of the acidic solution in the step (i), heating and reacting at 30-80 degrees C. In the step (iv), adding acidic solution and the alkaline solution into the reaction vessel, and maintaining the pH of the reaction at 5-8, controlling the reaction temperature at 30-80 degrees C, filling the bottom of the reactor with carbon dioxide, diluting the carbon dioxide with nitrogen, and where the volume concentration of carbon dioxide is 20-60 wt.%, and the flow rate is 150 to 1000 ml/minutes. In the step (v), controlling the aging temperature at 30-80 degrees C, controlling the aging pH at 5-8, and controlling the aging time for 10-70 minutes. In the step (ix), adding filter cake, deionized water containing potassium nitrate and sodium hexametaphosphate, stirring uniformly, and spray-drying to obtain copper-bismuth catalyst. The amount of potassium nitrate added is 1-20 wt.% of the water quality in the slurry. The dry basis of the slurry is 15-45 wt.%. Sodium hexametaphosphate is added in an amount of 5-25 wt.% of the catalyst. Diethylene glycol is added to the reaction system in an amount of 0.5-3.5 wt.% of the aqueous formaldehyde solution.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Treatment of waste brine produced during            preparation of sodium p-nitrophenolate, involves using            waste brine as electrolyte, and carrying out            electrolytic decomposition under action of catalyst            comprising aerogel carbon and chromium sesquioxide,            dispersed in waste brine.
AU XING M
   LI P
   ZHU Y
   LI W
   SHAO S
   ZHANG Y
AE HEBEI HAILI FRAGRANCES CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 2022A5170U
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment of waste brine produced during                preparation of sodium p-nitrophenolate, involves                using the waste brine as electrolyte, an inert                polar plate as positive electrode, and an iron                plate as negative electrode, and carrying out                electrolytic decomposition under the action of                direct current electric field and a catalyst                comprising aerogel carbon and chromium sesquioxide                loaded on the aerogel carbon, dispersed in the                waste brine.
   USE - Treatment of waste brine produced during                preparation of sodium p-nitrophenolate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses a catalyst having strong                chloride ion resistance, which enables direct                electrolytic decomposition of brine, does not                require dilution in advance, and saves a lot of                water. Under the action of catalyst and direct                current electric field, the organic matter in the                waste brine is decomposed into carbon dioxide,                water, or carbon dioxide, water, and small molecule                carboxylic acid organic matter. The small molecule                substance does not generate dioxins, which makes it                more environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The catalyst is obtained                  by dissolving chromium sesquioxide in 2-7 %mass                  bromate aqueous solution at 75-90&#8451;,                  adding aerogel carbon, and sequentially carrying                  out adsorption and curing. The mass ratio of                  chromium sesquioxide and bromate aqueous solution                  is 1:20-50. The mass ratio of chromium                  sesquioxide and aerogel carbon is 1:30-60. Curing                  is carried out at 105-120&#8451; for 3-5                  hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  catalyst and waste brine is 1:100-200.Preferred Method: After electrolytic                  decomposition, the method further involves                  filtering the electrolytically decomposed                  material liquid to obtain a filtrate and a filter                  residue, which is recycled as catalyst, adjusting                  the pH of filtrate to 2-4, decompressing and                  distilling, and recycling sodium chloride. The                  volume of viscous material obtained by                  distillation is 10-20% of the filtrate                  volume.Preferred Conditions: The direct current                  voltage is 1600-2500 V and the direct current                  voltage duty ratio is 5-10%.

TI Device for complete carbonaceous aerosol analysis            in real time, comprises a system combining two            different instruments, where the first instrument            measures total carbon using flash heating of collected            aerosol samples and generation of carbon dioxide.

   IVANCIC M
   RIGLER M

AB    NOVELTY - The device has a system combining two                different instruments, where the first instrument                measures total carbon (TC) using flash heating of                collected aerosol samples and generation of carbon                dioxide, while the second instrument performs an                optical attenuation analysis wavelengths from near                UV to near IR range in order to characterize                optical absorption of aerosols accumulated on a                glass-fiber/polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) filter                tape. The device, i.e. the system of the                instruments, collects and processes collected data                of both instruments.
   USE - Device for complete carbonaceous aerosol                analysis in real time. The device can also be used                for detecting and quantifying carbonaceous                matter.

PN CN114777281-A
TI Pre-warning control method for air conditioner,            involves determining target early warning model based            on user information of user to be identified and user            information-early warning model relation.
AU CAI Z
   JING T
   MA Z
AE HAIER SMART HOME CO LTD (HIER-C)
   QINGDAO HAIER AIR-CONDITION ELECTRIC CO (HIER-C)
   QINGDAO HAIER AIR-CONDITIONER CO LTD (HIER-C)
GA 2022A3875C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining user                information. A user information-pre-warning model                relation is constructed. A dangerous situation                information is obtained in a target space of an air                conditioner. The user information of a user to be                identified in the target space is obtained. A                target early warning model is determined based on                the user information of the user to-be identified.                The air-conditioner is controlled to pre-warn                according to the target early warning model.
   USE - Method for controlling pre-warning of an air                conditioner (Claimed) for detecting concentration                of carbon dioxide in air and based on carbon                dioxide concentration for fire identification and                fire alarm.
   ADVANTAGE - The method satisfies for pre-warning                requirement of different users, which is good for                realizing effective early warning.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) an                early warning control device; and (2) a computer                readable storage medium.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of an early                warning control method. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

PN CN114653092-A
TI Extracting industrial hemp useful as fragrance            additive comprising tobacco products comprises taking            industrial hemp flower leaf as raw material, propylene            glycol as cosolvent for supercritical carbon dioxide            extraction, carrying out molecular distillation, and            collecting obtained light component.
AU LI Q
   LI R
   CHANG T
   GAO W
   ZHAO L
   ZHENG J
AE YUNNAN HEMPMON PHARM CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 202286731K
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting industrial hemp comprises (i)                taking industrial hemp flower leaf as raw material,                propylene glycol as cosolvent for supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction, obtaining the extract ,                and (i) carrying out molecular distillation to the                extract , and collecting the obtained light                component.
   USE - The essential oil is useful as a fragrance                additive comprising tobacco products, electronic                cigarette oil, daily chemical products, food or                medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses the combination of                supercritical CO2 extraction technology and                molecular distillation technology; is simple and                convenient; has mild condition; and is suitable for                industrial application of large scale. The                essential oil: has rich and full-bodied aroma; has                full fragrance, exquisite, no impure gas, will not                introduce addictive substance and other harmful                components; is economical; has no addiction and                other toxicity and side effect, and has application                prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMs are also included                for:
   Industrial hemp essential oil.
   Electronic cigarette oil comprising 0.1-1 wt.%                hemp essential oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electronic                  cigarette oil further comprises 1-10 wt.%                  aromatic base and remaining amount of polyol                  solvent. The aromatic base comprises 5-15 wt.%                  flavor and remaining amount of polyalcohol                  solvent. The flavour comprises 0.5-2 pts. wt.                  methyl pyrrolidone, 0.5-2 pts. wt.                  p-hydroxyphenylethyl alcohol, 0.5-2 pts. wt.                  maltol, 0.5-2 pts. wt. dihydroactinide lactone,                  0.5-2 pts. wt. 4-hydroxy-beta-dihydro-damamarone,                  and 0.1-2 pts. wt. giant bean triketene. The                  polyol solvent comprises glycerol, propylene                  glycol and/or polyethylene glycol.Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises                  extracting at a pressure of 10-50 MPa, extraction                  temperature of 30-50&#8451;, and extraction                  time of 2-4 hours, ensuring the quality of the                  entraining agent of 0.8-1.2 times the quality of                  the industrial hemp mosaic, and ensuring the flow                  rate of supercritical carbon dioxide at 10-50                  times/hour. The step (ii) comprises performing                  molecular distillation by a scraper molecular                  distillation, ensuring the vacuum degree of                  0.01-1 MPa, distillation temperature of                  60-100&#8451;, and ensuring the rotating                  speed of the scraper at 150-350                  revolutions/minute. The step (i) further includes                  drying and pulverizing the industrial hemp mosaic                  before performing supercritical CO2 extraction,                  and passing through a 1-4 mesh sieve.

PN WO2022129258-A1
TI Method for treating offgas produced in processing of renewable feedstock, involves directing offgas stream to biological unit, converting majority of hydrogen sulfur in unit to elemental sulfur, and producing carbon-chromium-rich gas stream.
AU JANSSEN A H
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
GA 202281663J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves hydrotreating a renewable feedstock to produce an effluent comprising a hydrotreated liquid and a vapor phase comprising hydrogen, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and carbon monoxide. The effluent is separated into liquid streams and offgas streams (20), where one of the off-gas streams comprises carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfur. The offgas stream is directed to a biological desulfurization unit (18). A majority of the hydrogen sulfur in the unit is converted to elemental sulfur (22), and a carbon-chromium-rich gas stream is produced.
   USE - Method for treating an offgas produced in a processing of a renewable feedstock.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effectively treating the offgas produced in the processing of the renewable feedstock.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating a method for treating offgas produced in processing of renewable feedstock.
   Hydrotreating reactor (14)
   Separation unit (16)
   Biological desulfurization unit (18)
   Offgas streams (20)
   Elemental sulfur (22)
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC H04-A01; H04-A07; J01-E02H3; J01-E03H
IP B01D-053/52; B01D-053/84; C10G-003/00; C10K-001/00; C10K-001/12

PN JP2022086037-A
TI Ventilation control system for controlling ventilation of facilities, has control device which makes ventilator ventilate so as to satisfy reference value of frequency of ventilation in predetermined period with respect to first and second regions.
AU SUZUKI Y
   KINOSHITA T
   FUJIZONO T
   INAGAKI J
AE PANASONIC INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY MANAGEME (MATU-C)
GA 2022793536
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a ventilator (20) which performs the ventilation to a first region (10a) and a second region (10b) different from each other, and a control device (40) for controlling the ventilation in the ventilators. The control device is provided with respect to each of the first regions and the second region. The temperature control time zone of temperature control by the air conditioning device is given priority. Exhaust air from the first region is aggregated. An exhaust air passage (52) discharges from one exhaust port to the outdoors. A carbon dioxide sensor is provided for measuring the concentration of carbon dioxide in the exhaust air passages.
   USE - Ventilation control system for controlling ventilation of facilities.
   ADVANTAGE - The number of times of ventilation is efficiently secured.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the configuration of a house. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   First region (10a)
   Second region (10b)
   Ventilator (20)
   Control device (40)
   Exhaust air passage (52)

PT P
PN JP2022083507-A
TI Producing hydrogen comprises accelerating photochemical reaction using renewable organic resources such as tea cake, iron ions, water and light and/or heat energy including UV rays, visible rays and infrared rays.
AU TAKAGI Y
   HONDA A
   AOKI T
   FUCHIGAMI Y
   TANIMOTO R
   TANAKA H
   MOCHIZUKI R
   TAKAGI S
   NISHIMURA S
AE SHIZUOKA SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (SHIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2022779348
AB    NOVELTY - A method for producing hydrogen comprises accelerating a photochemical reaction using renewable organic resources such as tea cake, iron ions, water, and light and/or heat energy including UV rays, visible rays and infrared rays.
   USE - The method is used for producing hydrogen by using renewable organic resources such as tea lees, and iron ions and water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the cost for synthesizing a photocatalyst and enhances the reduction efficiency of iron ions, and provides hydrogen production apparatus using reductive organic matter and light. The hydrogen can be produced with less power than conventional water electrolysis. The method reduces production cost, without using fossil resources, disassembled energy and electrolysis of water and free from discharge of carbon dioxide, so the generation of carbon dioxide is avoided. The method efficiently uses the renewable organic resources that are the waste materials. The method increases the purity of the produced hydrogen gas, adopts simple-structured equipment and is easy to scale.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The method uses renewable organic resources such as tea lees, iron ions and water, light energy including UV rays, visible light rays and infrared rays and/or heat energy to promote photochemical reaction, and making components of renewable organic resources such as tea lees as hydrogen sources. The method supplies the iron ions without excess or deficiency by using an iron-carbon battery, and also recovers electric power generated by the iron-carbon battery. The method comprises continuously supplying the renewable organic resources to be used in a hydrogen production apparatus, stirring the supplied renewable organic resources in a hydrogen production apparatus and continuously discharging the used renewable organic resources. The method involves dissolving carbon dioxide generated with hydrogen generation in water to make carbonate ions, forming iron carbonate with iron ions eluted from the iron carbon battery, separating from the gas, and increasing the purity of the generated hydrogen gas. The method comprises dissolving carbon dioxide generated with hydrogen production in water to generate hydrogen ions, an optimum pH for photochemical hydrogen gas production using a polyphenol iron complex is created, and optimum pH is maintained by an iron carbon battery, and the method increases the sustainability of hydrogen gas production. Preferred Components: The complex of polyphenol and iron ion contained in the renewable organic resource is used. The renewable organic resource contains any material such as tea cake, tea leaves, coffee cake, coffee beans, fruit juice, foliage juice, and plant dry distillate. The tea cake, tea leaves, coffee cake, and coffee beans used as the renewable organic resource are in a prototype state, a broken state or a powder state.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E11-N; E11-P; E31-A02A; J04-X
